Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: これは‥‥古い写真、か。 ２枚かさねて、おいてあるぞ。
English: What's this? Old photos? There are two lying here.

Japanese: 写真のウラに、エンピツ書きで メモが書いてある。
English: Something's been written in pencil on the backs.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件・資料１》 《ＤＬ６号事件・資料２》か。
English: "DL-6 Incident - Exhibit A," "DL-6 Incident - Exhibit B"...

Japanese: どっちの写真を見よう‥‥？
English: Let's take a look at these...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... どこかで 見たようなフンイキが‥‥。
English: I'm sure I've seen this person somewhere...

Japanese: ‥‥この写真、ちょっと借りるか。
English: Perhaps I'll borrow this photo.

Japanese: １枚ぐらい持っていっても、 まあバレないだろう。
English: I'm sure no one will miss just one little photo.

Japanese: 何かの手がかりに なるかもしれない‥‥借りよう。
English: And it might be a valuable clue... I'll take it for now.

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を 法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Photograph quietly added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なぜ、大先生はこんな男の 写真を‥‥？
English: Why would Grossberg have a picture of this man...?

Japanese: 何かの手がかりに なるかもしれない‥‥借りよう。
English: This might be a valuable clue... I'll take it for now.

Japanese: ‥‥借りるだけだよ。 あとで返しておくさ。
English: I'm just borrowing it. I'll return it later.

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を 法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Photograph quietly added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、今はまだ いらないな。
English: I don't need it now, whatever it is.

Japanese: 他に手がかりがなくなったら、 また来ればいいや。
English: If I ever run out of clues, I'll know where to find these.

Japanese: （そのときまで、この写真が あるかどうか、わからないけど）
English: (Although I can't be sure the photos will still be here then...)

Japanese: デスクの上の写真だ。
English: A photo lies on the desk.

Japanese: 今、持っている写真と とりかえようかな‥‥？
English: Maybe I should switch it with the one I took?

Japanese: よし。とりかえよう‥‥。
English: I think I'll swap them...

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Photograph added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、やっぱり やめておこう。
English: No, on second thought, I'd better not.

Japanese: デスクの上の写真だ。
English: A photo lies on the desk.

Japanese: 今、持っている写真と とりかえようかな‥‥？
English: Maybe I should switch it with the one I took?

Japanese: よし。とりかえよう‥‥。
English: I think I'll swap them...

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Photograph added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、やっぱり やめておこう。
English: No, on second thought, I'd better not.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: （やれやれ。 あいかわらず、ルスか‥‥）
English: (*sigh* He's out again?)

Japanese: （あれ。机の上の写真が なくなってるぞ）
English: (Huh? No more photos on the desk...)

Japanese: あ、あれ？
English: Wait a second...

Japanese: 昨日はここに、巨大な絵が かざってあったような。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。 よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: さっきまであった写真が なくなっているな。
English: Those photos that were lying there are gone now.

Japanese: センセイ、一度は 帰ってきたのかな‥‥。
English: I guess Grossberg must have stopped in.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: （まったく。どこを ほっつき歩いているんだか‥‥）
English: (Alright, this is getting ridiculous. Where the heck is that loafer?)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: （お‥‥この、ワンパターンな セキ払いは‥‥）
English: (Ah, that old familiar clearing of the throat...)

Japanese: あー、またチミかね。
English: Ah hah! You again.

Japanese: どうも、センセイ。
English: Um, hello, Mr. Grossberg.
Well, well.

Japanese: なかなか ミゴトぢゃったな、今日は。
English: You were quite the thing, my boy.
Excuse me...?

Japanese: 法廷ぢゃよ、法廷。
English: The trial! The trial!

Japanese: （なんだ、見にきてたのか）
English: (He was there...?)

Japanese: 若いコロのワシを 見ておるようぢゃったワイ。
English: Reminded me of myself when I was a youth!

Japanese: （まあ、千尋さん仕込み ‥‥だからな）
English: (I guess something got passed down through Mia... maybe?)

Japanese: 思い出すのォ、あのコロを。
English: It brings back memories, it does!

Japanese: “青春の日々はぁ‥‥青いレモンの カオリぃ‥‥”かァ。
English: "Ah... the days of my youth... like the scent of fresh lemon..." you see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... もうしわけない。
English: I apologize.

Japanese: ？ ? ワシも‥‥ダメに なってしまったもんぢゃ。
English: I... It was all a bit too much for me, my boy.

Japanese: 今日のチミを見て、 ワシは‥‥。
English: Seeing you today, I... well, I...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... （反省するより情報をくれよ！）
English: (I appreciate the reminiscing, but I'd much rather you gave me some information!)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに 巨大な絵があったはずだけど。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。 よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: さっきまであった写真が なくなっている。
English: That photo that was lying there is gone now.

Japanese: １枚、借りていること、 やっぱりバレてるのかな？
English: Hmm... I wonder if someone noticed that one was missing?

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: 見にきていたんですね、法廷。
English: So you came to see the trial?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、の。
English: Yes, yes I did.

Japanese: ゆんべは、気になって ねむれなかった‥‥。
English: Something was bothering me all last night, you see. I couldn't get a wink of sleep.

Japanese: え？　どうしてですか？
English: Really? What was that?

Japanese: そりゃあチミ。千尋クンの 妹さんのコトぢゃからの。
English: Well, you see, it's just... Mia's sister, that poor girl.

Japanese: ‥‥チミには、ほんとうに カンシャしとる。
English: My boy, I owe you my thanks, truly.

Japanese: もし、負けそうになっていたら、 ワシは‥‥どうしていたぢゃろ。
English: I don't know what I would have done if things had gone poorly for the girl.

Japanese: （そんなに心配なら、なぜ 引き受けてくれなかったんだ？）
English: (If you were that worried about it, why didn't you offer to defend her!?)

Japanese: 依頼を断った理由。 昨日も聞きましたけど‥‥
English: I asked before, but, why did you refuse her request for defense?

Japanese: やっぱり、教えて もらえませんか？
English: I think I have a right to know.
A right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: すまん‥‥ す、少し考えさせてくれんか‥‥。
English: No, no, I'm sorry. It's just, I need more time to think about it, my boy.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... （かなり、なやんでいる みたいだけど‥‥、）
English: (He does seem troubled about something...)

Japanese: （口を開かせるためには、もっと 材料が必要なのかもしれない）
English: (Maybe I can find something that will inspire him to talk.)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに 大きな絵がエラそうに‥‥。
English: The other day I'm sure there was a big, pretentious-looking painting on that wall...

Japanese: “エラそうに”‥‥？
English: "Pretentious-looking"...?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、いえ。
English: Well... you know the one I mean.

Japanese: でも、どこ行っちゃったんですか？ ‥‥あれ。
English: Anyway, where did it go?

Japanese: う、うん‥‥その。
English: Ah, yes, well...

Japanese: あ、あきちゃったんでな。
English: I... got rid of it. Got quite tired of the thing, really.

Japanese: ‥‥う、その。 う、売っちゃったんぢゃ
English: I, er, sold it, you see. Yes, that's right.

Japanese: （売った？ そんな、バカな‥‥！）
English: (Sold it!? I'm not sure I buy that...)

Japanese: でも、あの絵、大事なもの だったんじゃないんですか？
English: Wasn't that painting rather important to you, sir?

Japanese: ‥‥何があったんですか？
English: Did something happen?

Japanese: うるさいんぢゃぁっ！
English: I don't see how it's any of your business!

Japanese: もう、絵のことは言わんでくれ！
English: Please, speak no more of that accursed painting.

Japanese: ‥‥ワシだって‥‥ ワシだってなぁ‥‥。
English: I didn't... I...

Japanese: ‥‥ぶつぶつ。
English: *mumble* *mumble*

Japanese: ‥‥星影先生？
English: Mr. Grossberg...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... す、すまん。 今は１人で考えたいのぢゃ‥‥。
English: Ah, my apologies. I need to think about things. Alone.

Japanese: （ぼくのことが目に入って いないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (He barely even glanced in my direction...)

Japanese: すみません。 この写真なんですけど‥‥。
English: Excuse me, I was wondering about this photograph...
Where did you get that!?

Japanese: お借りしていました。
English: I borrowed it.

Japanese: か、返しなさい！
English: Well, give it back at once!

Japanese: 星影先生。 この男のことを教えてください。
English: Mr. Grossberg. Tell me about the man in this photo.

Japanese: こいつは、事件の夜、梅世の 部屋にいたはずなんです。
English: He may have been in that hotel room with April May on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、 ごぞんじなんでしょう？
English: Do you know something about him... anything?
Hmm. Ahem!

Japanese: ‥‥わかった‥‥教える。
English: Very well. I'll tell you what I know.

Japanese: ただし‥‥。
English: However...

Japanese: ワシからの情報だということは、 もらさんでくれ‥‥。
English: You didn't hear it from me, if you follow.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I follow.

Japanese: そいつは 小中　大（こなかまさる）。
English: That man is Mr. Redd White.

Japanese: 情報処理会社《コナカルチャー》 の社長ぢゃ。
English: He's the President of Bluecorp.

Japanese: （コナカ‥‥マサル‥‥か）
English: (Redd White... Hmm.)

Japanese: “情報処理会社”‥‥探偵のような ものですか？
English: Bluecorp... they're something like a detective agency, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ヤツは、他人の情報で 商売する“プロ”ぢゃ。
English: They're information pros. They sell information, you see. Sensitive information.

Japanese: この国で、もっとも タチの悪い男ぢゃよ‥‥。
English: Despite his name, Mr. White has the blackest reputation of any man in this country.

Japanese: もし会うつもりなら、 妙なネタを握られんよう、
English: Should you run across him, watch what you say.

Japanese: くれぐれも注意するんぢゃよ。
English: You wouldn't want him digging up any dirt on you.

Japanese: （‥‥情報で商売する“プロ”か）
English: (Hmm... selling information. Sounds like blackmail.)

Japanese: とりあえず、コナカルチャーの 場所を教えてください。
English: Can you tell me where Bluecorp is located?

Japanese: う、うむ‥‥。
English: Hmm... I suppose.

Japanese: （小中　大、か！）
English: (Mr. Redd White, at last!)

Japanese: （ついに、男の手がかりを つかんだぞ！）
English: (Finally, a lead on this guy!)

Japanese: （梅世に犯行がムリだった以上、 こいつこそ、犯人のはず‥‥！）
English: (Even if April May couldn't have done it, he could have!)

Japanese: （さっそく、行動だ！）
English: (Time to take action!)

Japanese: 《写真》を星影先生に返した。
English: Returned photograph to Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: すまんが‥‥ １人にしてくれんかの。
English: Now, I must ask you to leave.

Japanese: 考えたいことがあるんぢゃ‥‥。
English: I need to think about things. Alone.

Japanese: すみません。 この写真なんですけど‥‥。
English: Excuse me, I was wondering about this photograph...

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥！ か、返しなさい！
English: Where did you get that!? Give it back at once!

Japanese: 勝手に持ち出して すみません。
English: I'm sorry to have taken it without your knowledge.

Japanese: でも‥‥誰なんですか？ この男は。
English: But... I have to know. Who is this man?

Japanese: 事件の夜、梅世の部屋にいたのは、 たぶん、こいつなんです。
English: He was most likely in April May's room the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥！ な、なんだって！ !
English: What's that you say!?

Japanese: ほ、ほ、ほんとうかね！ ‥‥それは！
English: A-are you QUITE sure? My boy?

Japanese: （‥‥な、なんだこの あわてぶりは‥‥？）
English: (What has him so flustered I wonder...?)

Japanese: す、す、すまんが、 帰ってくれんか！
English: I... I beg your pardon, but I must ask you to leave.

Japanese: １人にしてほしいんぢゃ‥‥！
English: I need to be alone.

Japanese: （‥‥！） (...!) （センセイ、なんだか だまりこんでしまったぞ‥‥）
English: (He's fallen silent as a stone. A rather large stone.)

Japanese: （とりあえず、この写真は返そう）
English: (I guess I should return that photo.)

Japanese: 《写真》を星影先生に返した。
English: Returned photograph to Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: すまんが、この件については、 何も言うことはできんのぢゃ。
English: I beg your pardon, but I am not at liberty to speak on the matter.

Japanese: ‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （あれ。センセイ、ぼくに 気づいてないみたいだぞ‥‥）
English: (Huh? I don't think he's noticed me standing here.)

Japanese: （セキ払いでも、してみるか）
English: (Maybe I should... clear my throat?)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: Aaa-HHHEM!

Japanese: ‥‥わっ！
English: Jumping Jehosephats!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃ、チ、チミか！
English: Oh! You!

Japanese: どうしたんですか？　“人生の タソガレ”みたいな顔をして。
English: What's wrong? You looked so pensive... like an old man at the end of his days.

Japanese: むむ、ワシゃまだボケとらんぞ！
English: Hmm? I'm not senile yet!

Japanese: ‥‥この事件のことを 考えておったんぢゃ。
English: I was just thinking about this whole mess...

Japanese: （星影先生‥‥そうとう ツラいことがあるみたいだ）
English: (Something's really bothering him, that much is clear.)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに 巨大な絵があったはずだけど。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。 よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: 見にきていたんですね、法廷。
English: So you came to see the trial?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、の。
English: Yes, yes I did.

Japanese: ゆんべは、気になって ねむれなかった‥‥。
English: Something was bothering me all last night, you see, couldn't get a wink of sleep.

Japanese: え？　どうしてですか？
English: Really? What was that?

Japanese: そりゃあチミ。千尋クンの 妹さんのコトぢゃからの。
English: Well, you see, it's just... Mia's sister, that poor girl.

Japanese: ‥‥チミには、ほんとうに カンシャしとる。
English: My boy, I owe you my thanks, truly.

Japanese: もし、負けそうになっていたら、 ワシは‥‥どうしていたぢゃろ。
English: I don't know what I would have done if things had gone poorly for the girl.

Japanese: 依頼を断った理由。
English: I asked before, but, why did you refuse her request for defense?

Japanese: やっぱり、まだ教えて もらえませんか？
English: I think I have a right to know.
A right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: すまん‥‥ す、少し考えさせてくれんか‥‥。
English: No, no, I'm sorry. It's just, I need more time to think about it, my boy.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... （かなり、なやんでいる みたいだけど‥‥、）
English: (He does seem troubled about something...)

Japanese: （ぼくにも、なんとなく 想像はついてきたんだよな‥‥）
English: (I'm starting to have a feeling I know what it is.)

Japanese: コナカルチャーに行ってきました。
English: So, I paid Bluecorp a visit.

Japanese: ‥‥ほ、ほう、そうかね。
English: Oh? Oh, I see.

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg...

Japanese: 実はさっきから、気になって いることがあるんですよ。
English: I have to admit, something has been bothering me.

Japanese: は、はて‥‥。 なんぢゃろうな。
English: Oh? What is it? Well, out with it, my boy!

Japanese: ええ。それは‥‥
English: You see, it's just...

Japanese: 小中の写真です。
English: That photo of Mr. White...

Japanese: なんで星影先生が、彼の 写真を持っているんですか？
English: Why would you have his photo, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: う、うむむ。 ‥‥昔、ちょっとな‥‥。
English: Umm... yes, well. It's been a long time since then...

Japanese: 昔‥‥ですか？
English: A long time ago?

Japanese: ぼくの考えは違います。
English: I'm sorry... but I think not.

Japanese: あなたと小中は、今でも つながっている。
English: I think you still have ties to Mr. White even today.

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと！ ‥‥バ、バカな！
English: R-really! Whatever gave you that idea!

Japanese: い、いったい、ヤツと どんな関係があると言うんぢゃ！
English: W-what connection could I possibly have to an individual such as he?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。 それは、
English: It's pretty straightforward, actually...

Japanese: ズバリ、飲み友だちです！
English: You go drinking together!

Japanese: どうです？ トモダチなんでしょ、２人は？
English: You're friends, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Now you're being foolish, my boy.

Japanese: なんでワシが、あんなヤツと 飲まにゃならんのぢゃ！
English: Why in the world would I ever drink with the likes of him!?

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Whoops... maybe that wasn't it.)

Japanese: あなたは、小中に弱みを 握られていますね？
English: Mr. White has something on you doesn't he? Blackmail?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! し、知らん‥‥！ なんのことか‥‥。
English: I don't... I don't know what you're talking about!

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg, sir...

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵が かかっていましたよね。
English: There was a giant painting hanging right there the other day, was there not?

Japanese: “ゼッタイに手ばなさない”と 言っていた、あの絵です。
English: The one you said you had "no intention of parting with"?

Japanese: 見かけたんですよ、今日。
English: Well, I saw it. Today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office at Bluecorp. Redd White's office.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... そうか‥‥。気がついたか。
English: So... you noticed.

Japanese: ま、あれだけデカい絵ぢゃ。 ‥‥気づくわな、そりゃ。
English: I suppose I should have guessed you would. It is a large painting...

Japanese: 話してください。 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg... tell me what happened.

Japanese: ズバリ、小中はあなたの 情報提供者なんじゃないですか？
English: Redd White is supplying you with information, obviously!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと？
English: What's that now?

Japanese: あなたはなぜか、彼のことを かばおうとするフシがあります。
English: You've been protecting him all along.

Japanese: それは、あなたが彼の会社を 利用しているからでしょう！
English: You protect him because you need his services!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Now you're being foolish, my boy.

Japanese: 誰が、あんなヤツに たのむもんか‥‥！
English: What possible use could I have for the services of the likes of him!?

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Whoops... maybe that wasn't it.)

Japanese: 最初から、不自然でした。
English: I thought it was strange.

Japanese: かわいい弟子だった千尋さんの妹が 巻き込まれた事件なのに、
English: Here was Maya, your own apprentice's little sister, in desperate need...

Japanese: それを引き受けないなんて。
English: Accused of killing her own sister no less, and you wouldn't take the case!

Japanese: ‥‥その理由が、わかりました。
English: I finally realized why.

Japanese: あなたと小中には、ある関係が あるんです。それは‥‥
English: You and Redd White are connected. He's...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、先輩と後輩の 関係にあるんじゃないですか？
English: I'll bet you and White go way back! He's your boss now, and was before, I bet!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃ？ ‥‥せ、先輩？
English: My what? Boss? Are you serious?

Japanese: なんか、お２人は同じニオイが ただよっていますからね。
English: Yeah! You both have that sort of "we're in the same club together" feel to you.

Japanese: ‥‥やめてくれ。
English: Please, stop.

Japanese: 冗談は、それぐらいでいいぢゃろ。
English: You have a very peculiar sense of humor, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。話そう。
English: Very well, I'll talk.

Japanese: （な、なんだよ冗談って‥‥）
English: (What does he mean... "sense of humor"? I was serious!)

Japanese: ぼくには理解できませんが‥‥、
English: It's not something I can claim to understand...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、愛しあって いるんですね？
English: But you and Mr. White are lovers, aren't you!

Japanese: な、な、な、なんぢゃ！
English: W-w-what! My boy!

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵が かかっていましたよね。
English: The other day, there was a giant painting hanging on that wall.

Japanese: あの絵、今日、 見かけたんですよ。
English: I saw that painting again, today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office of Bluecorp.

Japanese: プレゼント、だったんでしょう？
English: It was a present, wasn't it?

Japanese: い、いったいどこから そんな発想が出てくるんぢゃ！
English: Well, my boy, you have a splendid imagination!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには理解できません‥‥。
English: I... I don't understand how you could...

Japanese: 理解できん発想をするな！
English: That's because I'm not, we're not... Don't be ridiculous!

Japanese: （おかしいな‥‥ぼくの 見込み違いだったか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I guess I was wrong. But it just seemed so right!)

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg, sir...

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵が かかっていましたよね。
English: There was a giant painting hanging right there the other day, was there not?

Japanese: “ゼッタイに手ばなさない”と 言っていた、あの絵です。
English: The one you said you had "no intention of parting with"?

Japanese: 見かけたんですよ、今日。
English: Well, I saw it. Today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office at Bluecorp. Redd White's office.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... そうか‥‥。気がついたか。
English: So... you noticed.

Japanese: ま、あれだけデカい絵ぢゃ。 ‥‥気づくわな、そりゃ。
English: I suppose I should have guessed you would. It is a large painting...

Japanese: あなたと小中の間には、 何か“関係”があるハズです。
English: Mr. Grossberg, I know you and Mr. White are connected somehow!

Japanese: か、関係だと‥‥？
English: C-connected, you say?

Japanese: そうです！　それは‥‥、
English: Yes! And I know what it is...

Japanese: ズバリ、小中はあなたの 情報提供者なんじゃないですか？
English: You're his client, aren't you? He's giving you information!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと？
English: What's that now?

Japanese: あなたはなぜか、彼のことを かばおうとするフシがあります。
English: You've been protecting him all along.

Japanese: それは、あなたが彼の会社を 利用しているからでしょう！
English: You protect him because you need his services!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... あの絵は、何かの情報に対する お礼として、わたしたのでは‥‥？
English: You gave him that painting in exchange for some valuable information, didn't you?

Japanese: バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Utterly ridiculous.

Japanese: たとえ何があろうと、 あいつにだけは、たのまん！
English: Me, his client? I would never willingly deal with that man. Never!

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Did I get it wrong?)

Japanese: あなたは小中に、何か弱みを 握られているんじゃないですか？
English: Mr. White has something on you doesn't he? Blackmail?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! あの絵が、それを証明していると 思いますけどね。
English: I think that painting is fairly gaudy proof.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... わかった‥‥。ちょうどいい チャンスかもしれん。
English: Very well. This may be the chance I've been waiting for.

Japanese: すべて話して、 そろそろ‥‥ラクになるかのお。
English: Maybe it's time to get this off my chest, so I can finally rest easy again.

Japanese: チミが千尋クンの部下なのも、 何かの運命なのぢゃろ‥‥。
English: After all, you were Mia's understudy. Perhaps it was fate?

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ？）
English: (What's he talking about...?)

Japanese: ぼくには理解できませんが‥‥、
English: It's not something I can claim to understand...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、愛しあって いるんですね？
English: But you and Mr. White are lovers, aren't you!

Japanese: な、な、な、な、な、な、な、 なんぢゃ！
English: W-w-what! My boy!

Japanese: あの絵は、あなたから彼に 贈られた、
English: You sent that painting to him!

Japanese: とろけるようなアイの‥‥
English: As a sign! A sign of undying love!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、 ちょっと待ちなさい！
English: M-m-my boy, please! You're letting your fancies run away with you!

Japanese: い、いったいどこから そんな発想が出てくるんぢゃ！
English: Where do you get these bizarre ideas?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには理解できません‥‥。
English: I... I don't understand how you could...

Japanese: 理解できん発想をするな！
English: That's because I'm not, we're not... Don't be ridiculous!
Enough.

Japanese: 話すとしよう。 ‥‥ハジをしのんで、な。
English: I'll swallow my pride and tell you all.

Japanese: （やっぱり、２人は‥‥）
English: (I knew it! They are lovers!)

Japanese: だから、ちがぁう！
English: N-no! We are NOT lovers!

Japanese: 小中　大は、人を脅迫すること で生きておる。
English: Redd White is a man who makes his living through intimidation.

Japanese: 《コナカルチャー》は、人の弱みを 探り出すための調査会社ぢゃ。
English: Bluecorp is a company that excels in finding people's weaknesses, I'm afraid.

Japanese: ‥‥そしてワシは、この１５年間 ヤツにカネを払いつづけておる。
English: I've been paying them for 15 years now...

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》のネタでな。
English: All because of the "DL-6 Incident," as you may have guessed.

Japanese: （あの写真のウラに 書いてあった事件名だ‥‥）
English: (The name on the back of those photographs!)

Japanese: ワシが真宵さんの弁護を 引き受けなかったのは、
English: As you suspected, I could not stand in defense of Maya because of this.

Japanese: チミの考えているとおり、 小中に脅迫されておるからぢゃ。
English: White would have destroyed me if I did.

Japanese: （やっぱり、そうか‥‥）
English: (So that's the connection!)

Japanese: チミには言いにくいことぢゃが‥‥
English: It is hard for me to tell you this, my boy.

Japanese: 小中を逮捕させることは、 ゼッタイに不可能ぢゃ。
English: But arresting Redd White will be nigh on impossible.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか！
English: Impossible? Why!?

Japanese: やつは裁判官・弁護士・検事局長・ 警察‥‥そして、政治家。
English: He has information on everyone. It gives him an iron grip!

Japanese: 司法の要人のネタをにぎって、 自由にあやつっておるのぢゃ。
English: He owns judges, attorneys, prosecutors, police... and politicians.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですって‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: みんな、‥‥自分がキズつくことは できんのぢゃよ。
English: They are bound, unable to do harm to themselves, and therefore, to him.

Japanese: そんな目でワシを見るな‥‥。 それが‥‥老いというものぢゃ。
English: Don't look at me like that. What you see is nothing more than the weight of many years.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》って‥‥ なんですか？
English: What is the "DL-6 Incident"?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号というのは、警察の 事件分類ナンバーぢゃ。
English: "DL-6" is nothing more than the sorting code the police gave the case.
It was 15 years ago now...

Japanese: ワシは、ある霊媒師の 依頼を受けた。
English: I received a request from a medium. A spirit medium.

Japanese: （れ、霊媒師‥‥？）
English: (A medium...?)

Japanese: 名前は、綾里　舞子 （あやさとまいこ）といった。
English: Her name was Misty Fey.

Japanese: （アヤサト‥‥！）
English: (Fey!)

Japanese: そのとおり。 ‥‥千尋クンの、母親ぢゃよ。
English: Indeed. She was Mia's mother.

Japanese: 彼女は、警察の依頼で、ある 殺人事件の被害者の霊媒を‥‥。
English: She had been investigating a murder at the bequest of the police.

Japanese: そして‥‥失敗したんぢゃ。
English: And... she failed.

Japanese: そのため、彼女は警察から サギで訴えられかけておった。
English: As a result, the police called her a fraud.

Japanese: （‥‥昨日、真宵ちゃんが 話していた事件だ！）
English: (This is what Maya was talking about the other day!)

Japanese: ワシは彼女のために はたらき、助けてやった。
English: I did all I could for her, and in the end, cleared her of wrongdoing.

Japanese: 結局、その殺人事件は いまだ未解決、なんぢゃよ‥‥。
English: That murder case, however remains unsolved to this day.

Japanese: それが、ＤＬ６号事件、ぢゃ。
English: That case, is the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: それで、星影先生はなぜ、 小中に脅迫を‥‥？
English: But why were you blackmailed over this, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件は当時、 トップシークレットぢゃった。
English: The DL-6 Incident was top- secret at the time.

Japanese: 当然ぢゃな。 警察が霊媒にたよったなど、
English: It made sense. The police didn't want people to know they were using a medium!

Japanese: ゼッタイ、 知られてはならん。
English: They couldn't let people know.

Japanese: しかし。
English: But one person found out.

Japanese: このワシが‥‥ もらしてしまったのぢゃ‥‥。
English: I... I told him.

Japanese: 小中に‥‥ですか。
English: You told White?

Japanese: いっときの、カネにつられてな。 なさけない話ぢゃ。
English: He offered me riches... It is an embarrassment to me now.

Japanese: ワシがしゃべったせいで、警察は 歴史的なハジをさらした。
English: Because I talked, the police were mocked far and wide.

Japanese: 彼らは、情報をもらした人間を、 ウラで探し始めたんぢゃ。
English: In secret, they began looking for the one who sold them out.

Japanese: それを知った小中は、 ワシの前にふたたびあらわれた。
English: Of course White heard about it, and he came to me.

Japanese: こんどは、ワシを脅迫 するために、な‥‥。
English: Only this time, the offer was blackmail.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥そうだったんですか‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 小中は、この国の司法を 自由に動かすことができる。
English: White controls the law of this country as he sees fit.

Japanese: ‥‥それでもヤツと戦う と言うなら‥‥。
English: Yet if you would still challenge him...

Japanese: 千尋クンの事務所を よく調べてみたまえ。
English: Have a close look at Mia's office.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんの？
English: Mia's office...?

Japanese: 彼女は、小中のことを ずっと追っておった。
English: She followed his every move for years.

Japanese: もしかしたら、何か 記録を残しているかもしれん。
English: She may have recorded something of what she found.

Japanese: すみません。 これ、お借りしていました。
English: Sorry, sir. I borrowed this.

Japanese: やはり、チミか‥‥。
English: Ah, so it was you, my boy.

Japanese: この人は‥‥？
English: Who is this man?

Japanese: ちょいとワケありでな。 ‥‥すまんが、返してくれんか。
English: Umm... it's a long story. I'll be needing that back now.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、しゃべる気は ないみたいだ）
English: (He doesn't want to talk about it for some reason...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、この写真は返そう）
English: (I'd better give him back this photograph.)

Japanese: 借りていた《写真》を 星影先生に返した。
English: Returned borrowed photograph to Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: 千尋さんのファイルに、 こんなものが‥‥。
English: I found this in Mia's files...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり千尋クン、 小中を調べておったか‥‥。
English: So she was investigating Redd White, as I expected.

Japanese: ヤツにこれをつきつけて、 問い詰めてみてはどうかな？
English: Well, if you wanted to challenge him, you could present this in court.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだな‥‥）
English: (Not a bad idea...)

Japanese: すまんが、ワシにわかることは、 ないようぢゃよ。
English: Sorry, my boy. I don't think I can help you with that.

Japanese: ９月　７日　某時刻 板東ホテル　３０３号室
English: September 7 Gatewater Hotel Room 303

Japanese: ‥‥あ、お客サマ。
English: Ah, welcome, sir!

Japanese: 今日はミゴトでございましたね。
English: Quite the performance today, if I dare say so myself.

Japanese: あ、ああ。ありがとう。
English: Oh, um, thanks.

Japanese: きみにはちょっと、 悪いことをしたかな。
English: Sorry for putting you on the spot like that.

Japanese: いえいえ。 ぜーんぜん、かまいません。
English: No, no, not at all, sir!

Japanese: おかげさまで、当ホテルは これから、ビッグになりますので。
English: Your efforts today can only help the Gatewater's "rep," as they say.

Japanese: え、ビッグ？
English: Huh? "Rep"?

Japanese: “殺人犯が盗聴に使った部屋” ということで、
English: Yes! Our reputation will swell as the hotel where the murderer used a wiretap!

Japanese: この部屋にプレミアをつけて、 アザヤカにデビューさせるのです！
English: We can charge a premium for the room, of course. It will be great for business, sir!

Japanese: い、いや。梅世さんは“殺人犯”と 決まったワケじゃあ‥‥
English: Whoa, whoa! Miss May hasn't been charged with murder.

Japanese: そして、このワタクシめも。
English: I, too, will become famous!

Japanese: “殺人犯に茶を運んだボーイ” として、カレイにデビューを‥‥！
English: "The Bellboy who brought the murderer ice coffee"...!

Japanese: （悪いユメでも見てるみたいだ。 ‥‥ぼくも、コイツも）
English: (Why do I feel like we're both stuck in the same bad dream...)

Japanese: ささ。今日はエンリョなさらず、 なんでも、お申しつけください。
English: So! You are our honored guest. Please let me know if there is anything I can bring you!

Japanese: 思ったとおり、造花だ。
English: A vase, as expected.

Japanese: ひまわりとチューリップ以外、 ぼくは花の名前は知らない。
English: I'm not good with flower names, except maybe tulips and sunflowers.

Japanese: テーブルの上には、ボトルと ２つのグラス。
English: A bottle and two glasses rest on the table.

Japanese: はやくかたづければいいのに。
English: Why hasn't he cleaned these up by now?

Japanese: あ。申しわけありませんが、 それには、手をふれませぬよう。
English: Ah, I beg your pardon, sir, but if you could please refrain from touching those...

Japanese: “殺人犯の最後のカンパイ” ということで、
English: It's part of the decor. I call it: "The last drink... before murder!"

Japanese: ホテル界の話題をかっさらう 名所になる予定ですので‥‥。
English: We'll be famous! The talk of the hotel industry!

Japanese: 窓のそとは、今日もいい天気だ。
English: Nice weather again today.

Japanese: すぐ目の前には、もちろん 綾里法律事務所が見える。
English: I can see the Fey & Co. Law Offices, of course.

Japanese: あ。その窓には望遠鏡を置く 予定でございます。
English: Ah, yes. We plan to install a telescope in that window, of course.

Japanese: ３分５００円で“殺人現場を のぞこう！”という‥‥
English: Just $5.00 will earn you three minutes of a "view to a kill"!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... じょ、冗談ですよ、冗談。 あはははは‥‥。
English: J-just kidding, sir. Oh hoh hoh hoh!

Japanese: （いいや、今の目つきは ゼッタイ、本気だったぞ！）
English: (By that look in his eyes I'd say he was more than serious...)

Japanese: シンプルなデザインの ベッドだ。
English: A simple bed.

Japanese: きちんと整えられていて、 目を引くところはない。
English: It's been recently made. Nothing eye-catching here.

Japanese: 静物画‥‥あれ？　“生物画” だったっけ？
English: Ah. A still-scene painting. Wait, should that be "still life"?

Japanese: まあ、そんなような絵が かかっている。
English: Whatever. One of those is hanging on the wall.

Japanese: あれ。まだドライバーが 挟まってる。
English: Huh? There's still a screwdriver stuck in that drawer!

Japanese: あ。それはそのままに しておいていただけますか。
English: Ah, please leave that as it is, sir.

Japanese: “殺人犯が盗聴器をかくしていた 恐怖の引き出し”ということで、
English: That's the "Drawer of Terror, Hiding Place of the Murderer's Wiretap."

Japanese: このホテルの超有名スポットとして デビューする予定ですので‥‥。
English: It's set to become one of the most popular attractions here.

Japanese: （本気だぞコイツ‥‥）
English: (This guy's serious! I don't believe it...)

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: 梅世さんのことだけど‥‥。
English: About Miss May...

Japanese: ええもう。ワタクシには、 最初からわかっておりました。
English: Oh, her? Sir, not to boast but I knew the moment I saw her...

Japanese: このヒトは、やる！　と。
English: "She'd do it!" I said!

Japanese: （‥‥今回、一番のキケン人物は コイツなんじゃないのか？）
English: (Do WHAT!? I'm starting to think the most suspicious person here is this guy!)

Japanese: 梅世さんといっしょにいた 男のことを聞きたいんだけど。
English: I wanted to ask you about the man who was with Miss May...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね‥‥。
English: Ah, yes...

Japanese: なんと申しますか、 “マダムキラー”？
English: He struck me as a real "Lady Killer," if you'll pardon the expression.

Japanese: ‥‥そんな感じでしたね。
English: I knew it from the moment I saw him, sir.

Japanese: ワタクシと同じ、キケンな ニオイのする人物でした。
English: He and I are of the same ilk. We both carry the scent of... danger.

Japanese: （たしかに、こいつは キケンなニオイがするよな）
English: (There we are in total agreement, Mr. Psycho Bellboy.)

Japanese: その男の写真がありましたら、 間違いなくわかるのですが。
English: If you had a photo of that man, I'm quite sure I could identify him.

Japanese: （写真、ねえ‥‥）
English: (A photo. Hmm...)

Japanese: このホテルのこと なんだけど‥‥。
English: Could you tell me about this hotel?

Japanese: ハイ！　実はワタクシ、 １つアイデアがありまして。
English: Absolutely! And on that subject, I have an excellent idea, sir!

Japanese: このホテル、《板東ホテル》 というんですけれど、
English: Currently, this hotel is known as the "Gatewater."

Japanese: それに、サブタイトルを つけたらどうかと思うんですよ！
English: I propose that we add a subtitle!

Japanese: サ、サブタイトル？
English: A subtitle!?

Japanese: 《板東ホテル》 “～殺りくのヤカタ～”
English: The Gatewater Hotel -- Murder Manor --

Japanese: ‥‥とか、そんなやつですよ！ いかがです？
English: Well? What do you think?

Japanese: い、いいんじゃないかな？ （‥‥どうでも）
English: Um... sounds great! (Whatever floats your tea set...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？　この写真。
English: What about this photo?

Japanese: どうかなと申されましても。
English: Well, it's a fine photo, sir.

Japanese: 梅世サマといっしょだったのは、 男性ですよ。
English: However, the person Miss May was accompanied by was, well, a man.

Japanese: （あ、そういえば そうだったな‥‥）
English: (Oh... yeah. Right.)

Japanese: この写真、見てくれるかな。
English: Take a look at this photo.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... このヒトです、刑事さん。
English: That's him, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥いや。ぼく、弁護士さん。
English: Um... I'm the lawyer.

Japanese: イヤですね。わかってますよ！
English: Oh, I know that!

Japanese: やってみたかったんですよ、 こういうの。
English: I just wanted to say "Detective" once. You know how it is.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (No. No I don't.)

Japanese: 間違いなく、梅世サマといっしょに チェックインした男です。
English: Without a doubt, that is the man who checked in with Miss April May.

Japanese: なんでしたら、宣誓書を書いても いいですよ！
English: How about I write an affidavit swearing that that's him!

Japanese: センセイショ？ （どうしよう‥‥）
English: An affidavit? (This guy is way too excited about this...)

Japanese: ああ‥‥じゃ、せっかくだから 書いてもらおうかな。
English: Well, sure. Why not?

Japanese: うわあ。
English: Yes!

Japanese: 一度、書いてみたかったんですよ 宣誓書。
English: I've always wanted to write an affidavit, sir.

Japanese: これで“宣誓書を書いたボーイ” としてデビューを‥‥
English: From hence forth I will be known as the "bellboy who swore the affidavit"!

Japanese: いいから。早く、書く。
English: Just hurry up and write it.

Japanese: 《ボーイの宣誓書》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Bellboy's Affidavit added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （これで梅世も、トボける わけにはいかないだろう‥‥）
English: (Not even Miss May can play dumb to this!)

Japanese: そうだな‥‥。 まあ、今は必要ないから、いいよ。
English: Well thanks, but I don't think that will be necessary.

Japanese: そうですか。では、またいずれ。
English: Oh? Oh, I see. Well later then, if you need it.

Japanese: クドいようだけど、 この人に間違いない？
English: Sorry to press the matter, but are you sure this is him?

Japanese: クドいようですけど、 ‥‥その人です、刑事さん！
English: I, too, am sorry to press the matter, but that is him, Detective!

Japanese: ありがとう。
English: Umm... thanks.

Japanese: ありがとう。これ。
English: Thanks for this.

Japanese: いえいえいえ。
English: No no no, not at all, sir!

Japanese: これで、あの梅世サマを キュッとシメ上げておやりなさい！
English: I hope you'll use it to take that Miss May down hard, sir! Hard!

Japanese: （こいつ、カオはオダヤカだけど、 かなりコーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (Why do I sense an unhealthy level of excitement behind that unchanging smile?)

Japanese: （ティーセットがカタカタ いってるぞ‥‥！）
English: (His tea set is rattling!)

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: 法廷では、このホテルのこと ばかり考えていて、
English: All I could think about during the trial was the hotel...

Japanese: 証拠品のことは、よく聞いて なかったものでして‥‥。
English: I wasn't paying much attention to the evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥いないか、ボーイのヤツ。
English: Hmm. The bellboy seems to be out.

Japanese: ん？　シャワールームで 水音が聞こえるぞ。
English: Huh? The sound of water coming from the shower...

Japanese: “フンフ～ン、ま・る・も・う・け のブル～ス～‥‥”
English: "La-dee-da! Money-Makin'... Money-Makin'... I got the Money-Makin' bluuuues..."

Japanese: ‥‥ゴキゲンにフロそうじを してるようだな‥‥。
English: Someone seems to enjoy washing the showers.

Japanese: 思ったとおり、造花だ。
English: A vase, as expected.

Japanese: ひまわりとチューリップ以外、 ぼくは花の名前は知らない。
English: I'm not good with flower names, except maybe tulips and sunflowers.

Japanese: テーブルの上には、ボトルと ２つのグラス。
English: A bottle and two glasses rest on the table.

Japanese: さわったら、あのボーイに 何をされるかわからない。
English: I'd better not touch them. No telling what the bellboy would do to me.

Japanese: 窓のそとは、今日もいい天気だ。
English: Nice weather again today.

Japanese: すぐ目の前には、もちろん 綾里法律事務所が見える。
English: I can see the Fey & Co. Law Offices, of course.

Japanese: シンプルなデザインの ベッドだ。
English: A simple bed.

Japanese: きちんと整えられていて、 目を引くところはない。
English: It's been recently made. Nothing eye-catching here.

Japanese: 静物画‥‥あれ？　“生物画” だったっけ？
English: Ah. A still-scene painting. Wait, should that be "still life"?

Japanese: まあ、そんなような絵が かかっている。
English: Whatever. One of those is hanging on the wall.

Japanese: まだドライバーが 挟まってる。
English: The screwdriver is still stuck in the drawer.

Japanese: さわったら、あのボーイに 何をされるかわからない。
English: I'd better not touch it. No telling what the bellboy would do to me.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: ９月　７日　某時刻 コナカルチャー　社長室
English: September 7 Bluecorp Inc. CEO's Office

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... （なんだこの‥‥シュールな インテリアは‥‥？）
English: (What's with the surreal décor...?)

Japanese: ワッチュア・ネイン‥‥ッマッ！
English: Welcome! Please furnish me with the title of your personage!

Japanese: （な、な、な、なんだなんだ？）
English: (What the...?)

Japanese: ‥‥名前だよ、ナ・マ・エ。
English: Your name! What's your name?

Japanese: キミのユア・ネイムを聞いたのさ。 ‥‥ワッチュア・ネインッマ！
English: I was just inquirably asking the title that you go by.

Japanese: あ、な、成歩堂、ですけど。 （“ネインッマ！”ってなあ‥‥）
English: Uh... Wright. Phoenix Wright. ("Inquirably"...?)

Japanese: ミスタ・ナルホドーね。 ‥‥ナルホドー‥‥。
English: Mr. Wright, is it? Right, I see...

Japanese: アハン。 もしかして、キミ‥‥、
English: Splendiferous.

Japanese: イングリッシュは ノーサンキュー、なのかな？
English: Perhaps I have intimidated you with my giantesque vocabulary...?

Japanese: （な、なんなんだ このオッサンは‥‥）
English: (What is this guy's problem!)

Japanese: ぼかぁ小中　大（こなかまさる）。 ここの社長、いやプレジデントだ。
English: I'm Redd White, CEO of Bluecorp. You know, Corporate Expansion Official?

Japanese: ちょっと、アメリカぐらしが 長いもんでねえ。
English: My business dealings bring me into contact with the elite of the elite.

Japanese: ニガテなんだよねぇ、ニポンゴ。
English: So I'm afraid I am not used to conversing with the wordily-challenged.

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (What a fruitcake!)

Japanese: バイザウェイ、ところで。
English: Hmmm, let me guess...

Japanese: どうやら、キミは新人弁護士 らしいね‥‥。
English: You are an attorney fresh out of law school, are you not?

Japanese: でなきゃあ、このぼくに会おう なんて、考えるハズがない。
English: That's the only explanation for why you would come to meet me like this!

Japanese: （ど、どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What does he mean by that?)

Japanese: まあいい。
English: No matter.

Japanese: で？　弁護士ふぜいが このぼくに、なんの用かな？
English: So, what business does a "mighty" lawyer have with a man such as myself?

Japanese: （なんだ‥‥この自信に みちたタイド‥‥）
English: (Yipes! This guy's arrogance meter is off the scale!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ミスタ・ナルホドーだっけ？
English: Mr. Wrong, was it?

Japanese: はい。
English: Wright.

Japanese: キミは、自分の立場を わきまえていないねえ。
English: It appears you do not fully grasp your position here.

Japanese: キミはいったい、何者だ？
English: I ask again. Who are you?

Japanese: え‥‥べ‥‥弁護士ですけど。
English: Umm... huh? A lawyer?

Japanese: “しがない”弁護士だ！
English: No, my feeble friend. A "mere" lawyer.

Japanese: くだらない存在なんだよキミは！
English: Worth nothing. Zilch. Zippo. Nada!

Japanese: あのホシカゲ弁護士のように、ね！
English: Just like that sorry excuse for an attorney, Grodyburger!

Japanese: （な、なんだって！）
English: (Wh-what!?)

Japanese: ‥‥うわっ！ぐっ‥‥！
English: Ungh! Oof...!

Japanese: う‥‥ううっ！ （な、殴られた‥‥！）
English: Unh... uhhh... (He... he punched me!)

Japanese: どうだい？　暴行罪で、 ぼくを訴えてみるかい？
English: Well, Mr. Lawyer. What will you do, eh? Charge me with assault?

Japanese: ‥‥でもね。有罪になるのは、 なぜかキミの方なんだよ！
English: Charge away, I welcome it! For it is YOU who will be found guilty!

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: いいか！　ポリスや検事局は、 ぼくの言いなりなんだ。
English: Heed my exposition! The police, the courts, they all do my bidding.

Japanese: （こいつ、さっきからそんなことを 言っているが、）
English: (So you say...)

Japanese: （いったい、なぜ‥‥？）
English: (But I wonder... Is that kind of control really possible?)

Japanese: キミごときには、理解できない 世界だよ。
English: I don't expect you to understand. It is a world beyond your compensation.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... キミは、ホシカゲのところから 来たんだね？
English: You came here from Grodyburger's, I presume?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうですけど‥‥。
English: Mr. Grossberg's... yes.

Japanese: 聞いてみたまえ。彼に。 なぜ、この絵がここにあるか！
English: Then you must ask him: Why is it that this painting of his hangs here?

Japanese: あのジイさんに 教えてもらうといい。
English: Perhaps then he will tell you?

Japanese: 人間が、どれだけ自分勝手に 生きることができるか、をね。
English: Perhaps he will explain how a man can live life purely for personal profit!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ゴー・ホーム、帰んな。 ‥‥もう、話すことはない。
English: Go now! Skedaddle! There is nothing more to discuss!

Japanese: チキューを背負った男の像だ。
English: A statue of a man holding up the world.

Japanese: “コナカルチャー”‥‥の 社名がマブしい。
English: The "Bluecorp" sign certainly stands out enough...

Japanese: ‥‥背負っている男のモデルは、 当然、目の前の小中その人だ。
English: The model for the man is, of course, Mr. White.

Japanese: まさに、ビューティフォー‥‥！ キミには、目の毒だったかな？
English: Truly a work of art! But, probably too beautacious for you to appreciate, correct?

Japanese: （誰にとっても、目の毒だよ！）
English: (I think it's a little too "beautacious" for just about anyone to appreciate.)

Japanese: とりあえずこれは“デスク”と 呼ぶべきシロモノだが、
English: I'm guessing this is supposed to be a desk?

Japanese: ‥‥いやはや。 なんというか、すごいなこれは。
English: My my... This is quite the... thing.

Japanese: ぼくのマイ・バディ‥‥肉体だよ！
English: It is modeled on my body, see?

Japanese: どう？　バツグンの曲線美だと 思わないかい？
English: Well? Does its sleek roundature not... inspire you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ ...? どこかで見たような‥‥。 ニセモノ、だろうか？
English: Haven't I seen this somewhere...? Is this a replica?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、ホンモノしか キョーミがないんだよ。
English: Ridiculosity! I have no interest in anything but originals.

Japanese: そいつは、５億は間違いない ベリー・グーなシロモノさ。
English: That right there is a bona fide original! Worth five million, for sure.
Hmmm...

Japanese: トロフィーがならんでいる。
English: An impressive line-up of trophies.

Japanese: 《審査員特別残念賞》 《最優秀参加賞》
English: "Judges Special Runner-up" "Best Participation"

Japanese: 《審査員努力賞》 《特別がんばったで賞》
English: "Judges Cooperation Award" "Special Good Try Prize"

Japanese: 《審査員》だの《特別》といった コトバが目につくな‥‥。
English: Hmm. The words "Judges" and "Special" kind of stand out.

Japanese: ここは、２０階だてのビルの 最上階だ。
English: This is the top floor of a 20-story building.

Japanese: いかにも“シャチョー”的な ながめが楽しめる。
English: The view is quite... presidential.

Japanese: 梅世さんは、この会社の人間 なんですよね？
English: Miss May is an employee of Bluecorp, is she not?

Japanese: イエス。 秘書をやらせていたよ。
English: Correct. She was my secretariat.

Japanese: しかし、まさかあんなコトを しでかしてくれるとはねえ‥‥。
English: What a shock it was to hear what she has done!

Japanese: “あんなコト”って、 盗聴ですか‥‥？
English: "What she has done"... You mean the wiretap?

Japanese: イエス・アイドゥー！ ‥‥もちろん、そうだよ。
English: Indeed! She is paid to answer phones. Tapping them is NOT in her job description.

Japanese: たしかに、この会社は 情報を集めるのがシゴトさ。
English: She does gather information for us as part of her duties.

Japanese: でも、トーチョーなんて、 そんなヒレツなことは、
English: But, I assure you, we do not condone illegal methods!

Japanese: ネヴァー！ けっして、しないのさ。
English: It is ineffable that she would do this.

Japanese: （どうやら、梅世さんを見捨てる つもりらしいな‥‥）
English: (It sounds like he's trying to turn Miss May into a scapegoat...)

Japanese: 事件があった夜は、 ホテルの梅世さんの部屋に‥‥？
English: On the night of the murder were you in April May's hotel room?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。つまらないことは 忘れることにしてるんだ。
English: Who can say? I seldom pay attention to mundane details such as time and place.

Japanese: 好きなコトバは “ドンマイドンマイ！”でね。
English: My motto is: "Don't worry, be happy!"

Japanese: いや、でも小中さん。
English: Still, Mr. White...

Japanese: ホテルのボーイが、あなたを ハッキリ覚えているんですよ？
English: The hotel bellboy has stated on the record that he does remember you very clearly.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... エニウェイ。とにかく、 キミに話すつもりはない。
English: No matter! The bellboy can say what he pleases. I still won't talk to you.

Japanese: 聞きたきゃ、ぼくを 証人台に立てることだ。
English: If you want me to speak, put me on the witness stand!

Japanese: ま。キミふぜいでは、ちょっと ムリな話だけどねえ。
English: Although I doubt you'd be capable of doing that.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Hmm. He raises a good question, actually...)

Japanese: （なぜ検察側は、この男を 証人として呼ばなかったんだ？）
English: (Why didn't prosecution call him as a witness?)

Japanese: （松竹　梅世と同じものを “見た”はずなのに‥‥？）
English: (He should have "seen" the same thing as April May!)

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥。
English: Oh hoh hoh.

Japanese: 警察‥‥裁判所‥‥
English: The police... the courts...

Japanese: そんなものはさあ、 オモチャなんだよ。ぼくの‥‥ね。
English: To me they are mere toys. Playthings for my amusement!

Japanese: コナカルチャーって どんな会社なんですか？
English: What kind of company is Bluecorp, anyway?

Japanese: よくぞ聞いてくれたね！
English: Ah, excellent question!

Japanese: いろんな情報を 売ったり買ったりする‥‥、
English: We buy and sell various kinds of information.

Japanese: そんな、未来を先取りした 会社なんだよ！
English: We are a company of the future! You might say, we ARE the future!

Japanese: （情報を‥‥“売る”？）
English: (Sell... information?)

Japanese: この１０年で、ここまで大きな ビッグ・オフィスに育てたんだ！
English: In just 10 years, I've built this business up into the grand office you see now.

Japanese: あ、ちなみにコナカルチャー ってのは、
English: Ah, in case you were wondering, Bluecorp was named after the color "blue"!

Japanese: “小中”と“カルチャー”を ひっかけてるんだよ。
English: I, Redd White of Bluecorp, as founder and CEO, named it so!

Japanese: 気がついたかい？ ‥‥ちょっと、おシャレだろ。
English: And why, you ask? Because I like the color blue of course!

Japanese: ぼくのマイ・アイデアさ！
English: Fantabulistic, is it not?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 ちょっと気になることが。
English: Uh... there's something that's been bothering me.

Japanese: アハン。なんだい？
English: Yes? What might that be?

Japanese: そこの壁にかかっている、 大きな絵ですけど‥‥
English: That big painting on the wall over there...

Japanese: すばらしい絵ですね。
English: It's a very striking piece of art.

Japanese: だろ？
English: Magnificentatious, isn't it!

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （ぼくは、こんな会話を したかったのだろうか‥‥？）
English: (Why am I making small talk...?)

Japanese: 実はぼく、その絵を 見たことがあるんですよ。
English: You know, I've actually seen that painting before.

Japanese: ‥‥ほう。
English: Oh?

Japanese: しかも、昨日見たばっかりです。
English: Just yesterday, actually.

Japanese: ‥‥何が言いたいんだい？
English: Your point being...?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: My point is simple. Er, rather, my question is simple:

Japanese: なぜ、あなたが その絵を持ってるんですか？
English: Why is that painting hanging on your wall?

Japanese: その絵‥‥。 いつ、手に入れたんですか？
English: Say, when did you get that painting?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... いつだったかなあ。 忘れちゃったよ。
English: Hmm... no idea! I forgot!

Japanese: ぼくは、その絵を見たことが あるんです。しかも、昨日。
English: I've seen that painting before. Yesterday, in fact.

Japanese: その絵がなぜ、 今日はここにあるんですか？
English: Why do I find that painting here today?

Japanese: ‥‥シャラップエーン・ゲラウッ！
English: Would you be kind enough to cease your inane chattering and vacate the premises?

Japanese: あえてニポンゴにするなら、 “ダマれ、そして出て行け”だ。
English: Let me put it in language you are sure to understand: Shut up and get out!

Japanese: ‥‥それ以外に 言うべきコトは、ない。
English: I have nothing more to say.

Japanese: この盗聴器ですけど‥‥。
English: About this wiretap...

Japanese: ああ。梅世クンが 使ったヤツだね。
English: Ah, the one that Miss May used, right?

Japanese: まったく、ワルい子だねえ。
English: Bad girl. Bad, bad girl.

Japanese: 彼女は、あなたの指示で 盗聴した、と証言していますよ。
English: She testified that she placed the wiretap at your request.

Japanese: オヤオヤ、ヒトのせいに するなんて。
English: How pathetic. I guess she's trying to pass the buck.

Japanese: さらにワルい子だねえ。
English: Bad, bad girl.

Japanese: ぼかぁね。知らないよ、 なーんにも。
English: Sorry I can't help you. I know nothing.

Japanese: （こいつ、証拠がなければ 何もしゃべらないな‥‥）
English: (I'm going to need hard evidence to get anything out of this guy...)

Japanese: この写真なんですけど。
English: About this picture...

Japanese: オー！ ぼくの写真じゃないか！
English: Gorgeous! That's a picture of me!

Japanese: 若いころだねこりゃあ。 １０年以上、ムカシの写真かな。
English: Ah, I see it was taken in the prime of my youth. About 10 years ago, at a guess.

Japanese: ホテルのボーイは、この人物を 事件の夜に見た、と‥‥。
English: The hotel bellboy says he saw this man on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... なーんだ、よく見りゃあ 他人のそら似、ってヤツか。
English: On closer inspection, he does look a lot like me, but I'm afraid this is someone else.

Japanese: ぼくじゃないよ、それ。 ‥‥ちょっと若すぎるし。
English: Nope. That's definitely not me. He's far too young.

Japanese: ベリベリソーリー。 ‥‥あいすまないね。
English: I am terribly sorry.

Japanese: なんの心当たりも ないなあ。
English: But that really doesn't seem to concern me at all.

Japanese: ヘイ、キミもしつこいね。
English: Well, aren't you persistent.

Japanese: すみません‥‥。でも、 聞きたいことがありまして。
English: Sorry, but there's something I have to ask you.

Japanese: いいかねミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Mr. Lawyer,

Japanese: ぼかぁ、同じコトを２回言うのが 大キライなんだよ。
English: I really hate having to repeat myself...

Japanese: ドン・バザー・ミー！ ぼくのまわりをウロチョロするな。
English: But it seems the message has not yet penetrated your thick skull... Stop bothering me!

Japanese: ‥‥よくないアクシデントは キミにハプニングだよ‥‥。
English: If you try my patience further, I fear a nasty accident may occur.

Japanese: このイミ、わかるな？
English: Do I make myself clear?

Japanese: （わからないよ！）
English: (Transparent...)

Japanese: ‥‥ムチャな言いがかりだ。
English: What a bizarre accusation.

Japanese: ナルホドー‥‥ナルホドー‥‥。
English: Mr. Wrong...

Japanese: キミが今するべきことは、なんだ？ ボクを調べることかい？
English: What is it that you should be doing now? Investigating me?

Japanese: ノーノーノー。ちがうね。
English: No no no. I think not!

Japanese: ミス・チヒロを殺害した犯人を 探すこと‥‥そうだろう？
English: You should be searching for the one who killed Miss Mia!

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《ハイ、秘書室でございます》
English: [Secretary's Office, hello?]

Japanese: ミスタ・ナルホドーが お帰りだ。
English: Mr. Wrong will be leaving now.

Japanese: 《かしこまりました。 ただ今、迎えの者を‥‥》
English: [Yes, sir. I'll send someone right away.]

Japanese: 待った‥‥小中さん。 あなたの言うことは、
English: Wait a second, Mr. White...

Japanese: たしかに正しい。全面的に。
English: You are absolutely right.

Japanese: ぼくがするべきなのは、 犯人を探すことだ。
English: I should be looking for the killer now.

Japanese: そして今、ぼくは、 犯人を追い詰めている。
English: And actually, I've done better. I've found him.

Japanese: ‥‥目の前にね。
English: He's sitting right in front of me.

Japanese: 間違っているよ、小中さん。
English: You're wrong, Mr. White.

Japanese: なんだと？
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: 今、ぼくがするべきなのは、 あんたを追い詰めることなんだ。
English: What I should be doing now is going after you!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだい？
English: Just what are you insinuating?

Japanese: 千尋さんは、あなたのことを 調べていた。
English: Mia was on to you. She was keeping tabs.

Japanese: そしてあなたは、梅世を使って 千尋さんの電話を盗聴していた。
English: For this reason you had April May tapping her phone.

Japanese: ‥‥そして殺人事件が起こって、 あなたの資料が消えた。
English: Then, Mia was murdered, and all the documents about you mysteriously disappeared.

Japanese: さて、犯人は‥‥？
English: So, the culprit would be...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... コドモでもわかる。 ‥‥あなただ！
English: Even a child could work it out, Mr. White. You did it!
*beep*

Japanese: 《ハイ。秘書室でございます》
English: [Secretary's office.]

Japanese: 迎えの者はノーサンキューだ。
English: We won't be needing an escort for Mr. Wrong.

Japanese: かわりに、この電話を 検事局長につないでくれ。
English: Instead, please connect me to the public prosecutor's office.

Japanese: 《かしこまりました。 少々、お待ちください》
English: [Of course, sir. One moment please...]

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 《な、なんだ小中クン。 こんな時間に、こまるよ！》
English: [White? That you? What are you doing calling me at a time like this!?]

Japanese: ヘロー、検事局長かい。
English: Hello. Chief Prosecutor?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、気が変わったんでねえ。 明日、証言したいんだ。
English: I've changed my mind. I want to testify tomorrow.

Japanese: 《な、なんのことかね？》
English: [What's this about?]

Japanese: 綾里　千尋の事件だよ。 ぼかぁ、目撃したからねえ。
English: The Mia Fey case. I witnessed the murder, you see.

Japanese: 証言したいんだよ。 とびきりホットなヤツを、さ。
English: And, thus, as a very important witness, I would like to testify.

Japanese: 《ど、どうしたんだ？ 法廷には、出たくないって‥‥》
English: [What? Why now? I thought you said you didn't want to go to court?]

Japanese: シャラップ。 ‥‥気が変わったと言ったろ？
English: Quietude...! I told you I changed my mind, didn't I?

Japanese: あ。それから。
English: Oh, and one other thing.

Japanese: 大至急、誰か警官をここへ よこしてくれ。
English: Send the police over here right away.

Japanese: 目の前に、そいつがいるんだよ。 バカヅラぶらさげてさ。
English: The man is standing right in front of me. He looks dazed but could be violent!

Japanese: 《誰だね、“そいつ”って？》
English: [What? What man?]

Japanese: 決まってるだろ。
English: Are you even listening?

Japanese: 犯人だよ、ハンニン。 ‥‥ぼくが見た、ね。
English: The executioner! The hatchet- man! The liquidator... The killer, man!

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: What!?

Japanese: 《お、おい‥‥小中クン。 き、きみは、また何か‥‥》
English: [Mr. White... this isn't another one of those...]

Japanese: 検事局長。
English: Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: キミは、ぼくに意見できる 立場じゃないだろう‥‥。
English: I do not believe you are in a position to freely offer your opinions to me, correct?

Japanese: とにかく、早く警官をプリーズだ！
English: I'm telling you to send the police, now!

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 言っただろ、ナルホドー。
English: Did I not tell you, Mr. Wrong?

Japanese: キミは、 しがない弁護士だ、と。
English: You are a mere lawyer!

Japanese: ‥‥ミス・チヒロもそうだった。
English: As was Miss Mia.

Japanese: な、なんだと！
English: How dare you!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、キミを告発する。 ミス・チヒロ殺害の犯人としてね。
English: I'll point the finger at you, and you will be tried as Miss Mia's killer!

Japanese: そしてキミには、ロクな弁護士が つかない。ゼッタイに。
English: The case is as good as settled. No lawyer of any worth will defend you.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、この地区の弁護士協会にも トモダチがいるんでね。
English: I have friends in the local lawyer's association, you see.

Japanese: 想像を絶するような、ものすごい 弁護士をプレゼントしよう。
English: You'll be given a lawyer so stupendously inept that they make even you look competent.

Japanese: （く‥‥くらくらしてきた）
English: (I... I feel faint.)

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事、 ただ今、とうちゃくしたッス！
English: Detective Gumshoe, reporting, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！　アンタは たしか、ヤッパリくんッスね。
English: Aaa! Butz! Hairy Butz!

Japanese: ‥‥ナルホドです。
English: Wright, actually. Phoenix Wright. And my friend's name is "Larry"...

Japanese: あ、そうそう、ナルホドッス。 ヤッパリはたしか、殺人犯の‥‥
English: Oh, right! Sorry, pal. Butz was that murderer, right?

Japanese: イトノコギリくん。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 殺人犯をおわたしするよ。
English: I present to you, the man who killed Miss Mia Fey!

Japanese: な、なんスとぉぉぉッ！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: ゴォウィズヒム。 ‥‥つれていきたまえ。
English: Take this despicable human being into custody.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥グッバイ。ナルホドー。
English: Farewell, Mr. Wrong!

Japanese: チキューを背負った男の像だ。
English: A statue of a man holding up the world.

Japanese: “コナカルチャー”‥‥の 社名がマブしい。
English: The "Bluecorp" sign certainly stands out enough...

Japanese: ‥‥背負っている男のモデルは、 当然、目の前の小中その人だ。
English: The model for the man is, of course, Mr. White.

Japanese: とりあえずこれは“デスク”と 呼ぶべきシロモノだが、
English: I'm guessing this is supposed to be a desk?

Japanese: ‥‥いやはや。 なんというか、すごいなこれは。
English: My my... This is quite the... thing.

Japanese: 昨日までは、星影法律事務所に かかっていた絵だ。
English: This painting was, until yesterday, hanging in Mr. Grossberg's office.

Japanese: 小中がなぜ、 この絵を持っているのか？
English: Why does White have it now?

Japanese: そのワケを想像するのは、 むずかしいことではない。
English: It's not hard to imagine a few possibilities.

Japanese: トロフィーがならんでいる。
English: An impressive line-up of trophies.

Japanese: 《審査員特別残念賞》 《最優秀参加賞》
English: "Judges Special Runner-up" "Best Participation"

Japanese: 《審査員努力賞》 《特別がんばったで賞》
English: "Judges Cooperation Award" "Special Good Try Prize"

Japanese: 《審査員》だの《特別》といった コトバが目につく。
English: Hmm. The words "Judges" and "Special" kind of stand out.

Japanese: おおかた、審査員にニラミを きかせて、もらったんだろう。
English: He probably strong-armed them into giving him something.

Japanese: ここは、２０階だてのビルの 最上階だ。
English: This is the top floor of a 20-story building.

Japanese: いかにも“シャチョー”的な ながめが楽しめる。
English: The view is quite... presidential.

Japanese: （今さら、梅世のことを 聞く必要はないだろう）
English: (I think we're past needing to talk about April May.)

Japanese: （それより、今はこの男に 問い詰めるべきことがある！）
English: (I've got to put this guy on the spot, and quick!)

Japanese: ホワッツ？　どうした？ コワいカオ、しちゃって‥‥。
English: Hmm? What's the matter? You seem... distressed!

Japanese: （この男に何を聞いても、 はぐらかされるのがオチだ）
English: (This guy is a pro at pulling the wool over people's eyes.)

Japanese: （それより、今はこの男に 問い詰めるべきことがある！）
English: (I've got to put him on the spot, and quick!)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなにアツく 見つめないでくれるかな‥‥フフ。
English: Stop that! Your hot gaze is giving me goosebumps!

Japanese: （この男には、まともに 聞いてもイミがない！）
English: (There's no point asking someone this crooked a straight question!)

Japanese: （何か、武器になる証拠品を つきつけるべきだ！）
English: (I need some evidence I can use as ammo...)

Japanese: どうかしたかな？ ぼくのカオに、何かついてるかい？
English: What's wrong? Is something stuck to my face?

Japanese: 目と、ハナと、クチ‥‥。
English: Why yes! There's my eyes, and my nose, and my mouth...

Japanese: なーんてね。 ‥‥ガマンしなくていいよ。
English: But of course I jest. You need not restrain your mirth, my friend!

Japanese: これが本場のアメリカン・ジョーク ってヤツさ‥‥ふるえるだろ？
English: It is okay to indulge in my cosmopolitan sense of humor. I will not think less of you.

Japanese: （千尋さんが残してくれた たった１つの手がかりだ‥‥）
English: (This is the only clue that Mia left me...)

Japanese: （これで、勝負をかけるぞ！）
English: (I'd better make this one count!)

Japanese: ‥‥小中さん。見てください。
English: Mr. White... see this?

Japanese: 政治家が自殺したという 新聞記事です。
English: It's an article describing the suicide of a politician.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 彼は、政府の機密基金を 着服していました。
English: He was embezzling secret government funds.

Japanese: ある日突然、 それがスクープされた。
English: Then, one day, word got leaked to the press.

Japanese: ‥‥そして翌日、 彼は自殺しました。
English: The very next day he took his own life.

Japanese: ‥‥それがどうした？
English: And this concerns me how...?

Japanese: この記事は、千尋さんの部屋で 見つけました。
English: I found this article in Mia's office.

Japanese: ミス・チヒロの‥‥？
English: Miss Mia...?

Japanese: 彼女は、こういう記事を たくさんファイルしていた。
English: She had a file filled with articles like this.

Japanese: そして、その記事のほとんどに、 “小中”と書き込んでいました。
English: Every one of them was labeled with a single word... "White."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ...! 小中さん。 あなたは、この政治家を‥‥
English: Mr. White, I know what you did to this politician.

Japanese: 調査していましたね？
English: You were spying on him, weren't you?
"Spying"...?

Japanese: あなたは彼を調べあげて、 着服の事実をつかんだ。
English: You snooped on him, and found out about the embezzlement.

Japanese: それを公表したから、 この政治家は死んだのです！
English: Then, when you revealed it, he was driven to suicide!

Japanese: ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼかぁ、 そんなことは知らないよ。
English: I know nothing of this.

Japanese: そして、カリにそれを やっていたとしても、
English: And, even in the highly unlikely event that I did do such a thing...

Japanese: それは“報道”というものだ。 ‥‥犯罪じゃない、この国では。
English: All I would have done is provide "information." That is not a crime in this country.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥ おちつき払っているぞ）
English: (Gah! This guy's as cool as a cucumber...!)

Japanese: 買収していましたね！
English: You bribed him!

Japanese: バ、バイシュウ？
English: B-bribed!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥今までの話のながれで、 どこからそんなコトバが‥‥？
English: What exactly has led you to say this?

Japanese: （ま、まずい‥‥。つい、 イキオイで言っちまったよ）
English: (Um... gee, that's a good question. I guess I got ahead of myself there...)

Japanese: フン。キミのイマジネーションは、 まあ、そのテイドかもなあ。
English: Hmph. You certainly have an active imagination.

Japanese: （く、くそっ！）
English: (Dammit!)

Japanese: あなたは、 彼を脅迫していましたね。
English: You were blackmailing him!

Japanese: 脅迫‥‥。
English: Blackmail?

Japanese: 彼だけではない。多くの人間が、 あなたの脅迫に苦しんでいるんだ。
English: Not just him, either. You were threatening and coercing hundreds of others!

Japanese: 千尋さんが集めていた自殺記事には すべて、あなたがからんでいる！
English: You were involved in all of the suicide cases that Mia investigated!

Japanese: このコナカルチャーという会社は、 脅迫によってなりたっている‥‥。
English: This company is built on blackmail!

Japanese: そうなのでしょう？
English: I'm right, aren't I?

Japanese: この事件について、キミと トークするつもりはないよ‥‥。
English: I have no intention of parleying with you about this matter.

Japanese: ９月９日　午前９時５２分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 9, 9:52 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥い、い、いよいよ、 なんですね。
English: Well... I guess this is it!

Japanese: うん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: 今日で、この事件はケリがつくよ。
English: One way or another, this case gets decided today.

Japanese: あっ！ べ、弁護士さん、見て‥‥！
English: Aaa! Ph-Phoenix! Look!
(Prosecutor... Edgeworth.)

Japanese: ‥‥昨日、検事局長に呼ばれた。
English: I received a call from the public prosecutor's office yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥？ ? 今日の証人・小中氏が語ることは、 “絶対”だと言われた。
English: He told me that whatever Mr. White says today, it will be the "absolute truth."

Japanese: キミが、彼の証言に対して どんな攻撃をしようと、
English: No matter how you try to attack his testimony...

Japanese: 私が異議をとなえれば、裁判長は かならず聞き入れるだろう、と。
English: If I raise an objection, I have it on good faith that the judge will listen to me.

Japanese: （裁判長にまで、小中のチカラは およんでいるのか‥‥？）
English: (What, does White have the judge in his pocket, too!?)

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、ぼくの有罪は もう決まってる、というワケだ。
English: So... you're saying I'm going to be guilty. End of story?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 私は、有罪判決を勝ちとるため ならば、なんでもする男だ。
English: I will do anything to get my verdict, Mr. Wright. Anything.

Japanese: どうしてなんですか！
English: Why... Why!?

Japanese: あなたはそうやって、 罪のない人を苦しめて‥‥！
English: How can you torment an innocent person like this!?

Japanese: “罪のない”‥‥？
English: Innocent...?

Japanese: そんなことが、どうして 私たちにわかる？
English: How can we know that?

Japanese: 罰を逃れるためならば、彼らは どんなウソだってつく。
English: The guilty will always lie, to avoid being found out.

Japanese: 見わけることなんてできない。 ‥‥それならば。
English: There's no way to tell who is guilty and who is innocent!

Japanese: 私にできることは、１つ。 被告人を、すべて有罪にする。
English: All that I can hope to do is get every defendant declared "guilty"!

Japanese: それが私のルールだ。
English: So I make that my policy.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥。
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: 変わったな、お前。
English: You've changed.

Japanese: え？
English: Hmm?

Japanese: 弁護士さん！ 知ってるの、この人のこと‥‥？
English: Phoenix! You know him!?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 ‥‥手かげんはしないぞ。
English: Don't expect any special treatment, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: べ、弁護士さん‥‥？
English: Ph-Phoenix...?

Japanese: さ、そろそろ開廷時間だね。
English: Well... court will be opening for session soon.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、で、でも！
English: What? But wait!

Japanese: 弁護士さんを弁護する 弁護士さんが、まだ‥‥！
English: Your defense attorney isn't even here yet! He's not...

Japanese: 弁護は、ぼくが自分でやる。
English: I'll be defending myself.

Japanese: えええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: さ。行くよ。
English: Okay, let's do this.

Japanese: ９月９日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第１法廷
English: September 9, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 1

Japanese: これより、成歩堂　龍一の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥本当にいいんですね？
English: Mr. Wright... Are you sure you're up to doing this?

Japanese: はい。 自分の弁護は、自分でします。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I will be defending myself.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Understood.

Japanese: では、御剣検事。 ‥‥冒頭弁論を。
English: Very well. Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: 事件の流れは、すでに あきらかであるから、
English: As the details of the event are already quite clear to the court,

Japanese: 本日は、被告人の犯行を 見た証人の証言を聞いていただく。
English: today we will hear the testimony of another witness to the defendant's crime.

Japanese: いいでしょう。
English: I see.

Japanese: ‥‥では、さっそく呼んで もらいましょう。
English: The prosecution may call its witness.

Japanese: （‥‥話がスムーズに 進みすぎる‥‥）
English: (That went far too smoothly!)

Japanese: （ふつうなら、なぜ小中が 前回の法廷に出てこなかったか）
English: (Why didn't the judge ask Edgeworth why his witness didn't testify before!?)

Japanese: （問題になるはずなのに、 そんなようすはない‥‥）
English: (It's like... it's like he already knows why!)

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥。ムダだとは 思うけど、言ってみるか？）
English: (Hmm. If anyone's going to raise an objection about this, I suppose it's me...)

Japanese: 御剣検事に、お聞きしたい！
English: Mr. Edgeworth, you owe an explanation to the court!

Japanese: その証人はなぜ、綾里　真宵の 裁判には出廷しなかったのか！
English: Why didn't this witness testify in the trial against Ms. Maya Fey!?

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: すまなかったねえ。
English: I'm ever so sorry!

Japanese: 小中氏は多忙な人物なのだ。 そしてあのときは、
English: Mr. White is a busy man, and besides...

Japanese: 松竹　梅世の証言だけで じゅうぶんだと思ったものでねえ。
English: at the time, I thought that Ms. May's opinion was all that would be needed.

Japanese: もうしわけない。
English: Again, my sincerest apologies to the court.

Japanese: ‥‥さすが、御剣検事。 いさぎよいタイド、ですな。
English: Excellent, Mr. Edgeworth. I appreciate your demeanor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... （くそっ。ヤツがカッコイイところ を見せただけで終わっちまった）
English: (Great. He gets to show off and I get nowhere.)

Japanese: （‥‥やめておこう。 ワナが待っているかもしれない）
English: (Forget it. I smell a trap.)

Japanese: では、小中　大（まさる）氏を 入廷させていただこう！
English: I would like to call Mr. Redd White to the stand!

Japanese: 証人の名前をうかがいたい。
English: Please state your full name.

Japanese: イエス・アイアム！
English: You wish to know the title of my personage?

Japanese: 名前を、聞いている！
English: Er... your name?

Japanese: おー！　ソーリー。
English: Yes! That is what I said!

Japanese: アメリカ生活が長いもんでね。 ニガテなんだよ、ニポンゴ‥‥。
English: Oh dear, do my locutions confuse?

Japanese: 名前だッ！
English: Name!

Japanese: （‥‥いいコンビだ）
English: (These two are great together...)

Japanese: ぼかぁ、小中　大。 マサル・コナーカ。
English: My name is Redd White. But my friends call me Blanco Nino.

Japanese: 株式会社《コナカルチャー》の 社長、いやプレジデントだ。
English: I am the CEO, or to use a more common term, the President, of Bluecorp.

Japanese: 被害者の綾里　千尋を ごぞんじか？
English: Did you know the victim, Ms. Mia Fey?

Japanese: ネヴァー・ギブアップ！ ‥‥知らないねえ。
English: That would be a negatory! No, I did not.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 証人は、事件のあった夜 板東ホテルに‥‥？
English: You were at the Gatewater Hotel the night of the murder?

Japanese: イエス。
English: Correct.

Japanese: そこで、事件を目撃 したというわけだ。
English: And you witnessed the murder from there?

Japanese: アハン。 よくわかってるじゃないか。
English: Ahem. Why tell you what you already know?

Japanese: では、小中さん。‥‥《証言》を していただきましょう。
English: Very well, Mr. White. You may begin your testimony.

Japanese: （どうにかして、コイツの証言を くずさなきゃ、おしまいだ‥‥）
English: (If I can't rip this guy's testimony apart, I'm done for.)

Japanese: （まったく、どうしていつもこう、 ガケっぷちに立たされるんだ？）
English: (Why do I always feel like it's the end of the world and I'm the last man standing?)

Japanese: ふっふっふ‥‥。
English: Ho hoh hoh.

Japanese: カクゴはいいかい、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ‥‥。
English: I hope you have made your peace with God, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! 弁護士さん！　がんばって！
English: Let him have it, Phoenix!

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれはそう、ちょうど９時 ごろのことだったよ。
English: Let's see, it was about 9:00, I believe.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、窓のそばで書類を リーディング、読んでいたんだ。
English: I was quietly perusifying... er, that's "reading" to you, some papers by the window.

Japanese: すると、窓のそとで 物音がしたんだよ！
English: Then I heard a bedlam coming from outside!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、その音におどろいて、 向かいのビルを見たねえ。
English: Surprised, I turned to look at the building across the way.

Japanese: すると、ギザギザヘアの男が ロングヘアのレディを襲っていた！
English: It was then I saw him: a spiky-haired man attacking a woman with long hair!

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、キミだよ ミスタ・ベンゴシ！
English: Needless to say that man was none other than you, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、梅世クンを呼んだねえ！ 彼女も、それはビックリだよ！
English: I called Miss May over at once. She, too, was flabbergasted of course.

Japanese: 被害者は、その‥‥、必死に 逃げたが、キミは追いすがった！
English: The victim, she... she ran away, but you gave chase!

Japanese: ジャスト・フィット！ ‥‥それで、ジ・エンドさ。
English: Finally there was a terrible impaction! Then it was all over...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: その目撃に間違いがなければ、 被告人の有罪は決定ですね‥‥。
English: If things occurred as you testify, then I'm afraid the defendant is guilty.

Japanese: では、被告人‥‥‥、あ、いや、 弁護人。
English: Very well, defendant... er, I mean, Mr. Wright.
Your cross-examination...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれはそう、ちょうど９時 ごろのことだったよ。
English: Let's see, it was about 9:00, I believe.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、窓のそばで書類を リーディング、読んでいたんだ。
English: I was quietly perusifying... er, that's "reading" to you, some papers by the window.

Japanese: すると、窓のそとで 物音がしたんだよ！
English: Then I heard a bedlam coming from outside!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、その音におどろいて、 向かいのビルを見たねえ。
English: Surprised, I turned to look at the building across the way.

Japanese: すると、ギザギザヘアの男が ロングヘアのレディを襲っていた！
English: It was then I saw him: a spiky-haired man attacking a woman with long hair!

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、キミだよ ミスタ・ベンゴシ！
English: Needless to say that man was none other than you, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、梅世クンを呼んだねえ！ 彼女も、それはビックリだよ！
English: I called Miss May over at once. She, too, was surprised of course.

Japanese: 被害者は、その‥‥、必死に 逃げたが、キミは追いすがった！
English: The victim, she... she ran away, but you gave chase!

Japanese: 左に向かって、被害者は逃げた。 キミはそれに追いすがった！
English: The victim ran to the left, and you gave chase!

Japanese: ジャスト・フィット！ ‥‥それでジ・エンドさ。
English: Finally there was a terrible impaction! Then it was all over...

Japanese: （‥‥こいつも御剣の手ほどきを 受けているのか‥‥？）
English: (Did Edgeworth "prep" this guy, too, I wonder?)

Japanese: （とにかく、なんとかして 突破口を見つけるしかない！）
English: (Anyway, I'd better find a weakness in there...)

Japanese: その時間は、 どうしてわかるんですか？
English: How do you know what time it was?

Japanese: なぜなら、ぼかぁ常に、 カンペキにパーフェクトだからさ。
English: Because I am always abso-posi-lutely perfect, you know?

Japanese: いやいやいや。 それじゃダメです。
English: No no no, you're not getting away with that!

Japanese: うたぐりぶかい男だね。
English: You are so mistrusting, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: うーん、‥‥秘書‥‥ 英語で何て言ったっけ‥‥？
English: So... what was the proper term for "secretary" again...?

Japanese: とにかく、梅世くんが９時に ルームサービスをたのんだんだ。
English: Anyway, Miss May ordered room service for 9:00.

Japanese: それが届いたときの ことだったからね。
English: It happened soon after the room service arrived.

Japanese: （梅世もそんなことを 言っていたけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm. That's what Miss May said, too.)

Japanese: たしかに、コーヒーを運んだ ボーイは、梅世さんを見ています。
English: True, the bellboy who brought the coffee saw Miss May.

Japanese: でも彼は、あなたは見ていないと 証言している！
English: But he testified that he did not see you at the time!

Japanese: アハン‥‥そのことかい。
English: Ahem. This is your concern?

Japanese: 梅世クンは、部屋のそとで コーヒーを受け取ったからねえ。
English: Silly Lawyer! Miss May received the coffee outside the room!

Japanese: 見えなくてトーゼンだねえ。
English: Of course he could not see me. He would need X-ray vision to pull off something like that!

Japanese: （うーん‥‥）
その時間、証人は何をしていたか？
English: Tell us, what were you doing at that time?

Japanese: （ここを攻撃しても、おそらく ムダだろう）
English: (I don't see anything to gain by attacking him on this point any further.)

Japanese: そのときあなたは、 何をしていましたか？
English: Okay, what were you doing at the time?

Japanese: 窓というのは、綾里法律事務所の 真正面にある‥‥？
English: By window, you mean the one directly across from the Fey & Co. Law Office?

Japanese: イエス！　ホテルの窓は アレしかないよ。
English: Correct! That is the only window, you see.

Japanese: そこで、書類を？
English: And there you were reading... papers?

Japanese: イエス！
English: Correct!

Japanese: ビジネスホテルは、ビジネスを する場所だよキミ！
English: The Gatewater is a business- man's hotel, and I am a busy man who had business to do!

Japanese: “物音”ですか‥‥？
English: A "bedlam"?

Japanese: たぶん、そのときに 襲いかかったんだろうねぇ。
English: It must have been when you attacked, I assume.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥そして？
English: We see. Continue.

Japanese: その音を聞くまでは、書類を 読んでいたわけですか？
English: So you were reading your papers until you heard that sound?

Japanese: まあね。ぼかぁ、ノゾキは シュミじゃないんだなあ。
English: But of course. I am no snoop, peeping out of windows at night.

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (No snoop? Yeah, right! You've made a career out of snooping!)

Japanese: ギ、ギザギザヘア？
English: S-spiky-haired!?

Japanese: その証言は、松竹　梅世の 発言とムジュンしています！
English: What you just said directly conflicts with Miss May's testimony!

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、犯人の体型を “少女”だったと言いました！
English: Miss May clearly stated that the assailant looked like a "girl"!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、目がいいのが ジマンでね！
English: I've always been proud of my eyesight, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: 視力はいくつですか？
English: Just what is your eyesight?

Japanese: 両目を合わせて２．７さ！
English: Counting both eyes... 40!

Japanese: ご、合計しないでください！
English: 40!? Don't add them together!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、それならば、 よく見えるでしょうね。
English: I think the witness is trying to say his eyesight is good.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥裁判長はコイツの 味方か‥‥）
English: (Hey, whose side is the judge on, anyway?)

Japanese: それで、証人はどうしたか？
English: And what did you do then?

Japanese: そのとき、梅世さんは何を していましたか？
English: What was Miss May doing at that time?

Japanese: テレビドラマを見終わって、 泣いていたよ。
English: She had just finished watching a soap opera on the TV, and was weeping openly.

Japanese: あなたは、彼女が盗聴していた ことは知っていましたか？
English: Did you know she had been tapping the Fey office phone?

Japanese: それは、本件とは関係ないことだ。
English: Irrelevant! That has nothing to do with the case at hand.

Japanese: かまわないよ。‥‥答えよう。
English: I care not. I will answer the lawyer's bold inquiry.

Japanese: あれは、秘書が 勝手にやったことさ。
English: Miss May was acting alone when she tapped the phone of this Fey woman.

Japanese: ‥‥政治家みたいな 口のきき方ですね‥‥。
English: You'd make a good politician, Mr. White.

Japanese: ふふん。‥‥ぼかぁ、 “プレジデント”だからねえ。
English: Ho hoh! After all, I am El Presidente.

Japanese: それで、どうなりましたか？
English: Please continue.

Japanese: もう少し、 くわしく思い出せませんか？
English: Can you be a little more detailed about that?

Japanese: 大事なことですから。
English: I think it's worth knowing exactly what happened.

Japanese: アンダスタン、アンダスタン。 ‥‥了解したよ。
English: Of course! Comprende! I understand!

Japanese: 被害者は、キミの攻撃を受けて、 そう、左に逃げたんだ。
English: The victim was attacked, by you, and ran to the left.

Japanese: キミは、それを追いかけていって、 殴りつけた！
English: You gave chase, and struck her down!

Japanese: 間違いないだろうか？
English: Are you sure?

Japanese: ぼかぁ常に、カンペキに パーフェクトだからね！
English: As you know, I am always abso-posi-lutely perfect!

Japanese: では、そのように証言を 変えてもらいましょう。
English: Perhaps you could change your testimony to reflect this new detail.

Japanese: 間違いありませんか？
English: Are you sure about that?

Japanese: 何度も言いたかぁないがね。
English: How many times must I say it?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、カンペキに パーフェクトなんだよ！
English: I am abso-posi-lutely perfect! End of story!

Japanese: （こっちだって、何度も 聞きたかぁないよ‥‥）
English: (How many times must I hear that?)

Japanese: あれ‥‥。弁護士さん？
English: Psst! Phoenix!

Japanese: なんか、ちょっと 気になりません？　今の‥‥。
English: Doesn't something about that strike you as a little odd?

Japanese: あの‥‥“フィット”じゃなくて、 “ヒット”では‥‥？
English: Um... "impaction"? Don't you mean just "impact"?

Japanese: ナンセンス！ 大切なのは、フィーリングだよ！
English: Ridiculosity! I think you need to brush up on your language skills!

Japanese: （これにつっこむのは、 たしかにナンセンスだな‥‥）
English: (On second thought, perhaps I'd better leave the whole language thing alone...)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait right there!

Japanese: ‥‥小中さん。 墓穴を掘りましたね‥‥。
English: Mr. White, you've dug your own grave!

Japanese: な、なんのことだい？
English: What is this!?

Japanese: あなたは“左”に逃げたと 言いましたね。
English: You said the victim ran to the "left."

Japanese: しかし、それは梅世さんの 証言とムジュンします。
English: But that directly contradicts Miss May's testimony!

Japanese: 彼女は、ハッキリ“右”へ 逃げたと証言しています！
English: She clearly stated that the victim ran "right"!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! あはん。たぶん‥‥、 彼女の見間違いじゃないかな？
English: Oh hoh hoh. It is simple. You have misheard her.

Japanese: それはどうでしょうか。
English: I think not.

Japanese: ‥‥上面図を見てください。
English: Look at the floor plans...

Japanese: 犯人は、この位置にいました。
English: The killer was here.

Japanese: そして被害者は、このあたり。
English: And the victim, here.

Japanese: あなたの言うとおり、 被害者が左に逃げたとしたら‥‥
English: If the victim ran to the left, as you claim she did...

Japanese: そっちは、出口のドアとは 逆方向になります。
English: She would have been running directly away from the door!

Japanese: 彼女は、逃げ道がない方へ 逃げたことになる‥‥
English: She would have been running into a dead end!

Japanese: かなり不自然な状況です！
English: Don't you find that odd?

Japanese: ‥‥ッ！ ! ヘ、ヘンだなあ‥‥。
English: Very strange...

Japanese: たしかに左に逃げたのを 見たんだよ、ぼかぁ‥‥。
English: I did see her run to the left... I did.

Japanese: 弁護士さん‥‥。あのカオ、 ウソはついてないみたい‥‥。
English: Phoenix... look at his face. I don't think he's lying about this one.

Japanese: たしかにね。
English: True.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、小中は 被害者が左に逃げるのを、）
English: (Maybe he really did see the victim run left...?)

Japanese: （実際に、目撃したみたいだな）
English: (So he did witness the killing...?)
(Wait a second!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は“右”と言い、 小中さんは“左”と言っている。
English: Miss May says "right," and Mr. White says "left."

Japanese: このムジュンについて、 あなたはどう考えますか？
English: Can you explain this contradiction to the court?

Japanese: 小中さんの証言どおり、千尋さんは 左に逃げたと思います。
English: As Mr. White has testified, Mia ran to the left.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、 もしそうだとしたら、
English: But... But if that's the case...

Japanese: 被害者はわざわざドアとは逆の方へ 逃げたことになりますよ？
English: Why would she run in the opposite direction from the door?

Japanese: いいえ。当然、被害者はドアに 向かって逃げたはずです。
English: She did not. She was running towards the door, Your Honor.

Japanese: ハッ！ それではムジュンしてしまう！
English: Bwah! Now you're the one making a contradiction, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。
English: Not at all.

Japanese: 梅世さんの証言どおり、千尋さんは 右に逃げたんだと思います。
English: As Miss May has stated, Mia ran to the right after she was attacked.

Japanese: なんだあ？ ぼかぁウソなんかついていないよ！
English: What is this? I am not fashioning fabrications, I assure you!

Japanese: あのレディは、たしかに左に 逃げた！　ぼかぁ、見たんだ！
English: That lady ran to the left! I am sure of it!

Japanese: だとすれば、答えはただ１つです。
English: Then there can be only one explanation.

Japanese: ほう‥‥それは‥‥？
English: Eh...? And that is?

Japanese: ２人とも、本当のことを 言っていると思います。
English: Both witnesses are telling the truth. (For once...)

Japanese: ハッ！　お話にならない！
English: Hah! I doubt it!

Japanese: それでは、ムジュンは解決 しないではないか！
English: Er, rather, that does not clear up the contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥２つの証言がムジュンしない 答えが、たった１つだけあります。
English: There is one scenario that would explain their conflicting accounts.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: 小中さんが事件を目撃した場所は、 ホテルじゃなかったんですよ。
English: Obviously, the witness was not viewing the crime from the hotel!

Japanese: どういうことですか！ 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright! What do you mean?

Japanese: 証人が事件を目撃した場所は、 ホテルではなかった、だと！
English: Yes, what do you mean, he was not "viewing the crime from the hotel"!?

Japanese: じゃあ、どこだったと言うんだ！
English: If he was not in the hotel, where could he have been!?

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所ですよ、モチロン。
English: In the law offices of Fey & Co., of course!

Japanese: もっと正確に言うならば、 このポイントです！
English: More specifically, he was standing here!

Japanese: （事件があったとき、小中が いたポイントを示そう！）
English: (Show the court where Mr. White was standing!)

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: That's where he was!

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold it right there, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 事件があったとき、 その場所にいたとしたら、
English: If he was there during the very act of murder...

Japanese: それは“共犯者”ということに なりますよ！
