English: That would make him an accomplice!

Japanese: バカな！
English: Impossible!

Japanese: 事件があったとき、犯人の他に 共犯者が現場にいたなどという、
English: There was no one other than the criminal and victim in the room!

Japanese: そんな証言・証拠など、どこにも ないではないか！
English: There is no evidence, no proof to the contrary! None!

Japanese: す、すみません！ もう一度おねがいします。
English: S-sorry! Er... let me try that again!

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: That's where he was!

Japanese: べ、弁護人！ 我々をバカにするつもりか！
English: M-Mr. Wright! We are not fools!

Japanese: そこは、“被害者”が 立っていたポイントではないか！
English: How could he have been exactly where the victim was standing!?

Japanese: （あ、し、しまった！）
English: (Oh... oh, right!)

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥いいかげんにしなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: す、すみません！ もう一度おねがいします。
English: Sorry, Your Honor! Sorry! Let me try that again...

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: This is where he was!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか！
English: Look!

Japanese: 被害者がドアに向かって このように逃げたとき、
English: When the victim ran for the door...

Japanese: このポイントから見れば、“左”に 逃げたように見えるのです。
English: If he was watching from this point, to him it would appear that she ran to the "left."

Japanese: いいかげんにしたまえ！ そ、そのポイントは‥‥、
English: Please! This is no time for jokes in ill taste!

Japanese: “犯人”が立っていた ポイントではないか！
English: That is where the killer was standing!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! I will have order!

Japanese: 法廷の秩序を乱す者は、 退廷を命じます！
English: Anyone disturbing the order of this courtroom will be held in contempt!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ ‥‥なんということを！
English: Mr. Wright! What are you suggesting!?
R-r-rapscallion!!!

Japanese: 弁護人の推理は、コジツケに すぎないッ！
English: The postulations of the defense are a distortion of the truth, Your Honor!

Japanese: そうですね‥‥ かなり、ゴウインな感じが‥‥。
English: Indeed. They do seem a bit far-fetched...
Ho hoh hoh!

Japanese: 楽しませてくれるねえ、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: You provide us with so much entertainment, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: （なんだ？ ‥‥笑っている？）
English: (What now!? He's... laughing!?)

Japanese: リメンバーリメンバー。 ‥‥今のシゲキで、思い出したよ。
English: The hilarity of the moment made me remember something...

Japanese: ちょっと、ゴカイをまねく言い方を してしまったみたいだねえ。
English: It appears I have been unclear, and for this, I apologize.

Japanese: ミスタ・サイバンチョ。
English: Mr. Your Honor...

Japanese: もう一度、《証言》させて もらえないかな。
English: Might I be allowed to testify once more?

Japanese: わかりました。 では《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Very well, let's hear your revised testimony.

Japanese: （ふん‥‥。一度くずれた 証言は、もう立て直せないさ）
English: (Good luck. You can't fix a broken testimony, buddy!)

Japanese: ～“左に逃げた”という証言～
English: -- She Ran to the "Left" --

Japanese: 梅世クンの証言は、正しいよ。 ‥‥モチロン、ぼくのも、ね。
English: Miss May's testimony was correct... as was mine!

Japanese: キミがレディに襲いかかったとき、 彼女はまず、左に逃げたんだ。
English: When you assaulted the girl, she first ran to the left.

Japanese: そして思いきり、彼女を殴った。 ‥‥これが、ぼくが見た部分だ。
English: And then you hit her, savagely! That is what I saw.

Japanese: 次に彼女は、最後の力を ふりしぼって、右に逃げたんだ。
English: Next, with the last of her strength, she ran to the right.

Japanese: キミはそれを追いかけて、 彼女にトドメをさした。
English: You chased her, and delivered the final blow.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが、 梅世クンが見た部分だ。
English: That is what Miss May saw.

Japanese: どうだい？ キミは２回、殴ったワケさ。
English: You see? You hit her twice!

Japanese: 思い出したかい？ ミスタ・ベンゴシ‥‥！
English: Don't you remember, Mr. Lawyer?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 それなら、スジはとおりますね。
English: Hmm... That does seem to make sense.

Japanese: 弁護人。《尋問》は‥‥？
English: Will you be cross-examining the witness's testimony?

Japanese: しますよ、そりゃあ！
English: You bet I will! I mean, yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～“左に逃げた”という証言～
English: -- She Ran to the "Left" --

Japanese: 梅世クンの証言は、正しいよ。 ‥‥モチロン、ぼくのも、ね。
English: Miss May's testimony was correct... as was mine!

Japanese: キミがレディに襲いかかったとき、 彼女はまず、左に逃げたんだ。
English: When you assaulted the girl, she first ran to the left.

Japanese: そして思いきり、彼女を殴った。 ‥‥これが、ぼくが見た部分だ。
English: And then you hit her, savagely! That is what I saw.

Japanese: 次に彼女は、最後の力を ふりしぼって、右に逃げたんだ。
English: Next, with the last of her strength, she ran to the right.

Japanese: キミはそれを追いかけて、 彼女にトドメをさした。
English: You chased her, and delivered the final blow.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが、 梅世クンが見た部分だ。
English: That is what Miss May saw.

Japanese: どうだい？ キミは２回、殴ったワケさ。
English: You see? You hit her twice!

Japanese: どう‥‥弁護士さん？
English: What do you think Phoenix?

Japanese: 思ったとおり、ボロを 出したよ‥‥！
English: I think we've got him right where we want him! He slipped!

Japanese: ヤツが笑ってられるのも、 今のうちだけさ。
English: Let him laugh for now. I'll soon wipe that smile off his face!

Japanese: それはどういう‥‥！
English: What do you mean by that!

Japanese: これから、それを トークするんじゃないか。
English: That is what I am about to explain!

Japanese: ニッポン人はすぐ、ケツロンを いそぐ‥‥悪いクセだよ。
English: You Americans, always so impatient! It does not become you.

Japanese: （あんたもニッポン人だろうが！）
English: (Hey buddy, you're an American, too!)

Japanese: “まず”ってどういうことですか？
English: What do you mean "first"?

Japanese: “まず”っていうのは、 “次に”の前だよ。
English: First? That is what comes before what happens "next." You do speak English, right?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、おちついて聞きたまえ、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Please, sit back, relax. I will try to use simple words for your benefit.

Japanese: とにかく彼女は、左に逃げた。 そして‥‥、
English: First, she ran to the left, and then...

Japanese: ぼくは殴ってない！
English: I didn't hit anyone!

Japanese: まあまあ。 ‥‥かくしなさんな。
English: Now, now, Mr. Wright. There is no point hiding things from this court.

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: I'm not hiding anything!

Japanese: 弁護人。証言のジャマは やめてもらいたい！
English: The prosecution requests that the defense refrain from interrupting the testimony!

Japanese: やめてもらえますかな？
English: Mr. Wright, you have been warned.

Japanese: （なんでぼくが 怒られるんだよ‥‥）
English: (Why's he mad at me!?)

Japanese: “次に”ってなんですか！
English: What do you mean "next"!?

Japanese: “次に”っていうのは、 “まず”の次だよ。
English: Next is what comes after "first."

Japanese: そんなことはわかってます！
English: I know that!

Japanese: それだけわかっていれば ＯＫだよ、ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Then that is all you need to know, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: とにかく、彼女は向きを変えて、 必死で右に逃げだしたんだ。
English: As I said, she turned and made a desperate dash to the right.

Japanese: その“キミは”っていうの、 やめてください！
English: Stop saying it was me!

Japanese: だって、 あれはキミだったからねえ。
English: But, it was you.

Japanese: 弁護人！　キミではないと 言うなら、証拠を見せるんだな！
English: Mr. Wright! If you claim that it was NOT you, then show us proof!

Japanese: （くそー、ぼくのイメージが どんどん悪くなるぞ‥‥）
English: (Argh! Can I do nothing right? Nothing!?)

Japanese: つづけていいかな。
English: May I continue?

Japanese: ２人は、別の部分を目撃していた、 というわけですか？
English: So each of you saw different parts...?

Japanese: デァツライ！ そのとおりだよ！
English: Abso-posi-lutely! That's right, of course!

Japanese: （“デァツライ”って、 学校じゃ習わなかったよな‥‥）
English: (Where did he pick up that annoying phrase...?)

Japanese: エニウェイ、とにかく！
English: Anyway, moving along!

Japanese: あなたは、２回とも 見ていたんですか？
English: So were you watching both times?

Japanese: まあね。
English: I suppose I was.

Japanese: 最初からそう証言しなかったのは ソーリーソーリー、ワルかった！
English: And please excuse me for not testifying to this sooner!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか。
English: Mr. White!

Japanese: 被害者は、“１回だけ” 殴られて、死んでいるのです！
English: The victim died from a "single" blow!
What do you have to say to that?
Er... Erp!

Japanese: （今がチャンスだ！ たたみこむぞ‥‥！）
English: (Now's my chance to hit him where it counts!)

Japanese: 小中さん。あなた、“カンペキに パーフェクト”だったのでは？
English: Mr. White. Wasn't it you who told this court you were abso-posi-lutely perfect?

Japanese: うう。
English: Mmph.

Japanese: ‥‥そのフレーズは、もう 使わないことにするよ。
English: I will refrain from using this phrase from now on.

Japanese: 裁判長。小中さんに、もう一度 《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Your Honor, if you could ask the witness for a new testimony...

Japanese: 証人の記憶は、コンランしている。
English: The witness is obviously confused, Your Honor!

Japanese: ここで、１０分の休憩を 申し入れたい！
English: I would like to request a 10 minute break!

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Yes... yes, quite.

Japanese: 記憶がコンランしているのは、 証人がウソをついているからです！
English: The witness is confused because he's lying!

Japanese: 今ここで休憩を入れるのは、 断固、反対します！
English: I emphatically request that there be no break, Your Honor!

Japanese: （‥‥そうだ！　早く続きを‥‥）
English: (Yeah! We want justice...!)

Japanese: （逃げるな！　小中ァ！）
English: (Don't let him get away!)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 《証言》をつづけましょう‥‥。
English: If the witness would care to revise his testimony...

Japanese: （傍聴人が味方についたぞ。 これでもう、逃げられない！）
English: (The crowd's on my side! No slipping out of this now, White!)

Japanese: いいですか？　小中さん？
English: Mr. White?

Japanese: イ、イエス‥‥。
English: O... okay...

Japanese: ～２人の証言の食い違い～
English: -- The Two Accounts --

Japanese: そのぉ、なんだ。あれが倒れる音で ぼかぁ向かいの窓を見たわけだが。
English: Umm, well, see--I looked at the other window when I heard that thing fall.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、ミス・チヒロは左に 逃げていたんだよ！
English: Then, the next moment, I saw Miss Mia run to the left!

Japanese: 犯人‥‥キミは、襲いかかったが、 彼女はそれを‥‥かわしたんだ。
English: The killer, you, attacked her... but she dodged.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、そのぅ、
English: Umm... and then...

Japanese: 向きを変えて、 ドアの方へ逃げようとした！
English: She turned, and ran for the door!

Japanese: キミは、そこで１発、ポカリと やったんだ！
English: Then you did her in with a single blow! Thwap!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ポカリとねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm. "Thwap" indeed.

Japanese: では‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Very well, you may begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: ミ、ミスタ・サイバンチョ。
English: Mr. Your Honor...

Japanese: ぼかぁちょっと、おなかが イタいんだが‥‥。
English: My stomach, you see, it is hurting...

Japanese: ガマンしてください。
English: Deal with it.

Japanese: もうちょっとで 終わりますからね‥‥。
English: This is almost over.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! ～２人の証言の食い違い～
English: -- The Two Accounts --

Japanese: そのぉ、なんだ。あれが倒れる音で ぼかぁ向かいの窓を見たわけだが。
English: Umm, well, see--I looked at the other window when I heard that thing fall.

Japanese: 向かいの窓を見たら、電気スタンド が転がっていたんだよ。
English: A light stand was lying on the floor when I looked.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、ミス・チヒロは左に 逃げていたんだよ！
English: Then, the next moment, I saw Miss Mia run to the left!

Japanese: 犯人‥‥キミは、襲いかかったが、 彼女はそれを‥‥かわしたんだ。
English: The killer, you, attacked her... but she dodged.

Japanese: そして、向きを変えて、ドアの方へ 逃げようとした！
English: She turned, and ran for the door!

Japanese: キミは、そこで１発、ポカリと やったんだ！
English: Then you did her in with a single blow! Thwap!

Japanese: 弁護士さん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: ‥‥だんだん、 口数が減ってきましたね。
English: He's getting a lot less talkative.

Japanese: つっこまれるのを 恐れているんだろうね。
English: He's probably afraid I'll catch him on something!

Japanese: （‥‥やりにくいな‥‥）
English: (But it won't be easy!)

Japanese: “あれが倒れる音”‥‥。 “あれ”ってなんですか！
English: You heard "that thing fall"? What exactly was "that thing"?

Japanese: ん？　あ、あれ、“あれ”？ ‥‥ガラスの電気スタンドだよ！
English: Huh? Oh... oh, that? Umm... the glass light stand!

Japanese: （‥‥ああ。現場に倒れていた ヤツか‥‥）
English: (Right... the one that had fallen over at the scene.)

Japanese: 弁護士さん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ちょっと おかしくないですか？
English: Doesn't something about that strike you as odd?

Japanese: そうかな。 べつに、問題はないと思うよ。
English: Huh? No, not really.

Japanese: では、《尋問》を つづけてください。
English: Please continue your cross-examination.

Japanese: （たしかに、気になるな‥‥ もう少し、ゆさぶってみるか）
English: (Yeah, that is odd. I'll press further.)

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: な、なんだい？
English: Huh? W-what?

Japanese: あなたは、ガラスの電気スタンドを 見たわけですか？
English: You're saying you saw the glass light stand?

Japanese: そ、そうさ。
English: Y-yes.

Japanese: では、そのあたりを、証言 しなおしてください！
English: Then change your testimony to reflect that!

Japanese: ソ、ソーリーソーリー。 ‥‥すまないね。
English: S-sorry. My bad...

Japanese: では、小中さん。証言を 訂正してください。
English: The witness will revise his testimony.

Japanese: オ、オーケーオーケー。
English: Okay okay. Of course.

Japanese: “ミス・チヒロ”‥‥？
English: Miss Mia...?

Japanese: 小中さん。被害者のことを 知っていたんですか？
English: Mr. White, did you know the victim personally?

Japanese: の、の、の、のーだよ！ 知ってるハズがない！
English: No no no, triple no! How would I? How could I!

Japanese: 証人が被害者の名前を 知っていても、おかしくない！
English: I see nothing odd about the witness knowing the name of the victim.

Japanese: 弁護人！　証人を おどかさないでくれたまえ！
English: Mr. Wright! I will not have you harrying the witness!

Japanese: （御剣が、おちつきを なくしてきたな‥‥）
English: (You're looking a little flustered, Edgeworth!)

Japanese: さっきは、そこで被害者は 殴られた、と言ってましたよ？
English: Now before, you said that she was hit before she ran.

Japanese: カ、カン違いってヤツだ。
English: Y-yes, I must have been confused.

Japanese: ドンマイドンマイ！ 気にしないでくれたまえ。
English: But don't worry! We all know what happened now.

Japanese: そのとき、 あなたはどうしましたか？
English: What did you do then?

Japanese: もちろん、あわてて彼女を 追いかけたさ！
English: I gave chase, of course!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! のー！　ぼくじゃない！ は、ハンニンがだ！
English: No! No! Not me. Th-the killer, I mean! He gave chase!

Japanese: 証人。 あわてることはない。
English: Mr. White, please try to remain calm.

Japanese: 弁護人のハッタリには、 なんの証拠もないのだ！
English: Mr. Wright here is only trying to fluster you with baseless accusations!

Japanese: サ、サ、サンキュ、 ミスタ・ケンジ‥‥。
English: Oh... oh right. Thank you, Miles.

Japanese: “ポカリ”‥‥ですか？
English: Thwap...?

Japanese: 最初の証言から、ずいぶん おとなしい表現になりましたね？
English: That doesn't sound like the savage attack from your earlier testimony...

Japanese: は、ははっ！　ぼかぁ、 おとなしいオトコだからね。
English: Ho! Hoh hoh! Yes, well, I am a man of peace, you see.

Japanese: “電気スタンドが転がっていた”？ ‥‥初耳ですね。
English: The "glass stand was lying on the floor"? That's the first I've heard of this.

Japanese: なぜ、さっきはスタンドのことを 話してくれなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you tell us about the stand before?

Japanese: な‥‥なぜだったかな‥‥？
English: W-why? Me?

Japanese: 誰かに言われたから‥‥いや！
English: W-well... I was instructed not to... wait!

Japanese: ジャスタ・モメン！ ‥‥ちょっと待ってくれたまえ。
English: One moment! Give me a minute to gather my thoughts.

Japanese: アタマがコンランしてきた‥‥。
English: I am so... so confused.

Japanese: （小中のヤツ、もうヘロヘロだぞ）
English: (White's falling apart.)

Japanese: 裁判長！　これ以上、証人を 苦しめないようにしてもらいたい！
English: Your Honor! Please, I ask that you do not allow the witness to be badgered any further!

Japanese: べ、弁護人。
English: M-Mr. Wright.

Japanese: むやみに証人を おどしつけないように。
English: Please refrain from badgering the witness...

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 （御剣も、必死だな‥‥）
English: Yes, Your Honor. (Edgeworth is on the ropes!)

Japanese: では、証人。つづきを。
English: Very well. The witness may continue his account.

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: 電気スタンドが転がっているのを 見るのは、不可能なんですよ！

Er... erp!
Ri... dicu... losity...

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおお！

Mr. White.

Japanese: ～盗聴器を仕掛けたとき～

(No more...)
(I can't take this anymore...)

(M-Mia!?)

M-M-Mia...!

B-but!

English: You have that Receipt in the court record, right?

Japanese: バカね。それは小中が書いたに 決まってるでしょ。
English: Phoenix! White wrote that, not me!

Japanese: ‥‥で、 それがどうかしたんですか？

Japanese: ウラ‥‥ですか？

(It's a regular receipt...)

Japanese: （発行したのは、有名なデパート みたいだ）

("Date of purchase"... September 4...)
September 4!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！

Japanese: 証拠品《領収書》のデータを 書きなおした。

Japanese: ９月９日　午後１時１６分 地方裁判所　第１法廷
English: September 9, 1:16 PM District Court Courtroom No. 1

Japanese: これより、成歩堂　龍一の 法廷を再開します。
English: The court will now reconvene for the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶかな？ 成歩堂くん。
English: Is the defendant... rather, are you alright, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥すみませんでした。
English: Yes. Sorry, Your Honor. I'm fine now.

Japanese: ‥‥では、先ほどのつづきに もどりましょうか。
English: Then, let's start where we left off.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 我々に、もどるべき “つづき”など、存在しない！
English: There is nothing to go back to!

Japanese: すでに、小中氏の尋問は 終わっている！
English: The cross-examination of Mr. White is finished!

Japanese: あとは、被告人・成歩堂　龍一に 判決を言い渡すだけだ‥‥。
English: All that is required now is for you to pass judgment on the defendant Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: もう一度だけ、 チャンスをください。
English: Please, give me one more chance.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、最後です！
English: I promise you, this is the last time I'll ask you!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかし、もう勝負は ついてるのは、たしかですね。
English: But, as Mr. Edgeworth has noted, the trial is more or less finished.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。 どうしますか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, do you have an opinion on this matter?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。 最後のチャンスをくれてやろう。
English: I say... let us give Mr. Phoenix Wright his last chance.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well!

Japanese: では、小中氏の《尋問》を おねがいします！
English: You may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～盗聴器を仕掛けたとき～
English: -- The Wiretapping --

Japanese: あれはそう、９月のアタマだから、 事件の約１週間、前かな。
English: It was the beginning of September... the week before the murder.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、綾里法律事務所に侵入 したんだよ。
English: I had entered the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: モチロン、 盗聴器を仕掛けるためだよ。
English: Of course, I had done so to place the wiretap.

Japanese: そのときにね。たしかに見たんだ。 ガラス製の、電気スタンドを‥‥。
English: That is when I saw this glass light stand.

Japanese: （‥‥千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Now I've just got to show him the proof Mia gave me!)

Japanese: その証拠はあるんですか！
English: Do you have proof!?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、電話の会話内容を 知っていた。
English: Miss April May knew the details of Ms. Fey's phone conversation!

Japanese: それは、盗聴器が事件の起こる 以前に仕掛けられていた証拠だ！
English: This proves that the wiretap was placed before the murder!
Huh... right.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Gah! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: 本当に、あなたが 侵入したんですか？
English: Was it really you that went into the office?

Japanese: あるいは、梅世さんが‥‥
English: Or was it Miss May?

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所に、正体不明の 古い指紋があった。
English: Unidentified fingerprints several days old were found in the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: さっき、小中氏のものと一致した という報告が入ったよ。
English: Those were obviously Mr. White's.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Gah! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: さて、証人。‥‥なんのために、 綾里法律事務所へ‥‥？
English: Now, Mr. White. Tell us why you went to the Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: なぜ、千尋さんの電話に 盗聴器を仕掛けたんですか！
English: Why did you tap Mia's phone!?

Japanese: それは、本件とは関係のない ことだよ‥‥
English: This has no bearing on the current case, Your Honor.

Japanese: 異議を認めます。 ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Objection sustained. Mr. Wright...

Japanese: さっきと同じ質問をしていても、 意味がないと思いますがね‥‥。
English: I don't see how asking the same questions again will make any difference.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Right! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: なぜ、電気スタンドなんかを 目にとめたんですか！
English: Why did you notice something as innocuous as a light stand!?

Japanese: あのスタンド、すべてのパーツが ガラス製でね。
English: The light stand was made entirely out of glass.

Japanese: あまりにキレイだったんで、 印象に残ったんだ‥‥。
English: It was quite stylish, so I guess it made a lasting impression on me.

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Look closely at this.

Japanese: 血文字で“マヨイ”‥‥。
English: See the word "Maya" written in blood...?

Japanese: ハッ！ 今となっては、意味のない‥‥
English: Bwah hah! You're grasping!

Japanese: 問題は、これが領収書のウラ である、ということです。
English: I think not. Look at the other side of the receipt.

Japanese: な、なんだと？
English: Th-the other side?

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: その“領収書”には、 何が書いてありますか？
English: Would you tell the court what is written on the other side of that receipt?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...
Well!

Japanese: “ガラス製の電気スタンド‥‥”
English: A "glass light stand"!

Japanese: 発行日は‥‥
English: And the date of purchase...

Japanese: 事件の前日です！
English: Why, that's the day before the murder!
You see!

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたが綾里法律事務所に 侵入した９月のアタマには‥‥
English: When you allegedly entered Fey & Co. Law Offices at the beginning of September...

Japanese: あのスタンドは、 まだなかったんですよ！
English: The stand could not have been there!

Japanese: ホ、ホワァァァァァァァァァァァァ ァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァ
English: Kwaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: ァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァ ァァァァァァァァァァァァァット！
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaahh!?

Japanese: どうですか？　小中さん。
English: Well, Mr. White?

Japanese: まだ、言い逃れ、できますか？
English: Can't get out of this one, can you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ノー・アイ・キャント‥‥。
English: No... It's impossachievable...

Japanese: （おやおや、 放心しちまったよ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... he's losing it.)
Well, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなた方に、いくら強い圧力が かかっていても、
English: I understand there must be quite a bit of PRESSURE on you.

Japanese: こうなった以上、ぼくを有罪にする ことは、できないはずです。
English: But I think you'll agree you can't judge me "guilty" under these circumstances.
Very well.

Japanese: それでは、審理はこれで終了‥‥
English: Then, that is all for the trial of...

Japanese: そこまでだ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Not so fast, Phoenix Wright!
Eh?

Japanese: （おいおい、ウソだろ‥‥？）
English: (What! No way can he worm his way out of this one!)

Japanese: （まだ食い下がるつもりかよ！）
English: (Oh wait... I forgot. It's Edgeworth.)

Japanese: 弁護人の意見は、たしかに 一理ある‥‥。
English: There is a certain thread of logic to the defendant's claims.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一が無実であるという 決定的な証拠も、ないのだ！
English: There is no concrete proof that Phoenix Wright is innocent!
Ergo!

Japanese: ‥‥あと１日、 判決を待ってもらいたい。
English: I would like to request one more day before Phoenix Wright is granted his freedom.

Japanese: もう一度、 調査をしなおす時間がほしい。
English: I need time to make one more inquiry into this matter.

Japanese: ほ、ほう‥‥。
English: Hmm...!

Japanese: （も、もう一度、調査だと‥‥？）
English: (Another inquiry...!?)

Japanese: （まさか‥‥千尋さんの 解剖記録みたいに、）
English: (This isn't going to be another one of those updated autopsy reports!)

Japanese: （また、何か証拠を デッチ上げる気じゃあ‥‥！）
English: (This guy just makes up evidence as he pleases!)
(This is bad...!)

Japanese: 小中の有罪は、明白です！
English: Mr. White's guilt is obvious!

Japanese: これ以上、審理をのばす 必要は、まったくありません！
English: There is no need to prolong this trial any further!

Japanese: ふむう。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事、いかがかな？
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、小中氏を起訴するのは、 私たち検察側だ。
English: If anyone is going to call Mr. White to trial, it would be me, the prosecution.

Japanese: キミの推理が正しいかどうか、 １日、調査したい。
English: I need a day to ascertain whether your claims have any basis in factual evidence!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: 成歩堂くんの異議を却下します！
English: Objection denied!

Japanese: えええええ！
English: Whaaaat!

Japanese: （とりあえず、ようすを 見てみるか‥‥）
English: (Hmm. I don't know where he's going with this. I'd better hold back for now.)

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 弁護人の異議もないようですから、 御剣くんの意見を認めましょう。
English: As there is no objection from the defense, I grant Mr. Edgeworth his extra day.

Japanese: （し、しまった！）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一の審理は、 明日まで延期します！
English: The completion of the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright will be postponed until tomorrow.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　今ここで 決着をつけなかったら！）
English: (No! There's no telling what will happen if I can't end this now!)

Japanese: （御剣はきっと“何か” やるに違いない！）
English: (Edgeworth is sure to come up with--or just make up-- something!)

Japanese: （‥‥せっかく、千尋さんに 助けてもらったのに‥‥）
English: (And after Mia showed up to help me and all...)

Japanese: ミスタ・サイバンチョ！
English: Mr. Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、もう 帰っていいのかな‥‥？
English: May I go home?

Japanese: もちろんかまいません。 ‥‥ごくろうさまでした。
English: Of course. Thank you for your time.
(Gah!)

Japanese: 証人、待ちなさい！
English: The witness will stay!

Japanese: （ち、千尋さん‥‥！）
なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: このメモを読み上げなさい。
English: Read this note out loud.

Japanese: 千尋さん、これは‥‥？
English: Mia? What's this...?

Japanese: 千尋さんからメモを 受け取った。
English: Memo received from Mia.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥ちょっとよろしいですか。
English: Your Honor. If I may...?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。‥‥あなたもなかなか シツコイですねえ‥‥。
English: You're quite persistent today, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （あたりまえだ！　こっちは、 命がかかってるんだぞ！）
English: (You bet I am! My life is riding on this one!)

Japanese: これからぼくが読み上げるものを 聞いていただきたい！
English: I have something I would like to read to the court!

Japanese: （いや、違う！　‥‥ここで 間違ったら、おしまいだぞ！）
English: (No... that's not it! I can't mess this one up!)

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: い、いえ！
English: N-no, Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼくは、千尋さんがくれた リストを読み上げた。
English: The memo Mia had given me was a list of names.

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで聞いたことのある 名前が、ならんでいる。
English: Many of them sounded strangely familiar.

Japanese: これは‥‥政財界の 有名人たちの名前、か‥‥。
English: People in finance, famous celebrities...

Japanese: そのとき！
English: That's when it happened.

Japanese: スタァァァァァァァァップ！
English: S-s-stop! Desist! Halt!

Japanese: や、や、やめろ！ やめさせろォッ！
English: P-please, stop! Make him stop!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでキサマが‥‥ そのリストを持ってるんだ‥‥？
English: How... how did you get that list!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたがここで 罪を認めないのであれば、
English: Admit your guilt, right here, right now.

Japanese: このリストは、 マスコミに公表されるでしょう。
English: Or else this list will be released to the press!
I... I confess.

Japanese: 認めるよ‥‥
English: I confess.

Japanese: ぼかぁ‥‥ぼかぁ‥‥ ‥‥ミス・チヒロを‥‥
English: I... I did it. I hit her.

Japanese: あの‥‥《考える人》で‥‥ ‥‥殴ったんだ！
English: I hit Miss Mia with The Thinker!

Japanese: ... ‥‥以上です、裁判長。
English: Case closed, Your Honor.

Japanese: どうやら、これ以上の審理は 必要ないようですね。
English: Well, I see no reason to continue this trial.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: また、やってくれましたね‥‥。
English: You've done it again! That was quite a spirited defense!

Japanese: いやあ‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: たまたまツイていただけですよ。 （スゴ腕の霊が‥‥）
English: I guess you could say that. (If only you knew how spirit-ed it was!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥では！
English: Hmm. Well!

Japanese: 弁護人‥‥いや、被告人 成歩堂　龍一に、
English: This court finds the defense...

Japanese: 判決を言い渡します。
English: Ahem! Rather, the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ９月９日　午後２時２４分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 9, 2:24 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: またこうして“おめでとう”って 言えるなんて。
English: Well, I never thought I'd be saying this again...

Japanese: 綾里家に生まれたことに、 カンシャしないとね。
English: But, congratulations! You're lucky I was born a Fey!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんにも。
English: I'm lucky I had both you and Maya on my side.

Japanese: ‥‥そうね。
English: I'm glad you made it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ありがとう、なるほどくん。
English: Thank you, Phoenix.

Japanese: 私と真宵のために‥‥ こんな、あぶない目にあって‥‥。
English: You risked a lot to help me... and Maya.

Japanese: 私、一生わすれません。
English: I won't forget it as long as I live!
(As long as you "live"...?)
My time here's running out.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 真宵の霊力は、まだとても 弱いわ‥‥。
English: Maya's powers are still weak.

Japanese: そんなに長く、“こっち”には いられないの。
English: I can't stay here that long.

Japanese: そ、そんな！ ‥‥まだ、言いたいことが‥‥！
English: W-what!? No! There's still so much to say!

Japanese: 心配しないで。
English: Don't worry.

Japanese: ‥‥また会えるわ。きっと。
English: I'm sure we'll meet again.

Japanese: しょ、所長！
English: Ch-Chief!

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。 もう私は“所長”じゃないわ。
English: Hee hee. I'm not the "Chief" anymore.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix?

Japanese: 今夜９時。‥‥事務所に 来てくれるかしら‥‥？
English: Can you come to the office tonight... say, 9:00?

Japanese: 事務所に‥‥。
English: The office...?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥また会いましょうね。
English: I'll see you later.

Japanese: 所長‥‥千尋さん！
English: Chief... Mia!

Japanese: ９月９日　午後９時２分 綾里法律事務所
English: September 9, 9:02 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （‥‥あの夜のこと、 思い出しちまうな‥‥）
English: (Being here, it's hard not to think about that night...)

Japanese: よく来てくれたわね‥‥。
English: You came!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥。
English: Mia...

Japanese: 来てくれないんじゃないかと、 少し心配だったわ。
English: I was kinda worried you might not.

Japanese: そんなわけ、ないじゃないですか。
English: Huh? Of course I came...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。
English: Well then!

Japanese: おなかもすいたことだし、 みそラーメン食べに行きましょう。
English: I'm pretty hungry. How about a burger?

Japanese: ち、千尋さん‥‥？
English: M-Mia...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... あっはっはっは！ おもしろいカオー。
English: Bwah hah hah! You should see your face!

Japanese: 千尋さんッ！
English: Mia!

Japanese: さっきから“千尋さん”ばっかり。 ‥‥あたしですよー、真宵。
English: What are you talking about? It's me! Maya!

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん？
English: M-Maya...?

Japanese: ね、ね、似てました？　あたし？
English: What, did I look like my sister?

Japanese: （“似てた”、っていうか、 本人じゃないか‥‥）
English: (Look like... you WERE her!)

Japanese: ふーん。これは使えそうだな‥‥。
English: Hmm! I might be able to use that.

Japanese: “なるほどくん、私、お茶が 飲みたいわ”とか言っちゃって。
English: "Oh, Phoenix! Go to the store and buy me lunch, would you?"

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。 なんできみが、ここに？
English: Uhm, Maya... Why are you here?

Japanese: ほら！　‥‥これ。
English: Because of this!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、手紙くれたんです！
English: See? Mia wrote me a letter.

Japanese: “なるほどくんのこと、 よろしくたのむわ”‥‥って。
English: Take care of Phoenix for me.

Japanese: ‥‥何をよろしくたのむの？
English: Take care of... huh?

Japanese: 事務所ですよ、事・務・所。
English: She means the office! This office!

Japanese: 《成歩堂法律事務所》。 あたし、お手伝いしますから！
English: Someone has to help with the new Wright & Co. Law Offices, right?

Japanese: よろしくおねがいします！
English: And who better but me! Maya Fey, reporting for duty!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、カタ苦しいのは ナシにしましょうね！
English: Wait, no, on second thought, let's make this casual!

Japanese: よろしく！　なるほどくん！
English: Yo, Nick! Maya here, ready to get down to business!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、お名前はお姉ちゃんから 聞いたんだよ。
English: You... don't mind me calling you "Nick," do you?

Japanese: いい名前だねー、 “なるほどくん”なんて。
English: It's a great name! Mia said that's what your friend Larry calls you.

Japanese: （な、“なるほどくん”って‥‥）
ね。ほら、 あくしゅ、あくしゅ。
English: You know what this means? We're partners!

Japanese: ‥‥でもまあ、考えてみれば、
English: You know, when I think about it...

Japanese: ぼくがこうしてここに 立っているのも、
English: It is Maya's fault I'm here now.

Japanese: もとはと言えば、この子の せいであり、
English: But...

Japanese: 同時に、この子のおかげでもある。
English: If it wasn't for her I'd probably be in jail!

Japanese: 《成歩堂法律事務所》‥‥ ヒビキは悪くない、な。
English: Wright & Co. Law Offices. It's got a good ring to it.

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: よろしくたのむよ、真宵ちゃん。
English: Thanks, Maya!

Japanese: （‥‥なるほどくん。 しっかりね‥‥）
English: (Good luck, Phoenix.)

Japanese: （私はいつでも、 あなたを見てるわ‥‥）
English: (I'll always be here... watching.)

Japanese: じゃ！　なるほどくん。 行こう！
English: Right! Okay, Nick, let's do it!

Japanese: え、え‥‥？　どこに。
English: Huh? Do... what?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ。 みそラーメン。
English: Burgers, dummy! Burgers!

Japanese: 近くに、おいしいところが あるんだよ。
English: There's a great burger joint just down the street.

Japanese: ‥‥ほら。早く！
English: C'mon! Time's a wasting!

Japanese: はいはい‥‥。
English: O-okay! Wait up!

Japanese: おわり
English: THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 弁護人の反証は、検察側の主張を くつがえす力を持っていない！
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim.

Japanese: 弁護‥‥被告人、成歩堂　龍一に この場で判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defense, er, rather, the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより、その身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ええい‥‥ コシャクなり、トノサマン‥‥！
English: Grrah! You have disgraced me for the last time, Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 天空の月が、キサマの血を欲して 泣いておるわ‥‥！
English: The pale moon in the sky cries for your blood!

Japanese: 今宵の月‥‥よく 目に焼きつけておくがいい。
English: The moon? No, it is you who should gaze upon the moon...

Japanese: おヌシの見る、 最後の満月となろう‥‥！

Japanese: お昼まで、アクションの内容を 決めていまして。
English: We worked through some action scenes until noon.

Japanese: 午後は５時からリハーサル でしたので、
English: Rehearsal was to begin at 5:00, but I was a little tired.

Japanese: 昼食後は、楽屋で ヒルネしていました。
English: So after lunch, I took a nap in my dressing room.

Japanese: 目が覚めたら、集合時間の５時を 過ぎていたんです！
English: When I woke up, it was after 5:00! I was late for the rehearsal!

Japanese: あわててスタジオに行ったら、 みなさん怖い顔をしていて。
English: I hurried to the studio... and found everyone looking shocked.

Japanese: そのまま逮捕されて、 こうして留置所へ‥‥。
English: They arrested me on the spot, and brought me here.

Japanese: （事件があった午後は、 ずっと寝ていたわけか）
English: (So he was sleeping the entire afternoon of the murder?)

Japanese: （なんか、マヌケな ヒーローだなあ‥‥）
English: (Some action hero!)

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: What will the kids think? *sniff*

Japanese: あの、これ。
English: Um, here.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... （どうも、アドリブのきかない 人らしいな‥‥）
English: (Let me guess... this guy wasn't the top student in improv class.)

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: I'm sure that's very important and all.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I just don't see what it has to do with my problem.

Japanese: あ。 どうでした撮影所。
English: Hello! How was the studio?

Japanese: やっぱりボク、 ハンニンあつかいですよね‥‥。
English: They all think I did it, don't they?

Japanese: そ、そんなことないですよー。 ね。なるほどくん。
English: N-no, not at all! Isn't that right, Nick?

Japanese: え、ええ。せいぜい “容疑者”どまりだと思いますよ。
English: Right, not at all. At worst, you're a suspect.

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です‥‥。
English: *sniff*

Japanese: すみませんが、依頼状、 書いてもらえますか？
English: Do you think you could write up a letter formally requesting my representation?

Japanese: は、はあ。‥‥恐縮です。
English: Of c-course.

Japanese: 証拠品《荷星　三郎の依頼状》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Powers's Request added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （これで、あのオバチャンを 突破できればいいんだけど‥‥）
English: (I just hope this will get me past that security lady.)

Japanese: 荷星さん、何かぼくに かくしていること、ありませんか？
English: Mr. Powers, you aren't hiding anything from me, are you?

Japanese: え、えッ！ い、いえそんな、めっそうもない。
English: W-what? No! I'd never do that!

Japanese: さっきあなた、昼からは楽屋で ヒルネしていたって言いましたね。
English: Just now you said that you were sleeping in your dressing room after lunch.

Japanese: はい。 グッスリ寝てました。
English: Yes. Like a baby.

Japanese: でも、警備員のオバチャンは、 目撃してるんですよ。
English: But the security lady said she saw you that day.

Japanese: あなたが殺人現場へ向かう ところを！
English: She says she saw you heading towards the scene of the crime!

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: W-what!? That's not possible!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな、バカな。 ボクは、ヒ、ヒルネを‥‥。
English: I... I really don't know what to say! I was sleeping, I promise!

Japanese: 荷星さん。ホントのコトを 言ってくれないと。
English: Mr. Powers, if you want me to help you, you have to tell me the truth.

Japanese: ぼくは新米弁護士ですから、 すぐ負けちゃいますよ？
English: I'm new to this lawyer business, and I need every advantage I can get.

Japanese: ‥‥恐縮ですが。
English: I know... I-I'm sorry. *sniff*

Japanese: ボク、間違いなく 寝ていたんです。誓います。
English: But, I swear to you on my momma's grave, I was sleeping.

Japanese: オバチャンの見間違いだと 思いますけど‥‥。
English: Maybe the security lady only thought she saw me?

Japanese: （どうなってるんだ‥‥。 ウソをついてるようすは、ない）
English: (What did she see...? He doesn't seem to be lying.)

Japanese: あの‥‥。撮影所の正門にいる 警備員なんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm... about the security guard at the main gate to the studio...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 あのオバチャン。
English: Oh, the security lady?

Japanese: ヒドいんですよ！　あたしのこと、 “見た目からアヤシイ”って！
English: She's terrible! She called me "suspicious lookin'"!

Japanese: は、はあ。実は、前に ボクも言われました。
English: Y-yes... actually, she's said the same thing to me before.

Japanese: “そのお面をとれ！”って。
English: "Take off that mask!" were her words, I believe.

Japanese: あの人、エラい人には ものすごく弱いんですけど、
English: She sucks up to all the bigwigs at the studio...

Japanese: 一度ナメられちゃったら、一生 アタマが上がらないんですよ。
English: But let her see you stumble once and she'll never let up on you.

Japanese: うう、サイアク‥‥。
English: Grr! She's got some nerve!

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: I'm sure that's very important and all.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I just don't see what it has to do with my problem.

Japanese: 依頼状、ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you for the letter of request.

Japanese: あのオバチャンに見せれば、 通してくれると思いますよ。
English: Show that to the security lady and she should let you in.

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 16 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 荷星さん、取り調べ中だって。
English: Apparently they have Mr. Powers in questioning.

Japanese: うう‥‥ん。
English: Hmm... right.

Japanese: そこのカメラ、人が通ると 自動的に写真をとるッス。
English: Look pal, that camera takes a picture automatically whenever someone walks by.

Japanese: その写真に、荷星　三郎が 写っていたッス！
English: And it took a picture of Will Powers!

Japanese: ‥‥あのことを 聞きたかったのに‥‥。
English: I have to ask him about that photograph!

Japanese: しょうがないよ。 またあとで来よう。
English: Oh well. Let's try again later.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: どうやら今は、 することがないのだろう。
English: He must get pretty bored.

Japanese: 向かい側のカメラに向かって おもしろいカオをしている。
English: I bet he's making funny faces at that camera.

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 16 Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: うわあ。‥‥ここで トノサマンが作られてるんだ！
English: Wow! So this is where they make the Steel Samurai show!

Japanese: すごいなあ！ カンゲキだよねー！
English: Awesome! Aren't you excited!?

Japanese: こらッ！　アンタたちッ！
English: Hey! You there!

Japanese: 勝手に入っちゃダメでしょ！
English: You want in, you gotta go through me!

Japanese: い、いえあの。
English: O-oh... s-sorry!

Japanese: あたしたち、その、 べ、弁護士でして‥‥。
English: W-we're, um, lawyers.

Japanese: ああそお。 オバチャンは警備員なの。
English: Oh yeah? Well that's great. I'm security!

Japanese: アンタらみたいなヤジ馬を 入れさせないのが仕事なのョ。
English: And it's my job to make sure that gawkers like you stay OUT.

Japanese: ヤジ馬‥‥？
English: G-gawkers?

Japanese: アンタたちもアレでしょ？
English: Gawkers! Sightseers! Tourists!!!

Japanese: 昨日の事件を聞いて、 おもしろ半分、やって来たんだろ。
English: I know the type. You heard about the incident and came to snoop around.

Japanese: まったく最近の若いモンときたら、 何かあると、すぐにこうだね。
English: Youths today! Something horrible happens and all you can think of is sightseeing.

Japanese: 昼間っから、 こんなところで油売っちゃって。
English: Wasting your time at a place like this.

Japanese: シゴトはどうしたの？
English: Don't you have jobs you should be doing?

Japanese: 弁護士なんてそんなヒマなモンなの かいそれならオバチャンも弁護士に
English: Or do lawyers not work like the rest of us? Maybe I should become a lawyer too. Sounds

Japanese: なればよかったよオバチャンが若い ころはこんなことはなかったねえ
English: like a life of ease to me no work just loafing around all day as you please. Well when

Japanese: 若いムスメがこんなヘンなカッコウ してああなげかわしいねえ日本の将
English: I was young things were a mite different, mind you we didn't prance around in strange

Japanese: 来はどうなるんだろうねえまったく ムスコはムスコでもう３年も電話を
English: hippie clothes. What are the youths coming to these days my own son hasn't called in...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Umm... Nick?

Japanese: あたしもいつか、こんなふうに なるのかなあ‥‥。
English: Will I grow up to be like her? Please say "no."

Japanese: やっぱり、そうなんじゃないかな。
English: I dunno! It's possible.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと！
English: Hey!

Japanese: アンタたち、 人の話を聞きなさいよッ！
English: Listen when you're being spoken to! Youths today!

Japanese: じゃ。そろそろアンタたち、 おウチへ帰るんだねェ。
English: Isn't it about time for you to be heading home?

Japanese: ここにいたって、何も出ないよ。
English: There's nothing to see here. Move along.

Japanese: あの。ぼくたちは、荷星さんの 依頼で、調査に来たんですよ。
English: Um, actually, we're here at Mr. Powers's request.

Japanese: ふん！　あんたら、 見た目からアヤシイもんねェ！
English: Hmph! I thought you were suspicious lookin'!

Japanese: あの子の書いた依頼状を 見せてみな。
English: Show me a "letter of request."

Japanese: それならオバチャン、入場を 許可してやらんでもないわョ。
English: And maybe I'll just let you in.

Japanese: このオバチャン、なんで こんなにエラそうなの？
English: This lady here thinks she owns this place, obviously.

Japanese: この撮影所の正面入口だ。
English: The main entrance to the studio.

Japanese: 昨日は、ヤジ馬が押しかけて 大変だったらしい。
English: There was a big scene here yesterday with onlookers pressing up against the gate.

Japanese: この撮影所のライトバンだ。
English: Looks like a studio van.

Japanese: 撮影用の資材などを 運んでいるのだろう。
English: They probably use it to carry around film equipment.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャンがむさぼるのだろう。 テーブルの上は、おかしの山だ。
English: That security lady must like junk food. There's a mountain of it on the desk in there.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: この撮影所の見取り図が 描かれている。
English: An overhead map of the studios.

Japanese: ここから右へ行くと 《スタッフ作業場》
English: Right from here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 左へ行くと《スタジオ方面》に 通じているらしい。
English: To the left are the actual film studios.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: こらッ！　勝手に さわんないでちょうだい！
English: Hey! No touching!

Japanese: この撮影所は どんなところなんですか。
English: So, what do you do here at the studios?

Japanese: この英都撮影所は、子供たちの ユメを作るところサ。
English: At Global Studios, we make children's dreams come true!

Japanese: オバチャンも昔はねェ‥‥。
English: In fact, in my younger days...

Japanese: ま、まさか！ スターだったんですか？
English: No! You were a star!?

Japanese: 大道具仲間のあいだじゃァ、 ちょっとしたスターだったョ。
English: Only a little twinkle between the stars here, I'm afraid, dearie.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Wo... wow.

Japanese: ここもねェ。‥‥今はすっかり さびれちゃったけどさ。
English: This place has really gone downhill, you know?

Japanese: １０年ぐらい前までは、 まさにユメのスタジオだったョ。
English: But 10 years ago, now that was a studio of dreams!

Japanese: そのころは、イブクロちゃんも 大スターでねェ。
English: Hammer was a big star back then too, he was...

Japanese: イブクロちゃん‥‥？
English: Hammer...?

Japanese: 被害者だョ、被害者。
English: The victim, dummy!

Japanese: 怪人アクダイカーンのこと！
English: The Evil Magistrate!

Japanese: 今じゃ、怪人なんてシケた役 やってるけどねェ‥‥。
English: He's been reduced to playing villains now. Not exactly the best material to work with...

Japanese: 荷星　三郎さん、 どんな人なんですか？
English: Can you tell me about Mr. Will Powers?

Japanese: 悪い子じゃないんだケド、 ぶら下げてるツラがアレじゃねェ。
English: He's not a bad kid, but don't be fooled by his mask!

Japanese: とても、お茶の間に出せる シロモノじゃないよ。
English: You wouldn't want him on the silver screen without it, believe me.

Japanese: メシがまずくなる。
English: Little old ladies watching would lose their lunch!

Japanese: それだけに、今回は“チャンスだ” って燃えていたみたいだけど、
English: That's probably why he thought that the Steel Samurai was his "big chance."

Japanese: まさか‥‥ イブクロちゃんをねェ‥‥。
English: No one thought he was capable of doing what he did to poor Jack Hammer...

Japanese: でも、でも、まだ荷星さんだと 決まったわけじゃあ‥‥！
English: We don't know for certain that Will Powers is guilty!

Japanese: 決まってるの。
English: Powers? Of course he's guilty!

Japanese: オバチャンは なんでも知ってんだから。
English: How do I know? I know everything! That's my job.

Japanese: イブクロちゃん‥‥て、 どんな方なんですか？
English: What kind of person was Jack Hammer?

Japanese: “どんな方”だって？ ‥‥泣かせるわねェ。
English: What "kind of person"? Oh... if you only knew!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志（いぶくろたけし）。 永遠の大アクション・スターだよ。
English: Jack Hammer will live on in many hearts as the ultimate action hero!

Japanese: 今でも忘れられないねえ、 “ダイナマイト侍”シリーズ。
English: He was simply dashing in the Dynamite Samurai series!

Japanese: （聞いたことないぞ‥‥）
English: (Should I have heard of that...?)

Japanese: でもさ。５年前にね。 撮影中に事故が起こっちゃって。
English: But... there was an accident during filming five years ago.

Japanese: それで、ケチがついちゃった。
English: He got an unlucky break after that.

Japanese: 今じゃ、子供相手の番組の、 悪の使者だよ。
English: Reduced to playing the villain on a children's program!

Japanese: ギャラも格安でね。 ‥‥セツない話よねェ。
English: What's more, I heard they were paying him peanuts. It's enough to make you cry.

Japanese: なんで荷星さんを犯人だと 思うんですか？
English: Why are you so certain Mr. Powers is the killer?

Japanese: オバチャンねェ、 昨日もここに立っていたのよ。
English: I was standing right here yesterday, I was.

Japanese: 午後１時から、死体が見つかる ５時まで、ずーっとよォ？
English: I was here from 1:00 in the afternoon, to 5:00, when they found the body!

Japanese: 殺人現場のスタジオ、この道を 左に行けばいいんだけどォ、
English: Now the studio where the murder took place is to the left here.

Japanese: スタジオへ行こうと思ったら、 かならずここを通ることになるの。
English: So if you want to go to the studio, you have to pass by me!

Japanese: １時から、殺人があった２時３０分 まで、ここを通ったのは、１人！
English: Only one person went by here between 1:00 and 2:30, when the murder took place!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、荷星さんだった というわけですか。
English: And... that person was Mr. Powers?

Japanese: そう。 オバチャン、見てたから。
English: Yes. I saw him!

Japanese: でも、荷星さんは、楽屋で 寝てたって言ってましたよ。
English: But Mr. Powers says he was sleeping in his dressing room.

Japanese: そりゃ、そう言うでしょ。 あの子も、バカじゃないから。
English: Oh, I'm sure he would say that. He's no fool!

Japanese: でもね。ここを通ったのは あの子しかいないんだ。
English: But he was the only one that walked by.

Japanese: 間違いなく、ハンニンだよ。
English: He's the killer, you can bet your biscuits on it.

Japanese: オバチャン、これ。 荷星さんからの依頼状。
English: Ma'am, have a look at this. It's a letter of request from Mr. Powers.
Hmm...?

Japanese: この頭のワルそうな字は、 たしかに、あの子だね。
English: Yes, yes. I recognize the bold, childish scrawl.

Japanese: 証拠品《荷星　三郎の依頼状》を オバチャンにわたした。
English: Gave Powers's Request to the security lady.

Japanese: あの子も、こんなたよりなさそうな 弁護士をたのむなんて。
English: To think he would entrust his fate to youths of such unreliable appearance...

Japanese: ねえ？
English: Really!

Japanese: “ねえ”と言われましても‥‥。
English: Really?

Japanese: まあいいや。 通行を許可してあげるわヨ。
English: Anyway, you may pass.

Japanese: ただし、ここから左の スタジオ方面だけだよ。
English: But only left from here, toward the studios!

Japanese: 右方面はダメ。 スタッフの作業所があるんだけど、
English: No going to the right. That's the Employee Area.

Japanese: “誰も通しちゃダメッス”って
English: "No one allowed inside, pal" were my instructions.

Japanese: 刑事が言ってたからね。
English: The good detective told me himself.

Japanese: ‥‥ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, hey, Nick.

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 詰所のところに、地図があったよ。 この撮影所の。
English: There were maps in the security guard station.

Japanese: おお！　いいね、これ。
English: Hey! Not bad!

Japanese: コラ！　１枚５０円だョ！
English: Those are 50 cents a piece, thank you!

Japanese: よし。じゃ、行こうか なるほどくん。
English: Well, time to go, Nick.
50 cents!

Japanese: 英都撮影所の《上面図》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Guidemap of Global Studios added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんだいそれ？ オバチャンにくれるのかい？
English: What's that? You giving that to me?

Japanese: （カンベンしてくれぇ‥‥）
English: (Absolutely not!)

Japanese: 見て！　見て！ なるほどくん！
English: Nick! Look!

Japanese: オバチャン、詰所の中で ようかん食べて、お茶飲んでる。
English: That security lady is in the guard station stuffing her face with donuts!

Japanese: “ニッポンのオバチャン”って ふぜいだねー。
English: I guess all cops like their donuts!

Japanese: ちょっと“おばあちゃん”寄りな 気もするけど。
English: Yeah, and they're soft enough she doesn't need teeth to chew them...

Japanese: あら、アンタたち。
English: Hey, you!

Japanese: どう？　あの子が犯人だって ナットクした？
English: Well? Are you satisfied Powers is guilty yet?

Japanese: （くそー‥‥）
English: (Urk!)

Japanese: （このオバチャンが、あの刑事に 証拠写真をあげたんだよな‥‥）
English: (She had to make my life harder by giving that photo to Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: オバチャン、なんで写真のこと 教えてくれなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you tell us about the security photo, ma'am?

Japanese: ああ。あれ？
English: Hmm? Oh, that?

Japanese: 刑事から聞いた方が、よりスリルが あるかなって。
English: I just thought it would be more thrilling to talk with the detective himself!

Japanese: オバチャン、気をつかう方だから。
English: These things are important, you know.

Japanese: あれ、ここからすぐ左に行った ゲートのカメラが撮ったのョ。
English: Oh, just to the left from here is the gate with the camera that took that photo.

Japanese: 毎日、写真をチェックするのも、 オバチャンのシゴトでサ。
English: It's my job to check the photos every day, you see.

Japanese: この撮影所、どこへ行くにも、
English: This guard station is in a central position.

Japanese: かならず、この警備詰所の前を 通らなくちゃならないのョ。
English: No matter where you go here, you pass by my station first!

Japanese: イブクロちゃんがスタジオに 行ったのが、午後１時チョイ前。
English: Now poor Hammer, he went to the studio just before 1:00.

Japanese: 事件が起こったのが、２時半。
English: The murder happened right around 2:30, see?

Japanese: その間、ここでオバチャンが 見たのは、荷星だけ。
English: The only one I seen go through here between 1:00 and 2:30 was Powers himself!

Japanese: カメラにもそれが写っている。
English: The security camera got a good look at him too.

Japanese: これで犯人じゃなかったら、 誰が犯人よ。
English: If he's not the one what did it, I don't know who is!

Japanese: オバチャン、とか。
English: Maybe... it was you?

Japanese: ぎゃはははははは！ おもしろいコト言うねアンタ。
English: Gyah hah ha hah! Good one, sonny.

Japanese: （まったく 気にとめていない‥‥）
English: (She thinks I was joking.)

Japanese: 被害者のイブクロさん、 人気はあったんですか？
English: Was the victim, Mr. Hammer, a popular actor?

Japanese: それはもう、スゴいもん だったわョ。
English: Oh, he was the biggest star in the studio!

Japanese: （“スゴかった”、か。 過去の話なんだな‥‥）
English: ("Was"? Past tense?)

Japanese: あたし、小さいころ“ヒゲ武将” シリーズ、大好きでした！
English: Oh yes! He was great as the "Bearded Samurai"!

Japanese: ああ。あれねェ‥‥。
English: That too, yes.

Japanese: よかったよ、ホント、 むかしはさァ。
English: He shone the brightest! Back in the day.

Japanese: ‥‥それが今は“アクダイカーン” だもんねェ。
English: Making a star like him play the "Evil Magistrate"... It's a disgrace, I tell you!

Japanese: そりゃあ、イブクロちゃん、 荒れてもしょうがないよ。
English: Hammer took it pretty hard, and who can blame him?

Japanese: その、カメラの ことなんですけど‥‥。
English: About the security camera...

Japanese: ああ、カメラね。
English: Oh yes, the camera.

Japanese: あのゲートをくぐると、 センサーってヤツが反応してサ。
English: It's automatic. It can tell when someone walks through that gate, see?

Japanese: 勝手に写真を撮るワケ。
English: Then it snaps a photo!

Japanese: 写真を撮った時間も 記録されるんだって。
English: Apparently, it records the time when it takes a picture too.

Japanese: オバチャン、そのへんのことは よくわかんないケド。
English: But I don't bother myself with those details.

Japanese: 撮った写真は、あそこの詰所の コンピュータで見られるから。
English: I just view all the photos on the computer over in the security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャン、いつも帰る前に、 写真をチェックするワケョ。
English: I check 'em every day before going home, I do.

Japanese: オバチャン、これ‥‥。
English: Could you tell me about this, ma'am?

Japanese: アラそれ。スタジオのカギ じゃないの。
English: Oh that! Isn't that a studio cardkey?

Japanese: ダメよアンタたち！
English: You better watch it!

Japanese: おもしろ半分で 殺人現場に入っちゃ。
English: The scene of a murder is no place for sightseeing!

Japanese: オ、オバチャンがあずかります。 ホラ、貸しなさい！
English: I'll be taking that! Hand it over!

Japanese: わ、ちょ、ちょっと！ ダメですよ！
English: W-wait! No, that's mine!

Japanese: オバチャン、あのスタジオの カードキー、持ってないのョ！
English: Look, I don't have a cardkey to the studio, see?

Japanese: イブクロちゃんに、ちゃんと お参りしておきたいじゃないのサ！
English: I want to go pay my respects to Jack Hammer where he died.

Japanese: い‥‥今はまだダメです！
English: W... well you'll just have to wait!

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 16 Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: あら、アンタたち。 まだいたんだ。
English: What? You're still here?

Japanese: ‥‥どしたの。 コワいカオしちゃってェ！
English: Really! You look as though you've seen a ghost!

Japanese: 教えなさい！　早く言いなさいよ このトンガリ頭！
English: Alright you better tell me and tell me quick, you spiky-haired cretin!

Japanese: このオバチャンのシゴトにケチを つけるなんて！
English: Someone's been complaining about the work I do, eh!?

Japanese: あ、あの、第１スタジオにいた スタッフの子ですけど‥‥
English: Uh... um, we were just talking to the assistant in Studio One.

Japanese: あいつか！ アルバイトのぶんざいで‥‥！
English: Her! She's not even a full-time employee!

Japanese: だいたい今の若いムスメはみんな アレだョペラペラしゃべりすぎサ
English: All you young'uns are like that nowadays, running your mouths like there was no

Japanese: あることないことテキトーなこと ばっかりだョオバチャンがワカい
English: tomorrow saying this that and the other thing. Well when I was young let me tell you we

Japanese: コロはそんなことなかったョみん な思いやりってもんがあったそれ
English: knew a thing or two about respecting your elders. Back then we did and it's a crying

Japanese: が今じゃナニかと言えばカゲグチ ばっかりでオバチャンかなしいョ
English: shame that you youths today just say what you please about poor old ladies

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Graaaaaaaaaaaawr!

Japanese: 行っちゃったよ‥‥。
English: She left...

Japanese: さ、なるほどくん。
English: Well, Nick?

Japanese: 今のうちに、やれるコトは やっておこうよ！
English: This is our chance to do what we can without her looking over our shoulders!

Japanese: ね、ね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, hey, Nick!

Japanese: この写真があれば、 裁判、なんとか行けるんじゃない？
English: Can't we use this photo as evidence for the trial?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: これで、あのオバチャンに ぎゃふんと言わせてやれるね！
English: We'll put that security lady in her place with this!

Japanese: ぎゃふん‥‥。
English: Right, we'll put her in her place...

Japanese: なんで、なるほどくんが 言うの？
English: Hey, that's what I said! Anyway...

Japanese: ‥‥ま、いいや。 じゃあ、さっそくこの写真、 オバチャンにつきつけて‥‥。
English: Let's show her this photo and see what she says!

Japanese: いや。やめとこう。
English: Wait. Let's not.

Japanese: なんで？
English: Why not?

Japanese: 敵にわざわざ手の内を 明かすことはないよ。
English: It's never a good idea to reveal your hand to the enemy too soon.

Japanese: うわあ、なるほどくん。 おぬし、意外とワルだねえ。
English: Nick! You're craftier than I gave you credit for.

Japanese: ２代目、アクダイカーン。
English: Why, you could be the next... Evil Magistrate!

Japanese: 人を怪人にしないでほしいな。
English: Hey! Why do I have to be the villain.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。ま。じゃあ今日は、 そろそろ切り上げようよ。
English: Relax, it was just a joke! So, are we done for today?

Japanese: とりあえず、この写真があれば なんとか戦えるだろう。
English: I think this photo is what we needed for the trial.

Japanese: 犯人が誰なのか、ケントーも つかないのが気になるけど。
English: Still, it'd be better if we had some idea who the real killer was!

Japanese: まさか、ほんとにあの オバチャンだったりして‥‥。
English: Maybe it really is that security lady...?

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick!

Japanese: このコンピュータで、荷星さんの 写真、見られるんだよね。
English: Maybe we can see that photo of Powers with this computer!

Japanese: うん。たぶんね。
English: Yeah, maybe.

Japanese: （コンピュータを操作しようか？）
English: (Should I try the computer?)

Japanese: よし。 ‥‥ちょっとやってみよう。
English: Okay. Let's give it a try.

Japanese: なるほどくん、 コンピュータ、使えるの？
English: You know how to work these things, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥ナンバーを 入力するぐらいなら。
English: Looks like I just have to enter in a few numbers.

Japanese: とりあえず、事件当日の データを‥‥。
English: Let's see... first, I need the date of the murder.

Japanese: （事件当日は、午後１時から、 カメラは作動したらしい）
English: (It looks like the camera turned on at 1:00 PM that day...)

Japanese: （オバチャンが詰所に来たのが １時だからかな）
English: (1:00... That was when the security lady arrived at the guard station.)

Japanese: 《セキュリティカメラの ナンバーを入力してください》
English: [Please enter the number of the security camera.]

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥。勝手にイジると オバチャンにおこられそうだ）
English: (Hmm... I'd better not fool around with this, or that security lady will get me.)

Japanese: （やめておくか‥‥）
English: (Maybe next time.)

Japanese: えー。やめちゃうの？ ‥‥つまんない。
English: What? You're not going to look at it? Bo-ring!

Japanese: よし、 入力したぞ。
English: There! Entered!
Nothing happened.

Japanese: あれ。 ナンバーが違ったかな。
English: Huh? Maybe I got the number wrong.

Japanese: あーあ。 もう一度、見てこようか。
English: *sigh* Maybe we should go look at that camera again?

Japanese: よし、 入力したぞ。
English: There! Entered!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あっ！　データが プリントアウトされてきたよ！
English: Hey! It printed out the data for that day!

Japanese: どれどれ。
English: Let's see what we got...
Huh?

Japanese: なんでこれが “荷星　三郎”の写真なの？
English: How is this a picture of Will Powers?

Japanese: まあ、いつも中に荷星さんが 入っているから、
English: Well, he is the one who always wears that suit...

Japanese: オバチャンも“荷星だ”って 思い込んじゃったのかも。
English: I guess that's why the security lady thought it was him.

Japanese: ‥‥これをもらった刑事さんの 顔色がサエないのもムリないね。
English: I don't imagine the detective was very happy with this photo as evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 この写真のウラ‥‥。
English: Hmm? Something's printed on the back.

Japanese: ん？　《１０ガツ１５ニチ ゴゴ２ジ‥‥２ニンメ》か。
English: Huh? [Oct 15, 2:00 PM, Photo #2]

Japanese: どういうことかなあ‥‥？
English: What does that mean?

Japanese: 他に、データは？
English: Maybe there's more photo data from that day?

Japanese: ないよ。この写真だけ。
English: Nope--that's the only one in the computer.

Japanese: 《荷星（？）の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Powers's(?) Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: 事件当日のデータはいただいたし、 もう操作する必要はないな。
English: No point fiddling with this any more. We have all the data from the day of the murder.

Japanese: なんだい？　その、人を ウタガウような目つきは？
English: What? I saw that suspicious look on your face!

Japanese: 昨日のことを もう一度聞きますけど、
English: I wanted to ask you again about yesterday...

Japanese: オバチャンがここに来たのが 午後１時。
English: You came here at 1:00 PM, correct?

Japanese: で、被害者の死亡推定時刻の ２時３０分までに、
English: And the estimated time of Hammer's death was 2:30.

Japanese: ここを通ったのは、荷星さん １人‥‥。間違いないですか？
English: Are you sure that Powers was the only one to go through here between those times?

Japanese: ああ。ないョ。
English: Sure as can be!

Japanese: でも、“部外者の気配を感じた” って言ってる人がいるんですよ。
English: But we have a witness who thinks there was "someone from outside" here that day.

Japanese: なんだってェ‥‥？
English: What!?

Japanese: オバチャン、ホントに ちゃんと見張ってたんですか？
English: Are you absolutely sure you were here watching the whole time?
Ma'am?

Japanese: 誰が‥‥
English: Who was it?

Japanese: 誰がそんな、イイカゲンなコトを！
English: Who told you that? Who dares question ME!?

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 撮影所・スタッフエリア
English: October 16 Global Studios Employee Area

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ここ、スタッフの人たちが 作業するところだよ。
English: This is where they do all the behind-the-scenes stuff!

Japanese: あっ。あそこ。 荷星さんの楽屋がある！
English: Hey! Look! There's Powers's dressing room!

Japanese: 事件があったばっかりだから、 まだ誰もいないんだ。
English: No one's here this soon after the murder I guess.

Japanese: いろいろ見てまわろうよ！
English: Let's take a look around!

Japanese: “お茶”“みそ汁”“おしるこ” “くず湯”“おでん”‥‥
English: Let's see... cola, candy, chips, gum...

Japanese: ラインナップが 和風に統一されている。
English: Huh. It's so... normal.

Japanese: “ウーロン茶”すらないぞ。
English: I was expecting some cool theme snacks or something. Popcorn, at least!

Japanese: あ。なあに、この “トノサマン汁”って‥‥。
English: W-wait! Look! "Samurai Soda"!

Japanese: なるほどくん。飲んでみてよ。
English: Nick! Try this!

Japanese: （自分で飲めよ‥‥）
English: (Ladies first...)

Japanese: 楽屋の入口に、 張り紙がしてある。
English: There's a sign on the dressing room door.

Japanese: “荷星　三郎様”か。 ‥‥さすが、主役だな。
English: "Mr. Will Powers"... it must be neat to be the star and have your own room like this.

Japanese: 楽屋に入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていないみたいだ。
English: The door to the dressing room. It's not locked.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。 入ってみたいよー。
English: Hey, Nick! Let's take a look inside!

Japanese: 記念に、なんか もらっていっちゃおう！
English: Maybe we can find something as a souvenir...?

Japanese: “なんか”ってなんだよ？
English: What do you mean "find"!?

Japanese: “トノサマン・スピアー”とか。
English: You never know! There might be something like... the Samurai Spear!

Japanese: 凶器じゃないか、それ！
English: We don't need a spear! (And that's the murder weapon for crying out loud!)

Japanese: 楽屋とそとをむすぶ窓だ。
English: A window into the dressing room.

Japanese: くもりガラスなので、 部屋の中を見ることはできない。
English: The glass is frosted, so I can't see inside.

Japanese: 巨大な倉庫だ。
English: That must be the storage room.

Japanese: 撮影用の大道具が しまってあるようだ。
English: That's where they keep all the film equipment.

Japanese: すごーい、なるほどくん。 なんでも知ってるんだねー！
English: Wow, Nick! You know a lot about the industry!

Japanese: 見た感じを言ってみただけだよ。
English: Umm, what else would they keep in a film studio?

Japanese: モーターを乗せた、 電動自転車だ。
English: Looks like one of those electric-powered bicycles.

Japanese: なんともいえない中途ハンパな 感じが、ぼくはキライだ。
English: I dunno about these. I mean, what's the point?

Japanese: えー。でも、坂をのぼるの ラクだよ。
English: It makes it a lot easier to go up hills!

Japanese: 若いのに、あんなもの使うな！
English: Don't people cycle to get exercise!?

Japanese: 排水口の金網が、はずれている。
English: The metal grate on the drain here has been taken off.

Japanese: けっこう大きい穴だ。子供なら 通ることができそうだ。
English: That's a pretty big drain. A kid could fit through there, I bet.

Japanese: あ。あそこから入ってくれば、 入場料、払わなくてもいいね。
English: Yeah, if you snuck in that way you wouldn't have to pay at the gate!

Japanese: じゃあ、帰りは あそこから出たら？
English: Well, if you want to leave that way, be my guest.

Japanese: うん！　そうする‥‥！
English: Right!
Huh? Hey! I'm not a kid!

Japanese: 排水口にハマっていた 金網だ。
English: The grate that used to cover the drain.

Japanese: ちょっと曲がっている。 ハメなおすのはムリだな。
English: It's a little bent. I don't think it would fit if I tried to put it back on.

Japanese: スタッフたちの、 昨日の昼食のあとだ。
English: The remains of yesterday's lunch are scattered around.

Japanese: 事件があったため、 誰も片づけていないのだろう。
English: Everyone was probably too shocked to clean up.

Japanese: 皿の上には、ホネつきステーキの ホネだけが乗っている。
English: There's a t-bone on one of the plates, minus the steak.

Japanese: あたしも食べたかったな。 ホネつきステーキ。
English: Mmm! T-bone steak! That would have hit the spot.

Japanese: さっき、みそラーメン 食べただろ？
English: You just had a burger!

Japanese: みそラーメンは、別バラ。
English: Yeah, but I have a second stomach just for steaks.

Japanese: （どんなハラだよ‥‥）
English: (What!?)

Japanese: 描きかけの書き割りが 立てかけてある。
English: There's a half-finished backdrop for a stage here.

Japanese: ネオ・エドシティの城 ということは、
English: Looks like a castle.

Japanese: おそらく“ネオ・エドジョー” とでもいうのだろう。
English: Probably "Neo Olde Tokyo Castle."

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 撮影所・楽屋
English: October 16 Global Studios Dressing Room

Japanese: （‥‥荷星さんの楽屋。 事件があった昨日の午後は‥‥）
English: (Powers's dressing room...) (So this is where he was sleeping?)

Japanese: （ここでずっとヒルネをしていた、 って言ってたけど‥‥）
English: (Or at least, this is where he claims he was sleeping.)

Japanese: ヒルネしているところ、誰も 見ていないんだよね。
English: No one actually saw him taking a nap here, did they?

Japanese: そのかわり、殺人現場の近くで 写真を撮られてるんだよな。
English: Right, and there's a picture of him near the crime scene.

Japanese: （荷星さん‥‥。信用して、 いいんですよね‥‥？）
English: (I hope he's telling the truth... for his sake!)

Japanese: 撮影用の和服にまじって、 洋服もちらほら。
English: There's some regular suits in among the costumes here.

Japanese: 荷星さんの私服だろうか。
English: Those must be his own clothes.

Japanese: うう‥‥記念に 着てみたいなあ、衣装。
English: It'd be fun to wear one of the costumes...

Japanese: （似たようなの、着てるくせに）
English: (I'd say you were already wearing one, myself.)

Japanese: カギがかかっている。
English: It's locked.

Japanese: あまり開けられることは ないみたいだ。
English: Looks like he doesn't open his window much.

Japanese: まあ、そとはホコリっぽいから、 ムリもないかもしれないな。
English: I guess it is kind of dusty outside.

Japanese: スタッフからの差し入れかな。 おかしがならんでいる。
English: There's a bunch of snacks on the table. They must give these to the employees.

Japanese: 和菓子に、お茶、か。
English: Some tea and cookies...

Japanese: うー、なるほどくん。 食べたいねえ。
English: Nick... I'm hungry!

Japanese: さっき、みそラーメン 食べただろ。
English: You just had a burger!

Japanese: おかしは、別バラだよ。
English: Yeah, but I have a separate stomach for sweets!

Japanese: （女の子は、ハラをいくつ 持っているのだろう‥‥）
English: (How many stomachs does this girl have!?)

Japanese: あ。荷星さんのカバンかな。
English: Hmm? This must be Powers's bag.

Japanese: こら。‥‥勝手に開けない。
English: Hey, don't open that.

Japanese: あ、こんなものが‥‥。
English: Look!

Japanese: スタッフ用のカードキーだ。 荷星さんのものだろう。
English: An employee cardkey. That must be Powers's.

Japanese: 《第１スタジオ》と書いてある。
English: It says "Studio One."

Japanese: もらっておこうよ。 なるほどくん。
English: Let's take it, Nick!

Japanese: 借りるだけ、な。
English: Borrow it. You mean borrow.

Japanese: 《カードキー》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Cardkey added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 荷星さんのカバン、 他に何があるのかなぁ‥‥。
English: I wonder what else is inside Powers's bag...?

Japanese: こらこら。やめとけって！ （コリない子だなあ‥‥）
English: Stop that! (My partner, the thief in training.)

Japanese: 楽屋といえば、かならずある カガミだ。
English: They always have mirrors like this in dressing rooms.

Japanese: うーん。今日もバッチリ とがってるな、ぼくのアタマ。
English: I see my hair is still nice and spiky, just how I like it.

Japanese: メイク用の小道具なんかが ならんでいる。
English: There's a vast array of cosmetics here.

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ooh!

Japanese: “ちょんまげブギ”で使っている カツラがある！
English: This is the wig he used in "Samurai Boogey-Woogey"!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ かぶって！　かぶって！
English: Nick! Try this on!

Japanese: やだよ。
English: No.

Japanese: なんで、カツラを見ただけで 番組名までわかるんだよ。
English: And how can you tell the name of a show just by looking at a wig!?

Japanese: 時代劇は、ちょっと、ね。
English: I, um, kinda like samurai movies.

Japanese: （なにが“ちょっと”だよ‥‥）
誰かが寝たあとが 残っている。
English: Looks like someone was sleeping here.

Japanese: 昨日、依頼人の荷星さんが ヒルネしていたあとだろうか。
English: Maybe Powers did take a nap here yesterday...

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 第１スタジオ前
English: October 16 Studio One Entrance

Japanese: あっ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: アンタは、この前の殺人犯！
English: Aren't you that murderer from the other day!

Japanese: ああああっ！ この前の大マチガイ刑事！
English: Aah! It's that confused detective!

Japanese: アンタたちのせいで、御剣検事は ちょっとブルーになってるッス！
English: Hey pal, you know Prosecutor Edgeworth is all upset, and it's your fault!

Japanese: 午後のティーを飲みながら、 窓ぎわでタソガレているッス！
English: I saw him sipping tea and staring gloomily out the window!

Japanese: し、知りませんよそんなの。
English: Umm... so?

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: だいたい、あなたの捜査がデタラメ だから、いけないんでしょ！
English: If he's depressed, it's all your fault for doing sloppy detective work!
Um, Detective?

Japanese: 痛いトコロを突いたらしいね。
English: I think you hurt his feelings.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうしよう。 あたしが悪いのかな？
English: Oh no! I... I'm sorry!

Japanese: 意外にナイーブな男みたいだ。
English: Well, I think his feelings are easily hurt.

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。 自分のせいッス。
English: You're right, pal. It's all my fault.

Japanese: ヒトのせいにしても、 自分の心だけはごまかせないッス。
English: I can blame other people all I want, but I know the truth in my heart!

Japanese: ま、まあまあ。 次、がんばればいいんですよ！
English: Hey, hey! Don't take it so hard! There's always the next case!

Japanese: そ、そッスよね。
English: Yeah, I suppose you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥って。
English: Hey!

Japanese: なんでアンタたちが ここにいるッス？
English: What exactly are you two doing here?

Japanese: あのねー。
English: Um, well...

Japanese: あたしたち、この事件を 担当することになったッス。
English: We're on this case too, pal!

Japanese: あっ！　あっ！ マネしちゃダメッス！
English: Huh!? Hey! You can't just go saying "pal" like that!

Japanese: あ‥‥あいでんてぃてぃーが ホーカイするッス！
English: That's MY endearing character trait!

Japanese: おもしろい人だねー、 なるほどくん。
English: I'd say he's a character alright...

Japanese: どうしたッス？ なんか、元気がないッスね？
English: What's wrong, pal? You seem down.

Japanese: そんなうれしそうなカオして 言わないでください。
English: Don't look so happy when you say that.

Japanese: ハッハッハッハッハッハッハッ。
English: Oh hoh hoh hoh hoh!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: あたしたち、スタッフの人とか にも、話を聞きたいんですけど。
English: We'd like to ask the employees here some questions...

Japanese: あ、いッスよ。
English: Sure thing, pal.

Japanese: もお、どこでも好きなところに 行くッス。
English: Go wherever you like.

Japanese: 何をやってもムダッスけど。 ハッハッハッハッハッハッハッ。
English: Of course, you're not going to find any clues I haven't already found! Hah hah hah!

Japanese: （人間って、こんなにホガらかに 笑えるもんなんだ‥‥）
English: (I'm glad someone around here seems to be enjoying themselves.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick, let's go!

Japanese: 許可もらったんだから、 いろいろ、行ってみようよ。
English: We've got a free pass to the place. Now's our chance to check things out!

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ通じる入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が 死体で見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: なるほどくん、ちょっと 入ってみようよ！
English: Let's go in and check it out, Nick!

Japanese: 楽屋から借りてきた、この カードキー、使えるかな‥‥
English: I wonder if this cardkey we borrowed from the dressing room will work...

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: お、開いたぞ。
English: It opened!

Japanese: やったあ！ さ。さ。行ってみようよ！
English: Alright! We're in!

Japanese: （殺人現場に行くってのに、 ブキミなほど浮かれてるな‥‥）
English: (She's way too happy for someone visiting a crime scene.)

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ通じる入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が 死体で見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: なるほどくん、ちょっと 入ってみようよ！
English: Let's go in and check it out, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... あれえ。カギがかかってるよ！
English: Hey! It's locked!

Japanese: ‥‥スタジオに入るには、 カードキーがいるみたいだ。
English: Looks like you need a cardkey to get in.

Japanese: スタッフ以外の人間を 入れないためなんだろう。
English: It makes sense that they would want to keep the non-employees out.

Japanese: イトノコさん。左の道の 奥には何があるんですか？
English: Detective Gumshoe, what's down the path to the left?

Japanese: なんか、木がたおれてますけど。
English: It looks like a tree fell down over the path...?

Japanese: ああ。そっちは危ないから 行っちゃダメッスよ。
English: Oh, yeah. That way's kind of dangerous. You should stay out, pal.

Japanese: その奥には、何もないッス。
English: There's nothing down there, anyway.

Japanese: 昔は第２撮影スタジオが あったッスが、
English: That's where Studio Two used to be, they tell me.

Japanese: 今は使ってないッス。
English: They don't use it now, though.

Japanese: この撮影スタジオの マスコット・キャラクターだ。
English: The studio mascot.

Japanese: なんでも、“テングザル”を モチーフにしてるとか。
English: I guess it's a monkey of some kind.

Japanese: でも、テングって、こんな ダンゴっ鼻なのかなあ？
English: But what kind of monkey has a nose like that?

Japanese: お。手にはカンバンを 持っているな。
English: He's holding a sign in his hand.

Japanese: 《右　‥‥　第１撮影スタジオ 左　‥‥　第２撮影スタジオ》
English: [Right: To Studio One Left:  To Studio Two]

Japanese: そ、そんなことより、 どうしたんですか、あれ？
English: What's up with that thing!? It looks kind of... busted.

Japanese: 昨日、強い風で、クビが 折れたッス。
English: Oh, the wind was pretty strong yesterday, and the head broke off.

Japanese: そこの木も道づれッス。
English: It took that tree down with it, too.

Japanese: お。こんなところに カメラがある。
English: Hey, there's a camera here.

Japanese: スタジオに行く人間を、 写真に撮るためのカメラッス。
English: That's the security camera. It takes photos of people going to the studio.

Japanese: データを見れば、誰がスタジオに 行ったか、一目でわかるッス。
English: All you have to do is look at the data and you can see who went where!

Japanese: カメラに、プレートがついている。 《ＳＴ１－３０７》
English: There's a number plate on the camera... [ST1-307].

Japanese: このカメラのナンバーらしい。
English: That must be the camera ID number.

Japanese: ちょっとつかれたな。 ‥‥座って休もうか。
English: I'm a little bushed. Maybe I'll take a short rest here.

Japanese: お。いいッスね。
English: Hey, good idea, pal!

Japanese: なんで刑事さんが 休むんですか！
English: Detective! Aren't you on the case?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥。
English: W-well, yeah, but...

Japanese: 立ちっぱなしって、けっこう つかれるッス‥‥。
English: Hey, us detectives get tired of standing around too, pal!

Japanese: 『おいでませ』だって。
English: There's a big welcome sign above the path.

Japanese: このスタジオも 経営が苦しいッス。
English: Yeah, apparently the studio's fallen on tough times.

Japanese: 最近は、休日には一般の人に 解放して、
English: They've started letting people in on days they aren't filming.

Japanese: 見物料を取ってるらしいッス。
English: They make a bit of extra money by charging for admission.

Japanese: へー。 くわしいんだ、刑事さん。
English: Huh. You sure know a lot about this place, Detective.

Japanese: 刑事やめて、ここで はたらいたらどうですか？
English: You know, you could always quit being a detective and work here?

Japanese: そうですよ。あそこのサルの クビも折れたことだし。
English: Yeah! And what perfect timing, with the monkey's neck broken and all.

Japanese: 意味がわからないッス！
English: Huh? Just what's that supposed to mean, pal!?

Japanese: あの。今、そちらの 捜査は、どんな感じですか？
English: So, how is your investigation going, Detective?

Japanese: そんなコト、 教えるワケにはいかないッス。
English: Well, it's... Hey! I can't tell you that, pal.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: さすがに、そんなにストレートに 聞いてもダメなんじゃないかなあ。
English: Maybe you need to be a little more indirect with your questions?

Japanese: じゃあ、刑事さん。 例のヤツ、くださいよ。
English: Right. So, Detective, mind if I take a look at what you got?

Japanese: 例のヤツ？
English: What I got?

Japanese: 解剖記録です。 ‥‥今度は、最新のデータを。
English: The autopsy report! The latest version, if you please...

Japanese: うう‥‥わかったッス。
English: Hmm... right, right.

Japanese: この前の事件では、 メイワクかけたッスからな。
English: Sorry about what happened last time, pal.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あの。今、そちらの 捜査は、どんな感じですか？
English: So, how is your investigation going, Detective?

Japanese: これ以上の情報は、もう やれないッス。
English: I'm not at liberty to give you any more information, pal.

Japanese: あとは、法廷で聞けばいいッス！
English: You'll hear all you need to know in court!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh well, it was worth a try.)

Japanese: どうして、荷星さんが 逮捕されたんですか？
English: Why was Mr. Powers arrested?

Japanese: 簡単なコトッス。
English: Simple, pal!

Japanese: 殺人事件は、この先の 第１スタジオで起こったッス。
English: The murder took place right over there in Studio One.

Japanese: 被害者が第１スタジオに 入ったのが、午後１時。
English: Now, the victim entered Studio One at approximately 1:00 PM.

Japanese: このとき、被害者以外の人間は、 スタジオにはいなかったッス。
English: At that time, there was no one other than the victim in the studio.

Japanese: そして、被害者が死んだ時間は、 解剖の結果、午後２時３０分。
English: According to the autopsy report, the time of death was 2:30 PM.

Japanese: その間、スタジオに行った人物は、 たった１人。
English: Only one person went to the studio between 1:00 and 2:30!

Japanese: 荷星　三郎ッス。
English: And that person was none other than Will Powers!

Japanese: 彼以外、スタジオに行けた人物は いないッス。
English: No one else, pal!

Japanese: ウソだと思うなら、警備員の オバチャンに聞いてみるッス！
English: If you think I'm lying, ask the security lady at the main gate.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥それがもしホントなら、
English: Nick! If that's true...!

Japanese: 誰が聞いても、ハンニンは 荷星さんしかいないじゃない！
English: Anyone would think that Powers did it with that kind of evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥ブッソウなこと 言うなよ‥‥。
English: Thanks for cheering me up...

Japanese: あの、警備の オバチャンですけど‥‥。
English: About the security lady...

Japanese: ああ。とてもやさしい、 いいオバチャンッス。
English: Oh, that sweet old lady. What a charmer!

Japanese: えッ！ ‥‥ど、どこが‥‥？
English: Huh? A-are we talking about the same person?

Japanese: ケーサツ手帳見せたら、お茶と ようかんを出してくれたッス。
English: When I showed her my badge she gave me a donut and some coffee!

Japanese: ‥‥あのオバチャン、 権力のイヌだよ‥‥。
English: Remember what Powers said. She's a sucker for authority.

Japanese: しかも、ワレワレのために、 証拠品までくれたッス。
English: She even gave me a piece of valuable evidence!

Japanese: え、ええええッ！
English: Whaat!?

Japanese: な、なんですか？　証拠品って！
English: What kind of evidence!?

Japanese: まあ、その‥‥写真ッス。
English: Well, that, er, photo.

Japanese: あのトノサマン野郎が、現場に 向かっている写真ッス。
English: The photo of the Steel Samurai heading toward the scene of the crime!

Japanese: えーっ！ 誰が撮ったんですか？
English: What! Who took that!?

Japanese: そこのゲートに、 カメラがついてるッスね？
English: See the camera up on that gate, pal?

Japanese: あ。『おいでませ』って 書いてある、あれのことかな？
English: The gate? You mean the one with the welcome sign?

Japanese: そのカメラ、人が通ると、 自動的に写真を撮るッス。
English: Yeah. Whenever someone walks by, that camera automatically snaps a photo of them!

Japanese: ええええっ！
English: Oh no!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくぅん。 もう、ダメだぁ‥‥。
English: Nick, he has evidence! We're finished!

Japanese: （でも、証拠品を手に入れた割には パッとしないカオをしてるな）
English: (Funny, for someone with hard proof he doesn't look too happy.)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 刑事さん。ほら、これ。 弁護士がつけるバッジですよ。
English: Detective, see this? This is my attorney's badge.

Japanese: ハハン。
English: Uh huh?

Japanese: いちいち説明しなくちゃならない 身分証明なんて、悲しいッスね。
English: Don'tcha think it's a little sad when you have to explain what your badge means, pal?

Japanese: 男はだまって警察手帳ッス！
English: Real men show their P.D. badge and that's that!

Japanese: たしかに、このバッジのデザイン、 イミがわからないよな。
English: I'll admit the design of my badge leaves a lot to be desired...

Japanese: いっそ“弁”マークとかにすれば いいのにね。
English: Maybe it should just be a big "L" for "Lawyer"?

Japanese: それもどうかと思うけど。
English: Hmm... I'm not so sure about that.

Japanese: ね。ね。刑事さん。 これ、見てくださいよ！
English: Hey, Detective! Take a look at this!

Japanese: なんスか？
English: Whazzat?

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: Whoa whoa whoa!

Japanese: ダメだよ！ とりあげられちゃうだろ！
English: Don't show him that! He'll confiscate it!

Japanese: うう‥‥。 ジマンしたかったのに。
English: Aww... I just wanted to show it off.

Japanese: あの、刑事さん。 これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Um, Detective. Any ideas about this?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなものをつきつけられても 困るッス。
English: Look, I don't got time to look at every little trinket you got, okay pal?

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 第１スタジオ前
English: October 16 Studio One Entrance

Japanese: 第１撮影スタジオへ通じる入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が死体で 見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: 刑事さんがジャマをして 奥の方は見えない。
English: I can't see anything with Detective Gumshoe in the way.

Japanese: このスタジオに出入りした スタッフの人数を記録するための、
English: An infrared sensor.

Japanese: 赤外線カウンター装置だ。
English: This counts the number of people who have gone in and out of the studio.

Japanese: データは、どこか別の場所で 記録されているのだろう。
English: The data is probably stored someplace else.

Japanese: 装置に、プレートが貼ってある。 《ＳＴ１－ＣＮＴ．》
English: There's a number on the sensor. [ST1-CNT]

Japanese: ずいぶん古いみちしるべだ。
English: It's an old signpost.

Japanese: 《右　‥‥　第１撮影スタジオ 左　‥‥　第２撮影スタジオ》
English: [Right: To Studio One Left:  To Studio Two]

Japanese: あの、あの、刑事さん。
English: Um, Detective?

Japanese: 名前、何でしたっけ。 たしか、ナニのこぎり、とか。
English: What was your name again? Bobby, was it?

Japanese: 自分はイトノコギリ、ッス。
English: Dick Gumshoe, pal.

Japanese: ああそう、イトノコギリ！
English: Right, right! Dick Gumshoe!

Japanese: でも、なんか。そのズータイで イトノコギリ、もないですよね。
English: It's funny your name means what you do! Kind of like naming a custodian "Janitor"!

Japanese: それに、ミツルギ！　っていうと 切れ味が鋭そうだけど、
English: Yeah, just like prosecutor "Edgeworth" sounds like a pretty sharp character.

Japanese: イトノコギリ‥‥。 なんか、ジミだよなあ。
English: Still, I like "Gumshoe." It's got an old-fashioned ring to it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... な、なるほどくん。 また、黙っちゃった。
English: Nick. He's not talking again.

Japanese: こ、今度はぼくが悪いのか？
English: Did... I say something wrong?

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと、気をつけた 方がいいな‥‥）
English: (Got to remember, Detective Gumshoe may have thick shoes, but he's got a thin skin.)

Japanese: 今度の事件も、御剣検事が 担当するんですか？
English: Will Edgeworth be the prosecuting attorney for this case, too?

Japanese: その通りッス。
English: You bet, pal!

Japanese: （うわあ、またアイツと やり合うのか‥‥）
English: (Ugh. Not again.)

Japanese: （なんか、宿命みたいなものを 感じるよ‥‥）
English: (I'm cursed.)

Japanese: そういえば、なるほどくん。
English: Nick, that reminds me.

Japanese: この前の裁判で思ったんだけど、
English: I was wondering something during the last trial.

Japanese: なるほどくんて、 御剣検事と知り合いなの？
English: You know Edgeworth from before, don't you?

Japanese: それッス。 自分も気になっていたッス！
English: Yeah! I was wondering the same thing, pal.

Japanese: え、い、いや、別に‥‥。
English: Well, kind of, not really...

Japanese: アヤシイなあ。 ねえ、刑事さん。
English: He's acting suspicious! Wouldn't you agree, Detective?

Japanese: ことと次第によっては、 タイホもありうるッスよ。
English: Suspicious alright! I feel an arrest coming on!
(I have the right to remain silent!)

Japanese: ねえねえ刑事さん。
English: Say, Detective.

Japanese: その道の先には、 何があるんですか？
English: Do you know what's further down this path?

Japanese: この先は、ダメッス。 自分が通さないッス。
English: I do, but you can't go there. I can't go either, actually.

Japanese: 警察のエラい人たちが、 明日の法廷に向けて、
English: Some of the big wigs from the precinct are plotting.

Japanese: 対策を練ってるッス。
English: They're working out a strategy for the trial tomorrow, see?

Japanese: あの。今、そちらの 捜査は、どんな感じですか？
English: So, how is the police investigation going?

Japanese: そ、そんなコト、 教えるワケにはいかないッス。
English: H-hey, pal! You know I can't tell you that!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: さすがに、そんなにストレートに 聞いてもダメなんじゃないかなあ。
English: You might try being a little less direct about it...

Japanese: でも、これだけは 教えてあげるッス。
English: I will tell you one thing, though.

Japanese: こっちには、決定的な証拠写真が あるッス。
English: We have hard proof! Photographic evidence!

Japanese: 証拠写真？
English: Photographic evidence?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yessir!

Japanese: あのトノサマン野郎が、 現場に向かっている写真ッス。
English: A photo of the Steel Samurai heading toward the scene of the crime!

Japanese: えーっ。 ダレが撮ったんですか？
English: What!? Who took that!?

Japanese: あの警備員からもらったッス。
English: The security lady gave it to me, pal.

Japanese: （うーん、これは、かなり てごわい証拠になりそうだ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. That sounds like pretty solid evidence...)

Japanese: なるほどくん、 それって、そんなに不利なの？
English: Nick, what does that mean for our case?

Japanese: そりゃそうだよ。
English: Well, it's pretty bad, that's for sure!

Japanese: 事件が起こるまでに、現場に行った 人間は、１人しかいないんだ。
English: Only one person went to the crime scene before the murder.

Japanese: それが荷星さんだと立証されたら、 誰が考えたって犯人だよ。
English: If they can prove that was Will Powers... well, that's a pretty convincing case!

Japanese: 荷星さん、そんなこと 一言も言ってなかったのに‥‥。
English: Mr. Powers didn't say anything about going there.

Japanese: あたしたちに、 ウソをついているのかなあ‥‥。
English: Maybe he's lying to us?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなものをつきつけられても 困るッス。
English: Sorry pal, I got no information for you.

Japanese: １０月１６日　某時刻 第１スタジオ内
English: October 16 Studio One
What is it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... なんだよ、急に おとなしくなって。
English: You're real quiet all of a sudden.

Japanese: ‥‥だって。
English: Doesn't it give you the shivers, Nick?

Japanese: あの白いテープ。 なんか、リアル。
English: That white tape! It's so... so real!

Japanese: 怪人アクダイカーンが 死んだんだからね。
English: Well, the Evil Magistrate did die here.

Japanese: トノサマンにやっつけられて。
English: And the Steel Samurai killed him.

Japanese: 凶器はトノサマン・スピアー。 ‥‥そりゃ、リアルだよ。
English: The murder weapon was the Samurai Spear. Sounds pretty real to me...

Japanese: （あっ！　ダメです！ さわらないでください！）
English: Hey! You! No touching that!

Japanese: あ‥‥ごめん。この子、ちょっと アレなもんで。
English: Ah, um, sorry, my partner is kind of, y'know...

Japanese: 何よ“アレ”って。
English: "Y'know"!? No I don't know!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、きみは？
English: Umm... who are you?

Japanese: あ。私、ここの 撮影スタッフです。
