English: That has nothing to do with this!

Japanese: 何度も言うけど、なるほどくん、 まだ新米弁護士なんです。
English: I hate to repeat myself, but Nick here is a newbie lawyer! Fresh off the bar!

Japanese: 負けるときは、ホント、アッと いう間にポックリいきますからね！
English: When he loses, he's going to lose big!

Japanese: は‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: I... I see. *sniff*

Japanese: （ポ‥‥ポックリはないだろう）
English: (Whose side is she on!?)

Japanese: 他に何か、かくしていることは ないでしょうね！
English: You aren't hiding anything else from us, are you!?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ないと思います‥‥。
English: N-no, I sure hope not.

Japanese: スタジオには、ファンの子供は よく入って来るんですか？
English: Do, er, "fanboys" sneak into the studios often?

Japanese: そうでもないですよ。
English: No, not that I know of.

Japanese: あの警備員のオバチャン、 こうるさい人ですから。
English: That security lady's pretty strict with them.

Japanese: でも、今日の証言では、よく ウロウロしている、って‥‥。
English: But, in today's testimony, didn't she say they were often hanging around?

Japanese: 子供たちは、トノサマンが本当に 大好きなんです。
English: The kids really, really love the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: オバチャンの目をぬすんで、 入って来ちゃうんですよね‥‥。
English: They sneak past when she's not looking, I guess.

Japanese: 凶器のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding the murder weapon...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 重いんですか？　あれ。
English: Is it heavy, that spear?

Japanese: けっこう重いですよ。
English: It's pretty hefty, yeah.

Japanese: ふつうの人は、自由にふり回すのは ムリだと思います‥‥。
English: I don't think the average person would be able to swing it around effectively.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、なんか、午前中の 打ち合わせでコワれた、とか‥‥？
English: And... that's the spear that broke or something during the morning run-through?

Japanese: ええ。ボク、回しゲリを シッパイしちゃって‥‥。
English: Oh yes, I goofed on a round kick, you see...

Japanese: スピアーを、 折っちゃったんですよねぇ。
English: I broke the spear.

Japanese: とりあえず、オバチャンが なおしてくれましたけど。
English: The security lady fixed it for me right away though.

Japanese: ガムテープで、ね。
English: With duct tape. We heard.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding this photograph...

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Y-yes...?

Japanese: ほんとにコレ、荷星さんじゃ ないんですか？
English: This really, truly, absolutely isn't you, right?

Japanese: きょ、恐縮ですが‥‥。
English: R-right. *sniff*

Japanese: ボク、楽屋でヒルネを してましたからね‥‥ずっと。
English: I was sleeping in my dressing room the whole time, honest.

Japanese: でも、他には誰もいないじゃ ないですか！
English: But who else could have worn it!?

Japanese: あの、着ぐるみは楽屋に？
English: Your costume was in your dressing room?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ボク、グッスリ寝てましたから、 持っていくことはできたかと‥‥。
English: I guess they snuck in and took it while I was asleep.

Japanese: （でも、いったい誰が‥‥？）
English: (But who!?)

Japanese: このカードキー、荷星さんの 楽屋から‥‥。
English: We found this cardkey in your dressing room.

Japanese: あ。ボクのですそれ。
English: Oh, that's mine, yes.

Japanese: このカード、部外者は 手に入れられるんですか？
English: Can people outside the studios get these cards?

Japanese: ムリだと思いますよ。
English: I don't think so.

Japanese: ボクも、この番組の撮影が 終わったら、
English: Even I have to return the card.

Japanese: そのカードは返さなくちゃ いけません。
English: Once the show's done filming, that is.

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。
English: Huh...

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: E-excuse me.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thank you for showing me that, whatever it is.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I wish I could think of some way I could help you...

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-sorry. *sniff*

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 18 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥荷星さん、取り調べか‥‥。
English: I guess Mr. Powers is in questioning...

Japanese: しかたないわね。
English: Oh well.

Japanese: ‥‥今日のところは、 話を聞くのはあきらめましょう。
English: Let's try again another day, Phoenix.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ヒマなのだろう。 鼻歌をうたっている。
English: He's humming. It must be pretty boring, standing in an empty room like that.

Japanese: １０月１８日　午後２時１６分 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 18, 2:16 PM Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: ‥‥誰もいないよ。
English: No one's here.

Japanese: オバチャン、 つかまっちゃったからね。
English: Right, now that they have Ms. Oldbag in custody.

Japanese: 誰か、かわりの人がいなくて いいのかな‥‥？
English: I guess they don't have anyone else to replace her...

Japanese: あっ、詰所の中に、 おばちゃんのようかんがあるよ！
English: H-hey! In the guard station! Look! She left her donuts!
What? I wasn't going to eat them!

Japanese: この撮影所の正面入口だ。
English: The main entrance to the studios.

Japanese: 今日も、見張りの警官が 立っているはずだ。
English: They probably have a police officer on patrol out there.

Japanese: この撮影所のライトバンだ。
English: Looks like a studio van.

Japanese: 撮影用の資材などを 運んでいるのだろう。
English: They probably use it to carry around film equipment.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャンがむさぼるのだろう。 テーブルの上は、おかしの山だ。
English: Ms. Oldbag must like junk food. There's a mountain of it on the desk in there.

Japanese: この撮影所の見取り図が 描かれている。
English: An overhead map of the studios.

Japanese: ここから右へ行くと 《スタッフ作業場》
English: Right from here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 左へ行くと《スタジオ方面》に 通じているらしい。
English: To the left are the actual film studios.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: ‥‥おや、今日は スイッチが入っていないみたいだ。
English: Huh? It looks like it's been turned off for the day.

Japanese: あ。‥‥どうも。
English: Oh... hello.

Japanese: あはは。やっぱり、そのカッコ じゃ、にあわないね！
English: Ho hoh! You look a little out of place.

Japanese: やっぱり、ダメですか。
English: It's the clothes, isn't it?

Japanese: ミリタリールックのベストを 着ているので、
English: I thought my camo vest might do the trick...

Japanese: “あるいは”と思ったんですけど。
English: Kind of an "alternative guard fashion" thing.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: あ。詰所の中のものには、 手を触れないでください。
English: Oh, please don't touch anything in there.

Japanese: 私、あのオバチャンに ハリ倒されてしまいますから。
English: The security lady would knock the stuffing out of me if she knew.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: あ。詰所の中のものには、 手を触れないでください。
English: Oh, please don't touch anything in there.

Japanese: 私、あのオバチャンに ハリ倒されてしまいますから。
English: The security lady would knock the stuffing out of me if she knew.

Japanese: （コテージのカギを 借りたいんだけど‥‥）
English: (I really want the key to that trailer...)

Japanese: （どうも、今はムリみたいだな）
English: (Maybe next time...)

Japanese: どう、撮影所の方は？
English: So, how are the studios doing?

Japanese: 警察の人がウロウロして、 大変ですよ。
English: There's police wandering around everywhere, it's terrible.

Japanese: “現場保存”とか言って、 そうじもさせてくれないんです。
English: They won't even let me clean up. Don't want me "disturbing evidence."

Japanese: おかげで、事件の日のお昼ご飯の 後かたづけもできなくて。
English: I haven't even cleaned up our lunch plates from the day of the murder!

Japanese: ああ。スタッフエリアにあった、 あのホネの乗った皿？
English: You mean those plates with the steak bones left over on them in the employee area?

Japanese: ええ。 メイワクな話ですよね。
English: Yeah. Can you believe it?

Japanese: ここには、よく子供たちが 入り込んだりするの？
English: Do kids sneak in here a lot?

Japanese: うーん。
English: Well...

Japanese: 数は多くないんですけどね。 よく見かける子はいますよ。
English: I don't think there's that many of them, but I do see one in particular a bunch.

Japanese: カメラなんか持って、 撮影風景をのぞいてるんですよ。
English: He's always gawking at the sets, or snapping pictures.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンも 目を光らせているんですけど、
English: You should see Old Windbag's eyes flash when she sees him.

Japanese: やっぱり、子供の行動力には ちょっと‥‥。
English: She has a bit of trouble catching him though...

Japanese: 今日、 裁判所で聞いたんだけど。
English: I heard something at the trial today.

Japanese: 事件当日、ここにはカントクや プロデューサーもいたって。
English: They said that the director and producer were here the day of the murder...

Japanese: すいません。
English: Oh, sorry...

Japanese: 私は大道具倉庫の方にいたので 見てはいないんですけど、
English: I was in the prop storage room, so I didn't see them.

Japanese: どうも、いたみたいですね。
English: I guess they were here, though.

Japanese: 撮影所長から、口止め されていたので‥‥。
English: The studio head seemed pretty eager to keep us quiet.

Japanese: やっぱり、カントクさんを かばったってワケ？
English: So they were trying to protect the director?

Japanese: カントク、っていうか、 プロデューサーの方かな。
English: More the producer, really.

Japanese: なんか、すごいヤリ手の プロデューサーなんですよ。
English: The producer's our real star here.

Japanese: ほとんどツブれかけてた この撮影所を立て直しちゃって。
English: She saved these studios from the brink of disaster and kept them running.

Japanese: この撮影所にとっては、カミサマ みたいなヒトらしいです。
English: I don't think we'd still be in business if it weren't for that producer!

Japanese: （‥‥プロデューサー、か）
English: (The producer, huh?)

Japanese: あの、これなんだけど‥‥。
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。荷星さん。
English: That's Mr. Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、なんだか足を 引きずってるみたい。
English: Why is he dragging his leg like that?

Japanese: なんですか？　これ。
English: What is this, anyway?

Japanese: （この写真も、足のケガのコトも 知らないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she didn't know about this photo or the injury that morning.)

Japanese: いや。 ‥‥わからなければいいや。
English: Uh... Never mind.

Japanese: へえ。弁護士さんも そのカードキーを？
English: Wow, they gave a lawyer one of those cardkeys?

Japanese: ま、まあ、借りものなんだけど。
English: Well, I'm sort of borrowing it.

Japanese: そうなんですか。
English: I see.

Japanese: なくさないように 気をつけた方がいいですよ。
English: You better be careful not to lose that.

Japanese: ここでは、そのキーの管理、 かなりキビしいですから。
English: They're very strict about those cardkeys here.

Japanese: （部外者がこのカードキーを 手に入れるのはムリみたいだな）
English: (I guess they're pretty good about keeping these out of outsider's hands.)

Japanese: うん。気をつけるよ。
English: Thanks, I'll be careful.

Japanese: 凶器のことだけど‥‥。
English: Say, you work with the props, right? About that murder weapon...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 トノサマン・スピアーですか。
English: You mean the Samurai Spear?

Japanese: あれ、けっこう重いんですよ。
English: Yeah, it's a prop, but it's still pretty heavy.

Japanese: 私、あれを運ぶのキライです。
English: I hate having to carry that thing around.

Japanese: （あれを凶器としてあつかうには、 体力がいるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (So you'd have to be pretty strong to use that as a weapon, it sounds like.)

Japanese: （こう言っちゃなんだけど、 荷星さんだとピッタリだよな）
English: (Why is Mr. Powers the only person I can think of!?)

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 すみません、なんのことか‥‥。
English: Umm... Not really sure what that is. Sorry.

Japanese: アッ！
English: Ah hah!

Japanese: アンタたち！ また来たのかいッ！
English: You again!

Japanese: きゃあ！　出たぁ！
English: Eeeek!

Japanese: 人をオバケみたいに 言うんじゃないョ！
English: How rude, acting like you've seen a ghost!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、ずいぶん早い お帰りでしたね‥‥？
English: You... certainly got back to your post quickly.

Japanese: あれからケーサツに つれていかれてサ。
English: Oh the police took me away, they did.

Japanese: 連中、トノサマンの着ぐるみの スペアを引っぱり出してきて。
English: They pulled out a spare Steel Samurai costume!

Japanese: “着ろ”って言うワケョ。 その着ぐるみを。
English: Told me to "put it on." Can you imagine?

Japanese: そんなもん、着られるワケ ないでしょうが！
English: How could I, a sweet little old lady, wear a giant suit like that?

Japanese: 荷星さん、１８０ｃｍは ありますからねー。
English: Mr. Powers is pretty tall...

Japanese: 着られないってわかったら、 帰っていいって。
English: As soon as they saw there was no way I could wear it, they let me go.

Japanese: （そりゃそうか。‥‥犯人じゃ なくなるからな‥‥）
English: (I guess that would rule out her being the murderer.)

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway!

Japanese: オバチャン、このウラミは 一生わすれないかんね！
English: Know this, whippersnapper! This old lady NEVER forgets a slight or insult!

Japanese: もう、口もきかないんだから！
English: And you won't get any information out of me! My lips are sealed!

Japanese: さんざんしゃべってるクセに。
English: You sure are talking a lot for someone with sealed lips.

Japanese: 今からだよ！ ハイ、ヨーイ、スタート！
English: Starting now! One, two, three, mmmph!

Japanese: （コドモか、このオバチャン‥‥）
English: (This lady's too much...)

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: 勝手にさわっちゃダメだョ！
English: Hey! No touching!

Japanese: （今は、中を調べるのは ムリだな‥‥）
English: (Guess I'd better investigate there later...)

Japanese: あの。オバチャンが見た 子供のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm, about that kid you said you saw...

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハッたおす！
English: If I see him again, I'm taking him down!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ホンキだよ！
English: N-Nick! Look at her eyes! She's serious!

Japanese: 事件当日、ここにいた カントクさんたちのことですけど。
English: About the director, the one who was here on the day of the murder...

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハッたおす！
English: If I see him again, I'm taking him down!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 見さかいがなくなってるよ！
English: Nick! I think she's losing it!

Japanese: （今は、何を見せても ムダだな‥‥）
English: (I have a feeling she's not going to respond well to anything I show her now.)

Japanese: あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: オバチャンがいないよ。
English: Where's that old windbag?

Japanese: 不用心だな‥‥。
English: Odd of her to leave her post...

Japanese: （コラー！）
English: (H-hey!!!)

Japanese: あれ。‥‥オバチャンの声。
English: Nick! That was her!

Japanese: （待たんかコラー‥‥）
English: (S-stop! Whippersnapper!)

Japanese: ははぁ。どうやら、あの子供を 追いかけてるらしいな。
English: Y-yeah. Sounds like she's chasing after that boy.
Natch!

Japanese: あ、転んだ！
English: Uh oh, she tripped!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、 とてもうれしそうだ）
English: (Maya sure looks happy...)

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, hey, Nick.

Japanese: 今のうちに、ちょっと 調べてみようよ、詰所。
English: Now's our chance! Let's check out the guard station!

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Good idea.
Oh!

Japanese: これだよ、なるほどくん。
English: This is it, Nick!

Japanese: “コテージのカギ”って 書いてある！
English: The "Trailer Key"!

Japanese: ああ。あの第２スタジオに あったコテージの‥‥。
English: The key to that trailer in Studio Two!

Japanese: もちろん、借りるよね、 なるほどくん。
English: We'll be borrowing this, right, Nick?

Japanese: 《コテージのカギ》を ポケットにしのばせた。
English: Trailer Key slipped into your pocket.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 18 Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... huff... puff... huff...

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... huff... puff... huff...

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: W-what's wrong?

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥コシャクな！
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... whih... whippersnapper!

Japanese: （どうやら、今まであの子を 追い回していたらしいな‥‥）
English: (She's been chasing that boy this whole time.)

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥今度会ったら ハア‥‥‥‥か、かならず‥‥ハア
English: Huff... puff... when... when I ca... catch him... I... huff

Japanese: （どうやら、つかまえられなかった らしいな‥‥）
English: (Sounds like she hasn't had any luck catching him.)

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥こっちは‥‥ ハア‥‥ひ、人質をとったからネ！
English: Huff... puff... I... I got a hostage now, whippersnapper!

Japanese: （ヒ、ヒトジチって‥‥なんだ？）
English: (A "hostage"...!?)

Japanese: あの。 ‥‥あの子、どうなりました？
English: Umm... what happened to the boy?

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハア、ハア‥‥、 ヒネりつぶす！　ハア、ハア‥‥。
English: I-if I see him a-again, I'll... huff! L-lay him flat! Huff... puff...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。 ワイルドなマダムね。
English: My, what a violent old lady...

Japanese: さっき、カントクたちに 会ったんですけど‥‥。
English: Actually, I met with the director just now...

Japanese: ‥‥うう、シンゾウが イタい‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Eh? Hah... my heart, it don't feel so good.

Japanese: （だ、だいじょうぶかな‥‥）
English: (Is she okay!?)

Japanese: ‥‥死ぬ前に一度‥‥ イブクロちゃんに、お参りを‥‥。
English: B-before I go, I'd like to v-visit the place where poor H-Hammer died...

Japanese: （そういえば、殺人現場に お参りしたいとか言ってたな）
English: (Right... she was saying she wanted to visit the studio where he died.)

Japanese: あの、“ヒトジチ”って なんです？
English: What's this about a "hostage"?

Japanese: ハア、ハア‥‥、 あの子、逃げているときに、
English: Huff... huff... W-when that boy was running away...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥を、落として 行きやがったから、ハア、ハア、
English: ...he dropped this, a-and ran! Huff... huff...

Japanese: きっとコイツを、ハア、ハア、 ‥‥とりもどしに、くるはずョ！
English: H-he'll come back... huff... huff... for this one!

Japanese: （ううん。聞きとりにくい‥‥）
English: (It's kind of hard to understand her with all that huffing and puffing.)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ その“ヒトジチ”‥‥
English: Phoenix! That "hostage" might be what we need!

Japanese: 九太くんから話を聞くのに、 役に立つかもしれないわ。
English: Cody might talk to us if we gave him that!

Japanese: （なるほど‥‥） オバチャン！　その‥‥
English: (Good idea!) Ms. Oldbag! Might I...

Japanese: ダメだョ。
English: No!

Japanese: オバチャン、あの子を つかまえてやるんだから‥‥！
English: I'm catching that brat if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: なるほどくん。何か取り引き できる材料はない？
English: Phoenix. Do you have anything you might trade with her?

Japanese: （取り引き‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (A trade, hmm...)

Japanese: （何かあるかな‥‥？）
English: (I wonder...?)

Japanese: ダレが見たってハア、ハア、 ‥‥ニボシだョ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: I... I told you who I s-saw! Huff! Huff... it... it was Powers!

Japanese: （まだ言ってる‥‥）
English: (Well, at least she's consistent...)

Japanese: オバチャン、これ、借りてます。
English: Ms. Oldbag? I'm borrowing this... just so you know.

Japanese: あとで‥‥ハア、ハア、 返しとくんだョ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Oh... o-okay... huff. J-just be sure to... huff... give it b-back... puff.

Japanese: （つかれきっているから、 ちょっとタイドが弱くなってる）
English: (She's not as demanding when she's exhausted.)

Japanese: あ、それ‥‥ハア、ハア。 第１スタジオに入る‥‥？
English: H-hey... that... huff... That's a card... huff... Studio One?

Japanese: ええ。カードキーです。
English: Right. A cardkey to Studio One.

Japanese: ハア、ハア。オバチャン、 イブクロちゃんにお参りを‥‥
English: Huff... puff... I... I could visit poor Hammer...

Japanese: お参りしたいんだよネ‥‥。 ‥‥ファン、だったから。
English: I'd like to visit... huff... he died... whew. I was his... his fan.

Japanese: オバチャン、カード、 持ってないんですか？
English: You don't have your own card, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: あのへんは、担当じゃないから ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Studio One isn't my turf. Huff... huff...

Japanese: カード、貸しとくれよぉ‥‥。
English: You'll let me borrow... borrow it, then?

Japanese: （どうしよう。あのスタジオ、 入れなくなるぞ‥‥？）
English: (If I give her the cardkey, then I won't be able to get into Studio One...!)

Japanese: すみませんけど、ぼくたちも 調査しなくちゃいけないんで‥‥。
English: Sorry, we have our own investigating to do there.

Japanese: ハア、ハア‥‥。 ‥‥ハア、‥‥はあ。
English: Huff... huff... puff. Poo.

Japanese: （最後の“はあ”は、 タメ息みたい、だな‥‥）
English: (Was that last bit a breath, or a pout...?)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥貸してあげたら？
English: Why don't you let her borrow it, Phoenix?

Japanese: そうですね。 ‥‥はい、じゃあこれ。
English: I guess it can't hurt. Here you go, Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: 《カードキー》を オバチャンに貸した。
English: Cardkey lent to Ms. Oldbag.
Listen to me, sonny.

Japanese: オバチャン、ヒトに借りは 作らないタチでね。
English: I don't like having debts to no whippersnappers.

Japanese: これ、持ってお行き。
English: You take this, and we're even. Deal?

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、コレ。 トノサマンのカード‥‥ですか。
English: What's this...? A Steel Samurai trading card?

Japanese: あの子が落としていったんだョ。
English: That sneaky kid dropped it!

Japanese: アイツにとっちゃあ、 大事なモノなんじゃないかネ。
English: I figure it's pretty important to him, though.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。 ‥‥もらっておきます。
English: Thank you, I may have a use for this.

Japanese: （さっき言ってた“ヒトジチ” って、これのことか‥‥）
English: (This must be the "hostage" she was talking about.)

Japanese: 《トノサマンのカード》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Steel Samurai Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: じゃ。 オバチャン、行くから。
English: Right. I'm off.

Japanese: （オバチャン、スタジオの方へ よろよろと走っていったぞ）
English: (There she goes, hobbling off toward the studio...)

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア
English: Huff... huff... puff... ... huff... puff... haaah!

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥
English: ... Huff... huff... puff... huff... puff... huff... hooo!

Japanese: （‥‥答える気はないみたいだな）
English: (She's too busy catching her breath to tell me anything!)

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 撮影所・スタッフエリア
English: October 18 Global Studios Employee Area

Japanese: あれ。あそこにいるの、 スタッフの子だよ。
English: Look, it's that assistant girl.

Japanese: おーい。
English: Hey!

Japanese: あ。あなたたち、 ニボサブさんの弁護士さん。
English: Hi... WP's lawyers, right?

Japanese: 聞きましたよ！ ダイカツヤクだったそうですね！
English: I heard about the trial! Great job, guys!

Japanese: いやあ、ははは。
English: Oh? Oh hoh hoh. Don't mention it.

Japanese: でも、警備員のオバチャン つかまっちゃったんですって？
English: Is it true they caught the security lady!?

Japanese: い、いやぁ、ははは‥‥。
English: Oh! Oh... oh hoh hoh!

Japanese: さっきオバチャンから 電話があったんです。
English: Actually, she just called me.

Japanese: とりあえず、あの排水口の アナをふさいでおけ、って。
English: She told me to cover up that drain...

Japanese: あ、ああ、あそこの。 （ものすごくブキヨウだな‥‥）
English: You mean that one? (Wow, what a mess...)

Japanese: あ、あの、私‥‥。
English: I... I know...

Japanese: ちょっと、手作業はニガ手 なんですぅ‥‥。
English: I'm not so good with handiwork. Some assistant, right?

Japanese: えー。でも、大道具や小道具の 手入れとか、してるんでしょ？
English: Yeah, but you do work on the props, and the back- drops, right?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: R-right.

Japanese: でも、手入れする前よりも ヒドくなったりすることが‥‥。
English: Just... lots of times they end up looking worse than they did before I fixed them.

Japanese: ま。ま。気にしない気にしない。 ‥‥そんなこともあるって！
English: Oh? Oh! Well, I'm sure these things happen. Nothing to worry yourself about!

Japanese: そうですよね！ 私、気にしません！
English: You're right! I won't!

Japanese: （気にした方がいいと 思うけど‥‥）
English: (Personally, I think she should worry at least a little bit more...)

Japanese: あ、あの。私これから、 オバチャンのかわりに、
English: Um, if you'll excuse me, I have to go to the guard station.

Japanese: 詰所に入らなくちゃ なりませんので、これで‥‥。
English: I'm supposed to fill in for Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: そうなんだ。じゃあね！
English: Right! See you later, then!

Japanese: はい。 では、がんばってください！
English: Good luck with your investigation.

Japanese: “お茶”“みそ汁”“おしるこ” “くず湯”“おでん”‥‥
English: Let's see... cola, candy, chips, gum...

Japanese: ラインナップが 和風に統一されている。
English: Huh. It's so... normal.

Japanese: “ウーロン茶”すらないぞ。
English: I was expecting some cool theme snacks or something. Popcorn, at least!

Japanese: あ。なあに、この “トノサマン汁”って‥‥。
English: W-wait! Look! "Samurai Soda"!

Japanese: なるほどくん。飲んでみてよ。
English: Nick! Try this!

Japanese: 自分で飲めよ。
English: Be my guest.

Japanese: うん。‥‥あ、ザンネン。 売り切れだ。
English: Okay! Oh, too bad, it's sold out.

Japanese: 楽屋の入口に、 張り紙がしてある。
English: There's a sign on the dressing room door.

Japanese: “荷星　三郎様”か。 ‥‥さすが、主役だな。
English: "Mr. Will Powers"... it must be neat to be the star and have your own room like this.

Japanese: 楽屋に入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていないみたいだ。
English: The door to the dressing room. It's not locked.

Japanese: ねーねー、なるほどくん。 今日も、なんかもらっていこうよ。
English: Hey, Nick! Let's go in! Maybe there'll be something else to steal?

Japanese: ひ、人聞きが悪いな！
English: H-hey! We're not thieves!

Japanese: だって昨日は、カードキー もらったじゃない。
English: But we did take that cardkey...

Japanese: 借りたの、あれは！
English: W-we borrowed that! There's a difference.

Japanese: 楽屋とそとをむすぶ窓だ。
English: A window into the dressing room.

Japanese: くもりガラスなので、 部屋の中を見ることはできない。
English: The glass is frosted, so I can't see inside.

Japanese: 巨大な倉庫だ。
English: That must be the storage room.

Japanese: 撮影用の大道具が しまってあるようだ。
English: That's where they keep all the film equipment.

Japanese: すごーい、なるほどくん。 なんでも知ってるんだねー！
English: Wow, Nick! You know a lot about the industry!

Japanese: 見た感じを言ってみただけだよ。
English: Umm, what else would they keep in a film studio?

Japanese: モーターを乗せた、 電動自転車だ。
English: Looks like one of those electric-powered bicycles.

Japanese: なんともいえない中途ハンパな 感じが、ぼくはキライだ。
English: I dunno about these. I mean, what's the point?

Japanese: えー。でも、坂をのぼるの ラクだよ。
English: It makes it a lot easier to go up hills!

Japanese: 若いのに、あんなもの使うな！
English: Don't people cycle to get exercise!?

Japanese: 今日の裁判で言ってた 小学生の男の子って、
English: So, the fanboy they were talking about in the trial today...

Japanese: この排水口から 入って来たんだよね？
English: He came in through that drain?

Japanese: ‥‥らしいなあ。 大あわてでふさいだ、ってワケか。
English: So it seems. I guess they covered it up in a hurry.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey... Hey, Nick!

Japanese: あのアナをふさがれちゃったら、 その子、もう入れないんだよね。
English: If that drain's covered, the boy won't be able to get in.

Japanese: まあ、そのために ふさいだんだからなあ。
English: Uh, yeah. I think that was the idea...?

Japanese: なんかその子、カワイソウだよね。
English: I feel kinda sorry for him, though. Don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、また ぶっコワしておこうか？
English: What, you want to rip the grate off?

Japanese: え！　いいの！ なるほどくん！
English: Really, Nick? We can!?

Japanese: （‥‥げ。ホンキだ。 どうしよう‥‥）
English: (Whoa... she's serious? Uh oh...)

Japanese: やめておこうよ。 ‥‥せっかく修理したんだし、
English: Nah, I think we should leave it on. They only just repaired it.

Japanese: コワしちゃったら、あの子が オバチャンにしかられる。
English: And if we take it off, that assistant will get yelled at by you-know-who.

Japanese: ‥‥う、ううん‥‥。

Japanese: （なにが“ちょっと”だよ‥‥）
誰かが寝たあとが 残っている。
English: Looks like someone was sleeping here.

Japanese: スタッフからの差し入れらしい おかしがならんでいる。

Japanese: あっ。なるほどくん！
English: H-hey! Nick!

Japanese: ど、どうした？

Japanese: 賞味期限、切れかけてたし（食）。

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （目に涙をためて、クチビルを ふるわせて、ぼくをニラんでる）

English: She's a genius. M4d sk1llz, all the way. Scary, though. *sweats*

Japanese: 事件があった日は、いっしょに 会議をしていたんですよね。
English: You had a meeting with her on the day of the murder, right?

Japanese: 正午から４時まで、 ずっとイッショだったよ。
English: Yeah, we were together from noon to 4:00 PM, the whole time.

Japanese: あの、事件当日のこと、 聞かせてほしいんですけど。

Japanese: ああ。キミかぁ。オバチャンから、 ボクらのことを聞き出したの。
English: Oh, I know who j00 d00dz are. That security lady told j00 about us, eh?

Japanese: ゲッチュウ！　そのとおり！

Japanese: （イブクロさんが死んだのは、 午後２時３０分）
English: (Mr. Hammer's time of death was estimated at 2:30 PM.)

Japanese: （今の話が本当なら、 アリバイ成立だな‥‥）
English: (If he's telling the truth, that meeting gives him an alibi...)

Japanese: “おエラいさん”というのは、 どういう人たちですか？
English: Who exactly are these "bigwigs" people keep mentioning?

Japanese: 制作局の部長やスポンサー、 それにプロダクションの人かな。
English: Oh, the boss over at the network, and some sponsors. Also a few production guys.

Japanese: 彼らはみんなでリムジンに乗って、 正午前にここへ来たんだ。
English: They piled into a limousine and got here right around noon.

Japanese: キンチョーしたよなぁ。（汗）
English: Major tension! *sweats*

Japanese: その人たちとは、 ずっと一緒だったんですか？
English: Were all of them with you the entire time?

Japanese: まあね。
English: Yeah, unfortunately.

Japanese: みんな白髪まじりの ジイさん連中だよ。（苦）
English: They're all gray-haired geezers... *scowl*

Japanese: （うーん、信用できる 証人たちみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... sounds like they'd all be reliable witnesses.)
T-this is WP, right?

Japanese: なんでわかるんですか？
English: How do you know?

Japanese: だって、ホラ。 足を引きずってるだろ。
English: Well, look, he's obviously dragging his foot.

Japanese: （そうか。このカントクは、 荷星さんのケガ、知ってるんだ）
English: (Oh, right. The director would know about the injury; he was there.)

Japanese: ああ、そうだ。プロデューサーから あずかったんですけど。
English: Here. I got this from the producer...

Japanese: え‥‥“１３話の台本を 持ってこい”‥‥？
English: Huh? "Bring the script for Episode 13"?

Japanese: そういえば、どこへ やったかなあ‥‥。
English: Episode 13... where did I put that one?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... どこかで忘れてきた みたいだよ。（汗）
English: I must have left it somewhere... *sweats*

Japanese: ど、どうしよう‥‥。 また、ドナられる‥‥。（震）
English: U-uh oh... My ass is p0wned if I don't find it... *shakes*

Japanese: カントクさんが行きそうな ところ、探した方が早いよ。
English: It might be quicker to just look in all the places where he's likely to have been.

Japanese: ‥‥そうみたいだね。
English: I agree.

Japanese: 見つけてきましたよ。台本。
English: We found your script.

Japanese: あ。ありがとお！
English: W00t! THX!

Japanese: で、でもさ。
English: S-still... I dunno...

Japanese: 悪いけど、キミたち、持って いってくれないかな‥‥。（拝）

English: I try not to pay much attention to things that don't interest me. LOL.

Japanese: な、なんでそこで こっちを見るんですか‥‥。
English: W-why are you staring at me like that...?

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 撮影所・楽屋
English: October 18 Global Studios Dressing Room

Japanese: たしか、ここに逃げ込んだ はずだけど‥‥。
English: He's sure to be in here somewhere...

Japanese: あっ！　いた！
English: Found you!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! まずい、逃げられる！
English: Dammit! He's getting away!

Japanese: お待ちなさい。
English: Come back!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥よかった。もどってきたわ。
English: There. He's back.

Japanese: （ウソだろ！）
English: (How did she...!?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... ねえ。私たちに 協力してもらえないかな。
English: Hey there. Would you mind helping us out?

Japanese: ね？
English: Please?
I-I'm Cody.

Japanese: あら。 ‥‥綾里　千尋よ。
English: Hello, Cody. I'm Mia. Mia Fey.

Japanese: よろしくね。
English: Nice to meet you.

Japanese: よっ、よろしくっ！
English: Yo.

Japanese: ぼくは、成歩堂　龍一だよ。
English: And I'm Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 聞いてねえよ。
English: Who asked?

Japanese: じゃあ、なるほどくん。 あとは、おねがいします。
English: Phoenix, you can take it from here.

Japanese: （自信ないなあ‥‥）
English: (I'm not so sure I'm qualified...)

Japanese: スタッフからの差し入れらしい おかしのハコと、お茶。
English: There's a box of snacks on the table, and some cans of soda.

Japanese: おかしのハコは、カラッポだ。
English: They're all empty.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、この場に いなくてよかったな‥‥。
English: I'm glad Maya isn't here to see this. She'd be devastated.

Japanese: 真宵がどうかした？
English: Sorry? What was that about Maya?

Japanese: い、いいえ。
English: O-oh, nothing.

Japanese: 荷星さんのカバンだ。
English: Mr. Powers's bag.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 勝手に開けちゃダメよ。
English: Now, Phoenix, it's not polite to look through other people's belongings.

Japanese: 開けませんよ！ （真宵ちゃんに言ってよ‥‥）
English: H-hey, don't look at me! (Tell that to your sister!)

Japanese: メイク用の小道具なんかが ならんでいる。
English: There's an array of cosmetics here.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんによると、カツラは ある番組で使われているもので、
English: According to Maya, that wig was used in some old TV show...

Japanese: “ちょんまげブギ”も 知らねぇのかよ！
English: You don't know "Samurai Boogey-Woogey"!?

Japanese: 超ぉダッセェな！
English: You're even lamer than I thought!

Japanese: （そんな人気番組だったのか！）
English: (Why does everybody but me know about this show!?)

Japanese: なあ、きみは事件について 何を知っているんだ？
English: So, do you know anything about what happened here?

Japanese: おニイさんに、教えてくれよ。
English: Could you tell me, please?

Japanese: ‥‥なんにも知らねぇよ！
English: I don't know nothing!

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ。 何か、エサがいるな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... looks like a little persuasion's necessary. Maybe a bribe...?)

Japanese: トノサマン、かっこいいよな。 ぼくも好きだな、トノサマン。
English: The Steel Samurai's so cool. I think he's the, um, the "bomb."

Japanese: あんたにトノサマンの 何がわかるって言うんだよ！
English: Hah! What would a lamer like you know about the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: な、なんだよその言い方は！
English: H-hey, watch that attitude!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん。 少年を相手にドナっちゃダメよ。
English: Phoenix, you really shouldn't yell like that. He's only a kid.

Japanese: そうだよ！ 少年相手にドナるなよな！
English: Yeah! Don't yell like that I'm only a kid!

Japanese: きみは、事件があった日も ここに来ていたよね。
English: You were here on the day of the... incident, right?

Japanese: ‥‥う、うん。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: そのとき、何か見なかったかい？
English: Did you see anything unusual?
Cody.

Japanese: ！ ! これは、正義のための質問なの。
English: Cody, he needs to know because he's fighting for justice.

Japanese: トノサマンは、いつでも 正義の味方でしょう？
English: Isn't that the Steel Samurai's motto? "For great justice"?

Japanese: あなたも、そうでなければダメよ。
English: You have to help us fight for great justice, too.

Japanese: オ、オレ。 ‥‥オレ、見たんだ！
English: I... I saw...

Japanese: ゼンブ、見てたんだよっ！
English: I saw everything!

Japanese: （ううん‥‥コイツからは、まだ 聞き出せることがありそうだな）
English: (Well, well... We might get some useful information out of him yet.)

Japanese: トノサマン、好きなんだ。
English: So... you like the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: だって、カッコイイもん！
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうかなあ。
English: I... I guess.

Japanese: ニイちゃん。外見じゃないんだよ。 生きザマがカッコイイんだよ！
English: You're judging him by his looks! You have to look at his actions, at his life!

Japanese: （小学生が生きザマを 語るなよ‥‥）
English: (I'm being lectured on life by a grade schooler...)

Japanese: ニイちゃんも好きだろ？ トノサマン。
English: You like him too, don'tcha?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥大好きだよ。
English: Uh... Oh, me? Y-yeah! I, um, love the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: なあ、どこがイイ？ トノサマン。
English: So, tell me what your favorite thing about the Steel Samurai is!

Japanese: そ、そうだな‥‥。 （テキトーに答えるしかない！）
English: R-right... (C'mon, think of something!)

Japanese: 人情にアツい、その人がらに ホレちゃうかな。
English: I really like his, um, his kind nature. What a guy!

Japanese: だよな！　だよな！
English: Totally!

Japanese: ああ見えて、意外とヤサシイ ところがあるんだよ！
English: He looks so tough, but inside, he's as gentle as a kitten!

Japanese: うんうん。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: なあ、なあ！ 他にもあるだろ！　イイところ！
English: So, what else do you like about him?

Japanese: （‥‥うええ‥‥ まだあるのかよ‥‥）
English: (Don't tell me I have to keep this act up...)

Japanese: え、えーと‥‥。 （トノサマンなんて、知らないぞ）
English: W-well... (I've never even seen a full episode of that dumb show.)

Japanese: ぼ、ぼく、荷星さんの ファン、だから‥‥。
English: A-actually, I'm kind of a fan of Will Powers...

Japanese: うわぁ！　ニイちゃん、 マニアックだなあ！
English: Whoa! That's totally hard-core!

Japanese: （きみに言われたくないなあ‥‥）
English: (You're calling ME hard-core?)

Japanese: （‥‥テキトーに答える しか、ないな‥‥）
English: (Got to think of something...)

Japanese: テキをやっつける瞬間かな。 ‥‥スカッとするし。
English: I like it when he, uh, he vanquishes his foes!

Japanese: やっぱ、そうだよな！
English: Yeah! Whizzam!

Japanese: トノサマン、ゼッタイに 負けないもんな！
English: The Steel Samurai always wins! Always!

Japanese: 見てくれよ！　このアルバム！
English: Check this out! It's my fan album!

Japanese: な、なんだこれ。 ‥‥トノサマンの写真ばっかり。
English: What the...? It's all pictures of the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: オレ、トノサマンのショーには、 かならず行くんだよ！
English: I go to every live performance!

Japanese: （ああ‥‥。デパートの屋上や 遊園地でやってるやつか‥‥）
English: (Those stupid publicity stunts where they beat each other up in public...?)

Japanese: でさ。トノサマンが勝った瞬間を、 こうして写真に撮るのさ！
English: I always take a picture when the Steel Samurai lands the final blow! Whizzam!

Japanese: １つ残らず、ぜーんぶ写真に 撮ってるんだぜ。‥‥カンペキに！
English: I got 'em all! I never missed one! A perfect collection!

Japanese: 見てくれよ！　これ！ 新しいデジカメだよ！
English: Check it out! My new digital camera!

Japanese: あら。カッコイイわね。 最新のモデルじゃない。
English: Wow, that's very impressive. The newest model, isn't it?

Japanese: だろ！　だろ！ 買ってもらったばっかりなんだぜ！
English: You bet, lady! I just got it for my birthday.

Japanese: このアルバムのタイトルは、 “トノサマン・栄光の足跡”さ。
English: My album has a name, wanna hear it? "The Steel Samurai: Path To Glory."

Japanese: トノサマンは、どんなときでも、 ゼッタイに勝つんだよ！
English: The Steel Samurai always wins! Always!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、よかったらコレ、 あげるよ！
English: Hey, if you want this, you can have it, lady.

Japanese: え？　いいの？
English: Really? Are you sure?

Japanese: うん。これ、デジタルカメラで 撮ったからさあ。
English: Yeah, I took these with a digital camera.

Japanese: データ、残ってるし。 また作れるから。
English: I got all the data at home so I can always make another one.

Japanese: ふふ。じゃ、 もらっちゃおうかな。
English: Well, then I'm happy to accept! Thank you.

Japanese: 《“栄光の足跡”》をもらった。
English: "Path To Glory" received.

Japanese: （千尋さん、はたらかなくても 生きていけそうだよな‥‥）
English: (What's with people always giving stuff to Mia? What a life!)

Japanese: ？ ? トノサマン、好きなんだ。
English: So... you like the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: だって、強くてカッコイイもん！
English: Well, yeah! Duh! He's so strong!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうかなあ。
English: I... I guess.

Japanese: ニイちゃん。外見じゃないんだよ。 生きザマがカッコイイんだよ！
English: You're judging him by his looks! You have to look at his actions, at his life!

Japanese: （小学生が生きザマを 語るなよ‥‥）
English: (I'm being lectured on life by a grade schooler...)

Japanese: 九太くん‥‥聞かせてくれ。
English: Cody... I need to ask you something very important.

Japanese: 事件があった日、 きみは、何を見たのか‥‥？
English: What did you see on the day of the incident?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... あの日、ここに着いたのは ２時ぐらいだった。
English: I got here that day around 2:00...

Japanese: オバチャンに見られないように 道のそとの林の中を歩いていたら、
English: I had to come in through the woods out back so that old lady wouldn't see me.

Japanese: すっかり迷っちゃったんだ。 ３０分ぐらいかな‥‥？
English: I got kinda lost, though. I was in there for maybe a half hour or so.

Japanese: ‥‥で、やっとスタジオの前に 出たんだよ。
English: Then I finally got to the studio.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥、
English: After that...

Japanese: どうしたの、九太くん？
English: What is it, Cody?

Japanese: トノサマンが、悪いヤツを やっつけたんだよ！
English: Th-the Steel Samurai killed the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマン・スピアーでさ！ いつものように、イチゲキだよ！
English: He used his Samurai Spear! Just like always! One shot, one kill!

Japanese: すごい迫力だった‥‥。 オレ、コワくなっちゃって。
English: It happened so fast... I got scared.

Japanese: あの日は、それで帰ったんだ。
English: I went home after that.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうなんだ。 それはタイヘンだったねえ。
English: I... I see. It must have been hard for you.

Japanese: あんた、コトバに ココロがこもってないなあ。
English: Man, you say those nice things but it's so obvious you aren't speaking from the heart.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん。
English: (Umm... right.) Mia?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: これってつまり、トノサマンが 殺人犯ということですよね。
English: I think we can say for sure this means the Steel Samurai did it.

Japanese: そうね。
English: True enough.

Japanese: そして、カントクたちはアリバイが あるから、トノサマンじゃない。
English: And the director has an alibi, so it wasn't him.

Japanese: そのとおりよ。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥すると、犯人は、 荷星さんしかいないことになる！
English: So... the only person left who it could have been is Will Powers!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 予想外のことだけど、
English: I have to admit, it's kind of a shock.

Japanese: この子を法廷で証言させたら、 依頼人はカクジツに有罪ね。
English: But if you put this boy on the witness stand, your client will be "guilty" for sure.

Japanese: ‥‥法廷に呼ぶの、やめましょう。
English: Let's not call him then...

Japanese: そうね。 そっとしておきましょう。
English: I think that would be wise. Best to leave this one alone.

Japanese: そうは行かないッス！
English: Not so fast, pal!

Japanese: 今の話、聞かせてもらったッス！
English: I heard everything!

Japanese: この少年は、重要な証人ッス。
English: This boy here's a key witness!

Japanese: ワレワレが、手あつく ホゴするッス！
English: And he's under police protection, starting now!

Japanese: さあ少年！
English: C'mon, son.

Japanese: 刑事さんと、警察署まで ランデブーッス！
English: You've got a rendezvous with me down at the precinct!

Japanese: や、やだよ！ ‥‥うわっ！
English: No! I ain't going! Waah!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... こ、こまったわね。
English: That didn't go so well.

Japanese: 手がかりは、これで ゼロになっちゃったわ。
English: We're back to zero leads.

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクな形で。
English: And now we have a serious handicap in court.

Japanese: ど、どうするんですか！ 千尋さん！
English: W-what're we going to do, Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥え、えーと。
English: Well, hmm...

Japanese: 私、そろそろ帰らないと。
English: I... really need to be going.

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。 なんとかなりますから。
English: Don't worry, Phoenix. I'm sure you'll be fine!

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: Whoa whoa whoa!

Japanese: 明日、法廷、来てくださいよ！ ‥‥自信、ないから‥‥。
English: C-come to the trial tomorrow, please! I need you there!

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥１つ聞かせて。
English: Phoenix, can I ask you something?

Japanese: あなたは、荷星さんの 無実を信じているの？
English: Do you really believe that Mr. Powers is innocent?

Japanese: あたりまえじゃないですか！
English: Of course he is!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... そのヒトコトが聞きたかったの。
English: That's all I wanted to hear.

Japanese: いいわ。 ‥‥あした、法廷でね。
English: Excellent. I'll see you in court, then.

Japanese: （ここまで荷星さんに不利な 状況になると‥‥）
English: (I never expected things to go this badly for Powers's case...)

Japanese: （自信が なくなってくるよなあ‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure we can win this one.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix?

Japanese: あなたが信じなくて、 誰が信じられるの！
English: If you don't believe him, who will!?

Japanese: （ホンキでおこられてしまった）
English: (Uh oh, she's mad.)

Japanese: まあ、いいわ。 明日、法廷で会いましょう。
English: Oh well... I'll see you in court tomorrow then.

Japanese: はい！ ‥‥よろしくおねがいします。
English: Great! Thanks, Mia!

Japanese: よろしくね。
English: See you soon.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 九太くん。‥‥これ。
English: Cody... This look familiar?

Japanese: あっ！　オレのトレカ！
English: Hey! My UR!

Japanese: と‥‥“とれか”？
English: "You are"... huh?

Japanese: “トレーディングカード” のことだよ！
English: "U.R." You know, "ultra rare"! That card's really hard to get!

Japanese: オトナのクセに、 なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: これ、あげるからさ。 話、聞かせてよ。
English: I'll give you this... IF you help me.

Japanese: それ、もともとオレの じゃねえか！
English: But that's my card! By offering me something I already own, you're in effect

Japanese: シホン主義社会のキホンは 等価交換だぜ！
English: eschewing the very basis of our consumer society, namely the principle of fair trade!

Japanese: オトナのクセに、 なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: （うぐ。‥‥こムズカシイこと 言いやがって‥‥）
English: (W-what do they teach these kids in school these days!? Quantum physics!?)

Japanese: だいいちさぁ、 ‥‥いらねえよ、そんなカード。
English: Whatever, I don't need that card.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そのカードさ、 ダブって持ってるんだ。
English: I got a double.

Japanese: あんたにくれてやるよ。
English: Just keep it. It's yours.

Japanese: （おいおい、話が違うぜ‥‥）
English: (What!? After I went through all that trouble...)

Japanese: オレと取り引きしたけりゃ、 もっとレアなヤツを持って来な！
English: If you want to trade, find me a REALLY rare card!

Japanese: れあ？ ‥‥焼きカゲンのコトか‥‥？
English: "Really rare"!? You mean there's something rarer than "Ultra Rare"!?

Japanese: オトナのクセに、 なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Ultra Rare Premium cards are way rarer than plain Ultra Rare cards! Come ON!

Japanese: レアカード。めずらしいカードの コトだよ！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: レアカード、ねえ‥‥。
English: Premium... rare... why do I feel like we're talking about steaks?

Japanese: なあ、このカード‥‥。
English: See this card?

Japanese: あ、ああッ！　そのカード！
English: Yeah, so... Wait! That's it!!!

Japanese: オレの持ってない、最後の レアカードだ！
English: That's the last Ultra Rare Premium card I need!

Japanese: な、なあ、おい。クレ！ クレよぉぉ！
English: C'mon give it to me, please! You gotta give it to me!

Japanese: （な、なんだ？ このクイツキのよさは‥‥）
English: (Wow, I've never seen him so... eager.)

Japanese: よ、よおし。おニイさんと トリヒキをしないか？
English: R-right! Okay! How about a trade?

Japanese: いいぜ！
English: You're on!

Japanese: “トノサマン・スピアー”に “アクダイカーン”もつけるよ！
English: I'll give you a "Samurai Spear" and throw in an "Evil Magistrate" to boot!!

Japanese: い、いやいやいやいや。
English: No no no no... not that kind of trade.

Japanese: ‥‥カードはいいんだ。 質問に答えてくれよ。
English: I don't want cards. I want information.

Japanese: なーんだ、そんなことか。 いいよ。何でも聞いてよ。
English: Huh? You sure? Fine by me!

Japanese: 《レアカード》を 九太くんにあげた。
English: Gave URP Card to Cody.

Japanese: さあ。なるほどくん。 お話を聞きましょう。
English: Okay, Phoenix. Let's hear what he has to say.

Japanese: こちらに有利なことがあったら、 明日の法廷に来てもらうんです。
English: If he knows something that could help us, we can have him take the stand tomorrow.

Japanese: あっ。トノサマン。
English: Hey, it's the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: なんか、足を引きずってる みたいだケド‥‥。
English: Huh? Why's he dragging his foot like that?

Japanese: これがどうかしたかい？
English: Where'd you get this, anyway?

Japanese: カッコイイよな。 トノサマン・スピアー。
English: The Samurai Spear! That's totally cool.

Japanese: オレもほしいなー。
English: I wish I had one, too...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんと 話が合いそうだな‥‥）
English: (This kid and Maya would get along great.)

Japanese: ありがとな。コレ。
English: Thanks for this.

Japanese: それ、おねーちゃんに あげたんだぞ！
English: Hey, I gave that to the nice lady, not you, loser!

Japanese: あんたがカッテに 見るなよな！
English: No looking at that, okay!

Japanese: （かわいくねえよお‥‥）
English: (Believe me, I wasn't going to...)

Japanese: なんだよそれ。
English: What's that? Bo-ring!

Japanese: つまんないモノ、見せるなよな！
English: Man, how can one person be so lame!? If you were a superhero you'd be Lame-o-man!

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第１スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio One Entrance

Japanese: あれっ。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: 今日は、イトノコ刑事さん、 いないね。
English: It looks like Detective Gumshoe isn't here today.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: You're right.

Japanese: 今日の裁判、大騒ぎだったから、 きっと忙しいんだろう。
English: He's probably up to his neck in paperwork after the commotion at today's trial.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: So, Nick...

Japanese: 今日の裁判で言ってた “第２スタジオ”って、
English: Remember that "Studio Two" we heard about at the trial today?

Japanese: あの、木が倒れてる 道の方に行くとあるんだよね。
English: It was down that path with the fallen tree, right?

Japanese: ああ。そんなこと 言ってたっけね。
English: Yeah, I think that's what they said.

Japanese: カントクさんたち、 今日もいるかもしれないよ。
English: Maybe the director and the rest of them are there today?

Japanese: 今がチャンス！ ‥‥行ってみようよ！
English: Now's our chance, Nick! Let's check it out!

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ通じる 入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が 死体で見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: 裁判では、この奥には “第２スタジオ”があるという。
English: In the trial, they said Studio Two was down this path.

Japanese: サルのクビがジャマだけど、 道はなんとか、とおれそうだ。
English: The monkey head is in the way, but we should be able to get by it without a problem.

Japanese: うわあ。このサルのクビ、 ものすごく重いよ。
English: This monkey head is real heavy!

Japanese: そりゃ、そこの木を折っちゃう ぐらいだからなあ。
English: Well, it did knock that tree over when it fell.

Japanese: 事件があった日に折れたんだよね。 おサルさんのクビと、この木‥‥。
English: Both of these, the monkey head and the tree, fell the day of the murder, didn't they.

Japanese: ああ。イトノコ刑事が そんなこと言ってたね。
English: Yeah, that's what Detective Gumshoe said.

Japanese: この撮影スタジオの マスコット・キャラクターだ。
English: The studio mascot.

Japanese: なんでも、“テングザル”を モチーフにしてるとか。
English: Apparently it's a monkey of some kind.

Japanese: でも、テングって、こんな ダンゴっ鼻なのかなあ？
English: I've never seen a monkey with a nose like that before!

Japanese: 早くクビ、なおせばいいのに。
English: Why don't they put his head back on?

Japanese: あのスタッフの子に たのんでみようか。
English: Maybe we should ask that assistant?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリじゃないか、 あの子じゃ。いろんな意味で。
English: I'm not sure we really want her fixing anything.

Japanese: あのクビ、クレーンでも使わなきゃ ビクともしそうにないし。
English: And you'd probably need a crane to move that thing.

Japanese: たしかにねー。
English: Probably so.

Japanese: このゲートをとおった人間を 自動的に撮影するカメラだ。
English: That's the security camera that automatically takes pictures of people here.

Japanese: 事件当日は、被害者が殺害される までの時間に、２人撮影した。
English: It took two pictures on the day of the murder, before the actual time of death.

Japanese: １人は、トノサマン。 もう１人は、小学生の少年。
English: One of them was the Steel Samurai. The other was a grade school boy.

Japanese: どうやら今日は 作動してないみたいだ。
English: It doesn't seem to be switched on today.

Japanese: ちょっとつかれたな。 ‥‥座って休もうか。
English: I'm bushed. Maybe I'll sit down here for a bit.

Japanese: あーあ。これだから トシはとりたくないよね。
English: This is exactly why I don't want to get old, ever.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくはまだ ワカいぞ！
English: H-hey! I'm still young! I'm just tired!

Japanese: なるほどくん。“若さ”を 主張しだしたら、オシマイだよ。
English: Nick, when you start having to say "I'm still young," you know it's all over.

Japanese: （いやいや、ほんとうに 若いんだってば！）
English: (But... but I AM still young!)

Japanese: 『おいでませ』だって。
English: It says "Welcome."

Japanese: 休みの日は、一般の人たちが 遊びにくるからね。
English: They let people in here when they're not filming, right?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 今度、遊びにこようか。
English: Nick! Let's come here some day! Whaddya say?

Japanese: あのオバチャンが 入れてくれたら、ね。
English: Sure, if that security lady lets us in.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... あのサルマゲどん。
English: That's "Mr. Monkey."

Japanese: 元気なときは、時間を アナウンスするの。
English: When it wasn't broken, it announced the time... in "ooks."

Japanese: “４時でごザル”とか。
English: One "ook" per hour. Ook ook ook ook. Always with the ooking.

Japanese: （サルだからなあ‥‥）
English: (It IS a monkey, after all.)

Japanese: あのクビを調べてみなさい。
English: Check its head.

Japanese: クビがこわれた時間で、 時計が止まっているわ。
English: The clock inside stopped when it broke.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! なるほどくん。 時計、２時１５分で止まってるよ。
English: Nick, it's stopped at 2:15.
That's right.

Japanese: この道は、２時１５分から ４時すぎまでふさがっていたの。
English: This path was blocked from 2:15 till after 4:00.

Japanese: だから、アタクシたちは、 無実。
English: Therefore, we're innocent.

Japanese: イブクロさんが第１スタジオで 死んだのは、２時３０分‥‥か。
English: Mr. Hammer died in Studio One at 2:30...

Japanese: じゃ、そういうことだから。
English: See? Good bye.

Japanese: 《サルマゲどんのクビ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Mr. Monkey data added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥どうなってるの‥‥？
English: What do we do, Nick?

Japanese: ニボサブさん以外、イブクロさんを 殺せた人、いないじゃない‥‥。
English: There isn't anyone besides WP who could have killed Hammer!

Japanese: もう‥‥だめだあ‥‥。
English: It's over... We're finished!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (It sure doesn't look good.)

Japanese: （一度、事務所へもどって 考えてみるか‥‥）
English: (Guess we should head back to the office and plan our strategy...)

Japanese: この道は“第２スタジオ”に 通じている。
English: This is the path that goes to Studio Two.

Japanese: 事件当日は、午後２時すぎから ４時まで、
English: The mascot's head fell right on the path on the day of the murder.

Japanese: このサルのクビが道を しゃ断していたらしい。
English: The path was completely blocked between 2:00 and 4:00.

Japanese: たしかに、アタマの中には 時計とメガホンがセットされてる。
English: I see. There's a clock and a speaker in the monkey's head.

Japanese: いつもなら《３時でごザル》とか 言ってるのだろう。
English: It would usually tell the time by "ook"ing.

Japanese: 今、時計は２時１５分で 止まっている。
English: The clock is stopped at 2:15 now.

Japanese: ひとやすみしたいところだが、 そんな場合でもないだろう。
English: I really want to take a break, but we have work to do!

Japanese: しかし、誰もいないベンチが ならんでいる図っていうのは、
English: Still, it's kind of sad to see so many empty benches...

Japanese: なんかこう、サビしいな‥‥。
English: Kind of like if you threw a party, and nobody came.

Japanese: “おいでませ”‥‥か。
English: "Welcome"...

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、 ここも人があふれるのかな。
English: I wonder if the murder will make this place famous, too.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第１スタジオ内
English: October 18 Studio One

Japanese: ここ、あんまり来たくないな。
English: I... don't really want to be in here.

Japanese: まあ‥‥。 そりゃ、殺人現場だし。
English: Yeah... It is a murder scene.

Japanese: あたし、他のところに行きたい。
English: I want to go someplace else. Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。
“書き割り”っていうやつだ。
English: Looks like a backdrop for a stage.

Japanese: これ、トノサマンで使う 背景かな。
English: This must be used in the Steel Samurai show.

Japanese: そうだよ。“ネオ・フジヤマ” っていう山だね。
English: Of course! That's the mountain, Neo Fujiyama!

Japanese: トノサマンって、江戸の話だろ。 なんで富士山が見えるんだ？
English: Doesn't the Steel Samurai take place in Olde Tokyo? Can you even see Mt. Fuji from there?

Japanese: 江戸じゃなくて、 “ネオ・エドシティ”だからね。
English: Not Olde Tokyo, Nick! "Neo" Olde Tokyo!

Japanese: うわー。すごいなあ。 高そうなカメラ。
English: Wow, look at that camera! That must cost a ton!

Japanese: お、おい。さわるなって。
English: Yeah! So don't touch it!

Japanese: なるほどくん、 撮影してあげようか。
English: Hey, smile, Nick, you're a star!

Japanese: たのむからやめてくれ！
English: S-stop that!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志の死体の形が、 ロープでマーキングされている。
English: They outlined where Jack Hammer's body lay with tape.

Japanese: 死体は怪人アクダイカーンの 着ぐるみの中から発見されたため、
English: His body was found still inside the Evil Magistrate costume.

Japanese: その形どおりにマーキング されている。
English: I guess that's why the head part looks like a helmet.

Japanese: ヤリで刺されたのに血のあとが 見られないのも、
English: Funny, I expected a spear stabbing would leave at least a few bloodstains.

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中で殺害された ためだろう。
English: I guess the costume must have absorbed most of it...

Japanese: あ、これ！　カントクさんが 座るヤツだよね！
English: Hey! Look! That's the chair the director sits in!

Japanese: 一度、座って みたかったんだよねー。
English: I've always wanted to sit in one of these.

Japanese: なるほどくん、あれは？
English: Hey Nick, take a look at that.

Japanese: カメラの前で、カチンて 鳴らす、白黒のヤツ。
English: One of those black-and-white boards they clap at the start of filming!

Japanese: あと、メガホンメガホン。
English: And look! A megaphone! A real live megaphone!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... はい。なるほどくん。 ‥‥アクション！
English: Ready, Nick? Action!

Japanese: （‥‥ここまでヘンな子だとは 思わなかったな‥‥）
English: (This girl is more troubled than I thought.)

Japanese: 撮影のとき、光を反射させるために 使う。レフ板とかいったかな。
English: That's for reflecting light. They call it a reflecting screen or something like that.

Japanese: ふうん。
English: Huh.

Japanese: なんだよ。その気のなさそうな ヘンジは。
English: What? Where's your enthusiasm for all things entertainment?

Japanese: だってあたし、やるならカントクか トノサマンがいいもん。
English: Well, lighting is fine, but I'd want to be the director. Or maybe the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: （何をやる気なんだ この子は‥‥）
English: (I think she's being serious...!)

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Look, a ladder!

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: That's a "step"-ladder.

Japanese: どう違うの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: So? What's the difference?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You need to stop judging things based on narrow-minded cultural assumptions, Nick!

Japanese: ははは。
English: R-right... sorry. (This girl is OUT there!)

Japanese: また来ちゃったね‥‥殺人現場。
English: We're back at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 調べることをすませたら、 すぐ他のところ、行こうね。
English: Let's find what we need and get out of here quick, Nick.

Japanese: あ、これ！　カントクさんが 座るヤツだよね！
English: Hey! Look! That's the chair the director sits in!

Japanese: 一度、座って みたかったんだよねー。
English: I've always wanted to sit in one of these.

Japanese: （“カントクが座る”‥‥？）
English: (The director...!)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ちょっと、そのイスのあたり 調べてみてよ。
English: Take a look around that chair for me, would you?

Japanese: さっき、カントクが言ってたろ。
English: Remember that script the director was talking about?

Japanese: “どこかに台本、 置き忘れてきた”って‥‥。
English: Didn't he say he'd left it somewhere?

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Ahah!

Japanese: あったよ！　なるほどくん。 ‥‥台本。
English: Found it, Nick! The script!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱりね。
English: Good work!

Japanese: 《台本》をポケットにつっこんだ。
English: Script placed in pocket.

Japanese: “書き割り”っていうやつだ。
English: A stage backdrop.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんによると、 “ネオ・フジヤマ”というらしい。
English: This is "Neo Fujiyama," according to Maya.

Japanese: どう見ても“絵”なんだけど、 子供たちにはバレないのだろうか。
English: It looks incredibly fake, but I guess the kids don't mind.

Japanese: 撮影用の機材がならんでいる。
English: A collection of expensive film equipment.

Japanese: この中のどれか１つでも コワしたら、
English: I can't even begin to imagine how much just one of these costs.

Japanese: その日のうちに成歩堂法律事務所は ツブれてしまうだろう。
English: If we broke one, we'd go bankrupt for sure.

Japanese: 衣袋　武志の死体の形が、 ロープでマーキングされている。
English: They outlined where Jack Hammer's body lay with tape.

Japanese: 死体は怪人アクダイカーンの 着ぐるみの中から発見されたため、
English: His body was found still inside the Evil Magistrate costume.

Japanese: その形どおりにマーキング されている。
English: I guess that's why the head part looks like a helmet.

Japanese: ヤリで刺されたのに血のあとが 見られないのも、
English: Funny, I expected a spear stabbing would leave at least a few bloodstains.

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中で殺害された ためだろう。
English: I guess the costume must have absorbed most of it...

Japanese: ディレクターズ・チェア。 カントクさんが座るイスだ。
English: A classic director's chair.

Japanese: あの宇在カントクのシリが、 こんなイスにおさまるのだろうか？
English: I'm surprised they don't have an extra large one for Mr. Manella.

Japanese: 撮影のとき、光を反射させるために 使うもの。
English: A panel for reflecting light.

Japanese: たしか、レフ板とかいったかな。
English: They call it a "reflecting board" or something.

Japanese: なんのヘンテツもない キャタツだ。
English: A perfectly normal stepladder.

Japanese: ぼくは高いところが大キライ なので、のぼったことがない。
English: I've never actually used one. I don't like heights.

Japanese: ニンゲンは、地に足をつけて 生活すべきだと思うな。
English: I'm a firm believer that humankind was made to keep both feet on the ground.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第２スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio Two Entrance

Japanese: ここ、第２スタジオ、だよね？
English: This... is Studio Two?

Japanese: そのはずだけど。
English: I thought so...

Japanese: あんまり“スタジオ”って感じ じゃないよねー。
English: It doesn't really look much like a "studio," does it?

Japanese: たしかになあ。
English: No, that it does not.

Japanese: あ‥‥。 コテージがあるよ。
English: Hey... There's a trailer over there.

Japanese: 撮影用のセット‥‥にしては シッカリ建てられているなあ。
English: It looks pretty sturdy for a movie set trailer.

Japanese: なるほどくん。ほんとにここ、 スタジオなのかなあ‥‥？
English: Are we sure this is a studio?

Japanese: スタッフ移動用の、 大型のライトバンだ。
English: A light van for moving staff and equipment around.

Japanese: よく手入れされていて、 免許さえあれば、すぐ動かせる。
English: They've kept it in pretty good shape. Looks like it'd run fine.

Japanese: え！　じゃあ、じゃあ、 ドライブしようよ、撮影所１周。
English: Well, let's go for a drive!

Japanese: 免許があれば、の話だよ。
English: Sure... if I had a license.

Japanese: うわあ。なるほどくん。 免許、持ってないんだ。
English: Nick! You don't have a driver's license!?

Japanese: そのかわりに、ホラ。
English: But, look, I have this!

Japanese: こんなところで弁護士バッジ つきつけたって、ダメだよ！
English: Why are you showing me your attorney's badge, Nick?

Japanese: ２人で食事をした あとらしい。
English: Looks like two people ate here.

Japanese: 皿の上には、何も残っていない。
English: There's nothing left on the plates.

Japanese: あ。この人たちも食べたんだね、 ホネつきステーキ。
English: Hey, so they ate t-bone steaks too.

Japanese: こだわるなあ、ステーキに‥‥。
English: What is it with steaks around here?

Japanese: そりゃ、ステーキは別バラだから。
English: Hey! There's always room for steak!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 なんかちょっと、ヘンだなあ‥‥。
English: But... something does seem out of place...

Japanese: 何が？
English: What?

Japanese: イワ感、ていうの？ なんだろ、この感じ‥‥。
English: I... don't know. It just feels "odd."

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: ここが“第２スタジオ” だからじゃないかな。
English: Probably because this is Studio Two?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんそうだ。 スタジオには見えないけどね。
English: Probably... Though it doesn't look anything like a studio.

Japanese: 最近、取りつけられたらしい 小さな焼却炉だ。
English: An incinerator. Apparently they just put this one in.

Japanese: コゲたススがこびりついていて、 あまり手入れされていない。
English: It's already covered in soot. They must not clean it much.

Japanese: 撮影セットを組むための 資材みたいだけど‥‥。
English: These look like materials for making a movie set.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いこと、 置きっぱなしになっているみたい。
English: They've been sitting here for quite some time.

Japanese: まるで、資材置き場だね。
English: This might as well be a junkyard.

Japanese: よく見ると、建てられてから 数年はたっているようだ。
English: On closer inspection, this trailer looks like it's been here for a while.

Japanese: これはもう、“セット”なんて もんじゃなさそうだ。
English: I don't think this is part of some temporary film set.

Japanese: 何やら花が咲いている。
English: There're some flowers here.

Japanese: わあ、キレイだねー。 ちゃんと手入れされてるよ。
English: Wow, so pretty! They're taking good care of these.

Japanese: あんまり近づくなよ。 その鉄柵、あぶないから。
English: Don't get to close. That fence looks dangerous.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん！ 入ってみようよ！
English: Nick! Let's go inside!

Japanese: 怒られないかなあ‥‥。
English: You think it's okay...?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 カギがかかってるよ。
English: Oh well. It's locked.

Japanese: うう‥‥つまんない。
English: Aww... shucks.

Japanese: 名もなき花が咲いている。
English: Nameless flowers bloom in solitude.

Japanese: もしかしたら名前は あるのかもしれないが、
English: They probably have a proper name...

Japanese: ぼくは一生、知ることなく 終わるのだなあ。
English: But it is not for me to know. Not now, nor ever, till the end of my days.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... イミもなく しみじみしてしまった。
English: I need a vacation.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第２スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio Two Entrance

Japanese: （誰もいない、か‥‥）
English: (This place is deserted...)
*crash!*

Japanese: きゃっ！　ななな、なるほどくん。 いいい、今の音、なに？
English: Eek! N-N-Nick! W-w-what was that noise!?

Japanese: コテージの中から聞こえて きたみたいだけど‥‥。
English: It sounded like it came from inside the trailer...

Japanese: ‥‥中に誰か、いるみたいだね。
English: Someone must be inside...

Japanese: す‥‥すみませーん。
English: H-Hello?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 返事がないぞ。
English: No answer.

Japanese: アヤしいよ、なるほどくん！
English: Pretty suspicious, if you ask me, Nick!

Japanese: よし‥‥入ってみよう！
English: Let's go in!

Japanese: 怒られないかなあ‥‥。
English: I'm not sure we should be barging in...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥あれ。 ‥‥カギがかかってるよ。
English: ...Huh. It's locked.

Japanese: えー。カギは？
English: What? Don't we have a key?

Japanese: そりゃまあ、正門前の 警備員の詰所にあるんじゃないか。
English: No. But there's probably one in the guard station at the main gate.

Japanese: 調査のためだよ。借りてこよう！
English: Then what are we waiting for? Let's borrow it!

Japanese: （貸してもらえるかなあ‥‥）
English: (If they'll let us...)

Japanese: さっき詰所から借りてきた カギ、使えるかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if the key we borrowed from the guard station will work?
It opened.

Japanese: よし！　行くよなるほどくん！
English: Great! Let's go, Nick!

Japanese: （お。やる気マンマンだな）
English: (She seems eager all of a sudden...)

Japanese: さ。お先にどうぞ。 なるほどくん。
English: Well, you first, Nick!

Japanese: （コシは引けているみたいだ）
English: (Ah, not THAT eager.)

Japanese: コテージの入口だ。 カギがかかっている。
English: The trailer door. It's locked.

Japanese: たぶん、カギは警備員の詰所に あると思うんだけど‥‥。
English: The key is probably in the security guard station.

Japanese: 借りられるかどうか、 自信はない。
English: I doubt they'd let us borrow it.

Japanese: コテージへ入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていない。
English: The trailer door. It's unlocked.

Japanese: スタッフ移動用の、 大型のライトバンだ。
English: A light van for moving staff and equipment around.

Japanese: よく手入れされていて、 免許さえあれば、すぐ動かせる。
English: They've kept it in pretty good shape. Looks like it'd run fine.

Japanese: ２人で食事をした あとらしい。
English: Looks like two people ate here.

Japanese: 皿の上には、何も残っていない。
English: There's nothing left on the plates.

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: おそらくここが“第２スタジオ” だからなのだろう。
English: Probably because this is Studio Two?

Japanese: ‥‥あまり、“スタジオ”という フンイキでもないのだが。
English: Probably... Though it doesn't look anything like a studio.

Japanese: 最近、取りつけられたらしい 小さな焼却炉だ。
English: An incinerator. Apparently they just put this one in.

Japanese: コゲたススがこびりついていて、 あまり手入れされていない。
English: It's already covered in soot. They must not clean it much.

Japanese: 撮影セットを組むための 資材みたいだけど‥‥。
English: These look like materials for making a movie set.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いこと、 ほったらかしになっている。
English: They've been sitting here for quite some time.

Japanese: スタジオというよりは、 資材置き場という感じだ。
English: This might as well be a junkyard.

Japanese: よく見ると、建てられてから 数年はたっているようだ。
English: On closer inspection, this trailer looks like it's been here for a while.

Japanese: これはもう、“セット”なんて もんじゃなさそうだ。
English: I don't think this is part of some temporary film set.

Japanese: 名もなき花が咲いている。
English: Nameless flowers bloom in solitude.

Japanese: もしかしたら名前は あるのかもしれないが、
English: They probably have a proper name...

Japanese: ぼくは一生、知ることなく 終わるのだなあ。
English: But it is not for me to know. Not now, nor ever till the end of my days.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... イミもなく しみじみしてしまった。
English: I need a vacation.

Japanese: ドアにはカギが かかっていない。
English: The door is unlocked.

Japanese: 今なら、自由に入ることが できそうだ。
English: We could probably just walk right in.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第２スタジオ・コテージ内
English: October 18 Studio Two Trailer
Eek!

Japanese: びっくりしたぁ！
English: S-someone's in here!

Japanese: 誰。
English: Names.

Japanese: え。あ、あたしたち、二、 ニボサブさんの弁護士ですけど。
English: O-our n-names? Um, w-we're WP's lawyers, and, um...

Japanese: そう。
English: I see.
A-and who might you...?

Japanese: 姫神。 ‥‥プロデューサーの。
English: Dee Vasquez. The producer.

Japanese: （スラリとした‥‥キレイな ヒトだな‥‥）
English: (Dee Vasquez... She's quite beautiful.)

Japanese: なるほどくん、もう行こう！ 他のところ、調べようよ。
English: Nick, let's get out of here. Isn't there someplace else we have to check?

Japanese: そうだな。
English: Y-yeah.

Japanese: ちょっと。
English: Wait.

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: ウザイに会ったら、 これ、わたしておいて。
English: If you see Manella, give him this.

Japanese: な、なんで あたしたちがそんなコト‥‥！
English: W-why do we have to do your errands...!
I... I don't think I like her. *sniff*

Japanese: 泣くなよ。
English: Don't cry. She'll take it as a sign of weakness.

Japanese: わかりました。 見かけたら、わたしておきます。
English: Alright. We'll give it to him if we see him.

Japanese: 《姫神のメモ》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Vasquez's Memo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 古びたポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: “ダイナマイト侍 主演、衣袋　武志”か。
English: "'Dynamite Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: 被害者のイブクロさん、 昔はすごかったんだなあ。
English: I guess Mr. Hammer used to be quite the star.

Japanese: “武者ぶるいの夏 主演、衣袋　武志”。
English: "'Samurai Summer' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: これまたずいぶん 古いポスターだな。
English: This poster's quite old.

Japanese: あー、これ！ なつかしいなあ。
English: Wow! It's been years since I saw that show!

Japanese: 誰にでもある“ひと夏の ロマンス”がテーマなんだよね。
English: It was one of those typical summer romance stories... but it was great!

Japanese: テーマはともかく、それがなんで サムライにウマなんだよ。
English: A typical summer romance... with a samurai on a horse?

Japanese: 昔の映画のポスターだな。 “武士節　主演、衣袋　武志”
English: Another old movie poster. "'The Singing Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: “武士節”って、やっぱり “ぶしぶし”って読むのかな。
English: What the hell do samurai sing about? Chopping off heads?

Japanese: あー、それ！
English: Oh. My. God.

Japanese: ジマンのノドで大スターに のし上がる、武士の話だよ。
English: That movie was the best! It's about a samurai who sings his way to fame and fortune!

Japanese: イブクロさん、 歌もうまかったからね。
English: Mr. Hammer was a very accomplished singer, you know.

Japanese: スターにのし上がる話なのに、 なんで時代が室町なんだよ‥‥。
English: Singing samurai stars... What's next? Ninja ballet?

Japanese: ‥‥特に手がかりになるものは 入っていないな。
English: Nothing in here that could be a clue.

Japanese: 紙コップとか、丸めた資料 なんかが捨ててあるだけだ。
English: Just some paper cups and crumpled documents.

Japanese: ここで会議をしていたらしい。
English: This is where they had that meeting.

Japanese: 机の上には、資料にお茶、 紙コップなどが並んでいる。
English: There are documents and paper cups scattered around.

Japanese: くもりガラスで外は見えない。
English: The glass is frosted. I can't see outside.

Japanese: １０月も半ばをすぎて、 陽ざしもすっかりやわらいできた。
English: This far into October, the dimming daylight makes it feel like winter's coming.

Japanese: トノサマンで使うセットの デザインを決めていたようだ。
English: It looks like they were designing a set to use for the Steel Samurai show.

Japanese: セットを組む予算なんかも メモされている。
English: They've got some figures for how much the set costs...

Japanese: ‥‥なんでこんなに お金がかかるんだ‥‥？
English: I can't believe they spend that much money on those sets!

Japanese: 事件当日の ことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I was wondering if you could tell me about the day of the murder...
M-Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: 台本。
English: Script.

Japanese: は、はい？
English: E-excuse me?

Japanese: 台本、探してるの。
English: Script. I'm looking for a script.

Japanese: だ、台本、ですか？
English: A s-script?

Japanese: トノサマンの第１３話。
English: "The Steel Samurai, Episode 13."

Japanese: 今、読みたいの。
English: I need it.

Japanese: あのお、事件があった日の こと、聞きたいんですけど。
English: Umm... could we ask you a bit about the day of the murder?

Japanese: アタクシは、 台本を読みたいの。
English: I need to read it.
(We're getting nowhere fast.)

Japanese: あの、あの、トノサマンについて 話が聞きたいんですけど！
English: Um, w-we'd really like to ask you about the Steel Samurai!
Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: テレビ、やってるわ。 毎週。
English: It's on TV. Every week.

Japanese: 言うべきことは全部、 そこで言ってるから。
English: That's all I have to say about that.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ テレビ見てろって！
English: N-Nick! She's telling us to go watch TV!? The nerve of her!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくに怒るなよ。
English: Hey, don't get mad at me.

Japanese: カントク‥‥ えーと、宇在さん、でしたっけ。
English: About the director... Sal Manella, was it?

Japanese: 彼は、どういう‥‥？
English: What, er, exactly is his role here...?

Japanese: 言わなかったかしら。
English: Perhaps I didn't make myself clear?

Japanese: アタクシ、今、台本を 探しているの。
English: I'm looking for a script.

Japanese: 他のことは、興味がないわ。
English: I can't be bothered with anything else.
Nick?

Japanese: 撮影所って、こんなヘンなのしか いないの？
English: Are all people in the entertainment business this... weird?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん‥‥たしかに。
English: It's starting to look like it.

Japanese: 事件当日、道がふさがって いたんですか？
English: On the day of the murder, the path that leads here was blocked?

Japanese: ここへ来る途中、 サルマゲどん、見たでしょ？
English: You saw Mr. Monkey on the way here, correct?

Japanese: さ‥‥“サルマゲどん”？
English: M-"Mr. Monkey"?

Japanese: クビの折れたサルよ。
English: The monkey with the broken head.

Japanese: ああ‥‥あれですか‥‥。 （すごいネーミングだな‥‥）
English: Oh, right, that... (What an original name...)

Japanese: あのクビ、事件があった日に 風でこわれてね。
English: Its head fell over in the wind on the day of the murder.

Japanese: クビをどかす作業が始まったのが ３時すぎ。
English: They didn't start moving the head out of the way until after 3:00.

Japanese: 道を通れるようになったのが、 ４時すぎだったの。
English: It was after 4:00 by the time the path was unblocked.

Japanese: いい。
English: Capice?

Japanese: イブクロが死んだのは、２時半。
English: Everyone in this trailer was stuck here until the path was cleared.

Japanese: でも、ここにいた人間は、 午後４時すぎまで、
English: Stuck in this trailer. Stuck until after 4:00.

Japanese: このコテージに 閉じこめられていたの。
English: Hammer died at 2:30.

Japanese: 第１スタジオになんか、 行けたはずがないわ。
English: Thus, none of us could have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: これはホント。
English: It's true.

Japanese: ３時すぎに、クレーンを使って クビをどけてもらったの。
English: A crane came just after 3:00 to move the head.

Japanese: そとの会社の人にたのんでね。 作業した人たちは覚えてるはず。
English: We called some people in to clear the way. I'm sure they'd corroborate my story.

Japanese: で、でも！
English: B-but wait!

Japanese: クビがこわれたのが、２時３０分 より後だったら、
English: What if the head fell over after 2:30?

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ行くことは できたはずです！
English: Then you could have gone to Studio One!

Japanese: ２時３０分‥‥ イブクロが死んだ時間ね。
English: 2:30... the time of death.
Very well.

Japanese: 来なさい。
English: Come.

Japanese: 持ってきましたよ。 はい！　台本です！
English: Here, we found it! Your script!

Japanese: そう。
English: Ah.

Japanese: 《台本》を 姫神さんに渡した。
English: Script handed to Dee Vasquez.
Umm... uh...

Japanese: おしまいですか、それで。
English: You're not going to talk to us?

Japanese: 静かに。
English: Quiet.

Japanese: 今、台本を読んでるから。
English: I'm reading.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! ちょっと！ イイカゲンにしてください！
English: Just you hold on! What's the big idea!?

Japanese: ナニサマのつもりですか！ あた、あたしたちは‥‥！
English: Who do you think you are anyway!? And, and do you even know who we are!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 荷星の弁護士？
English: Powers's lawyers?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: Umm... right.

Japanese: うたがってるの？　アタクシを。
English: Am I a suspect?

Japanese: あ、いえ、その。 ‥‥そういうワケじゃあ‥‥。
English: N-no, it's just, well, no, but...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 事件のあった日のこと、 だったかしら？
English: You wanted to know about the day of the murder?

Japanese: は、はい。聞かせてください。
English: Y-yes. Anything you could tell us would be a big help.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 正午から、ここで会議があったのは 知ってるわね？
English: You know there was a meeting here at noon?

Japanese: はい。カントクとか、テレビ局の 人たちといっしょに‥‥。
English: Yes. With the director and the people from the network...

Japanese: そう。
English: Correct.

Japanese: じゃあ、よく聞きなさい。
English: Now, listen closely.

Japanese: あの日の午後、 このコテージにいた人間は、
English: None of the people in this trailer that afternoon went to Studio One.

Japanese: 誰も、第１スタジオに行くことは 不可能だったの。
English: It was impossible for us to leave.

Japanese: な、なぜですか？
English: Impossible? Why?

Japanese: 道がふさがってたから。
English: The path was blocked.

Japanese: ‥‥道が？
English: The... path?

Japanese: ‥‥興味ないわ。
English: I'm not interested.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 第２スタジオ・コテージ内
English: October 18 Studio Two Trailer

Japanese: あら、こんにちは。
English: Oh, hi.

Japanese: あ、どうも。 こんなところで、何を‥‥？
English: Hello. What brings you here?

Japanese: あ、ええ。 ちょっと、資料の整理を‥‥。
English: Oh, well, I had to arrange some stuff...
Umm...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: そちらのカノジョ‥‥なんか、 フンイキ、変わりましたねー。
English: The, um, girl with you. Doesn't she seem a little different?

Japanese: まるで別人みたい。
English: Like, is she even the same person as before?

Japanese: 気のせいですよ。
English: Um, nope! She's the same as always.

Japanese: そうですよ。
English: Same as always.

Japanese: ‥‥違うと思うけど‥‥。
English: Okay... whatever you say...

Japanese: “武者ぶるいの夏 主演、衣袋　武志”。
English: "'Samurai Summer' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: これまたずいぶん 古いポスターだな。
English: This poster looks like it's been hanging here for years.

Japanese: それ、真宵が好きな映画よ。
English: This is one of Maya's favorite movies.

Japanese: うちの事務所のポスター、 これにしましょうか。
English: Maybe we should replace the poster in the office with this one?

Japanese: それは‥‥ ちょっとカナシイかな。
English: That... would be kind of sad.

Japanese: 昔の映画のポスターだな。 “武士節　主演、衣袋　武志”
English: Another old movie poster. "'The Singing Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: “武士節”って、やっぱり “ぶしぶし”って読むのかな。
English: What the hell do samurai sing about? Chopping off heads?

Japanese: 室町時代をブタイにした ミュージカル映画よ。
English: It's a samurai musical set in ancient Japan.

Japanese: ミュ、ミュージカルですか。
English: A s-samurai musical? Is that even a genre!?

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん、けっこう くわしいんですね。
English: Well, I'm impressed. You know your stuff, Mia.

Japanese: 真宵が大好きだから、 そのおかげでちょっと、ね。
English: Oh, Maya's the real fan. I just picked a little up.

Japanese: あれから、あの子は見かけた‥‥？
English: Have you seen that boy since then?

Japanese: いいえ。
English: Nope.

Japanese: さっきまで、 タイヘンだったんですよ。
English: You missed it, though.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャン、あの子を 追いかけ回してたんですけど、
English: The security lady was chasing around after the boy so fast I thought she'd collapse.

Japanese: 結局、逃がしちゃって。 ‥‥すごく怒ってました。
English: Of course, he got away in the end. Boy, was she mad!

Japanese: うん。ぼくたちも見たよ。
English: Actually, we saw them. How's the old windbag doing?

Japanese: さっき、詰所で ようかんをニギりつぶしてました。
English: She was squeezing donuts through her clenched fists back at the guard station.

Japanese: （オバチャンも、そこまで ムキになるなよ‥‥）
English: (Some people take their jobs a little too seriously.)

Japanese: カベのポスター、 みんなイブクロさんの‥‥？
English: Are all the posters on the wall here of Hammer?

Japanese: そうですよ。‥‥ホント、 こんなことになってザンネンです。
English: Yes. It's really a terrible loss for the studios.

Japanese: でも、最近は 人気が落ちていた、とか‥‥。
English: But... his popularity had been waning recently, right?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: That's true.

Japanese: ‥‥あのことがあってから、 ですね‥‥。
English: Then again, after what happened...

Japanese: “あのこと”‥‥？
English: After WHAT happened?

Japanese: 弁護士さん、知らないんですか？ イブクロさんのこと‥‥。
English: Y-you mean, you don't know about Hammer...?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないけど。
English: No, what?

Japanese: ゴ、ゴメンなさい。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: 私、よけいなこと、 言っちゃったみたい‥‥。
English: I shouldn't have said anything.

Japanese: 気になるなあ。 ‥‥何？　“あのこと”って。
English: Wait, now you have to tell me. What happened with Hammer?

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: わ、私の口からは‥‥。
English: I'm probably not the one who should tell you.

Japanese: （やれやれ。また“口どめ” ってヤツか‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. Why do I get the feeling something's being covered up here?)

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: すみません。 私には、なんのことか‥‥。
English: Sorry. I'm really not sure what that is.

Japanese: あの、これ、どう思う？
English: Do you know anything about this?

Japanese: あ。トノサマンの トレーディングカード。
English: Hey, that's a Steel Samurai trading card.

Japanese: 実は、私も集めてるんですよ。
English: I collect those, actually.

Japanese: あと１枚で コンプリートするんですよねー。
English: I'm one card away from a complete set!

Japanese: こんぷりーと？
English: A complete set?

Japanese: カードを全種類、 そろえることです。
English: Yeah, a set of one of each card.

Japanese: ‥‥へえ。
English: Huh...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あ、ああああああああああああッ！
English: Wait...Waitwaitwaitwait!!!

Japanese: これ！　これ！　このカード！ ずっと探していた、最後の１枚！
English: That's it!!! That's the card I need! You don't know how long I've been looking for it!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: Uh...

Japanese: は、はしたない女だと 思われるかもしれません。でも！
English: I don't care what kind of girl you might think I am, I need that card!

Japanese: おねがいします！　できましたら、 私と、その、トレードを‥‥。
English: P-please! I beg you! Please trade with me!

Japanese: ト、トレード？
English: T-trade with you?

Japanese: 交換です！
English: Yes, trade cards!

Japanese: あの、私、レアカードが あまっているんです。
English: Look, I'll even trade you an Ultra Rare Premium card!

Japanese: レ、レアカード？
English: Trade me a... huh?

Japanese: 珍しいカードのことです。 なにとぞ、おねがいします！
English: Yes, it's a good deal for your Ultra Rare!

Japanese: なにとぞォッ！
English: Please!!!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　オトメに ハジをかかせてはダメよ！
English: Phoenix! Can't you see she's desperate?

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ、この 意味不明なモリアガリは！）
English: (W-what's everybody getting so excited about...?)

Japanese: いいですよ。
English: Okay.

Japanese: ホントですかッ！ うれしいッ！
English: Really!? Th-thanks!!!

Japanese: じゃ、これ。はい。
English: Then this is for you.

Japanese: 《レアカード》を 法廷記録にはさんだ。
English: URP Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: きゃっほう！
English: Yaa-hoo!

Japanese: （行っちまった‥‥）
English: (And there she goes...)

Japanese: ‥‥いいことをすると、 すがすがしい気持ちになりますね。
English: Doesn't doing good things like that make you feel great?

Japanese: ほんとにそうですね。
English: Yeah. Yeah, it does.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、これ、ぼくのものじゃ ないから‥‥。
English: No... no, I can't. See, this card isn't really even mine.
I... I see.

Japanese: そうですよね‥‥
English: O-of course.

Japanese: そんなもんですよね‥‥
English: Of course it wouldn't be yours.

Japanese: そんなもんなんですよね‥‥
English: Of course you couldn't trade it with me.

Japanese: どうせそんなもんなんですよね‥
English: Of course I won't ever get that card, ever.

Japanese: 私なんてどうせそんなもんなんです
English: Of course I don't even really deserve that card.

Japanese: なるほどくん、どうするんですか！ 心神喪失状態になってますよ！
English: Phoenix, do something! She's blacking out!

Japanese: わ、わ、わかりましたよ。 交換しましょう。
English: O-o-okay, okay! I'll trade!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前９時４２分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: October 19, 9:42 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1
Umm... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: さっきから、なんで そんなケワしいカオを‥‥？
English: Why do you look so... unhappy?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。ははは。
English: O-oh! Oh, nothing! Eh heh heh.

Japanese: なんでもないですよ‥‥
English: Really, it's nothing.

Japanese: ね、千尋さん‥‥。
English: Right, Mia?

Japanese: （‥‥うわ、コワいカオ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. She looks even unhappier...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は今、 ほとんど死にかけているわ。
English: Your client is now practically a dead man walking...

Japanese: そういう意味で、彼には とても親近感をおぼえるわね。
English: Perhaps that's why I feel particularly close to him.

Japanese: 悪い冗談はやめてくださいよ‥‥。
English: This is no time for dead- people jokes, please.

Japanese: 今回は、こちらに武器が まったくないわ。
English: You know we're going into this trial utterly defenseless.

Japanese: でも、荷星さんが本当に 無実ならば、
English: Yet, if Mr. Powers is truly innocent...

Japanese: 私たちは、どこかで 見落としをしているはず‥‥。
English: We should be able to find something overlooked in the evidence to prove it.

Japanese: “見落とし”‥‥。
English: Something "overlooked"...?

Japanese: この法廷で、それを 見つけ出せなければ、
English: We have to find something, Phoenix, today.

Japanese: 私たちは負けるわ。 ‥‥今日中に、かならず。
English: It's that, or lose the trial.

Japanese: （キビシイ戦いになるな‥‥）
English: (This isn't going to be easy.)

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 19, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、荷星　三郎の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側も、 準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: では、御剣検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: 昨日の法廷で、意外な事実が あきらかになった。

Japanese: そのころは、会議中だったわけ ですか‥‥。
English: And according to your testimony, you were in a meeting at that time.

Japanese: では、弁護人。 ‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: はい。

English: (Oh, great. Is that all...)

English: You've been doing your homework, haven't you?

Japanese: 法廷記録をよく見てみたまえ。
English: Look at the Court Record.

Japanese: 法廷記録‥‥？
English: The Court Record...?

Japanese: ２時１５分から４時まで、 あの道は通れなかったのだよ？
English: Surely, you knew that from 2:15 to 4:00 the path between the studios was blocked?

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビが 道をふさいでいたのだから！
English: Mrs. Monkey's head had fallen across the path!

Japanese: あっ！　そういえば！
English: Whoa! You're right!

Japanese: たしかに被害者が死んだ時間、 コテージは休憩に入っていた。
English: True, the break in the trailer meeting came right at the time of death...

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: スタジオへ通じる道は、 ふさがっていたのだ！
English: The path from there to the scene of the crime was blocked!

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビによって！
English: The fallen Mrs. Monkey head barred the way!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥。 ... （ホントは“サルマゲどん” なんだけど‥‥）
English: (It's actually "Mr. Monkey," but Edgeworth has a point...)

Japanese: （それにツッコミを入れる気にも なれないな‥‥）
English: (And somehow, I feel no desire to correct him.)

Japanese: 返すコトバも 見つからないようですね。
English: No counter-argument, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あなたの負けですな、弁護人。
English: Then I'm afraid you lose this round.

Japanese: （‥‥ここで認めたくは ないけど‥‥）
English: (I don't want to write off so many possible suspects...)

Japanese: （ムリだとわかっていることを 主張するワケにも行かない‥‥）
English: (But I can't keep claiming the impossible, either...)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、コテージの人間が スタジオに行くのは、不可能です。
English: I agree that it was impossible for anyone in the trailer to go to the studio in that time.

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Ha hah!

Japanese: いつものように、考えもなく つっかかってくるかと思えば、
English: I thought you might be thoughtlessly treading on thin ice again!

Japanese: なかなかモノわかりがいいな‥‥。
English: But I see you had at least an inkling of the truth.

Japanese: どういうことですか？ ‥‥御剣検事。
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: カンタンなこと‥‥。
English: It's quite simple...

Japanese: たしかに被害者が死んだ時間、 コテージは休憩に入っていた。
English: True, the break in the trailer meeting came right at the time of death...

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: スタジオへ通じる道は、 ふさがっていたのだ！
English: The path from there to the scene of the crime was blocked!

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビによって！
English: The fallen Mrs. Monkey head barred the way!

Japanese: 強風によってクビが折れたのは、 ２時１５分だった。
English: At around 2:15, a strong gust of wind knocked off the studio mascot's head.

Japanese: つまり。
English: Ergo!

Japanese: ２時３０分に休憩をとっても、 あの道は通れなかったわけだ。
English: When the people in the trailer took a break at 2:30, the way to the studio was blocked.

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビが あったのだから。
English: Blocked... by Mrs. Monkey's severed head!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥。 ... （ホントは“サルマゲどん” なんだけど‥‥）
English: (It's actually "Mr. Monkey," but Edgeworth has a point...)

Japanese: （それにツッコミを入れる気にも なれないな‥‥）
English: (And somehow, I feel no desire to correct him.)

Japanese: 以上をもって‥‥。
English: I believe we have seen enough evidence.

Japanese: この証人に対する質問は、 終了する。
English: I would like to relieve Mr. Manella from the stand.

Japanese: （お、終わっちまったよ‥‥）
English: (Wh-what? It's over?)

Japanese: では、 私の意見を申し上げましょう。
English: Very well. The court's opinion on this case is as follows:

Japanese: 事件当日、第２スタジオの コテージに、数名の人間がいた。
English: We have found that there were several other people in Studio 2 on the day of the murder.

Japanese: しかし、彼らは、第１スタジオに 行けなかったわけだから、
