English: However, it is also clear that none of these people could have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: この事件に関係ないことは あきらかです。
English: They therefore have no relation to this case.

Japanese: また、このトノサマンの 写真ですが、
English: Furthermore, with regards to the photo of the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: 体格から考えて、被告人以外の 人間は、着ることはできない。
English: Given the size of the costume, no one other than Mr. Powers could have worn it that day.

Japanese: あとは、このトノサマンが 犯人であるという、
English: All that is lacking is decisive evidence that he is the one who did it.

Japanese: その決定的な証拠があれば、 それで決まるのですが‥‥。
English: If we had that, I'm afraid I would have to find Mr. Powers guilty.

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 当然、検察側は用意している。 ‥‥決定的な証人を。
English: The prosecution is pleased to announce that we indeed have decisive evidence: a witness.

Japanese: 御剣検事！　“決定的な証人” とは、どういう‥‥？
English: Who is this witness, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 彼は、トノサマンが被害者を 殺害した瞬間を目撃しているッ！
English: My witness saw the very moment when the Steel Samurai skewered the victim!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! I will have order!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: １０分間の休憩を挟んで、 その証人に話を聞きましょう。
English: The court will take a 10 minute recess, after which we will hear your witness.

Japanese: それでは、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned for recess!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１１時４分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: October 19, 11:04 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... ち、千尋さん。 どうしましょう‥‥。
English: What do we do now, Mia?

Japanese: コテージの人間は、 事件には無関係、ってことに‥‥。
English: If everyone in that trailer has an alibi...

Japanese: ‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥ゴメンね、なるほどくん。
English: I'm sorry, Phoenix.

Japanese: どうやら、 私の見込み違いだったみたいね。
English: I guess I was wrong.

Japanese: そ、そんな！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: 千尋さんがあきらめちゃったら、 ぼくは、もう‥‥！
English: Don't tell me you're giving up! If you give up, what hope do I have...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... カン違いしないで。
English: Don't get me wrong.

Japanese: 私は今まで、あきらめた ことなんて、一度もないわ。
English: I've never given up on a trial before, not while there was a chance.

Japanese: 今の尋問であきらかになったのは、 ただ１つよ。
English: Only one thing became clear in your cross-examination.

Japanese: “コテージにいた人間は、 第１スタジオには行けなかった”
English: The people in the trailer could not have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: 私、そこに何か秘密があると 思っていたんだけど‥‥。
English: I thought there was more to it than that...

Japanese: そうじゃなかった。 ‥‥それだけのことよ。
English: But I was wrong. That's all there is.

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ボク‥‥、 どうなっちゃうんでしょう？
English: Er... What's going to happen to me?

Japanese: なんだか、もう、法廷中の人たちが “お前がやったんだ！”って、
English: It kinda seems like everyone in that courtroom thinks I did it...

Japanese: そう言ってるような気がして‥‥。
English: They think I'm a murderer!

Japanese: だいじょうぶです。
English: Don't worry, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: あなたが無実ならば、私たちは かならず、それを証明します。
English: If you are innocent, we will prove it. I guarantee it.

Japanese: 私たちにまかせて、 堂々としていてください。
English: Leave it to us, and... be yourself. Be strong.

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンなんですから。 あなたは。
English: You are the Steel Samurai, hero to children everywhere, after all.

Japanese: きょ‥‥
English: You...

Japanese: 恐縮です。
English: Thanks. *sniff*

Japanese: さあ。なるほどくん。
English: Okay, Phoenix.

Japanese: がんばらないとね。
English: This one's for the kids!

Japanese: トノサマンが大好きな、 子供たちのためにも‥‥！
English: Let's do it!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１１時１５分 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 19, 11:15 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court is back in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥検察側としては、
English: The prosecution has a concern.

Japanese: まだおさなく、イタイケな小学生を 殺人事件の証人として呼んだうえ、
English: As our witness is a grade schooler of tender years, and this is a murder case...

Japanese: そこの弁護人に、イジの悪い 質問をさせたくはない‥‥。
English: We worry that the defense might cause unnecessary trauma with his cruel questioning!

Japanese: （好きなコト言いやがって‥‥）
English: (Nice to see Edgeworth taking the moral high ground...)

Japanese: しかし、こうなっては いたしかたない。
English: However, we have no choice.

Japanese: 証人として、大滝　九太少年を 入廷させていただこう！
English: The prosecution calls Cody Hackins to the stand.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥とりあえず証人に、何か台を。
English: Your Honor. Perhaps you could arrange a box for him to stand on?

Japanese: そ、そうですね。
English: Oh, r-right.

Japanese: 係官。 ‥‥みかん箱を。
English: Guard, please bring him a box. One of those donut crates should do.

Japanese: では証人。名前と学年を。
English: Will the witness state his name and grade in school.
Witness!

Japanese: なんだよ！ オトナだからって、イバるなよな！
English: What!? Just 'cause you're all grown-up don't mean you can push me around!

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥。
English: Mrph...

Japanese: だめよ、九太くん。
English: Cody?

Japanese: ちゃんと、質問には答えるの。
English: Answer his question, okay?

Japanese: あっ、お姉ちゃん！
English: H-hey, it's you! The nice lady!

Japanese: ボク、大滝　九太。 小学校２年生です！
English: I'm Cody Hackins. I'm in 2nd grade!

Japanese: （先が思いやられるな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling this is going to be a long, long day...)

Japanese: 御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 相手は子供なんですから、 もっとこう、ヤサシクするように。
English: Please remember that you're speaking to a child. Try to be gentle.

Japanese: む、むむむむむ。
English: M-mrrrrph!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。いや、‥‥九太くん。
English: Witness! Er, I mean, Cody.

Japanese: （なんか、イタイタしいな‥‥）
English: (He's having trouble with this "gentle" thing.)

Japanese: キミは、その、事件のあった日、 英都撮影所に行ったね？
English: You were present, er, you were at Global Studios on the day of the, er, incident?

Japanese: 行っちゃ悪ィかよ！
English: You got a problem with that!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき見たことを、その。 ワレワレに聞かせてほしいなあ。
English: Please tell us what you saw that day.

Japanese: え？　オジさんと、そこの エラそうなヒゲのおジイさんに？
English: What, pops? You want me to tell you and gramps with the beard over there?

Japanese: “おニイさん”だ。
English: Just... Mr. Edgeworth will be fine.

Japanese: “ヒゲのおじさん”です。
English: I prefer "bearded gentleman" myself.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (A very long day...)

Japanese: ところで。
English: Incidentally,

Japanese: 法廷内は、カメラの持ち込みは キンシされているはずですが。
English: photographic equipment is strictly forbidden in this courtroom.

Japanese: う、うむむ。
English: M-mpht!

Japanese: “カメラがダメなら証言しない”と 言われてしまったもので‥‥
English: M-my apologies, Your Honor. He said he wouldn't testify if he couldn't bring it...

Japanese: トクベツに許可していただきたい。
English: I'd like special permission, if that's possible.

Japanese: コドモとトリヒキして、 しかも負けるなよ！
English: Wait, so you're saying you had to bargain terms with a kid... and you LOST?

Japanese: このカメラ、こないだ買って もらったばっかりなんだぜ！
English: Hey! I just got this new camera!

Japanese: まだ、ちょっと使い方、 よくわかんないんだけどさ！
English: Don't really know how to use it all that good yet!

Japanese: 最近は、どこかへ行くときは、 ゼッタイ持っていくんだぜ！
English: But I bring it with me wherever I go!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: あのカメラ、ビミョーに 気になるわ‥‥。
English: I wonder if he had that camera on the day of the murder...

Japanese: ちょっと、法廷記録に ファイルを作った方がいいわね。
English: You'd better make a note of it in the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《九太のデジタルカメラ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Cody's Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、九太くん。
English: Very well, Cody.

Japanese: 事件のあった日、見たことを 《証言》してもらいましょう。
English: Please testify to the court about what you saw the day of the incident.

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オレ、一度トノサマンのリハーサル が見たくてさあ。
English: I wanted to see a Steel Samurai rehearsal, just once.

Japanese: インターネットで地図を見て、 あの日、撮影所に行ったんだ。
English: I found a map on the Internet, and went to the studios that day.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンに見つからない ように、道のそとの林を通って、
English: I went through the woods, off the path, so that old lady wouldn't catch me.

Japanese: オレ、とにかく スタジオに行こうとしたんだ。
English: I was going for the studio.

Japanese: とちゅう、３０分ぐらい道に まよっちゃったんだけど。
English: I got kinda lost on the way, though. For about 30 minutes.

Japanese: スタジオのところに出たら、 トノサマンがいたんだ！
English: When I came out by the studio, there was the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: オレ、うれしくて。 見てたら、悪人が出てきてさあ。
English: It totally rocked! Right before my eyes, out came the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマンは‥‥モチロン、 その悪人をたおしたよ！
English: Of course, the Steel Samurai took him down! Pow!

Japanese: もしカメラを持っていれば、 シャッターチャンスだったよ。
English: If I had my camera with me, that woulda been the time for a shot, I tell you.

Japanese: ケッキョク、スタジオには 入れなくて、その日は帰ったんだ。
English: Anyway, I couldn't get into the studio, so I went home.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では弁護人、《尋問》を おねがいしますが、
English: Very well. The defense may begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: 相手は少年です。 ‥‥ヤサシクするように。
English: And, be gentle. Remember you're talking to a child.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... （そんな必要、 ないような気がするけど‥‥）
English: (This kid is tougher than most adults we see in here, honestly...)

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オレ、一度トノサマンのリハーサル が見たくてさあ。
English: I wanted to see a Steel Samurai rehearsal, just once.

Japanese: インターネットで地図を見て、 あの日、撮影所に行ったんだ。
English: I found a map on the Internet, and went to the studios that day.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンに見つからない ように、道のそとの林をとおって、
English: I went through the woods, off the path, so that old lady wouldn't catch me.

Japanese: オレ、とにかく スタジオに行こうとしたんだ。
English: I was going for the studio.

Japanese: とちゅう、３０分ぐらい道に まよっちゃったんだけど。
English: I got kinda lost on the way, though. For about 30 minutes.

Japanese: スタジオのところに出たら、 トノサマンがいたんだ！
English: When I came out by the studio, there was the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: オレ、うれしくて。 見てたら、悪人が出てきてさあ。
English: It totally rocked! Right before my eyes, out came the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマンは‥‥モチロン、 その悪人をたおしたよ！
English: Of course, the Steel Samurai took him down! Pow!

Japanese: もしカメラを持っていれば、 シャッターチャンスだったよ。
English: If I had my camera with me, that woulda been the time for a shot, I tell you.

Japanese: （それを見て、コワくなって 家に帰ったわけか‥‥）
English: (I guess seeing the killing freaked him out and he went home...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 相手は少年よ。
English: Remember, he's a child.

Japanese: あまりキツく言わずに、 でもヨウシャしちゃダメよ。
English: Use gentle words... but be firm.

Japanese: （‥‥そんなムチャな‥‥）
English: (Easy for you to say...)

Japanese: そんなにトノサマンが 好きなんだ。
English: You really like the Steel Samurai, huh?

Japanese: アッタリマエだろ！
English: Course I do!

Japanese: オレ、テレビはゼンブ、 ビデオに録画してるし、
English: I've taped every show!

Japanese: デパートのショーも、 ゼッタイに行くんだ！
English: And I go to all the live performances!

Japanese: たいしたもんだね。
English: That's impressive.

Japanese: 遊園地とかにも行くの？
English: Have you been to the amusement park?

Japanese: アッタリマエだろ！
English: Course! Duh!

Japanese: オレのお父さん、 つきあいがいいんだから！
English: My Dad always takes me!

Japanese: （“オトウサン”か。 タイヘンな職業だよな‥‥）
English: (Poor, poor Dad...)

Japanese: スタジオに行ったのは、 あの日が初めてだったの？
English: Was that day the first time you'd been to the studio?

Japanese: 実は、何回か行ったことが あるんだけどさ。
English: Uh, actually I've been a couple of times.

Japanese: まだ、リハーサルの日に 当たったことがないんだよ！
English: I never manage to get there on a rehearsal day!

Japanese: 警備員のオバサンは うるさいしさあ‥‥。
English: And that security lady's always yelling...

Japanese: ほほお。‥‥では、つづきを。
English: Right, right. Please continue.

Japanese: 言われなくても話してやるよ。
English: Chill, pops! I'm getting to it!

Japanese: 人の話のコシを折るなよな！
English: How am I supposed to talk with you cuttin' me off all the time? Geez!
"Off the path"...?

Japanese: うん。あの撮影所、山を切り開いて 作っただろ？
English: Yeah, you know how the studios are in a valley there?

Japanese: だから、ちょっと道をはずれると、 もう林になってるんだよ。
English: If you go off the path a little, there's woods. That's where I was!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどね。
English: I see.

Japanese: それでさあ。
English: Anyhow...

Japanese: スタジオに行くとちゅう、 何か変わったことはなかった？
English: Did nothing unusual happen on your way to the studio?

Japanese: “変わったこと”？
English: "Unusual"?

Japanese: たとえば、こんなサルは見た？
English: Did you see this monkey by any chance?

Japanese: ああ。“サルマゲどん”ね。
English: Oh, you mean Mr. Monkey?

Japanese: そいつは見たけど。 クビは折れてなかったよ！
English: Yeah I saw him, but his head wasn't busted!

Japanese: スタジオには、すぐ 行けたのか‥‥な？
English: So you went straight to the studio?

Japanese: 林の中でまよったの？
English: You got lost in the woods?

Japanese: そうだよ。もう、どこに向かって 歩いてるのか、わかんなくて。
English: Yeah. I couldn't figure out which way I was walking.

Japanese: スタジオの青いトビラが見えた ときは、ホント、うれしかったよ。
English: Man, was I relieved when I saw the blue studio doors.

Japanese: それでさぁ。
English: But guess what happened next!

Japanese: トノサマンが？
English: The Steel Samurai?

Japanese: うん。スタジオの門の ところにいたんだ。
English: Yeah, he was standing by the studio gate.

Japanese: ちょっと門のところで 何か考えていたみたいだけど、
English: It looked like he was thinking about something before going in.

Japanese: そのうち、門を開けて、 中に入っていったんだよ。
English: Then he opened up the gate and went inside.

Japanese: ふうん。‥‥それから？
English: I see. What then?

Japanese: “悪人”って、どんなヤツだった？
English: What sort of "bad guy" was it?

Japanese: ‥‥人間だったよ。 背が高くて、ヤセたヤツ。
English: A guy. A person. Kinda tall. Skinny.

Japanese: 男？　女？
English: Are you sure it was a guy?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... わかんない。‥‥遠かったし。
English: I dunno. He was kinda far away.

Japanese: （なんだよ。‥‥ハッキリ しないな‥‥）
English: (Huh, he's being awfully vague...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... その悪人は、どうなった？
English: What happened to the bad guy?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ええと‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: うん‥‥。動かなくなっちゃった。 死んだみたいに、ピクリとも。
English: Well, he kinda didn't move. Not a bit. Like he was dead.

Japanese: さすが、プロのアクションスター だよね！
English: I-I guess he's a real pro! What a great actor!

Japanese: そ、そうだね。
English: Y-yes, I suppose he was.

Japanese: “もし”カメラを持っていれば？
English: "If" you had your camera?

Japanese: なんだ、カメラ、なかったの？
English: You mean, you weren't carrying your camera then?

Japanese: そ、そうだよ。
English: N-no, I wasn't.

Japanese: オ、オレだって、いつも カメラ持ってるわけじゃないさ！
English: I-it's not like I have it all the time!
(Really...?)

Japanese: 九太くん。‥‥今の話、 ちょっとおかしいな。
English: Cody, what you just said seems, well, a little strange.

Japanese: きみ。さっきは、いつでも デジカメは持ち歩く、って、
English: Didn't you say before that you always bring your digital camera wherever you go?

Japanese: ハッキリ、そう言ってたじゃない。
English: You were quite clear about that.

Japanese: え！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: ダメだよ。ウソついちゃあ！
English: Cody, you shouldn't lie here. You understand that, right?

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥ちょっといいですかな？
English: Mr. Wright! A word with you...

Japanese: （しまった。 ‥‥キツく言いすぎたか？）
English: (Uh oh... was I putting the pressure on too much?)

Japanese: なんですか？ “でじかめ”って？
English: What is this "digital" camera contraption you're talking about?

Japanese: えーと。‥‥“デジタル・カメラ” のことです。
English: It's umm... a digital camera, Your Honor.

Japanese: 最近はやっている、 カメラの一種です。
English: It's kind of a new sort of camera.

Japanese: （セツメイのしようがない‥‥）
English: (How do I explain that!?)

Japanese: なるほど。
English: I see.

Japanese: とにかく。九太くん。
English: Anyway, Cody...

Japanese: きみが撮影所に行くとしたら、 カメラをわすれるはずがない！
English: I can't believe you wouldn't bring your camera on a trip to the studios!

Japanese: ‥‥持っていたんだろう？
English: You did bring it, didn't you?

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 弁護人ッ！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 少年をオドすとは、 見下げはてた男だな！
English: How cruel you are, to terrorize a poor child so!

Japanese: 少年だろうが検事だろうが、 ウソはよくない！
English: I don't care if he's a child or a prosecuting attorney! No one should lie in court!

Japanese: なんでそこに“検事”が 出てくるのだ‥‥。
English: What do you mean "or a prosecuting attorney"!?

Japanese: どうなのかな、九太くん。
English: Well, Cody?

Japanese: な、なんだよ！
English: Wh-what!

Japanese: ああ。たしかにカメラは 持ってたよ！
English: Yeah, so I had my camera. So what!?

Japanese: それがなんだってんだよぉ！
English: You got a problem with that!?

Japanese: じゃあ、そのカメラは？
English: So, you did have a camera?

Japanese: 使わなかったのかな？
English: And did you use this camera?

Japanese: つ、使うわけないよ。
English: W-why would I use it?

Japanese: ‥‥オレ、ムチュウで 見てたから‥‥。
English: I... I was too busy watching.

Japanese: ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では、その“見ていたこと”を 《証言》してもらいましょうか。
English: Very well. Please testify to the court about what you were so busy watching.

Japanese: ～見ていたことについて～
English: -- What I saw --

Japanese: た、たしかにカメラは 持ってたけど。
English: Y-yeah, I had my camera with me.

Japanese: もう、イッシュンも目が はなせなかったんだ。
English: But I was glued to the action! I couldn't take my eyes off it!

Japanese: トノサマンに悪人が おそいかかってさあ！
English: The Steel Samurai, he goes for the bad guy... wham!

Japanese: そして‥‥悪人は、 動かなくなったんだ！
English: Then... then the bad guy stopped moving!

Japanese: 強いよなぁ！ やっぱり、トノサマンは！
English: He's so strong! The Steel Samurai rules!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あれ。もう終わりですか。
English: Ah? Is that all?

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶんアッサリした 証言でしたね。
English: Well, that was brief.

Japanese: ‥‥では、《尋問》を。
English: Mr. Wright, your cross- examination.

Japanese: ～見ていたことについて～
English: -- What I saw --

Japanese: た、たしかにカメラは 持ってたけど。
English: Y-yeah, I had my camera with me.

Japanese: もう、イッシュンも目が はなせなかったんだ。
English: But I was glued to the action! I couldn't take my eyes off it!

Japanese: トノサマンに悪人が おそいかかってさあ！
English: The Steel Samurai, he goes for the bad guy... wham!

Japanese: そして‥‥悪人は、 動かなくなったんだ！
English: Then... then the bad guy stopped moving!

Japanese: 強いよなぁ！ やっぱり、トノサマンは！
English: He's so strong! The Steel Samurai rules!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、不自然な 感じがするんだよな‥‥）
English: (Something about his account doesn't seem right...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: それは、あの子の証言に、 アイマイなところがあるからよ。
English: It doesn't seem right because he's being deliberately vague!

Japanese: そこをトコトンゆさぶって、 ハッキリさせることね‥‥！
English: Press him on the facts, Phoenix! Find out what he saw!

Japanese: じゃあ、なんで 使わなかったのかな？
English: So, why didn't you use it?

Japanese: これからそれを言おうとしている。
English: He's getting to that!

Japanese: おちついて、聞きたまえ。
English: Relax and listen.

Japanese: ‥‥えーと。
English: Umm...

Japanese: トノサマンのアクションって、 すごくカッコイイからさ！
English: I didn't use it 'cause I was too busy watching the real thing!

Japanese: じゃあ、トノサマンが戦ってるのを ずっと見ていたんだ。
English: So you were watching the Steel Samurai. Did you watch the whole fight?

Japanese: そ、そりゃあそうさ！
English: Y-yeah, of course!

Japanese: 目をはなさなかったんだね？
English: You didn't turn away, not even once?

Japanese: う‥‥うん！
English: N... no!

Japanese: じゃあ、聞かせてくれるかな。 ‥‥トノサマンの戦いのこと。
English: Okay, then tell us exactly what happened.

Japanese: い、いいぜ！
English: Y-yeah! I'm getting to that!

Japanese: 悪人のほうから、 おそいかかったんだね？
English: So the bad guy attacked him first?

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: Yeah!

Japanese: どんなヤツだった？ その“悪人”って？
English: What sort of person was this "bad guy"?

Japanese: さっき言わなかったっけ？ 人間だよ、ニンゲン。
English: I told you, a person. He was a person.

Japanese: どんなカッコウをしてた？
English: How was he dressed?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... よく、おぼえてないや。
English: I kinda don't remember.

Japanese: オレ、トノサマンしか 見てなかったから。
English: I was too busy watching the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: なるほど。 ‥‥それで、どうなったかな？
English: I see. And what happened next?

Japanese: どうして？
English: Why?

Japanese: え？　‥‥何が“どうして” なんだよ？
English: Huh? Whaddya mean, "why"?

Japanese: 悪人は、どうして動かなく なったのかな？
English: Why wasn't the bad guy moving?

Japanese: そ、そりゃあ、トノサマンが やっつけたからだよ。
English: W-well 'cause the Steel Samurai brought him to justice!

Japanese: だから、どうやって やっつけたのかな‥‥？
English: And how exactly did he do that?

Japanese: ど、どうやって、って‥‥。
English: H-how did he do it?

Japanese: トノサマン・キックに トノサマン・パンチ、
English: With a Samurai Kick! And a Samurai Punch!

Japanese: トノサマン・チョップに トノサマン・ビンタ‥‥
English: Samurai Chop! ... Samurai Slap!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんな感じかな。
English: S-something like that.

Japanese: （どうも、このあたりが スッキリしないんだよな‥‥）
English: (He's still being vague about this...)

Japanese: なんだよォ！　その目は！
English: What! Don't gimme that look, pops!

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう？）
English: (How should I play this?)

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥）
English: (Yeah...)

Japanese: （ヘタに揺さぶって、裁判長の 心証を悪くするのはマズい）
English: (If I press too hard now, the judge might keep me from continuing.)

Japanese: （ここは、ようすを見ておこう）
English: (I'll sit back and see how the rest of this goes.)

Japanese: 悪人をたおしたあと、 九太くんはどうしたの？
English: What did you do after the bad guy fell, Cody?
Um, actually...

Japanese: その戦いが、すごいハクリョク だったから、コワくなっちゃって。
English: The fight was so... so intense that I got kinda scared.

Japanese: そのまますぐ、ウチに 帰ったんだ‥‥。
English: So I went straight home.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥この九太くんの 証言なんだけど‥‥）
English: (Something isn't adding up here...)

Japanese: 九太くん。
English: Cody.

Japanese: な、なんだよ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: きみは最初に、こう言ったね。
English: Something's bothering me.

Japanese: トノサマンから“イッシュンも目を はなせなかった”って。
English: Before you said that you "couldn't take your eyes off" the action.

Japanese: そ‥‥それがなんだってんだよ。
English: Y-yeah? So what?

Japanese: なのになぜ、一番カンジンな部分 を見ていないんだい？
English: Yet you missed the most important part!

Japanese: 少年は、キチンと証言を‥‥！
English: What is the meaning of this? The witness has stated what he saw quite clearly...

Japanese: キチンと証言していないことは、 あなたにもわかっているでしょう？
English: You know as well as I do that he's being vague!

Japanese: 殺人犯は、ふつう “トノサマン・ビンタ”はしない！
English: Tell me, what kind of murderer uses a "Samurai Slap"!?

Japanese: ぐぐぅ！
English: M-mmph!

Japanese: つまり！
English: My point is this:

Japanese: 九太くんは、トノサマンの戦いの、 一番重要なところ、
English: Cody, you may have seen some of the Steel Samurai's fight...

Japanese: テキにトドメを刺すところを 見ていないのです！
English: But you missed the most important part: the killing blow!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: しかし、それはタイヘン おかしなことです！
English: How could this be?

Japanese: その理由を、あなたは 説明できますか？
English: Can you explain how he might have missed something so vital?

Japanese: え‥‥、そ、それは‥‥ ちょっと‥‥。
English: Um, well... er, that's the thing...

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: あなたは今、とても いいセンを行っているわ！
English: I believe you're on the right track with this!

Japanese: “なぜ、九太くんは戦いの クライマックスを見てないか？”
English: Think: why didn't Cody see the climax of the fight?

Japanese: その理由もわかるはずよ！ あなたなら！
English: I know you! You can figure this out!

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥答えてもらいましょう。
English: Mr. Wright, your answer!

Japanese: トノサマンの 大ファンである少年が、
English: We have ascertained that this young boy is a great fan of the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: なぜ、戦いのクライマックスを 見ていなかったのですか！
English: Why wouldn't he watch the climax of the fight?

Japanese: 九太くんは、見ていることが できなかったのです！
English: Because he couldn't watch it!

Japanese: だってそれは、殺人の瞬間 だったわけですからね！
English: How could he calmly watch a murder taking place at his age!

Japanese: ちょっと待ってほしい。
English: Just a moment, please...

Japanese: 今までの証言の内容から、
English: One thing has become quite clear in this boy's testimony.

Japanese: 少年は、“殺人を目撃した” と思っていないのは、あきらかだ。
English: He did not believe he was witnessing a murder!

Japanese: 彼は、あくまでトノサマンの 戦いを見たつもりでいる。
English: He was watching the Steel Samurai fight, like on TV.

Japanese: ならば、戦いがリアルであるほど、 よけいに目をそらすハズがない！
English: The more real the fight, the more he would have watched!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。
English: Umm... huh.

Japanese: そのとおりですな。
English: I have to agree with Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: では、弁護人。‥‥もう一度 答えてもらいましょう。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. Do you have another explanation?

Japanese: トノサマンがトドメをさしたとき、
English: He missed the climax of the fight...

Japanese: おそらく九太くんは、 他のものを見ていたのです！
English: because he was looking at something else! Probably!

Japanese: “おそらく”？
English: "Probably"?

Japanese: “他のもの”？
English: "Something else"?

Japanese: 成歩堂！ ‥‥答えてもらおう！
English: Mr. Wright, explain yourself!

Japanese: あの少年にとって、トノサマンの 戦いよりも気を引くものなどない！
English: What could possibly distract this boy from seeing his idol, the Steel Samurai, fight!?

Japanese: いったい、何を見ていた と言うのか！
English: What do you claim he was looking at?

Japanese: ぐぐうっ！ （‥‥思いつかない‥‥）
English: O-ooof! (C'mon, think of something... think of something... Nothing.)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥コンキョのない発言は、 ひかえてください！
English: Please refrain from making baseless claims!

Japanese: は‥‥はい。 スミマセン‥‥。
English: Y-yes... Sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおしてください。
English: Do you have another explanation for this?

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥証拠品があるんです。
English: Your Honor, I have evidence.

Japanese: “証拠品”‥‥ですか？
English: "Evidence"...?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: “なぜ九太くんは、戦いから 目をそらしたか”‥‥？
English: Why did Cody look away from the fight?

Japanese: その証拠は‥‥！
English: Here's my proof!

Japanese: 証拠は？
English: Proof?
Um, uh...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください。 ‥‥もう一度、考えます。
English: Can I... do that again?

Japanese: （さすがに、 これは違うだろう‥‥）
English: (This doesn't prove anything...!)

Japanese: ハッ！　自信がなくなったか！
English: Hah! Lost your nerve, have you?

Japanese: ‥‥しかたがありませんね。 もう一度、聞きます。
English: Very well... I'll ask you the question again.

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: Here's my proof!

Japanese: なんですか‥‥？ そのカメラが、どういう‥‥？
English: What's that? The camera...?

Japanese: このカメラは、最近、 買ってもらったばかりです。
English: The witness stated that he recently received this camera.

Japanese: それは知っています。
English: Yes, I'm aware of that.

Japanese: まだ彼は、カメラのあつかいに 慣れていません。
English: He wasn't entirely familiar with its operation.

Japanese: それもわかっています。 ‥‥あっ！
English: I'm aware of that, too. Ah!

Japanese: 九太くんは、トノサマンが トドメをさす瞬間、
English: Correct! Why would Cody be looking somewhere else at the critical moment?

Japanese: カメラを見ていたんですよ！
English: Because he was looking at his camera!

Japanese: もちろん、写真を撮るためにね！
English: He was trying to take a picture!

Japanese: あッ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: どうやら、図星だったみたいだね。
English: Bullseye!

Japanese: なんなんだよ、オマエ‥‥。 オレをイジメて、たのしいかよ！
English: W-what's your problem, pops! You got a thing for picking on little kids!?

Japanese: （イジメているわけじゃ ないんだけど）
English: (Pressing, not picking...)

Japanese: よくやったわ、なるほどくん！
English: Good job, Phoenix!

Japanese: 九太くんは、ウソをついていた。
English: Cody was lying, clearly.

Japanese: 目の前でトノサマンが戦っていて、 彼が写真を撮らないなんて、
English: Smart of you to realize there was no way he could just stand there, watching his hero...

Japanese: あり得ないものね。
English: and not take a picture!

Japanese: はい！
English: Right!

Japanese: いいかい。
English: Cody?

Japanese: きみがトノサマンの戦いから 目をそらした理由は、ただ１つ。
English: There was only one reason why you would have looked away from the fight.

Japanese: それを写真に撮ろうと思って、 カメラをいじっていたからだ。
English: And that was because you wanted to take a picture.

Japanese: 買ったばかりで、まだ操作に 慣れていなかったから、
English: But having just received your camera, you weren't used to using it yet!

Japanese: 決定的なシュンカンを 見のがしちゃったんだろう？
English: So, you missed the climax of the fight. Correct?
Yeah.

Japanese: さあ、なるほどくん。
English: Well, Phoenix.

Japanese: この子は、まだ何かを かくしているわ！
English: I'll bet you anything he's hiding more than that.

Japanese: もう一度、《証言》させるの！
English: Have him testify again!

Japanese: は、はい。 （千尋さん、コワい‥‥）
English: R-right. (Mia means business!)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 九太くんに、もう一度 《証言》をおねがいします。
English: The defense would like to request that Cody Hackins testify once more.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。
English: V-very well.

Japanese: では、九太くん。
English: Cody?

Japanese: そのカメラと、写真を撮らなかった ことについて、
English: Could you please tell us about your camera...

Japanese: もう少し、くわしく話して もらえるかな？
English: And about why you didn't take a picture of the fight?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: （オトナの世界は キビシイんだぞ‥‥！）
English: (Welcome to the real world, kid!)

Japanese: ～“写真は撮らなかった”？～
English: -- No Photo? --

Japanese: オマエの言うとおりだよ。
English: Yeah, you're right, pops.

Japanese: トノサマンが、悪人の手を ふりほどいたから、
English: The Steel Samurai had just escaped from the clutches of the villain.

Japanese: オレ、写真を撮ろうと思って、 カメラをかまえたんだけど。
English: So I held up my camera to take a picture!

Japanese: レンズが開かなくって。 ちょっと間に合わなかったんだ。
English: But the lens wouldn't open in time, so I missed it.

Japanese: そ‥‥それで、オシマイかな。
English: Th-that's all that happened. Yup.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: この証言に、何か おかしなところはありますか？
English: Anything strange in that testimony, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりません。
English: I'm not sure...

Japanese: でも、《尋問》は させてもらいます。
English: But I'd like to proceed with the cross-examination anyway.

Japanese: ～“写真は撮らなかった”？～
English: -- No Photo? --

Japanese: オマエの言うとおりだよ。
English: Yeah, you're right, pops.

Japanese: トノサマンが、悪人の手を ふりほどいたから、
English: The Steel Samurai had just escaped from the clutches of the villain.

Japanese: オレ、写真を撮ろうと思って、 カメラをかまえたんだけど。
English: So I held up my camera to take a picture!

Japanese: レンズが開かなくって。 ちょっと間に合わなかったんだ。
English: But the lens wouldn't open in time, so I missed it.

Japanese: そ‥‥それで、オシマイかな。
English: Th-that's all that happened. Yup.

Japanese: 少しおくれて何枚か撮ったけど、 データはもう、消しちゃったよ。
English: I took a few shots, but it was too late, so I erased 'em.

Japanese: （こんなみじかい証言で、 何かおかしなところがあるか？）
English: (How can I pick holes in such a short testimony?)

Japanese: 何かあるはずよ。
English: There has to be something.

Japanese: 探り出すの。 ‥‥あの子のホンネを！
English: Dig up the dirt, Phoenix! Find out what this kid's all about!
"Pops"...?

Japanese: なんだよ！ トンガリアタマのオッサン！
English: Yeah, I called you "pops," you spiky-haired geezer!

Japanese: オ‥‥オッサン‥‥？
English: G-geezer...?

Japanese: 私は“おニイさん”だ。
English: I'm "Mr. Edgeworth."

Japanese: “オジさん”ということで。
English: And I guess I'm the "bearded gentleman"...

Japanese: オトナは、メンツで 生きているからイヤだよ‥‥。
English: That's what I hate about grown-ups. You're all so self-conscious!

Japanese: もう、ヨウシャしないぞ！ ‥‥証人！　つづきを！
English: Okay, off with the kid gloves! Testify, witness, testify!

Japanese: ひひィィッ！
English: Yipes!

Japanese: そこまでは見ていたんだ。
English: So you did see the beginning of the fight?

Japanese: うん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: 何か、気がついたことは なかった？
English: Did you notice anything unusual?

Japanese: ‥‥う‥‥ううんと‥‥。
English: Umm... well... uh...

Japanese: ‥‥いつものトノサマンと、 ちょっと動きが違ったみたい‥‥。
English: I guess the Steel Samurai was moving a little strange...

Japanese: （動きが‥‥違った？）
English: (Moving... strangely?)

Japanese: なんで写真が撮れなかったの？
English: Why couldn't you take a picture?

Japanese: 新しいカメラ、ボタンが いっぱいあって、ややこしいんだ。
English: My new camera has too many buttons. I couldn't figure it out.

Japanese: シャッターを 押そうと思ったんだけど‥‥、
English: I tried to take a picture, I really tried.

Japanese: レンズが開いたら、 何が見えたの？
English: When the lens opened, what did you see?

Japanese: ‥‥えーと‥‥ たおれた人と、トノサマン。
English: Uh... well, a person on the ground. And the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: “オシマイ”‥‥？
English: That's all?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだよ！ 写真は、撮らなかったんだ！
English: Y-yeah! I told you, I didn't take a picture!

Japanese: （ぼくだったら、写真、 撮ると思うけどなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I would have, once I got that lens open...)

Japanese: （この少年は、本当に写真を 撮らなかったのだろうか？）
English: (Did he really not take a picture? Not even one?)

Japanese: （九太くん、半分泣いてるしなあ）
English: (He looks ready to burst into tears...)

Japanese: （ここは、カンベンしておいて あげるか‥‥）
English: (I'd better lay off him for now.)

Japanese: （九太くんだったら、シャッターを 押さないはずはない！）
English: (Nah, he definitely would have taken a picture.) (There's got to be something here I can use!)

Japanese: 九太くん！
English: Cody!

Japanese: ぼくには、何もかも わかってるんだよ！
English: Listen up, Cody Hackins. I know exactly what happened that day.

Japanese: きみは、写真を撮ったはずだ！
English: You took a picture!

Japanese: ‥‥あッ！
English: ... Hey!

Japanese: （思ったとおり、か‥‥）
English: (Bullseye...?)

Japanese: ど、どうしてわかるんだよ‥‥？
English: H-how did you know!?

Japanese: おニイさんには、九太くんの ウソが、みんなわかるんだよ。
English: I see through all your lies Cody Hackins. It's one of my powers!

Japanese: ‥‥す、すげェや！
English: W-wow!

Japanese: （お。‥‥なんか、いいキモチ）
English: (This feels great!)

Japanese: オレ‥‥、 たしかに撮ったよ‥‥写真。
English: Y-yeah... Yeah, I took a picture.

Japanese: では、そのように証言を 変えてもらいましょうか。
English: Perhaps you can change your testimony to reflect this, Cody?

Japanese: データを‥‥“消した”？
English: You "erased" them?

Japanese: そ、そうだよ！
English: Y-yeah!

Japanese: なんで？　大好きなトノサマンが 勝負に勝った写真なのに‥‥。
English: Why would you erase a picture of your beloved Steel Samurai, victorious?

Japanese: べ‥‥べつに。
English: Well... y'know.

Japanese: とっておいても、 ‥‥しょうがないだろ？
English: W-why would I want to keep a picture like that?

Japanese: （‥‥ヘソを曲げたか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... he's becoming less and less cooperative.)

Japanese: （そろそろ、ムジュンでも たたきつけてやらないとな‥‥）
English: (I better find a clear contradiction soon... or some solid evidence.)

Japanese: 九太くん！
English: Cody!

Japanese: な、な、な、なんだよ！
English: Wh-wh-what!?

Japanese: さっきからアンタに名前を呼ばれる と、ロクなことがないや！
English: Man, every time you say "Cody" you follow it with something bad.

Japanese: 昨日は、これ、どうもありがとう。
English: I just wanted to thank you for giving me this the other day.
Huh? O-oh... right.

Japanese: きみ、昨日、こんなことを 言ってたよね？
English: What was it you told me then?

Japanese: オレ、トノサマンのショーには、 かならず行くんだよ！
English: I go to every live performance!

Japanese: でさ。トノサマンが勝った瞬間を、 こうして写真に撮るのさ！
English: I always take a picture when the Steel Samurai lands the final blow! Whizzam!

Japanese: １つ残らず、ぜーんぶ写真に 撮ってるんだぜ。‥‥カンペキに！
English: I got 'em all! I never missed one! A perfect collection!

Japanese: いいかい。
English: Cody.

Japanese: もし、トノサマンが戦いに勝った シーンをきみが撮ったのなら、
English: Did you really get a picture of the Steel Samurai standing victorious over his foe?

Japanese: それを消すハズがない。
English: If you did, I find it hard to believe that you would just erase it!

Japanese: このアルバムに、かざらなければ ならないからね！
English: Wouldn't you keep it for your album?

Japanese: ああああッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: そのアルバム、なんですか？
English: What exactly is this album?

Japanese: これは、“栄光の足跡”といって、
English: It's called "Path to Glory."

Japanese: 九太くんが、トノサマンの写真を 撮って、集めたものです。
English: It's a collection of pictures of the Steel Samurai, all taken by Cody Hackins.

Japanese: これまでの、トノサマンの勝利の シーンが、すべて撮影されている！
English: He claims it's a perfect collection of every battle the Steel Samurai has won!

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: しかし！
English: Don't you find this very odd, Your Honor?

Japanese: 事件当日のトノサマンの写真は、 このアルバムには、ない！
English: Among all his pictures of the Steel Samurai, none were taken that day!

Japanese: 静粛に！
English: Order!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥それは、なぜでしょう？
English: Mr. Wright. Have you an explanation?

Japanese: トノサマンが誰かをやっつけた のならば‥‥、
English: If the Steel Samurai had just defeated his adversary...

Japanese: そのアルバムに、写真が なければおかしい‥‥。
English: I certainly would expect to see a picture of it in this album...

Japanese: そのとおりです！
English: Exactly my point, Your Honor!
(No... wait... no way!)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix?

Japanese: どうやら私たちは、 たどりついたみたいね。
English: I think we've finally discovered the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥とんでもない“真相”に。
English: And what a truth it is!

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia... are we sure about this?

Japanese: じゃあ、やっぱり‥‥？
English: Did he actually...?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: Yes.

Japanese: あなたが考えていること ‥‥それはたぶん、正しいわ‥‥。
English: I'm pretty sure what you're thinking is right.

Japanese: 教えてあげなさい。 裁判長に‥‥真相を、ね。
English: Tell them, Phoenix. Tell them what the truth is.

Japanese: 弁護人！ どういうことですか？
English: Mr. Wright! Please explain to the court what is going on here!

Japanese: アルバムには、なぜ事件当日の 写真がないのですか？
English: Why was there no picture in the album from the day of the murder?

Japanese: なぜ、少年は写真のデータを 消してしまったのですか？
English: Why would the boy have erased the photos he took?

Japanese: おそらく、九太くんは、 “芸術家”なのです！
English: I believe that Cody Hackins is what we call an "artiste."

Japanese: “ゲージュツカ”？
English: An... "artiste"...?

Japanese: 彼は、撮った写真が 気に入らなかったのです！
English: He didn't like the photos he had taken!

Japanese: ピントがボケていたか、 センターがズレていたか‥‥。
English: They were blurry, or maybe his framing was off...

Japanese: とにかく、そういう理由で 彼は、データを消したのです。
English: Anyway, for some reason, he erased the data!

Japanese: そうなのかな、九太くん？
English: Is that right, Cody?

Japanese: “センターがズレていた” って、なんのことだよ！
English: Wh-what do you mean "framing was off"!?

Japanese: ‥‥違うみたいですね。
English: It seems the only thing "off" is your guesswork, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Um, uh...

Japanese: じゃあ、きっと、本当の答えは‥‥
English: Yes, well, I'm sure the real reason is...

Japanese: なんですか？ 聞かせてもらいましょう。
English: Yes? The real reason is?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: Simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: 九太くんは、カメラのあつかいに 慣れていなかった。
English: Cody wasn't used to using his new camera.

Japanese: だから、間違ってデータを 消してしまったのです！
English: He erased those photos he took by mistake!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待って、 なるほどくん。
English: W-wait a second, Phoenix.

Japanese: そんな答えじゃ、 何も状況は変わらないじゃない！
English: If that's your answer, we're back where we started!

Japanese: え？　‥‥違うんですか？
English: Huh? You mean, that wasn't it?

Japanese: 弁護人！　コソコソ話はしない！
English: Mr. Wright! If you have something to say to the court, speak up!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Um, well...

Japanese: すみません。‥‥あの、もう一度、 発言させてください。
English: S-sorry, Your Honor. Can I change my answer?

Japanese: ふう‥‥。やれやれ‥‥。
English: Hmm... *sigh*

Japanese: その理由は、１つしか 考えられません！
English: I can think of only one reason, your honor.

Japanese: “トノサマンは勝たなかった”
English: The Steel Samurai didn't win!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、九太くんは 写真のデータを消したんです。
English: That's why Cody deleted those photos.

Japanese: トノサマンが‥‥“負けた”？
English: You mean the Steel Samurai... lost?

Japanese: そうなんだろ？　九太くん。
English: Well, Cody? I'm right, aren't I?

Japanese: トノサマン、ほんとうは、悪人に やられちゃったんじゃないか？
English: Tell the truth. The bad guy defeated the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: ナ、ナ、ナ、ナニ言ってんだ！
English: N-n-n-no way! No!

Japanese: ト‥‥トノサマンは負けないんだ！ ‥‥ゼ、ゼッタイ！
English: I-it's impossible! Th-the Steel Samurai never loses!

Japanese: 誰にも、負けたりなんか、 するもんかァッ！
English: He never loses to anyone! Ever!

Japanese: 裁判長‥‥。
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: 今の九太くんのコトバが、 すべてを物語っています。
English: The witness has revealed everything with his words.

Japanese: “写真を撮らなかった”と ウソをついた理由‥‥、
English: There was a reason why he lied and told us he didn't take a picture.

Japanese: そして、せっかく撮った写真の データを消した理由‥‥。
English: The same reason he erased his precious photos!

Japanese: 九太くんにとって、トノサマンは ゼッタイ負けてはならなかった。
English: For Cody, it was inconceivable that the Steel Samurai could be defeated.

Japanese: しかし九太くんは、とんでもない シーンを目撃してしまったのです！
English: However, Cody witnessed the impossible!

Japanese: そう！　トノサマンが 戦いに敗れるシーンを！
English: He saw the Steel Samurai lose!

Japanese: 九太くんは、それを認める ワケにはいかなかった！
English: Yet to admit what he saw would destroy everything he believed in!

Japanese: だから、“トノサマンが勝った ところを見た”と‥‥
English: That's why he lied and said the Steel Samurai won.

Japanese: ウソをついてしまったのです！
English: He couldn't handle the truth!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order! I will have order!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうなんですか！ 証人‥‥九太くん！
English: W-witness! I mean, Cody! Is this true!?

Japanese: あ、あなたはいったい‥‥ 何を見たんですか！
English: W-what did you see? Tell the court what you saw!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ うわああああああああああああああ ああああああああああああああん！
English: Uwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: ‥‥九太くん。
English: Cody...

Japanese: あの日、きみが見たのは、 トノサマンが負けるところだった。
English: That day, you saw the Steel Samurai lose a fight.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだろう？
English: Right?

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！
English: Waaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: そうだよ！
English: Okay okay!

Japanese: そうなんだよ！
English: Y-you're right!

Japanese: トノサマンが ‥‥た、たおれちゃって！
English: Th-the Steel Samurai f-fell down...

Japanese: う‥‥うごかなく なっちゃったんだ‥‥
English: Th-then he didn't m-move...

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！
English: Waaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 成歩堂！　い、いったいこれは、 なんのさわぎだッ！
English: What kind of stunt are you trying to pull, Wright!?

Japanese: トノサマンは、 “殺人者”なのだぞ！
English: The Steel Samurai was the murderer, not the victim!

Japanese: しかし証言によれば、トノサマンは たおれて動かなくなっている！
English: Yet according to your witness's testimony, the Steel Samurai was the one who fell!

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥どういうことなんですか！
English: Mr. Wright! What's going on?

Japanese: ぼくたちは、大きなカン違いを していたことになります‥‥。
English: Apparently, we have all made a serious error.

Japanese: カン違いだと‥‥？
English: An... error?

Japanese: それはいったい、 どういうことかッ！
English: What's this all about!?

Japanese: つまり、被害者はイブクロさん だったのです！
English: Quite simply, the victim was Jack Hammer!
Umm... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: それはみんな、知ってます。
English: We all know that.

Japanese: はい。
English: Right.

Japanese: 自分が言ったことを、 もう一度、考えてごらんなさい。
English: Perhaps you'd like to reconsider your last statement?
I'm sorry.

Japanese: 大きなカン違いをしていたのは、 ぼくだったんですね‥‥。
English: It was I who was wrong.

Japanese: 成歩堂ッ！ ふざけるにも、ほどがあるぞ！
English: Okay, Wright! First you're right, now you're wrong!? Which is it!?

Japanese: し、しかし！　どこかに見落としが あるのは間違いない！
English: L-look, I'm sure there's something we're overlooking!

Japanese: ‥‥ならば、それを ズバリと言ってもらいたい！
English: If you're so sure, then tell us what it is!

Japanese: この事件では、被害者は いなかったのです！
English: There was no victim in this case!

Japanese: な、な、な、なんですとォ！
English: Wh-wh-what!? Has the defense taken leave of its senses!?

Japanese: たしかに、衣袋　武志は 死んでしまった。
English: Of course, Jack Hammer died.

Japanese: しかし、トノサマンの方も、 たおされているのです！
English: But the Steel Samurai fell too!

Japanese: これはもう、“引きわけ” ということに‥‥。
English: It was a draw!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あきれて、ちょっと コトバが見つからなかった。
English: Sorry. I was too shocked to say anything for a bit there.

Japanese: いいか！
English: Listen, Wright!

Japanese: この事件で死んだのは、 衣袋　武志、１人だけだッ！
English: Only one person died in this incident, and that is Jack Hammer!

Japanese: 被害者はいなかった、だと？
English: Yet now you claim there "was no victim"?

Japanese: 何年ニンゲンをやっているんだッ！
English: Are you insane?

Japanese: （うう‥‥そこまで言うか‥‥？）
English: (Uh oh, he's pulling out all the stops now.)

Japanese: わかってみれば カンタンなことです。
English: If you understand what really happened, it's actually quite simple.

Japanese: 戦った結果、トノサマンが たおれて、動かなくなった。
English: At the end of the fight, the Steel Samurai fell to the ground, and lay still.

Japanese: つまり、トノサマンは“殺人者” ではなく、“被害者”だった‥‥。
English: In other words, the Steel Samurai was not the killer, he was the victim!

Japanese: ということは！
English: Don't you see?

Japanese: トノサマンは、 衣袋　武志だったのです！
English: Jack Hammer was the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: では、この写真に写っている トノサマンも‥‥、
English: So, the Steel Samurai in this photograph...

Japanese: 被害者の、衣袋　武志が 中に入っていたと言うのですか！
English: You're saying the man in this costume was the victim, Mr. Jack Hammer?

Japanese: そういうことになります。
English: That's what I'm saying, Your Honor.

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、午前中、アクション シーンの打ち合わせをしていた。
English: Jack Hammer was present at the action scene run-through that morning.

Japanese: だから当然、荷星さんが足をケガ したコトを知っています。
English: Thus, he obviously knew about Will Powers's foot injury.

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！
English: B-but wait!

Japanese: イブクロさんは、第１スタジオに 行ったはずでは‥‥？
English: Hadn't Mr. Hammer gone to Studio One already?

Japanese: みんなそう思っていました。
English: That's what everyone thought!

Japanese: しかし、警備員のオバチャンは、 こう証言していた！
English: But remember what Ms. Oldbag said in her testimony?

Japanese: オバチャン、午後１時に 詰所に入ったわけョ。
English: On the day of the murder, I arrived at the guard station at 1:00 PM.

Japanese: イブクロちゃん？ ああ、見てないよ。
English: Did I see poor old Hammer? Nope.

Japanese: オバチャンが詰所にもどる前に、 もうスタジオへ行ったみたいだネ。
English: He'd already gone to the studio before I got back to the guard station.

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、昼食後、 スタッフエリアを去った。
English: Mr. Jack Hammer left the Employee Area after lunch.

Japanese: しかし、彼が第１スタジオに 行ったのは、誰も見ていない。
English: However, no one saw him going to Studio One. Nor was there a picture of him.

Japanese: 彼は、荷星さんが楽屋で ヒルネするのを待った。
English: He waited for Mr. Powers to take a nap in his dressing room.

Japanese: そして楽屋に忍び込み、 着ぐるみを盗んだのです。
English: Then he snuck into the dressing room and stole the Steel Samurai costume.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥ ... しかし！　いったい、なぜ 被害者は、そんなことを‥‥？
English: But why would the victim do such a thing?

Japanese: それは‥‥わかりませんけど。
English: I... don't know.
I get it!

Japanese: ‥‥？ ? オレ、トノサマンの動きが、 いつもと違うと思ったんだけど、
English: I thought that the Steel Samurai was moving strange.

Japanese: 中に入ってる人、違ったのか‥‥。
English: So it was a different person inside the suit!

Japanese: おじさん！
English: Pops!

Japanese: な、なんだい？
English: Huh, me? What?

Japanese: 実はオレ、１枚だけ 消していないデータがあるんだ。
English: Actually, there was one piece of data I saved.

Japanese: “でーた”？
English: Data?

Japanese: 写真だよ。デジカメの！
English: Yeah, a photo on my digital camera!

Japanese: ! な、なんだって！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: み、見せるのだッ！
English: Show us, quick!

Japanese: そ、そんなコワいカオ、 すんなよな‥‥。
English: No way man, not if you're gonna look at me like that!

Japanese: 早くするッ！
English: Now!

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: *sniff* Here... This is it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... これだけ見たのでは、トノサマンの 中に誰が入っていたのかは、
English: Well, looking at this it's still a little hard to say.

Japanese: ちょっと判断ができませんね。
English: I'm afraid that it could be anyone in that costume. Your opinion, Mr. Wright? I agree, Your Honor. This isn't decisive evidence.

Japanese: 証人。ザンネンですが、これは 証拠にはなりそうもありません。
English: I'm sorry, this doesn't look like it's the proof we need.

Japanese: お返ししておきましょう。
English: I'll give it back.

Japanese: 待って！ なるほどくん！
English: Wait, Phoenix!

Japanese: な、なんですか！
English: Mia? What?

Japanese: 写真を もう一度、よく見て！
English: Look at that photo once more!

Japanese: その写真は‥‥、
English: That's it Phoenix!

Japanese: この事件のすべてを物語る、 最強の証拠品よ！
English: That photo is all the evidence you need to win this trial!

Japanese: え‥‥ええええっ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: さ、裁判長！
English: Y-Your Honor!

Japanese: その写真、もう一度 見せてください！
English: May I see that photo once more please?

Japanese: は、はあ。 ‥‥かまいませんが。
English: C-certainly. I don't see why not.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 裁判長に教えてあげなさい！
English: Phoenix! Show him!

Japanese: この写真がはらんでいる、 決定的なムジュン点を！
English: There's a glaring, decisive inconsistency with the facts as we know them in that photo!

Japanese: （この写真で、ムジュンしている ポイントを示そう！）
English: (Find the inconsistency in the photo!)

Japanese: ここ、じゃないでしょうか。
English: I think it's here.

Japanese: いつにもまして、自信が なさそうな口ぶりですね。
English: You seem to be lacking even more confidence than usual.

Japanese: まあ、自信ありませんから。
English: I suppose that's because I'm not confident about it at all.

Japanese: ‥‥考えなおした方が いいようですね。
English: Maybe you want to rethink your case, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥そうします。
English: Yes, please, Your Honor.

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: It's right here!

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマン、ですか？
English: The Steel Samurai...?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥この写真の中で、 何かムジュンしそうなのは、
English: Yes, that's about the only thing in this picture that could be inconsistent.

Japanese: この人しかいませんから。
English: The Steel Samurai is inconsistent with... something.

Japanese: 成歩堂！
English: Wright!

Japanese: イイカゲンなことを 言わないでもらいたいッ！
English: This is no time for daydreaming and pointless pontification!

Japanese: 聞いている方の身にも なってくれ！
English: Think about your client, and us, who are forced to listen to your rambling!

Japanese: 弁護人。もう一度、 考えなおしなさい。
English: Mr. Wright, please rethink your position.

Japanese: 裁判長！ ここを見てください！
English: Your Honor! Look at this!

Japanese: ‥‥門柱、ですか‥‥。
English: I see... a gate?

Japanese: 重要なのは、門柱に書かれている 数字です。
English: Might I draw your attention to the number on that gate?

Japanese: ‥‥数字？
English: The number?

Japanese: これは‥‥“２”ですか？ ハッキリしませんが‥‥。
English: Ah yes. Well, it's hard to see, but it looks like a "2."

Japanese: ハッキリしているのは、 “１”ではない、ということです。
English: Clearly not a "1," Your Honor, correct?

Japanese: ああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: どうやらわかったようだね、 御剣検事。
English: I believe Mr. Edgeworth sees what I'm getting at.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥
English: B-but...

Japanese: そんな、バカなッ！
English: Th-that's impossible!

Japanese: え？　え？ ‥‥なんですか？
English: Hmm? Eh? What's this all about?

Japanese: 私にも、わかるように セツメイしてください！
English: Please explain so that I might be shocked along with the rest of the court.

Japanese: 上面図で説明しましょう。
English: I'll use the studio guidemap.

Japanese: 死体は、ここ。第１スタジオで 発見されました。
English: The body was found here, in Studio One.

Japanese: しかし、事件のとき撮影された 写真の門柱には、
English: However, what do we see in this photo taken at the time of the murder?

Japanese: “１”とは書かれていない。
English: It does NOT say "1" on that gate in the photo.

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 事件は、ほんとうは ここで起こったのです！
English: Here is the true scene of the crime!

Japanese: （裁判長に、ほんとうの 殺人現場を示そう！）
English: (Show the judge where the murder took place!)

Japanese: それは、ここだったのです！
English: It was here!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、門柱の数字と 何かカンケイがあるのですか。
English: What does that have to do with the number on the gate in the photograph...?

Japanese: ええ‥‥と。
English: Umm... well...

Japanese: そこまでは考えていませんでした。
English: Sorry, Your Honor, I guess I hadn't thought it that far through yet.

Japanese: そこまで考えて ポイントを指定してください。
English: Think it through before you point at the map this time, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: もう一度、示しなさい！
English: Once again! Where is the scene of this crime?

Japanese: ここです。第２スタジオ。
English: Here, at Studio Two.

Japanese: な‥‥、なるほど。 門柱に“２”とありますからな。
English: I see! That would explain the "2" on the gate in the photo.

Japanese: この第２スタジオには、 重要なポイントがあります。
English: Your Honor, I find it very significant that the murder took place in Studio Two!

Japanese: ここは、撮影には使って いないんですよ！
English: Studio Two is no longer being used for filming!

Japanese: そうですか。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥それが、何か？
English: And this is significant... why?

Japanese: （なんでぼくは、自分でも よくわからん発言をするんだ？）
English: (Good question! I really should think these things through more.)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、行ってみますか？
English: Would you like to take another stab at it? So to speak?

Japanese: はい。‥‥行ってみます。
English: S-stabbing away, Your Honor.

Japanese: コテージに通じる道は、 ふさがっていました！
English: The path to the trailer was blocked!

Japanese: それは聞いています。
English: So we have heard.

Japanese: 道がふさがったのは、 ２時１５分です。
English: The path was blocked at 2:15...

Japanese: つまり被害者は、それ以前に 第２スタジオに行ったんです！
English: In other words, the victim went to Studio Two before that time!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなりますね。
English: Yes... yes, I suppose that would be the case.

Japanese: 宇在カントクの証言を 思い出していただきたい！
English: Remember Mr. Sal Manella's testimony?

Japanese: 彼はこう言った。
English: Allow me to remind the court:

Japanese: 第１スタジオへは行けなかったから コテージの人間は犯人でない、と。
English: He said no one in the trailer was guilty because they could not have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: しかし、事実は逆だったのです！
English: Yet, in actuality, the reverse was true!

Japanese: 犯人は、コテージにいた人間しか あり得ない！
English: Only someone in the trailer could have committed this murder!

Japanese: なぜなら、犯行は第２スタジオで 行われたわけですから！
English: They were the only ones with access to the scene of the crime: Studio Two!

Japanese: 第２スタジオには、 コテージがあります。
English: As you may recall, there is a trailer in Studio Two.

Japanese: コテージでは、事件当日、 カイギが行われていた。
English: Now, on that day, a meeting was held in that very trailer.

Japanese: そして、被害者が死んだ時間に 休憩時間に入った。
English: There was a break in the meeting corresponding to the time of death.

Japanese: 宇在カントクとプロデューサーの 姫神は、ステーキを食べました。
English: During that break, Mr. Sal Manella and Ms. Dee Vasquez were outside, eating steak.

Japanese: そこはまさに、 殺人現場だったのです！
English: They were at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: 弁護側は、 次のように主張します！
English: The defense makes the following claims:

Japanese: 殺人現場は、第２スタジオだった！
English: The scene of the crime was Studio Two!

Japanese: そして、オバチャンが見たのは、 被害者の衣袋　武志だった！
English: The person that the security guard, Ms. Oldbag saw was the victim, Mr. Jack Hammer!

Japanese: イブクロさんは、なんらかの理由で トノサマンの着ぐるみを盗み、
English: Mr. Hammer, for some reason, stole a Steel Samurai costume.

Japanese: 第２スタジオへ行ったのです！
English: Then he went to Studio Two!

Japanese: バカげている！
English: This is madness!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志は、被害者なのだぞ！
English: Jack Hammer is the victim! The victim!

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみを盗んだのは なんのためだ！
English: Why would he steal a Steel Samurai costume!?

Japanese: わざわざ殺害されるために、 そんなことをしたと言うのか？
English: Are you suggesting he did so to cover up the details of his own murder!?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥。
English: W-well, no, of course not...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: それとも、何か証拠でもあるのか！
English: Or do you have proof!?

Japanese: 被害者の衣袋氏が、 着ぐるみを盗んだ証拠だ！
English: Give me proof that the victim, Jack Hammer, stole the costume!

Japanese: ‥‥あります！
English: I have proof!

Japanese: な、なんだと！
English: Y-you do!?

Japanese: あるんですか‥‥証拠が？
English: You do, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （そ、そんなにおどろかれると 自信がなくなるなあ‥‥）
English: (Now that they're so surprised suddenly I'm much less confident...)

Japanese: （たぶん、間違いないとは 思うんだけど‥‥）
English: (I think I'm right, though!)

Japanese: イブクロさんが、トノサマンの 着ぐるみを盗んだ証拠は！
English: Here's my proof that Jack Hammer stole the costume!

Japanese: （いやいや、それはさすがに 違うはずだ）
English: (Wait, no, that can't be right...)

Japanese: （もっとこう、人をナットク させられそうな‥‥）
English: (I've got to have some proof that will knock their socks off...)

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥早くおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright! We don't have all the time in the world, here.

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: （‥‥くやしいけど、 そんな証拠は持ってないな‥‥）
English: (Proof? Uh oh... I'm pretty sure I don't have anything like that...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: たしかに、１００％確実とは 言えないけど、
English: Even if you're not 100% sure about this, you have to try.

Japanese: じゅうぶん、コンキョになる 証拠品を、あなたは持っているわ！
English: And I think you've got proof that's good enough to win them over!

Japanese: イチかバチか、 たたきつけてみましょう！
English: Just go for it! All or nothing!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: なんでしょう。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ええと、やっぱりお見せします。 ‥‥証拠品を。
English: Actually... I do have proof.

Japanese: 証拠は、これです。
English: This is it.

Japanese: 薬の‥‥ビンですか。
English: An empty... medicine bottle?

Japanese: これは、荷星さんたちが 昼食を食べた‥‥、
English: I found this on the table in the Employee Area.

Japanese: スタッフエリアのテーブルから 見つけました。
English: The same table where Mr. Hammer and Mr. Powers ate.

Japanese: ラベルには“睡眠薬”と 書いてあります。
English: The label reads "sleeping pills."

Japanese: “スイミンヤク”‥‥？
English: "Sleeping pills"...?

Japanese: あの日、被告人の荷星さんは、 午後はずっとヒルネをしてました。
English: The defendant, Mr. Powers, spent that entire afternoon sleeping.

Japanese: 眠らされていたんですよ。 ‥‥イブクロさんによって。
English: He was drugged... by Mr. Hammer!

Japanese: 待ってください！
English: Wait a moment...

Japanese: たしかに、そのビンは アヤシイですが、
English: That bottle does raise some suspicions, yes.

Japanese: イブクロさんがそれを 使ったか、その証拠はありません。
English: But there is no proof that Mr. Hammer used it.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥提案があります。
English: Your Honor, I have an idea.

Japanese: ‥‥提案、ですか？ なんでしょう。
English: An... idea? Continue.

Japanese: 荷星さんの血液を 検査してください。
English: Run a test of Powers's blood.

Japanese: きっと、睡眠薬のコンセキが あるはずです。
English: You should find traces of the sleeping pills.

Japanese: 事件があってから、何日 たつと思っているのかな？
English: This much time after the day of the murder!?

Japanese: それに、コンセキが 残っていたとしても、
English: Even if there were traces it still proves nothing!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志が睡眠薬を使った 証拠にはならない！
English: No blood test could tell that Hammer was the one who drugged him, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （また、１本とられたな‥‥）
English: (He's right. Darn.)

Japanese: （しかたない。‥‥もう１回、 提案をしてみるか‥‥）
English: (That didn't work. Time to try another idea.)

Japanese: ‥‥提案、ですか？ なんでしょう。
English: An idea, Mr. Wright? And that would be?

Japanese: ええと。‥‥イブクロさんの 死体を調べてほしいんですけど。
English: Er... we should examine Mr. Hammer's body.

Japanese: “イブクロさん”‥‥ですか？
English: Mr. Hammer...?

Japanese: 睡眠薬を飲まされたというのは、 荷星さんなのでは‥‥？
English: I thought that it was Mr. Powers who was made to take the sleeping pills?

Japanese: そ、そうでしたね。
English: I-indeed it was, Your Honor.

Japanese: （また、間違えたみたいだな）
English: (Wrong again...)

Japanese: ‥‥提案、ですか？ なんでしょう。
English: An... idea? Very well, let's hear it.

Japanese: もちろん、このビンの 指紋を調べるんですよ。
English: I want to check this bottle for fingerprints!

Japanese: ‥‥イブクロさんの 指紋が見つかるはずですッ！
English: If my claim is true, Mr. Hammer's fingerprints should show up on this bottle!

Japanese: うむむ‥‥ッ！
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I suppose you're right.

Japanese: わかりました。 ビンをあずかりましょう。
English: Very well. The court will take possession of the bottle.

Japanese: 証拠品《カラッポの小ビン》を 裁判長に渡した。
English: Empty Bottle given to the judge.

Japanese: 本日の審理は、 ここまでとします。
English: This court will suspend proceedings on the current trial for today.

Japanese: 九太少年の証言によって、
English: Cody Hackin's testimony has revealed new possibilities in this case.

Japanese: 事件は、まったく違った可能性を 見せ始めました。
English: In fact, things may have happened very differently than we previously thought.

Japanese: 警備員が見たトノサマンは、 被害者の衣袋　武志だった。
English: The Steel Samurai seen by Ms. Oldbag may have been the victim, Jack Hammer.

Japanese: 殺人現場は、第１スタジオでは なく、第２スタジオだった。
English: The scene of the crime was not Studio One, but Studio Two.

Japanese: そして、コテージにいた人間には、 犯行を行う時間的よゆうがあった。
English: And those in the trailer did have time to commit murder.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたに与える宿題は、 次の問題の答えを見つけること。
English: Your homework is to find the answer to the following question:

Japanese: 衣袋さんは、なぜ着ぐるみを 盗まなければならなかったのか？
English: Why would Mr. Hammer steal the Steel Samurai costume?

Japanese: そして、誰が彼を殺害したか？ ‥‥その動機は？
English: Also, who killed him, and why?

Japanese: 明日までに、その答えを 見つけてください。
English: Find the answers for me by tomorrow.

Japanese: （多いよ‥‥宿題）
English: (That's a lot of homework!)

Japanese: 御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: うむ。
English: Mm?

Japanese: あなたの方は、 荷星さんに対する容疑を、
English: You will need to reconsider your stance in this case.

Japanese: もう一度、はじめから あらいなおすように。
English: Above all, you will need to reconsider your suspicion of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: こころえた。
English: As you say, Your Honor.

Japanese: この審理は、最終日の 明日まで持ち越すことにします！
English: This trial will be extended until tomorrow. This is the last extension!

Japanese: ‥‥では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Very well... Court is adjourned!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午後２時４７分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: October 19, 2:47 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: いやー‥‥。
English: Whew!

Japanese: ホント、アブなかったなあ！
English: That was a close one!

Japanese: まさに危機イッパツ、といった ところだったわね。
English: We were saved by a hair, but that's all that counts.

Japanese: とちゅうで、もう帰っちゃおうかな って、３回ぐらい思いました。
English: I thought seriously about going home about three times during the trial.

Japanese: あ、ぼくもです！
English: M-me too!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうだったんですか！
English: Really?

Japanese: お２人とも、自信タップリに 見えましたけど。
English: You both seemed so... so confident!

Japanese: トンでもない！ フリですよ、フリ。
English: Hah! Maybe I should take up a career in acting?

Japanese: もう、“荷星さん、ご臨終！”って ３回ぐらい思っちゃいました。
English: I was ready to pronounce you dead about three times back there.

Japanese: あ、私も！
English: Me too!

Japanese: ‥‥ひ、ヒドイ‥‥。
English: D-dead!?

Japanese: ‥‥ふふふ。さすがにそれは 冗談ですから。
English: Hoh hoh. Of course, we're kidding.

Japanese: そうなんですか？
English: Are you sure?

Japanese: （ぼくはべつに、冗談を 言ったつもりはないぞ‥‥）
English: (I wasn't kidding...)

Japanese: じゃあ、荷星さん。
English: Now, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: ぼくたちはこれから、 最後の調査に行きますから。
English: We have to go make our final investigation.

Japanese: 明日の法廷では、かならず 真犯人をつきとめて見せますよ。
English: But I promise you, we'll find the true killer by tomorrow's trial!

Japanese: は、はい！　恐縮です‥‥。
English: R-right! Thanks! *sniff*

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: （し、しまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: 被告人、荷星　三郎に この場で判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Will Powers...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午後３時２７分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 19, 3:27 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。今日は 危なかったですね、千尋さん‥‥。
English: That was a close one, huh, Mia?

Japanese: ふーん、そうなんだ。 ‥‥あたしも見たかったな。
English: Really? Too bad, I'm sorry I missed it.

Japanese: ? ま、真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: ね。ね。 ‥‥ケッキョク、どうなったの？
English: So, what happened?

Japanese: なんとなく、犯人の目星は ついてきたよ。
English: Well, I think I've got a pretty good idea who did it.

Japanese: あとは、証拠と‥‥動機、かな。
English: Now all I need is a motive... and proof.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ！
English: Wow! Good going, Nick!

Japanese: “序審法廷”のシステムでは、 明日が最終日だからね。
English: Under the "initial trial" system, tomorrow is the last day we have.

Japanese: “序審法廷”？
English: "Initial trial"...? What's that?

Japanese: 聞いたことない？　２、３年前から 始まった法廷のシステム。
English: That's the new court system they introduced two or three years ago.

Japanese: 要するに、犯罪の件数が多いから、 サッサと片づけようっていう制度。
English: They had so many cases in the system, they decided to speed the whole process up.

Japanese: その制度だと、裁判って、３日で 終わっちゃうの？
English: So, under that system, trials have to end in three days?

Japanese: まあ、とりあえずはね。
English: Yeah, pretty much.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことだから、 さっそく、行こうか。
English: Well, we've no time to waste!

Japanese: 千尋さんのお気に入りだった 観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんがさんざん 水をやるので、ふやけぎみだ。
English: Maya has been watering it so much lately it looks a little... swollen.

Japanese: かわいがっているつもりらしいが、 “水責め”に見えないこともない。
English: She's either trying to make it grow... or she's perfecting her water torture technique.

Japanese: 法律関係のムズカシイ本が イヤというほど並んでいる。
English: There's a horrendous amount of legal books here.

Japanese: これからは、ぼくが 読まなくちゃいけないのかな‥‥。
English: Scarier still is the thought that I have to read all of these now.

Japanese: 窓の外は、今日もいい天気だ。
English: It's a nice day outside.

Japanese: 最近、向かいの部屋は、 客の入らない夜はない。
English: The hotel across the way is always full these days.

Japanese: ボーイが楽しそうに 部屋をそうじしているのが見える。
English: There's the bellboy, happily cleaning the room.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 今は、ぼくが使わせて もらっている。
English: Actually... it's my desk now.

Japanese: ここに座ると、どんなときでも 気持ちが引きしまる。
English: Sitting here always makes me feel like a professional.

Japanese: 千尋さんが好きだった、 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: A poster of an old movie that Mia liked.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみようかと 思ったのだが、
English: I was thinking I should go see it myself.

Japanese: このポスター、タイトルが 書かれてないぞ‥‥。
English: Then I realized that the poster doesn't actually say the title of the movie.

Japanese: なんでイブクロさん、
English: I don't get it, Maya.

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみなんて 盗んだのかなあ。
English: Why would Mr. Hammer steal the Steel Samurai costume?

Japanese: え！　あれって、 イブクロさんが入ってたの！
English: What!? You mean Mr. Hammer was wearing the costume!?

Japanese: そうなんだよ。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: で、でも、イブクロさんて、 被害者でしょ！
English: B-but Mr. Hammer was the victim, Nick!

Japanese: なんで、わざわざそんなことを？
English: Why would he go through the trouble of stealing it?

Japanese: その答えを、これから 見つけるんだよ。
English: That's what we have to find out.

Japanese: カントクたちの証言は どうなったの？
English: What happened with the director's testimony?

Japanese: カントクやプロデューサーたちが コテージにいたことは間違いない。
English: Well, it's pretty clear that the producer and the director were both in the trailer.

Japanese: そうなんだ‥‥。
English: Huh...

Japanese: でも、だからこそ、犯人は 彼らしかいないんだ。
English: Which means that the killer has to be one of them!

Japanese: えっ！　どうして！
English: Really!? Why?

Japanese: 殺人現場は、実はコテージのある、 第２スタジオだったんだよ。
English: Because the real scene of the crime was Studio Two... where that trailer is!

Japanese: うええええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: （ここまでおどろかれると、 なんかやりにくいなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... was Maya always this excitable?)

Japanese: なんか気づいたこと、ない？
English: Well, have you noticed anything that might help us?

Japanese: あたし、お姉ちゃんを呼び出して いるときは意識がないの。
English: I can't see what's going on when Mia is here...

Japanese: 気づきたくても、気づきようが ないんだ‥‥ごめんね。
English: So, no. I haven't noticed anything.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: ちょっと、ニボサブさんの話でも 聞きに行ってみようか。
English: Maybe we should go talk to WP?

Japanese: （ニボサブさんか‥‥）
English: (WP... Powers... right.)

Japanese: これ、どう？ ‥‥あの子の宝物だって。
English: What do you think about this? It's that fanboy's prized possession.

Japanese: うわ、すごい！
English: Wow! Impressive!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... でも、これ、いつもの トノサマンと、なんか違う。
English: It's a little different from the Steel Samurai on TV...

Japanese: まあ、デパートの屋上でやってる ショーの写真だからなあ。
English: These are mostly taken at those PR shows they do at stores and fairs...

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中に入っているのも、 荷星さんじゃないだろうし。
English: I'm pretty sure it's not even Will Powers inside that suit.

Japanese: ふっ。こんなニセトノサマンに マンゾクしているなんて。
English: No true fan would be satisfied with anything less than the real thing!

Japanese: まだまだ、彼もコドモだね。
English: He's still a child, I suppose...

Japanese: オトナは、ホンモノしかキョーミ ないもんね、なるほどくん！
English: I guess it takes an adult to appreciate the real deal. Right, Nick?

Japanese: そ、そんなもんかな。
English: Um, yeah, whatever you say.

Japanese: これ、九太くんの‥‥
English: This is Cody's--

Japanese: ああっ！　でじかめだ！
English: Hey! A digital camera!

Japanese: コ、コドモのクセに、 こんなモノを‥‥。
English: I'm impressed a kid like him would have something this expensive.

Japanese: さ、最近のコドモは、 ゼータクだよね！
English: Kids these days!

Japanese: （修行中の霊媒師は、どうやら 生活が苦しいようだ‥‥）
English: (I don't imagine spirit mediums-in-training get a lot of toys...)

Japanese: （まさか、これがイブクロさん だったとはね‥‥）
English: (Who would have thought that it was Jack Hammer inside!)

Japanese: （問題は、イブクロさんがなぜ、 こんなことをしたか、だ‥‥）
English: (But why did he steal it? That's what I have to figure out.)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ ...? あたしもほしいな。 トノサマン・スピアー。
English: I wish I had my own Samurai Spear!

Japanese: なるほどくんを 思うさまつついてみたい。
English: Just think, I could poke you as much as I wanted!

Japanese: な、なんでぼくなんだよ‥‥。
English: Me!? Why me!?

Japanese: ‥‥それがどうかした？ 何も思いつかないけど。
English: Sorry, I don't know how that will help us figure this out.

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 19 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。 今日もイカしてましたね。
English: Mr. Wright! You did very well again today... thank you!

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます。
English: Oh... thanks.

Japanese: ‥‥その、うるんだヒトミは やめてください。
English: Um, could you not look like you're about to burst into tears...?

Japanese: へえ。なるほどくん、 イカしてたんだぁ。
English: Wow, Nick! You must have really wowed 'em in there.

Japanese: ‥‥その、失礼なニヤケヅラも やめてほしいな。
English: You could stop looking so... vulnerable, too.

Japanese: ニボサブさん！　とにかく、 残すは明日、１日だけです。
English: Hey, WP! Only one day left!

Japanese: がんばりましょうね！
English: Let's make it count!

Japanese: はあ。
English: Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥といって、ボクに何ができる というワケでもないんですけど。
English: Though there's not really anything I can do to help.

Japanese: あはは。ま、あたしも そうなんですけどねー。
English: Hah hah hah! I'm not really sure what I can do, either.

Japanese: “あはは”じゃないだろ。
English: Please... this is no laughing matter.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 荷星さんの迫力にビビって 動けないみたいだ。
English: He seems frozen in awe of Will Powers.

Japanese: 姫神さんのこと、 何か話してもらえますか。
English: Could you tell me more about Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: ああ‥‥、 あのプロデューサーの方ですか。
English: Oh... the producer?

Japanese: あの人は、業界で“天才”と 言われている人です。
English: She's well respected in the industry. They say she's a genius.

Japanese: “テンサイ”‥‥。
English: A genius...?

Japanese: 英都に来てから、もう ５年ぐらいたちますけど‥‥。
English: She's been at Global Studios for five years now.

Japanese: 今じゃあ、あの人には 誰もアタマが上がりません。
English: Right or wrong, nobody dares tell her different these days.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: あの人は、ここへ来てから たてつづけにヒット作を作って、
English: Ever since she came on board, we've had nothing but hits.

Japanese: 当時つぶれかけていた英都を、 ミゴトよみがえらせたんです。
English: Global Studios was on its knees, but she picked it up and made it shine.

Japanese: それに‥‥。
English: Oh, and...

Japanese: それに‥‥なんですか？
English: And... what?

Japanese: いいえ。なんでもないです。
English: N-nothing. Forget it.

Japanese: ‥‥ボクも、ウワサしか 知らないんで‥‥。
English: I... I've only heard rumors, anyhow.

Japanese: 宇在カントクって、 どんな人ですか？
English: Can you tell me anything about Mr. Manella?

Japanese: あの人は、これまでマイナーな ビデオ作品ばかり作ってたんです。
English: Oh, he used to be a minor straight-to-video director.

Japanese: それを、あの姫神プロデューサーが 目にとめて、
English: But something in his work caught Vasquez's eye.

Japanese: トノサマンの話を 持っていったんです。
English: She brought the Steel Samurai idea to him.

Japanese: おかげで、宇在カントクは 今じゃ有名人です。
English: Now everyone knows the name Sal Manella.

Japanese: カントク、プロデューサーには アタマが上がらないみたいですよ。
English: I hear he's pretty much at her beck and call, though.

Japanese: 彼女の言うことなら、なんでも ハイハイ聞いてます。
English: Whenever she says "jump!" he asks "how high?" and all that.

Japanese: （うーん、目に浮かぶなあ‥‥）
English: (I can imagine him being at her beck and call... though I can't imagine him jumping.)

Japanese: あの、イブクロさんのこと なんですけど。
English: I was wondering about Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: ムカシはあの人、大スターだった じゃないですか。
English: He was a big star back in the day, right?

Japanese: 急に名前を聞かなくなっちゃった んですけど‥‥。
English: But then he just dropped off the face of the Earth.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ボクも、あの人にアコガレて この世界に。
English: Yes, you're right. In fact, he was my role model when I started in this business.

Japanese: あの人、急に大きなシゴトを しなくなったんですよ。
English: But he just stopped taking on big roles.

Japanese: そして、この英都で 小さな作品に出るように‥‥。
English: That's when he began appearing in little productions at Global...

Japanese: ええ！　どうしてなんですか？
English: How could he do that!?

Japanese: あたし、“武者ぶるいの夏”の 続編、たのしみにしてたんですよ！
English: I was really looking forward to a sequel to "Samurai Summer."

Japanese: イブクロさん、スターの座を下りた のが、５年ぐらい前なんですよ。
English: He just gave up being a star five years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥考えてみると。
English: Actually, now that I think about it...

Japanese: ちょうどそのころなんですよね。 ‥‥姫神さんが英都に来たの‥‥。
English: That was right when Ms. Vasquez came to Global.
(Five years ago...?)

Japanese: 荷星さん。これ‥‥。
English: Have you seen this, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: な、なんですかこれ。
English: W-what's that!?

Japanese: ‥‥身におぼえのないトノサマンが たくさんならんでいますケド‥‥。
English: I've never seen these Steel Samurais in my life...

Japanese: 遊園地とかのショーに出ている トノサマンです。
English: These are the Steel Samurais that appear at live shows.

Japanese: ファンの男の子が、たった１人で 集めたんですよ。
English: A fanboy collected all these pictures himself.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... こういうものを見ると、
English: Seeing things like this really makes me think...

Japanese: “ガンバラなきゃ！” って、思います‥‥。
English: I mean so much to these kids, you know? *sniff*

Japanese: 凶器のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding the murder weapon...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 重いんですか？　あれ。
English: Is it heavy, that spear?

Japanese: けっこう重いですよ。
English: It's pretty hefty, yeah.

Japanese: ふつうの人は、自由にふり回すのは ムリだと思いますよ‥‥。
English: I don't think an average person would be able to swing it around effectively.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、なんか、午前中の 打ち合わせで壊れた、とか‥‥？
English: And... that's the spear that broke or something during the morning run-through?

Japanese: ええ。ボク、回しゲリを シッパイしちゃって‥‥。
English: Oh yes, I goofed on a round kick, you see...

Japanese: スピアーを、 折っちゃったんですよねぇ。
English: I broke the spear.

Japanese: とりあえず、オバチャンが なおしてくれましたけど。
English: The security lady fixed it for me right away though.

Japanese: ガムテープで、ね。
English: With duct tape. We heard.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: Here's that picture again.

Japanese: ホ、ホントに、着ぐるみの中には イブクロさんが‥‥？
English: I-is that really Jack Hammer inside that suit?

Japanese: そうとしか考えられないんです。
English: He's the only one who it could have been.

Japanese: でも、どうしてそんなことを？
English: But why would he...?

Japanese: そうだよ！　どうして？
English: Yeah! Why?

Japanese: 霊媒師だろ。 ‥‥呼び出してよ、イブクロさん。
English: You're the medium! Why don't you summon up Mr. Hammer's spirit and ask him yourself?

Japanese: うう‥‥。 あと３年、待って。
English: *sigh* Gimme about three more years.

Japanese: （やれやれ。おたがい、 半人前、か‥‥）
English: (Look at us, the two novices.)

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: E-excuse me.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thank you for showing me that, whatever it is.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I wish I could think of some way for me to help you...

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-sorry. *sniff*

Japanese: １０月１９日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 19 Global Studios Main Gate
The old windbag sure is quiet today.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... いつもこうだと、 この撮影所も平和なのに。
English: You know, this place is really... peaceful, like this.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... なるほどくん。
English: Nick?

Japanese: ちょっと、ハリアイがないね。
English: I think her guard's down...

Japanese: ま、まあね。
English: Y-yeah?

Japanese: 今なら、詰所のようかん、 食べてもだいじょうぶかも。
English: I bet we could even eat those donuts in the guard station... if we wanted.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... ‥‥食べたら、ハッたおす。
English: You eat, you die.

Japanese: あ。よかった、生きてたよ。
English: Whew! She's alive!

Japanese: この撮影所の正面入口だ。
English: The main entrance to the studios.

Japanese: 今日も、見張りの警官が 立っているはずだ。
English: They probably have a police officer on patrol out there.

Japanese: この撮影所のライトバンだ。
English: Looks like a studio van.

Japanese: 撮影用の資材などを 運んでいるのだろう。
English: They probably use it to carry around film equipment.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャンがむさぼるのだろう。 テーブルの上は、おかしの山だ。
English: Ms. Oldbag must like junk food. There's a mountain of it on the desk in there.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: この撮影所の見取り図が 描かれている。
English: An overhead map of the studios.

Japanese: ここから右へ行くと、 《スタッフ作業場》
English: Right from here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 左へ行くと、《スタジオ方面》に 通じているらしい。
English: To the left are the actual film studios.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: ‥‥おや、今日は スイッチが入っていないみたいだ。
English: Huh? It looks like it's been turned off for the day.

Japanese: あの‥‥姫神プロデューサーの ことですけど‥‥。
English: Um, I was wondering about Dee Vasquez, the producer.

Japanese: 英都のおエラ方は、あの女を 気に入ってるからね。
English: Oh, the studio bigwigs LOVE her.

Japanese: あの女の思いどおりにならない ことは、ないョ。
English: So she always gets her way.

Japanese: （ニガニガしい口調だ‥‥）
English: (She seems bitter...)

Japanese: キライなんですか？ プロデューサーのこと？
English: Do you... not like the producer?

Japanese: あの女のことは話せないョ。 ‥‥撮影所の人から言われたから。
English: Look, the studio people don't want me talking about her, so, sorry.

Japanese: 宇在カントクですけど‥‥。
English: Can I ask you about Sal Manella, the director?

Japanese: アイツは、姫神の言いなりに動く、 フヌケた男だよ。
English: Sal? He's soft--a pushover. Does whatever Vasquez tells him to.

Japanese: どうも、姫神にイジメられるのが、 ウレシイみたいだネ。
English: She treats him like dirt, but I think he likes it.

Japanese: えー？　なんでイジメられるのが ウレシイんですか？
English: Huh? Why would anyone like being treated like dirt?

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。なんで？
English: That doesn't make any sense, does it, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥さ、さあねえ。
English: Er... No, no it doesn't, Maya.

Japanese: イブクロさんのことですけど‥‥。
English: About Mr. Hammer...

Japanese: アンタ！
English: Arrgh!

Japanese: 今日はよくも、イイカゲンなことを 言ってくれたネ！
English: I heard about what you said today! Cheeky whippersnapper!

Japanese: あの、あの大スターのイブクロ ちゃんを、こともあろうに‥‥
English: Poor old Hammer! You'd drag his star down from the sky and stomp on it!

Japanese: ハンザイシャ呼ばわり するなんて‥‥
English: Calling him a criminal! A thief!

Japanese: オ、オバ、オバチャン、 許さないよぉ！
English: I-I-I-I... I won't forget this!!!

Japanese: （ホ、ホンキで怒ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, she's really pissed this time!)

Japanese: いいかい！ イブクロちゃんはねェ！
English: You listen to me, whipper- snapper! My poor old Hammer would never do such a thing!

Japanese: オバチャンの目をごまかすために、 荷星の着ぐるみを盗むような、
English: Why would he steal Powers's costume so he could sneak by me?

Japanese: そんなヒキョウなことは ゼッタイ、しないんだ！
English: He would never stoop so low! It's impossible!

Japanese: あの、オバチャン、これ‥‥。
English: Um, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: そんなモノ、見せるんじゃないョ！
English: What!? Get that filth out of my sight!

Japanese: それがイブクロちゃんだって？
English: And you're claiming that's poor old Hammer in there!?

Japanese: デタラメもいいとこだよ！
English: And I thought you were such an innocent youth... Bah!

Japanese: あの、オバチャン、これ‥‥。
English: Um, Ms. Oldbag? What do you think of this?

Japanese: ‥‥今は、何も 考える気にならないョ‥‥。
English: Think? I'm not in the mood for thinking, thank you very much.

Japanese: なんだいアンタたち。 まだいたのかい。
English: What, you're still slinking around?

Japanese: そんなにここが好きなら、 オバチャンのかわりに働くかい？
English: If you like the place so much, why don't you take over for me?

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥ 元気ないですね。
English: The old windbag doesn't look so good...

Japanese: みんな、イブクロちゃんのことを わすれようとして、必死だョ。
English: E-everybody's doing their darnedest to forget Hammer.

Japanese: 英都撮影所がここまで大きく なったのは、誰のオカゲだい？
English: Who do they think made Global Studios what it is today?

Japanese: みーんな、イブクロちゃんの オカゲじゃないか‥‥。
English: Hammer! It's all due to Hammer!

Japanese: オ、オバチャンが グチをこぼしてる‥‥。
English: She's starting to froth at the mouth, Nick...

Japanese: キケンだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Maybe we should keep a safe distance?

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ５年前のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I wanted to ask you about five years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥ッ！ ! アンタ！ ‥‥ダレに、それを！
English: Who told!? Who did you hear that from!?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: I, uh... huh?

Japanese: 間宮だね！
English: Nichols!

Japanese: ま、まみや‥‥？
English: N-nickels?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 スタッフの子の名前だよ。
English: Nichols, Nick. Penny Nichols. That assistant we talked to.

Japanese: と、とにかく。
English: O-oh, right.

Japanese: 聞いたんですよ。
English: Anyway, we heard about the accident.

Japanese: イブクロさん、撮影中の事故で、 人を‥‥
English: It was during filming with Jack Hammer...

Japanese: その。 ‥‥死なせちゃった、って。
English: He killed a man. Didn't he?

Japanese: アンタたち！
English: Wh-whippersnappers!

Japanese: そうやって、人のムカシを ほじくりかえして、
English: Dredging up dirt on someone's past like that... and the recently departed, no less!

Japanese: たのしいかい！
English: I suppose you think this is fun!?

Japanese: い、いや。これは、荷星さんを 助けるためですから‥‥。
English: N-no, I'm just doing my job to protect Mr. Powers...

Japanese: イブクロちゃんが、荷星の 着ぐるみを盗んだって！
English: And you claim that Hammer stole Powers's costume!

Japanese: そんな言いがかり、 オバチャンは信じないよ！
English: You expect me to believe that rubbish!?

Japanese: それとも、何か証拠でも あるのかい！
English: Or do you have some kind of proof?

Japanese: （イブクロさんが着ぐるみを盗もう とした証拠か‥‥どうだろう？）
English: (Proof that Hammer stole Powers's costume? Hmm...)

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥ありませんけど。
English: Proof? Well... no.

Japanese: ほら、ごらんョ！
English: See! See!?

Japanese: ツゴウの悪いことを、ゼンブ 死んだ人に押しつけようなんて。
English: I see how you work! Anything that doesn't fit, you just blame on the dead!

Japanese: そ、そんなこと‥‥ やめておくれ！
English: Well... well... Just butt out, lawyer-boy!

Japanese: （‥‥このオバチャンは、 やはり何かを知っている！）
English: (She knows something!)

Japanese: （なんとしてでも 聞き出さなきゃな‥‥）
English: (But how am I going to get her to talk...?)

Japanese: オバチャンには悪いけど‥‥ あるんですよ、証拠。
English: I'm sorry Ms. Oldbag, but I do have proof.

Japanese: イブクロさんが、着ぐるみを 盗もうとしたという証拠は‥‥、
English: I can prove that Mr. Hammer stole Mr. Powers's costume.

Japanese: フン！ なんだい、そんなもの！
English: Hmph! What's that supposed to prove!?

Japanese: オバチャンは認めないよ。
English: That means nothing to me!

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥あたしにも 意味がわからないよ。
English: Nick... I'm kinda having trouble figuring that one out too.

Japanese: （‥‥これはどうも、 違ったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... wrong evidence?)

Japanese: （‥‥でも、このオバチャンは 何かを知っている！）
English: (But I know Oldbag knows something!)

Japanese: （なんとしてでも 聞き出さなきゃな‥‥）
English: (I have to get her to talk!)

Japanese: これが証拠です。
English: Here's my proof.

Japanese: なんだい、その セコいビンは‥‥？
English: What? What's a little empty bottle supposed to prove?

Japanese: 睡眠薬です。
English: It's a bottle of sleeping pills.

Japanese: ‥‥イブクロさんの 指紋がついています。
English: With Mr. Hammer's fingerprints on it...

Japanese: そ、それがどうしたんだい！
English: Wh-what does that prove?

Japanese: イブクロちゃんだって、 眠れないコトはあるサ！
English: I... I'm sure old Hammer had some sleepless nights!

Japanese: そのクスリを、荷星に使った っていう証拠はあるのかい！
English: Where's your proof that he used those pills on Powers?

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、おヒルに ホネつきステーキを食べましたね？
English: Mr. Powers ate a t-bone steak for lunch, correct?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥食べたケド。
English: W-well... yes. So?

Japanese: これは、そのときの 荷星さんの皿です。
English: This is the plate that he used to eat that steak.

Japanese: 睡眠薬のコンセキが 見つかっています。
English: There are traces of sleeping pill powder on the plate!

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、おヒルに ホネつきステーキを食べましたね？
English: Mr. Powers ate a t-bone steak for lunch, correct?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥食べたケド。
English: W-well... yes. So?

Japanese: これは、そのときの 荷星さんの皿です。
English: This is the plate that he used to eat that steak.

Japanese: 睡眠薬のコンセキが 見つかっています。
English: There are traces of sleeping pill powder on the plate!

Japanese: は‥‥はん！
English: Mmmph? Hah!

Japanese: 荷星が自分で 入れたんじゃないのかい！
English: I-I bet Powers put it in there himself!

Japanese: あんな男でも、眠れないこと ぐらい、あるだろうからね。
English: Even a lightweight like him must have trouble sleeping sometimes.

Japanese: この睡眠薬、イブクロさんが 入れたという証拠があるんですよ。
English: Except, I have proof that Mr. Hammer put the pills in.
