Japanese: （とりあえず、お金は 払っておくか‥‥）
English: (I guess we should pay her for the film...)

Japanese: 気ィつけや！
English: Watch it!

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yes ma'am.

Japanese: （あとで払えよ！）
English: (On second thought, I'll pay later...)

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: I'm really sorry!

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: このカメラ、なんかスゴいよ‥‥。
English: Check out this camera!

Japanese: ホントだ。‥‥ヘンなキカイと マイクがついてる。
English: Yeah... what's with the big mic and that box on top, I wonder?

Japanese: どうやら、何かの音に反応して シャッターを切るみたいだな。
English: It looks like some sort of automatic system for taking a photo when a noise is heard.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ。 ‥‥ちょっと、やってみようか。
English: Wow! Hey, let's see if it works!

Japanese: コホン。
English: *cough*

Japanese: “ぼく、なるほどくん！”
English: "Hi, I'm Nick!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 声が小さいのかな。
English: Maybe I'm not saying it loud enough.

Japanese: “なーるーほーどーくーん！！”
English: "YO! I'M NICK!!!"
Huh.

Japanese: なあーるうー ほおーどおー‥‥
English: NIIIIIIIIII......IIIIIIIIIICK!

Japanese: や、やめてくれ ハズかしい！
English: Will you stop that?

Japanese: コワれてるよ、このキカイ！
English: I think it's broken, Nick.

Japanese: けるな！
English: D-don't kick it!

Japanese: （反応する音が、人間の声じゃ ないんじゃないかな‥‥）
English: (It must not be set up to respond to voices.)

Japanese: このカメラ‥‥。 マイクとキカイがセットされてる。
English: This camera has a mic and some sort of attachment.

Japanese: たぶん、何かの音に反応して シャッターを切るんだろう。
English: It must take pictures when triggered by a noise.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ。 ‥‥ちょっと、やってみようか。
English: Wow! Cool! Let's try it out!

Japanese: コホン。
English: *cough*

Japanese: “ぼく、なるほどくん！”
English: "Hi, I'm Nick!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 声が小さいのかな。
English: Maybe I'm not saying it loud enough.

Japanese: “なーるーほーどーくーん！！”
English: "HEY! I'M NICK!!!"
Huh.

Japanese: なあーるうー ほおーどおー‥‥
English: NIIIIIIIIII... ...IIIIIIIIIICK!

Japanese: や、やめてくれ ハズかしい！
English: Will you stop that?

Japanese: コワれてるよ、このキカイ！
English: Maybe it's broken?

Japanese: けるな！
English: D-don't kick it!

Japanese: ‥‥人間の声には 反応しないんじゃないか？
English: Maybe it isn't set to respond to voices?

Japanese: じゃあ、なに？ これなら反応するの？
English: Well what then? I know!

Japanese: クラッカー？
English: The party popper...?

Japanese: パァァァン！
English: *BANG*

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik
Yep. It responded.

Japanese: 音に反応して、自動的に シャッターが切られるカメラ。
English: This camera is rigged to respond to noise.

Japanese: クラッカーなどの、ハレツ音に セットされているようだ。
English: But it only responds to loud noises... like a party popper.

Japanese: ‥‥あのう。
English: Umm...

Japanese: なんや！
English: What!?

Japanese: ウチは今、カメラのフィルムを かえとるんや！
English: Can't ya see I'm changin' the film on my camera here!

Japanese: どこかのアホが、１本まるまる ムダにしてくれたし！
English: Someone--I'm not namin' any names--but someone used up a WHOLE ROLL.

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: Sorry...

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ）
English: (That didn't work...)

Japanese: （何か、気を引けそうなものを 見せた方がいいな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I have anything to show her that would get her attention...)

Japanese: なあなあ。サツジン事件て、 いつやったん？
English: Oh yeah, when was that murder, anyway?

Japanese: ウチ、ここんとこずっと テレビ見てへんねん。
English: I ain't seen much television lately.

Japanese: クリスマスイブの深夜です。
English: It happened late in the night on Christmas Eve.

Japanese: ふうん。 くりすますいぶ、ねえ‥‥。
English: That so? Christmas Eve?

Japanese: 男の人がボートの上で 撃たれたんですけど‥‥、
English: A man on a boat was shot...

Japanese: 何か見ませんでした？
English: Did you see anything?

Japanese: ううーん。
English: Well, lemme see.

Japanese: ‥‥ボートなあ。
English: A boat, ya say?

Japanese: 見たような気もするねんけど‥‥。 ハッキリせえへんなあ。
English: I reckon I mighta seen one... not sure though.

Japanese: ここ３日間、ずーっと湖を 見てるやろ？
English: Y'all gotta remember I've been watching this here lake for a good three days now.

Japanese: ボートもクサるほど見とるワケや。 いつのコトかは、おぼえてへん。
English: I seen enough boats to choke a mule. Kinda hard to remember which I seen when.

Japanese: なんか、すごいカメラですね？
English: That's quite a camera you have there.

Japanese: そうやろ？ ドイツ製やで。ゾリンゲン。
English: Y'all better know it! It's German-made. A genuine Solingen!

Japanese: （そりゃ刃物だろ‥‥）
English: (Isn't that where they make knives...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: カメラについてる 装置は、なんですか？
English: So, what's that device you have stuck to the camera?

Japanese: あ、ああ。あれ‥‥？
English: Huh? Device?

Japanese: クラッカー鳴らしたら、 急に動き出しちゃって‥‥。
English: It started moving all by itself when I fired my party popper...

Japanese: 大きな音がしたら、自動的に シャッターを切るから。
English: Oh that? That triggers the shutter whenever it detects certain sounds.

Japanese: ハレツするような音が、 装置にプログラムされてるんや。
English: It's programmed to pick up loud noises right now.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。 ぷろぐらむされてるんですねえ。
English: A programmable camera! Neat!

Japanese: 《ナツミのカメラ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんか、すごいカメラですね？
English: That's quite a camera you have there.

Japanese: そうやろ？ ドイツ製やで。ゾリンゲン。
English: Y'all better know it! It's German-made. A genuine Solingen!

Japanese: （そりゃ刃物だろ‥‥）
English: (Isn't that where they make knives...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: カメラについてる 装置は、なんですか？
English: So, what's that device you have stuck to the camera?

Japanese: あ、ああ。あれ‥‥？
English: Huh? Device?

Japanese: クラッカー鳴らしたら、 急に動き出しちゃって‥‥。
English: It started moving all by itself when I fired my party popper...

Japanese: 大きな音がしたら、自動的に シャッターを切るから。
English: Oh that? That triggers the shutter whenever it detects certain sounds.

Japanese: ハレツするような音が 装置にプログラムされてるんや。
English: It's programmed to pick up loud noises right now.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。 ぷろぐらむされてるんですねえ。
English: A programmable camera! Neat!

Japanese: ナツミさんて、 どういう方なんですか？
English: So, what is it you do, Lotta?

Japanese: え、ウチ？
English: Huh? Me?

Japanese: は、はは。 どうだってええやん、そんなコト。
English: Hah hah. Y'all don't really want to know that, do ya?

Japanese: 大阪のな、大学生や。 ‥‥研究員やってるんや。
English: Actually, I'm a research student at Country U., right in the heart of the heartland.

Japanese: うわあ、スゴい！
English: Wow! Neat!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　大阪で、大学で 研究員だよ！
English: Nick! She's a research student at a university! Country U.!

Japanese: ‥‥そのまんまだな。
English: Uh... so I hear.

Japanese: それで、いつから ここにいるんですか？
English: So, when did you come up here?

Japanese: そ、そうやなあ‥‥。 ３日間ぐらい、やろか‥‥。
English: Hmm, lemme see... I guess it was 'bout three days ago.

Japanese: 何を撮影してるんですか？
English: What are you photographing?

Japanese: さ、さっきも言ったやろ？
English: D-didn't I tell y'all that already?

Japanese: 流星や。そう、流星群を 撮っとるんや。
English: Meteors! Yep, meteor showers!
(Falling stars...?)

Japanese: あの。ぼく、こういうモノ なんですけど‥‥。
English: I, er, this is my badge.

Japanese: どういうモノや？　そんな こきたないバッジ、知らん。
English: Huh? Aren't badges supposed t' be all shiny and impressive? You a cop or something?

Japanese: 弁護士なんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm... I'm a lawyer.

Japanese: な、なんや！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: さっきのフィルムのことで、 インネンつける気ィなん？
English: Y'all ain't gonna try and pull one o' them lawsuits on me over that film now?

Japanese: ええで。 ‥‥出るトコ出よか！
English: Cause I'll have y'all know I'm a fighter and I wrassled meaner lookin' things'n you!

Japanese: い、いえいえそうじゃなくて。
English: N-no, that's not it at all.

Japanese: ぼくたち、この湖で起こった 殺人事件を調べてるんですよ。
English: We're here investigating a murder that took place here, on the lake.

Japanese: サツジンジケン‥‥？
English: A murder...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... なんや、オモロそうやん！
English: Sounds cool!

Japanese: ‥‥うん。ええわ。 なんでも聞きィ。
English: Why didn't y'all say that in the first place! Go ahead, ask me anything ya like!

Japanese: （おお。やった！ キゲンがなおったみたいだ）
English: (Finally! Some cooperation!)

Japanese: アンタも、かくれてないで 出て来ィ。
English: You too. Y'all can come out of hiding now. I won't bite. Hard.

Japanese: （そういえば、真宵ちゃんの すがたが‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it, where did Maya get to?)

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥
English: S-sorry...

Japanese: あたし、早くしゃべるの ニガテで‥‥。
English: I-I was feeling a little overwhelmed. The culture gap and all...

Japanese: ええてええて。 ウチが合わせたる。
English: Never you mind, honey. I kin' talk Yank for ya if... ahem... if it pleases you?

Japanese: は、はい！　‥‥ありがとう！
English: Th-thanks. I think I'll be okay.

Japanese: ウチは大沢木　ナツミ （おおさわぎなつみ）や。
English: Great then! I'm Lotta, Lotta Hart, but y'all can call me Lotta!

Japanese: 大学の研究生で、 流星の撮影に来てるんや。
English: I'm here photographing meteor showers for a research project.

Japanese: ヨロシクな。
English: Mighty pleased ta meet ya!

Japanese: それにしても、 アンタが弁護士ねぇ‥‥。
English: So yer a lawyer, huh?

Japanese: ハッキリ言って、 バッジ負けしてるなァ。あははは。
English: Tell the truth, yer badge is a lot more impressive than you are.

Japanese: あははは。
English: Hah hah!

Japanese: キッ！
English: *glare*

Japanese: うううう。
English: Hah... hah... oh. Ahem.

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Lotta?

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: “ハレツ音”で作動するんですね？ ‥‥あのカメラ。
English: So, your camera... it triggers on loud explosion noises?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Ayup.

Japanese: この事件の被害者、ピストルで 撃たれてるんですよ。
English: Actually, the victim in the case we're researching--he was shot with a pistol.

Japanese: ぴすとる‥‥？
English: A pistol...?

Japanese: 銃声って、クラッカーと同じ “ハレツ音”ですよね？
English: Right. Now wouldn't a gunshot make a similar noise to our party popper...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうやね。
English: I guess it would.

Japanese: もしかして、何か撮影されて いないでしょうか‥‥？
English: Your camera... didn't get a picture of the murder, did it?
Hey!

Japanese: なんか、カッコエエなあ ニイちゃん。
English: Y'all are pretty bright!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥うんうん、なるほどなあ。
English: I see what yer sayin'...

Japanese: ちょっとウチ、調べてみたるわ。
English: Tell ya what, I'll have a look-see at my film.

Japanese: 昨日の晩の写真なんですけど。
English: It would have been a photo taken late last night...

Japanese: チェックはしたんやけど、 何か写っとるかもしれんもんな。
English: I checked 'em once, don't remember if there was anything on 'em though.

Japanese: へへ。ちょっとワクワク するなあ。
English: But what if I got sumtin'! I could be witness to a genuine murder! Yeehaw!

Japanese: ほな、またあとで来ィ。
English: I'll go check that film. Y'all come back now, y'hear?

Japanese: クルマの中に入っちゃったよ。
English: She went inside her SUV.

Japanese: しょうがないね。 ‥‥またあとで来ようか。
English: I guess we should come back later...

Japanese: なんやそれ。
English: What y'all got there? Say, deep fry that and it'd make some fine hush puppies!

Japanese: （‥‥関心がないものには ものすごくタンパクだな）
English: (I'd better hang on to any evidence I want to keep...)

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: あっ！　アンタら！
English: Hey y'all!

Japanese: ナツミさん！
English: Lotta!

Japanese: 待っとったんやで！ ‥‥ビンゴやビンゴ！
English: Wait up a sec! We got bingo!

Japanese: ‥‥びんご？
English: Bingo...?

Japanese: 昨日の晩なぁ、２回、 自動シャッターが下りてん。
English: My automatic camera took two pictures last night!

Japanese: うんうん。
English: Hey!

Japanese: その２発目が、これや！ ちょっと、見てみィや！
English: This is them. Take a look!

Japanese: これは‥‥！
English: Wait...!

Japanese: な？　な？　思いっきり 撃っとるやろ？　ピストル。
English: See? See? He's shooting him with that pistol!

Japanese: え、ええ。
English: I-it looks like that, yes.

Japanese: でも、誰が撃ったかまでは 見えませんねー。
English: But you can't really tell who that is shooting.

Japanese: そーやねえ。 ‥‥キリが濃かったからなァ。
English: Yeah, well there was enough fog out there last night t' strangle a bullfrog.

Japanese: でもな。
English: But, y'know...

Japanese: この写真見て、ウチ、 思い出したんや。
English: Seeing these photos reminded me of something.

Japanese: な‥‥何をですか？
English: What...?

Japanese: この事件なァ、ウチ、 目撃しててん。
English: I saw the murder happen. I'm a witness!

Japanese: ええええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaaaaa!?

Japanese: ほ、ほんとですかナツミさん！
English: A-are you serious!?

Japanese: モチや。
English: Course!

Japanese: （ま‥‥また、トンデモナイ コトに‥‥）
English: (How do you forget... never mind.)

Japanese: なあなあなあ、これってヤッパ、 ケーサツにゆうた方がええよなァ？
English: Y'all reckon I should tell the cops?

Japanese: ‥‥ゆうた方が ええんちゃいます？
English: I... reckon so!

Japanese: アンタ！
English: What's that?

Japanese: インチキな関西弁、 使ォたらアカンで！
English: Now don't y'all go tryin' to mock my accent!

Japanese: ‥‥まァ、ええワ。
English: I'm a sensitive lady!

Japanese: ‥‥やめといた方が ええんちゃいます？
English: I... reckon no!

Japanese: アンタ！
English: What's that?

Japanese: インチキな関西弁で ゴマかそうゆうつもりやな！
English: Now don't y'all think y'all can pull one over on me just cause you speak the lingo!

Japanese: サツジンを見たら、ケーサツに タレこむ。
English: I know enough to know that when you see a murder, you'd best tell the cops!

Japanese: これは、市民のギムや。
English: It's my obligation as a citizen!

Japanese: ‥‥ほな、ウチ、ちょっと ケーサツ行ってくるわ。
English: Hey, so, I'm off to talk to the cops.

Japanese: この写真、やるから。
English: Y'all can have this photo.

Japanese: ほな。
English: Later.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょっと！ ナツミさん！
English: W-w-wait! Lotta!

Japanese: なんや？ ‥‥忙しいんや、ウチは。
English: What? Can't y'all see I'm kinda busy?

Japanese: ぼくたちにも、その話、 聞かせてくださいよ！
English: T-tell us what you saw, too! Please?

Japanese: アホ。
English: Nice try, honey, but I wasn't born yesterday.

Japanese: ウチ、目撃者やで。 セイギの味方、ケーサツの味方や。
English: I'm a witness, and that means I'm on the side of justice, and that means the cops!

Japanese: アンタらに教えるわけ、 ないやん。
English: I'd sooner eat the south side of a north-bound skunk than tell you!

Japanese: ひどい、ナツミさん‥‥。
English: L-Lotta!

Japanese: まま、ええやん。 昨日のテキは今日の友。逆か。
English: Don't let it get your skivvies in a bunch. Friends today, enemies tomorrow!

Japanese: とにかく、そーゆーことや。 ‥‥ほな、行くで。
English: Or was that the other way round? No matter. I'm gone!

Japanese: へへ。証言とかするんかなァ。 ‥‥楽しそうやなー。
English: Hey! Maybe they'll let me do some testifying! Hot darn!

Japanese: 行っちゃった‥‥。
English: She left...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... どうしよう。 目撃者、ふやしちゃったよ‥‥。
English: Well, that's one more witness. What do we do now, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥でも、見ちゃったものは しょうがないもんなあ‥‥）
English: (Well, if she saw something, there's not much we can do about it.)

Japanese: （問題は、“ナツミさんが 何を見たか”だけど‥‥）
English: (The question is: what exactly did she see...?)

Japanese: （それはどうやら、裁判まで 知りようがないな‥‥）
English: (I guess we'll find out in the trial tomorrow.)

Japanese: 証拠品《湖の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Lake Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... イトノコさん、いないね。
English: Looks like Detective Gumshoe isn't here.

Japanese: どうしたの？　おじょうちゃん？
English: Something wrong, miss?

Japanese: ん？　自首かな。
English: Hmm? Turning yourself in?

Japanese: 何やったの？　まんびき？
English: Okay, what did you do? Shoplifting? Larceny? Public indecency...?

Japanese: ち、ちち違いますよ！
English: N-no! None of those things!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事さん、 探してるんですけど‥‥。
English: We're looking for Detective Gumshoe... is he around?

Japanese: イトノコ‥‥？
English: Gumshoe?

Japanese: ああ。 今、捜査会議やってるよ。
English: Oh yeah. He's in a meeting right now.

Japanese: まだ、かかるんじゃないかなあ。
English: I don't think he'll be out any time soon.

Japanese: じゃあ、また来ます。
English: Okay, we'll come back.

Japanese: そうしなさい。
English: You do that.

Japanese: まっすぐ帰るんだよ。
English: Oh, and go straight home and stay out of trouble.

Japanese: まんびきなんかしちゃ ダメだからね。
English: No more shoplifting, you got that?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... あたし、悪人ヅラなのかな‥‥。
English: Do I look like a criminal or something?

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... イトノコさん、 まだカイギなのかな‥‥。
English: I guess Detective Gumshoe is still in that meeting.

Japanese: おおッ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: よく来たッス！
English: Thanks for coming down, pal!

Japanese: あっ！　イトノコさん！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 捜査会議が終わったッス。 ‥‥やれやれッス。
English: We just finished the meeting. For better or for worse...

Japanese: （あまり、いい知らせは なさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling we're in for some bad news...)

Japanese: 被害者のこと、 何かわかりましたか？
English: Do you know anything about the victim yet?

Japanese: それが‥‥。 まだ、身元がわからないッス。
English: No, no... Still can't I.D. him.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、なんて‥‥？
English: Has Mr. Edgeworth said anything?

Japanese: 何も言ってくれないッス‥‥。
English: Not a word.

Japanese: どうなりましたか？ ‥‥捜査会議。
English: So, how did the meeting go?

Japanese: そんなこと、弁護士になんか 話せるワケ、ないッス！
English: I can't tell you, pal! You're a lawyer!

Japanese: そ、そうですよね‥‥。
English: T-true...
Y'know...

Japanese: 自分はもう、何を 信じていいかわからねッス。
English: I don't know what to believe anymore.

Japanese: たしかに、御剣検事も ワレワレと同じ、ニンゲンッス。
English: Sure, Mr. Edgeworth's human like you or me.

Japanese: でも。
English: Still...

Japanese: もしあの人がツミを犯したら、 かくさないと思うッス。
English: I get the feeling that if he'd done something wrong, he wouldn't go hiding it.

Japanese: あの人は、そういう人ッス。
English: That's just the kind of guy he is.

Japanese: なんでみんな、それが わからないッスか！
English: Why can't anyone else see that?

Japanese: やっぱり、御剣検事が 犯人ということで‥‥？
English: So, they think that Mr. Edgeworth did it...?

Japanese: 明日の裁判は、予定どおり やるッス。
English: Well, the trial's starting tomorrow, as scheduled.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... ‥‥捜査会議のこと、結局 ゼンブしゃべっちゃいましたね。
English: Umm... hey, in the end you did tell us about the meeting!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 みんなにはヒミツッス。 ...
English: Don't go telling anyone else, pal.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes, sir!

Japanese: とにかく、おねがいするッス！
English: And... do me a favor!

Japanese: どうか、御剣検事を見捨てないで ほしいッス！
English: Stand by Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: あの人には今、味方が必要ッス！
English: He needs help, and you're the ones to help him!

Japanese: 何も話してくれないのは、 きっと理由があるハズッス！
English: I'm sure he's got some reason why he won't talk to us.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥。
English: Thanks, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: なぜ、そこまで御剣検事を 信じられるんですか？
English: How come you trust Mr. Edgeworth so much?

Japanese: そんなこと、トーゼンッス。
English: Well, I'd think that was obvious.

Japanese: ワレワレには、カターい 信頼関係があるッス。
English: We got a strong working relationship, us two.

Japanese: おたがいを信じているから、 いっしょにがんばれるッス。
English: We trust each other, and that's how it works.

Japanese: “信頼関係”‥‥？
English: A "working relationship"?

Japanese: 御剣検事は、起訴された被告人を 必ず有罪にするッス。
English: See, Mr. Edgeworth always gets his defendants declared "guilty" every time.

Japanese: たしかに、行きすぎた 方法を使うときもあるッス。
English: Yeah, his methods might be a little extreme at times.

Japanese: でも、それは‥‥
English: But... there's a reason!

Japanese: ワレワレの捜査を、信頼して くれているからこそッス。
English: He trusts our investigation, see? He trusts us to get the right man!

Japanese: そして、その信頼に こたえるために、
English: That's why I work extra hard, pal.

Japanese: ワレワレは、必死で捜査を しなければならないッス。
English: We've got to earn that trust he places in us.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんですか。
English: I see...

Japanese: 御剣検事は、信頼できる 人物ッス！
English: Mr. Edgeworth is a man you can trust...

Japanese: それだけは、この糸鋸　圭介、 誓って言えるッス！
English: And you have my word on that!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥。 解剖記録、どうなりました？
English: I was wondering, did you ever get that autopsy report?

Japanese: ああ。それッス。 コピーしといたッス。
English: Oh, that? Yeah, I made a copy for you.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Added Autopsy Report to the Court Record.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。なるほどくん？
English: ...Nick?

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ちょっと見せて‥‥ その、被害者の写真。
English: Can you show me that photo of the victim?
That face...!

Japanese: 知ってる人？
English: Someone you know?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん、わからない。
English: I... I don't know.

Japanese: ムカシどこかで‥‥ 会ったことがあるような‥‥。
English: I just have this feeling that I met him somewhere a long time ago.
(Maya seems lost in thought.)

Japanese: どう？　何か思いだした？
English: You remember anything?
(Guess not.)

Japanese: 何か役に立てば、なによりッス。
English: Well, I hope it helps you, pal.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、思い当たるコト、 ないッスね‥‥。
English: Sorry, I'm drawing a blank right now.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... またいないよ、イトノコさん。
English: Not a Gumshoe in sight.

Japanese: イトノコなら、取り調べ室だよ。
English: If you're looking for Detective Gumshoe, he's in the questioning room.

Japanese: ちょっと、重要な証人が あらわれたんでね。
English: Apparently, an important witness turned up.

Japanese: 時間、かかると思うよ。
English: He'll be in there a while.

Japanese: ナツミさんだよ‥‥。
English: Lotta Hart... it has to be.

Japanese: まいったなあ‥‥。
English: Uh oh...

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: どういうことッスか！
English: What's going on here!?

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: W-what's wrong, Detective?

Japanese: さっき、ヘンな女が たずねて来たッス！
English: This wild lady comes in here just a while ago...

Japanese: “なるほどーさんの しょうかいで来ましてん”とか
English: Says she came "to talk to y'all after hearing what Mr. Wright had to say"!

Japanese: 言ってたッス！
English: What's this all about, pal!?

Japanese: （‥‥ナツミさんか‥‥）
English: (Lotta Hart...)

Japanese: 目撃者、ふやして どーするッスか！
English: Why are you going around finding more witnesses!?

Japanese: 御剣検事を死刑にする気ッスか！
English: You want to give Mr. Edgeworth the death sentence, pal!?

Japanese: い、いや、でも‥‥。
English: N-no, not at all...

Japanese: 見ちゃったモノは しょうがないですよ。
English: Just... I mean, she did see something. There's nothing I can do about that.

Japanese: かくすワケにもいかないでしょう？
English: I can't go around covering up evidence!

Japanese: ううううう。
English: Er...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、キベンッス！
English: You trying to say something about the way I do my job?

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: No sir!

Japanese: それで‥‥ナツミさんは、なんて？
English: So... what did Ms. Hart say?

Japanese: 御剣検事がピストルを撃つところを 見ていたそうッス。
English: She says she saw Mr. Edgeworth fire the pistol.

Japanese: えええええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: しかも、証拠写真まであったッス。
English: She even had a photograph to prove it.

Japanese: ああ。 ‥‥それなら、ぼくも見ましたよ。
English: Right. I saw it too...

Japanese: この写真じゃあ、誰が撃ったか 見えませんけどね‥‥。
English: But you really can't tell from the photo who it is shooting.

Japanese: 彼女、これからその写真を 拡大するって言ってたッス。
English: That's why she said she's going to enlarge the photo.

Japanese: 画像はアラくなるけど、もう少し ハッキリするはずや！　‥‥とか。
English: She said "it'll drop the quality a mite, but should let us see who's who!"

Japanese: カ、カクダイ‥‥。
English: She can do that!?

Japanese: （間違いなく、御剣に不利な証拠 として、拡大写真が出てくるぞ）
English: (Okay, so there's going to be an enlarged photograph that shows Edgeworth in the act.)

Japanese: （‥‥マズいなあ）
English: (Great. Just great.)

Japanese: とにかく、明日の証人は 彼女ッス。
English: In any case, she's going to be the one testifying tomorrow.

Japanese: あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: もう１人の目撃者は どうしたんですか？
English: What happened to the other witness?

Japanese: それが‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: とりあえず、キャンセルに なったみたいッス。
English: Apparently, there was a cancellation.

Japanese: （“キャンセル”‥‥？）
English: (A "cancellation"...!?)

Japanese: 明日は、御剣検事にとっては、 いきなり絶体絶命ッス。
English: I'm afraid tomorrow is going to be life or death for poor Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 目撃者は、ハッキリ犯行の シュンカンを見ていると言うし、
English: We got a witness who says she saw the very moment of the murder.

Japanese: 発砲したときの写真まで 出てくるッス。
English: And we got a photo taken when the shot rang out.

Japanese: （‥‥どう考えても、 勝てそうもないぞ‥‥）
English: (I'd say that sounds like a pretty unwinnable case...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥。 千尋さんが教えてくれた）
English: (But wait... What did Mia used to say?)

Japanese: （アイツが無罪なら、どこかに 見落としがあるんだ‥‥）
English: (If he's innocent, there's got to be something I've overlooked...)

Japanese: 御剣検事は、国選弁護人を たのむつもりらしいッス。
English: It sounds like Mr. Edgeworth is going to ask the state to assign a public defender.

Japanese: さっき、手続きをたのまれたッス。
English: I was just asked to file the paperwork.

Japanese: でも、まだ間に合うッス！
English: But you still got time, pal!

Japanese: もう一度、検事に会って ほしいッス！
English: Go talk to him again, for me, please!

Japanese: そして‥‥アンタが！
English: You have to convince him!

Japanese: アンタが、彼の弁護をするッス！ おねがいッス！
English: You have to make him let you defend him! Please!

Japanese: 彼を助けられるのは、 アンタしかいないッス！
English: I know you're the only one who can do it, pal. You're the only one who can save Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: もらってきましたよ。これ。
English: Look what I got.
Hey, you did it, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥ギリギリまで待った カイがあったッス！
English: Glad I waited till the last minute to file those papers!

Japanese: じゃあ、さっそく手続きを しておくッス！
English: I'll rip 'em up and start new ones for you!

Japanese: おねがいします。
English: Thanks, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、明日、法廷で。
English: Well, see you in court tomorrow, then.

Japanese: よろしく、たのむッス。
English: Good luck, pal.

Japanese: おっ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: そういえば、さっき地震が あったッスが、
English: You guys feel that earthquake a little while back?

Japanese: だいじょうぶだったッスか？
English: I was worried!

Japanese: やだなあ。
English: Worried?

Japanese: 地震ぐらい、どうってこと ないですよお。
English: We're fine! I've lived out here my whole life. I'm pretty used to them by now.

Japanese: アンタたちじゃないッス。
English: Oh, I wasn't worried about you two.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、だいじょうぶ だったッスか？
English: I was worried about Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば‥‥）
English: (Oh, right...)

Japanese: なんか、オーバーに こわがっていましたけど‥‥。
English: He did seem to over-react a little, now that you mention it.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。さっきの地震は ちょっと大きかったッスからね。
English: Yeah, well, I'm not surprised. It was a pretty big quake.

Japanese: ちょっと、見てくるッス。
English: I'm going to go check on him.

Japanese: アンタたちは、明日にそなえて メシ食って寝るッス。
English: You two go eat and get your rest for tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: じゃッ！
English: Later!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... どういうこと？　なるほどくん？ 御剣検事、地震がニガテなの？
English: I wonder what it is with Mr. Edgeworth and earthquakes?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: I wonder...

Japanese: そんなの、聞いたことないぞ。
English: He was never that scared of them when he was in school.

Japanese: （ぼくたちがいっしょだったのは、 小学４年生の１年間）
English: (Then again, I was only in the same class as him for 4th grade.)

Japanese: （その後、御剣は転校していった）
English: (He transferred to another school after that.)

Japanese: （‥‥いったい、やつの身に 何があったんだろうか‥‥）
English: (I wonder what happened to Edgeworth...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: アンタに見せてもらいたいのは、 ただ１つ！
English: Hey, pal, there's only one thing I want to see from you!

Japanese: 御剣検事からの依頼状ッス！
English: And that's a letter of request from Mr. Edgeworth for you to be his defense attorney!

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 25 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりだなあ。 センセイの事務所。
English: It's been a while since I was here last.

Japanese: あいかわらず、 お出かけ中らしいな。
English: Mr. Grossberg is out as usual.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。 ‥‥あそこのカベ。
English: Hey, Nick. Look at the wall.

Japanese: （あの絵‥‥。 かえってこないんだ‥‥）
English: (That painting is still missing...)

Japanese: （千尋さん、星影センセイが 小中に脅迫されていたこと）
English: (Mia must have known about the deal with Mr. Grossberg and Redd White...)

Japanese: （もちろん、 知っていたんだろうな‥‥）
English: (She kept track of all of White's extortion and blackmail rackets.)

Japanese: あそこのカベ、なんで 色が違うんだろ？
English: Why is the wall there a different color?

Japanese: この前まで、大きな絵が かかっていたんだよ。
English: A big painting was hanging there until recently.

Japanese: ふうん。 ‥‥どうしたの、その絵？
English: Huh. What happened to it?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ヒトに あげちゃったんだ。
English: Uh... well, he gave it to someone.

Japanese: ‥‥わあ。 “オイラクの恋”ってヤツだね。
English: I bet he gave it to some romantic interest! Love blooms eternal, Nick!

Japanese: ま、そんなトコかな。
English: Uh... yeah.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 あたしも、あんな机がいいなあ。
English: Hey, Nick! I want a desk like that in our office too!

Japanese: まだまだ、ぼくには にあわないと思うよ。
English: I don't know. I don't think I'm ready to sit at a desk like that yet.
Huh?

Japanese: あたしが使うんだよ？
English: I meant for me!

Japanese: ‥‥おこづかいためて買えば。
English: You'd better start saving your allowance, then.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、かな？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: うちも、お客さんのテーブルに 何か置こうよ。
English: We should put things on our client table too.

Japanese: タバコ、キライなんだけど。
English: I'm not really into smoking.

Japanese: あめとか、ガムでもいいよ。
English: We could put out candy, or gum.

Japanese: ‥‥いいね、それ。
English: Classy...

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: でも、うちのチャーリーの方が かわいいよね。
English: I think our Charley is cuter.

Japanese: ま、まあね。
English: Right. He's a really cute... plant.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... They don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、うちの事務所に もらっていこうよ。
English: Well then let's take them back to our office! He won't miss them!

Japanese: いらないよ。
English: Uh, we don't need them.

Japanese: せめて、あの木ボリのクマさん だけでも。
English: Then, can we take that wooden bear?

Japanese: （うん。‥‥あれはほしいな）
English: (Hmm. It is kind of cute.)

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 25 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: （お‥‥この、ある種ナツカシイ セキ払いは！）
English: (Ah, that old familiar clearing of the throat!)

Japanese: あー、チミ！ たしか、千尋クンの‥‥！
English: Ah hah! You're Mia's... something, are you not?

Japanese: はい。弟子の成歩堂です。
English: I was her understudy, yes. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: おー、それにチミは！ もしかして、千尋クンの‥‥！
English: Ah hah! And you, you're Mia's... something too, are you not?

Japanese: はい！　妹の真宵です！
English: Her little sister, yes!

Japanese: 大きくなったのォ！
English: You've grown!

Japanese: なんぢゃ、お姉さんに よく似てきたワイ。
English: You've come to look a lot like your sister, you know?

Japanese: 思い出すのォ、あのコロを。
English: It takes me back.

Japanese: “青春の日々はぁ‥‥青いレモンの カオリぃ‥‥”かァ。
English: "Ahh... the days of my youth... like the scent of fresh lemon..." you see.

Japanese: あの。センセイ。
English: Um, Mr. Grossberg, sir?
Hmm...?

Japanese: スマン。若いモンが、用もなく こんなところに来るワケ、ないな。
English: Ah, yes. I beg your pardon. Of course you came here to discuss something.

Japanese: どうしたんぢゃ。 何かあったのかな。
English: What is it then? Something the matter?

Japanese: 昨日の夜、殺人事件が あったんですけど‥‥。
English: There was a murder last night...

Japanese: ほお。
English: A murder?

Japanese: 知らないみたいですね。
English: You haven't heard...?

Japanese: さっき、 起きたところなんでなァ‥‥。
English: I, er, just got up, you see.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍が、ピストルで 男を撃ったんです。
English: Well, Miles Edgeworth shot someone with a pistol.

Japanese: ナニッ！　‥‥御剣とな‥‥？
English: Edgeworth!? What!?

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、ダレを！
English: W-who!?

Japanese: それが‥‥被害者の身元は 不明らしいんです‥‥。
English: Well, the identity of the victim is still unknown.

Japanese: ‥‥なんということぢゃ‥‥。
English: T-this is terrible news indeed!

Japanese: （センセイ、事件のことは 何も知らないらしいな‥‥）
English: (I guess he hadn't heard anything...)

Japanese: センセイ。 ‥‥あの絵は‥‥？
English: Mr. Grossberg? Whatever happened to that painting?

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Oh, yes...

Japanese: もう、帰ってこないぢゃろうな。
English: I do not think it shall ever be coming back home to this office.

Japanese: ヒガイ届けなぞ、出せるワケが ないからのォ。
English: I can't exactly claim it as stolen...

Japanese: ‥‥これも、ムクイなんぢゃ。 “老い”“悔い”“ムクイ”‥‥。
English: I suppose it's my just desserts. Old, bitter desserts.

Japanese: いったい、ナマクラさんって、 どういうヒトなんですか？
English: Who is this Hammond guy, anyway?

Japanese: この男はな‥‥。
English: Mr. Hammond...

Japanese: あの事件の弁護をしたんぢゃ。
English: He was the defense attorney in that case...

Japanese: “あの事件”‥‥？
English: "That case"...?

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》ぢゃ。
English: Yes, the "DL-6 Incident."

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件？ （聞いたことがあるような‥‥）
English: "DL-6"...? (Why does that sound so familiar?)

Japanese: おぼえておるかの。
English: Perhaps you remember?

Japanese: （たしか、千尋さんの事件の ときに聞いた‥‥）
English: (I'm sure someone mentioned it during the trial for Mia's murder.)

Japanese: 警察が、被害者の霊媒までして 犯人をつきとめようとした事件。
English: That was the incident where the police were so at a loss they used a spirit medium.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! も、もしかして‥‥それ。
English: Wait... you don't mean?

Japanese: あたしのおかあさんが‥‥？
English: Was that medium my mother?

Japanese: そうぢゃ。
English: Yes, my dear.

Japanese: 霊媒師・綾里　舞子が 被害者の霊媒をおこない、
English: The spirit medium, Misty Fey, your mother, contacted the spirit of the victim.

Japanese: そして‥‥シッパイした 事件ぢゃ‥‥。
English: But... the case was a loss. No conviction was made.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件‥‥。
English: The DL-6 Incident, yes...

Japanese: 今から１５年前に起こった、 奇妙な殺人事件ぢゃ。
English: Happened 15 years ago. A very strange case, indeed.

Japanese: たしか、犯人はまだ つかまっていない、とか‥‥。
English: They never caught the criminal, right?

Japanese: そうぢゃ。
English: Correct.

Japanese: 綾里　舞子が霊媒で被害者を 呼び出して犯人を聞いた。
English: Misty Fey used her powers to talk to the spirit of the late victim.

Japanese: その霊媒によって、１人の男が 逮捕されたが、
English: Her testimony led to charges being laid against one man.

Japanese: この、ナマクラ弁護士が 無罪にしたんぢゃよ。
English: But Mr. Hammond won the case and the suspect was declared innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... ‥‥おかあさんは、そのせいで 警察にサギだ、って‥‥。
English: And the police blamed my mother, calling her a fraud.

Japanese: それを助けてくれたのが、 星影センセイなんですよね‥‥？
English: You were the one who helped her out then, right, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: う‥‥うム。そ、そうぢゃ‥‥。
English: Er... y-yes. Yes, quite.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you!

Japanese: い‥‥いや。 れ、礼など言わないでクレ‥‥。
English: N-no... please. D-don't mention it.

Japanese: （こんなときに、またこの 名前を聞こうとは‥‥）
English: (Never thought I'd hear that name again...)

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But wait...

Japanese: その事件が、御剣検事と 何かカンケイがあるんですか？
English: What does that case have anything to do with Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: “何か”どころぢゃない。
English: It has everything to do with Mr. Edgeworth, my dear!

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の被害者は、 御剣　怜侍の父親なんぢゃよ。
English: The victim in the DL-6 Incident was none other than his father, Gregory Edgeworth!

Japanese: なんですってェェェッ！ お、おとうさん‥‥？
English: Wh-what!? His father!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... くわしいことは、本人の口から 聞きタマエ。
English: If you want to know more, you should ask him yourself.

Japanese: これを見せるんぢゃ。 ‥‥きっとタイドが変わるぢゃろ。
English: Show him this... I'm sure he'll talk to you.

Japanese: これは‥‥。
English: Wait...

Japanese: おかあさんの写真だ‥‥。
English: This is a photograph of my mother!

Japanese: 《綾里　舞子の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Misty Fey's Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: チミも、リッパになった もんぢゃのォ。
English: You've really grown up in the past few years, my dear!

Japanese: 千尋クンも、そのすがたを 見たら、喜んだぢゃろうに。
English: How proud Mia would be if she could see you now!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、まだまだ 不安だ、って言ってたよ。
English: She said she was more worried about me than anything.

Japanese: うるさいな。‥‥ちょっとぐらい、 いい気分にさせてくれよ。
English: Aww, just go on and take the compliment, will you?

Japanese: こ、この男は‥‥
English: Hmm? Strange...

Japanese: はて。見たことがあるような、 ないような‥‥。
English: I feel as though I've seen this man somewhere before!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... ああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 思い出しましたか？
English: Did you remember!?

Japanese: この事務所にいた弁護士ぢゃ！
English: He was a lawyer! Here, in my office!

Japanese: “生倉　雪夫（なまくらゆきお）” ぢゃよ！
English: That's Hammond! Robert Hammond!

Japanese: ナマクラさん‥‥？
English: Mr. Hammond...?

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍が、この男を 射殺したというのか‥‥。
English: And you say this is the man Miles Edgeworth shot?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　舞子さん。
English: Misty Fey.

Japanese: 千尋クンと、真宵さんの おかあさんぢゃ。
English: Mother of Mia and Maya Fey.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に 見せてやるといい。
English: Show this photograph to Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 何か、しゃべる気に なるぢゃろう。
English: He'll have something to say to you then.

Japanese: これが‥‥犯行の瞬間、かね。
English: So this is the moment the crime took place, eh?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ハッキリ御剣とは、わからんな。
English: You can't really say for sure that's Edgeworth.

Japanese: それが、あの男にとって 幸運なのか、不運なのか‥‥。
English: I'm not sure whether that's a good thing or a bad thing.

Japanese: ワシには、なんとも言えん‥‥。
English: Not sure at all...

Japanese: ハレツ音に反応して シャッターを切るカメラ‥‥？
English: So, this camera on the lake takes a photo whenever it hears an explosion sound?

Japanese: そうなんですよ。
English: That's what she said.

Japanese: そんなモンが、ツゴウよく セットされていたと‥‥？
English: And it just happened to be set up on the beach, pointing out on the lake?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥“流星を撮る”って 言ってましたけど。
English: Yes... she said it was to photograph "shooting stars."

Japanese: “流星”と“ハレツ音”‥‥。 なんのカンケイもないのォ。
English: Hmm. Shooting stars... explosions... what's the connection?

Japanese: ちょっとクサいの。 ‥‥その婦女子。
English: I'd be suspicious of this witness of yours.

Japanese: すまんの。
English: Er... my apologies.

Japanese: ワシにわかることは ないようぢゃ。
English: I'm not sure I can help you with that.

Japanese: “ひょうたん湖　自然公園” と書いてある。
English: The sign says "Gourd Lake Nature Park."

Japanese: 休日は、家族づれでにぎわう、 都会のオアシスといったところだ。
English: This place is full of families picnicking on the weekend.

Japanese: でも、滝はないんだよね。
English: But... no waterfall.

Japanese: 都会の人間は、 修行はしないからね。
English: Not many picnickers come here for spiritual training, Maya.

Japanese: 葉を落とした木々に、 冬の訪れを感じる今日このごろ。
English: I feel winter's chill from the bare leaf trees today...

Japanese: うーん。冬は人を 詩人にさせるなあ。
English: *sigh* What is it about winter that turns people into poets?

Japanese: ねー、サムいよなるほどくん。 早くどこか行こうよ。
English: I don't know, but my toes are starting to feel numb.

Japanese: （サムいって、ぼくの詩か？）
English: (Yes... my poetry has that effect on some people.)

Japanese: プラスチック製の ベンチが並んでいる。
English: A lineup of plastic benches.

Japanese: “マンジュウを買って、食って 行け”というつもりだろうが、
English: I guess the idea is you buy a dog and eat it here.

Japanese: こう寒くては、座るヤツも いないだろう。
English: I doubt anyone would sit here and eat on a day like this.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、マンジュウがあれば、 真宵ちゃんなら座るかも。
English: Except maybe Maya... if she had a Samurai Dog.

Japanese: 妙に目立たない道しるべが 立っている。
English: Huh... I almost didn't see that signpost.

Japanese: “左‥‥ボートのりば” “右‥‥公園出口”
English: "Left - Boat Docks" "Right - Exit"

Japanese: マンジュウ屋の屋台らしい。 ‥‥今は、やっていない。
English: A hotdog stand. It's closed...

Japanese: 和風のたたずまいに、悪趣味な クリスマスのかざりつけ。
English: The Christmas fringe looks a little half-baked.

Japanese: そして、ノボリには “とのさまんじう”の文字。
English: The banner reads "Samurai Dogs"...

Japanese: ‥‥この節操のなさは すごいな‥‥。
English: Somebody needs to redecorate.

Japanese: ゴミは入っていない。
English: The trashcan is empty.

Japanese: この公園は、管理のよさが ウリだからな‥‥。
English: At least the place is well maintained.

Japanese: あ。ベンチだよなるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick, benches!

Japanese: おマンジュウ食べながら、 ちょっと休もうか。
English: Let's take a break... maybe have a dog?

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: I think not.

Japanese: ‥‥このサムいのに、 ベンチでマンジュウもないよ。
English: It's too cold to sit and eat hotdogs out here!

Japanese: ダメだねー都会っ子は。
English: Wimpy city boy!

Japanese: 滝にでも打たれて、もっと キタえないと！
English: You should try standing under a freezing waterfall some time!

Japanese: ふーん。 ‥‥広いね。ひょうたん湖。
English: Wow. Gourd Lake is really big.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。 どうして“ひょうたん湖”なの？
English: Say, Nick. Why is it called "Gourd Lake"?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: ムカシこの湖は、ひょうたんの 名産地だったんだ。
English: Well, a long time ago, they used to grow gourds here.

Japanese: えっ！　それは意外！
English: Whoa! No way!

Japanese: あたしはまた、上から見たら ひょうたんの形、とか、
English: I was sure it was because the lake looked like a gourd when viewed from above!

Japanese: そんなありふれた理由かと 思ってたよ‥‥。
English: You know, like an hourglass shape?

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そういう説も あるみたいだけど。
English: Well, it is shaped like a gourd, actually. But that's just a coincidence.

Japanese: へー、なるほどねえ‥‥。
English: Oh. Okay.

Japanese: それにしても “トノサマンジュウ”ねえ‥‥。
English: I can't get over the Samurai Dogs...

Japanese: しかも、“元祖”だよ。
English: The "Original" Samurai Dogs, no less.

Japanese: いや、ホレ。“元祖”って、 言ったモン勝ちだからサ。
English: Hey, man, whoever calls their product the "original" first wins!

Japanese: ついでに“本舗”とか つけといたらどうだ？
English: Why don't you add "world- famous" to the sign?

Japanese: おっ！　それ、いただき！
English: Hey, good idea!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (What have I done...?)

Japanese: 高そうなカメラが 湖面に向けてセットされている。
English: An expensive-looking camera faces the lake.

Japanese: その横には、大きなマイクと、 青いシートをかぶった‥‥
English: Next to it is a large microphone and a blue plastic sheet.

Japanese: これは、コンピュータみたいだな。 カメラにつながっている。
English: Hmm. Looks like a computer is attached to the camera.

Japanese: カンバンにはヒトコト、 “キャンプ禁止”と書かれている。
English: The sign says "No Camping."

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、その前に キャンプを張るとは‥‥。
English: Funny place to pick to pitch your tent.

Japanese: じゃあ！　もしこのカンバンが “放火禁止”だったら‥‥！
English: Wait, what if the sign said "No Setting Tents on Fire"?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... そんなわけないだろ。
English: I don't think they have signs like that.

Japanese: そうかなあ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ずいぶんランボーに あつかわれているらしい。
English: This SUV has seen better days.

Japanese: ボディのそこら中が デコボコしている。
English: It's dented all over.

Japanese: こんなところまで クルマで入ってくるとは‥‥。
English: I can't believe anyone would drive their car down here.

Japanese: ビニールシートの上には、 食料に、情報雑誌。
English: There's food and some magazines on the sheet.

Japanese: この寒空の下でキャンプとは。 ‥‥そうとうのツワモノだな。
English: It takes a pretty tough skin to camp in this cold.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: なんだ、また食いたいとか 言うんじゃないだろうな。
English: What, don't tell me you're hungry again?

Japanese: ちがうよ。
English: No, no.

Japanese: なんで飯ごうって、ペコッて ヘコんでるの？
English: I was just wondering, why are camping pots and pans made of aluminum?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 六法全書には 書いてなかったよ。
English: They didn't talk about that in any of the law books.

Japanese: じゃあ、法律で決まってる わけじゃないんだ‥‥。
English: So, there's no law saying they have to be made out of aluminum, then!

Japanese: （勝手にナットクして くれているみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I'm not having this conversation...)

Japanese: さまざまな木が ビッシリ立ちならんでいる。
English: The trees grow quite thick here.

Japanese: 奥の方は、闇に溶けている。 陽の光がとどいていないのだろう。
English: Further back, the trees fade into the shadow where the sunlight can't reach them.

Japanese: 貸しボート屋の 管理小屋だ。
English: A small boat rental shop.

Japanese: 人がいるようなケハイはない。
English: Doesn't look like anyone is around.

Japanese: 事件があったせいだろう。 今日は営業していないようだ。
English: They're probably closed because it's Christmas.

Japanese: この奥も森になっているみたいだ。
English: There's more forest off that way.

Japanese: こっちの方に行っても、 調査のヤクには立たないだろう。
English: I doubt I'd find any helpful clues in there.

Japanese: 湖の上に、ボートが浮かんでいる。
English: There are some boats floating at the dock.

Japanese: 殺人事件は、このボートの上で 起こったわけか‥‥。
English: Was one of these boats used in the murder, I wonder?

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?
Huh?

Japanese: やっぱり、乗りに来るの、 やめよう。
English: I changed my mind. I don't really want to go for a boat ride.

Japanese: 警察のマスコットキャラクター ‥‥って、こんなんだったっけ？
English: Hey, is that the police department's mascot...?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger"!

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。 ワシだけのマスコットさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! It's my mascot!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: I... see. How nice.

Japanese: コイツを刑事課のマスコットに するのが、ワシの最後の仕事だよ。
English: I'll get him assigned mascot of the Criminal Affairs Dept. if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: ‥‥がんばってください。
English: Um, good luck!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “クニのおっかさん、 泣いてるぞ‥‥”
English: "...your mama could see you know she'd be cryin'..."

Japanese: “吐いちまえよ。 ラクになるぞ‥‥”
English: "...a break and spit it out, you lowlife scumbag..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 取り調べのイメージ・トレーニング をしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for interrogations.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ “ひょうたん湖で‥‥”
English: Wha...!? "Gourd Lake...!"

Japanese: “ヒョッシーを目撃”だと‥‥！
English: "Gourdy sighted"!!! I don't believe it!

Japanese: （‥‥もっとダイジな事件が あるだろう！）
English: (Shouldn't you be reading something more important...?)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “よーし、全員カベに向かって 手をつくんだ‥‥”
English: "All right, hands against the wall, all o' you..."

Japanese: “逃げようったってムダだぜ。 オレは背中に目があるんだ‥‥”
English: "Don't even think about escaping! I got eyes in the back of my head!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 現場に踏み込むときのイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for arrests.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “なるべく目立たないカッコウで、 まわりにとけ込む‥‥”
English: "...move in the crowd. Wear drab clothes..."

Japanese: “ターゲットの視界には ゼッタイ入ってはイケナイ‥‥”
English: "Never enter the target's field of vision..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 尾行するときのイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for trailing.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “近所の人との接触は、 ゼッタイに避けるべし‥‥”
English: "Never talk to people in the neighborhood..."

Japanese: “部屋のデンキは消して、 カーテンは閉じておく‥‥”
English: "Turn all the lights in the room off, and close the curtains..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 張り込みするときのイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for a stake-out.

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前９時４４分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 26, 9:44 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥カルマ？
English: Karma...?

Japanese: そう。 狩魔　豪（かるまごう）検事。
English: That's right, Manfred von Karma. He's the best prosecutor there is.

Japanese: ‥‥この４０年間、まだ一度も 負けたことがない。
English: He hasn't lost a case in his 40-year career.

Japanese: まさに、奇跡を生きる男だ。
English: He is a god of prosecution, Wright! A god!

Japanese: い‥‥一度も‥‥？
English: Not a single case?

Japanese: 彼は、有罪判決のためならば それこそ、なんでもする。
English: He'll do anything to get a guilty verdict, anything.

Japanese: なんか、お前みたいだな、御剣。
English: Hmm. Sounds like someone else I know, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: わかっていない。
English: You don't understand.

Japanese: 彼はまさに“なんでも”する。 ‥‥恐ろしい人なんだ。
English: I mean he'll really do anything. Manfred von Karma is a man to be feared.

Japanese: （証拠をデッチ上げるような男が ナニを言ってるんだよ‥‥）
English: (That's quite a claim coming from someone who forges evidence...)

Japanese: 私は、彼の下で“検事”というもの をテッテー的に、たたきこまれた。
English: He taught me what it really means to "prosecute."

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 私の１０倍タチの悪い男、 と考えてもらいたい。
English: Just picture a prosecutor as vicious as me... multiplied by a factor of ten.

Japanese: うえー‥‥。
English: Ugh...

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、御剣さんの お師匠さんなんですか？　その人。
English: So... so was he your teacher, then, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: うム、‥‥まあ、そんなものだな。
English: Something like that...

Japanese: ヒドい人だね！　カワイイ弟子を 有罪にしようなんて‥‥
English: And now he's trying to get you found guilty!? What a creep!

Japanese: あ、それとも。
English: Oh, wait...

Japanese: ワザと手を抜いて、御剣さんを ムザイに‥‥
English: Maybe he's planning on losing on purpose to help you out!

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずだ。
English: Not a chance...

Japanese: 彼は、この４０年間、 負けたことがない。
English: He hasn't lost once in 40 years. 40 years!

Japanese: そして、 私の２０倍、タチが悪いのだ。
English: He's as ruthless as me, times twenty!

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクだぁ。
English: That's pretty ruthless...

Japanese: 私にとっては、神のような存在だ。
English: Like I said. He's a god among prosecutors.

Japanese: （ぼくにとっての千尋さん みたいなもんか‥‥）
English: (I guess that's something like Mia was to me.)

Japanese: （千尋さんといえば‥‥）
English: (Speaking of Mia...)

Japanese: あのさ。
English: Um, Maya?

Japanese: なあに？
English: Uh huh?

Japanese: こんなときこそ、千尋さんの チカラを借りたいと思うんだけど。
English: We could really be using Mia's help right now... don't you think?
Oh...

Japanese: ？ ? ダメなの。
English: I can't.

Japanese: ‥‥がんばってみたんだけど、 お姉ちゃんに、とどかないの。
English: Sorry. I tried, I really tried, but I couldn't reach.

Japanese: “とどかない”？
English: You couldn't "reach"?

Japanese: 最近、修行の方も お休みしてるから‥‥
English: I think it's because I haven't been training.

Japanese: 霊力が、また弱く なっちゃったみたい‥‥。
English: My powers are weak again...

Japanese: ‥‥こまったな‥‥ このカンジンなときに。
English: Oh man, what bad timing!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメンね。
English: I'm really sorry.

Japanese: なんとか、がんばってみるから。
English: I'll try my best!

Japanese: たのむよ。
English: I hope so!

Japanese: ‥‥何をコソコソやっている？
English: What are you whispering about?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥なんでもない。
English: O-oh, it's nothing.

Japanese: そろそろ時間だな。 ‥‥行こうか。
English: Well, it's time. Let's head in...

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: December 26, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: これより、御剣　怜侍の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... あの‥‥狩魔検事。 準備の方は‥‥？
English: Erm, Mr. von Karma? Is the prosecution ready?
Fool...

Japanese: 準備もなく、このワガハイが ここに立つわけがなかろう。
English: You seriously think that I would stand here were I not completely prepared?

Japanese: は、はい。 ‥‥スミマセン。
English: R-right, my apologies!

Japanese: （弱ェ！）
English: (He's even got the Judge scared!)

Japanese: あ、では、 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 決定的な証拠。
English: Decisive evidence...

Japanese: 決定的な証人。
English: A decisive witness...

Japanese: ‥‥他に何か、必要なものは？
English: What else could possibly be required?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。それで じゅうぶんだと思います。
English: Ah... er, nothing of course. That should be fine.

Japanese: では、さっそく証人を 呼んでいただきましょう。
English: The prosecution may call its first witness.

Japanese: な、なんか“カク”が 違うよ、あのヒト‥‥。
English: What's with this guy? Is he royalty or something?

Japanese: ヤダなあ、あんなのと やり合うのかよ‥‥。
English: How am I supposed to fight against this!?

Japanese: 捜査にあたった イトノコギリ刑事を。
English: I call the detective in charge of this case, Detective Dick Gumshoe.

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事か‥‥）
English: (Okay, Gumshoe's first... Let's see how this goes.)

Japanese: まず、事件の説明をしたまえ。
English: Describe the incident. Now!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-yessir!

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ キンチョーしてるみたいだな）
English: (Detective Gumshoe looks nervous...)

Japanese: では、その、上面図を 見ていただくッス。
English: Er, please take a look at the map.

Japanese: 事件は、クリスマスイブの 深夜、零時ごろ起こったッス。
English: The murder happened late Christmas Eve, around midnight.

Japanese: 湖の中央に、ボートが１つ。
English: There was one boat in the very middle of the lake.

Japanese: ボートの上には、男が２人。
English: There were two men on the boat.

Japanese: 午前零時１０分ごろ、 ここでキャンプをしていた女性が、
English: Now, there happened to be a woman camping here on the edge of the lake.

Japanese: ２発の銃声を聞いているッス。
English: At 12:10 AM, she heard two pistol shots.

Japanese: ボートは、貸しボート小屋の方へ 逃げて行ったッス。
English: Then the boat started to move. It went towards the boat rental shop.

Japanese: ‥‥ウム。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: 証拠品《上面図》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Overhead Map added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、逮捕したときの 状況を《証言》したまえ。
English: Testify to the court about your arrest. Now!

Japanese: あ、ちょ、ちょっと！ 狩魔検事。
English: W-w-wait! Mr. von Karma...

Japanese: 何かね。
English: Yes?

Japanese: いちおう、この法廷は 私が進行役ですので‥‥。
English: Actually, I'm the one that's supposed to be handling these proceedings...

Japanese: キサマのすべきことは、１つ。
English: Wrong. There is only one thing you need to do here.

Japanese: その木づちをたたいて “有罪”と言えばよい。
English: You will slam down your gavel and say the word guilty. That is your role!

Japanese: は、はあ。 ‥‥了解しました。
English: Y-yes, of course. You're quite right.

Japanese: （了解するなよ！）
English: (No he's not!!!)

Japanese: ～御剣を逮捕するまでのこと～
English: -- The Arrest of Edgeworth --

Japanese: あれは零時３０分すぎだったッス。 署に、男から通報が入ったッス。
English: A man called into the station around 30 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ワレワレはパトカーで 現場に急行したッス。
English: We headed to the scene of the crime as fast as we could.

Japanese: そこでワレワレは、 御剣検事と会ったッス。
English: That's where we found Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: まさか、御剣検事が アヤシイとは思わなかったッス。
English: Now, I didn't suspect him of anything at all.

Japanese: しかし‥‥翌朝、湖から 男の死体が見つかって‥‥、
English: But... the next morning, a body was found in the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、御剣検事を 逮捕することになったッス‥‥。
English: So we had to arrest Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。では、
English: Hmm... I see. Very well...

Japanese: 《尋問》をしてくれたまえ。 ‥‥弁護士。
English: Begin your cross-examination, attorney. Now!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... ～御剣を逮捕するまでのこと～
English: -- The Arrest of Edgeworth --

Japanese: あれは零時３０分すぎだったッス。 署に、男から通報が入ったッス。
English: A man called into the station around 30 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ワレワレはパトカーで 現場に急行したッス。
English: We headed to the scene of the crime as fast as we could.

Japanese: そこでワレワレは、 御剣検事と会ったッス。
English: That's where we found Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: まさか、御剣検事が アヤシイとは思わなかったッス。
English: Now, I didn't suspect him of anything at all.

Japanese: しかし‥‥翌朝、湖から 男の死体が見つかって‥‥、
English: But... the next morning, a body was found in the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、御剣検事を 逮捕することになったッス‥‥。
English: So we had to arrest Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ボートから見つかった凶器が 決定的な証拠になったッス。
English: The murder weapon we found in the boat was decisive evidence.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick.

Japanese: なんか、イトノコさん、 少しアイマイだね‥‥。
English: Doesn't it seem like Detective Gumshoe is being a little vague?

Japanese: たぶん、あの検事が 指示しているんだよ。
English: I bet von Karma is feeding him lines!

Japanese: ヘタにゆさぶると、 地雷がバクハツするんだろ。
English: His testimony's probably filled with landmines just waiting for me to press.

Japanese: どうしよう‥‥？
English: What do we do?

Japanese: バクハツをカクゴで ゆさぶってみるしかない、かな。
English: Grit our teeth and press, I guess. What else can we do?

Japanese: オトコだねえ、なるほどくん！
English: Well said, Nick! You're the man!

Japanese: “男”から通報が 入ったんですか？
English: You received a call from a "man"?

Japanese: そ‥‥そッス。
English: Er... yup.

Japanese: しかしさっき、あなたは こう言った。
English: But you said there was a woman camping there?

Japanese: “キャンプをしていた女性が” ２発の銃声を聞いた、と。
English: She was the one who heard the two gunshots, right?

Japanese: その女性と、通報した“男”は 別人だ。
English: That woman and the "man" who called in the report are two different people, obviously!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: Different people?

Japanese: 目撃者は、２人いたのだ。
English: There were two witnesses! (Urk!)

Japanese: ２人の話は、ほぼ 同じ内容だったのでね。
English: Their testimonies were quite similar however.

Japanese: 今日は、キャンプをしていた 女性の方に来てもらったのだ。
English: Today I've summoned the woman who was camping.

Japanese: （キャンプをしていた女性って、 ナツミさんのことだよな‥‥）
English: (The woman who was camping... Lotta Hart.)

Japanese: 刑事。‥‥それからどうなった？
English: What happened next, Detective?

Japanese: 通報を受けてから湖につくまで、 どれぐらいかかりましたか？
English: How long was it between receiving the report and your arrival at the lake?

Japanese: そうッスね‥‥たぶん、 ３分ぐらいだったッス。
English: Er, well... I'd say it was about three minutes.

Japanese: また、ずいぶん早いですね。
English: That's pretty fast!

Japanese: ウチの署の今月の標語は、 “急がば回らずまっすぐに”ッス。
English: Our motto for the month is "get there quick."

Japanese: 刑事！　署の機密事項を カンタンにもらすな！
English: Detective! You will refrain from casually revealing department secrets!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！ モウシワケないッス！
English: Y-yessir! Sorry, sir!

Japanese: 来年の給与査定、楽しみに しておくことだ。
English: Do that, and you'll be able to look forward to your next salary review.

Japanese: ‥‥また楽しみが増えたッス‥‥。
English: So much to look forward to, these days...

Japanese: 何をしている！　つづけたまえ！
English: This is no time for daydreaming! Continue!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-yessir!

Japanese: そのとき、御剣検事は どんなようすでしたか？
English: What was Mr. Edgeworth like when you saw him then?

Japanese: そッスね‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: 自分の見たところ、落ちついていて とても殺人など‥‥
English: From what I saw, he looked pretty relaxed. Not like a murderer at all, really.

Japanese: 刑事！　主観的な意見を 言うのはやめたまえ！
English: Detective! The court requires the facts, NOT your opinion!

Japanese: 何年、刑事をやっているッ！
English: How many years have you been on the force!?

Japanese: 意味があるのは、客観的な データだけだ！
English: Facts only, Detective! Hard, cold, objective facts!
Y-y-yessir!

Japanese: （‥‥くそ。いちいち 異議の多い検事だな‥‥）
English: (Man, he's got his share of objections...)

Japanese: なぜ、アヤシイと 思わなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you think he was suspicious?

Japanese: それは！
English: You should know!

Japanese: ワレワレと検事には、 カタい信頼関係が‥‥
English: We have a deep, trusting relationship with the prosecutors.

Japanese: 刑事！　いいかげんな 証言はするな！
English: Detective! The court isn't interested in your musings!

Japanese: “信頼関係”だと！ ‥‥アマッたれるな！
English: Deep? "Trusting"? Poppycock!

Japanese: そんなタワ言を並べる前に、 誤認逮捕をへらすことだ！
English: I've never heard so many flippant comments from an active detective on the force!

Japanese: ‥‥ムギュウ‥‥
English: Mmph!

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事、究極まで ヘコんだな‥‥）
English: (Detective Gumshoe doesn't look so good...)

Japanese: さっさと、つづけたまえ！
English: Continue! Now!

Japanese: 死体には、何か 手がかりがありましたか？
English: Did you find any clues on the body?

Japanese: 死体から、弾丸が１発 見つかったッス。
English: A single bullet was recovered from the body.

Japanese: 心臓を撃ち抜いていたッス。 ‥‥それが致命傷ッス。
English: He was shot through the heart... fatally.

Japanese: 裁判長。 その“弾丸”を提出しよう。
English: Judge! Here's the bullet...

Japanese: うまくホネをはずれていたので、 しっかり原型をとどめている。
English: It didn't strike bone, so its shape is well preserved.

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥受理します。
English: Very well. The court accepts this bullet into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストルの弾丸》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol Bullet added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: W-why is that?

Japanese: ボートから、凶器が 発見されたッス。
English: Well, we found the murder weapon in the boat.

Japanese: 凶器‥‥？
English: The murder weapon...?

Japanese: ピストル‥‥ッス。
English: A pistol.

Japanese: 証人。
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: それは重要なことです。 証言しなおしてください。
English: That is a vital piece of information. Please revise your testimony.

Japanese: わ‥‥わかったッス。
English: Right! S-sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: なぜ、それが“決定的” なんですか？
English: What about the pistol made it "decisive evidence"?

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: （‥‥うわあ‥‥御剣ソックリな、 イヤな笑い‥‥）
English: (Ack! He has the same evil laugh as Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ボートから見つかったピストルには 指紋があったッス‥‥。
English: There were fingerprints on the pistol found in the boat.

Japanese: 御剣検事の右手の指紋が、 ハッキリと‥‥。
English: They were clear prints from Mr. Edgeworth's right hand.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 凶器に、御剣検事の指紋が ついていたんですか！
English: So Mr. Edgeworth's finger- prints were found on the murder weapon!?

Japanese: は‥‥はッ。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥これが問題の凶器だ。
English: Judge! This is the weapon in question.

Japanese: じゅ‥‥受理します。
English: A-accepted into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストル》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol added to the Court Record.

Japanese: よろしいか。
English: Members of the court...

Japanese: ‥‥これで、凶器の《ピストル》と 《弾丸》がそろったわけだ。
English: We now have the pistol used in the murder, and the bullet found in the body!

Japanese: 刑事。
English: Detective!

Japanese: はは、は、はッ！
English: Y-y-yessir!

Japanese: 死体から発見された弾丸は、 そのピストルから撃たれたものか？
English: Was the bullet found in the body fired from this pistol?

Japanese: はッ。弾丸の線条痕が、その ピストルと一致したッス。
English: Yes. The ballistic markings on the bullet match the pistol.
Hmm...

Japanese: ね、ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: なあに？ “せんじょうこん”って。
English: What does he mean, ballistic markings?

Japanese: おどろいたな‥‥
English: Shocking!

Japanese: この場に、線条痕も知らない ドシロウトがいようとは‥‥。
English: To imagine someone here does not know something as basic as ballistic markings!

Japanese: うわぁ‥‥。こっち、ニラんでるよ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: N-Nick! He's glaring at me!

Japanese: ふぅ‥‥いたしかたない。 説明してやろう。
English: Tsk... very well, I'll explain.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。やりたまえ。
English: Actually, Judge! You do it.

Japanese: えッ！　わ、私が‥‥？
English: Eh? M-me?
Erm, ahem.

Japanese: 線条痕というのは、ピストルの “指紋”みたいなものです。
English: Ballistic markings are like the "fingerprints" of a gun.

Japanese: ピストルで撃たれた弾丸には 必ず、銃身によるキズがつきます。
English: The barrel leaves distinctive marks on each bullet it fires.

Japanese: そのキズを調べれば、弾丸が どのピストルから撃たれたかが、
English: You can examine these ballistic fingerprints to see which gun fired the shot.

Japanese: ハッキリ特定できるのです。
English: It's quite accurate.

Japanese: そう。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: そして、この被害者の心臓から 見つかった弾丸は、
English: This leads to one, inevitable conclusion: the bullet found in the victim's heart...

Japanese: 間違いなく、このピストルから 発射されているのだ。
English: was, without a doubt, fired from this pistol.

Japanese: この、被告人の指紋がベッタリ ついているピストルから、な。
English: This pistol which, as you may recall, was covered with the defendant's own fingerprints!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: （まずいな‥‥）
English: (This is bad...)

Japanese: （これじゃあ、御剣が撃ったとしか 考えられないぞ‥‥！）
English: (This makes it look like Edgeworth did it!)

Japanese: いかがか？　裁判長。
English: Well, Judge?

Japanese: ほぼ、決定的といって いいでしょう。
English: I'd say it's almost decisive, yes.

Japanese: 正直なところ、この時点で、 判決を下すことも可能です。
English: Honestly, I could declare a verdict at this point.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: 目撃証人の証言も気になる、と。 ‥‥了解した。
English: You wish to hear the witness speak, no doubt.

Japanese: では。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ワガハイ、少しつかれたので、 ここで休憩をとろう。
English: I am somewhat fatigued, and so I will take a brief break.

Japanese: 証人を呼ぶのは、それからで よかろう。
English: I will call my witness after the recess.

Japanese: 休憩は１０分でいいかな。 ‥‥裁判長！
English: Which will last ten minutes. Judge!

Japanese: は、はい？
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: 何をしている。 １０分間、休憩だ。
English: What are you doing? A ten minute recess! Now!

Japanese: い、いや、それは‥‥。
English: B-but, wait, I...

Japanese: 早く、そのウスぎたない 木づちを鳴らさんかッ！
English: Just bang your flimsy gavel and get on with it, man!

Japanese: はいィッ！
English: Y-yes!

Japanese: では、ここで１０分間の休憩を とります。
English: Ahem. This court will take a ten minute recess.

Japanese: （‥‥イイのか？　コレで！）
English: (Who's running this court, anyway!?)

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前１１時９分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 26, 11:09 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: 御剣！　どういうことだよ！
English: Edgeworth! What's going on here!?

Japanese: 凶器のピストルには、 お前の指紋がついてたんだぞ！
English: Your fingerprints were on the murder weapon!

Japanese: う‥‥ううむ‥‥。
English: Uh... hmm...

Japanese: しかも、この写真を見れば わかるとおり、
English: And that foggy photo makes one thing clear...

Japanese: 被害者を撃つことができたのは、 この写真の男しかいない！
English: The only one who could have shot that man was the person in the photo!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥。
English: True...

Japanese: このボートに乗っているのは、 お前なのか？
English: Was that you in the boat?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... そうだ。‥‥それは、私だ。
English: Yes. It was me.

Japanese: なんてこった‥‥。
English: What!?

Japanese: しかし‥‥信じてくれ。 私は撃っていない。
English: But... you must believe me. I didn't shoot him.

Japanese: じゃあ、いったい誰が！
English: Th-then who did!?

Japanese: それが‥‥よくわからない。
English: I... don't know.

Japanese: ‥‥わからない？ 目の前で見ていたんだろう！
English: You don't know!? Weren't you right there!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... いきなり、至近距離で 銃声が聞こえたんだ。
English: I heard a gunshot from very close by.

Japanese: そして、あの男はボートから 落ちた。
English: Then... the other man fell from the boat.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜかは知らんが‥‥、
English: I can't say why, but...

Japanese: あの男は、自分で自分を 撃ったのではないだろうか‥‥。
English: I thought, at the time, that he had shot himself!

Japanese: じ‥‥自殺だ、っていうのか？
English: Y-you mean it was a suicide!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... それしか考えられない。
English: That's the only explanation I can come up with.

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。 （そんなことが、あるのか？）
English: Huh... (How am I going to convince anyone of that!?)

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、真宵ちゃん？
English: Say, Maya?

Japanese: え‥‥な、なに？
English: Huh? Wh-what?

Japanese: 千尋さんは？
English: Any progress with Mia?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ごめんなさい。 ‥‥ダメ、みたい‥‥。
English: Sorry. It's no good.

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。
English: Ugh...

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: I know...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、あたしじゃ なんにもできないのかな？
English: I'm no good for anything, am I, Nick?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんのいない あたしなんて、意味ないのかな？
English: If I can't call my sister, I might as well not be here, right?

Japanese: ええと、それはその‥‥。
English: Um, well, actually...

Japanese: （法律の知識もない、法廷の テクニックも知らない‥‥）
English: (You don't know law, you don't have any trial experience or techniques...)

Japanese: （助けにはならないケド‥‥ そんなこと、言えないよな）
English: (Yeah, you're pretty much useless... but I can't say that!)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 カオに出てるよ。
English: It's okay Nick. You don't have to say anything. Your face said it for you.

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった‥‥！） い‥‥いや、その‥‥ゴメン。
English: (Whoops!) N-no, o-of course we... I'm sorry.

Japanese: いいんだよ、なるほどくん。
English: It's okay, Nick.

Japanese: そ、そんなことはないよ。
English: No, of course not, I need you here!

Japanese: 一生けんめい助けようと してくれているのはわかるから、
English: I can see you're always trying to help out.

Japanese: そのキモチだけでも‥‥
English: Even if you don't actually help... it's the thought that counts, right?

Japanese: ムリしなくていいよ、 なるほどくん。
English: It's okay Nick, you don't have to make me feel better.

Japanese: あたし、弁護のことなんて なんにも知らないもんね。
English: I don't know anything about trials, or defense...

Japanese: 霊媒師なのに、霊も呼び出せ ないんだもん‥‥。
English: What's more, I'm a spirit medium who can't even contact spirits...

Japanese: ‥‥誰だって、調子がワルい ときはあるよ。
English: Aww, everyone has their off days!

Japanese: ぼくだって、今はたまたま うまいコトやってるけど、
English: I mean, I've just been getting lucky lately...

Japanese: こんなの、いつまでつづくか わからないんだからさ！
English: But you never know when my luck is going to run out!

Japanese: ‥‥ホント？
English: Really...?

Japanese: ななな、成歩堂！
English: W-w-whoa! Wright!

Japanese: フキツなコトを言うな！ ‥‥心臓にワルい。
English: Don't jinx this case any worse than it already is! It's bad for my heart...

Japanese: ははは。 （マイったな‥‥）
English: Oh? Oh! S-sorry... (Whoops!)

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court is back in session.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 ‥‥証人をおねがいします。
English: Mr. von Karma, call your witness.

Japanese: ウム。
English: Yes.

Japanese: では、大沢木　ナツミを 入廷させてほしい。
English: Will Ms. Lotta Hart take the stand?

Japanese: 大沢木　ナツミ。 大学の研究生をしている？
English: Lotta Hart, you are a research student at a university?

Japanese: はい。
English: That I am.

Japanese: さっそくだが、事件の夜、 目撃したことを話してもらおう。
English: Good. Begin by telling us what you saw the night of the incident.

Japanese: よけいなことは、いっさい ハブいてもらいたい。
English: And don't add anything trivial or subjective.

Japanese: いいな。
English: Understand?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... なんやアンタ、そのタイドは‥‥
English: Y'all need to learn some manners.

Japanese: ‥‥いいな。
English: UNDERSTAND?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええわ。わかったワ。
English: Y-yeah, I understand, I understand!

Japanese: えー、では。 ‥‥《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Er... very well. Your testimony, please.

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、クリスマス・イブの 夜の、零時をすぎたころやったわ。
English: It was Christmas Eve, just after midnight, I reckon.

Japanese: ウチ、クルマの中におったんや。
English: I was in my car.

Japanese: 湖の方で、“パアン”て 音がしてなあ。
English: I heard this "bang" come up from the lake.

Japanese: 窓から見ると、ボートの上に 男が２人、おったんや。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw two gents in a boat.

Japanese: そのとき、 もう１回、音がしたんや。
English: Then there was another bang...

Japanese: 湖の上には、間違いなく そのボートしか、なかったワ。
English: There wasn't nary a thing on the lake but that boat.

Japanese: それで、けっこう。
English: Enough.

Japanese: ‥‥うう。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Judge!

Japanese: 彼女はたまたま、このときの ようすを撮影していた。
English: She happened to take a photo of the incident!

Japanese: そのときの写真だ。 ‥‥受理していただきたい。
English: This is that photo. Accept it as evidence.

Japanese: こ‥‥これは！
English: W-well! This is a surprise!

Japanese: まさに、発砲のシュンカンでは ありませんか！
English: This looks like... the very moment of the murder!

Japanese: し‥‥静かにしなさい！ 退廷を命じますぞ！
English: O-Order! I will remove you from the court if I do not have order immediately!

Japanese: 証言にあったとおり、 発砲があったとき、
English: As the witness testified, she looked at the lake when she heard the shot.

Japanese: 湖の上には、このボートしか 存在しなかった。
English: There were no other boats on that lake!

Japanese: つまり、撃ったのは、 いっしょに乗っていた男‥‥
English: So, the man in the boat with the victim must have been the one who shot him...

Japanese: すなわち、御剣　怜侍しか いない！
English: Yes. It was the defendant, Miles Edgeworth!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥静まりなさい！
English: Order! Order! Order...! I will have order!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... いかがか？　裁判長。
English: Well, Judge?

Japanese: これで、決定的ですね。
English: The evidence is... decisive.

Japanese: ‥‥私の迷いも、完全に なくなりました。
English: I have very little doubt about this case.

Japanese: では、この場で御剣　怜侍に 対する判決を‥‥
English: Very well, this court finds the defendant...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait! Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼくの《尋問》がまだです！
English: I haven't cross-examined the witness yet!
A cross-examination?

Japanese: 尋問も何も、証拠写真がある。
English: We have photographic proof! What question can there possibly be!?

Japanese: この写真は、 すべてを物語っている！
English: This photo is worth a thousand words... and they all read guilty!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダだ。
English: You lose.

Japanese: それともキサマは‥‥
English: Or...

Japanese: 今の証言に、何かムジュンした 部分を見つけたのかね？
English: Do you claim to have found a contradiction in her testimony!?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! それならば、《尋問》を やってもらおうじゃないか。
English: Very well. If you have to, you may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: しかし、もし当てずっぽうに 探りを入れたあげく、
English: You will only flounder and ask meaningless questions!

Japanese: 何も出なかったら！
English: You will fail to find anything!

Japanese: そのときは、法廷侮辱罪を 適用してもらうぞ。
English: And then, I will have you held in contempt of court!

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん？ “ほうてい”‥‥何？
English: Uh, Nick... contempt...?

Japanese: “ブジョク罪”。 ‥‥意味はわかるだろ？
English: Contempt of court, you know?

Japanese: うん。‥‥なんとなく。
English: I... guess I understand.

Japanese: それで、どう？
English: Well... what are you going to do?

Japanese: さっきの証言、ムジュンした 部分はあった？
English: Do you really think there was a contradiction with the facts in her testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥い、１カ所“これかな” という部分が‥‥。
English: I... think I noticed one little thing...

Japanese: すごいね、なるほどくん！ ‥‥あたし、気づかなかったよ。
English: Wow, I'm impressed, Nick! I didn't notice anything!

Japanese: じゃあ、アイツの挑戦、 受けてやろうよ！
English: Right! Let's take him on!

Japanese: う‥‥うん。 （自信、ないんだけどなあ‥‥）
English: Y-yeah... (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、 見つからなかったんだよな‥‥。
English: Honestly, I found nothing.

Japanese: ナツミさんの証言、 ちょっとおかしかったよね。
English: I dunno, I thought her testimony was strange.

Japanese: ハッキリしたこと、なんにも 言わないんだもん。
English: She was so vague about everything!

Japanese: 狩魔検事が、 言わせなかったんじゃないかな。
English: I bet von Karma probably fed her her lines.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: ここで尋問しなかったら、 あの裁判長‥‥。
English: But if you don't cross- examine her, the judge will...

Japanese: 間違いなく木づちをたたいて “有罪”だな‥‥。
English: He'll bang down that "flimsy gavel" of his and declare Edgeworth guilty.

Japanese: ここは、ハッタリで 押しとおすしかないね。
English: I guess we'll just have to bluff it.

Japanese: でも、シッパイしたら‥‥ 法廷侮辱罪だぞ。
English: But if we mess up... contempt of court.

Japanese: 負けちゃったら‥‥ いっしょだよ！
English: Hey, if we lose we'll be held in public contempt!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだな。
English: Er... right.

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥尋問をします！
English: I understand... I will cross-examine the witness!

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥ ‥‥よかろう。
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Very well!

Japanese: 時間のムダにならないことを 祈ってますよ。
English: I pray for your sake this isn't a waste of time.

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、クリスマス・イブの 夜の、零時をすぎたころやったわ。
English: It was Christmas Eve, just after midnight, I reckon.

Japanese: ウチ、クルマの中におったんや。
English: I was in my car.

Japanese: 湖の方で、“パアン”て 音がしてなあ。
English: I heard this "bang" come up from the lake.

Japanese: 窓から見ると、ボートの上に 男が２人、おったんや。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw two gents in a boat.

Japanese: そのとき、もう１回、音が したんや。
English: Then there was another bang...

Japanese: 湖の上には、間違いなく そのボートしか、なかったワ。
English: There wasn't nary a thing on the lake but that boat.

Japanese: （む‥‥ムジュンなんて、 見つからないぞ‥‥）
English: (Th-there weren't any contradictions in there!)

Japanese: ごめんね、なるほどくん。 お姉ちゃんがいれば‥‥。
English: Sorry, Nick. If only my sister were here...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、気にしてる みたいだな‥‥）
English: (Maya's really taking this hard.)

Japanese: “零時をすぎたころ”‥‥ ということは、
English: Just after midnight, you say?

Japanese: 正確にはクリスマスイブではなく、 もうクリスマスになっていた？
English: In other words, it was no longer Christmas Eve... but Christmas Day!

Japanese: え？　ま、まあ、そうやなあ。
English: Huh? Uh, yeah, well, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、ムジュンを 指摘するという約束では‥‥？
English: I know you want to find contradictions, but really!

Japanese: うむむ‥‥。
English: Mmmph.

Japanese: そのとおりですね。
English: I hope your next contradiction is a little more relevant to the trial.

Japanese: 証人は、証言を つづけてください。
English: Witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: あの場所でキャンプをしていた のは、なぜですか？
English: Why were you camping there, anyway?

Japanese: ウチ、大学の研究員やねん。
English: I'm a research student at my university.

Japanese: 研究に使う写真を 撮ろうと思ってなァ。
English: I was taking pictures to use in my research.

Japanese: （‥‥さっきから、どうも ハッキリしないんだよな‥‥）
English: (What research? This all sounds suspicious...)

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: 研究というのは、 具体的にはどういう‥‥？
English: Could you be more specific about your "research"?

Japanese: “証人がなぜ、あの場で キャンプをしていたのか？”
English: What does the witness's motive in camping by the lake have to do with this case!?

Japanese: そんなことは、事件には なんのカンケイもない！
English: The answer is "nothing"! I object to this line of questioning!

Japanese: 質問は却下する。
English: Objection sustained!

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。 ‥‥決めるのは私です！
English: W-w-wait, now, I'm the one who says that!

Japanese: じゃあ、サッサと却下 したまえ！
English: Well, then say it already!

Japanese: 質問を却下します。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: （ダメだ、コイツは‥‥）
English: (Thanks for nothing, Your Honor...)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、今のところは 見逃しておくか‥‥）
English: (I think I'll let that one slide for now.)

Japanese: そのときは、湖は 見ていなかったんですか？
English: So, you weren't looking at the lake at that time?

Japanese: そうやなあ。
English: Nope.

Japanese: ウチが湖を見たのは、 その音を聞いてから‥‥
English: I looked after I heard that noise.

Japanese: 何度も言わせるな。
English: She's said that already!

Japanese: ワガハイは“ムジュンを指摘しろ” と言ったはずだ。
English: I asked you to find contradictions!

Japanese: ちまちまと、情報収集など してもらっては困る！
English: Not leisurely chat with the witness!

Japanese: （くそぉ‥‥）
ハッキリ見たんですか？ ２人の男を‥‥。
English: Could you clearly see the two men?

Japanese: 写真、見てみィ。 ちゃんと写っとるやん？
English: Just look at the picture! Clear enough for you?

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう‥‥？）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: ぼくは、写真のことを 聞いたんじゃない！
English: I wasn't asking you about the photo!

Japanese: “あなたが”見たのかどうかを 聞いているんです！
English: I was asking if YOU saw the two men!

Japanese: そ‥‥そりゃあ、モチロン‥‥‥
English: Uh, yeah, well, of course...

Japanese: 証人は見たと証言している！ それに、写真もある！
English: The witness has testified that she saw them! There's also a photo!

Japanese: いいかげん、指摘したまえ！ ‥‥おトクイの“ムジュン”を！
English: You'd best look elsewhere for your precious contradictions!

Japanese: （何か、かくしてることが ありそうなんだけど‥‥）
English: (He jumped in quick... He's hiding something!)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、今のところは 見逃しておくか‥‥）
English: (I think I'd best let that one slide...)

Japanese: そのときは、見たんですか？ 撃ったシュンカンを‥‥。
English: Were you watching the very moment the shot rang out?

Japanese: ええ、まあ、そりゃあ‥‥。
English: Well, yeah, sure...

Japanese: 弁護人は、どうでもいいこと ばかり聞いておる！
English: You're asking meaningless questions! Meaningless!

Japanese: “ムジュン”を指摘したまえ！ ‥‥さあ！
English: Contradictions, Mr. Wright. Not meaningless babble!

Japanese: （狩魔のやつ‥‥）
English: (von Karma, I think I hate you.)

Japanese: （ぼくに、証人と話をさせない つもりらしいな‥‥）
English: (He's trying to keep me from talking to the witness...)

Japanese: （いったい、なぜ‥‥？）
English: (To what end...?)

Japanese: それは、たしかですか？
English: Are you sure about that?

Japanese: ああ、間違いないで！
English: Yeah, sure as a country gal can be!

Japanese: （どうやら、ずいぶん、 自信があるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (That sounds pretty sure.)

Japanese: どうしてそんなに 自信があるんですか？
English: How come you're so sure?

Japanese: そりゃあアンタ、湖を スミズミまで見たからなァ。
English: Well, heck, I scanned the whole lake.

Japanese: （“湖をスミズミまで見た”？）
English: ("Scanned the whole lake"?)

Japanese: （まるで、ボートよりも、湖の方に 興味があったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It almost sounds like she was more interested in the lake than the boat...)

Japanese: ナツミさん。‥‥あなたは、
English: Ms. Hart... you--

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人はすでに答えておる。 ‥‥じゅうぶんな答えを、な。
English: The witness has answered the question in full. Mmph!

Japanese: これ以上の質問に、 意味はない。却下！
English: No need for further questions! Objection sustained!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥それは 私が‥‥。
English: U-uh, that's what I'm...

Japanese: 却下でいいな！
English: Sustained!

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Y-yes, of course.

Japanese: （‥‥まいったな‥‥）
English: (Oh great...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうすればいい！）
English: (What am I supposed to do now?)

Japanese: （ナツミさんも 自信マンマンだ‥‥）
English: (Lotta seems pretty sure of herself.)

Japanese: （ヘタにつっこまない方が いいかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better not forge blindly ahead here.)

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥）
English: (Still... this whole thing stinks.)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: もうけっこうです、 成歩堂くん。
English: I think we've heard all we need to hear, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: どうやらあなたは、“ムジュン”を 指摘することはできないようだ。
English: It seems you are unable to find a contradiction in the testimony worth noting.

Japanese: いや、しかし‥‥！
English: B-but, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥約束は守ってもらう。
English: You keep your promise!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: これ以上の発言には、 法廷侮辱罪を適用します。
English: I am afraid that I will have to penalize any further outbursts... By holding you in contempt of court!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなったら、即座に 退廷しなければならない。
English: And if that happens, you'll have to leave the courtroom immediately!

Japanese: いいのかな‥‥？
English: Understood...?
Uh... uh huh.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...!

Japanese: やっぱり、ナツミさんの証言は 少しおかしいよ‥‥。
English: Lotta's testimony is fishy, Nick! Real fishy!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに なんかスッキリしないよなあ。
English: I... know what you mean.

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: 発言を封じられたら、 もう、どうすることも‥‥。
English: If I can't say anything, what can I do?

Japanese: この時点で、審理は じゅうぶんだと考えます。
English: I believe we've covered the evidence sufficiently to make a decision.

Japanese: では、被告人に判決を 言い渡していただこう。
English: Then, pass your judgment!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、御剣　怜侍。 証言台についてください。
English: Mr. Miles Edgeworth, please take the stand.

Japanese: い‥‥いまのは、 ‥‥どなたが‥‥？
English: Wh-who was that!?

Japanese: あ、あた‥‥あたしですっ！
English: It... was me.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん！）
English: (Maya!)

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしましたか？ トイレなら‥‥
English: I-is something wrong? D-do you need to use the facilities?

Japanese: そうじゃありません！
English: No, I do not!

Japanese: ナツミさん！ あなたの証言はオカシイです！
English: Lotta Hart! Your testimony stinks!

Japanese: 湖を見ていたのかどうかは アイマイだし、
English: It's unclear whether you were actually looking at the lake!

Japanese: 御剣検事さんを見たかどうかも ハッキリしないし‥‥。
English: It's highly doubtful that you actually saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ホントのコト、話してください！
English: Tell us the truth!

Japanese: 人のいのちが、 ‥‥かかってるんですよ！
English: This is a matter of life or death!
Lotta!

Japanese: あなたは、ホントに御剣検事を ハッキリ見たんですか！
English: Did you really clearly see Mr. Edgeworth that night!?

Japanese: 御剣検事が、ピストルを 撃つところを‥‥！
English: Did you see him fire that pistol!?

Japanese: やめなさい！
English: You will stand down!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の発言は 認めません！
English: The court does not acknowledge the defense's outburst!

Japanese: 答えて！　ナツミさん！
English: Answer me, Lotta!

Japanese: なんや！　ヒトを ワルモノあつかいしよって！
English: What's the big idea, treating me like some kind of criminal!?

Japanese: 見たでウチは！ ‥‥御剣を、ハッキリと‥‥
English: I saw him! I swear it! I saw Edgeworth...

Japanese: そこまでだ！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Judge...

Japanese: ‥‥法廷侮辱罪で、 弁護士を退廷させたまえ。
English: Declare the defense in contempt of court!

Japanese: ‥‥わ‥‥わかりました。
English: Y-yes... yes, of course.

Japanese: 約束ですから、 しかたありませんね。
English: I'm sorry, but you WERE warned.

Japanese: 係官！　成歩堂　龍一の 身柄を拘束しなさい！
English: Guard! Escort Mr. Wright out of the courtroom!

Japanese: 法廷侮辱罪により、 退廷していただきます‥‥
English: He is in contempt of court, and must leave.

Japanese: （ここまでか‥‥！）
待ってください！
English: Wait!

Japanese: は、発言をしたのは、 あた、あたしです！
English: I-I was the one who made the outburst, Your Honor!

Japanese: なるほどくんは、 カンケイありません！
English: Nick is innocent!

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Hah!

Japanese: たいした違いはあるまい。
English: What's the difference?

Japanese: どっちにしろ、残っているのは もう、有罪宣告のみだ。
