English: All that remains is for the guilty verdict to be declared!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな？ 弁護士・成歩堂　龍一！
English: Isn't that right, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: それは違います！
English: Wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと？
English: What!?

Japanese: （くやしいけど‥‥ そのとおりだな‥‥）
English: (Dammit, he's right...)

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ どうにかならないの！
English: N-Nick! There must be something we can do!

Japanese: ‥‥ム‥‥ムリだよ。
English: I... don't think there is.

Japanese: だって、もう尋問は 終わっちゃったんだから‥‥。
English: The cross-examination is over already!

Japanese: で‥‥でも！　でも！ 今、ナツミさん、言ったよ！
English: B-but... but didn't Lotta just say she saw him?

Japanese: “御剣検事を見た”って。
English: She did, Nick! She just said that she saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: さっきは、そんなこと‥‥ 言ってなかったよ！
English: She didn't say that in her testimony, did she?
Y-you're right!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: たった今、大沢木　ナツミさんは こう言いました！
English: Did you hear what Ms. Hart just said?

Japanese: “御剣検事を、ハッキリ見た”と！
English: She said she clearly saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: これは、 今までにない《証言》です！
English: That was not in the testimony!

Japanese: 弁護側は、この証言に対して、 《尋問》をする権利があります！
English: That changes her testimony, and I have a right to cross-examine her again!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order! Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: そんな勝手な言いぶんが とおるか！
English: You're in contempt of court! It's too late for wild claims!

Japanese: 裁判長！ ‥‥そんな意見は、却下だ！
English: Judge! Sustain my objection!
I'm sorry, Mr. von Karma, but I cannot.

Japanese: なにっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 大沢木　ナツミさんは、 新しい《証言》をしました。
English: Ms. Lotta Hart has made a new testimony.

Japanese: 当然、弁護人は《尋問》を する権利があります！
English: The defense does have a right to cross-examine her again.

Japanese: しかし！　弁護人は 法廷侮辱罪で‥‥！
English: B-but he is in contempt of court!

Japanese: ブジョクしたのは、あたしです！
English: No, I am!

Japanese: 逮捕するなら、あたしだけに してください！
English: If you're going to arrest someone, arrest me!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵！
English: Maya Fey!

Japanese: あなたには、 退廷してもらいましょう。
English: You will leave the courtroom immediately.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、こんなことしか できないから。
English: I did what I could...

Japanese: あとは、たのんだよ。 ‥‥おねがい！
English: You have to do the rest! Good luck!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥！
English: M-Maya...!

Japanese: フン！　くだらん‥‥。
English: Peh! I care not for this melodrama!

Japanese: わかっているだろうな、 弁護士！
English: Listen well, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ワガハイの証人に、ゆさぶりなど 許さんぞ！
English: I do not tolerate badgering of my witnesses!

Japanese: （なんとしても‥‥）
English: (I'm running out of time...)

Japanese: （ムジュンをつきつけなくては ならないワケか‥‥）
English: (I'd better find a contradiction in here or else...)

Japanese: では！　《尋問》を していただきましょう。
English: Mr. Wright! Begin your cross-examination!

Japanese: ～最後の発言について～
English: -- That Last Statement --

Japanese: ウチ、ハッキリ見たんや。ボートの 男は、たしかに御剣やった。
English: I saw it clear as day. The man on the boat was Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥証言は、たったこれだけ）
English: (That's it...?)

Japanese: （ここにムジュンを 見つけられなかったら‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. I don't know if I can find anything in that...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに合わせる カオがないな‥‥）
English: (What will Maya say...?)

Japanese: では、もう１人の男は‥‥
English: Well, what about the other man?

Japanese: さきほどからの約束を、 何度ムシするつもりか！
English: You cannot expect to be allowed to blithely ignore your promise, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人の証言に“ムジュンがある”と キサマは言ったはずだ。
English: I believe you claimed there was a contradiction in the witness's testimony!

Japanese: さっさと、それを示したまえ！ できるものならな！
English: Well, find it! If you can!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥ペナルティを 受けてもらいましょう。
English: Mr. Wright, I have to assign you a penalty.
(Damn...!)

Japanese: ‥‥やっと。
English: Got you.

Japanese: やっとつかまえましたよ。 ‥‥ナツミさん。
English: Got you, Ms. Hart! Finally!

Japanese: な‥‥なんや。
English: Wh-what!? You got what?

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this photograph.

Japanese: ウチが撮った写真‥‥？
English: The photo I took...?

Japanese: いいですか。写真は ハッキリ写しています。
English: The very same. There's something I want you to see in this photo... it's quite clearly visible.

Japanese: 湖に立ちこめる、キリをね。
English: The fog, Ms. Hart.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことや‥‥？
English: So... so?

Japanese: あなたのごジマンの 高価なフィルムでも、
English: This picture was taken with professional, high quality film, correct?

Japanese: ボートに乗っている男のカオは、 まったく写っていません。
English: Yet even it could not capture the faces of the men on the boat!

Japanese: なのに、なぜ！
English: Yet you claim you saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: あなたには、 御剣が見えたのですかッ！
English: How!?

Japanese: あッ！
English: Whut!? Wha--!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そのとおりです！
English: Mr. Wright has a point!

Japanese: だからワガハイは、そのことは 証言させなかったのだ！
English: That's why I told her not to say that in her testimony! Please!

Japanese: しかし、証人は今、こうして 証言をしてしまった！
English: Yet, now she has said it, Mr. von Karma.

Japanese: なぜ御剣を見ることができたか！ 説明をしていただきたい！
English: How could you possibly see Mr. Edgeworth!? Explain yourself!

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: な‥‥なんや！
English: What!

Japanese: あなたには、被告人が 見えたのですか？
English: Could you see the defendant that night?

Japanese: ト‥‥トーゼンや！
English: O-of course! I said I could and I meant I could!

Japanese: では、そのことを《証言》 していただきましょう。
English: Then, please testify as to the circumstances of your sighting.

Japanese: （‥‥やったぞ！）
English: (I did it!)

Japanese: （ついにスキを見つけた！ ‥‥今度こそ‥‥！）
English: (I finally found a hole in von Karma's carefully vague testimony!)

Japanese: ～どうして御剣を目撃できたか？～
English: -- How Edgeworth Was Seen --

Japanese: たしかに、あの晩は寒くて、 ぎょうさんキリが出ててん。
English: Yer right. It was a cold night, and the fog was thick as grits.

Japanese: せやからウチ、カメラをセットして クルマの中に戻ったワケや。
English: So, once I was finished setting up my camera, I got back in the car.

Japanese: でもな。ウチ、ちゃんと 双眼鏡を持ってきてたからな。
English: Still, I brought my binoculars with me.

Japanese: 湖で音がしたときは、双眼鏡を 使ォて見たわけや。
English: When I heard that noise out on the lake, I looked with my binoculars.

Japanese: どや。これなら問題ないやろ？
English: See? No problem!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。双眼鏡を使った‥‥。
English: Hmm...You used binoculars?

Japanese: わかりました。 では、弁護人。‥‥尋問を。
English: Very well. You may begin your cross- examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なんとしても、ムジュンを 見つけなければ‥‥）
English: (This one'd better be good!)

Japanese: ～どうして御剣を目撃できたか？～
English: -- How Edgeworth Was Seen --

Japanese: たしかに、あの晩は寒くて、 ぎょうさんキリが出ててん。
English: Yer right. It was a cold night, and the mist was thick as grits.

Japanese: せやからウチ、カメラをセットして クルマの中に戻ったワケや。
English: So, once I was finished setting up my camera, I got back in the car.

Japanese: でもな。ウチ、ちゃんと 双眼鏡を持ってきてたからな。
English: Still, I brought my binoculars with me.

Japanese: そのカメラは、流星を撮影する ためにセットしたものや。
English: The camera was set up to take pictures of a meteor shower.

Japanese: 湖で音がしたときは、双眼鏡を 使ォて見たわけや。
English: When I heard that noise out on the lake, I looked with my binoculars.

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔のジャマが 入ろうと‥‥）
English: (I don't care how many von Karmic objections I get...)

Japanese: （なんとしても、突破口を 見つけてやるぞ‥‥）
English: (I'm going to find a hole in this testimony if it's the last thing I do!)

Japanese: じゃあなぜ、御剣を 目撃できたんですか！
English: So? How could you see Edgeworth!?

Japanese: まあまあ、待ちィな。 今、話すワ。
English: Now, just hold your horses for a second!

Japanese: セッカチな男は モテへんで。
English: You hasty Yankee types'd never find a gal where I'm from.

Japanese: モテませんよ弁護人。
English: Defense attorneys have trouble with that as it is.

Japanese: （ダメ押しされてしまった‥‥）
English: (Nobody loves me...)

Japanese: “カメラ”‥‥ですか？
English: Your "camera"...?

Japanese: そうや。 自動シャッターをセットして‥‥、
English: Yeah. It's got an automatic...

Japanese: 今、問題になっているのは、 “御剣検事を見たこと”だ。
English: The issue we are concerned with here is Ms. Hart seeing Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: カメラのことなど、カンケイない！
English: The camera has nothing to do with this at all!

Japanese: ‥‥異議を認めます。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: （なぜ狩魔は、質問に 答えさせないんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Argh! He's not letting her answer any of my questions!)

Japanese: “双眼鏡”ですか？
English: Binoculars?

Japanese: せや。双眼鏡や。
English: Yeah, binoculars.

Japanese: 昨日のお話では、たしか、あなたは 流星を撮影していたんですよね？
English: Yesterday, you mentioned that you were out looking for shooting stars, correct?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうやけど。
English: Well... yeah.

Japanese: 星を見るなら、双眼鏡ではなく、 望遠鏡じゃないんですか‥‥？
English: Wouldn't you need a telescope, not binoculars, for that?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! そもそも、 あなたがセットしたカメラは、
English: I've got doubts about your camera, too!

Japanese: ほんとうに、流星を撮影する ためのものだったのですか？
English: Was that really to take pictures of meteor showers?

Japanese: カメラのことなど、事件には なんのカンケイもない！
English: The camera is irrelevant to this case!

Japanese: そんなことは言い切れない！
English: You can't say that for certain!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: カメラのことは、それほど 重要なことなのですか？
English: Is the camera really relevant to this case?

Japanese: もし重要なことならば、 つづけて尋問をゆるしましょう。
English: If you believe it is, you may continue with this line of questioning.

Japanese: だが！
English: But know this!

Japanese: 何も出なかった場合は、 カクゴしてもらうぞ！
English: If you find nothing with this, there will be consequences!

Japanese: 弁護人、どうしますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: カメラの問題を、 さらに追求しますか？
English: Do you wish to press further about the camera?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 質問を取り下げます。
English: Okay, Your Honor... I withdraw my question.

Japanese: （狩魔検事のことだ‥‥）
English: (I have to remember who I'm dealing with!)

Japanese: （ぼくに異議をとなえさせて、 発言を封じる作戦かもしれない）
English: (I bet von Karma wants to trap me so he can put me in contempt!)

Japanese: （ここは、やめておいたほうが よさそうだ‥‥）
English: (I think I'll hang back for now and see what happens.)

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Hah!

Japanese: （ここは、勝負どころだ！ 強気にいこう！）
English: (This is make-it-or-break-it time!)

Japanese: カメラの問題は、とんでもなく 重要です。
English: The camera is of utmost importance, Your Honor.

Japanese: この事件のすべてと言っても いいでしょう。
English: It is, perhaps, the key to this entire case!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、証言をして いただきたい！
English: Therefore, I will continue my line of questioning!

Japanese: （‥‥なんて、出まかせを 言ってしまった‥‥）
English: (Wow! Maybe I went a little overboard there...)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well!

Japanese: 証人！　カメラについて、 キチンと証言してください。
English: Ms. Hart! You will testify to the court about the camera!

Japanese: はいはい。
English: Yeah, yeah, I hear ya.

Japanese: ‥‥キリがかかっていたら、 双眼鏡で見ても同じなのでは‥‥？
English: If there was a heavy fog, how would binoculars change that at all?

Japanese: どういうこと？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 双眼鏡で見たからと言って、 キリが晴れるわけではありません。
English: Even binoculars can't see through fog!

Japanese: それで、ハッキリ見えたんですか？
English: But... you say you clearly saw him?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Er...

Japanese: 見えたと‥‥思うで‥‥。
English: I did... yeah.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ギモンの余地はないな‥‥。
English: There is no room for doubt in her testimony!

Japanese: （ナツミさん、最後は 口ごもっていたぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm. She sounded pretty doubtful to me!)

Japanese: （でも、ムジュンをつきつけない ことには、先へ進めないか‥‥）
English: (But... I have to find a clear contradiction first.)

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: 流星を撮影するのに、なぜ あの湖を選んだんですか？
English: What made you choose that lake to photograph meteors?

Japanese: あの湖は、キリが濃くて、 星の撮影には向かないような‥‥。
English: You know the fog gets thick on that lake. It's not very suited to stargazing.

Japanese: え、あ‥‥。 そやね。その、なんだ。
English: Yeah, well... Y'see, I...

Japanese: ウチ、そこまでアタマが 回らへんかったんやァ。
English: I guess I wasn't thinking too straight! Har!

Japanese: 弁護士！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人のアタマがワルいことを 攻撃するのは、やめてもらおう。
English: I will not have you badgering my witness because of her challenged intellect!

Japanese: な‥‥なんや！
English: Now wait a minute!

Japanese: 証言をつづけたまえ。 ‥‥御剣を見た方法について、だ。
English: Continue your testimony. You were saying how it was that you saw Edgeworth?

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Grr...

Japanese: よけいなことは 言わんでよろしい。
English: No unnecessary comments, please.

Japanese: “流星を撮影していた” ‥‥その証言は、ウソですね！
English: You were photographing shooting stars? That's a lie!

Japanese: な、なんやてェ！
English: S-says who!?

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Take a look at this photograph.

Japanese: これは、自動シャッターで 撮影した写真ですね？
English: This was taken on an automatic setting, was it not?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうや。
English: Y-yeah, that's right.

Japanese: 流星を撮影するなら、カメラは 空を向いていなければならない！
English: You have to point a camera upwards to take photos of the stars, Ms. Hart!

Japanese: しかし、この写真では、
English: But, as this photograph clearly shows...

Japanese: あきらかにカメラは、 湖面を向いている！
English: The camera was pointed directly at the lake!

Japanese: “流星を撮影していた” ‥‥その証言は、ウソですね！
English: You were photographing shooting stars? That's a lie!

Japanese: な、なんやてェ！
English: S-says who!?

Japanese: ぼくは昨日、実際に セットされたカメラを見ました。
English: I saw the camera you set up yesterday.

Japanese: あきらかにカメラは、湖面に向けて セットしてありました！
English: It was pointed directly at the lake!

Japanese: 流星を撮影するなら、カメラは 空を向いているはずです！
English: You have to point a camera upwards to take photos of the stars, Ms. Hart!

Japanese: ううッ！
English: Ooof!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ どういうことですか？
English: Mr. Wright! What are you driving at?

Japanese: 証人は、流星を撮影して いたのではなかった！
English: The witness was not at the lake to photograph shooting stars, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ 何を撮影していたんですか？
English: W-well then, what exactly was she photographing?

Japanese: いや‥‥そこまでは わかりませんが‥‥。
English: Well... that I don't know.

Japanese: 証人が何を撮影しようと していたか‥‥
English: What the witness takes photos of is entirely her business!

Japanese: そんなことは問題ではない！ その上‥‥、
English: This has nothing to do with the case!

Japanese: その答えすら提示することが できないとは笑止！
English: Not to mention that you can't even show us another reason why she might have been there.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: これ以上、材料がないなら、 尋問は終了してもらうぞ！
English: If you are out of material, I suggest we end this mockery of a cross-examination.

Japanese: （まずい‥‥このまま終わる ワケにはいかない！）
English: (Uh oh... I can't let the trial end like this!)

Japanese: （ここは、とにかく何か 見せておかないと！）
English: (I have to show them something!)

Japanese: 裁判長！ ‥‥これを見てください。
English: Your Honor! Take a look at this...

Japanese: ナツミさんが何を撮影 しようとしていたか‥‥！
English: What was Ms. Hart trying to photograph at the lake?

Japanese: いかがです？
English: Well, Your Honor? What do you think?

Japanese: 正直、サッパリわかりません。
English: I... think I'm confused.

Japanese: しょせん、弁護士など そんなものだろう。
English: Wrong again, Mr. Wright. So very wrong.

Japanese: ここでは、ハッタリは 通用しないのだ。
English: You can't get through this one with your weak bluffs!

Japanese: さ、裁判長！
English: Y-Your Honor!

Japanese: お遊びはここまでにして、 今度こそ証拠品を見せましょう。
English: Enough playing around! Here's the real evidence!

Japanese: お遊びをやめるのは、 あなたの方です！
English: Enough playing around is my line, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Uh...

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: あなたが撮影しようと していたのは、これです！
English: This is what you were trying to photograph!

Japanese: なんですか？ ‥‥雑誌の記事‥‥？
English: What's this? A newspaper article?

Japanese: “ひょっしー”‥‥？
English: Gourdy...?

Japanese: ああ‥‥最近、ひょうたん湖で 目撃された‥‥。
English: Ah, the sighting at Gourd Lake...

Japanese: ナツミさん！ ‥‥どうなんですか？
English: Well, Ms. Hart?

Japanese: ‥‥し、知らんで！ なんや、証拠でもあるん？
English: I... I never heard of no lake monster! You got proof or something?

Japanese: ウチが、その“ひょっしー”の 写真を撮ろうとしてた証拠や！
English: Let's see you prove that I was down at the lake trying to photograph this "Gourdy."

Japanese: ‥‥証拠は、ありませんけど‥‥。
English: Well, I don't have proof...

Japanese: ‥‥実にくだらない男だ！
English: Bah! Your ineptitude is entertaining, Mr. Wright, but enough is enough!

Japanese: 証拠もないのに、あらぬ 言いがかりを‥‥
English: I've had enough of baseless claims made without a hair of evidence to support them!

Japanese: そこまで言うなら、 証拠を見せましょう！
English: Well all right, if that's how you want to play... I'll show you evidence!

Japanese: 弁護人！ だいじょうぶなんですか？
English: Mr. Wright! Are you sure about this?

Japanese: （どうも、あの検事にバカに されると、ガマンできない！）
English: (I'm sure sick of that smarmy prosecutor putting me down!)

Japanese: （これって、挑発に乗っている だけなのかな‥‥？）
English: (Or... did he taunt me so I'd get mad and make a mistake?)

Japanese: 証拠！　もちろんありますよ！
English: I have it! Proof!

Japanese: ふむう。 ‥‥それはおもしろい。
English: Hmm... Intriguing!

Japanese: では、見せていただきましょう。 ‥‥慎重におねがいしますよ。
English: Very well, let's see it. And... no joking around this time, please.

Japanese: 証人が、ヒョッシーの写真を撮ろう としていたことを示す、証拠品を！
English: Here is proof that the witness was trying to photograph Gourdy, the lake monster!

Japanese: 証拠は、これです！ ‥‥カンタンなことですよ。
English: Here's my proof! It's simple!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... カンタンなことなら、 間違えないでいただきたい。
English: If it's simple... then why have you obviously made an error, Mr. Wright?
Urk...?

Japanese: なんやそれ？
English: What's that!?

Japanese: 帰れ。
English: Go home. Consider a career change.

Japanese: （しまった！）
English: (Gah!)

Japanese: （ぼくもふくめて、みんな リカイ不能か！）
English: (How can they so casually toss aside this eviden... Oh. Whoops. Wrong evidence.)

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 もう一度、おねがいしましょう。
English: Luckily for you, I'm in a patient mood, Mr. Wright. One more time please.

Japanese: 証拠は、ナツミさん！ あなたのカメラですよ！
English: The proof, Ms. Hart, is your own camera!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! あなたのカメラは、“破裂音”に 反応するようセットされていた。
English: Your camera was set to take photos in response to loud noises, correct?

Japanese: この写真が撮られたのも、銃声に カメラが反応したからです。
English: Thus this photograph here, taken when a gun fired on the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... そして、ここに“ヒョッシー”の 記事があります。
English: And here, this article about "Gourdy."

Japanese: この記事によると、
English: According to this article...

Japanese: ヒョッシーがあらわれるときには、 “破裂音”がするらしいですね。
English: Gourdy made a loud noise when it emerged!

Japanese: どうですか！
English: Well?

Japanese: あなたはヒョッシーを撮影しようと していた！
English: You were trying to photograph Gourdy, weren't you!

Japanese: だから、カメラの自動シャッターを “破裂音”にセットしていたんだ！
English: That's why you had set your camera to respond to loud noises!

Japanese: 証拠は、この“ヒョッシー”の 記事です！
English: The proof is this article about Gourdy the lake monster!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! この記事によると、
English: According to this article...

Japanese: ヒョッシーは“破裂音”に反応して あらわれるらしいですね。
English: When Gourdy breaks through the water, it makes a loud noise!

Japanese: そして、あなたのカメラも、
English: Which is why you set up your camera with that microphone!

Japanese: “破裂音”に反応してシャッターが 切られるよう、セットされていた！
English: You had it set to respond to loud noises!

Japanese: この写真が撮られたのも、カメラが 銃声に反応したからです！
English: And that's how you got this picture when the pistol fired!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 私もじつは、オカシイと 思っていたんですよ‥‥。
English: I, too, thought it was a little strange.

Japanese: （ホントかよ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, sure!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうです？　ナツミさん。
English: Well, Ms. Hart?

Japanese: あなたは、ヒョッシーを撮ろうと、 キャンプをしていたんですね？
English: You were camping there to try and take a photo of Gourdy, weren't you?
Yeah.

Japanese: ‥‥オミゴトやね。 さすがは弁護士さんや。
English: Not bad. Are all you lawyers that smart?

Japanese: でも。
English: So, smart boy, I was down there trying to photograph Gourdy, you got me.

Japanese: だから、何なん？
English: So what?
Huh...?

Japanese: ウチの見たものに、 変わりはないで。
English: That don't change what I saw, does it?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 長い時間をかけて、キサマが 証明したことは、
English: What you just used several precious minutes of our time to prove...

Japanese: この証人が、カイジュウの撮影を ユメ見る、バカな女ということだ。
English: is nothing more than that the witness is an idiot who thinks monsters exist!

Japanese: な、なんやソレ！
English: H-hey!

Japanese: しかし、 だからどうだというのだ？
English: But, as she so succinctly said, so what!?

Japanese: 何も変わらないではないか？
English: It changes nothing!

Japanese: （そんなことはない）
English: (Not true!)

Japanese: （お前たちは、ヒョッシーのことを かくそうとしていた）
English: (You were hiding the whole thing about Gourdy for some reason, I know it!)

Japanese: （かくさなければならない ワケがあったからだ！）
English: (But what could it have been...?)

Japanese: （今度は、 それを暴いてやるぞ‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, I'm getting to the bottom of this!)

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: なぜ、ヒョッシーのことを かくそうとしたのですか？
English: Why did you hide the fact that you were searching for Gourdy from the court?

Japanese: 最後に、すべてを 《証言》してください。
English: Please revise your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... わかったワ。‥‥証言、する。 ‥‥なんも変わらんと思うけど。
English: Right. Fine, I'll testify. It won't change nothing, though.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、何かが 変わっているはずだ）
English: (Something will change... it has to!)

Japanese: （それを‥‥見抜く！）
English: (And I'm going to spot it!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... ～ナツミのこと・事件当夜のこと～
English: -- Lotta's New Testimony --

Japanese: 実はウチ、大学の研究員 ちゃうんや。
English: Actually, I'm not a research student at a university.

Japanese: ホンマはな‥‥、 フリーカメラマンやってんねん。
English: I'm an investigative photographer.

Japanese: 今、話題のヒョッシーの ハッキリした写真を撮れば、
English: Imagine what a scoop it'd be if I got a picture of that monster!

Japanese: スクープになる思うて、 キャンプ張っとったんや。
English: That's why I was camping out by the lake.

Japanese: でも、かくしてたんは、 それだけやで。
English: But, that's all I was hiding.

Japanese: “パアン”て音がして、見たら 湖にボートが浮かんでたんや。
English: When I heard the "bang" I looked right straight out at that lake.

Japanese: 他に見るモンもないねんから、 ずっとボート見てるしかないやん。
English: There wasn't much else to look at, so I just watched that boat the whole time.

Japanese: で、男の手もとが光って、 もう１回、音が聞こえたんや。
English: Then I saw a flash, near one of the men's hands, and I heard another gunshot.

Japanese: ウチはホンマ、ボートをジッと 見てたんや。‥‥ホンマやで。
English: I was looking right at that boat, the whole time, cross my heart and hope to fry.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 では成歩堂くん、《尋問》を‥‥。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright. You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: 証人の証言は、 さっきと変わっておらぬ。
English: The witness's testimony is unchanged from before!

Japanese: 証人が研究員だろうと カメラマンだろうと、同じこと！
English: Whether she is a research student or a photographer has no bearing on this case!

Japanese: 《尋問》など、いらぬッ！
English: There is no need to waste more of our time with another pointless cross-examination!

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: Er... hmm.

Japanese: ぼくはあくまで、 弁護側の権利を主張します！
English: I claim the defense's right to cross-examine the witness, Your Honor!

Japanese: （狩魔のコトバには、 かならず意味がある‥‥）
English: (von Karma's up to something, I know it!)

Japanese: （尋問をさせたくないのは、 今の証言にムジュンが‥‥）
English: (He doesn't want me to cross- examine her because... why? Was there a contradiction?)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 《尋問》を認めます。
English: Very well. You may begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: そこまで言うなら、何か 考えがあるのでしょう。
English: You seem sure of yourself, you must have something in mind.

Japanese: あるか、そんなもの！
English: Hah! That would be a first!

Japanese: （ははは‥‥）
English: (Hah hah, very funny.)

Japanese: わかっていると思いますが、 これは最後の尋問です。
English: You understand that this is your last chance at a cross-examination, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 問題がなかったら、証人は 退廷してもらいます。
English: If there is no problem with the testimony this time, we will let the witness leave.

Japanese: その時点で、判決を 言い渡したいと思います。
English: I will announce my verdict at that time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしいですね。
English: Understood?
Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～ナツミのこと・事件当夜のこと～
English: -- Lotta's New Testimony --

Japanese: 実はウチ、大学の研究員 ちゃうんや。
English: Actually, I'm not a research student at a university.

Japanese: ホンマはな‥‥、 フリーカメラマンやってんねん。
English: I'm an investigative photographer.

Japanese: 今、話題のヒョッシーの ハッキリした写真を撮れば、
English: Imagine what a scoop it'd be if I got a picture of that monster!

Japanese: スクープになる思うて、 キャンプ張っとったんや。
English: That's why I was camping out by the lake.

Japanese: でも、かくしてたんは、 それだけやで。
English: But, that's all I was hiding.

Japanese: “パアン”て音がして、見たら 湖にボートが浮かんでたんや。
English: When I heard the "bang" I looked right straight out at that lake.

Japanese: 他に見るモンもないねんから、 ずっとボート見てるしかないやん。
English: There wasn't much else to look at, so I just watched that boat the whole time.

Japanese: で、男の手もとが光って、 もう１回、音が聞こえたんや。
English: Then I saw a flash, near one of the men's hands, and I heard another gunshot.

Japanese: （‥‥この証言の中に ムジュンを見つけなければ）
English: (I'd better find some sort of contradiction in this testimony...)

Japanese: （あの検事に勝つことは できない‥‥）
English: (I won't be able to beat von Karma any other way...)

Japanese: （何かあるはずだ‥‥！）
English: (There has to be something!)

Japanese: じゃあ、あなたは何者なんですか！
English: Then what manner of person are you!?

Japanese: “なにもの”ゆうほど 大したモンやあらへん。
English: I'm not sure I'm high- falutin' enough to be called a "manner" of anything.

Japanese: “フリーカメラマン”‥‥ですか。
English: An "investigative photographer"?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: スクープ写真撮って、マスコミに 買うてもらうんや。
English: You get your photo, and sell it to the press. It's that kind of business.

Japanese: あのな。去年、妹の成人式で 写真を撮ってやったんや。
English: Hey, I was taking pictures at my sister's graduation last year...

Japanese: ほしたら！
English: N' guess what!

Japanese: ほしたら？
English: Umm... what?

Japanese: 空に、ＵＦＯが写ってたんや！
English: There was a yoofoh just a' hanging in the sky!

Japanese: “ゆーほー”‥‥？
English: A "yoofoh"...?

Japanese: 空とぶ円盤や。
English: You know, an "Unidentified Flying Object"? A yoofoh!

Japanese: このときウチは、さとったんや。
English: That's when I had sort of a revelation.

Japanese: “自分は、スクープカメラマンに なるべき女や”ってな。
English: I knew I should become an investigative photographer!

Japanese: そ‥‥それはよかったですね。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: （“スクープ”のイミを、完全に とり違えているな‥‥）
English: (Kind of a shaky basis for a career...)

Japanese: ‥‥話題なんですか？ “ヒョッシー”って。
English: Is "Gourdy" really all that newsworthy?

Japanese: あた、あたりまえやんか！ ‥‥テレビでもやってたで！
English: Heck yeah! They even had him up on the TV!

Japanese: （ローカル局の“まちのうわさ” のコーナーじゃないか‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure that appearing on the local news "rumor of the month" show qualifies.)

Japanese: （先月はたしか“どんぐり山に ヤマンバあらわる！”だった）
English: (Last month's show was "Bigfoot Sighted on Acorn Hill, I believe.)"

Japanese: なんや！　今回は雑誌に写真も 出たんや！　ホンモノやで！
English: Hey! They also had a picture of him in the newspaper! For real!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この証人、ツッコミ出したら キリがありません。
English: This is one fight I do not believe you can win.

Japanese: 先に進みましょう。
English: Let's keep moving, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: それで、カメラに自動シャッターを とりつけて‥‥？
English: That's why you put the automatic sensor on your camera?

Japanese: そうや。大学のセンセから 借りてきたマシーンや。
English: Yep. Borrowed it from a friend at a university.

Japanese: まわりの音の波形をブンセキして、 破裂音がしたら‥‥
English: It analyzes every sound it picks up, and when it gets a "bang!"...

Japanese: シャッターが下りる、と。
English: It snaps a shot.

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: ‥‥で？　今まで、何枚ぐらい 撮影したんですか？
English: So, how many pictures has it taken so far?

Japanese: カメラのシャッターが作動 したんは、あの晩だけや。
English: The only time the camera triggered was that night.

Japanese: そもそも、なんで かくしてたんですか？
English: I think it's time you told us why you felt you had to hide your true purpose at the lake.

Japanese: 正体バラしたら、ライバルが 増えるやん。
English: Heck, if word got out what I was up to, the lake'd be swarmin' with competitors!

Japanese: ライバル？
English: Competitors...?

Japanese: ヒョッシーを狙うカメラマン どもや！
English: Yeah! Second-rate shutterbugs trying to steal my scoop!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Ah...

Japanese: かくしていた理由は、 それだけですか？
English: Is that the only reason you were hiding the truth?

Japanese: いや、実はナ‥‥
English: W-well, actually...

Japanese: 事件にカンケイないことは 聞かないでもらおう！
English: Mr. Wright! I'll not have you asking questions with no relevance to this case!!!

Japanese: （やっぱり、狩魔が口止め していたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Whatever you say, von Karma. I know you told her to keep quiet.)

Japanese: どんな音でしたか？
English: Exactly what sort of sound was it?

Japanese: 聞いたことないけど、やっぱり 銃声だったんちゃうか？
English: Well, I never heard one before so I can't say for sure... but it sounded like a gunshot.

Japanese: 意外にアッサリした音やったで。
English: It was a lot sharper a sound than I would have expected.

Japanese: ‥‥“他に見るモノがなかった”？
English: There wasn't "much else to look at"?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだろうか‥‥。　もし、 彼女が破裂音を聞いたら‥‥）
English: (I dunno. If she heard a bang... and she thought Gourdy was out there...)

Japanese: （ボートなんか、 見ているハズがないぞ‥‥）
English: (I kind of doubt she'd waste any time looking at a boat.)

Japanese: なんや？　ヒトのカオ、 そんなに見つめちゃって！
English: What? What did I do now? What're you giving me that look for?

Japanese: （‥‥どう考えてもオカシイ。 あれをつきつけてみるか‥‥）
English: (Definitely suspicious... Maybe it's time for some evidence...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥先を。
English: Witness! Continue.

Japanese: せかすなや、オッサン。
English: Hold your hushpuppies, pops, I'm getting there.

Japanese: そのとき、湖にはボートしか なかったんですか？
English: Was there nothing on the lake but the boat at that time?

Japanese: ははあ。どこかベツの場所から 撃たれた、とか考えてるんやな？
English: Huh? Wait, so you're thinking maybe he was shot from some other place?

Japanese: それはないと思うで。
English: I don't think so, nope.

Japanese: 湖は、さざ波ひとつなかったし、 岸にも人影はなかったワ。
English: The lake was smooth as glass, and nobody was on the shore, neither.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥）
ナツミさん！
English: Ms. Hart!

Japanese: あなたは、本当にボートを 見ていたんですか？
English: Were you REALLY looking at that boat!?

Japanese: な‥‥何を言うんや！ アタリマエやん！
English: W-what's with you!? Course I was looking at it!

Japanese: 湖には、ボートしかなかったんや！ ‥‥フツー、それを見るやん！
English: It was the only thing out there! Any normal person'd be looking at it!

Japanese: フツーはそうかもしれません。
English: I agree, any normal person would.

Japanese: しかし、あなたはフツーでは なかった！
English: But you are far from normal!

Japanese: な‥‥、なんやて！
English: Wh-what!? Y'all wanna step over here and say that!?

Japanese: あなたは、ヒョッシーの写真を 撮るためにキャンプをしていた！
English: You were camping at the lake to take a picture of Gourdy!

Japanese: それならば！
English: Think about it--

Japanese: 破裂音を聞いたあなたは、 何はともあれ、
English: What would you do if you heard a loud noise?

Japanese: 湖の上に、ヒョッシーのすがたを 探したはずです！
English: You'd be scanning the lake for any sign of Gourdy, that's what!

Japanese: だから、ボートなんかに 注意をはらうハズがない！
English: You wouldn't give the boat a second thought!

Japanese: あッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: 静粛に！　つづけてください！ 成歩堂くん！
English: Order! Continue, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたは、双眼鏡でボートを 見ていたと証言した！
English: You testified that you were watching the boat through binoculars!

Japanese: しかし、ただボートを見るのに、 双眼鏡はいらない！
English: However, you wouldn't need binoculars to watch that boat!

Japanese: あなたは、必死にヒョッシーを 探していたんです！
English: You needed them to search for Gourdy... and that's what you were doing!
Well!?
Hmph...

Japanese: そういえば、ウチ‥‥。
English: Well, now that y'all mention it...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥双眼鏡を取って、 湖をスミズミまで、何度も‥‥
English: I did sort of take my binoculars and kind of scan the lake a bit...

Japanese: ヒョッシーがいないかと 思って‥‥。
English: I mean, Gourdy might be out there, n' all...

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人！
English: M-Ms. Hart!

Japanese: では、証人は、ボートは 見ていなかったと‥‥？
English: A-are you saying that you were NOT watching the boat, then?
S-sorry, y'all.

Japanese: ウソついてたワケじゃ ないねん‥‥。
English: I wasn't fibbing, really.

Japanese: ウチ‥‥殺人事件の目撃者に なれる思うて、その‥‥。
English: I was, just... I thought y'know, I could be witness to a murder n' all!

Japanese: ちょっとウレシなってもうてな。
English: I kinda got excited.

Japanese: ボートを見た、て‥‥ 思い込んでもうてん‥‥。
English: I was sure I was watching that boat... till now.
This... this is totally uncalled for--

Japanese: で、でも‥‥、でもな！
English: B-but hey!

Japanese: この写真は、バッチリ証拠に なるやろ！
English: You got the photograph! You got proof!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかし、誰が撃ったかまでは、 見えないようですが‥‥
English: Still, we can't see who is shooting who in this.

Japanese: そうやろ！　そうやろ！
English: Right! Right!

Japanese: ‥‥だからなァ、 ウチ、この写真を‥‥‥
English: That's why I took this photo n'...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。もうケッコウだ。
English: Witness... That's enough.

Japanese: ごくろうだった。‥‥もう、 キサマは口を開く必要はない。
English: You've had a long day. Shut your pie-hole.

Japanese: キ‥‥“キサマ”？
English: Sh-shut my what!?

Japanese: （ナツミさん、今、何を 言いかけたんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What was she going to say?)

Japanese: （“だからなァ、ウチ、 この写真を‥‥”か）
English: (She took the photo... and what?)

Japanese: （そういえば、昨日‥‥）
English: (Wait a second...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかも彼女、 証拠写真まで撮っていたッス。
English: She even had a photograph to prove it.

Japanese: この写真じゃあ、誰が撃ったか 見えませんね‥‥。
English: But you really can't tell from the photo who is shooting.

Japanese: 彼女、これからその写真を 拡大するって言ってたッス。
English: That's why she said she's going to enlarge the photo.

Japanese: 画像はアラくなるけど、もう少し ハッキリするはずや！　‥‥とか。
English: She said "it'll drop the quality a mite, but should let us see who's who!"

Japanese: （彼女は あの写真を拡大したはずだ）
English: (She enlarged that photo!)

Japanese: （なぜ狩魔は、その写真を 提出させないんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why won't von Karma let her show it?)

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥）
English: (I've got a hunch!)

Japanese: （もしかしたら、その拡大写真に、 何か狩魔に不利なことが‥‥？）
English: (I bet that enlarged photo shows something bad for von Karma!)

Japanese: （‥‥ここは、勝負どころだ！）
English: (This is my chance!)

Japanese: （ぼくのカン違いだったら、 御剣の有罪は決定的になる！）
English: (If I'm wrong, though, it'll mean prison for Edgeworth... or worse.)

Japanese: （さて‥‥どうする‥‥！）
English: (What should I do!?)

Japanese: （これが狩魔のワナだったら‥‥）
English: (If this a trap...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、ここはようすを 見るべきか‥‥）
English: (I'd better hold back and see how things go.)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥このまま見すごしたら もう尋問は終わってしまう‥‥）
English: (But... if I wait now, the cross-examination will be over!)

Japanese: （ホントに、このまま何もしないで いいのか‥‥！）
English: (Can I really risk that!?)

Japanese: では、これで大沢木　ナツミさんの 尋問を終了します。
English: This hereby ends the cross- examination of Ms. Lotta Hart.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 ずいぶん回り道をしたものだ‥‥。
English: And none too soon. That was a flagrant waste of my time.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 ‥‥他に何か、ありますか？
English: Mr. von Karma, do you have anything to add?

Japanese: 最初に言ったはずだ。
English: I stated everything I needed to when this trial began.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠。
English: Decisive evidence.

Japanese: 決定的な証人。
English: A decisive witness.

Japanese: ‥‥他に何か、必要なものは？
English: What else could possibly be required?

Japanese: そうですね。ありません。
English: Nothing, of course.

Japanese: （しまった！　やっぱり、 食いさがるべきだったか！）
English: (Oh no! I should have pressed further!)

Japanese: （ここは、勝負に出るしかない！ 一気に行くぞ‥‥！）
English: (I have to fight now or I could lose everything!)

Japanese: ナツミさん！ この写真を見てください。
English: Ms. Hart! Look at this photograph.

Japanese: あなたは、この写真を 拡大したそうですね！
English: You enlarged this photograph, did you not?

Japanese: そ、そうや！　そうなんや！
English: Y-yeah! I did!

Japanese: その写真は、 なぜ提出されないんですか！
English: Why has that enlargement not been presented to the court!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな写真は 存在しない！
English: B-because it does not exist!

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんや！
English: What're y'all talking about!?

Japanese: “それは提出してはならん” ってドナりつけたのは、
English: You were the one who told me not to show it in court in the first place!

Japanese: アンタやないか！
English: You old fool!

Japanese: どういうことですか！ 狩魔検事！
English: What's the meaning of this, Mr. von Karma!

Japanese: ウ‥‥ウム‥‥
English: Er... erm...

Japanese: ナツミさん！
English: Ms. Hart!

Japanese: 提出してください！ ‥‥拡大写真を！
English: Show the photo to the court! Show us the enlargement!

Japanese: 検察側は、提出に反対する！
English: The prosecution objects to the submission of this evidence!

Japanese: 異議は却下します。
English: Objection... denied.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥写真を見せてください。
English: The witness will show the enlargement to the court.

Japanese: これが、そうや！
English: Here it is.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、誰が撃ったかは 見わけられませんね‥‥。
English: Hmm. We still cannot see who is firing in this.

Japanese: 被告人であるような、 そうではないような‥‥。
English: It could be the defendant... or maybe it's not.

Japanese: とにかく、証拠として 受理しましょう。
English: Regardless, I'll accept this as evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《湖の写真》を 拡大写真に差しかえた。
English: Lake Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥気がすんだか？　弁護士。
English: Happy now, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。 （何か、あるはずなんだが‥‥）
English: Hmm... (There has to be something!)

Japanese: 拡大写真‥‥？
English: You asked for the enlargement, you got the enlargement.

Japanese: ハッ！ ‥‥なんの証拠にもならん。
English: And little good it has done any of us!

Japanese: だからワガハイは、提出するなと 言ったのだ！
English: That's why I requested she not show it!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: となると、この証人に対する 尋問は‥‥
English: I suppose this means that the cross-examination...

Japanese: 当然、終了だ！
English: Is over! Obviously!

Japanese: では、これで大沢木　ナツミさんの 尋問を終了します。
English: Then, I would like to close the cross-examination of Ms. Lotta Hart.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 ずいぶん回り道をしたものだ‥‥。
English: And none too soon. That was a flagrant waste of my time.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 ‥‥他に何か、ありますか？
English: Mr. von Karma, do you have anything to add?

Japanese: 最初に言ったはずだ。
English: I stated everything I needed to when this trial began.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠。
English: Decisive evidence.

Japanese: 決定的な証人。
English: A decisive witness.

Japanese: ‥‥他に何か、必要なものは？
English: What else could possibly be required?

Japanese: そうですね。ありません。
English: Nothing, of course.

Japanese: では、ついに判決を くだすときが来たようですね。
English: Then, I believe it is time for me to declare my verdict.

Japanese: （‥‥こんなハズはない！）
English: (Wait... it's not supposed to go like this!)

Japanese: （この写真には、何か手がかりが かくされているはずだ！）
English: (There has to be a clue in this photo... somewhere!)

Japanese: （くそっ！　‥‥どうする‥‥！）
English: (This is bad! Real bad! What should I do!?)

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってください！
English: W-wait!

Japanese: 裁判長！ この証拠品を‥‥
English: Your Honor, This evidence...

Japanese: 証拠品については、じゅうぶん ギロンしたはずだ！
English: I believe we have spent enough time talking about evidence!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。そうですね。
English: Hmm... indeed.

Japanese: この拡大写真の他は、 すでに意見は聞きましたね‥‥。
English: We've heard opinions on every piece of evidence but this enlargement.

Japanese: 今さら、他の証拠品を見ても しかたがないでしょう。
English: I see no point in retracing our steps.

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！ 何も思いつかない‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... think of something!)

Japanese: （ここは、ようすを‥‥）
English: (No good... I guess I'll just sit back and see how this turns out.)

Japanese: わかりました！ では、私の考えを述べましょう。
English: Very well! Allow me to state my verdict.

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: この拡大写真には、決定的に おかしい点があります！
English: There is something decidedly strange with this enlargement!

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: W-what might that be?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: そのおかしな点とは なんですか！
English: You will show the court what you mean! What about this photo is "strange"?

Japanese: （ええい！　もう あとには引けないぞ‥‥！）
English: (Okay... here goes nothing!)

Japanese: （裁判長に、この写真の おかしいところを示そう！）
English: (I'll show the judge what's strange about this photo.)

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: Here, your honor!

Japanese: ‥‥発砲している人物、ですか。
English: The shooter...?

Japanese: もう少し、具体的に 教えてもらえますかな。
English: I'm not sure I understand. What about the shooter is strange? Can you be more specific?
(Specific, right...)

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: Here, Your Honor!
Where...?

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥あなたが 追いつめられているのはわかる。
English: Mr. Wright, I understand you're in a tight spot.

Japanese: しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: ムリヤリ証拠にケチを つけるのは‥‥。
English: You have nothing to gain by making baseless claims of faulty evidence!

Japanese: い、いえ！　今のは、 ちょっと指がすべっただけです！
English: Y-Your Honor! Never mind that... my, my finger slipped!

Japanese: もう一度、おねがいします！
English: Please let me show you again!
Hmm...

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: Here, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥発砲している人物、ですか。
English: The shooter...?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりません。この人物の どこがオカシイのでしょう？
English: I'm not sure I understand. What about the shooter is strange?

Japanese: ピストルを持っている手に 注目していただきたい。
English: Look at the hand holding the pistol, Your Honor!
The hand...?

Japanese: その手は、ある証拠と 決定的にムジュンしているのです。
English: That hand directly contradicts another piece of evidence!

Japanese: この男の左手が‥‥ですか？
English: This man's left hand does what...!?

Japanese: では、お見せしましょう。
English: Let me show you.

Japanese: 写真の男の左手とムジュンする、 決定的な証拠を！
English: I'll show you the evidence that left hand contradicts!

Japanese: （いやいや、これで“くらえ”は ないだろう‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I'm not sure this warrants a "take that!")

Japanese: （もう一度、考えなおすんだ！）
English: (I'd better think this one through!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥早くしてください。
English: Mr. Wright, please hurry up. We haven't got all day.

Japanese: は、はい！ ‥‥今、お見せします！
English: Y-yes, Your Honor! Right away!

Japanese: ハッキリしています。
English: The evidence is clear.

Japanese: 写真の男は、ピストルを 間違いなく左手で撃っています。
English: The man in this photograph is holding that pistol in his left hand.

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: ピストルには、御剣検事の “右手”の指紋がついていた！
English: The prints on the murder weapon were from Edgeworth's right hand!

Japanese: つまり！
English: Ergo!

Japanese: この写真でピストルを 撃っている人物は、
English: The man shooting the pistol in this photograph...

Japanese: 御剣検事では、あり得ないのです！
English: Is not Mr. Edgeworth!!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、みなさんも おちついたようですから、
English: Now that everyone in the courtroom has quieted down...

Japanese: 審理を再開しましょう。
English: I would like to reconvene this court of law!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたは、ミゴトに 証明しました。
English: You have given us definitive proof today.

Japanese: 御剣検事が、ピストルを 撃っていないことを。
English: We now know that it was not Mr. Edgeworth who fired the pistol that night.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: However...

Japanese: そうなると、大きな問題が 生まれてしまいます。
English: This leaves us with a rather large problem.

Japanese: いったい被害者は 誰に撃たれたのか？
English: If Mr. Edgeworth didn't do it, then who shot our victim?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Precisely!

Japanese: 湖には、ごらんのとおり、 彼ら以外に人影はない‥‥！
English: As we have seen, there were no other people on the lake that night!

Japanese: 被告人の他に誰が、被害者を 撃てたというのか！
English: Who but the defendant could have shot the victim!?

Japanese: それは、目撃者の 大沢木　ナツミさんです！
English: Who else but the witness, Ms. Lotta Hart!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: しょ‥‥証拠はあるんですか！
English: Do you have proof of this!?

Japanese: （やれやれ。何かと言えば 証拠、証拠ってうるさいな‥‥）
English: (Proof-shmoof! Always with the proof! Oh... wait, I do need proof, don't I.)

Japanese: コラ！　なんですか そのタイドは！
English: Mr. Wright! I'll have you remember this is a court of law!

Japanese: （怒られてしまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. The Judge is mad.)

Japanese: もう一度、聞こう。
English: Let me ask again!

Japanese: （い、いやいや、それは マズいだろう‥‥）
English: (Wait, wait--I can't do that.)

Japanese: （友人を助けるために、他の 友人を売ってどうするんだ！）
English: (Sacrifice one friend to save another? What's the point!?)

Japanese: どうした！　弁護士！ 答えたまえ！
English: Well, Wright! Your answer!

Japanese: 答えは１つしか残っていない！
English: There is only one explanation remaining!

Japanese: 被害者自身です。
English: The man who shot the victim was none other than... the victim himself!!!

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: つまり‥‥被害者は、 “自殺”だった、と‥‥？
English: So... you are saying that the victim committed suicide?

Japanese: そうです。それ以外に 考えようがありません。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I can think of no other explanation.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たしかに、それ以外は 考えられない‥‥
English: Indeed, that does seem to be the only remaining option.

Japanese: ザンネンだが、弁護士‥‥。
English: I'm so very, very sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 自殺は、あり得ない。
English: But suicide is out of the question.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 被害者の傷あとを調べれば、 撃たれた距離がわかるのだ。
English: An examination of the victim's wound reveals the distance at which he was shot. The... distance?

Japanese: 被害者は、１ｍ以上はなれた 場所から撃たれている。
English: The victim was clearly shot from further than a meter away!
A meter! Th-that's three feet!

Japanese: 自殺では、あり得ないのだよ。
English: There is no way it could have been suicide!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 狩魔検事！　そのデータは 間違いないのですか！
English: Mr. von Karma! Are you sure of the accuracy of your data!?

Japanese: 検察側も、自殺の可能性は 考えたからな‥‥。
English: Of course! I had already considered the possibility of suicide, you see.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Autopsy Report updated in the Court Record.
Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: では、私の考えを述べましょう。
English: Very well, allow me to state my opinion.

Japanese: 状況を考えれば、犯人は 被告人・御剣氏しかあり得ません。
English: Considering the situation, the shooter had to be the defendant, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: ピストルの指紋は、撃った人間は 御剣氏でないことを示している。
English: The prints on the gun reveal that the shooter was not Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥そこで。
English: This is a conundrum.

Japanese: 本日の審理は、ここで 中断したいと思います。
English: Therefore, I would like to suspend proceedings for this trial for the day.

Japanese: 弁護側・検察側、それぞれに、 さらなる調査を命じます。
English: The court orders the defense and the prosecution to further investigate this matter.

Japanese: よろしいですか。
English: Understood?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned.

Japanese: １２月２６日　午後１時１５分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 26, 1:15 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: あー、あぶなかった。
English: Whew, that was a close one.
Hey!

Japanese: 何か、言うことはないのか？
English: Don't you have anything to say!?

Japanese: まだ、無罪判決を 受けたわけではないからな。
English: No. I have yet to be declared innocent, Wright.

Japanese: やれやれ。
English: Well, yeah, but...

Japanese: しかし、いったい どうなってるんだ？
English: What happened out there on that lake, anyway!?

Japanese: 被害者が自殺じゃなかったら、 いったい誰が‥‥？
English: If he didn't commit suicide, then who...?

Japanese: しかも、撃たれた距離は、 約１ｍだぞ‥‥。
English: The shooter was about a meter away, too!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ な、なんだ！　その目つきは！ ...
English: W-what? Don't give me that look!

Japanese: わた、私じゃないぞ！
English: I did not kill him!

Japanese: 冗談だよ。
English: I was just kidding around.

Japanese: ‥‥うむむ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: まあ、いいや。
English: Look...

Japanese: じゃあ、ぼくはとりあえず、 真宵ちゃんのところに行くから。
English: I'm going to go check on Maya.

Japanese: ああ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.
What?

Japanese: 彼女に、伝えてくれないか。
English: Tell her something for me.

Japanese: 何を？
English: What?
Tell...

Japanese: 発言するときは、 もっと注意しろ、とな。
English: Tell her to watch what she says in court.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけだ。
English: That's all.

Japanese: （‥‥スナオじゃないヤツ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I'm sure she'll be happy to hear you say that, Edgeworth. Jerk!)

Japanese: 明日の法廷の武器にするため、
English: I requisitioned a transcript of Lotta's entire testimony.

Japanese: 大沢木　ナツミさんの証言を まとめた書類をもらった。
English: I thought it might give me some ammunition for the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: けっきょく彼女、撃った人物は 見ていなかったので、
English: Of course she didn't see the shooter...

Japanese: 聞いた“音”だけが、証言として 認められたみたいだ。
English: So the only part of her testimony that stood was the "bang" she heard.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミの証言書》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Deposition added to the Court Record.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、御剣　怜侍に 判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 26 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... 誰もいないと この事務所、しずかだな。
English: Sure is quiet here with nobody around.

Japanese: そうだ‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: 早く、真宵ちゃんを 出してあげないと‥‥。
English: I have to get Maya out of that detention cell soon!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's favorite plant. Its name is "Charley."

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんも 世話に慣れてきたようで、
English: Maya's gotten the knack of watering it lately.

Japanese: チャーリーくん、元気を 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: Charley's been perking up these days.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ボーイが、引き出しのドライバーの 角度をなおしているのが見える。
English: I can see the bellboy, getting the angle of that screwdriver in the drawer just right.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていた デスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: お客がもっと増えれば 座る機会もあるだろうが、
English: If we had more clients, I would probably sit here more often.

Japanese: 今はソファでテレビを 見ていることの方が多い。
English: Lately, I've been spending more time on the couch, watching TV.

Japanese: 一度、真宵ちゃんがトノサマンの ポスターを持ってきて、
English: Maya brought in a poster of the Steel Samurai the other day.

Japanese: 張る、張らないで大ゲンカに なってしまった。
English: We had a big fight over whether to put it up or not.

Japanese: まだあきらめきれないらしく、 スキをうかがっているみたい。
English: I know she's just waiting for a chance to sneak it up on the wall.

Japanese: いやあ。やっぱりいいねえ、 シャバの空気は。
English: Ahh! Fresh air! I gotta say, freedom feels great.

Japanese: 明日の法廷、もう ムチャしないでくれよ。
English: Behave yourself in the courtroom tomorrow, okay?

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。 それは、どうかな？
English: Heh heh heh. Misbehaving's much more fun.

Japanese: もう御剣も、保釈金 払ってくれないぞ、たぶん。
English: It's not going to be so much fun when Edgeworth refuses to pay your bail again.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... うん。ムチャしない。
English: Right. I'll behave.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (Oh dear...)

Japanese: どうしようか？ これから‥‥。
English: Well, what should we do?

Japanese: あたし、留置所にいたから 何も知らないんだよ。
English: I don't know! I've been in detention this whole time.

Japanese: なるほどくんにまかせた！
English: I think I'll let you decide what we should do. Deal?

Japanese: どう？ ‥‥何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Any thoughts you want to share?

Japanese: とにかくあたし、留置所に いたからねー。
English: Well, I was in detention all day.

Japanese: ちょっと、考える時間が ほしいなあ。
English: I think I'd like more time to think.

Japanese: （留置所では、きっと千尋さんの ことでも考えていたんだろうな）
English: (Poor Maya, she probably thought about Mia the whole time she was in there...)

Japanese: あたしに何か見せるより、 早く調査に行こうよ！
English: Don't waste time showing me things! We have to get cracking on this case!

Japanese: テキは、またベツの証人を つれてくるんだから！
English: You know the enemy has more tricks up his sleeve! He's bringing in another witness!

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: あっ！　なるほどくん！
English: Hey! Nick, it's you!

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶだった みたいだね‥‥御剣検事。
English: I'm glad Mr. Edgeworth made it through the day okay.

Japanese: よかった‥‥。
English: It's a relief... Hey.

Japanese: なんで、あんなこと したんだよ！
English: Why'd you do that, anyway?
I don't know.

Japanese: でも‥‥あたし、何か しなくちゃ、って思って。
English: I... just knew I had to do something.

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃあ、あたしは お姉ちゃんじゃないけど‥‥。
English: I know I'm not the lawyer my sister was...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ゴメンナサイ。
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: まあ、おかげで 助かったんだけどさ‥‥。
English: Well, you did save the trial...

Japanese: もう、ムチャしないでくれよ。
English: Just, behave from now on, okay?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: O... okay.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch since I came in. A real pro.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: もう、取り調べとか受けたの？
English: Have you been questioned yet?

Japanese: ううん。‥‥まだ。
English: No... not yet.

Japanese: さっき、イトノコさんが 来たんだよ。
English: Detective Gumshoe was here just now.

Japanese: “初犯だし、取り調べがすんだら 出られるッス”だって。
English: He said "seeing as this is your first offense, we'll let you go after questioning."

Japanese: ‥‥よかった。
English: Whew...

Japanese: あ、それと、“保釈金を用意しとく ッス”って言ってたんだけど。
English: Oh, and he wanted me to get bail money ready.

Japanese: なるほどくん、払っといてね。
English: You can pay for me, okay?

Japanese: やれやれ。‥‥いくら？
English: Huh? How much?

Japanese: わかんない。あとで 請求書が来るんじゃないかな。
English: I don't know. I guess they'll send you a bill or something.

Japanese: （“保釈金”‥‥か。なぜか、 高そうなイメージが‥‥）
English: (Why do I picture giant bales of money every time I hear the word "bail"?)

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんは、どう‥‥？
English: Any luck with Mia...?

Japanese: そ‥‥それが‥‥ 全然、通じないの。
English: None... I can't get through to her at all.

Japanese: 必死でがんばったのに‥‥。
English: I tried... I really did!

Japanese: もう、どうしていいか わからない。
English: I don't know what to do...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... やっぱり、修行を休んでたのが マズかったのかなあ。
English: I think I probably shouldn't have stopped my training.

Japanese: （こっちはこっちで、 シンコクだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... she sounds like she really did do her best.)

Japanese: （今度、近所に滝がないか、 調べてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should check and see if there are any waterfalls in the local area...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ もう会えないのかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if I'll ever see my sister again...

Japanese: なるほどくんは、いいね。
English: It must be nice, Nick.

Japanese: それがあれば、自分が “弁護士だ”って言えるもん。
English: You can just show that to anyone and say "I'm a lawyer!"

Japanese: その点、霊媒師はねえ‥‥。
English: But, spirit mediums, well...

Japanese: そのカッコをしてれば、 わかる人には、わかるかも。
English: I dunno, with your clothes, I bet at least some people would recognize you...

Japanese: ‥‥でもこれ、修行中の霊媒師の 装束だから。
English: But these are medium-in -training clothes...

Japanese: あたし‥‥ １人前に、なれるかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if I'll ever be a proper medium...

Japanese: あ‥‥それ、ナツミさんが言ってた カクダイ写真だね。
English: Isn't that the enlarged photo Lotta was talking about?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Uh huh.

Japanese: うーん。
English: Huh...

Japanese: 大きいのはいいけど、やっぱり よく見えないねー。
English: It's nice and big... but you can't really see the faces any clearer, can you?

Japanese: あたしの‥‥おかあさん。
English: My... mother.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... （写真を見せたはいいけど、 かけるコトバが見つからない）
English: (Now that I've shown her the picture, I'm not so sure it was the right thing to do.)

Japanese: （‥‥見せるんじゃなかったな）
English: (No... I'm pretty sure it was the wrong thing to do.)

Japanese: あたしも見たいよ！　ヒョッシー。
English: I want to see Gourdy, too!

Japanese: いれば、の話だろ。
English: If he really exists.

Japanese: だって、こうして写真が あるわけでしょ。
English: Well, I'd say a photograph is pretty good proof!

Japanese: この事件が終わったら、 ツリザオ持って、湖に行こうね！
English: Once this trial is over, let's get some fishing poles and go down to the lake!

Japanese: （湖に行くのはいいけど、 “ツリザオ”ってなんだ‥‥？）
English: (Even if there is a monster in that lake, fishing poles sound like a BAD idea...)

Japanese: ‥‥この弾丸の線条痕が、凶器の ピストルと一致したんだよね。
English: So the markings on this bullet and the barrel of the pistol matched?

Japanese: お。よくおぼえてたな。
English: Hey, I'm surprised you remembered that!

Japanese: カルマ検事に、 さんざんイヤミ言われたからね。
English: How could I forget after von Karma rubbed it in so much?

Japanese: で？　どれがセンジョーコンなの？
English: So, which one of these are the ballistic markings?

Japanese: ‥‥実は、ぼくもわからない。
English: I'm... not really sure.

Japanese: そのピストル、御剣検事の指紋が ついてるんだよね。
English: Mr. Edgeworth's prints are on this pistol, aren't they?

Japanese: そう。右手の指紋がね。
English: Yes... the prints from his right hand.

Japanese: でも‥‥、撃ったときについた わけじゃ、ないみたいなんだ。
English: But they must have gotten on there at some time other than the shooting.

Japanese: え！　そうなの？
English: What? Really!?

Japanese: （犯人は、左手で撃っている からな‥‥）
English: (The shooter used his left hand, after all...)

Japanese: ごめんね。 ‥‥特に思い当たること、ないな。
English: Sorry... I'm fresh out of ideas.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あっ！　なるほどくん！ やっと来てくれたよ‥‥。
English: Hey, Nick! You finally came!

Japanese: さっき、手続きが終わったから、 もう帰っていいって。
English: They just finished the paperwork, I'm free to go.

Japanese: おつとめ、ごくろうさま。
English: Free at last, eh?

Japanese: 聞いて！　聞いて！ 取り調べの人、ヒドいんだよ！
English: Those interrogators were really mean!

Japanese: “今度は何やったの？” だって。
English: They were like "okay, what did you do THIS time?"

Japanese: あたし、何もやったこと ないのに‥‥！
English: Like I was some kind of criminal! Can you believe it?

Japanese: ま、まあまあ。 出られたんだし、いいだろ？
English: Well, they let you out in the end, didn't they?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Mmm...

Japanese: あ、そういえば‥‥、 保釈金、ありがとね。
English: Oh, that reminds me... Thanks for bail.

Japanese: お礼なら、御剣に言いなよ。
English: Thank Edgeworth.
Huh?

Japanese: あいつが払ってくれたんだよ。
English: He posted bail for you.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんに カンシャしてるってさ。
English: Said he was grateful for what you did.

Japanese: ミツルギさんが‥‥。
English: Mr. Edgeworth did that...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... よし！　がんばろうね なるほどくん！
English: I have to make it up to him!

Japanese: ゼッタイ、助けてあげるんだから！
English: We've got to win this case, Nick!

Japanese: どうしたらいいと思う？ これから。
English: What do you think we should do next?

Japanese: 手がかり、少ないもんね。
English: We're kind of lacking in the clues department.

Japanese: 公園に行って、ヒョッシーでも 探そうか。
English: We could go to the park and look for Gourdy.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... じょ、じょうだんなのに‥‥。
English: I-I was kidding!

Japanese: でも、何かあるとしたら、 あの公園しかないもんな。
English: Still, if there are any clues out there, the park's as good a bet as any.

Japanese: ‥‥行ってみようか。
English: What do you say? Shall we head down there?

Japanese: うん！
English: Sure!

Japanese: 何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Have you noticed anything lately?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 留置所にいて 思ったんだけど‥‥、
English: You know, I did notice one thing while I was here in detention.

Japanese: けっこうここ、あったかくて いいね。キレイだし。
English: It's really pretty comfortable here. It's warm, and they keep it very clean.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことを 聞きたいんじゃないんだ。
English: I meant have you noticed anything about the case...?

Japanese: まあ。 今は、そんなもんかな。
English: Well... Not much more than that, no.

Japanese: （千尋さんのこともあるし、 そっとしておくか‥‥）
English: (She's probably still upset about Mia. I should leave her alone.)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 26 Detention Cell Visitor's Room

Japanese: 御剣は今、取り調べを 受けているらしい。
English: Looks like Edgeworth is in questioning.

Japanese: また、あとで来ようよ。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: ‥‥そうするか‥‥。
English: Guess so...

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: ‥‥昨日ほどではないけど、 今日も警官のすがたが見える。
English: There are fewer than there were yesterday, but the cops are still around in the park.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事も 来ているかもしれないな‥‥。
English: I wonder if Detective Gumshoe is here today?

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: 今日は警察の人、 あんまりいないね‥‥。
English: There aren't many cops around today, are there?

Japanese: 御剣検事の証拠固めを しているんじゃないかな。
English: They're probably back at the precinct, working up the case against Edgeworth.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Mmm...

Japanese: よっ！　アンタら！
English: Hey y'all!

Japanese: あっ！　ナツミさん！
English: Hey, it's Lotta!

Japanese: アンタ。‥‥今日は やってくれたなァ。
English: Y'all really did it today...

Japanese: な、なんですか。 なんか、モンクでも‥‥
English: Wh-what did we do now?

Japanese: ギャクや。
English: Naw, I'm not complaining!

Japanese: ‥‥ウチ、ちょっと ハンセイしてるんや。
English: See, I did a little thinking. A little... self-reflection, you might say.

Japanese: 証人になるって、ホンマ、 セキニン重大なんやな‥‥。
English: I realized that bein' a witness is a mighty big responsibility.

Japanese: それやのにウチ、浮かれて イイカゲンなコト言うてもうて。
English: But I just went up there and started blabbing any old thing that came to mind.

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥。
English: Lotta...

Japanese: でな。‥‥ウチ、ツミほろぼしが したいねん。
English: So, you see, I want to make it up to y'all.

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”‥‥？
English: "Make it up"...?

Japanese: どうでした？　法廷は‥‥。
English: What did you think of the trial?

Japanese: 正直なハナシ、ウチ、 おもしろ半分やったんや。
English: To be honest, I was doin' it half just to say I'd been a witness...

Japanese: いや。ジッサイ、事件を 見たつもりでいたんやけどな。
English: Even though I didn't really see anything.

Japanese: 見てないモンまで、見たと 思い込んでいたんやなァ。
English: I kinda convinced myself I had though...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタには、 メイワクかけたな‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, I know I caused y'all a lot of trouble.

Japanese: むずかしいですよね。 ‥‥記憶って、アイマイだから。
English: Well, memory is a tricky, vague little thing.

Japanese: うん。‥‥いいベンキョウ させてもらったワ。
English: Yeah, I sure know that now.

Japanese: 今度、サツジンを目撃したときは もう、だいじょうぶや。
English: I'll be fine the next time I witness a murder!

Japanese: そうですよね！
English: Right!

Japanese: （ぼくは目撃したくないぞ‥‥）
English: (You mean the FIRST time you witness a murder...)

Japanese: ヒョッシーの方は どうするんですか？
English: What about Gourdy?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Right!

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、ヒョッシーは また、注目を集めたワケやん！
English: Well, the way I figure, the trial's only stoking the flames of Gourdy fever!

Japanese: なんとしてでも、このウチが スクープしたるねん！
English: I'll get my exclusive photos and rocket to stardom!

Japanese: わあ！ ナツミさん、カッコイイ！
English: All right, Lotta! You go, girl!

Japanese: あたしもなろうかな、 すくーぷかめらまん。
English: I wish I could be an investigative photographer too!

Japanese: （まず、霊媒師の方を なんとかしろって‥‥）
English: (Finish your spirit medium training first!)

Japanese: ナツミさん。 “ツミほろぼし”って‥‥？
English: Lotta, what do you mean by "making it up to us"?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Well, ya see...

Japanese: あのな。ウチ、実は 情報があるねん。
English: Actually, I got a bit of information for you.

Japanese: えええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: カルマのオッサンに口止め されてたんやけどナ。
English: That von Karma didn't want me to say nothin' about it.

Japanese: ど、どんな情報なんですか？
English: Wh-what information!?

Japanese: それやッ！
English: Now we're getting to the heart of it!

Japanese: そこで、アンタらと “取り引き”しよういうワケや。
English: See, I reckoned we might be able to do ourselves a little "exchange."

Japanese: と、“とりひき”‥‥？
English: E-"exchange"?

Japanese: あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”を するんでしたよね‥‥？
English: I thought this was to "make it up to us"...?

Japanese: そうやッ！
English: Right!

Japanese: “ツミほろぼし”するために “取り引き”するんや！
English: I propose a little exchange, to make it up to you!
What!?

Japanese: 情報は高いねんで！ タダなんて、マッピラや！
English: Information don't come cheap, my friend!

Japanese: ううう。
English: Uh...

Japanese: コラ、そこ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: “あら、カンサイ人てケチですね” みたいなカオして！
English: I see you thinking "my, how unsophisticated these southern folks are"!!! It's written all over yer face!

Japanese: ゆうとくけど、 カンサイ人は、ケチちゃうで！
English: Let me tell you, most southerners are WAY more sophisticated than you...

Japanese: ウチが個人的にケチなだけや！
English: I'm just the exception, okay?

Japanese: さー、どうするんや！ ウチと取り引き、するんか！
English: Well, what'll it be? We gonna deal, or not!?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうするの？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Wh-what do we do, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥すみませんが。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: ぼくは、 取り引きする気はありません！
English: But I've no intention of dealing with you!

Japanese: それに、お金もありませんし。
English: Nor do we have any money, anyway!

Japanese: アホやなァ、アンタら。
English: You guys make a fine team, you know that?

Japanese: カネがないことぐらい、 カオ見りゃわかるて。
English: And I can tell you don't have any money, just look at those clothes!

Japanese: （他に手がかりがないんだ、 しかたないな‥‥）
English: (We don't have any other leads so I don't think we have a choice here...)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥いくらですか？
English: Okay. How much?
Huh?

Japanese: ア、アホ言うたらアカン。
English: You completely off your rocker?

Japanese: いくらウチでも、カネは とらへん。
English: I may not be sophisticated, but I'm not trying to rob the poor!
Huh?

Japanese: “情報”と取り引きするには、 やっぱり“情報”や。
English: The only fair exchange for information is... information!

Japanese: ええか。
English: Listen good--

Japanese: アンタらが、ウチにくれるのは、 “ヒョッシー”に関する情報や。
English: What I need from you is information about Gourdy!

Japanese: ひょひょひょ、 “ひょっしー”ですか！
English: Whoa-whoa-whoa! G-Gourdy!?

Japanese: ‥‥そんな！ いないかもしれないのに！
English: B-but Gourdy doesn't... I mean, Gourdy might not exist!

Japanese: いないなら、いないという証拠を 持って来ィ！
English: Then bring me proof that shows he don't!
Uh...

Japanese: ほな、ウチはクルマの中で 張り込みしてるから。
English: I'll be keepin' watch from the car, okay?

Japanese: 何か見つけて、ゼッタイ 来るんやで。ええな！
English: You see something, y'all come to me first, got it?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: O-okay...

Japanese: ん。‥‥ほな！
English: Right! See y'all later!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... よし！　なるほどくん！ 探すよ！
English: Okay, Nick, let's get hunting!

Japanese: さ、探すって、まさか‥‥。
English: H-hunting...? You don't seriously mean...

Japanese: ヒョッシーに決まってるでしょ！
English: Gourdy? I sure do!

Japanese: み、御剣はどうするんだよ‥‥。
English: What about Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 御剣さんのために、 ヒョッシーを探すの！
English: We're searching for Gourdy for him, Nick! Don't you get it!?

Japanese: （探すっていっても‥‥ どうすればいいんだ‥‥？）
English: (Okay... and how exactly do we search for a make- believe monster...?)

Japanese: （探しものの専門家に 意見を聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe we can find a monster myth specialist?)

Japanese: もう少し、マイクの感度を 上げよう思ォてんねん。
English: I'm thinking I should up the sensitivity of my camera mic.

Japanese: だから、あのへんウロつくときは、 クシャミしたらアカンで！
English: Y'all just be sure y'all don't sneeze or nothing when you're in my part of the woods!

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: Right...

Japanese: やっぱ、拡大しといて 正解やったなァ。
English: I'm glad I did that enlargement.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。
English: No kidding.

Japanese: その写真がなかったら、 有罪になっていましたからね。
English: If we didn't have that, Edgeworth would be in prison now, or worse!

Japanese: なるほどくん、毎回 ドラマチックだからねー。
English: There's never a lack of drama when you're on a case, is there, Nick?

Japanese: アンタ、毎回そんな ガケップチでやってるん？
English: What, are you always on the verge of horrible failure like this!?

Japanese: はあ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥早死にするで。 気ィつけな。
English: Careful or the stress'll send you to an early grave.

Japanese: （よけいなおせわだよ！）
English: (Thanks for the cheerful message of support!)

Japanese: あの。あの。 前から思ってたんですけど。
English: Umm... uh... You know, I was wondering...

Japanese: なんや？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: こんな写真が出ちゃったら、
English: What if you get a picture of Gourdy...

Japanese: 今さら“すくーぷ”には ならないんじゃあ‥‥？
English: and it turns out like this enlargement did?

Japanese: アホタレ！
English: Y'all crazy!?

Japanese: こーんなボヤけた写真、 ウチは撮らへん！
English: A blurry picture like this doesn't make a hit story!

Japanese: ダレが見ても“ヒョッシーやん！” て叫ぶような写真！
English: I need a picture that screams "I am Gourdy! Hear me roar!"

Japanese: それがスクープなんや！
English: I can't turn something like this into the paper! What kind of a fool do y'all think I am?

Japanese: （誰が撃ったかわからないような 証拠写真を撮っておいて）
English: (The kind of fool that would turn a blurry picture in as evidence in a murder trial?)

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (Not to mention claiming she saw something she didn't see!)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: ‥‥今日は矢張のヤツ、 来てないな‥‥。
English: Haven't seen Larry around today at all.

Japanese: カズミさんと、カネのかかる デートでもしているのかな？
English: Probably off paying through the nose on a date with the lovely Kiyance...

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: な‥‥なんだ、ありゃあ‥‥。
English: Wh-what's that?

Japanese: ト‥‥トノサマン、だよね。
English: Th-the Steel Samurai, Nick.

Japanese: よっ！　真宵ちゃん。
English: Yo! Maya!

Japanese: おい、矢張。 ‥‥なんだよアレ！
English: Larry! What the heck is this!?

Japanese: いやー、カズミだよ、カズミ。 オレのカノジョ。
English: Oh, it was my girl Kiyance's idea...

Japanese: “これ置くとォ、インパクト？ あるしィ、売り上げが‥‥”って、
English: She was all "if you like, put this here, it would be, like, really cool!"

Japanese: ノボリといっしょに プレゼントしてくれたんだよ！
English: Dude, she gave it to me along with the banner!

Japanese: すごーい！
English: Wow!

Japanese: カズミさんて、なんでも できるんですねー！
English: That's real impressive she could find those for you!

Japanese: カノジョ、カオが 広いからさ。
English: Well, she knows a lot of people.

Japanese: あの番組、終わったらしくて。 タダでもらってきたんだとさ。
English: And that show's finished now, so she got 'em for free.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Right...

Japanese: ‥‥あのね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick...
Hmm?

Japanese: なんか、さっきから ミサイルのようすがヘンなの。
English: Missile's been acting strangely...

Japanese: “みさいる”？　‥‥ああ、あの たよりなさそうな小動物か。
English: "Missile"? Oh, oh right. That little... creature of yours.

Japanese: お。‥‥オレ、子犬って ダイスキなんだよな。
English: Hey, I love lil' doggies!

Japanese: よしよし‥‥。
English: Good boy, good boy.

Japanese: ぐるるるるるるるるるるるる‥‥
English: Grrraaaaaaarh!

Japanese: ど、どうしたの、ミサイル？
English: Wh-what's wrong, Missile?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... わわわわわわわわわわわわわわわわ わわわわわわわわわわわわわわん！
English: Yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip yip!

Japanese: きゃあああっ！
English: Eeeeek!

Japanese: ミサイル！　みさいるぅぅ！
English: Missile! Missssssssssile!

Japanese: わぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわ ぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐ
English: Munch munch munch munch munch munch munch munch!

Japanese: わぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわ ぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐわぐ
English: Munch munch munch munch munch munch munch munch!

Japanese: うわわわっ！　やめろ！　やめろ！ オレのトノサマンジュウがああっ！
English: Wh-whoa! S-stop that thing! C-cannibal! It's eating my Samurai Dogs!
My Samurai Dogs... *sniff*

Japanese: スゴい‥‥。 ぜーんぶ食べちゃった‥‥。
English: Wow. He ate every single one.

Japanese: ゴメンな、矢張。
English: I'm sorry, Larry...

Japanese: ゴメンですむかッ！
English: "Sorry"!? "Sorry" don't pay my bills, Nick!

Japanese: ははは‥‥。 （ゴメンですますけどな‥‥）
English: Ah hah hah... (It's going to have to this time...)

Japanese: おい、成歩堂！
English: Yo, Nick!

Japanese: 御剣はどうなったんだよ！
English: What happened with Edgeworth?

Japanese: 今日の法廷は、なんとか 逃げ切ったけど‥‥、
English: Well, we made it through the first day in court all right...

Japanese: まだ、なんとも言えないな‥‥。
English: I don't know how good our prospects are from here on, though.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。
English: Huh.

Japanese: ねえ、ねえ、知ってました？
English: Hey, Larry, did you know Edgeworth's secret weakness?

Japanese: 御剣検事って、地震が ニガテなんですよ！
English: He's terrified of earthquakes!

Japanese: コドモみたいですよねー。
English: He acts like a little boy!
Huh?

Japanese: おかしいな‥‥。
English: That's weird.

Japanese: ムカシは、そんなこと なかったけどなあ。
English: I don't think he was ever like that in school.

Japanese: あれ‥‥そうなんですか？
English: No? Really?

Japanese: まあ‥‥アイツとのつきあい、 １年ぐらいだからな。
English: Well, we were only in the same class for a year.

Japanese: すぐに、転校して いっちまったから‥‥。
English: He transferred schools pretty quickly...

Japanese: 転校‥‥。
English: Transferred?

Japanese: （“ＤＬ６号事件”が 起こったからだ‥‥）
English: (Right... when the DL-6 Incident happened.)

Japanese: （矢張も、あの事件のことは 知らないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Doesn't look like Larry knows about it, though.)

Japanese: おい、矢張。
English: Hey, Larry.

Japanese: あの目ざわりなシロモノは、 いつから‥‥？
English: What was that big... thing up there before?

Japanese: ああ、あのデカいヤツか？
English: Huh? Oh, the big guy?

Japanese: そうだな。もう１ヶ月ぐらいに なるかな‥‥。
English: I've had that for about a month, yeah.

Japanese: 子供たちには、けっこう ウケがいいんだぜ。
English: It's a big hit with the kids!

Japanese: じゃあ、なんで昨日は なかったんだ？
English: Why wasn't it there yesterday?

Japanese: え！　ええ？　いや、その。
English: Huh? Huh!? Oh... right.

Japanese: コ‥‥コンプレッサーが こわれちまってサ。
English: Th-the compressor was busted.

Japanese: こんぷれっさー？
English: Compressor?

Japanese: ホレ、屋台の横にあるだろ？ 小さなキカイが。
English: Yeah--it's that little unit by my hotdog stand.

Japanese: あれを使って 空気を入れるんだけどサ。
English: That what I use to put air in the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: ちょっと前にコワれちまって。 修理に出してたんだよ。
English: It broke a little while ago so I sent it in for repairs.

Japanese: なんだ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: ヤッパリさんが ふくらませたんじゃないんだ。
English: And here I thought you'd inflated it by yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: トノサマンをくっつけたボンベが 飛んだのはわかったんですけど、
English: So, the tank and the Steel Samurai you were trying to fill up flew away...

Japanese: ‥‥それから、 どうなったんですか？
English: What happened next?

Japanese: アレが起こったのが、 ２０日ゴロでさ。
English: Well, all that happened on the 20th or so.

Japanese: （２０日‥‥。 今から、約１週間前か‥‥）
English: (The 20th... a week ago.)

Japanese: どうもボンベのヤツ、湖の方へ 飛んでいったみたいだから、
English: Now, as far as I could see, the tank went flying out into the lake.

Japanese: それから毎晩、ボートで 必死に探したんだよ。
English: So I went out every night in a boat looking for it.

Japanese: なんたって、カズミがくれた 愛のトノサマンだからさァ。
English: I mean, Kiyance gave me that Steel Samurai after all!

Japanese: で、いつ見つけたんですか？
English: And when did you find it?

Japanese: ああ。やっと、おとといの夜さ。
English: Just the night before last!

Japanese: スゲェとおくまで飛んでたよ。 探すのに４日もかかっちまった。
English: It flew way out there! Took me four whole days to find it.

Japanese: （おとといの夜‥‥って、 事件があった夜じゃないか！）
English: (The night before last... was the night of the murder!)

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメンな、成歩堂。
English: Sorry for not telling you, Nick.

Japanese: 事件の夜、じつはオレ、ここに いたんだよ。
English: Actually, I was here on the night of the murder.

Japanese: でもな。オレ、１２時より前に 帰っちまったから‥‥。
English: But, you see, I went home before midnight.

Japanese: 湖で事件があったことは 知らないんですか‥‥。
English: So you didn't know about what happened?

Japanese: まあね。
English: No...

Japanese: うーん‥‥ザンネン。
English: That's too bad...

Japanese: でもさ。
English: It's not all bad.

Japanese: どうやら、ヤッカイなナゾが１つ、 カイケツしたみたいだな‥‥。
English: We've solved one mystery at least.

Japanese: ヤッカイなナゾ‥‥？
English: A mystery...?

Japanese: （報告しに行くか‥‥彼女に）
English: (Maybe we should go tell her...)

Japanese: ヤッパリさん。 ホラ、ミサイル。
English: Hey Larry! Look, it's Missile!

Japanese: カワイイでしょ。
English: Isn't he cute?

Japanese: そ、そのイヌを近づけるな！
English: K-keep that mutt away from me!

Japanese: 親方に、なんて 言えばいいんだよ‥‥
English: What am I going to tell the big boss?

Japanese: 今どき、マンジュウ屋で “親方”っていうのも、
English: There's a "big boss" in charge of your hot dog stand?

Japanese: フゼイがあっていいねえ、 なるほどくん。
English: Nick! Maybe the stand is a front for a mafia money laundering scheme!!!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ちょっとは“すみません”ぽい カオをした方がいいよ。
English: I think you should probably try to look a little sorry about what happened.

Japanese: はあい！
English: Oh, right!

Japanese: ‥‥とほほ。 オレのマンジュウよぉ‥‥。
English: Heh... My poor dogs...

Japanese: コイツのおかげで、 マンジュウも売れてるよ。
English: That's the guy that's selling my dogs faster than I can cook 'em!

Japanese: ヒョッシー、ホントに いるのかなあ。
English: Do you think Gourdy really exists?

Japanese: たぶん、何かの見間違いだと 思うけどサ。
English: Nah, I think somebody probably saw something else that they just thought was Gourdy.

Japanese: その正体がわかるまで、 マンジュウを売りまくるぞ！
English: But I'll keep selling Samurai Dogs until the truth's out!

Japanese: ‥‥この湖、魚は ほとんど釣れないぜ。
English: You know, you can't catch many fish in this lake.

Japanese: ザコに用はないんだよ。
English: I'm not after small fry.

Japanese: あたしのエモノは、ズバリ “ヒョッシー”だからね！
English: I'm after the biggest fry of them all... Gourdy!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥ ... ホンキで釣る つもりなのか‥‥？
English: You really going to try to fish out a monster?

Japanese: 御剣さんを助けるためだよ！
English: To save Edgeworth, yes!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... 泣かせるなあ。 ‥‥いろんなイミで。
English: Brings a tear to my eye, in more ways than one.

Japanese: なんだよ。 ‥‥ボンベ、か‥‥？
English: What? An air tank?

Japanese: それがどうかしたか？
English: What about it?

Japanese: 矢張。 このボンベだけど‥‥、
English: Larry... I wanted to ask you about this tank...

Japanese: 見おぼえがないか？ ‥‥このボンベ。
English: Have you seen this before?

Japanese: え。‥‥いや。 知らねェぞオレは。
English: Huh? Me? N-no. Never.

Japanese: ‥‥このサムいのに、 ダイビングなんかしないって。
English: Who the heck would go diving in the middle of the winter!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、目がオヨいでるぞ）
English: (There's something about the way his eye twitched when I showed him the tank...)

Japanese: （もっと追求してみた方が よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I think I'd better pursue this line of questioning a little further...)

Japanese: このボンベ‥‥ お前のじゃないか？
English: Say... is this air tank yours?

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでだよ！
English: Wh-why would I have a thing like that?

Japanese: ホラ、ボンベの栓のところに、 万国旗がからまっているだろ。
English: Look--see how there's a string of flags around the tank valve?

Japanese: うしろのトノサマンの万国旗と 似ているからさ。
English: It's just like the string of flags around your Steel Samurai there.
M-must be a coincidence!

Japanese: 万国旗なんて、どこにでも あるからな‥‥
English: There're strings of flags everywhere these days!

Japanese: ホレ。小学校とか‥‥中学校とか。
English: L-like elementary schools! A-and used car dealerships!

Japanese: だいいち、ボンベなんて、 オレが何に使うって言うんだよ！
English: L-look, why would I need a tank anyway!?

Japanese: お前、これを使って 湖にもぐったんじゃないか？
English: You used this to go diving in the lake, didn't you?

Japanese: な、な、な、なんだって！
English: Wh-wh-what!?

Japanese: なんでオレがそんなこと しなくちゃならねェんだよ！
English: Why the heck would I go diving in the lake!?

Japanese: あッ！
English: Wait!

Japanese: まさかお前‥‥、
English: You... you're not...

Japanese: オレが何か、あの殺人事件と カンケイがあるとでも‥‥！
English: You're not saying I had something to do with that murder!?

Japanese: い、いや、 そういうワケじゃあ‥‥。
English: N-no, not at all, just...

Japanese: ‥‥そうは行かねぇぞ！ べ、弁護士を呼んでやる！
English: Hey, you watch it buddy! I'm not saying anything until my lawyer gets here!

Japanese: なるほどくん。弁護士だって！
English: Larry... Nick is your lawyer.

Japanese: なんだ？　矢張。
English: Well, Larry? She's right.

Japanese: フザけんなッ！
English: Bah!

Japanese: と、とにかく、そんなボンベ 知らねェや！
English: A-anyway, I never seen that air tank! Okay?

Japanese: （‥‥ホントかぁ‥‥？）
English: (Yeah, right...)

Japanese: （このタイド、ゼッタイ 何かありそうだぞ‥‥）
English: (Larry is hiding something, I can tell...)

Japanese: お前、これを使って ふくらませたんじゃないのか？
English: You used this to inflate that, didn't you?

Japanese: な‥‥何をだよ！
English: I-inflate what!?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ？
English: What else?

Japanese: うしろでイバってる、 あのトノサマンだよ。
English: That big puffy Steel Samurai!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！！ !!! ... そ、そんなことを聞いて、 どうしようってんだよ。
English: N-now why would you go asking me a question like that.

Japanese: （どうやら、図星らしいな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I hit the nail on the head.)
Right... right...

Japanese: 実はさァ。
English: Actually, umm...

Japanese: いつも使ってるコンプレッサーが コワれちまってよ。
English: See, the compressor I always use was on the fritz.

Japanese: １回、ためしに使って みたんだけど‥‥
English: So I tried using the tank to inflate it, just once.

Japanese: ダメ。うまくいかなかったよ。
English: And, er, it didn't go so well.

Japanese: （やっぱりそうだったか‥‥）
English: (As I suspected!)

Japanese: （まあ、ボンベのことは こんなところでじゅうぶんかな）
English: (I guess I've gotten to the bottom of that mystery!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。やれやれ。
English: Heh. Sorry!

Japanese: “うまくいかなかった”‥‥？
English: It "didn't go so well"?

Japanese: ま、まあな。
English: Er, yeah.

Japanese: その話、もっとくわしく 聞かせてくれないか？
English: Do you think you could be a little more specific?

Japanese: な、なんでよ？
English: C-c'mon...

Japanese: じつはこの話、あんまり したくないんだよなァ‥‥。
English: Look, it's embarrassing so I really don't want to talk about it...

Japanese: えー。聞きたい聞きたい！
English: Tell us! Tell us!
Fine.

Japanese: いやナ。さっきも言ったけど、 コンプレッサーがコワれたからさ、
English: Whatever. It's like what I said, the compressor was busted.

Japanese: ボンベの空気でふくらまそうと してみたワケよ。
English: So I took the tank and tried to fill the Samurai up with that.

Japanese: ところが！
English: And then...

Japanese: パアァァン！
English: BLAAAAM!

Japanese: すげェ音がして 栓がハジけ飛んじまってさァ！
English: The valve busted open and made this incredible noise!

Japanese: ボンベのヤツ、ロケットみたいに “しゅぽーん！”って‥‥
English: And that tank there took off like a rocket.

Japanese: しわしわのトノサマンくっつけて、 飛んでいっちまったんだよ！
English: And it took my poor deflated Steel Samurai with it!

Japanese: ええーっ！
English: What!? Off into Gourd Lake?

Japanese: そりゃあすげェ音がして、 オレもビビっちまったよ！
English: It sure scared me out of my gourd, that's for sure.

Japanese: そのボンベで、トノサマンを ふくらまそうとしてサ。
English: See, I tried to use this tank to fill up the Samurai.

Japanese: トノサマンにボンベを くっつけた、まさにそのとき！
English: I was attaching the tank, when it happened!

Japanese: パアァァン！
English: BLAAAAM!

Japanese: 栓がハジけ飛んじまってさァ！
English: The valve just busted open and the whole thing took off!

Japanese: ボンベのヤツ、ロケットみたいに “しゅぽーん！”って‥‥
English: The tank shot up in the air like a rocket...

Japanese: しわしわのトノサマンくっつけて、 飛んでいっちまったんだよ！
English: And it took my poor deflated Steel Samurai with it!

Japanese: ありゃあ、スゲェ光景だったね。
English: Man, what a sight that was.

Japanese: あたしも見たかった！
English: I wish I could have seen it!

Japanese: ぼくもだ。
English: Me too.

Japanese: 悪いな、成歩堂。
English: Sorry, Nick.

Japanese: コドクなマンジュウ売りにゃ、 なんのことかわかんねえよ。
English: I don't know much about that. I am a mere seller of hotdogs.

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 26 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: （今日もボート屋は 休みみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like the boat rental shop is closed today, too.)

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 26 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ここって、いつも しずかだねえ。
English: It's always so quiet, here.

Japanese: ボート屋さん、 やってないのかな。
English: I wonder if the boat shop is closed for good?

Japanese: 湖で殺人があった わけだから、
English: Well, with the murder on the lake and all...

Japanese: しばらく休むんじゃないかな。
English: They're probably just taking a vacation till it blows over.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I get it.

Japanese: ピー　ピー　ピー
English: *beep* *beep* *beep*

Japanese: な！　なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick!

Japanese: 反応してるよ！ き、金属探知機がッ！
English: It's beeping! The metal detector's found something!

Japanese: ‥‥き、近所メイワクな 探知機だな‥‥。
English: Sure is loud enough about it.

Japanese: （どうやら、奥のシゲミに 反応しているみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, it must be in those bushes...)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。ちょっと見てきて。
English: Go check it out, Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥なんであたしが‥‥。
English: Why do I have to check it out?
Nick.

Japanese: こんなものが。
English: Look!

Japanese: ‥‥それって‥‥ ボンベ、だよね。
English: Huh...? An air tank?

Japanese: 栓の部分がこわれてるみたいだ。
