Japanese: “ムダな抵抗はよせ。 このビルは完全に包囲した！”
English: "Resistance is futile! We have you surrounded!"

Japanese: “今ならまだ、間に合う。 人質を解放して、出てくるんだ”
English: "It's not too late... Release the hostages and come out with your hands up."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 犯人説得のイメージ・トレーニング をしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for negotiating a hostage situation.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “‥‥うう‥‥オレはもう‥‥ ダメです‥‥”
English: "I... I'm not gonna make it..."

Japanese: “あの犯人‥‥必ず‥‥ 逮捕してください‥‥‥ガクッ”
English: "Just... just arrest that guy for me... please... uggh!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 殉職のイメージ・トレーニングを しているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for dying in action.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ こ、この局面で‥‥
English: Wha...!? Rook to Bishop 7...

Japanese: ‥‥飛車を取られるとは‥‥
English: Checkmate!? I don't believe it!

Japanese: （‥‥インターネットで 将棋を指していた）
English: (He must be playing chess on the computer...)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: ガラスケースの中には、 ファイルが並んでいるみたいだ。
English: It looks like there are files inside that glass case.

Japanese: ガラスに厚くホコリが かぶっているから、よく見えない。
English: The case is so dusty I can't see what's inside.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥カギもかかってるよ。
English: Nick... it's locked.

Japanese: 大事な資料がしまってある みたいだな‥‥。
English: They must keep important case files in there.

Japanese: このキャビネットには、 証拠品が保管されているようだ。
English: This cabinet is where they keep evidence for current cases.

Japanese: あきらかに凶器とわかるものから、 “なんだこりゃ”的なものまで、
English: Some of the things are obviously murder weapons. Others are... who knows what.

Japanese: ガラクタにしか見えない証拠が ひしめいている。
English: Most of it just looks like random junk.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 なあに、この洗濯バサミ？
English: Nick... what do you think this clothespin is for?

Japanese: だから、さわるなって。 ‥‥いちおう、証拠品だから。
English: Don't touch that! It's evidence...

Japanese: 事件の調書をまとめた ファイルだ。
English: Here are files of collected case reports.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、 ものすごい量だな‥‥。
English: There's quite a large volume of reports here.

Japanese: ふえー。‥‥これ、全部 事件のことが書いてあるの？
English: Wow... these are all case reports!?

Japanese: そうだよ。ここは、事件の墓場 みたいなもんだから。
English: Yeah. It's like a graveyard of police cases.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの事件も、ここで ホコリをかぶっていくんだね‥‥。
English: I guess my sister's case report is in here too... quietly gathering dust.

Japanese: 部屋の奥にも、ぎっしりファイルが 詰まった棚が並んでいる。
English: There are shelves stuffed with case files in the back of the room, too.

Japanese: 誰もなつかしむことのない、 事件の亡きがらたちだ。
English: Forgotten cases, rotting away for eternity...

Japanese: なるほどくん。早く 用事をすませちゃおうよ。
English: Nick, let's get what we need and get out of here.

Japanese: 肺の中、まっクロに なっちゃうよー！
English: All this dust is getting to me!

Japanese: １２月２６日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 26 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: む。
English: Mmm.

Japanese: ‥‥このあたりは、絶好の ポイントだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Okay, Nick, this looks like a good spot!

Japanese: ‥‥な、何が？
English: A good spot... for what?

Japanese: いよいよ、コイツの出番だね！
English: Time to do some fishing!

Japanese: （‥‥ホンキだよ‥‥）
English: (She's serious...)

Japanese: あのさ。 ‥‥エサはどうするの？
English: Umm... What are you going to use for bait?
Oh...

Japanese: “あ”じゃないだろ。
English: Yeah. "Oh."

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Mmm...

Japanese: やれやれ。‥‥こんなことなら、
English: I figured something like this would happen.

Japanese: ミサイルもいっしょに 借りてくりゃよかったな。
English: We should have brought Missile along with us, too. At least then we'd have bait.

Japanese: ‥‥！！‥‥ ...!!! な‥‥なるほどくん。 なな‥‥なんてことを‥‥
English: N-Nick...! How could you!

Japanese: じょ、冗談に決まってるだろ！
English: I'm kidding! I'm kidding!

Japanese: もう！　言っていいコトと 悪いコトがあるよ！
English: Hmph! Some jokes are better left untold.

Japanese: （‥‥パンチされた‥‥）
English: (Oof! She hit me!)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ。 見ててね！　なるほどくん。
English: Okay... Watch this, Nick!

Japanese: たのむから、空きカンと長グツ だけは引っかけるなよ。
English: Just try not to reel in any empty cans or boots, okay?

Japanese: せーの！
English: Here we go!

Japanese: きゃっ！　あ、足がぁッ‥‥！
English: Ack! M-my leg!
*whump*

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: コラァ！ なな、何してくれてんねん！
English: Hey! Wh-what are you doing!

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: Sorry, Lotta...

Japanese: アンタ、 フィルム屋の回しモノかッ！
English: Don't tell me y'all are on some film company's payroll!

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥払っといて。
English: Nick, pay her.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (Dear, dear...)

Japanese: １２月２７日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: December 27, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: これより、御剣　怜侍の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... はい。では、検察側も 準備ができている、ということで。
English: Very well, apparently the prosecution is also ready.

Japanese: （まったく、どっちがエラいんだか わからないな‥‥）
English: (Who is the judge here, anyway!?)

Japanese: では、狩魔検事。 ‥‥冒頭弁論を。
English: Mr. von Karma, your opening statement.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... はい。では、冒頭弁論は 特になし、ということで‥‥
English: Er... very well, no opening statements so...

Japanese: カッテに進めるな、裁判長！
English: Not so fast, Judge!

Japanese: ワガハイは今、意味シンな “間”をとっておったのだ！
English: I was taking a meaningful pause before speaking!

Japanese: は、はあ。 ‥‥それはどうも。
English: R-right, of course.

Japanese: 予告しよう。
English: A prediction!

Japanese: 本日の法廷は、これより ３分以内で終わるだろう！
English: Today's trial will end three minutes from now!

Japanese: せ‥‥、静粛に！
English: Order... order!

Japanese: 狩魔検事！　今の発言は、 いったいどういう‥‥
English: Mr. von Karma! What is the meaning of your statement just now!?

Japanese: キサマもいちいちうるさい男だ！
English: Bah! Must you question everything!

Japanese: ３分と言った以上、 ムダな時間はない。
English: It will be over in three minutes! We have no time to waste!

Japanese: さっさと証人を呼ばせて もらうぞ。
English: I'll call my witness now!

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: R-right...

Japanese: では、決定的な目撃者を 入廷させていただこう！
English: I call my witness... my decisive witness to the stand!

Japanese: （‥‥あの、ボート小屋の ナゾのオジさんか‥‥）
English: (It's that mysterious boat shop owner.)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥職業を。
English: Witness... state your profession.

Japanese: もが‥‥はあ。
English: Mmph... zzzz...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ひょうたん湖公園で、 ソバ屋“長寿庵”を‥‥
English: I, er, am the proprietor of the restaurant the Wet Noodle at Gourd Lake.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... あ、その。ボート、 貸したりしとりますハイ。
English: And I, er, also rent boats.

Japanese: 証人は、事件の夜は、 ボート管理小屋に‥‥？
English: The night of the incident, you were in the boat rental shop, correct?

Japanese: え‥‥はあ。 おりましたですハイ。
English: Er... ayup, yup I was.

Japanese: では、《証言》を。
English: Please testify.

Japanese: （ちょっと待てよ）
English: (Wait a second...)

Japanese: （あのオジさん、ケッキョク 誰なんだよ‥‥）
English: (We still haven't heard who this old guy is!)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、あのオジさんが 誰かなんて、どうでもいいか）
English: (On second thought, I guess it doesn't really matter.)

Japanese: よくないよ！　なるほどくん。
English: Doesn't matter!? What are you thinking, Nick!?

Japanese: あのオジさん、ＤＬ６号事件の カンケイ者かもしれないじゃない！
English: What if that old man had something to do with the DL-6 Incident?

Japanese: 誰だかわからなかったら、 きっとあとで、困るよ！
English: If we don't find out who he is, we won't be able to build a case here!

Japanese: （たしかにな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, she has a point.)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a minute!

Japanese: 証人はまだ、 名前を言っていません！
English: The witness hasn't stated his name yet!

Japanese: 聞いていなかったのか、弁護士！
English: Because I did not ask him, Mr. Wright! Bah!

Japanese: ワガハイは“３分で法廷を 終わらせる”と予告したのだ！
English: I have predicted this trial will end in three minutes!

Japanese: くだらぬ質問をせずに、 協力したまえ！
English: Stop asking trivial questions and cooperate!

Japanese: （‥‥するワケないだろ！）
English: (Yeah, right!)

Japanese: 証人。 名前をおねがいします。
English: The witness will state his name.
Zzzz...
Mmph! Well, er...

Japanese: ハッキリしないもんでハイ。
English: I'm not really sure, ayup.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: キオクが‥‥そのお‥‥。
English: My, er, memory...

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your honor...

Japanese: この証人には、数年前より 記憶がないのだ。
English: The witness does not remember anything beyond the last several years...

Japanese: したがって、 名前もわからない。
English: Ergo, he cannot recall his own name.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥キオクが‥‥。
English: Hmm... he can't recall, you say?

Japanese: しかし、事件を目撃したのは ３日前。
English: Yes, but the incident in question took place three days ago.

Japanese: 証言するのに、問題はない。
English: He can testify.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、《証言》をして いただきましょう。
English: Let's hear his testimony then, shall we. Witness?

Japanese: ～事件のあった夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: あれは２４日の夜、１２時 ちょっとすぎのことでしたハイ。
English: It was the night of the 24th, just after midnight, ayup.

Japanese: いつもどおり、ソバ屋‥‥ ボート管理小屋にいたです。
English: I was in the restaurant... where I er... rent boats, as usual.

Japanese: そしたら、パアンという音が 聞こえたですハイ。
English: Then I heard a "bang!" Ayup.

Japanese: 窓から外を見ると、ボートが１つ、 浮かんでおりまして。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw a boat just a' floating on the lake.

Japanese: そのとき、もう１発、パアンと。
English: Then I heard another "bang."

Japanese: そのうち、ボートが帰ってきて、 男が１人、窓の外をとおりました。
English: Just about then the boat comes back to shore, and a man walks by my window.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。では、 《尋問》を‥‥
English: Hmm... Very well. I'd like to begin the cross-examination...

Japanese: この証言に、モンクを つけられるところなど、ない！
English: There is nothing to question in my witness's testimony!

Japanese: したがって、《尋問》など 必要なし！
English: Ergo, no need for cross-examination!

Japanese: ‥‥というか、もう予告した ３分まで、あと１０秒しかない。
English: Besides, there are only 10 seconds left before our three minutes are up!

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥判決を！
English: Judge! Your verdict, now!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Er... yes...

Japanese: な、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥どうしますか？
English: M-Mr. Wright...?

Japanese: （たしかに、つけ入るスキは ないな‥‥）
English: (He's right. That testimony sounded pretty solid.)

Japanese: （ここは、やめておくか）
English: (Maybe I should hold back.)

Japanese: で、でも！　なるほどくん！ 《尋問》しないと！
English: B-but, Nick! You have to cross-examine him!

Japanese: 逆転できるものも、できなく なっちゃうよ！
English: This is your only chance to turn this trial around! Your last chance!

Japanese: （そ、そりゃそうだな‥‥）
English: (O-oh... right.)

Japanese: 何を言ってるんですか！ 当然、するに決まってるでしょう！
English: What are you saying!? Of course I'll cross- examine the witness!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 そうですね、わかりました。
English: Hmm... Very well, you may begin.

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Raaaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: ど、どうしました？ ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: E-excuse me? Mr. von Karma?

Japanese: ‥‥３分たってしまった。
English: Three minutes just passed.

Japanese: そうですか。 では、ゆっくり行きましょう。
English: I see. Well then, let's just take our time.

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥《尋問》を。
English: You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～事件のあった夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: あれは、２４日の夜１２時 ちょっとすぎのことでしたハイ。
English: It was the night of the 24th, just after midnight, ayup.

Japanese: いつもどおり、ソバ屋‥‥ ボート管理小屋にいたです。
English: I was in the restaurant... where I er... rent boats, as usual.

Japanese: そしたら、パアンという音が 聞こえたですハイ。
English: Then I heard a "bang!" Ayup.

Japanese: 窓から外を見ると、ボートが１つ、 浮かんでおりまして。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw a boat just a' floating on the lake.

Japanese: そのとき、もう１発、パアンと。
English: Then I heard another "bang."

Japanese: そのうち、ボートが帰ってきて、 男が１人、窓の外をとおりました。
English: Just about then the boat comes back to shore, and a man walks by my window.

Japanese: その男は被告人で“まさか人を撃つ ことになるとは”と言いました。
English: That man was the defendant... he was saying "I can't believe he's dead."

Japanese: （狩魔が呼んだ証人だ‥‥）
English: (If I know von Karma, he spent time preparing his witness.)

Japanese: （そうカンタンに、ムジュンなど していないだろう‥‥）
English: (I'm not going to find contradictions very easily in this testimony.)

Japanese: （弁護士も、なかなかツラいなあ）
English: (Whew... it's tough being a lawyer.)

Japanese: “１２時ちょっとすぎ”ですか？
English: Just after midnight, you say?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Ayup, just around then.

Japanese: 間違いないですか？
English: Are you sure?

Japanese: ないと思うがなあ。
English: Pretty sure, ayup.

Japanese: 昨日は、時間に関しては アヤフヤでしたが、
English: When I talked to you yesterday, you were rather vague about the time...

Japanese: なぜ今日は、そんなに ハッキリと‥‥？
English: I'm surprised you seem so sure about it today.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ワガハイからたのんで、 よく思い出してもらったのだ。
English: I asked him, and he remembered.

Japanese: そうだな！　証人！
English: Isn't that right!?
Zzzz...

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんなに ニラまないでください‥‥。
English: D-don't glare at me like that...!

Japanese: そ、そのお。‥‥ハッキリ 思い出したですハイ。
English: I, er... I remembered it clearly, I did. Ayup.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうことだ。 つづけてもらおう。
English: You see? Continue!

Japanese: それを証明してくれるヒトは いますか？
English: Is there anyone who can verify that?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... サユリさん、かなあ。
English: Well, I guess Polly could.

Japanese: ダ‥‥ダメですよ そんなの！
English: Th-that's not good enough for a court of law!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。“そんなの”とは なんですか！
English: Mr. Wright... exactly what's not good enough?

Japanese: サユリさんにシツレイでしょう。 い、いや。“サユリさん”って、 オウムなんですよ。
English: Ah, uh, Your Honor, this Polly is a parrot.

Japanese: オ、オウム‥‥？
English: A... parrot?

Japanese: カタいこと言うなよ、 ユキヒロ‥‥。
English: Don't be so hard on the girl, Keithy-boy!

Japanese: ユ、ユキヒロ‥‥？
English: Keith...?

Japanese: 証人が管理小屋にいたことは、 証明できない。それは認めよう。
English: The prosecution concedes that we cannot prove the witness was in the shop.

Japanese: とにかく、証人。 ‥‥先をおねがいする。
English: Witness... please continue.

Japanese: その音は、どこから‥‥？
English: And where did the "bang" seem to come from?

Japanese: 湖の方から、だったかなあ。
English: From the lake, I figure.

Japanese: 間違いないな、証人。
English: Are you certain?

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: A-ayup.

Japanese: では、つづきを。
English: Good. Continue!

Japanese: ボートには、誰が 乗っていましたか？
English: Was there someone in the boat?

Japanese: ‥‥遠かったもんでな。 ハッキリとは‥‥。
English: It was pretty far out there... I couldn't see clearly...

Japanese: たぶん、男が２人、かなあ。
English: But I figure there was two men out there, ayup.

Japanese: ハッキリ見えなかったんですね！
English: But you couldn't see them clearly!

Japanese: うん。 ‥‥そのときはな。
English: Ayup... at the time, that is.

Japanese: （そのときは‥‥？）
English: (At the time...?)

Japanese: 銃声は、合計２発 聞こえたわけですね？
English: So you heard two gunshots total?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Ayup.

Japanese: （昨日、ナツミさんも そう証言していたな‥‥）
English: (That's what Lotta said in her testimony yesterday.)

Japanese: “窓の外”というのは？
English: By your window?

Japanese: いや。そのまんまだよ。 小屋の窓の外のことだよ。
English: Ayup, by my window. Right outside the window of my little shack.

Japanese: じゃあ、その人物のカオを ハッキリ見たわけですか？
English: And, could you see the man's face?

Japanese: まあなあ。キリがいくら濃くても、 目と鼻の先だったからねえ。
English: Well, the fog was pretty darn thick, but he was right there in front of me. I saw him.

Japanese: そ、それは重要なことです。 証言につけ加えてください。
English: This is a rather important detail! Please add it to your testimony.
Tsk, tsk, tsk...

Japanese: （‥‥イヤな予感‥‥）
English: (I have a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: ま‥‥間違いありませんか！
English: A-are you sure!?
(Uh oh!)

Japanese: おとーさん！
English: D-Dad!

Japanese: 間違いないぞお、ユキヒロ！
English: Dead certain, Keith!

Japanese: “まさか撃ってしまうとは” と言ったんだよあの男は！
English: He said "I can't believe he's dead" as he was walking by, too.

Japanese: 証人！　それは御剣　怜侍 に間違いないか！
English: Witness! Are you sure that the person you saw was Miles Edgeworth!?

Japanese: そいつだあ！ ‥‥その、ミツルくんだあ！
English: It was him! That Edgeworth boy!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... これは、決定的な証言ですね。 ギモンの余地はありません！
English: This... sounds like decisive evidence indeed. I see no room for doubt.

Japanese: （狩魔のヤツ‥‥）
English: (von Karma...)

Japanese: （ワザとぼくに尋問させて、 証言にインパクトを与えたな！）
English: (He lured me into cross- examining so he could set me up for a fall!)

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk, tsk, tsk...

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん。 法廷の空気が、ヤな感じだよ‥‥。
English: N-Nick...! I don't like the way things are going here!

Japanese: みんな、あたしたちを ニラんでる‥‥！
English: Everyone in the courtroom is glaring at us!

Japanese: （ここで何か手を打たないと、 審理は終了してしまう！）
English: (I'd better act quick, or this trial is going to be over!)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし‥‥証拠もないのに 異議を申し立てるわけには‥‥）
English: (But... how can I raise an objection without any proof?)

Japanese: 裁判長！もう、疑う余地はない！
English: Judge! There is no room for doubt in the witness's testimony!

Japanese: ただちに判決を 下されるよう要求する！
English: I demand that you declare your verdict!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 御剣が発砲していないことは、 昨日の法廷で証明したはずです！
English: We proved in yesterday's court that it could not have been Edgeworth who fired that gun!

Japanese: 弁護人。 もしかしてそれは、
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ピストルについているのは 被告人の右手の指紋なのに、
English: Are you referring to the fingerprints from Edgeworth's right hand found on the gun,

Japanese: 写真で発砲しているのは 左手だから、という話か？
English: and the photograph showing a man firing with his left hand...?

Japanese: そうです！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: そんなことは、なんとでも 説明がつく！
English: That is easily explainable!

Japanese: 撃ったあと、一度指紋を ふいたのかもしれない。
English: He could have wiped his prints after he fired!

Japanese: そんなことより！
English: You are ignoring the truth of the matter here!

Japanese: 今の証言で、すべては あきらかではないか！
English: Everything in this witness's testimony is true!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （裁判長が考え込んでいる。 ‥‥どうすればいい！）
English: (The judge is lost in thought... what should I do?)

Japanese: （‥‥異議をさし挟む余地がない）
English: (I can't see any room to raise an objection...)

Japanese: （ここは、 ようすを見るしかないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better hold back and see how things develop.)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ なんとかならないの！
English: Nick! We have to do something!

Japanese: 今、何かしないと、 御剣検事、有罪になっちゃう！
English: If we stay quiet now, Mr. Edgeworth'll be found guilty for sure!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 御剣が“まさか撃つことに なるとは”と言ったかどうかは、
English: This witness claims that Edgeworth said "I can't believe he's dead"...

Japanese: その証人の証言があるだけです！
English: But his word is all we have!

Japanese: もしかしたら、 ウソをついているのかも‥‥
English: If he were telling a lie...

Japanese: 弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥法廷では、証拠が すべてを語る。
English: In a court of law, the evidence tells all.

Japanese: そんなことすら、キサマはまだ 理解していないのか。
English: Apparently, you have yet to realize even this basic fact.

Japanese: 証言がウソだと言うなら、 その証拠を提示したまえ！
English: If you say his testimony is a lie, show us proof!
Urk...

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 証拠は‥‥？
English: Nick! Do we have evidence...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ダメだ！ 今度こそ、何もない！
English: It's no good! There's nothing I can do.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうにもならないの？
English: A... are you sure?

Japanese: 正直なところ‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: もう、どうしていいか わからないんだ‥‥。
English: I don't know what to do anymore.
Please...

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥たすけてよ‥‥
English: Can you hear me, Sis? Please...

Japanese: ‥‥負けちゃうよ‥‥なるほどくん
English: We need your help... Nick needs you...

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: ３分はさすがにムリだったが‥‥
English: Three minutes was perhaps too high an expectation.

Japanese: 開廷から１５分。 これは新記録だろうな‥‥。
English: However, fifteen minutes isn't bad! This must be a new record.

Japanese: そこまで！ 証人は下がってください。
English: Enough! The witness may leave the stand.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ぐううううう‥‥‥‥
English: Zzzz...

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.
(W-what!? No!)

Japanese: フン。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 被告人、御剣　怜侍に 判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ちょっと、待てぇぇぇぇぇぇぇい！
English: W-w-waaaaaaait!

Japanese: ‥‥い‥‥ 今のは、いったい誰が‥‥？
English: Wh-who was that just now?

Japanese: オレだああああッ！
English: Me!!!
Huh?

Japanese: ああああああああああああああっ！
English: Waaaaah!

Japanese: やは、矢張ィィィィッ！
English: L-Larry!

Japanese: なな‥‥なんですか、あなたは！
English: Wh-what are you doing here!?

Japanese: オレ‥‥オレの話を 聞いてくれよ！
English: Listen! Y-you gotta listen to me!

Japanese: オレ、オレ‥‥
English: I... I was...

Japanese: 事件があった日の夜、 あの公園にいたんだよ！
English: I was there, in the park, the night of the murder!

Japanese: 昨日まで、ハッキリ しなかったんだけど‥‥
English: I... I wasn't sure about it until just yesterday.

Japanese: 今日になってオレ、 思い出したんだよ！
English: B-but today I remembered it!

Japanese: 何をですか？
English: Remembered what?

Japanese: 銃声だよ！ オレも聞いてたんだよ！
English: The gunshot! I heard it too!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！
English: O-order!

Japanese: これはなんのさわぎだッ！
English: What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 判決はすでに下された！ ワガハイは閉廷を求める！
English: The verdict has been decided! I call for adjournment!

Japanese: 待ってください。 狩魔検事。
English: One moment, Mr. von Karma.

Japanese: あなたは、 銃声を聞いたのですか？
English: So, you say you heard a gunshot?

Japanese: そうなんだよ！ 聞いたんだよ！　あの夜！
English: Yeah, I did! A gunshot! That night!

Japanese: オレ、さっきから客席で 証言を聞いてたんだけどよォ、
English: I was sitting here in the audience, listening to the testimony...

Japanese: なんか、オレのキオクと違う 部分があるんだよ！
English: Then I realized, something he said was different from what I remember!

Japanese: それなのに、 御剣がユーザイなんて、
English: A-anyhow, I can't just sit here and let you call Edgey a murderer!

Japanese: オレ、 ナットクできねェんだよ！
English: It's... It's just not right!

Japanese: オレ、証言するからさ！ 聞いてくれよ！
English: I'll testify! Lemme testify!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥こんな事態は 初めてです‥‥。
English: Well, this is the first time something has happened like this in my court.

Japanese: どうすればいいのか‥‥。
English: I'm not quite sure how to proceed...

Japanese: 裁判長！ すでに判決は下った！
English: Judge! You've already given your decision!

Japanese: 審理は終了だ！
English: The trial is over!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ヤッパリさんがくれた、 最後のチャンスだよ！
English: This is it! Larry's given us one final chance at this!

Japanese: （たしかに！）
English: (She's right!)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし、アイツが矢張だけに シンパイだ‥‥）
English: (If only it wasn't Larry...)

Japanese: （事態が、より悪化する可能性も あるからな‥‥）
English: (He could make things even worse...)

Japanese: 御剣検事、“有罪”判決 受けちゃったんだよ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth was just declared guilty, Nick!

Japanese: 状況が今より悪くなるなんてコト、 あり得ないよ！
English: It doesn't get any worse!

Japanese: そうだね‥‥わかった！
English: You're right! Okay!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 目撃証人がいる以上、ぼくたちは 話を聞くべきです！
English: If there is another witness, it is our duty to hear him speak!

Japanese: 今すぐ、この場で！
English: Right here! Right now!

Japanese: 必要なし！
English: A waste of time!

Japanese: 判決をくつがえすことなど できない！
English: The verdict cannot be overturned!
Hmm...
Allow me to speak my opinion.

Japanese: 裁判では、いかなる場合でも、 あやまった判決はさけるべきです。
English: In all court proceedings, it is our duty to prevent an inaccurate verdict.

Japanese: そのためには、目撃証人の話は すべて、聞かなければならない！
English: In order to make sure no mistake has been made, every witness should be heard!

Japanese: な‥‥何を言うッ！
English: Wh-what is this!?

Japanese: 先ほどの有罪判決は、撤回します！
English: I withdraw my previous verdict of "guilty"!

Japanese: 狩魔検事！
English: Mr. von Karma!

Japanese: この証人に、証言をもとめる ことを要請します。
English: I order you to call this new witness to testify.

Japanese: 今すぐに！
English: Now!

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: Whaaaat!

Japanese: これより、５分間の 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: The court will adjourn for a five minute recess.

Japanese: その後、この証人の話を 聞くのです！
English: After that, we will hear this new witness.

Japanese: では、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２７日　午前１０時２８分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 27, 10:28 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2
Whew...

Japanese: あぶなかった‥‥。
English: That was too close.

Japanese: すまないな、御剣。 ハラハラさせて。
English: Sorry to keep you on the edge of your seat like that, Edgeworth.

Japanese: フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: これぐらいのシュラ場、 なんてことはないさ。
English: I've seen worse.

Japanese: （‥‥アブラ汗びっしょりかいて、 何か言ってるよ‥‥）
English: (Yeah right, Edgeworth. You're sweating bullets!)

Japanese: それにしても、矢張のヤツ‥‥ 何を言うつもりなんだろう。
English: I just wonder what Larry plans to say in there.

Japanese: ヤッパリさん、あの夜 公園にいたんだっけ？
English: Larry was at the lake that night?

Japanese: たしか‥‥、
English: Yes...

Japanese: 湖に飛んでいった、トノサマンの 人形を探していた、とか‥‥。
English: He said he went looking for the Steel Samurai balloon that flew into the lake.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そういえば、
English: Oh right.

Japanese: あの夜、見つけたんだっけ。 トノサマンの人形とボンベ。
English: And he found the balloon and the air tank that night?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yeah.
Hey, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ム？　な、なんだ成歩堂？
English: Huh? You say something, Wright?

Japanese: なんだじゃないよ。
English: Yeah, a lot of things.

Japanese: どうしたんだ？ ボケーッとして。
English: You seem out of it. What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥い、いや‥‥。 ちょっと、な‥‥。
English: It... it's nothing.
Hmm?

Japanese: あの。あの。御剣検事？ 前から聞きたかったんですけど、
English: Umm... Mr. Edgeworth? There's something I've been meaning to ask you.

Japanese: なんだろうか？
English: What's that?

Japanese: どうして凶器に、検事さんの 指紋がついているんですか？
English: Why are your fingerprints on the murder weapon?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: 相手が湖水に落ちたとき、 私はボーゼンとなった。
English: When he fell into the lake, I went into a daze.

Japanese: 何が起こったか、 わからなかったのだ。
English: I couldn't understand what had happened. I couldn't think straight.

Japanese: そのとき、目の前にピストルが 落ちていた。
English: Then I saw the pistol lying on the floor of the boat in front of me.

Japanese: ‥‥つい、考えなしに 拾ってしまったのだ‥‥。
English: I picked it up without thinking.

Japanese: 特に理由はない。
English: I didn't have a reason, really.

Japanese: なるほどねえ。
English: I see...
Wright...
Yeah?

Japanese: これは、チャンスかもしれない。
English: This might be our chance.

Japanese: ‥‥チャンス？
English: Our chance?

Japanese: 狩魔検事はこれまで、完全な裁判 しか経験したことがない。
English: von Karma has only ever run perfect trials.

Japanese: “完全な裁判”‥‥
English: Perfect trials...?

Japanese: 完全に手なずけた証人に、 完全にそろえた証拠品。
English: Perfectly prepared witnesses, perfectly complete evidence.

Japanese: 彼が、決して負けない理由は そこにあるのだ。
English: That's the secret to his success.

Japanese: しかし。‥‥今回、初めて それがくずされた。
English: This is the first time he's ever had to deal with something unexpected!

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、一度も話したことの ない証人に証言させることになる。
English: He has let someone he hasn't even talked to testify before the court!

Japanese: しかも証人は、あの矢張だ。
English: And that someone... is Larry!

Japanese: ‥‥ということは‥‥？
English: What are you getting at?

Japanese: スキだらけの証言になる 可能性が高い‥‥。
English: It's likely his testimony will be full of holes, Wright.

Japanese: やったね！　なるほどくん！
English: That's right, Nick!

Japanese: これで、１０分でやられちゃう なんてこともないね！
English: No ten minute trial this time! We'll milk this one for all it's worth!

Japanese: ‥‥うるさいよ。 （すべては矢張しだい、か‥‥）
English: Hey, it was fifteen minutes! Fifteen! (Everything depends on Larry now...)

Japanese: １２月２７日　午前１０時３５分 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: December 27, 10:35 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: では、審理を再開します。
English: Court is now back in session.

Japanese: 証人。
English: Witness...

Japanese: 事件のあった１２月２４日の夜、 あなたが見たことを、
English: Please testify to the court about everything that you saw...

Japanese: すべて《証言》してください。
English: on the night of December 24th.

Japanese: ま‥‥まかせてくれヨ！
English: Right... leave it to me!

Japanese: （たのむぞ‥‥矢張）
English: (Please, Larry, don't mess this one up!!!)

Japanese: （認めたくないが、お前が 最後のチャンスなんだ‥‥）
English: (I hate to admit it, but you're our last chance!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （狩魔も、証人を手なずける ヒマはなかった）
English: (von Karma didn't even have time to prep his witness.)

Japanese: （御剣の言ったとおり、スキが 生まれればいいんだけど‥‥）
English: (I just hope Edgeworth is right about this being our big break...)

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: オレ、あの夜、湖でボートに 乗っていたんだよ。
English: That night, I was out in a boat on the lake.

Japanese: 探しモノがあって。 で、それが見つかってさあ。
English: I was looking for something, and I, er, found it.

Japanese: ボートを、こっそりボート屋に 返したワケよ。
English: So I quietly slipped the boat back in at the rental shop dock.

Japanese: で、帰ろうかなと思ったときに、 パアンて音が聞こえたんだ。
English: Then, just as I was thinking about going home, I heard this "bang"!

Japanese: オレが湖を見たときは、 ボートには気がつかなかったぜ。
English: I looked out over the lake, but I didn't notice the boat.

Japanese: その１発の音を聞いたあとは 何もなかったんで、帰ったんだ。
English: So after I heard that single gunshot I went home.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、イロイロ問題が ありそうな証言でしたね。
English: That was an unusually vague testimony, even for this court.

Japanese: とりあえず、弁護人。 《尋問》をおねがいします。
English: In any matter, Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross- examination.

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What's wrong, Nick?

Japanese: 矢張だけに、何が出てくるか わからなくて。
English: It's Larry! I have no idea what he's going to say if I press him.

Japanese: ちょっと、コワい。
English: I'm a little scared.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: でも、ここまできたら やってみるしかないって！
English: Well, we've come this far. There's no way to go but forward, Nick!

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- The Night of the Murder --

Japanese: オレ、あの夜、湖でボートに 乗っていたんだよ。
English: That night, I was out in a boat on the lake.

Japanese: 探しモノがあって。 で、それが見つかってさあ。
English: I was looking for something, and I, er, found it.

Japanese: ボートを、こっそりボート屋に 返したワケよ。
English: So I quietly slipped the boat back in at the rental shop dock.

Japanese: で、帰ろうかなと思ったときに、 パアンて音が聞こえたんだ。
English: Then, just as I was thinking about going home, I heard this "bang"!

Japanese: オレが湖を見たときは、 ボートには気がつかなかったぜ。
English: I looked out over the lake, but I didn't notice the boat.

Japanese: その１発の音を聞いたあとは 何もなかったんで、帰ったんだ。
English: So after I heard that single gunshot I went home.

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。
English: Well, Nick?

Japanese: そうだなあ‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、キモチワルイ 証言だなあ‥‥。
English: It was a pretty wishy-washy testimony, wasn't it?

Japanese: とにかく、ムジュン点を たたきつけてみるよ。
English: I guess I should just start working on the contradictions.

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんね。
English: Sorry...

Japanese: あたしが、 お姉ちゃんさえ呼べれば‥‥。
English: I wish I could be more helpful. I wish I could call my sister...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... どうしましたか？　弁護人？
English: Something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ツッコミどころが多くて、 どこから始めようかと‥‥。
English: There were so many things wrong I don't know where to begin...

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Ah...

Japanese: とりあえず、それは 何時のことでした？
English: Um, well, okay. First of all, what time was it?

Japanese: 湖にボートを出したのは、 １１時すぎだったぜ。
English: Oh, it was after 11:00 when I went out in the boat.

Japanese: あの公園、１１時をすぎると 人がいなくなるからさあ。
English: By that time everyone had gone home for the night.

Japanese: その時間になるまで 待ってたんだ。
English: So I waited until the coast was clear, so to speak.

Japanese: なぜ、そんな時間にボートを？
English: And why were you out on a boat at such a late hour...?

Japanese: “探しモノ”‥‥？
English: Looking for something...?

Japanese: ま、まあな。
English: Er, yeah.

Japanese: 証人。 何を探していたんですか？
English: Mr. Butz, what was it you were looking for?

Japanese: 証人が何を探そうが、 事件にはカンケイがない！
English: What the witness was searching for is irrelevant!

Japanese: おおかた、ヒョッシーでも 探していたのだろう！
English: Most likely he was hunting for this "Gourdy"!

Japanese: （‥‥実は、そのとおり だったりするんだよな‥‥）
English: (You know, it wouldn't surprise me one bit if that was the truth.)

Japanese: そんなことはどうでもいい。 ‥‥早く終わらせてもらいたい！
English: This is all irrelevant! Let's get it over with.

Japanese: それは、何時ごろのことですか？
English: Around what time was that?

Japanese: ‥‥うーん。 それなんだよなあ。
English: Uh... Well, let's see...

Japanese: 探し回ってたのは１時間 ぐらいだから‥‥、
English: I figure I was out searching for about an hour...

Japanese: たぶん、１２時ごろだと 思うけど‥‥。
English: I guess it was around 12:00. Yeah.

Japanese: ハッキリしないわけですか？
English: You're not sure?

Japanese: なんだよ！　そのカオは！
English: Hey! Don't give me that face!

Japanese: オレ、明石の天文台じゃ ねェんだぞお！
English: I'm not some sort of human sundial, okay!?

Japanese: （‥‥進歩のないヤツ‥‥）
English: (People use watches these days, Larry...)

Japanese: その音は、どこから？
English: Where did the sound come from?

Japanese: それがさァ、 わからなかったんだよなあ。
English: Yeah, well, I wasn't too sure about that.

Japanese: あたりを見回したんだけど‥‥。
English: I looked around, y'know.

Japanese: 湖の方も見ましたか？
English: Did you look at the lake?

Japanese: ああ、見たぜ。
English: Yeah, I looked.

Japanese: 湖の上に、ボートは なかったんですか！
English: Wasn't there a boat on the lake!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: そうなのですか！ 証人！
English: Well? Mr. Butz!

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ。
English: Whoa, whoa.

Japanese: そんなに盛り上がられると コマるなあ。
English: Everybody just calm down, okay?

Japanese: キリが濃かったし‥‥、
English: I mean, it was real foggy that night.

Japanese: ボートがあったかどうか、 ハッキリわかんねえや。
English: I'm not sure whether there was a boat out there or not.

Japanese: （とほほ。‥‥大事な ことなのに‥‥）
English: (Oh, okay, no problem. That's just the MOST IMPORTANT PART of this case!!!)

Japanese: フン！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: じゃあ結局、パアンという音を １発、聞いただけ‥‥？
English: So, you only heard one "bang" correct?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yeah.
(Huh.)

Japanese: 湖に、ボートはなかった‥‥
English: Are you sure there wasn't a boat on the lake?

Japanese: しかし、この写真を 見てください！
English: Look at this picture, Larry!

Japanese: 銃声があったとき、 ボートはあったんですよ！
English: There was a boat on the lake when the gun fired!

Japanese: そのとおりですね。 ‥‥いかがですか？　証人‥‥。
English: It's true. Well, Mr. Butz?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥。 そんなこと言われてもなあ。
English: W-well, that's great you got a photo and all, but...

Japanese: オレ、そんなに注意して 湖を見ていたワケじゃないし、
English: I wasn't really paying that much attention when I looked out over the lake, okay?

Japanese: ボートが“なかった”とは 断言してねぇぜ。
English: I'm not saying there wasn't a boat, just I didn't see one.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Correct!

Japanese: 弁護人の指摘は、 ムジュン点とは言えない！
English: The defense has pointed out a vague statement, not a contradiction!
Hmm...

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥証言に もどってください。
English: Mr. Butz, you may continue your testimony.

Japanese: （くそ！　シッパイか‥‥）
English: (Dammit! I thought I was on to something there!)

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥別のムジュンを 探してみようよ。
English: It's okay Nick, we'll find another contradiction...

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。
English: Yeah, you're right.

Japanese: ちょっと待てよ矢張！
English: W-wait a sec, Larry!

Japanese: な、なんだよ。
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: お前、“パアン”ていう音、 １発しか聞いていないのか？
English: You only heard one bang!? You're sure!?

Japanese: そう言ったろ？
English: That's what I said!

Japanese: でも、昨日の証人の 大沢木　ナツミさんも、
English: But Ms. Lotta Hart testified yesterday that she heard two "bang"s!

Japanese: さっき証言したオジさんも、
English: And the old man just now said the same thing!

Japanese: “パアン”という音を ２回、聞いているんだぞ！
English: They both heard two gunshots that night!

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: お前、ほんとに ちゃんと聞いてたのかよ！
English: Were you even listening!?

Japanese: 耳そうじ、ちゃんとしてるか？
English: Were you paying attention at all to what they said?

Japanese: おい！　成歩堂！
English: Yo, Nick, please!
Huh?

Japanese: さっきから、ちょっと 気になってたんだけどよお。
English: You know, something's been bothering me.

Japanese: オレ、証人だぜ証人。 お客さんよ、いわば。
English: I'm a witness, see? I'm like a customer here!

Japanese: その、カジュアルな口の きき方、やめろよな！
English: So you got to treat me nice and stuff, okay!?
Mr. Butz.

Japanese: なんだよ。
English: What!?

Japanese: ホントに、１回しか 音を聞かなかったんですか？
English: You only heard one gunshot? Are you sure?
Umm...

Japanese: 実はさァ。 ‥‥ハッキリしないんだよね。
English: Well, to tell ya the truth... I'm not sure.

Japanese: ハッキリしない？ ど、どういうことだ？
English: Not sure!? H-how could you not be sure?

Japanese: いや、オレさ。
English: Yeah, well...

Japanese: もしかしたら、１発、 聞き逃していたカモ。
English: I, uh, I might have missed the other gunshot.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえばよォ、オレ。 アレ、聞いてたから。
English: I was, uh, listening to something else...

Japanese: “アレ”‥‥って？
English: Something... else?

Japanese: ラジオだよラジオ。 ‥‥ヘッドフォンでさ。
English: My radio, dude! On my headphones.

Japanese: ナニィィィィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: Whaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥ブーイングはやめてください！
English: Order! Order! And stop that booing!

Japanese: しょ、証人！
English: M-Mr. Butz!

Japanese: あなた、ラジオを 聞いていたんですか？
English: You were listening to a radio on earphones?

Japanese: な、なんだよ！　その、 人をさげすんだような目は！
English: Y-yeah! So what! That a crime!?

Japanese: 悪ィかよ！　ラジオ聞いちゃよお！
English: I listen to my radio! Everybody listens to the radio! What's the big deal!?
Hmm...

Japanese: 狩魔検事。何か意見は‥‥？
English: Mr. von Karma... your opinion?

Japanese: ‥‥時間のムダだ。
English: Waste of time.

Japanese: ワガハイは、こんなウサンクサイ 証人は、認めぬ。
English: I do not accept this witness, nor his shoddy testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうしますか？　成歩堂くん。 《証言》をつづけてもらいますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Should he continue the testimony?

Japanese: （これ以上ハジをかくのは ゴメンだ！）
English: (No, I think I've heard enough. This is all too embarrassing...)

Japanese: （もう、じゅうぶんだろう‥‥）
English: (In fact, I think we've all heard enough.)

Japanese: ナニ言ってんの！　なるほどくん！
English: What are you saying, Nick!?

Japanese: 御剣検事、もう有罪判決を 受けてるんだよ！
English: If you stop now, Mr. Edgeworth will be found guilty!

Japanese: ここで逆転できなかったら、 終わっちゃうんだよ！
English: We have to turn this trial around now!

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Uh...

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします。 《証言》をつづけてください。
English: Please... please allow the witness to continue his testimony.

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Bah!

Japanese: ‥‥引きギワを知らぬ弁護士ほど ミジメなものはないな‥‥。
English: Nothing is more pitiful than a lawyer who doesn't know when he's lost!

Japanese: では、証人。
English: Very well, Mr. Butz.

Japanese: ラジオのこともふくめて、もっと くわしく《証言》を。
English: Please give your testimony, and be sure to include details like your RADIO.

Japanese: おう！　まかせな！
English: Right! Leave it to me!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I wouldn't if there were any other way out of this, believe me.)

Japanese: ～事件の夜、聞いたこと～
English: -- What Larry Heard --

Japanese: クリスマスイブだってのに １人なんて、さみしいじゃねえか！
English: It's lonely, being alone on Christmas Eve!

Japanese: だからよ！　リクエスト番組 聞いてたワケ。
English: That's why I was listening to an all-requests show on the radio, see?

Japanese: それはもう、ガンガンの 大音量でさあ。
English: I was listening to it real booming loud, like.

Japanese: でも、銃声っぽい音を 聞いたのは、間違いないぜ！
English: But I'm sure I heard that gunshot!

Japanese: 音が聞こえた瞬間のＤＪのコトバも ハッキリ覚えてるよ。
English: I remember exactly what the DJ was saying when I heard it, too.

Japanese: ラジオを‥‥しかも大音量で 聞いていたんですか！
English: You were listening to your radio... at a high volume!?

Japanese: なんなんだよ、さっきから！
English: Yeah, what's the big problem?

Japanese: いいじゃねェかよ、ラジオぐらい 聞かせてくれたってよぉ！
English: Can't a man listen to his radio in peace? Isn't this a free country!?

Japanese: （アイツ‥‥何が問題なのか、 カンゼンにわかってないな‥‥）
English: (I truly believe Larry has no idea what the problem here is.)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Judge.

Japanese: こんな証人のコトバが 信用できるだろうか？
English: Can you believe a word this witness says?

Japanese: おおかた、西洋ダイコの音とでも 聞き間違えたのだろう。
English: What he heard was probably nothing more than a drum beat from the radio!

Japanese: たしかに、そうですね‥‥。 証言には、信頼が‥‥
English: True enough, it is difficult to believe this testimony.

Japanese: 待ってください。
English: Wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: 証人は、“音がした瞬間のＤＪの コトバをおぼえている”と言った。
English: The witness said he remembers exactly what the DJ said when he heard the gunshot!

Japanese: でぃーじぇい？
English: Excuse me? Dee-jay...?

Japanese: アナウンサー。おたよりを 読む人のことです！
English: An announcer... the guy who says things on the radio.

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway!

Japanese: 問題の音がした瞬間、音楽は 鳴っていなかったのです！
English: What this means is, when he heard the sound, no music was playing!

Japanese: だから、音が聞こえた可能性は じゅうぶん、あります！
English: The DJ only talks between songs! So he could have heard the gunshot from the lake!

Japanese: 《尋問》をさせてもらいますよ！
English: I'd like to cross-examine the witness, Your Honor!

Japanese: わ‥‥わかりました。
English: V-very well, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （あぶないあぶない‥‥）
English: (I can't believe I'm continuing this charade...)

Japanese: ～事件の夜、聞いたこと～
English: -- What Larry Heard --

Japanese: クリスマスだってのに １人なんて、さみしいじゃねえか！
English: It's lonely, being alone on Christmas Eve!

Japanese: だからよ！　リクエスト番組 聞いてたワケ。
English: That's why I was listening to an all-requests show on the radio, see?

Japanese: それはもう、ガンガンの 大音量でさあ。
English: I was listening to it real booming loud, like.

Japanese: でも、銃声っぽい音を 聞いたのは、間違いないぜ！
English: But I'm sure I heard that gunshot!

Japanese: 音が聞こえた瞬間のＤＪのコトバも ハッキリ覚えてるよ。
English: I remember exactly what the DJ was saying when I heard it, too.

Japanese: “クリスマスはもう目の前ですね” って言った瞬間に、音がしたんだ。
English: Just when she said "Hey! it's almost Christmas!" I heard the gunshot!

Japanese: （‥‥なんのヘンテツもない 証言だよな‥‥）
English: (This is the most ludicrous testimony I've ever heard.)

Japanese: （あの１カ所をのぞいては！）
English: (But there is one gleaming ray of hope in there!)

Japanese: （あそこを重点的に つっこんでみるか‥‥）
English: (I've got to press it until we get to the bottom of what happened!)

Japanese: だから、ラジオを‥‥？
English: So, you turned on the radio?

Japanese: そうよ！
English: Right!

Japanese: 誰かに、語りかけて ほしかったんだよォ。
English: I just wanted to hear someone's voice, y'know?

Japanese: わかるだろ、なあ！　なあ！ この、セツないキモチ！
English: You don't know what it's like out there, alone, on Christmas Eve! Alone!

Japanese: （‥‥こんなところで ゆさぶるんじゃなかった‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't have said anything...)

Japanese: ちなみに、なんという 番組ですか‥‥？
English: Do you by any chance remember the name of the program you were listening to?

Japanese: 事件にはカンケイない！ 答えを聞く必要もない！
English: This has nothing to do with the case, Your Honor!

Japanese: 異議を認めます。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: 証人は、ラジオを聞いていた。 ‥‥それでじゅうぶんです。
English: The witness was listening to the radio. That is all we need to know.

Japanese: どれぐらいの音量で 聞いていたのですか？
English: Tell us, Mr. Butz, how loud was your radio set to that night?

Japanese: ガ‥‥ガンガン？
English: Real "booming" loud?

Japanese: まあな。
English: Yeah, y'know.

Japanese: ヘッドフォンをつけて いたんですよね‥‥？
English: And you had headphones on?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yup.

Japanese: 外の音など、聞こえそうも ない気がしますね‥‥。

Japanese: それは、証人の写真が示している。
English: This was shown by the witness's photograph.

Japanese: ボートには、被告人の御剣と、 被害者の生倉弁護士が乗っている。
English: The defendant, Edgeworth, and the victim, Robert Hammond were on that boat.

Japanese: ボートの上で銃声がして、 生倉弁護士が湖に落ちた。
English: There was a gunshot fired on that boat, and Robert Hammond fell into the lake.

Japanese: 被害者が撃たれた距離は、約１ｍ。 自殺ではあり得ない。
English: The distance of the shooting was one meter. It couldn't have been suicide!

Japanese: ‥‥どうだ？
English: Well?

Japanese: ボートの上に残った、もう１人の 男が犯人に決まっている！
English: The guilty party has to be the other man on that boat!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、それ以外は 考えられないと思いますが？
English: I admit, it is hard to imagine any other possibility.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: Yes...

Japanese: でもそれは、被害者が撃たれたのが 零時１５分だった場合の話です。
English: But this assumes that the victim was shot at 15 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ... ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean by that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: このとおり、発砲の瞬間の 写真もあるじゃないですか。
English: We have photographic evidence of the time of the shooting.

Japanese: この写真は、零時１５分に 撮影されたものですよ。
English: The timestamp on the photo says 00:15.

Japanese: しかし、それより２５分前にも、 矢張が銃声を聞いている！
English: But Larry heard a gunshot 25 minutes before that!

Japanese: 生倉弁護士は、そのときに 殺害されたのです！
English: Robert Hammond was killed then! 25 minutes before the shot on the lake!

Japanese: （そう考えないと、犯人は 御剣しかいなくなるからな‥‥）
English: (That's the only way that Edgeworth could be innocent!)
Mr. Wright. Are you quite mad?

Japanese: では、このボートの上の 人影は、いったい誰なのだッ！
English: Explain who this is sitting on the boat!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍と、生倉弁護士です！
English: Miles Edgeworth and Robert Hammond!

Japanese: 何を言っておる！
English: Yes, I believe you are mad!

Japanese: それは、検察側の主張どおりの 考え方だろうが！
English: That is exactly what I've been telling the court this whole time!

Japanese: キサマは今、言ったばかり ではないか！
English: You're agreeing with me! And yet, what did you just say?

Japanese: 生倉弁護士は、この銃声の 約２５分前に殺害されたのだと。
English: That Robert Hammond had been killed 25 minutes before the shot on the boat!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そのとおり！
English: Y-yes, that's what I said!

Japanese: あなたをためしたんですよ。 狩魔検事。
English: I was just testing you, von Karma!

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は、すでに一度、 有罪判決を受けているのですぞ！
English: Your client has already been declared guilty once!

Japanese: 今の発言には、ペナルティを 与えます！
English: I'm going to have to penalize you for this foolishness.
(*sigh*)

Japanese: フン！　もう一度聞くぞ！
English: Bah! I'll ask you again!

Japanese: それは、犯人と生倉弁護士です！
English: It was the murderer and Robert Hammond!

Japanese: 何を言っておる！
English: What are you saying!?

Japanese: キサマはさっき、 こう言ったばかりではないか！
English: That contradicts what you just told the court.

Japanese: 生倉弁護士は、この銃声の 約２５分前に殺害されたのだと。
English: You said that Robert Hammond had been killed 25 minutes before this gunshot!

Japanese: そ、そうでしたっけね。
English: Y-yes, that's right.

Japanese: それに、成歩堂くん。
English: Also, might I mention...

Japanese: 被告人は、ボートに乗って いたことを認めているんですよ。
English: The defendant, Mr. Edgeworth, has admitted to being on that boat.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。 （大失敗だ‥‥）
English: Er, right, Your Honor. (Crash and burn...)

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、 御剣と犯人ですよ！
English: Of course, it was Edgeworth and the murderer!

Japanese: 犯人は、生倉弁護士を １１時５０分に殺害したあと、
English: After the murderer killed Robert Hammond at 11:50...

Japanese: 生倉弁護士になりすまして、 御剣に会ったのです！
English: He assumed the guise of Mr. Hammond and met Edgeworth!

Japanese: そ‥‥ そんなバカなことが‥‥！
English: Wh... What!? Are you serious?

Japanese: 御剣は、なぜ事件の夜、湖に 行ったのかを話してくれません。
English: Yes. Edgeworth won't tell us why he went to the lake that night.

Japanese: しかし、おそらく。
English: However, I have a hunch.

Japanese: あの夜、御剣は、生倉弁護士に 呼び出されて、湖に行ったのです。
English: That night, Robert Hammond called Edgeworth to the lake.

Japanese: 御剣は、生倉弁護士のカオを よく知らなかった。
English: Now, Edgeworth didn't know Robert Hammond's face that well.

Japanese: だから、犯人が入れ替わっていても わからなかったのです！
English: That's why he didn't suspect anything when the murderer took Robert Hammond's place!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... なんというコトでしょう‥‥。
English: I'm not sure what to make of all this.

Japanese: バ‥‥バカなッ！
English: L-Ludicrous!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: いったい、あなたの言う 犯人とは、誰なのですか？
English: Tell us the name of the murderer then!

Japanese: 犯人ですか。 ‥‥そいつの名は‥‥！
English: The murderer's name...? Right! It's...

Japanese: （‥‥いやいや。 コントじゃないんだから）
English: (On second thought, that's a really dumb answer...)

Japanese: （ここまで引っぱって、 その答えはないだろう）
English: (I might get a laugh or two out of the crowd, but that's about it.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 なに１人でローバイしてるの？
English: Uh, Nick? Something on your mind?

Japanese: え？　あ、いやべつに。 ははは。
English: Huh? Oh, uh, nothing. Hah hah. *groan*

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ 笑ってないで、犯人の名前を！
English: Mr. Wright! Stop laughing and tell us who the murderer is!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: 犯人は、大沢木　ナツミです！
English: The murderer is none other than Ms. Lotta Hart!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... あの、カメラマンの‥‥？
English: The investigative photographer...?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええ。
English: Um, yes.

Japanese: あんな若い女性が、生倉弁護士に 変装した、と‥‥？
English: You're saying that young lady dressed up like Robert Hammond and fooled Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ま、まあ。意外にフケ顔 でしたからね、彼女。
English: Y-yes, well, she did look sort of manly.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなクダランことで、 ワガハイに口を開かせるとは。
English: I am ashamed I actually listened to this utter nonsense.

Japanese: いいか。
English: Listen!

Japanese: キサマのその主張は、 ある証拠品から思いついたものだ。
English: You came to this conclusion based on a piece of evidence.

Japanese: この、１１時５０分に撮影された という写真だ。
English: That is, this photograph taken at 11:50.

Japanese: この写真がなければ、キサマは こんなことは思いつかなかった。
English: If you didn't have this photograph, you never would have made this claim!
Correct?

Japanese: その証拠を、わざわざキサマに 与えたのは誰だ？
English: And just who was it who went out of her way to give you that piece of evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! 大沢木‥‥ナツミ本人‥‥か。
English: It was... Lotta Hart.

Japanese: 犯人が、わざわざキサマに 致命的な証拠をくれたと言うのか！
English: Why would the murderer go to all that trouble just to deliver you decisive evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... そりゃあ、ないですねえ。
English: I guess... they wouldn't.

Japanese: 弁護人！　今の発言には、 ペナルティを与えます！
English: Mr. Wright. I'm going to penalize you for this.

Japanese: （‥‥しまった‥‥）
English: (Whoops.)

Japanese: 弁護人！　あなたは本当に、 犯人の名前を言えるのですか！
English: Mr. Wright! Can you really tell us the name of the murderer!?

Japanese: （マズい！　うたがいのマナコだ。 ‥‥ハッタリとおせ、成歩堂！）
English: (Uh oh, he looks suspicious! I'd better bluff and bluff good!)

Japanese: 犯人の名前‥‥。 そんなの、わかりませんよ。
English: Actually, I don't know the murderer's name.

Japanese: わ、わからない！
English: Y-you don't know!?

Japanese: キサマ！　フザけるなッ！
English: Bah! Again, you waste my time!

Japanese: ‥‥だって。 名乗りませんでしたからね！
English: I don't know because he never told us!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! 犯人は、ボート小屋の管理人。 あのオジさんですよ。
English: The murderer is the caretaker of the boat shop, that old man!

Japanese: 彼は１１時５０分、 生倉弁護士を殺害したんです。
English: At 11:50, he was the one who killed Robert Hammond.

Japanese: か‥‥管理人のオジさんが‥‥！
English: The caretaker of the boat shop!?

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、どこで！
English: Wh-where did he do this!?

Japanese: その時間、湖にボートは なかったんですよ！
English: There weren't any boats on the lake then!

Japanese: 誰かを撃つために、わざわざ 湖の真ん中に出る必要はない‥‥。
English: Why would he have to go all the way out on the lake just to shoot someone?

Japanese: 今回の本当の殺人現場は、 ボートの上ではなかったのです。
English: May I suggest... That the real scene of this crime was not in a boat!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: で、では！　いったい、 どこだったというのですか！
English: W-well then, where did the murder take place!?

Japanese: （裁判長に、ほんとうの 殺人現場を示そう！）
English: (Show the judge where the murder really took place!)

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: It was here!

Japanese: ... そこはたしか、カメラマンの ナツミさんがいた場所ですね。
English: Where Ms. Hart, the photographer was camping? Yes.

Japanese: なぜボート小屋の管理人が、 そんなところで殺人を？
English: Why would the caretaker of the boat shop go there to kill someone?

Japanese: ちょっとしたカン違いでも したのでしょう。
English: It was probably an honest mistake.

Japanese: ボート小屋の管理人が？
English: The caretaker made a mistake?

Japanese: いえ。この、ぼくが。
English: No, I did.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... もう一度、やりなおしなさい。
English: Maybe you'd like to show us again?

Japanese: このへん、でしょうか。
English: Around here, maybe?

Japanese: “このへん”‥‥？
English: Around... where?

Japanese: 何か、コンキョはあるのですか？
English: You have some reason for thinking this?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: Yes, well...

Japanese: “コンキョなしも、またコンキョ なり”‥‥というのは‥‥？
English: Maybe because there's no good reason, the murderer knew we'd never expect it!
Try again.

Japanese: （軽くあしらわれてしまった）
English: (He wasn't even surprised I goofed up.)

Japanese: もちろん、ここです！ 彼が住んでいる、管理小屋ですよ。
English: Here, of course! The boat shop, where he lives.

Japanese: 誰にも見られず、被害者と 会うことができますからね！
English: That way he could meet with the victim without anyone seeing!

Japanese: そこが殺人現場だったという 証拠はあるのか！
English: Do you have proof that the boat shop was the scene of the crime!?

Japanese: さっきの、矢張の証言を 思い出してください。
English: Recall Larry's testimony if you will.

Japanese: 矢張はあの夜、ボートで 探しものをしていました。
English: That night he was out on the lake in a boat, searching for something.

Japanese: それを見つけて、 彼はボートを返します。
English: He finds it, and returns the boat.

Japanese: そして、帰ろうとしたとき！ 彼は銃声を聞いた。
English: Then, just as he's starting to head for home, he hears a gunshot!

Japanese: ‥‥さて、このとき‥‥。
English: He heard a gunshot, Your Honor!

Japanese: 彼はヘッドフォンをしていました。 でも、銃声は聞いている。
English: Even though he was wearing headphones at the time!

Japanese: つまり銃声は、矢張のすぐ近くで 鳴ったことになります！
English: In other words, the gunshot was very, very close by!

Japanese: それは、どこか‥‥？
English: And where would that be if he had just returned a boat?

Japanese: ボートの管理小屋‥‥ですね。
English: The boat shop...!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あの夜、ひょうたん湖で いったい何があったのか‥‥？
English: What happened that night on Gourd Lake!?

Japanese: 最初から、セツメイして もらえませんか！
English: Please tell the court, from the beginning!

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ だいじょうぶなの‥‥？
English: Nick, are you sure about this...?

Japanese: ど、どうかなあ。
English: Um, not really.

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、最初から順序だてて 考えていけば、
English: But, I think if I start at the very beginning...

Japanese: なんとか なるんじゃないかな‥‥。
English: And I take it slow, I might just be able to figure this out...

Japanese: ‥‥あの夜。
English: That night...

Japanese: ボート屋の管理人は、生倉弁護士を 管理小屋に呼び出しました。
English: The caretaker of the boat shop called Robert Hammond to his shop.

Japanese: ‥‥これが、１１時５０分ごろの できごとです。
English: This was around 11:50.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張の聞いた銃声は、もちろん このときのものです。
English: That was when the gunshot that Larry heard was fired.

Japanese: その後、管理人は 生倉弁護士のコートを着て、
English: After that, the caretaker put on Robert Hammond's coat...

Japanese: 彼になりすまします。
English: He became Robert Hammond!

Japanese: そして、御剣とボートに乗り、 湖の中央へ出た‥‥。
English: Then he got in the boat with Edgeworth, and went out into the middle of the lake.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。では、ボートの上で ピストルを撃ったのは‥‥？
English: Then... who fired the pistol on the boat, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: それは、御剣　怜侍‥‥
English: It was Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ どうしてそうなるの！
English: N-Nick! How could that be!?

Japanese: ピストルを持ってるのは オジさんの方じゃない！
English: Wasn't the old man holding the pistol!?

Japanese: そ、そうだね。 よくわかったなあ。
English: Uh, yeah. Good thinking!

Japanese: そんなの、 あたしでもわかるのに。
English: That was easy, Nick! What I'm worried about is what you're thinking!

Japanese: もちろん、ピストルを 撃ったのは、犯人の方です。
English: Of course, it was the murderer who shot the pistol.

Japanese: 御剣に当たらないように、 適当に２発、撃ったのです。
English: He shot twice. Both missed Edgeworth, on purpose.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください。
English: Wait a minute... Y-yes?

Japanese: 当てるつもりがないなら、 なぜ２発も撃ったんですか？
English: Why would he shoot twice if he didn't mean to hit anyone?

Japanese: う‥‥。 （細かいコトを聞くなよ‥‥）
English: Uh... (Details! Details!)

Japanese: どうした、弁護士。
English: Know this, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 言っておくが、セツメイに詰まった 時点で、キサマの負けだぞ。
English: The moment you run out of explanations is the moment you lose.

Japanese: なぜ犯人は２発、撃たなければ ならなかったのか！
English: Tell us why the murderer had to fire twice!

Japanese: い‥‥１発目をはずしたから ではないでしょうか？
English: B-because the first shot missed?

Japanese: はずした？
English: Missed?

Japanese: 御剣を狙ったのに、 当たらなかったから‥‥
English: Yeah, he was aiming for Edgeworth, and he missed...

Japanese: 何を言っている！
English: What are you saying!?

Japanese: さっきキサマは“わざと はずして撃った”と言ったぞ！
English: Did you not just tell us that he missed Edgeworth on purpose!?

Japanese: あれ。‥‥そうでしたっけ。
English: Huh? Oh, uh, right.

Japanese: （マズいぞ！　さっさと 方向転換をしよう‥‥！）
English: (Uh oh... I'd better figure out just what it is I'm trying to prove here.)

Japanese: それは、目撃者を作るため だった、と考えられます。
English: I believe he shot twice to create a witness, Your Honor.

Japanese: “目撃者を作る”‥‥？
English: Create a witness...?

Japanese: 犯人は、まず１発、 ピストルを撃ちます。
English: The murderer lifts his pistol and fires one shot.

Japanese: そうすれば、その音を聞いた 人は、かならず湖を見る。
English: That ensures that anyone who heard the shot would look at the lake.

Japanese: 実際、ナツミさんも、音がしたから 湖の方を見ています。
English: Indeed, Ms. Hart did exactly that after hearing the first gunshot.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Next!

Japanese: 犯人は、少し間をおいてから、 ２発目を撃ちました。
English: The murderer waits a bit and he fires again.

Japanese: そして‥‥、
English: Then...

Japanese: 犯人は、自分から 湖に飛び込んだのです。
English: The murderer jumps from the boat himself!

Japanese: ピストルは、ボートの上に 残して‥‥。
English: Leaving the pistol in the boat behind him.
I see!

Japanese: それを湖畔から見ていれば、
English: To someone looking from the edge of the lake...

Japanese: もう片方の人物に撃たれた、 と思ってしまうでしょうね‥‥。
English: It would appear that one of the men on the boat had shot the other!

Japanese: 犯人は、カメラが設置されている とは知りませんからね。
English: The murderer didn't know about the automatic camera, of course.

Japanese: わざと目立つように２発撃って、 誰かに見てもらおうとしたんです。
English: That's why he shot twice to draw attention to the boat!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あとは、カンタンです。
English: Once you realize that, everything else falls into place!

Japanese: 湖を泳いでもどった 管理人のオジさんは、
English: The boat shop caretaker swam back to his shop.

Japanese: 生倉弁護士の死体に もとどおりコートを着せて、
English: Then he put Mr. Hammond's wet coat back on the body.

Japanese: その死体を湖に投げ込んだのです。
English: And threw the body into the lake!

Japanese: ‥‥これが。
English: This is what happened, Your Honor.

Japanese: 今回の事件の真相です。
English: These are the events that transpired that night on Gourd Lake.
Bailiff!

Japanese: 今すぐ、先ほどの証人を つれてきなさい！
English: Bring out the witness from before!

Japanese: あの、ボート小屋の 管理人です！　早く！
English: The boat shop caretaker! Quickly!

Japanese: ‥‥では、あの管理人が 来るまでのあいだ、
English: Very well. While we are waiting for the caretaker...

Japanese: 被告人・御剣　怜侍に 話をうかがいましょう。
English: I would like to ask the defendant, Miles Edgeworth, a few questions.

Japanese: 被告人。 証言台についてください。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Please take the stand.

Japanese: 御剣くん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 弁護人の話は聞きましたね。
English: You heard what the defense has said?

Japanese: ‥‥うム。
English: Yes.

Japanese: いかがですか。
English: Well?

Japanese: なぜあなたは、あの晩、湖に 行ったのでしょう？
English: Why did you go to the lake that night?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 成歩堂弁護士の言ったことは、 だいたいにおいて正しい。
English: What Wright has said was mostly correct.

Japanese: 気味がワルいぐらいだ‥‥。
English: Astonishingly so, actually.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: Yes...

Japanese: 私は数日前、 １通の封書を受けとった。
English: Several days ago, I received a letter.

Japanese: 生倉弁護士という男からの 手紙で、
English: The letter was signed, Robert Hammond.

Japanese: “クリスマスイブの夜の１２時、 湖のボート小屋に来い”
English: He asked me to come to the boat shop by the lake at midnight on Christmas Eve.

Japanese: “‥‥大事な話がある”、と。
English: He said he had something very important to discuss with me.

Japanese: “大事な話”とは‥‥？
English: Something important?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 ... それは、言えない。
English: I'm sorry. I can't say what it was.
Hmm...

Japanese: 裁判長どのッ！
English: Your Honor, sir!

Japanese: 係官！　‥‥審理中です。 しずかに‥‥
English: Bailiff! We are conducting a trial here, I ask that you remain quiet...

Japanese: 管理人が消えました！ 管理小屋にも、帰っていません！
English: The witness has disappeared! He isn't at the boat shop, either!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: ど、どうしましょう‥‥？
English: What should I do?

Japanese: 早く、あとを追うのです！ 逃がしてはなりませんッ！
English: F-find him, quickly! We cannot allow him to get away!

Japanese: 狩魔検事！ 逃げた管理人は‥‥！
English: Mr. von Karma! Your witness has disappeared!
A search warrant has already been issued.
Hmm...

Japanese: さて。 言うまでもなく、この状態で 判決を下すことは不可能です。
English: It goes without saying that I cannot declare a verdict under these circumstances.

Japanese: よって、審理を明朝、 最終日まで延期します。
English: I will extend the trial until tomorrow, the final day allowed.

Japanese: 警察は、全力をあげて 管理人のゆくえを探すように！
English: I request that the police department utilize all its forces to find that witness!

Japanese: いいですね！
English: Am I understood?
One more thing.

Japanese: いったい、あの管理人は誰なのか？
English: Just who is that boat shop caretaker?

Japanese: ‥‥彼の身元も、 非常に重要だと考えます。
English: I think his identity has become very important to this trial.

Japanese: あわせて、調査を命じます。
English: I want him, and I want to know who he is.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Very well. Court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２７日　午後１時２２分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 27, 1:22 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: なるほどくん！　やったね！
English: Yay, Nick! You did it!

Japanese: うん‥‥。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: なんとか、有罪判決を ウヤムヤにできたよ。
English: Well, at least we got out from under that guilty verdict...

Japanese: でも、でも。
English: And what about Larry! That was something else!

Japanese: カルマ検事も、ヤッパリさんの デタラメぶりにはカタナシだね。
English: Even von Karma didn't know what to do with his testimony!

Japanese: ヤッパリさんのおかげだよ！
English: Larry really helped us out!

Japanese: 矢張か‥‥。 テキに回すと怖ろしい男だな。
English: Sure, once I sifted through his "unique" testimony.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥。
English: Still... he did save us.

Japanese: 毎回まいかい、なんでこう ギリギリまで追いつめられるかな。
English: I just wish our cases weren't so down to the wire all the time.

Japanese: ホントにねえ。
English: I know what you mean.

Japanese: 依頼人を助ける前に、 こっちがまいっちゃうよ‥‥。
English: Sometimes I feel like it's us on trial instead of our clients!

Japanese: なあ、御剣？
English: Hey, Edgeworth...
Umm... Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! な‥‥なんだろうか？
English: D-did you say something?

Japanese: なんでそんな、苦しそうな カオをしてるんですか？
English: Don't look so pained!

Japanese: このぶんなら、明日は たぶん、無罪っぽいですよ？
English: I mean, it looks like you're probably going to get off the hook!

Japanese: たまには、ちょっと にこにこしてみましょうよ！
English: You could try to smile just a little...? Relax!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... キミたちには すまないのだが‥‥。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: どうも、そういうワケには 行かないかもしれない‥‥。
English: But... I fear it's not over for me yet.

Japanese: ど、どういうことだ？
English: Wh-what do you mean?

Japanese: 成歩堂。
English: Wright...

Japanese: ずっと、迷っていることがある。
English: There's something that's been troubling me for a long time now.

Japanese: キミに話すべきかどうか‥‥。
English: And I don't know whether or not to tell you...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣？
English: Edgeworth?

Japanese: もう、時間がない。
English: No... there's so little time left.

Japanese: 話してしまって、ラクになりたい。 ‥‥しかし。
English: I want to tell you, to get it off my chest, but...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥やはり、今は 決心がつかない。
English: Hmm. I can't make up my mind.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それは、なんの話だ？　御剣。
English: What is this about, Edgeworth?
It's... a nightmare I've had.

Japanese: 私が犯した罪の記憶‥‥。
English: A memory of a crime... that I committed.

Japanese: お前の‥‥罪？
English: A crime you committed?
A memory... of a murder.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、御剣　怜侍に 判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２７日　午後２時１１分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 27, 2:11 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ なんで、あんなことを‥‥？
English: What was Mr. Edgeworth talking about...?

Japanese: 私が犯した罪の記憶‥‥。
English: A memory of a crime... that I committed.

Japanese: 殺人‥‥の記憶だ。
English: A memory... of murder.

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ まさか、誰かを‥‥？
English: Do you really think Mr. Edgeworth killed...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... そんなこと、あるわけがないよ。
English: I don't believe it. Not Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかに、あいつは今、何かの 記憶に苦しんでいるけど‥‥。
English: Some painful memory has been troubling him recently...

Japanese: 誰かの命をうばうなんて‥‥ あり得ない！
English: But he'd never take someone's life! Never!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: よお！　元気かねショクン！
English: Yo! How's everyone doing?

Japanese: どうよ？　どうよ？ 今日のオレサマの証言ぶり！
English: Whaddya think of my performance today?

Japanese: シビれたっしょ？ 真宵ちゃん！
English: I had 'em swooning in the aisles! Huh, Maya?

Japanese: え‥‥あ、あたし？
English: S-swooning? Me?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですね。 ビリッと来ちゃいました。
English: Oh... Oh, yes. I do remember feeling faint.

Japanese: だろ？　だろ？ もう、ホレちゃっただろ？
English: Right on! Tell me the truth, it was like love at first sight, right?

Japanese: なあ、成歩堂！
English: Right, Nick?

Japanese: え‥‥ぼ、ぼく？
English: H-huh? Me!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだな。 キュン、と来たかな‥‥。
English: I... uh, well, maybe my heart skipped a beat or two...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... なんか、イマイチ ピンと来ねぇなあ。
English: I think you can do better than that!

Japanese: 御剣が助かったのは、 オレのおかげよ？
English: C'mon! I saved Edgeworth in there, dude! Edgey!

Japanese: もっとこう、カンゲイしてくれよ！ このオレをよぉ！
English: You guys should be bowing before me! Yeah! Bow before your hero!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: かならず助けようね！ 御剣検事。
English: We have to save Mr. Edgeworth if it's the last thing we do, okay?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Right. (It very well may be...)

Japanese: とにかく、あのボート小屋の オジさん？
English: First, there's that rental boat shop caretaker.

Japanese: あの人のこと、調べようよ！
English: We need to find out who or what he is!

Japanese: そうだね。
English: I'd settle for who.

Japanese: （とりあえず、もう必要のない 証拠品を、少し整理するか‥‥）
English: (I guess I can clean out some of this evidence I no longer need...)
Okay, let's go!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's favorite plant. Its name is "Charley."

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんも 世話に慣れてきたようで、
English: Maya's gotten the knack of watering it lately.

Japanese: チャーリーくん、元気を 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: Charley's been perking up these days.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ボーイが、引き出しのドライバーの 角度をなおしているのが見える。
English: I can see the bellboy, adjusting the angle of that screwdriver in the drawer.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていた デスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: お客がもっと増えれば 座る機会もあるだろうが、
English: If we had more clients, I would probably sit here more often.

Japanese: 今はソファでテレビを 見ていることの方が多い。
English: Lately, I've been spending more time on the couch, watching TV.

Japanese: 一度、真宵ちゃんがトノサマンの ポスターを持ってきて、
English: Maya brought in a poster of the Steel Samurai the other day.

Japanese: 張る、張らないで大ゲンカに なってしまった。
English: We had a big fight over whether to put it up or not.

Japanese: まだあきらめきれないらしく、 スキをうかがっているみたい。
English: I know she's just waiting for a chance to sneak it up on the wall.

Japanese: 矢張。 今日の法廷‥‥助かったよ。
English: Larry... You really helped out in the trial today.

Japanese: そうだよね。
English: You did!

Japanese: ヤッパリさんがいなかったら、 ユーザイだったもんね、御剣検事。
English: If you weren't there, Larry, I'm sure Mr. Edgeworth would have been found guilty!

Japanese: はっはっはっはっはっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはっはっ
English: Hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah!

Japanese: でもよ、成歩堂。
English: But, seriously, Nick.

Japanese: たしかに、ボート小屋の管理人も アヤシイけどさあ。
English: That boat shop caretaker guy is pretty suspicious...

Japanese: 御剣のウタガイだって、まだ 晴れたワケじゃねえんだぜ。
English: But Edgey ain't off the hook yet!

Japanese: うう‥‥ん。 ヤッパリさん、シビアだなあ。
English: Way to spoil the mood, Larry.

Japanese: 客席で聞いているとよお。 こう言っちゃナンだけど‥‥、
English: Hey, I'm just a guy sitting in the audience, you know?

Japanese: 御剣だって、けっこう アヤシイ感じがするぜ‥‥。
English: But from where I was sitting, Edgey seemed pretty... edgy.

Japanese: おマエ、なんでそこまで アイツのこと、信じられるんだ？
English: I mean, can you really know he's telling the truth about that night?
Nick?

Japanese: ぼくは‥‥。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: たとえ何があっても、 何が起こっても‥‥
English: But... what I do know is...

Japanese: お前たち２人のことだけは、 信じぬくつもりなんだよ。
English: I'm going to believe in you two until the end.

Japanese: ２人って？
English: Us two?

Japanese: 御剣検事と‥‥あたしでしょ？
English: Edgeworth and... who else? You mean me right?

Japanese: ナニ言ってんだよ！ オレだろ？　成歩堂！
English: Nah! He means me! Right, Nick?

Japanese: ああ。‥‥お前だよ、矢張。
English: Yeah, you, Larry.

Japanese: あたしじゃないんだ‥‥。
English: Not... me?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... でも。でも。 どうしてなの！　ヤッパリさん！
English: B-but why you, Larry!?
Huh?

Japanese: さ‥‥さあ。 どうしてだ？　成歩堂‥‥？
English: Um, actually, yeah. Why me, Nick?
Hmph. Enough with the silent treatment!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: どうしてそんなに、 御剣検事にこだわるの？
English: Why do you trust Mr. Edgeworth so much?

Japanese: たしかに、最近はちょっと 感じが変わったけど、
English: I mean, he's changed recently, true.

Japanese: ただのイヤな男だったでしょ、 初めて会ったときは。
English: But when we first met him, he was kind of a jerk, don't ya think?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 真宵ちゃんは、知らないんだよ。
English: You didn't know him back then.

Japanese: 弁護士を目指していたころの 御剣　怜侍を‥‥。
English: Back when he wanted to become a defense attorney.

Japanese: それって‥‥
English: Wait...

Japanese: なるほどくんたちがクラスメート だったっていう‥‥？
English: Was that when you two were classmates?

Japanese: うん。ぼくらが小学生のころの 話だ。
English: Yes. In grade school...

Japanese: ぼくは、助けられたんだ。 御剣　怜侍と‥‥
English: They saved me... Miles...

Japanese: 矢張　政志にさ。 ‥‥一生わすれないよ。
English: And Larry. They saved me and I'll never forget it.

Japanese: そのおかげで、こうして 弁護士にもなったんだ。
English: That's why I became a defense attorney, you know.

Japanese: ええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ね。ね。矢張さん。 なんのこと‥‥？
English: Hey, hey, Larry. What's he talking about?

Japanese: え！　ええと‥‥その。
English: Huh? Uh... umm...

Japanese: ‥‥悪ィ。 ゼンゼンおぼえてねえや、オレ。
English: Er, sorry. I kinda forgot.

Japanese: ううう。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: こら、なるほどくん！ 今日こそ聞くんだからね、その話！
English: Okay, Nick. Out with it! I'm going to hear this story today, and that's final!

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。
English: Okay, okay.

Japanese: 少し長くなるかもしれないけど、 聞いてもらおうかな‥‥。
English: It's kind of a long story, so hang in there.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あれは、そう。 小学４年生の夏‥‥。
English: It was the beginning of Spring, 4th grade.

Japanese: ぼくは、学級裁判に かけられたんだ。
English: I was on trial. A class trial.

Japanese: が‥‥、がっきゅうさいばん？
English: A c-class trial!?

Japanese: 矢張、おぼえてないか？ 小学４年生の夏だ‥‥。
English: You remember, Larry? Spring, 4th grade?

Japanese: ぼくのクラスの１人の子が、 給食費を盗まれたんだ。
English: A kid in my class got his lunch money stolen.

Japanese: 給食費？
English: Lunch money?

Japanese: ぼくたちの小学校、 とても小さなところでさ。
English: Our school was really small.

Japanese: 毎月、親がお金を入れてくれた 封筒を、先生に提出するんだ。
English: Every month, kids would bring in an envelope with money for lunch from home.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。
English: Huh, I see.

Japanese: その封筒が‥‥消えたんだよ。 ３８００円、入っていた。
English: Anyway, this kid's envelope disappeared. With $38.00 still inside.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。そんなことが あったっけなあ。
English: Oh... Yeah, now that you mention it, I do remember that!

Japanese: お前はよく知らないかも しれないな、矢張。
English: I can see why you'd forget, though.

Japanese: その日はお前、 学校を休んでいたから。
English: You were out of school that day.

Japanese: とにかく、封筒が盗まれたのは 体育の時間だった。
English: Anyway, the envelope had been stolen during PE class.

Japanese: ぼくはその日、カゼぎみで、 体育の授業を休んでいたんだ。
English: I was coming down with a cold, so I'd skipped PE that day.

Japanese: 授業を休んだのは、 ぼくだけだった。
English: I was the only one not in class.

Japanese: だから‥‥うたがわれたの？
English: So... they thought you did it?

Japanese: うん。クラス中のヤツが‥‥ ぼくをサイバンにかけろ、って。
English: Yeah. The kids in class said I should be put on trial.

Japanese: サイバン‥‥。
English: Trial...?

Japanese: 次の日、学級裁判が開かれた。 ‥‥もちろん、被告人はぼくだ。
English: So the next day we held a classroom trial, with me as the defendant.

Japanese: 「ぼ‥‥ぼくはやってないよ‥‥」
English: "I... I didn't do it!"

Japanese: 「有罪」「ゆうざい」「有罪」 「お前だろ！」「どろぼう！」
English: "Guilty!" "He did it!" "Guilty!" "It was you!" "Thief!"

Japanese: 「返してやれよ、お金」「ヒドい人 信じらんない！」「ゼッコーだ！」
English: "Give me my money back!" "You're such a meanie!" "No one play with him!"

Japanese: 「ぼくがやったって言えよ」「かく すんじゃねえよ」「言いなさいよ」
English: "Just admit you did it!" "You can't hide the truth!" "Tell us the truth!"

Japanese: 「遊んでやらないからな！」「もう ケシゴム貸してあげないからね！」
English: "We're not gonna play with you any more!" "Yeah, and no borrowing my eraser!"

Japanese: 「リレーの選手、やめさせようよ」 「図書委員もだ」「飼育係もよ！」
English: "He shouldn't be allowed in the relay race!" "Or on the library committee!"

Japanese: 「こないだの５０円返せ」「となり 町の銀行強盗、お前じゃねえのか」
English: "Gimme back my 50 cents I loaned you!" "Hey, did you rob that bank the other day?"

Japanese: 「龍一くん。ダメでしょ？ 人のお金を盗んじゃあ‥‥」
English: "Now, Phoenix, you know you shouldn't steal people's money! It's not right."

Japanese: ‥‥最後に、先生がそう言った。
English: In the end, even the teacher thought I'd done it.

Japanese: 「あやまりなさい。龍一くん」
English: "Apologize to the class, Phoenix."

Japanese: ‥‥その瞬間、ナニがなんだか わからなくなって‥‥
English: I... I didn't know what was happening.

Japanese: とても悲しくって‥‥ なみだが止まらなくって‥‥
English: I was so sad... I couldn't stop crying.

Japanese: クラス中の視線がぼくに集まった。 “オマエガハンニンダ”って‥‥。
English: Everyone was staring at me like I'd done it...

Japanese: ぼくは、あやまろうとした。 ‥‥その子の席の前まで行って。
English: I tried to apologize. I went over to where the boy was sitting...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... そのときだった。
English: That's when it happened.

Japanese: 「その必要はない！」
English: "He shouldn't have to apologize!"

Japanese: 「サイバンでモノを言うのは、 証拠品だけなのだ！」
English: "The only thing that belongs in a trial is evidence!"

Japanese: 「その他のものはすべて、 沈黙すべし！」
English: "Anything else has no place!"

Japanese: 「そんなコトも知らないのか‥‥ シロウトどもめッ！」
English: "You should all be ashamed... amateurs!"

Japanese: 「ミ‥‥ミツルギくん？」
English: "M-Miles?"

Japanese: 「キミじゃないのだろう？ ボクの封筒を盗んだのは‥‥」
English: "It wasn't you who stole my money, was it?"

Japanese: 「う‥‥うん」
「それならば、堂々としたまえ」
English: "Then you shouldn't apologize!"

Japanese: 「これだけ話し合ったのに、 キミがやったという証拠はない」
English: "Everyone's been shouting you did it, but no one has any proof!"

Japanese: 「ということは、裁判長！ ‥‥この少年は、ムザイだ！」
English: "That is why, Your Honor, this boy is innocent!"

Japanese: 「ナニを言ってるの、レイジくん！ 盗まれたのは、あなたのお金よ！」
English: "B-but Miles, it was your money that was stolen!"

Japanese: 「そうだよ！」 「そいつがやったんだよ！」
English: "Yeah yeah!" "He did it! He's the one!"

Japanese: 「証拠なんて必要ないわ」 「あやまらせろよ！」
English: "We don't need proof!" "Make him say he's sorry!"

Japanese: 「‥‥イイカゲンにしろよ！‥‥」
English: "Why don't you all just shut up!!!"

Japanese: 「おマエたち、いつもそうやって グルになって１人を責めて‥‥」
English: "This is always how it is, everybody ganging up and picking on one person."

Japanese: 「言われるヤツの身にも、 なってみろってんだよ！」
English: "Just think how he feels!"

Japanese: 「コイツ、“やってない”って 言ってるじゃねえかよォ！」
English: "He said he didn't do it, so he didn't do it!"
"Very well."

Japanese: 「お金は、 先生が立て替えておきます」
English: "I will replace the money myself."

Japanese: 「‥‥学級裁判は、これで おしまいにしましょう‥‥」
English: "This class trial is over."

Japanese: それからだよ。
English: That's how it happened.

Japanese: ３人がなんとなく、 仲よくなったのは‥‥。
English: After that, the three of us were always friends.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことが あったんだ‥‥。
English: Wow... I had no idea!

Japanese: あ、あったような気も するなあ‥‥。
English: Yeah... I had no idea, either! I mean, I forgot.

Japanese: あのとき、ぼくは 思い知らされたんだよ。
English: That's when I learned what it meant to be alone.

Japanese: 味方がいない、究極のコドク。 ‥‥その、ツラさをね。
English: Totally alone, without a friend in the world.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤッパリさん、 いいヒトだったんですね‥‥。
English: You did a good thing, Larry!

Japanese: い、いや、まあ‥‥。
English: Um, yeah, well...

Japanese: たまたまその日、学校 休んでたからよかったケド、
English: I was just lucky that I took the day off from school.

Japanese: そうじゃなかったらゼッタイ、 オレがうたがわれてたから。
English: If I'd been there they would have thought I'd done it!

Japanese: ヒトゴトじゃなかったんだわ、 コレが‥‥。
English: So, I took it kind of personally, see.

Japanese: （“事件のカゲに、ヤッパリ矢張” って言われつづけてたもんな）
English: ("When something smells, it's usually the Butz.")

Japanese: ‥‥学級裁判が終わって、 御剣から聞いたんだ。
English: Anyway, Edgeworth and I talked after that class trial.

Japanese: お父さんが弁護士だってこと。
English: That's when I heard his father was a defense attorney.

Japanese: 父親のコトを話すときだけは、 ヤツの目は子供っぽく光ってた。
English: I remember, his eyes would shine when he talked about his father.

Japanese: 「ボクはかならず、父さんのような 弁護士になるのだよ」
English: "I'm going to become a defense attorney, just like my Dad!"

Japanese: 「リッパな弁護士に‥‥」
English: "A famous defense attorney!"

Japanese: しかし‥‥それから数ヶ月後。 あいつは突然、転校していった。
English: Then, a few months later, he suddenly transferred to another school.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件‥‥。
English: The DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: そう。たぶん、お父さんが 亡くなったためだろう。
English: Right. I'm not sure, but the transfer probably had to do with his father's death.

Japanese: かわいそう‥‥。
English: That's so sad!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 御剣の名前をひさしぶりに 見たのは、数年たってからだ。
English: It was several years later when I heard Edgeworth's name again.

Japanese: 新聞に、彼の名前が大きく のっていた。
English: There was an article about him in the newspaper.

Japanese: 《鬼検事に、黒い疑惑》 とかいう見出しでね。
English: The headline was something like "Dark Suspicions of a Demon Attorney."

Japanese: 証拠のでっち上げ、証言の操作、 もみ消し‥‥。
English: Fabricating evidence, manipulating testimonies, covering up facts...

Japanese: 有罪のためならば、なんでも する男だ、と書いてあった。
English: The article said he'd do anything to get a guilty verdict. Anything.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてだ？
English: But why?

Japanese: ナニがあったんだよ！ まるで別人じゃねえか！
English: What happened!? I mean, that's not the Edgey I used to know at all!

Japanese: ぼくもそう思った。
English: That's what I thought too.

Japanese: ぼくは御剣に会おうと、 何度も連絡をとった。
English: I tried to get in touch with him I don't know how many times...

Japanese: しかし、彼は返事を してくれなかった。
English: He never replied.

Japanese: ムカシの友だちに‥‥ 会いたくなかったんだ‥‥。
English: I guess he didn't want to see his old friends...

Japanese: でもぼくは、 あきらめられなかった。
English: I couldn't just drop it, though.

Japanese: どうしても彼に会って、 ナゾを解くんだ、と‥‥
English: I wanted to meet him, to learn why he had become who he became.

Japanese: そう決心したんだ。
English: That's when I decided.
Wait... You don't mean...?

Japanese: まさか、なるほどくん‥‥ そのために‥‥？
English: That's why!?

Japanese: 御剣検事に会うために、 弁護士になったの‥‥？
English: That's why you became a defense attorney!? To meet Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 弁護士になれば、あいつは イヤでもぼくに会わざるをえない。
English: If I was a defense attorney, I knew he'd have to meet me whether he wanted to or not.

Japanese: 法廷でね。
English: In court.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ぼくを信じてくれた御剣を、 ぼくは信じている。
English: Edgeworth believed in me, and I believe in him.

Japanese: あいつは苦しんでいる。 そして、味方がいない。
English: He's in pain... And no one's on his side.

Japanese: チカラになれるのは、本当の あいつを知っている、ぼくだけだ。
English: I'm the only one who knows the real Edgeworth. I'm the only one who can help him.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂‥‥。
English: Whoa... Nick.

Japanese: じゃあ、おマエがオレを、 タダで助けてくれたのも‥‥？
English: S-so, is that why you helped me out for free?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Uh... yes.

Japanese: お前を信じていたからだ。
English: I helped you because I believed in you.

Japanese: （‥‥ただし、タダと言った おぼえは、ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (Except I don't remember saying I'd do it for free...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥なるほどうよお‥‥‥‥
English: Aww, Nick! Nick!!!

Japanese: あたしに何か見せるより、 調査に行こうよ！
English: Stop showing me stuff and let's get cracking!

Japanese: もう、今日しかないんだよ！
English: We only have today to get to the bottom of this!

Japanese: いやあ、はっはっ。 カッコよかったよなー、オレ。
English: Woo hoo, I was hot out there. Hot!

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥。 カンゼンに舞い上がってる）
English: (I'm glad someone's happy about how this case is going...)

Japanese: （今は、 何を見せてもムダだな‥‥）
English: (He seems too happy to care about anything I show him.)

Japanese: あと１日だね、なるほどくん。
English: One day left, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。
English: Yeah... I know.

Japanese: さあ。休んでるヒマはないよ！ 出かけようよ！
English: Well, no time to waste! Let's get going!

Japanese: どうしようか？ これから‥‥。
English: What do you think we should do now, Maya?

Japanese: それは、なるほどくんが イチバン、よく知ってるでしょ。
English: You would know best, Nick!

Japanese: そのとおりにがんばれば、 いいと思うよ。
English: Just do what you do! That should work.

Japanese: どう？ ‥‥何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Had any good ideas?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、ＤＬ６号事件‥‥。
English: This is all tied to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: 明日までに、できるだけ 調べた方がよさそうだよね。
English: We'd better find out as much as we can about that murder before tomorrow!

Japanese: （御剣をがんじがらめにしている、 運命のクサリ‥‥か）
English: (Something that happened back then has a hold on Edgeworth and it won't let go.)

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 27 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... 例によって、御剣は 取り調べを受けているらしい。
English: Looks like Edgeworth is back in questioning.

Japanese: 御剣検事には、聞かなきゃ いけないこと、あるよね。
English: We have our own questions for him!

Japanese: あとでまた来ようよ。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: そうだな‥‥。
English: Yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: わすれちゃダメだよ。
English: Don't forget, okay?

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
