English: Frankly, I can't believe that you're speaking to the parrot.
(Remember... two days ago...)

Japanese: サユリさんサユリさん。 何かわすれたこと、ないかな？
English: Pol-ly, Pol-ly. Have we forgotten something?
*squawk*

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件を忘れるな”
English: "Don't forget DL-6!" *squawk*

Japanese: （ここでサユリさんに あのコトバをしゃべらせれば）
English: (If I can get Polly to say that here...!)

Japanese: （あのオヤジが、ＤＬ６号事件の 関係者だと証明できるはずだ）
English: (That will prove that the caretaker had something to do with DL-6!)

Japanese: ‥‥ねえ、サユリさん。 何かわすれたこと、ないかな？
English: Um, Polly? Have we forgotten something?
"Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: ‥‥！！ ...! そ‥‥そうじゃないよ、 サユリさん！
English: Th-that's not what you're supposed to say!

Japanese: わ、わすれたこと！ わすれたことは、ない？
English: Forgot! Something we forgot!

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: ダメだよ！　なるほどくん！ ‥‥言ってくれない！
English: Uh oh. It's not working, Nick! She won't say it!

Japanese: （‥‥そんな、バカな！ なぜ、言わないんだ‥‥！）
English: (This is ridiculous! Why won't she say it!?)

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥ どうかしたかな？　弁護人‥‥。
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Something the matter, Mr. Wright?
(Wait...)

Japanese: （まさか、狩魔のヤツ‥‥）
English: (Don't tell me von Karma expected this!)

Japanese: （こうなることを予想して、 オウムを訓練しなおしたのか！）
English: (He couldn't have retrained the parrot... could he!?)

Japanese: （“わすれたこと”を聞かれても、 答えないように、と‥‥！）
English: (Did he train her not to respond when we asked if we'd forgotten anything!?)

Japanese: （‥‥名前を言わせてみるか‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should get her to say her name?)

Japanese: サユリさん、サユリさん。 ‥‥お名前は？
English: Polly! Polly! What's your name?

Japanese: “サユリサン‥‥サユリサン‥‥”
English: "Pol-ly! Pol-ly!" *squawk*

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: このオウムが“サユリさん” であることはわかりました。
English: I think we've established that this parrot is named "Polly."

Japanese: それが、管理人の正体と 何かカンケイがあるのですか？
English: Does this have anything to do with her owner's identity?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、カンケイ‥‥ ないかもしれませんね。
English: Uh, well, I guess it doesn't really have anything to do with that, no.

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: カンケイある質問だけ、 おねがいします。
English: Please only ask questions pertaining to the matter at hand.

Japanese: では、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well, witness. Continue your testimony.

Japanese: もちろん、あります！
English: Yes it does!

Japanese: ！ !!! ‥‥フン。 おもしろい‥‥。
English: Hah! Fascinating!

Japanese: オウムの名前が、管理人の 正体を証明するというのか！
English: You claim that the parrot's name will prove her owner's identity?

Japanese: ‥‥では、その証拠を 見せてもらおうか‥‥。
English: Then show us this proof!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ハッタリもほどほどに‥‥
English: Nick! Don't you think you're taking the bluffing a little too far?

Japanese: いいかい。
English: Listen.

Japanese: ぼくたちが証明するのは、 “管理人は誰か？”じゃない。
English: We're not here to answer the question of "who is the caretaker."

Japanese: “管理人は灰根だ”という ことなんだ。
English: We're here to prove that he is Yanni Yogi!

Japanese: つまり、“サユリ”という名前と “灰根”を結びつければいいのさ。
English: All we have to do is tie the name "Polly" to Yogi!

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: オウムの名前が、管理人の正体を あきらかにするという証拠は‥‥
English: The proof that the parrot's name reveals the caretaker's identity is...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そろそろキミも気づくべき ではないかな。
English: I had hoped you would have learned this by now...

Japanese: “当てずっぽうは、時間のムダだ” ということにね‥‥。
English: Making random guesses here is a waste of court time!

Japanese: （‥‥また間違っちまったか‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... wrong again.)

Japanese: もう、よろしい！
English: That is all!

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の資料‥‥ ですか？
English: The DL-6 Case File?

Japanese: 資料といっても、 かなりの量がある！
English: That's quite a large file you have there!

Japanese: 弁護人が言う“証拠”とは、 どのページなのか！
English: Which page is this "proof" on, then?

Japanese: そこまで示してもらいたい！
English: Show us, or stop wasting our time!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 弁護人。では、示してください。
English: Very well. Mr. Wright, please show us the page.

Japanese: オウムの名前とカンケイする 資料のページは、どれですか？
English: Where in this file is the information connected to this parrot's name?

Japanese: それは“事件概要”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Case Summary" page!

Japanese: それは“被害者のデータ”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Victim Data" page!
I'm sorry...

Japanese: “サユリさん”という名前と カンケイのある記述は、
English: But I don't see anything here that relates to the name "Polly."

Japanese: このページには 見あたりませんね‥‥。
English: At least, not on this page.

Japanese: ‥‥フン！ 思ったとおり、ハッタリか‥‥。
English: Hmph! A bluff! As I expected!

Japanese: 弁護人の主張は認めません。
English: I'm afraid I have to deny your claim, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証人。証言をつづけてください。
English: The witness may continue.

Japanese: それは“容疑者のデータ”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Suspect Data" page!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥？ ...? ここには、灰根　高太郎のデータが 書かれています。
English: This page has all the information about Yanni Yogi!

Japanese: 彼が逮捕された直後、婚約者が 自殺していますね？
English: Right after he was arrested, his fiancee committed suicide, see?

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。たしかに、 資料に書かれていますね‥‥。
English: Hmm... Indeed, it does say that, yes.

Japanese: 婚約者の名前は？
English: What was his fiancee's name?

Japanese: “松下　サユリ”‥‥！
English: "Polly Jenkins"... "Polly"!

Japanese: そういうことです。
English: Exactly, Your Honor!

Japanese: 彼は、自殺した婚約者のことを おぼえていた。
English: He remembered the name of his fiancee who committed suicide.

Japanese: だから、ペットのオウムに 婚約者の名前をつけたんです！
English: That's why he named his parrot after her!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥考えられる ことですね‥‥。
English: I see! I guess that is possible.

Japanese: フン！　そんなのは、 偶然にすぎない！
English: Bah! A mere coincidence, that's all!

Japanese: ワガハイのマゴが飼っておる イヌは“リュウ”というが、
English: My granddaughter has a dog she calls "Phoenix."

Japanese: 弁護士！　キサマ、 ワガハイのマゴの婚約者か！
English: Well, Mr. Phoenix Wright? Does this make you my granddaughter's fiancee!?

Japanese: マゴはまだ、７才だぞ！
English: She's only seven years old!!!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥そうですね。
English: Hmm... Indeed.

Japanese: たしかに、それだけでは、 コンキョとして弱い‥‥。
English: Alone, it is a little weak for evidence in a murder trial.

Japanese: もう１つぐらい、何か ほしいところですね‥‥。
English: We would need some other corroborating evidence...

Japanese: （うう‥‥そんなあ‥‥）
English: (Where am I going to find that!?)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥でも、１歩前進だよ！
English: Nick! We're getting closer!

Japanese: あと１つ！　何か手がかりを つかめば‥‥！
English: One more! If we can just get one more piece of evidence...!

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥）
English: (Right... but what?)

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: では、証人。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well, witness. You may continue.

Japanese: （金庫の番号を言わせてみよう）
English: (Maybe I'll get her to say the number of that safe...)

Japanese: え？　きんこ？ ‥‥なんで？
English: Huh? The safe? Why?

Japanese: まあまあ。とにかく、 やってみなきゃ始まらない。
English: Let's just try to get her to say anything, okay?

Japanese: サユリさん。お部屋にあった 金庫の番号を教えて。
English: Polly! What was the number of the safe in the shack?
My, what a reckless parrot.

Japanese: で？　成歩堂くん。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: まさかこの番号が、管理人の正体と 何かカンケイがあるんですか？
English: You aren't claiming that this number has something to do with the caretaker?

Japanese: ‥‥カンケイがあるからこそ、 証言してもらったんですよ！
English: Actually it does! That's why I had her say it!

Japanese: ハッ！　バカバカしい‥‥。
English: Hah! Ridiculous!

Japanese: 金庫の番号が、管理人の正体を 語るというのか！
English: How can the number to a safe tell us who the caretaker is?

Japanese: それならば！　証拠を見せたまえ！
English: Show us your proof!

Japanese: 金庫の番号と、管理人の正体を むすびつける証拠だ！
English: What could possibly link this number to the caretaker's true identity!?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、最初から キタイはしていませんでしたがね。
English: Admittedly, I didn't think you had anything to show us.

Japanese: 金庫の番号が、管理人の正体を 証明するなどとは‥‥。
English: And now I see I'm right. How could a safe number reveal someone's identity?

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、違ったらしいな）
English: (Uh oh... I think that was the wrong thing to show them.)

Japanese: フン。‥‥気がすんだか。
English: Hmph. I hope you're happy now, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証人。証言をつづけてください。
English: The witness may continue her testimony...

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の資料‥‥。
English: The DL-6 Case File?

Japanese: キサマは、よほどその事件が お気に入りのようだな‥‥。
English: What is this obsession you have with that case?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この資料のどこに、金庫の番号が カンケイするというのですか？
English: Where in this file is something relating to that safe number?

Japanese: それは“被害者のデータ”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Victim Data" page!

Japanese: それは“容疑者のデータ”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Suspect Data" page!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: これは、１５年前の事件の 資料なのです‥‥。
English: This file is from a case 15 years ago.

Japanese: どこにも、金庫のことなど 書いてありませんよ！
English: I don't see anything about a safe in here at all!

Japanese: いつものように、弁護士の ハッタリだったわけだな。
English: I believe this is another one of Mr. Wright's bluffs.

Japanese: ふう‥‥。では、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Hmph. Very well, witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: それは“事件概要”の ページです！
English: It's on the "Case Summary" page!

Japanese: 事件‥‥概要‥‥ですか？
English: The Case... Summary?

Japanese: 正確には、事件の起こった “日付”です！
English: Specifically, the date on which the DL-6 Incident occurred!

Japanese: 事件が起こった日？ “１２月２８日”‥‥
English: The date of the incident? December 28...?

Japanese: １５年前の‥‥まさに、 今日の日付ですね‥‥。
English: Why, that's today's date. Fifteen years ago!

Japanese: そして、金庫の番号は “１２２８”です。
English: And the number on that safe is 1228!

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件が起こった日付を 金庫の番号にしてあるんですよ。
English: He used the date of the DL-6 Incident as the number for his safe, Your Honor!

Japanese: 彼にとって、その日付は、それほど 意味のあるものなのです！
English: That's how important that date was to him!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥ フシギな一致、ですね‥‥。
English: I see... It certainly is an interesting coincidence.

Japanese: たしかに、日付を暗証番号にする というのは、よくすることです。
English: People often do set their secret numbers to dates.

Japanese: フン！　そんなのは 証拠にはなるまい！
English: Bah! This is not tangible proof!

Japanese: ワガハイのキャッシュカードの 暗証番号は“４６４９”だが、
English: I set my ATM card's number to "0001" because I'm number one!

Japanese: これは“ヨロシク”の語呂あわせで 日付とは何のカンケイもない！
English: This has nothing to do with a date! Nothing!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、結論が出た ように思います‥‥。
English: I think we've reached a conclusion here.

Japanese: 考えるまでもない！ これは、単なる偶然だ。
English: This is a mere coincidence, that's all!

Japanese: そうかもしれません。
English: True, that is a possibility.

Japanese: しかし、“単なる偶然”は、 ２つ重なることはありません。
English: However, two coincidences at the same time seems more like a "pattern" to me.

Japanese: な‥‥何を言う！　裁判長！
English: Wh... what are you saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥ボート小屋の管理人を 呼んでください。
English: Summon the caretaker of the boat shop.

Japanese: すぐに！
English: Immediately!

Japanese: 証人。
English: Witness...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたの名前を聞かせて いただきたい‥‥。
English: Tell us your name.

Japanese: 待ちたまえ。
English: Wait!

Japanese: この証人は、 自分の名前を‥‥
English: This witness, he doesn't remember...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいですよ‥‥。
English: No... it's okay.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! 私は、目的を果たした。 ‥‥それで、じゅうぶんです。
English: I've accomplished what I wanted to do. I'm done.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　なんだか、 フンイキがゼンゼン違うよ！
English: Nick! He looks totally different!

Japanese: あれが‥‥彼の本当のすがた だったんだよ‥‥。
English: This is the real Yogi, I think. Finally.

Japanese: 世間から身をかくすために、 彼はずっと‥‥
English: He's been acting feeble to hide his true identity.

Japanese: ‥‥１５年間、 シバイをつづけていたんだ。
English: Acting... for 15 years!

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人‥‥。
English: W-well...!

Japanese: あらためて うかがいましょう。
English: Let me ask you again.

Japanese: あなたの名前は？
English: Please state your name for the court!

Japanese: ‥‥灰根　高太郎と申します。
English: My name... is Yanni Yogi.

Japanese: １５年前まで、この法廷で 係官をつとめておりました。
English: 15 years ago, I served as a bailiff in this very court.

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎‥‥。
English: Yanni Yogi!

Japanese: あなただったんですか？ 生倉弁護士を殺害して‥‥、
English: So was it you who killed Robert Hammond...?

Japanese: その罪を、御剣　怜侍に 着せようとした‥‥？
English: And tried to frame Miles Edgeworth for his death?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... そのとおり。 ‥‥すべて、私がやりました。
English: Yes. It was me. I did it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... １５年前の事件で、 私は被告席に立たされました。
English: They put me on the witness stand 15 years ago...

Japanese: 生倉弁護士は‥‥私の精神状態を 問題にしました。
English: Robert Hammond... he said I was mentally unsound.

Japanese: “無罪になるためだ”と 生倉に説得されて、
English: He told me it would make me innocent. Get me off the hook.

Japanese: 私は心神喪失のシバイを するハメになりました‥‥。
English: So... I pretended to have brain damage...

Japanese: 私は無実だったのに！ 彼は信じようとはしなかった！
English: I was innocent, really! But he didn't believe me!

Japanese: ‥‥無罪にはなったものの、 私はすべてを失っていました‥‥。
English: We won the trial... But I lost everything.

Japanese: 仕事も、婚約者も、社会的な立場も なにもかも‥‥。
English: I lost my job, my fiancee, my social standing...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それから１５年たった、 今年になって‥‥
English: Then, this year, 15 years later...

Japanese: 私のもとに、１通の封書と ピストルが届いたのです。
English: A package arrived. It was a letter... and a pistol.

Japanese: それには、今回の計画が 細かく書かれていました。
English: The plan was written out in careful detail.

Japanese: 私の人生を破滅させた人物に、 “復讐”する計画‥‥。
English: It was a plan for me to take my revenge on the people who ruined my life.

Japanese: ‥‥差出人が誰なのか？ そんなことは問題ではなかった。
English: I didn't care who had sent it.

Japanese: ‥‥１５年たった今になって、 やっとチャンスを得たのです‥‥。
English: I thought this was my chance, after 15 years, this was it!

Japanese: 生倉　雪夫と御剣　怜侍に 復讐できるチャンスを‥‥。
English: Finally, a chance to have my revenge on Robert Hammond and Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、 思い残すことはありません。
English: I have no regrets.

Japanese: ちょ‥‥、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait a moment!

Japanese: 御剣検事に対する、“復讐”？
English: Revenge... against Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥それはいったい、 どういう意味なのですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、私の口から 言うべきことではありません。
English: I'm not at liberty to speak on that matter.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍本人に 聞いてください。
English: Why don't you ask Mr. Edgeworth yourself?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥生倉弁護士を 殺害したのは、私です。
English: Anyway, I admit it, I was the one who killed Robert Hammond.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。 灰根　高太郎は‥‥？
English: von Karma... Where is Mr. Yogi?

Japanese: 緊急逮捕した。
English: Under arrest, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥自白の内容に、 おそらく間違いはないだろう。
English: I saw no room for error in his confession.

Japanese: ‥‥ということは、 御剣　怜侍被告は‥‥？
English: Then... the defendant, Miles Edgeworth is...

Japanese: 無罪だろう。 ‥‥この件に関しては。
English: Innocent. In this case, at least.
Hmm.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥では、被告人。 証人席についてください。
English: Will the defendant please take the stand?

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、いくつもの謎が 残っています。
English: There are a few mysteries left unsolved.

Japanese: しかし、とりあえず、 あなたの容疑は晴れました。
English: Still, you are cleared of suspicion for this particular case.

Japanese: 生倉弁護士殺害事件について、 判決を下したいと思います。
English: So I would like to pass judgment on the murder of Mr. Robert Hammond.

Japanese: 異議はありませんね？
English: Any objections?
(I don't believe it!)

Japanese: （なぜ、狩魔検事は 何も言わないんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why isn't von Karma saying anything?)

Japanese: では。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に対する判決を 言い渡しましょう。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: い‥‥今の異議は、 いったい、どなたが‥‥？
English: D-did someone just say "objection"?

Japanese: （か‥‥狩魔じゃない‥‥）
English: (It wasn't von Karma...!)

Japanese: （‥‥ということは‥‥ まさか‥‥）
English: (Wait, but that means...)
No...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ィィッ！
English: Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: ただ今の判決に、 異議を申し立てるッ！
English: I object to your judgment.

Japanese: ‥‥ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Wh-what do you mean?

Japanese: 私は無罪などではない！
English: I'm not innocent at all!

Japanese: 聞いてのとおり、灰根　高太郎は 復讐のため、殺人の罪を犯した。
English: As we have heard, Yanni Yogi killed Robert Hammond in revenge.

Japanese: なんのための復讐だったのか？ ‥‥それは‥‥‥
English: But, revenge for what?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥、自分が 犯人だって言おうとしてるよ！
English: Edgeworth is trying to confess! He's going to say he's guilty!

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の真犯人は、 自分だった、って‥‥！
English: He's going to tell them he was the murderer in the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: お父さんを死なせちゃったのは 自分だ、って‥‥！
English: He's going to tell them he killed his own dad!

Japanese: （まずい！　‥‥どうする！）
English: (Uh oh! What do I do?)

Japanese: 判決はすでに下されました！ ‥‥御剣の発言は‥‥
English: The judgment has already been passed! I object to Edgeworth's outburst...

Japanese: 昨日も、こんなことがあった‥‥。
English: Didn't something like this happen yesterday, too?

Japanese: 有罪判決が下った直後、 ある証人が異議をとなえた。
English: I believe a certain witness raised an objection after a guilty verdict was passed.

Japanese: （矢張のことか‥‥）
English: (That would be Larry...)

Japanese: ワレワレは、 聞かなければならない！
English: We must hear this new statement!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍のコトバを！
English: We must hear Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: He's right.

Japanese: 私たちは、 聞かなければなりません。
English: We have a duty to hear Mr. Edgeworth out.

Japanese: （‥‥御剣‥‥。 苦しみぬいた末の結論だろう）
English: (No... I'm sure Edgeworth thought about this one long and hard.)

Japanese: （ぼくには、ジャマできない！）
English: (This isn't my place to interfere!)

Japanese: なるほどくん！　いいの！
English: Nick, are you sure!?

Japanese: ‥‥しかたがないよ。 これは、あいつの問題なんだ。
English: There's nothing we can do about it. This is his problem, now.
For fifteen years...

Japanese: 私は、ある“夢”に 苦しみつづけてきた。
English: I have had a recurring dream.

Japanese: ‥‥ただの夢だ‥‥ そう、自分に言い聞かせてきた。
English: A nightmare... it's only a nightmare. That's what I told myself.

Japanese: しかしそれは、夢ではなかった。
English: But now I know, it wasn't a dream.

Japanese: ‥‥灰根　高太郎は、 犯人ではなかった。
English: Yanni Yogi wasn't the killer.

Japanese: それは‥‥あなたの父上が 亡くなった事件のことですか‥‥？
English: You mean... in the incident where your father died?

Japanese: 撃たれた距離から考えて、 父は自殺でもなかった‥‥。
English: From the distance of the shot, it wasn't suicide, either.

Japanese: そう。すべては明白だった。
English: Everything was as clear as day.

Japanese: 犯人は‥‥
English: The murderer...

Japanese: あの事件の犯人は‥‥
English: The criminal in the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: この、私自身だったのだ！
English: It was me!

Japanese: 裁判長！ 私は、ここに罪を告白する！
English: Your Honor! I confess my guilt!

Japanese: まさに今日、時効となる ＤＬ６号事件について！
English: I am guilty for DL-6, the statute of limitations of which ends today!

Japanese: 犯人は、この私なのだッ！
English: The culprit... is me!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: おそるべき事態が発生しました！
English: This is certainly unexpected!

Japanese: 無罪になった被告人が、 まったく別の罪を告白している！
English: The defendant, declared innocent, is confessing to a different crime!

Japanese: しかも、その事件は‥‥ 本日が時効なのです！
English: A crime for which the statute of limitations runs out today!

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、どうすべきか‥‥
English: I'm not really sure how I should deal with this...

Japanese: フン！
English: Bah!

Japanese: 決まっておる。
English: It's obvious.

Japanese: この場で裁いてしまうのだよ。
English: We hold a trial. Right here. Right now.

Japanese: この男の、１５年前の犯罪を、な！
English: We try this man for his crime of fifteen years ago!

Japanese: ここで、５分間の休憩を とりたいと思います！
English: I think... I think I would like to take a five-minute recess.

Japanese: その間に、私たちがとるべき 道を決定したいと思います！
English: During this time, I will consider the appropriate course of action to take.

Japanese: では、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２８日　午後２時２４分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 28, 2:24 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥本当に、すまない。 成歩堂‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, Wright.

Japanese: キミの努力を ムダにしてしまって‥‥。
English: I've just wasted all of your effort.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... 御剣検事‥‥。 自分には、まだ信じられないッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth... I just don't believe it, pal!

Japanese: まさか、あなたが‥‥ お父さんを‥‥！
English: I mean, you... kill your dad?

Japanese: ‥‥私だって、信じたくないさ！
English: I didn't want to believe it myself, Detective!

Japanese: しかし‥‥事実だ。 私は、罰を受けなければならない。
English: But... it's the truth. I deserve to be punished.

Japanese: いかなる理由があっても、 殺人は‥‥殺人なのだ。
English: Murder is murder, no matter what the circumstances.

Japanese: うう‥‥なんてこったッス！
English: This is crazy! Just crazy!
Nick? What are you doing?

Japanese: ‥‥ん？　ああ。
English: Huh? Oh...

Japanese: 法廷記録の内容を、もう一度 チェックしてるんだよ。
English: I was just reading through the Court Record once more.

Japanese: これから、証明しなければ ならないからね。
English: I'm getting my case ready.

Japanese: ‥‥証明するって、‥‥何を？
English: Your case... for what?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ。 御剣　怜侍の“無実”だよ。
English: Huh? Isn't it obvious? I'm going to prove that Miles Edgeworth is innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! な‥‥何を言ってるッス！ 御剣検事は、認めてるッスよ！
English: Wh-what are you talking about, pal! He just admitted to it!

Japanese: その‥‥自分の、罪を‥‥。
English: He confessed that he did it! In court!

Japanese: 悪いけど、御剣。
English: I'm sorry, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ぼくは信じてないんだ。 お前の“悪夢”なんて。
English: But I don't believe your "nightmare."

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: “悪夢”はしょせん“悪夢”だよ。 ‥‥現実じゃない。
English: It's just a dream. It's not real.

Japanese: 現実に起こったことは、 この法廷記録が知っている。
English: The truth is right here in this Court Record.

Japanese: とにかく。 本当の勝負はこれからさ。
English: In any case, tighten your belts. The real fight is just beginning.

Japanese: ぼくには証明できるはずだ。 ‥‥キミの無実を、ね。
English: I'll prove you're innocent. Trust me.
W-Wright...

Japanese: １２月２８日　午後２時３０分 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: December 28, 2:30 PM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Then, I would like to resume our trial.

Japanese: いいか、裁判長。
English: Judge!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は、自らの罪を 認めている。
English: Miles Edgeworth has admitted his own guilt. He has confessed his crime.

Japanese: まず、彼の《証言》を聞こう。
English: Let us begin by hearing his testimony.

Japanese: そして、ムダだと思うが、 弁護士が《尋問》をする。
English: Then, though pointless, let the defense do their cross-examining.

Japanese: ‥‥ＤＬ６号事件は、今日で 時効が成立してしまう。
English: The statute of limitations on the DL-6 Incident runs out today.

Japanese: 変則的だが、こういう 手順で審理を進めたい。
English: Though it's unconventional for me, I'd like to run this one by the book.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。 弁護人、異議はありますか？
English: I see. Does the defense have any objections?

Japanese: ありません。 ‥‥それで、けっこうです。
English: No, Your Honor.

Japanese: （狩魔‥‥こうなることを、 最初から計算していたのか！）
English: (von Karma... you knew this was going to happen from the very beginning, didn't you!)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、御剣　怜侍。 ‥‥証人台についてください！
English: Will Miles Edgeworth take the stand?

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業は？
English: Will the witness state his name and profession.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍。 検事をしている。
English: Miles Edgeworth... I am a prosecuting attorney.

Japanese: 今から１５年前。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 証人は、父親である御剣　信を あやまって死なせてしまった。
English: Fifteen years ago, you mistakenly killed your father, Gregory Edgeworth.

Japanese: 間違いないか？
English: Is this correct?
It is correct.

Japanese: ‥‥では、そのときのことを 《証言》してもらおう。
English: Then testify about this matter to the court.

Japanese: （昨日、御剣の 話を聞いていて‥‥）
English: (When Edgeworth was telling me about his dream yesterday I noticed something...)

Japanese: （１つだけ、ナットク できなかった点があった）
English: (One detail didn't quite fit.)

Japanese: （そこを追求していくしか ないだろうな‥‥）
English: (That will be the key... but only if I can get it to work!)

Japanese: おねがい‥‥‥‥‥
English: Please... Please...

Japanese: ～ＤＬ６号事件・自供～
English: -- The DL-6 Incident --

Japanese: 私はその日、父親の法廷を 見るため、裁判所へ行った。
English: That day, I had gone to the courtroom to observe one of my father's trials.

Japanese: その帰り。地震によって私たちは エレベータ内に閉じこめられた。
English: As we went to leave, an earthquake struck, trapping us in the elevator.

Japanese: 父と灰根は、しだいに落ちつきを なくして、争いはじめた。
English: My father and Mr. Yogi lost their composure, and began to argue.

Japanese: そのとき、私の足もとに、何か 重いモノが当たった。
English: Just then, something heavy fell at my feet.

Japanese: 私はそれを、灰根に投げつけた。 争いを止めようと思ったのだ。
English: I picked it up, and threw it at Mr. Yogi. I wanted them to stop fighting.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、１発の銃声。 そして、悲鳴が聞こえた。
English: A moment later, there was a single gunshot, then a scream.

Japanese: ものすごい悲鳴だった。 ‥‥今でもわすれられない‥‥。
English: It was a terrible scream. I remember it to this day.

Japanese: ‥‥それがすべてだ‥‥。
English: That's all.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証人はこれまで、その記憶を “夢”だと思っていたのですね？
English: And, until now, you thought this memory was a "dream"?

Japanese: 私たちは、５時間、エレベータに 閉じこめられていた。
English: We were stuck in that elevator for five hours.

Japanese: エレベータの中は酸欠状態になり、 私はそのときの記憶を失ったのだ。
English: The oxygen in the elevator ran out, and I lost my memory of the events.

Japanese: フン！　灰根と同じ症状を 主張するワケか‥‥。
English: Bah! The same claim Mr. Yogi has made!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥《尋問》をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright? Your cross- examination, please.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～ＤＬ６号事件・自供～
English: -- The DL-6 Incident --

Japanese: 私はその日、父親の法廷を 見るため、裁判所へ行った。
English: That day, I had gone to the courtroom to observe one of my father's trials.

Japanese: その帰り。地震によって私たちは エレベータ内に閉じこめられた。
English: As we went to leave, an earthquake struck, trapping us in the elevator.

Japanese: 父と灰根は、しだいに落ちつきを なくして、争いはじめた。
English: My father and Mr. Yogi lost their composure, and began to argue.

Japanese: そのとき、私の足もとに、何か 重いモノが当たった。
English: Just then, something heavy fell at my feet.

Japanese: 私はそれを、灰根に投げつけた。 争いを止めようと思ったのだ。
English: I picked it up, and threw it at Mr. Yogi. I wanted them to stop fighting.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、１発の銃声。 そして、悲鳴が聞こえた。
English: A moment later, there was a single gunshot, then a scream.

Japanese: ものすごい悲鳴だった。 ‥‥今でもわすれられない‥‥。
English: It was a terrible scream. I remember it to this day.
(There it is!)

Japanese: （証拠とムジュンしている 部分がある‥‥）
English: (One part of that testimony clearly contradicts the evidence...)

Japanese: （それがいったい何を 意味するかはわからない‥‥）
English: (But I don't know what it means...!)

Japanese: （とにかく、そこを追求する しかないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better find out, and quick...)

Japanese: その日、お父さんは どんな事件を‥‥？
English: What was the trial your father was involved in on that day?

Japanese: よく覚えていない。
English: I don't remember things very clearly.

Japanese: 覚えているのは、２つ。
English: Only two things...

Japanese: 父が負けたことと、 狩魔検事が相手だったことだ。
English: I know my father lost, and Mr. von Karma was the prosecuting attorney.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。‥‥あなたが あつかった事件でしたか‥‥。
English: Mr. von Karma? You were handling that case?

Japanese: ‥‥１５年前のことだ。 細かいことは、覚えておらぬ。
English: It was fifteen years ago. I don't remember the details.

Japanese: （たしか御剣の父親に、不正を 指摘されたんだっけ‥‥）
English: (That was when Edgeworth pointed out the problem in von Karma's evidence...)

Japanese: 閉じこめられたのは、 きみもふくめて３人だったな。
English: So, there were three people including yourself trapped in that elevator?

Japanese: そうだ。私と、父と、灰根係官。
English: Yes. Myself, my father, and Yanni Yogi.

Japanese: 最初は、どうということも なかったのだが‥‥
English: We were fine at first...

Japanese: 数時間待っても、 助けは来なかった‥‥。
English: But then as time passed, and no one came to help...

Japanese: そのとき、きみはどうした？
English: What did you do then?

Japanese: 私は当時、９才の子供だった。 何もできるはずがない。
English: I was a nine-year old boy at the time. What could I do?

Japanese: 怖くて、すみっこで ふるえていた。
English: I was scared... Trembling, in the corner.

Japanese: しかし‥‥。
English: But then...

Japanese: それは、なんだった？
English: What was it?

Japanese: ‥‥ピストルだ。
English: A pistol.

Japanese: おそらく、灰根係官の ものだったのだろう。
English: I assume it was the bailiff, Yanni Yogi's.

Japanese: ホルスターから落ちたとき、 安全装置がはずれたのだ。
English: The safety must have come off when it fell from his holster.

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、それを拾った。 そして、どうしたか？
English: And you picked it up. What happened next?

Japanese: きみは、それをピストルだと 知っていたのか？
English: Did you know it was a pistol when you threw it?

Japanese: ‥‥なんとなく、わかっていた。 どんなにキケンなモノなのか‥‥。
English: I think I knew. I knew it was dangerous.

Japanese: しかし、酸素はうすく、私は パニックにおちいっていた‥‥。
English: But... the air was getting so thick. I panicked.

Japanese: とにかくキサマは、ピストルを 灰根に向かって投げたのだな？
English: So, you're saying that you threw the pistol at Mr. Yogi.

Japanese: ‥‥夢中だった‥‥。
English: I was... in a daze.

Japanese: 銃声は、１発だけだった？
English: The gun fired once?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。
English: Yes...

Japanese: あれを投げつけたあと、私は 気を失ってしまったらしい。
English: I think... after I threw it, I lost consciousness.

Japanese: あれ以来‥‥。
English: Since then...

Japanese: アタマの中に、いつも こだましているのだ。
English: They've echoed in my head, every day.

Japanese: １発の銃声と、おそろしい 悲鳴が‥‥な。
English: That gunshot, and that horrible scream.
The scream...
"To this day"...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ。 ハッキリ、耳に残っている。
English: Yes. I can practically hear it now.

Japanese: おそらく一生、 消えることはないだろう‥‥。
English: I doubt I will ever forget that scream as long as I live.

Japanese: 銃声は“１発”だったんだな？
English: So, there was only one gunshot?

Japanese: ‥‥それは間違いない。
English: Yes. I'm sure of that.

Japanese: エレベータの中に響いた あの音、そして悲鳴。
English: I heard the shot, and the scream...

Japanese: それを聞いた私は、 気が遠くなって‥‥
English: Then everything faded.

Japanese: そのまま、救助されるまで 気を失ってしまったのだ。
English: I was unconscious until the rescuers came.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: それはおかしい。
English: It must have been scary.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this photograph.

Japanese: ‥‥これは、１５年前の 現場の写真です。
English: This is a photograph of the scene of the crime, fifteen years ago.

Japanese: 今の証言と、この写真の間には 決定的なムジュンがあります。
English: There is a clear contradiction between your testimony and this picture. I can see that the victim lying there is Gregory Edgeworth...

Japanese: この写真に“ムジュン”が‥‥？ はて、どういうことでしょう。
English: What do you mean, "contradiction"?

Japanese: （例によって、裁判長は わかってないみたいだ‥‥）
English: (As always, the judge is clueless.)

Japanese: （裁判長に、この写真の おかしいところを示そう！）
English: (Show the judge the contradiction in the photo!)

Japanese: ‥‥ここ、だと思います。
English: The contradiction is... here, Your Honor.

Japanese: それは‥‥被害者、御剣　信 のことですか？
English: You mean the victim, Gregory Edgeworth?

Japanese: 御剣　信は、弾丸を１発撃たれて 死んでいます。
English: Gregory Edgeworth was shot once, fatally.

Japanese: それのどこがおかしいのですか？
English: What is contradictory about that?

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、もっとハッキリ したムジュンを示すべきらしい）
English: (Maybe I need to be a little more specific...)

Japanese: もう一度、おねがいします！
English: Again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: それは‥‥ このあたり、だと思われます！
English: The contradiction is... around here, I think!

Japanese: ... 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: あなたは、自信がないとき、 目がおよぐクセがある。
English: Your eyes have a habit of shifting back and forth when you're uncertain.

Japanese: 地に足をつけて もう一度、考えなおすように。
English: Try taking a deep breath, and think this over once more.
R-right, Your Honor.

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、ここです。
English: As should be obvious, the contradiction is here.

Japanese: エレベータのドアに、 弾痕が残っていますね‥‥。
English: I see... a bullet hole in the door...

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は“銃声は１発だった” と証言しています！
English: Miles Edgeworth testifies that there was only one gunshot!

Japanese: しかし！ この写真では、 ２発の銃弾が撃たれたことになる！
English: However, according to this photograph, not one, but two shots were fired!

Japanese: １発は被害者に当たり、もう１発は エレベータのドアに当たった。
English: One shot hit the victim, the other hit the elevator door.

Japanese: それならば！
English: I ask you, then!

Japanese: なぜ御剣　怜侍は、銃声を１発 しか聞いていないのでしょうか！
English: Why did Miles Edgeworth only hear one gunshot?

Japanese: たしかに、その写真では ２発の発砲がみとめられる。
English: This photograph does seem to confirm a second shot.

Japanese: しかし！　エレベータの ドアの弾痕は、いつできたのか？
English: However, when was the hole in the elevator made?

Japanese: 事件が起こる前日には、すでに あったかもしれない。
English: It might have been there before the incident occurred!

Japanese: そうならば、このドアの弾痕は、 事件とは無関係ということだ！
English: If it was there before, it has nothing to do with this incident!

Japanese: むむむ‥‥。
English: Urk...

Japanese: 弁護士！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: エレベータの弾痕が、事件の際に できたという証拠はあるのか！
English: Do you have proof that this bullet hole was caused at the time of the incident!?

Japanese: （‥‥くそ‥‥そこまでは 考えてなかったぞ‥‥）
English: (Grr... I didn't think of that.)

Japanese: それはその‥‥ありません。
English: I, uh, don't have proof.

Japanese: ハッ！　それはそうだろうな！
English: Hah! As I suspected!
Hmm...

Japanese: １５年も前の事件ですからな。 証拠も残っていないでしょう‥‥。
English: Given that all this happened fifteen years ago, it's doubtful any proof remains.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥ちょっと、これ見て。
English: Nick! Take a look at this!

Japanese: 法廷記録‥‥？
English: The Court Record...?

Japanese: これって、もしかして‥‥。
English: Look... doesn't this make you think...

Japanese: あ‥‥あっ！ （たしかに‥‥これはおかしい！）
English: Wh-whoa! (She's right! That is strange!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　どうやら、証拠を 示すのは可能のようです‥‥。
English: Your Honor. I think I will be able to show you proof.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！ そんなバカなことが‥‥！
English: Wh-what!? Impossible!

Japanese: まあまあ、狩魔検事。おどろくのは 証拠を見てからにしましょう。
English: Now, now, Mr. von Karma. Save your surprise for after you've seen the evidence.

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 見せてもらいましょう。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Show us your proof.

Japanese: エレベータのドアの弾痕が、 事件と関係があるという証拠は？
English: What do you have that proves the bullet hole on the door was related to this incident?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ムチャな証拠をたたきつけられる 身にもなってください。
English: Please, think how we feel having to look at this evidence time and time again.

Japanese: そのたびに感じるのです。 “また１パイくわされた！”と。
English: Every time you show us ridiculous evidence, I feel, well, mocked.

Japanese: 悪気はないんですけどね。
English: Hah hah... Uh, I mean, I really don't mean any harm by it, Your Honor.

Japanese: “悪気がない”のは、
English: "Not meaning any harm"...

Japanese: “悪気がある”の次に 悪いことです！
English: and "not doing any harm" are two different things, Mr. Wright. Try again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。 答えは、この資料の中にあります。
English: Your Honor... The answer is in this file.

Japanese: あきもせず、またそのファイルを 持ち出してきたか。
English: You do enjoy dragging out that file, don't you?

Japanese: ワガハイは認めないぞ！ ページを示さんかぎりはな！
English: I don't accept this evidence! Unless... you can tell us what page it's on!

Japanese: ドアの弾痕と事件の関係を 証明するページはどれだ？
English: Which page shows the link between this bullet hole and the DL-6 incident!?

Japanese: 資料の、“被害者のデータ”を 見てください。
English: Look at the "Victim Data" in this file!

Japanese: ここに、ハッキリ書かれています。 “凶器は２発撃たれている”と！
English: It says it quite plainly: "the murder weapon was fired twice"!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は、銃声を１発しか 聞いていない！
English: Miles Edgeworth only heard one gunshot!

Japanese: しかし現実に、凶器のピストルは ２発、撃たれているのです！
English: Yet, the murder weapon was fired twice!

Japanese: 銃声は“１発”だったんだな？
English: Are you sure you only heard one gunshot?

Japanese: ‥‥それは間違いない。
English: Yes. I'm sure of that.

Japanese: エレベータの中に響いた あの音、そして悲鳴。
English: I heard the shot, and the scream...

Japanese: それを聞いた私は、 気が遠くなって‥‥
English: Then everything faded.

Japanese: そのまま、救助されるまで 気を失ってしまったのだ。
English: I was unconscious until the rescuers came.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: それはおかしい。
English: But that doesn't make sense!

Japanese: もう一度、 この資料を見てください。
English: Look at this file one more time.

Japanese: 被告人の証言と、あきらかに ムジュンしている‥‥
English: This plainly contradicts the witness's testimony.

Japanese: あきもせず、またそのファイルを 持ち出してきたか。
English: You do enjoy dragging out that file, don't you?

Japanese: ワガハイは認めないぞ！ ページを示さんかぎりはな！
English: I don't accept this evidence! Unless... you can tell us what page it's on!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍の証言と、ムジュン しているページはどれだ？
English: Which page contradicts Miles Edgeworth's testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥ ついに、運がつきたようだな。
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Looks like your luck has run out.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。‥‥そのページには、 特にムジュンは認められません。
English: Mr. Wright... This page has no contradictions that I can see!

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってください！ ムジュンはあります！
English: W-wait! There is a contradiction in the file!

Japanese: ならば、正しいページを示せ！ うちのマゴでもできるぞ！
English: Then show us the right page! Even my grandchild could do better than you!

Japanese: 資料の、“被害者のデータ”を 見てください。
English: Look at the "Victim Data" in this file!

Japanese: ここに、ハッキリ書かれています。 “凶器は２発撃たれている”と！
English: It says it quite plainly: "the murder weapon was fired twice"!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は、銃声を１発しか 聞いていない。
English: Miles Edgeworth only heard one gunshot!

Japanese: しかし現実に、凶器のピストルは ２発、撃たれているのです！
English: Yet, the murder weapon was fired twice!

Japanese: １発は、御剣がピストルを投げた ことによる、暴発。
English: The first shot was the accidental firing when the pistol was thrown.

Japanese: ‥‥すると、もう１発は、 いったい誰が撃ったのか！
English: So... who fired the remaining shot!?
Hmm...

Japanese: すると、２発目を撃った人物が、 他にいたということですか‥‥？
English: Was there, perhaps, another shooter who fired that second shot?

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥わすれてはいないだろうが、
English: Your Honor. As I'm sure you're aware...

Japanese: この事件は、１５年前に起こった。 ‥‥証拠も古い‥‥。
English: This incident occurred fifteen years ago. The evidence is dated...

Japanese: ピストルは、たしかに ２発、撃たれている。
English: The pistol did fire twice.

Japanese: しかし、その２発目が“いつ” 撃たれたのか？
English: However, we do not know WHEN that second shot was fired.

Japanese: もしかしたら、事件の前日に 撃たれたものかもしれない。
English: It might have been fired the day before the incident!

Japanese: ２発目の発砲が、事件と関係がある という証拠は、ないのだ！
English: There is no proof that the second shot had anything to do with this incident!

Japanese: （なんだと‥‥！）
English: (What...!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see, I see.

Japanese: たしかに、そうですね。 ‥‥成歩堂くん、どうですか？
English: You do have a point. Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 凶器のピストルは、たしかに ２発撃たれています。
English: The murder weapon was fired twice, as we have heard.

Japanese: そして、そのうちの１発は 当時少年だった被告人が撃った。
English: One of those shots was fired by the defendant, a boy at the time.

Japanese: では、もう１発が、事件の際に 撃たれたという証拠はありますか？
English: Do you have any proof that the other shot fired had something to do with the case?

Japanese: （‥‥くそ‥‥そこまでは 考えてなかったぞ‥‥）
English: (Grr... I didn't think of that.)

Japanese: それはその‥‥ありません。
English: I, uh, don't have proof.

Japanese: ハッ！　それはそうだろうな！
English: Hah! As I suspected!
Hmm...

Japanese: １５年も前の事件ですからな。 証拠も残っていないでしょう‥‥。
English: Given that all this happened fifteen years ago, it's doubtful any proof remains.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥ちょっと、これ見て。
English: Nick! Take a look at this!

Japanese: 法廷記録‥‥？
English: The Court Record...?

Japanese: これって、もしかして‥‥。
English: Look... doesn't this make you think...

Japanese: あ‥‥あっ！ （たしかに‥‥これはおかしい！）
English: Wh-whoa! (She's right! That is strange!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　どうやら、証拠を 示すのは可能のようです‥‥。
English: Your Honor. I think I will be able to show you proof.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！ そんなバカなことが‥‥！
English: Wh-what!? Impossible!

Japanese: まあまあ、狩魔検事。おどろくのは 証拠を見てからにしましょう。
English: Now, now, Mr. von Karma. Save your surprise for after you've seen the evidence.

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 見せてもらいましょう。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Show us your proof.

Japanese: 凶器のピストルが２発撃たれた のが事件当時だったという証拠は？
English: Do you have evidence that the second firing of the pistol is related to this incident?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ムチャな証拠をたたきつけられる 身にもなってください。
English: Please, think how we feel having to look at this evidence time and time again.

Japanese: そのたびに感じるのです。 “また１パイくわされた！”と。
English: Every time you show us ridiculous evidence, I feel, well, mocked.

Japanese: 悪気はないんですけどね。
English: Hah hah... Uh, I mean, I really don't mean any harm by it, Your Honor.

Japanese: “悪気がない”のは、
English: "Not meaning any harm"...

Japanese: “悪気がある”の次に 悪いことです！
English: and "not doing any harm" are two different things, Mr. Wright. Try again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this photograph.

Japanese: これは‥‥１５年前の 現場写真、ですか‥‥。
English: This is a photograph of the scene of the crime, fifteen years ago. I can see that the victim lying there is Gregory Edgeworth...

Japanese: 凶器のピストルは、事件の際に ２発、撃たれたのです。
English: This proves the murder weapon was fired twice, at the time of the incident!

Japanese: 写真が、それを証明している！
English: This photo proves it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。 ... ... はて、どういうことでしょう？ この写真の、どこにそんな‥‥？
English: So, let me get this straight. This photo proves two shots were fired? Where?

Japanese: （こ‥‥この裁判長、 ホントにわかってないのか？）
English: (Y-Your Honor, please... Please get a clue!)

Japanese: （裁判長に、この写真の おかしいところを示そう！）
English: (Show the judge the contradiction in the photo!)

Japanese: ‥‥ここ、だと思います。
English: It's here... I think.

Japanese: それは‥‥被害者、御剣　信 のことですか？
English: You mean the victim... Gregory Edgeworth?

Japanese: 御剣　信は、弾丸を１発撃たれて 死んでいます。
English: Gregory Edgeworth was shot once... and he died, as we can see here.

Japanese: それのどこがおかしいのですか？
English: What's strange about that?

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、もっとハッキリ したムジュンを示すべきらしい）
English: (Er... Maybe I need to be more specific here.)

Japanese: もう一度、おねがいします！
English: Once more, please!

Japanese: それは‥‥ このあたり、だと思われます！
English: Well, I think... it's here!

Japanese: ... 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: あなたは、自信がないとき、 目がおよぐクセがある。
English: Your eyes have a habit of shifting back and forth when you're uncertain.

Japanese: 地に足をつけて もう一度、考えなおすように。
English: Try taking a deep breath, and think this over once more.
R-right, Your Honor.

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、ここです。
English: As should be obvious, the contradiction is here.

Japanese: エレベータのドアに、 弾痕が残っていますね‥‥。
English: I see... a bullet hole in the door...

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 御剣　信は、ピストルで撃たれて 死んでいる！
English: Gregory Edgeworth was killed by a shot from the pistol!

Japanese: エレベータのドアにも、弾痕が 残っている！
English: Yet, there is also a bullet hole in the elevator door!

Japanese: そして！　凶器のピストルは ２発、弾丸が撃たれている！
English: We also know that the murder weapon was fired twice!

Japanese: つまり！
English: Thus...

Japanese: 御剣以外の誰かが、 ２発目を撃ったことになる！
English: Someone other than Edgeworth fired that second shot!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ どういうことですか！
English: Mr. Wright! What are you driving at?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。
English: It's simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、２発の弾丸が 撃たれました。
English: At the time of the incident, two shots were fired.

Japanese: １発は、御剣　信の心臓を 撃ち抜き‥‥、
English: One went into Gregory Edgeworth's heart.

Japanese: もう１発は、エレベータの ドアに当たった。
English: The other hit the elevator door.

Japanese: しかし被告人は、銃を暴発させた あと、気を失っています。
English: Remember that the defendant lost consciousness after the shot he fired rang out.

Japanese: つまり！
English: In conclusion...

Japanese: ２発目は、“誰か別の人物”が 撃ったとしか考えられない！
English: We must agree that the second shot was fired by someone else!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほど‥‥！ その“誰か別の人物”とは‥‥？
English: M-Mr. Wright! But who could that someone else be!?

Japanese: トーゼン、“真犯人”です！
English: The murderer, of course!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ダマっておればいい気に なりおって‥‥。
English: I knew I should have stepped in before your wild fantasies got out of hand.

Japanese: ‥‥いいか。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の資料を もう一度、よく見たまえ。
English: Look once more at the DL-6 Incident case file. Look closely.

Japanese: “事件概要”のページだ。
English: Try the "Case Summary" page.

Japanese: （“事件概要” ‥‥１ページ目か‥‥）
English: (The "Case Summary"... that's on page 1.)

Japanese: そこに、こう書いてある ではないか！
English: Look what is written there!

Japanese: “現場付近からは、いっさい 手がかりは発見されず”
English: "Not a single clue was found on the scene."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ ...! もしピストルが２発 撃たれたのならば‥‥、
English: If the pistol had indeed been fired two times...

Japanese: 現場付近から、もう１発の 弾丸が発見されているはずだ。
English: Then the other bullet would have been discovered on the scene!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そうですね‥‥。
English: He does have a point.

Japanese: しかし、もう１発の弾丸は 発見されていない！
English: That second bullet has never been found!

Japanese: なぜか！
English: Why?

Japanese: ‥‥２発目の弾丸など、 存在しなかったからだ。
English: Because the second bullet does not exist!

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍によって発射された １発の弾丸が、父親の命を奪った。
English: The bullet that claimed Gregory Edgeworth's life was the one fired by his own son!

Japanese: ‥‥それがすべてなのだ。
English: That is the truth of this matter. The whole truth.

Japanese: ドアの弾痕は、何か他の原因で できたのだろう‥‥。
English: It was undoubtedly something else that made that bullet hole in the door.

Japanese: 静粛にィッ！
English: Order! I will have order!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 成歩堂くんは、ミゴトに 証明しました‥‥。
English: Mr. Wright has proven one thing to us quite clearly...

Japanese: 凶器のピストルは、事件があった とき、２発撃たれたはずだ、と。
English: That the murder weapon was fired twice at the time of the incident.

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: 狩魔検事の言うとおり、２発目の 弾丸は、発見されていません。
English: As Mr. von Karma says, the second bullet fired was not found.

Japanese: 警察の捜査で、弾丸を見落とす ことなど、まずあり得ません。
English: It is highly unlikely that the police merely overlooked this second bullet.

Japanese: 私は、弁護人の証明は、無効と 考えます。
English: So, all we have is the single bullet fired.

Japanese: なぜなら、現場からは、弾丸は １発しか発見されなかった！
English: I'm afraid I have to discount the defense's claim.

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: 賢明な裁定にカンシャする‥‥。
English: I praise the judge for his wisdom in this matter.

Japanese: （な‥‥なんてこった‥‥！）
English: (Gah! How did this happen!?)

Japanese: （２発目の弾丸なんて、 存在しなかったのか‥‥！）
English: (I don't believe that the second bullet didn't exist!)

Japanese: （すべては‥‥）
English: (Was I wrong?)

Japanese: （すべてはぼくの、 カン違いだったのか？）
English: (Have I been wrong about this whole incident?)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん！
English: What are you doing, Nick!?

Japanese: い‥‥異議、申し立てないの？
English: Why aren't you raising an objection!?

Japanese: ... ゴメン、真宵ちゃん。
English: I'm sorry, Maya.

Japanese: え。
English: What?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ツメが 甘かったみたいだ‥‥
English: I... It looks like I was wrong.

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick...?

Japanese: ２発目の弾丸が存在しなければ、 ぼくの推理は成立しない！
English: If the second bullet wasn't there, then all my conjectures are for nothing!

Japanese: そんな‥‥
English: N-no...

Japanese: なるほどくん、言ったじゃない！ かならず、助けるって！
English: But you said you'd do it, Nick! You said you'd get Edgeworth declared innocent!
I'm sorry...

Japanese: 現場写真を見たとき、２発の 弾丸が撃たれてる、って思って。
English: It's just, when I saw the photograph, I thought that two shots had been fired.

Japanese: それで、勝てるって 思い込んじゃったんだ‥‥。
English: I was so certain of it. I thought I'd won!

Japanese: 誰か別の人間が、２発目を撃って 被害者を殺害したんだ、って。
English: I thought there was another person, someone else who fired the killing shot.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But now...

Japanese: そんな、カンタンなハズ ないよな‥‥。
English: I was wrong to think it could be that simple.

Japanese: １５年間、解決されなかった 事件なんだ‥‥。
English: This case has stood unsolved for fifteen years!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥おかげで、事件は ハッキリ整理されたようですね。
English: Well, it seems that we have finally cleared up this incident.

Japanese: 現場から、弾丸は１発しか 発見されていない。
English: Only one bullet was found at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: その１発は、御剣　怜侍が 暴発させたもの‥‥。
English: That shot was fired by Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Precisely.

Japanese: ‥‥最後に、御剣　怜侍に カクニンします。
English: I would like to ask one thing of Miles Edgeworth before passing my verdict.

Japanese: 今までの審理を聞いていましたね？
English: Have you been paying attention to the trial so far?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 何か、異議はありますか？
English: Do you have any objections?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ありません。
English: No... No, I do not.

Japanese: あなたは、殺意はなくとも、 父親を殺害しましたか？
English: So you killed your father, though that was not your intention?
Yes, I did.
Oh no...

Japanese: ‥‥みとめちゃった‥‥
English: He's confessing...
Very well.

Japanese: 御剣　信殺人事件は、本日で 時効が成立します。
English: The statute of limitations on the murder of Gregory Edgeworth runs out today.

Japanese: したがって被告人には、本日中に 判決を下さなければなりません。
English: Therefore, I must pronounce a verdict on the defendant today, right here...

Japanese: 本日と言わず、 “今”おねがいしたい。
English: Right now!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 何か異議のある方は‥‥？
English: Indeed. Does anyone have any objections?

Japanese: （‥‥以前にも こんなことがあった‥‥）
English: (I've been here before...)

Japanese: （たしか、はじめて この席についたときだ‥‥）
English: (It's just like my first day in court...)

Japanese: （言うべきコトは、いくらでも あるはずなのに‥‥）
English: (There are so many things I know I should be saying...)

Japanese: （アタマがまっ白になって‥‥ 何もコトバが見つからない）
English: (But my mind's gone blank, I can't find the words...)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥いかがでしょうか？
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥カンペキに 手がかりをつぶされた‥‥）
English: (Every lead I thought I had has been squashed.)

Japanese: （今度こそ‥‥おしまいだ）
English: (This is really the end.)

Japanese: （‥‥ごめんな‥‥御剣‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth... I'm sorry.)

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくは目を閉じた‥‥
English: I closed my eyes.

Japanese: ‥‥今までやってきたことは なんだったんだろうか‥‥
English: What was it that I had been doing all this time?

Japanese: ‥‥すべては、今日このときの ためだったはずだ‥‥
English: Wasn't my whole life leading up to today?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには、ムリだった‥‥
English: But now that I'm here... I know I've failed.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません‥‥‥千尋さん‥‥
English: Mia... I'm sorry.

Japanese: ‥マダ‥
‥アキラメチャダメ‥
English: ...Not yet...
H-huh?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥？
English: Maya...?

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What?

Japanese: ‥イギ　ヲ‥
English: ...Object!...

Japanese: ‥ベンゴシノ　ブキ‥
English: ...Your only weapon...

Japanese: だ‥‥だいじょうぶ？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: A-are you okay? Nick?

Japanese: （ど‥‥どうなってるんだ‥‥）
English: (What's happening to me...? I have to think...!)

Japanese: （いや‥‥ 考えてるヒマはないな！）
English: (Wait... don't think... act!)

Japanese: 裁判長！ ‥‥その。異議を申し立てます！
English: Your Honor! I... I object!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk tsk tsk.

Japanese: 弁護士。‥‥どこに異議を 差し挟む余地があるのかな？
English: Mr. Wright, on what grounds do you object, hmm?
Oof!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick...?

Japanese: （ダメだ！　モンクの つけようがない‥‥！）
English: (I... I don't know! His case is perfect!) Oh no...

Japanese: ‥‥クソッ！
English: Grah!

Japanese: ‥２ハツメの　“タマ”‥
English: ...it must exist...

Japanese: ‥ソンザイスルハズ‥
English: ...the second bullet...
Wh-what?

Japanese: い‥‥今、何か言わなかった？
English: What did you just say?

Japanese: し‥‥知らないよ！
English: N-nothing!

Japanese: （２発目の“弾丸”が‥‥ 存在する‥‥？）
English: (The second bullet must exist?)

Japanese: （そんなバカな！　いったい、 どこに‥‥！）
English: (But where!?)

Japanese: ‥ダレカガ　モチサッタ‥
English: ...Someone took it...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... どうやら、待つのは ムダのようですね。
English: It seems waiting is not going to produce us any answers from Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 待ってください！
English: Wait, Your Honor!
Hmm?

Japanese: ええと、その‥‥
English: I, uh...

Japanese: に、２発目の弾丸は、 ええと‥‥存在したんです！
English: Th-the second bullet! It, uh, it exists!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: そんなものはなかったと 証明されたばかりですよ！
English: But we've just heard proof that it did not exist!

Japanese: わ、わかってるんですけど‥‥ （‥‥もう、ヤケだ！）
English: I-I realize that, Your Honor. (I'm really grasping here!)

Japanese: その‥‥誰かが‥‥現場から 持って行っちゃったんですよ！
English: I-it's just, someone took it from the scene of the crime! That's what happened!

Japanese: バカな！ ‥‥いったい、誰が‥‥？
English: But... Who!?

Japanese: ハハ、ハンニンですよ犯人！
English: Th-th-the murderer!

Japanese: “犯人”‥‥だと？ いったい、誰のことだ？
English: The murderer? Then tell us, just who is this "murderer"?

Japanese: ‥‥それは今、考え中です。
English: I'm... still thinking about that one.
Hmm...

Japanese: 犯人が“弾丸”を持ち去った？ いったい、なぜですか？
English: So the criminal took the second bullet? Why would he?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: だいいち、どうやって 見つけたんですか？
English: First of all, how would he have found it?

Japanese: 撃った弾丸を探すなんて、 タイヘンな作業ですよ？
English: It's not easy to find a stray bullet, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 犯人には、弾丸を探す 必要があったわけですか？
English: Was there some pressing need for the murderer to search for that bullet?

Japanese: もも、もちろん、必要が あるから、持ち去ったんです！
English: O-of course there was a need. That's why he took it!

Japanese: ハッ！ いったいそれは、どんな理由だ！
English: Bah! What possible reason could he have had!?

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: W-well...

Japanese: 犯人が現場から弾丸を 持ち去った理由は！
English: The reason the murderer took the bullet away from the scene with him is...!

Japanese: たぶん‥‥弾丸が証拠に なると思ったのでは‥‥？
English: Er... Maybe he thought that the bullet would be used as proof?
Proof...?

Japanese: 特別な弾丸だったので、 わざわざ持ち去った‥‥
English: It was a special bullet, so he took it with him...

Japanese: それならば、御剣　信の体内の 弾丸も持ち去ったはずだ！
English: If that was the case, then he would have taken the bullet from inside Gregory as well!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: １発だけ持ち帰るなんて、 不自然ではないか？
English: Why would he only take one of the two shots fired!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。
English: Oh, right.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 もっと、ちゃんと考えなさい！
English: Mr. Wright? Have you really thought this through?

Japanese: ペナルティを与えます！
English: I'm going to have to penalize you!

Japanese: （くそ！　イタいな‥‥）
English: (Argh... This isn't going so well.)

Japanese: ええと‥‥犯人は、用心深い 性格だったんですよ。
English: Uh... Well, the murderer was a very cautious sort, you see.

Japanese: だから、わざわざ弾丸を 探して‥‥
English: That's why the murderer had to search for that bullet...

Japanese: （犯人が、わざわざ弾丸を 持ち去る理由‥‥？）
English: (Why would the murderer have spent the time to look for that stray bullet...?)

Japanese: （そんなの、思いつかないぞ！）
English: (I haven't got a clue!)

Japanese: どうしましたか？　弁護人！
English: What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。
English: Uh... Um...

Japanese: ハッ！　犯人に、弾丸を持ち去る 必要などない！
English: Bah! The murderer had no reason to take that bullet!

Japanese: それを認めたくないのだろう？
English: You don't want to admit it, but it's true!
Urk...

Japanese: ‥ヤムヲエズモチサッタ‥
English: ...had to take it...

Japanese: （やむを得ず‥‥？ どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (Had to take it...? The murderer? What does that mean?)

Japanese: ‥ハッソウヲギャクテンシテ‥
English: ...you're thinking too normal...think crazy...

Japanese: “モチサッタリユウ” デハナク
English: ...don't think why the bullet was taken...

Japanese: “モチサラザルヲエナカッタ” リユウヲカンガエルノ
English: ...think why the bullet HAD to be taken...

Japanese: 成歩堂くん？
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい！ （よくわからないけど‥‥！）
English: Y-yes, Your Honor! (I have no idea what I'm doing...)

Japanese: た、たしかに、犯人に 弾丸を持ち去るつもりはなかった。
English: U-uh, well, the murderer had no intention of taking the bullet from the scene.

Japanese: でも‥‥やむを得ず、 持ち去ったんだと思います。
English: But... uh, the murderer HAD to take that bullet.

Japanese: ‥‥やむを得ず、ですか？ どういうことでしょう？
English: "Had to," Mr. Wright? What do you mean?

Japanese: たとえば、その‥‥ （わかるかよ、そんなの！）
English: Well, for instance... (For instance WHAT!?)

Japanese: たとえば、その弾丸は‥‥ 犯人に当たった、とか。
English: Uh, maybe the bullet, uh... hit the murderer?

Japanese: 弾丸が‥‥犯人に‥‥？
English: The bullet... hit the murderer?

Japanese: いや、たとえば、ですよ。
English: J-just saying, for instance.

Japanese: 自分に当たっちゃったら、 持ち去らざるを得ないでしょう？
English: I mean, if it hit you, you would have to take it with you, wouldn't you?

Japanese: その場で手術するワケにも いかないし。
English: It's not like you could perform surgery right there.

Japanese: ‥‥なーんて‥‥。
English: Y-y'know?
(Wait a second...)

Japanese: （テキトーに言ってみたけど、 これって、あり得るんじゃあ？）
English: (I was just talking off the top of my head, but what if that's really what happened?)

Japanese: つまり、こういうことですか！
English: Let me get this straight.

Japanese: 犯人は、犯行の際、 自分も撃たれてしまった。
English: So at the time of the murder, the murderer himself was shot?

Japanese: そして、自分の体内に２発目の 弾丸を入れたまま立ち去った。
English: And he left with the second bullet still inside!?

Japanese: だから、現場には弾丸が１発しか なかった、と‥‥？
English: Thus leaving only one bullet at the scene of the crime?

Japanese: は、はあ。 ‥‥そうなりますねえ。
English: Uh, yes... I guess that's how it would work, yes.

Japanese: しかし！
English: But there's a problem with that!

Japanese: エレベータから救助された 他の２人‥‥
English: The other two people rescued from that elevator...

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍も、灰根　高太郎も、 ‥‥ケガなどしていなかった！
English: Miles Edgeworth and Yanni Yogi were both unharmed!

Japanese: と、なると‥‥？
English: So that would mean...

Japanese: 犯人は、外部から来たことに ‥‥なっちゃいますね。
English: The murderer came from outside, yes.

Japanese: エレベータの中で、 ２人の男が争っている。
English: The two men fight inside the elevator.

Japanese: 少年は、それを止めようと 足下のピストルを投げつける。
English: Trying to stop them, the boy picks up the pistol at his feet and throws it.

Japanese: ピストルは暴発して、 弾丸は‥‥
English: The pistol discharges, and the bullet...

Japanese: エレベータのドアをつらぬき、 外に立っていた犯人に当たる！
English: The bullet goes through the elevator door and hits the murderer outside!

Japanese: 少年はそこで、 気をうしなってしまう。
English: The boy loses consciousness...

Japanese: その後、犯人はエレベータの ドアを開けて、被害者を‥‥。
English: Then the murderer opens the elevator door and sees the men inside...
Hmm.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。ホントにあなたは フシギな弁護士ですね‥‥。
English: Mr. Wright. You are truly the most unpredictable defense attorney I've ever known.

Japanese: あきらかに苦しまぎれな発言 なのに、なぜかムシできない‥‥
English: I can tell you're grasping, yet I cannot deny the possibility of what you say.

Japanese: ナニを言ってる！ ムシしろ！　裁判長！
English: What are you saying! Deny it! Deny it!

Japanese: 事件の関係者で、ケガをした 人物などいない！
English: No one involved with the incident was wounded!

Japanese: つまり、犯人などいない、 ということではないか！
English: There was no "murderer"!
Hmm...

Japanese: （事件のとき、ケガをした人物 ‥‥か‥‥）
English: (No one was wounded at the time of the incident...)

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、そんな人物は 見当たらないよな‥‥）
English: (He's right. I can't think of anyone...)

Japanese: ね、ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.
Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥とんでもないこと、 考えちゃったんだけど‥‥
English: I just thought of something really crazy.

Japanese: とんでもないこと？
English: Crazy?

Japanese: 昨日の、星影センセイのコトバ、 覚えてる？
English: Remember what Mr. Grossberg said yesterday?

Japanese: 狩魔は、御剣　信によって、その カンペキな経歴をキズつけられた。
English: Gregory Edgeworth dealt a blow to his perfect trial record.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。
English: Wow.

Japanese: そのコトが、よほどショック だったのぢゃろう‥‥、
English: It must have been quite a shock for von Karma.

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、その日から数ヶ月、 休暇をとったほどぢゃ。
English: He took a vacation for several months after that, you see.

Japanese: 長い検事生活で、狩魔が “休暇”をとったのは、
English: Yes, an unusual event for the man.

Japanese: それが最初で‥‥そして、 最後ぢゃったよ。
English: That was the first, and the last vacation he's taken in his many years of prosecuting.

Japanese: もしかして、カルマ検事が お休みしたのって‥‥
English: What if von Karma didn't take that vacation because of shock...

Japanese: “ケガ”をしたから‥‥か！
English: But took it because he was injured!

Japanese: と、いうことは‥‥！
English: Which would mean...

Japanese: ハンニンは‥‥
English: It could only mean one thing!

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の真犯人は‥‥
English: He was the murderer in the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: 御剣　信を撃ったのは‥‥
English: He was the man who shot Gregory Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥カルマ検事なの！
English: It was... von Karma!

Japanese: （なんてこった‥‥！）
English: (Oh man!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。どうしました？ フヌケたカオをして‥‥。
English: Something wrong, Mr. Wright? You seem... dazed.

Japanese: い‥‥いえ、べつに。
English: Uh, n-no, Your Honor.

Japanese: いかがですか？
English: Well?

Japanese: あなたは、外部の人間が 犯人である可能性を示した。
English: You have indicated the possibility that the murderer came from outside.

Japanese: 具体的に、容疑者の名前を 挙げることができますか？
English: Can you give us the name of your suspect?

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥。 今、言うべきか‥‥！）
English: (Uh oh. Should I come out and say it now!?)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠がない‥‥）
English: (Wait... I don't have enough proof yet.)

Japanese: （“切り札”は、最後まで とっておくものだ‥‥）
English: (This is my trump card, I'd better save it for the right time.)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん？ どうしました？
English: Mr. Wright? Something the matter?

Japanese: いえ。なんでもありません。 ‥‥審理を進めてください。
English: I-I'm fine, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥ では、審理を進めてもらおうか。
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Shall we carry on with the trial then, hmm?

Japanese: しかし、もう進むべき道はない。 あるのは、ゴールだけ。
English: That said, we have no farther to go. All that is left... is the finish.

Japanese: ‥‥すなわち、“判決”だ。
English: In other words... the verdict!

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: Wh-what! Not yet!

Japanese: 考えてみたまえ。
English: Think, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: キサマは、“外部の人間が犯人だ” などと言い出した。
English: You have said that someone from the "outside" was the murderer.

Japanese: しかし、それに当たる容疑者を 挙げることができない。
English: Yet you cannot suggest anyone as a possible suspect!

Japanese: ‥‥とすれば。その推理は、 意味がないということだ。
English: Which means your conjecture... is worthless.

Japanese: 当然、却下されることになる。
English: And will be rejected. Of course.

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: “切り札”なんてキドってる 場合じゃないよ。
English: Now's no time to be holding on to that trump card!

Japanese: 終わっちゃうじゃない、 サイバン。
English: The trial's almost over!

Japanese: わ、わかった！ イキオイで追いつめてみるよ！
English: A-alright! I may not know what I'm doing... but here goes!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 容疑者は存在します！ ‥‥たった１人だけ‥‥。
English: There is a suspect... one lone suspect!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それはおもしろい。
English: Well, this is certainly interesting news.

Japanese: ‥‥では、 聞かせてもらいましょう。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 誰ですか？　その人物は。
English: Who is your suspect?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥キンチョー するなあ‥‥）
English: (Urk! My h-hands are shaking!)
V-what?

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事です！
English: von Karma!

Japanese: か‥‥‥、狩魔検事だとッ！
English: von Karma!?
You mean, THE von Karma? The prosecutor? Sitting right there? Bah. You... don't object? Hmph. I see no need. Why honor this ridiculous outburst with my objection?

Japanese: 事件の翌日から数ヶ月、 あなたは休暇をとっている！
English: Because you took a vacation for several months starting the day after the incident!

Japanese: “カンペキな経歴”を よしとするあなたが、
English: Yet you pride yourself on a perfect record!

Japanese: 理由もなく休暇をとるハズがない！
English: Why would you take such a long vacation without any reason!?

Japanese: ‥‥事件の際に負った ケガの治療をしたと言うのか‥‥。
English: So you're claiming that I took a vacation to heal my "injury" from the incident?

Japanese: おもしろい。
English: Fascinating!

Japanese: では、証明してみたまえ。
English: Prove it.

Japanese: ワガハイが、いつ、どこで 治療を受けたのか？
English: I would have needed surgery, no? Where did I go under the knife at, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 手術した医者をつれて来たまえ！
English: Bring the doctor that operated on me! Have him testify!
Urk...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: かかりつけのお医者さんとか、 調べてもらおうよ！
English: Let's find out who his doctor is!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダだ。
English: It's no use.

Japanese: み‥‥御剣！
English: E-Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ‥‥私は、狩魔検事という 男を、よく知っている。
English: I know von Karma. Perhaps too well.

Japanese: 彼は、手がかりを残さない、 “カンペキ”な男なのだ。
English: He's perfect. He wouldn't leave clues.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、手術など 受けていないだろう。
English: He probably didn't undergo surgery.

Japanese: “医者”という弱点が できてしまうからな‥‥。
English: That would leave a doctor as a witness.

Japanese: （‥‥く‥‥ ここまできたってのに‥‥！）
English: (Grr... Nobody's that perfect!)

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、なるほどくん！
English: So... so what, Nick?

Japanese: カルマ検事、自分で弾丸を ほじくり出したの？
English: Did von Karma pull the bullet out by himself!?

Japanese: そんなランボーな‥‥。
English: That's insane!

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: No... he couldn't have.

Japanese: シロートに、弾丸なんか 摘出できるワケないよ！
English: You can't just pull bullets out of yourself!
(Wait...)
(What does that mean...?)

Japanese: （弾丸は‥‥いったい。 今、どこにあるんだ‥‥？）
English: (That bullet has to be somewhere! But... where?)

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥ さて、弁護士！
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Well, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: どうかね。ワガハイが撃たれた ことを、証明できるかね？
English: Can you produce evidence to prove that I was shot?

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔がもし、手術を 受けていたとしても‥‥）
English: (It would be impossible to find the doctor who operated on von Karma now...)

Japanese: （今から、その医者を探し出す なんて、不可能だ‥‥！）
English: (Even if von Karma did undergo surgery!)

Japanese: フン！　‥‥どうやら、 わかったらしいな。
English: Hmph! It seems you have finally come to your senses.

Japanese: ‥‥証明など、 不可能だということを‥‥。
English: You've realized the truth: You can't prove something that didn't happen!
(Urk!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: ‥“ホウテイキロク”ヲ‥
English: ...the Court Record...

Japanese: ‥ナニカ　アルハズ‥
English: ...there must be something...

Japanese: （法廷記録だって‥‥！ いったい、何があるってんだ？）
English: (The Court Record...? What could be in there!?)

Japanese: （考えている時間はない！ ‥‥ナリユキにまかせるか！）
English: (Gah! There's no time! I'd better think on it as I go...)

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。 証明してあげましょう。
English: Alright, von Karma, I'll prove it.

Japanese: あなたたちが大好きな “証拠品”でね‥‥。
English: And I'll even use evidence... I know how you like it so much.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 狩魔検事が撃たれたことを 証明するのは‥‥！
English: The evidence that proves von Karma was shot is...!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだこれは？
English: What exactly is that?

Japanese: シツレイしました。
English: Er... Sorry.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥どう思うかね。
English: Judge, if you would?

Japanese: は。ペナルティを 与えましょう‥‥。
English: Right. Penalty!

Japanese: （このガケップチのタタカイは いつまでつづくんだ‥‥）
English: (I'm really not sure how I've managed to make it this far...)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥考えなおしなさい。
English: Mr. Wright, please reconsider your evidence.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: “狩魔検事はカンペキな男だ。 手術などしない‥‥”
English: von Karma is perfect... He wouldn't risk surgery, leaving an evidence trail.

Japanese: ‥‥もしそうならば、今、その 弾丸はどこにあるのか？
English: So then, I ask, where is that bullet now?

Japanese: 狩魔検事に、自分を手術する 技術はありません。
English: I think it unlikely that von Karma performed surgery on himself!
You... you don't mean!

Japanese: ‥‥そう。
English: I do.

Japanese: まだ、彼の体内に残っている 可能性があります！
English: There is the possibility that the bullet is still inside von Karma!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなバカなことが ありますか！
English: I-is that even possible? For all these years!?

Japanese: バカかどうか‥‥ カンタンにわかることです。
English: Well, there's one way to find out.

Japanese: この“金属探知機”を使えば！
English: We could use this metal detector!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。いいですね。
English: Well, von Karma?

Japanese: これからコイツで、あなたを 調べさせてもらいます！
English: I'm going to run this over you, and see what we find.
I... refuse!

Japanese: こ‥‥‥‥断るんですって！
English: Y-you refuse...!?

Japanese: い、いったい、なぜ！ 断るということは‥‥
English: But, refusing this means...

Japanese: 弾丸が体内にあることを “認める”のと同じですよ！
English: You acknowledge that the bullet is still inside you?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 裁判長！　弁護側は、 検査を主張します！
English: Your Honor! The defense requests that we be allowed to use the metal detector!

Japanese: 裁判長！　休廷を提案する！ これは、人権の侵害だ！
English: Judge! I call for a suspension of this trial! This is an invasion of privacy!

Japanese: この事件は、本日が時効です！
English: The statute of limitations runs out on this case today!

Japanese: 今日中に判決を下すべきだと 言ったのは、あなたです！
English: It was you who said we had to end it right here, right now!
Mm... mmmph!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ‥‥“金属探知機”の使用を 許可します。
English: I permit the use of the metal detector.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。検査を受けるように！
English: Mr. von Karma, you will submit yourself to testing!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥どうなの‥‥？
English: Nick! What does this mean...?

Japanese: わからない。 ‥‥とにかく、やってみよう‥‥！
English: I don't know... but we have to give it a shot!
*beep* *beep* *beeeeeep*

Japanese: （‥‥右の肩に、反応した‥‥！）
English: (It reacted! Something's inside his right shoulder!)

Japanese: （やはり‥‥、弾丸が‥‥！）
English: (The bullet!)
Mr. von Karma...?
You!

Japanese: あなただったんですか‥‥！
English: It was you!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥こうなることを 恐れたゆえ、
English: I was afraid this would happen.

Japanese: ワガハイはあえて、 ダマっておったのだ‥‥。
English: And so, I remained silent.

Japanese: ‥‥？ ...? たしかに、ワガハイの肩には 弾丸が残っておる。
English: Indeed, there is a bullet in my shoulder.

Japanese: しかし、それは事件とは マッタク関係がない。
English: However... it has nothing to do with this incident!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: What!?

Japanese: 肩の弾丸は、ＤＬ６号事件より 以前に受けたものだ。
English: I was shot in the shoulder long before the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: ワガハイは“事件とは関係ない”と 主張する！
English: I claim that the bullet in my shoulder has no relation to DL-6!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、狩魔検事！ それを‥‥証明できるのですか？
English: B-but, Mr. von Karma! Can you prove that?

Japanese: ‥‥“証明”だと？
English: "Prove"?

Japanese: ワガハイにそんなギムはない。
English: I have no obligation to prove anything!

Japanese: ‥‥証明しなければならないのは、 キサマの方だ、弁護士。
English: It is you who must prove something here, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥ワガハイではない。
English: Not I.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: M-Mr. Wright?

Japanese: どうですか？ 証明できるのですか？
English: Well? Can you prove it?

Japanese: 狩魔検事の肩の弾丸は、 ＤＬ６号事件によるものだ、と？
English: Can you prove that the bullet in von Karma's shoulder was from DL-6?

Japanese: ‥‥できるわけがない。
English: Of course he can't!

Japanese: キサマらには、ＤＬ６号事件の 資料など、ないのだからな！
English: You don't have any of the DL-6 evidence!

Japanese: （‥‥昨日、あわてて資料室から 持ち出したクセに‥‥）
English: (That's because you took it out of the Records Room yesterday!)

Japanese: ‥‥証拠がない以上、ワガハイを 罪に問うことは不可能だ。
English: With no proof, you cannot convict me of any crime!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、な。
English: So sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ザンネンなのはアンタの方だ、 ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: No... I'm the one who's sorry, Mr. von Karma.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと？
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 惜しかったね。 ‥‥あと、もう１日だったのに。
English: You were close... one day away from freedom.

Japanese: あるんだよ‥‥証拠ならな！
English: You see... I have proof!

Japanese: な‥‥なにッ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 御剣を有罪にするために、 自分で墓穴を掘るとはね‥‥。
English: Who would have thought you would have dug your own grave trying to convict Edgeworth!

Japanese: あんたの肩の弾丸と、 ＤＬ６号事件を結びつける‥‥
English: I can link that bullet in your shoulder to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: これが、最後の証拠だ！
English: And here's my final proof!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥。
English: N-Nick...?

Japanese: 最後ぐらい、カッコよく キメてよお‥‥。
English: Umm... If this is your final proof, don't you think you should choose a little better?

Japanese: 違ったかな？　今の証拠？
English: Huh? You mean, that was the wrong evidence?

Japanese: みんな、妙にケワしいカオ してるけど‥‥。
English: Everyone looks kinda puzzled...

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.
(Uh oh, he's really mad.)

Japanese: はやくやりなおした方が よさそうだよ‥‥！
English: I think you'd better dig out the right evidence, and quick!

Japanese: わわ、わかった。
English: R-right!

Japanese: ‥‥ラッキーだったな、狩魔検事。 しかし、次はハズさない！
English: You got lucky, von Karma, but this next one is the real evidence, okay!?

Japanese: あんたの肩の弾丸と、 ＤＬ６号事件と結びつける‥‥
English: I can link that bullet in your shoulder to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Th-that's...

Japanese: 弾丸‥‥ですか？
English: A bullet?

Japanese: キ‥‥キサマッ！ どうしてそれを‥‥ッ！
English: Where did you get that!?

Japanese: これは、ＤＬ６号事件で 使用された弾丸です。
English: This is the bullet used in the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: 被害者の御剣　信の心臓から 摘出されました。
English: This was taken from the heart of the victim, Mr. Gregory Edgeworth.

Japanese: 保存状態は良好で、線条痕も ハッキリ残っています。
English: The bullet is preserved quite nicely, with all the ballistic markings intact.
Ballistic markings...

Japanese: ‥‥今から２日前。
English: You may recall the term.

Japanese: 最初の法廷でも、線条痕の 話が出ましたね‥‥。
English: It came up in the first trial, two days ago.

Japanese: 線条痕というのは、ピストルの “指紋”みたいなものです。
English: Ballistic markings are the fingerprints of a weapon.

Japanese: ピストルで撃たれた弾丸には 必ず、銃身によるキズがつきます。
English: All bullets fired from a gun are marked with that weapon's unique pattern.

Japanese: そのキズを調べれば、弾丸が どのピストルから撃たれたかが、
English: By examining the markings, you can tell which weapon fired the bullet.

Japanese: ハッキリ特定できるのです。
English: It's quite accurate.

Japanese: ここに、２つの弾丸があります。
English: We have two bullets in our possession.

Japanese: 御剣　信の心臓から摘出された、 この弾丸。
English: One, the bullet removed from Gregory Edgeworth's heart.

Japanese: そして、狩魔検事。 あなたの肩に埋まっている弾丸。
English: The other, Mr. von Karma, is the bullet buried in your shoulder.

Japanese: ２つの弾丸の線条痕を 調べた結果‥‥、
English: We could analyze both bullets...

Japanese: もし、その線条痕が一致したら、
English: Then, if the markings matched...

Japanese: ２つの弾丸は、同じピストルから 発射されたことが証明されます！
English: We would know that both bullets had been fired from the same gun!

Japanese: 同じピストル‥‥すなわち、 御剣　信を撃った“凶器”です！
English: The very same pistol... in other words, the murder weapon that killed Gregory Edgeworth!

Japanese: う‥‥ぐぐ‥‥‥‥むむむ‥‥
English: Mmmph... mmmph!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: Mr. von Karma?

Japanese: その肩に埋まっている弾丸を 摘出してください。
English: You will let us remove the bullet from your shoulder.

Japanese: そして‥‥この弾丸の線条痕と 比べてみましょう。
English: Then we'll compare the ballistic markings to those on this bullet...

Japanese: それで‥‥この事件は解決です！
English: And solve this case once and for all!

Japanese: いかがですか！　狩魔検事！
English: Well, Mr. von Karma!?
Mmmrrrrrrgggh!

Japanese: うぐおおおおおおおおおおおおお をををををををををををををッ！
English: Uuaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: おおおおお‥‥おお‥‥おおおおお ヲヲお‥‥‥‥おおヲヲヲヲヲ‥‥
English: Uaaaaarrrr... aaa...oooh. Worrrrrrrggg... aaa...oooooh...
That scream...

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで聞いたことのある‥‥
English: I've heard that scream before...

Japanese: ‥‥‥そうだ‥‥あのときだ‥‥‥
English: Wait... I know!

Japanese: 「助けてくれッ！　い、息が‥‥」
English: "H-help! I can't breathe!"

Japanese: 「うるさい！　ダマるんだ！ ‥‥こっちまでおかしくなる！」
English: "Quiet! I said quiet! You're not making this any easier!"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うな！ い‥‥息の根を止めてやる！」
English: "Stop breathing my air! I'll... I'll stop you!"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うなァァァァ！」
English: "Stop breathing my aaaaair!"

Japanese: （ぼくのおとうさんから‥‥）
English: (Get away...!)

Japanese: （おとうさんから、 はなれろぉっ‥‥！）
English: (Get away from my father!)
*BANG*

Japanese: うぐおおおおおおおおおおおおお をををををををををををををッ！
English: Uuaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: あのときの悲鳴だ‥‥。
English: It's that scream I heard in the elevator!

Japanese: ‥‥１５年前。 私がエレベータで聞いたのは‥‥
English: Fifteen years ago...

Japanese: 狩魔検事！ あなたの悲鳴だったのか！
English: von Karma! It was you who screamed!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事‥‥？
English: Mr. von Karma?

Japanese: おおおヲヲおおおおおおヲヲヲおお おヲヲヲおおおヲヲヲヲヲおおおお
English: Wooorrrrrrroooorrgggh

Japanese: ‥ミツルギィィィィぃぃぃ‥‥‥‥ ‥‥‥‥ミツルギィィィィぃぃぃ‥
English: Eh...Edgewoooooorrth......... ...Ehh...Edgewoooooorrth......

Japanese: マタシテモ‥‥ワガハイニ‥‥ ‥‥タテツクトハ‥‥‥‥
English: Only...you would... dare...defy me...!

Japanese: やはり‥‥あなたが‥‥！
English: So... it was you!

Japanese: ‥‥親子そろって‥‥ 目ざわりなヤツらだ‥‥
English: You and your father are my curse!

Japanese: ワガハイの経歴に“処罰”の ドロをぬったオヤジに‥‥
English: Your father shamed me with a penalty on my record!

Japanese: ワガハイの肩に、生涯消えぬ 傷痕を刻んだムスコ‥‥
English: And you... you left a scar on my shoulder that would never fade!

Japanese: ‥‥この手で葬ってやるうぅぅ‥‥ 死刑台に送ってやるぞおぉぉ‥‥
English: I... I'll bury you! I'll bury you with my bare hands! Death! Death!
Fifteen years earlier...

Japanese: 検事局長。‥‥もうしわけない。
English: Mr. Chief Prosecutor... I am sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔くん。キミらしくもない ヘマをしたものだ‥‥。
English: von Karma! It's not like you to make this kind of error...

Japanese: あのミツルギ弁護士に、 シッポをつかまれるとはね‥‥。
English: I never would have thought that Edgeworth would be the one to catch you.

Japanese: ‥‥油断‥‥したようだ‥‥。
English: I... was careless.

Japanese: ザンネンだが、キミには 処罰を受けてもらうよ。
English: I'm sorry, but you will have to be penalized.

Japanese: さすがに、今回ばかりは かばうわけにもいかないのでね。
English: I've covered for you in the past, but not this time.
Edgeworth!!!

Japanese: ‥‥味わったことのない ショックだった。
English: It was a shock like none I had ever known.

Japanese: このワガハイが、 処罰されるなど‥‥
English: Me? Penalized!?

Japanese: 精神の落ちつきを取りもどすのに、 数時間かかった‥‥。
English: It took hours for me to regain my composure.

Japanese: ‥‥気がつくとワガハイは、 闇の中にいた。
English: Suddenly, I found myself in the darkness...

Japanese: そこは、裁判所の資料室だった。
English: I was in the court records room.

Japanese: いつの間にか、そんなところに 来ていたらしい。
English: I must have wandered in there without thinking where I was going.

Japanese: 部屋の中は真っ暗だった。 ‥‥どうやら停電しているようだ。
English: The room was pitch black. The lights must have gone out.

Japanese: 廊下に出て、手さぐりで エレベータの前に出た。
English: I went out in the hall and felt my way to the elevator.

Japanese: エレベータのボタンを押す。 ‥‥しかし、反応がない。
English: I pressed the button, and nothing happened.

Japanese: 次の瞬間‥‥。
English: Then... there was a noise!

Japanese: 何が起こった‥‥！ ‥‥肩に焼きつく、この痛み‥‥
English: I was in pain! A horrible, burning pain in my shoulder!

Japanese: そのとき‥‥電力が回復した。 目の前で、ドアが開く。
English: Just then, the lights came back on. The elevator door opened before my eyes.

Japanese: ３人の人間が見えた。 ‥‥彼らは、酸欠で気絶していた。
English: I saw three people inside, all lying unconscious from oxygen deprivation.

Japanese: フシギなことに、足下に ピストルが転がっていた。
English: Much to my surprise, a pistol lay at my feet.

Japanese: ‥‥これは運命なのだ‥‥ ワガハイはそう知った。
English: I knew then... it was destiny.

Japanese: 最後の瞬間、御剣　信は 気絶していた。
English: In his last moments, Gregory Edgeworth was still unconscious.

Japanese: ヤツは、誰に撃たれたかも 知らぬまま、死んだのだ。
English: He died, never knowing who had shot him.

Japanese: 後日、霊媒師の口を介して 灰根を告発したようだが‥‥。
English: Later, he spoke through a medium, blaming Mr. Yogi.

Japanese: ワガハイは、被害者自身をも カンペキにあざむいてやったのだ！
English: He was fooled! It was the perfect crime!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: あのエレベータのトビラを、 ふたたび開ける男が現れるとはな。
English: Who would have thought another man would have come to open that elevator door?

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Judge!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Wh-what?

Japanese: 何をしておる！
English: What are you doing!?

Japanese: このささやかなゲームに幕を引く のは、キサマの役目だろう！
English: Do your job! Bring an end to this miserable charade!

Japanese: 今がそのときだ！ さっさと、幕を引くのだ！
English: Now! End it!

Japanese: わ‥‥わかりました‥‥。
English: V-very well.

Japanese: 私たちはどうやら、迷宮の出口に たどりついたようです。
English: It appears that we have come a very long way to the end of this maze.

Japanese: １５年の歳月の涯てに‥‥。
English: Fifteen years later...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Mr. Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたは、無実だった。
English: You were innocent. You are innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ ... すべては、“悪夢”に すぎなかったのです。
English: As you said, it was all a "nightmare."
Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に対する判決を 言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２８日　午後５時３８分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 28, 5:38 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: なるほどくん！　なるほどくん！ ついにやったね！
English: Nick! Nick! We did it!

Japanese: 見た？　見た？　カルマ検事の しみったれたカオ。
English: Did you see his face!? von Karma looked even paler than usual!

Japanese: あれ、ヘーキなフリしてるけど、 心の中はドシャ降りだよ！
English: He's pretending to be all cool, but inside you crushed him, Nick! Crushed!

Japanese: いいキミだねー。いやまったく。
English: I gotta say, I'm impressed.

Japanese: ううう‥‥それにしても あぶなかった。
English: Heh... It was pretty close, though.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、ゼッタイ ダメだと思ったよ‥‥。
English: I was sure we'd had it.

Japanese: そうだね。あたしも、 ずーっと半泣きだったよ。
English: I know, I was on the verge of tears the whole time, myself.

Japanese: ま。今となっては イイ思い出だけどね！
English: But now it's all just a good memory!
So, it's finally over, Edgeworth.
Wright.

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥その‥‥、 なんと言えばいいのか‥‥。
English: I... I'm not sure how to say this.

Japanese: そういうときはね。 “ありがとう”って言うんだよ！
English: I know! I know! Try "thank you."

Japanese: そ、そうなのか。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あ、ありがとう、成歩堂。
English: Th-thank you, Wright.

Japanese: ど、どういたしまして。
English: Y-you're welcome.

Japanese: うーん‥‥イマイチ、 ハリアイがないなあ‥‥。
English: I think you could have done better than that!

Japanese: ム‥‥スマン。 こういうの、ニガテで‥‥。
English: Oof! S-sorry... I'm not good at this sort of thing.

Japanese: もう！　ダメだなあ、御剣検事は。
English: You got a lot to learn, Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Dear, dear...)

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Whoooooooooooooop!

Japanese: さすがッス！　自分が見込んだ だけのコトはあったッス！
English: Amazing, pal! You pulled through just like I thought you would!

Japanese: 糸鋸　圭介。この恩は一生、 わすれないッス！
English: I'll never forget this! I owe you one, pal.

Japanese: 今夜は、パーッとやるッス！ 自分がゴチソウするッス！
English: And tonight, let's party! Dinner's on me!

Japanese: 今月から給料がちょっぴり 下がったッスが、気にしねッス！
English: Yeah, my salary went down a bit this month... But who cares!

Japanese: ほらほらほら、御剣検事。 イトノコ刑事を見ならうの！
English: See, Mr. Edgeworth? You should take a lesson from Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: あんな感じにやると、カンシャの キモチが伝わるんだよ！
English: That's how you say "thank you"!

Japanese: ‥‥ム。 な‥‥なるほど。
English: Mmm. I... I see.

Japanese: コホン。
English: *ahem*

Japanese: う、うおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Whooooooooooooop!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ダメだ。‥‥ヤッパリ、 私なんか‥‥。
English: I... I feel foolish.

Japanese: まあまあ、少しずつ 慣れていきましょうよ。
English: Don't worry. Take it a little at a time. You'll get used to it.

Japanese: （こんなスナオな御剣を 見るの、１５年ぶりだな‥‥）
English: (It's been fifteen years since I've seen Edgeworth this... unguarded.)

Japanese: おっ、いたいた！
English: Hey, y'all!

Japanese: あ、ナツミさん！
English: Lotta!

Japanese: いやー、アンタら。 カッコよかったでー。
English: Y'all were great in there!

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you!

Japanese: おッ。ニイちゃん。 無罪、おめでと！
English: Yo, Edgeworth! Congrats!

Japanese: ム‥‥お、おおきに。
English: Er... thank y'all very much.

Japanese: ウチには、最初から わかってたんやで！
English: I knew you were innocent from the start, of course.

Japanese: あんたが無罪だ、ってなあ！
English: Just look at you! You wouldn't stick your hand in the cookie jar even if no one was there!

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、初日の 検察側の証人だったような‥‥。
English: You... were the witness on the first day of the trial, weren't you.

Japanese: まま。まあまあ。 細かいこと、気にしなさんな！
English: Yeah, well, let bygones be bygones, eh?

Japanese: そういえば、ナツミさん‥‥、 今は、何を‥‥？
English: Speaking of which, what are you doing now, Lotta?

Japanese: おー、今か？ 大学にもどったで。
English: Who, me? Aww, I went back to college.

Japanese: スクープカメラマンになる夢は、 もうオシマイや。
English: I gave up trying to be an investigative photographer pretty quick.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: Really? That's too bad.

Japanese: あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ？ ? あそこにいるの、公園の マンジュウ屋、ちゃうか？
English: Isn't that the hotdog guy from the park?
Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥死ぬんだぁ‥‥。
English: It's over, Nick! My life is over!

Japanese: な、なんでおまえが そんなカオしてるんだよ！
English: Wh-why the sad face, Larry!? What happened now!?

Japanese: 成歩堂ぉ‥‥。 オレ、そろそろ死ぬからさぁ。
English: Oh, Nick... I'm not long for this world.

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: Uh... you don't look sick...

Japanese: カズミがよお‥‥パリで 暮らすって言い出して‥‥、
