Japanese: フン‥‥！　検事局のボウヤが アタマのいいトコを見せたワケ？
English: Very good, Mr. Prosecutor... I suppose you think you're clever now?

Japanese: でもね。あの女はアキラカに やっつけるつもりだったのさ！
English: But you know as well as I do that she planned on killing him! It was planned!

Japanese: それでなければ、あんな‥‥
English: If she wasn't, why would she have been wearing...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥その先は、証言して いただいたほうがいいようです。
English: I believe I'd like to hear your testimony again.

Japanese: 証人。“見たことだけ”‥‥ おねがいしよう。
English: Witness, please tell us only what you "saw," not what you "thought."

Japanese: おだまりなさいな。
English: How dare you!

Japanese: おキョウのスイリは、ボウヤの目玉 ５０コぶんのビタミンがあるのさ！
English: My powers of deduction are not to be underestimated!

Japanese: （コワいなあ‥‥）
English: (Really now...)

Japanese: ～おキョウのスイリ～
English: -- Angel's Deduction --

Japanese: 宝月　巴は、多田敷捜査官を 殺害するつもりだったのさ！
English: Lana Skye intended to murder Detective Goodman!

Japanese: そのために、わざわざ被害者を 検事局に呼び出したんだからねぇ。
English: That's why she called the victim all the way to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 主席検事サマは、被害者に かなりのウラミがあったハズ。
English: I'm sure the Chief Prosecutor had a grudge against the victim.

Japanese: そうでなきゃ、あの人間マシーンが 何度もナイフを使うはずがない！
English: Nothing else could drive that human machine to plunge the knife in again and again...

Japanese: ‥‥警察局にいたはずの被害者が、 検事局に呼び出された‥‥
English: The victim was summoned from the Police Department to the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: たしかに、そこにはある種の “計画性”が感じられます！
English: It does sound a lot like premeditation, doesn't it!?

Japanese: そんなコト言ってたら、ラーメンの 出前もたのめませんよねー。
English: So, if I order pizza, does that mean I'm planning to kill the delivery boy?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします！
English: In any case, the defense may now cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～おキョウのスイリ～
English: -- Angel's Deduction --

Japanese: 宝月　巴は、多田敷捜査官を 殺害するつもりだったのさ！
English: Lana Skye intended to murder Detective Goodman!

Japanese: そのために、わざわざ被害者を 検事局に呼び出したんだからねぇ。
English: That's why she called the victim all the way to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 主席検事サマは、被害者に かなりのウラミがあったハズ。
English: I'm sure the Chief Prosecutor had a grudge against the victim.

Japanese: そうでなきゃ、あの人間マシーンが 何度もナイフを使うはずがない！
English: Nothing else could drive that human machine to plunge the knife in again and again...

Japanese: （おキョウさん‥‥見た目どおり 短気だったみたいだな）
English: (Ms. Starr has turned out to be as short-tempered as she looked when we met her.)

Japanese: （捜査官のプライドがキズついて、 スキだらけだ‥‥！）
English: (Challenging her abilities as a detective really set her off!)

Japanese: “短気はソン気”‥‥ カガク的にも証明されています！
English: The short wick burns out the fastest! It's a scientific fact!

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、 前から思ってたんだけど‥‥
English: I wonder... wouldn't it depend on the size of the candle?

Japanese: “短気”はわかるんだけど。 なんなんだろ。“ソン気”って。
English: I mean, add more wax and even a really short wick will burn longer...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 世の中には、カガクでは 解明できないコトもあります！
English: Obviously, more scientific testing is required!

Japanese: 彼女の赤いマフラーが、血に見えた ‥‥それほど鬼気迫ってましたわ。
English: Her red muffler looked like blood to me... that's how ghastly the whole scene was.

Japanese: だから、どうして それがわかるんですか！
English: You've said that, but you haven't told us how you know!

Japanese: だから、それをこれから 話すんじゃないか！
English: That's what I'm about to tell you, Rookie!

Japanese: 今のは、話の“マクラ”と 言うヤツでしょう。
English: I believe what she just said was a mere prelude to the story she is about to tell.

Japanese: ダマって聞いておくように。
English: Try not to interrupt her again.

Japanese: ボウヤ‥‥いっそ、おキョウの ひざマクラで眠らせてやろうかッ！
English: Rookie... Never interrupt a storyteller! It's like pulling a bun out of the oven half-baked!

Japanese: （‥‥タンカになってないような）
English: (Something's half-baked here alright, and it's you!)

Japanese: その弁護人なら、自分の ひじマクラでじゅうぶんだろう。
English: Try not to confuse the defense, witness. They're not very quick on their feet.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、被告人に殺意があると 考えたのか？　理由をうかがおう。
English: Now, why did you believe the suspect had intentions to murder the victim?

Japanese: 被告人の行動を見ればわかるさ。
English: Her actions speak for themselves!

Japanese: ともえさんが呼び出したという 証拠はありません！
English: You have no proof that Ms. Skye called him there!

Japanese: 呼び出していないという 証拠もございませんのよ。
English: You have no proof that she didn't!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥御剣検事。 いかがですか？
English: Hmm... Mr. Edgeworth, thoughts?

Japanese: 被告人‥‥宝月主席検事の 携帯電話には、通話の記録はない。
English: There is no record of a call made on the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye's phone.

Japanese: 文通をしていたのかも しれなくてよ。
English: She might have written him a letter!

Japanese: （せめて“公衆電話”ぐらい 言ってほしいな‥‥）
English: (C'mon! You could have tried "public phone" first, at least!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥事件当日。被害者は 検事局を訪れ、殺害された。
English: In any case, the victim came to the Prosecutor's Office, where he was murdered.

Japanese: 何か“理由”があった はず、でしょうなあ。
English: I'm sure he had a reason to be there.

Japanese: 証人。被害者を呼びだしたのが、 なぜ被告人だと思うのですか？
English: Witness? Why do you think it was the suspect who summoned the victim that day?

Japanese: どんな“ウラミ”だと 言うんですか！
English: What kind of "grudge"!?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 そこまでは知りませんわ。
English: Well, I wouldn't know that.

Japanese: “さあ”じゃありませんよ！
English: Of course you don't! That's because she didn't have a grudge!

Japanese: では‥‥ボウヤ。
English: Rookie...

Japanese: ここにお弁当がございます。 ‥‥オカズは何かしら？
English: I have a lunchbox here. Now... what's inside?

Japanese: わ、わかるワケ、ないでしょう！
English: H-how am I supposed to know!?

Japanese: そう、お弁当はたしかに存在する。 ‥‥でも、中身まではわからない。
English: See? We agree there is a lunchbox here, but we don't know what's inside!

Japanese: 人生は、シャケ弁当のようなもの。 ‥‥そうは思いませんコト？
English: A person's life is like a lunchbox with pretzels. Don't you agree?

Japanese: な、なるほど！　だからシャケは こんなにも、しょっぱいのですね！
English: I-I get it! That's why my lunch was so salty!

Japanese: （たとえ話にヨワいんだよなあ、 あの裁判長‥‥）
English: (This judge isn't very good with metaphors...)

Japanese: 被告人が、多田敷捜査官を ウラんでいた‥‥
English: The suspect had a grudge against Detective Goodman.

Japanese: そう考えた“コンキョ”を 聞かせていただこうか。
English: Will you tell us your basis for thinking this?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコト‥‥
English: It's simple...

Japanese: “人間”で“マシーン”‥‥ 証人の発言はムジュンしているッ！
English: A "human machine"!? That's a contradiction!

Japanese: ふう‥‥
English: Please...

Japanese: もう少し、マシなケチを おねがいできないだろうか。
English: Can't you find fault with something of substance, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事のタメイキは シトラスの香り、と。
English: Note to self: Mr. Edgeworth's sighs smell like citrus fruit.

Japanese: ええと‥‥“何度も”というのは、 具体的に何回ですか？
English: Umm... You say "again and again"... how many times did she stab him, exactly?

Japanese: ‥‥キャベツの千切りといって、 １０００回切るワケじゃない‥‥
English: We often say "chop into a thousand pieces," but we don't actually mean 1,000 pieces.

Japanese: セイカクな回数になんの イミがあるのさッ！
English: What difference does it make if the deed is done!?

Japanese: （なんでこっちが 怒られるんだよ‥‥）
English: (How come she's getting mad at me!?)

Japanese: ま。とりあえず、何回か刺した、 というコトにしておきませんか。
English: Let's just say she stabbed him several times and leave it at that.

Japanese: （なんでそんなテキトーな ケツロンに落ちつくんだよ‥‥）
English: (Leave it at that!? This is a murder case, people!)

Japanese: 思ったコトは、クチに出して 行きましょう、成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright, you should speak up if you have an objection, you know!

Japanese: 赤いマフラー‥‥？
English: Her red muffler?

Japanese: 主席検事サマ、いつもクビに お巻きになっておいででしょう？
English: Yes, like a scarf. The Chief Prosecutor always wears one around her neck.

Japanese: ‥‥いつ、吊るし上げられても いいように、ね。
English: So she can be easily hanged at a moment's notice, I suppose...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (She's right...)

Japanese: （ともえさん、赤いマフラーを 巻いていたっけな‥‥）
English: (Ms. Skye was wearing a red scarf... wasn't she?)

Japanese: でも‥‥“返り血”と見まちがえた なんて、不自然な気がしますが。
English: But wait... Isn't it odd that you mistook that for splattered blood?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まあ‥‥私もよく、このヒゲを 見まちがえられますからな。
English: Well, people often mistake my beard...

Japanese: ヨダレかけと。
English: For a bib.

Japanese: （そりゃ、モンダイだろ）
English: (A judge with a bib. That's why this place feels so much like kindergarten sometimes.)

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥
English: Actually...

Japanese: ワタクシ、ムネのあたりに血痕を 見たような気がいたしますの‥‥
English: I do think I saw some traces of blood on her chest...

Japanese: しかし‥‥解剖記録はゼッタイだ。 ナイフのキズは、１カ所のみ。
English: However, the autopsy report is clear on this matter. There was only one knife wound.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （おキョウさん‥‥自分の証言に 自信がないみたいだな）
English: (Apparently, Ms. Starr isn't entirely sure of her own testimony.)

Japanese: ちゃ‥‥ チャンスですぞ、成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright! This is our chance!!!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだよ、それ。
English: Chance for what, I wonder...?

Japanese: “何度も”刺した‥‥ そんなの、目撃できるはずがない！
English: You say she stabbed him again and again... But you couldn't have witnessed that!

Japanese: このおキョウを試すつもりなら‥‥ １パイ食らわせるよ！
English: Are you testing me...? Then I'll test you!

Japanese: この、青ノリ弁当をッ！
English: With my Moss Surprise!!!

Japanese: ‥‥１パイ食らうのは、 恐らくキミのほうだろう。
English: I'm afraid the moss is growing under our feet as we wait, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...!? ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: W-what do you mean?

Japanese: 説明するのもバカらしいが‥‥ これを見ていただこう。
English: I shouldn't have to explain this... But, take a look.

Japanese: 《ナイフによるキズは１カ所》‥‥ 解剖記録に、そう書かれている。
English: The autopsy report states that death was due to a loss of blood... from one stab wound.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああッ、ホントです！ さすが、御剣検事！
English: Ah hah! You're right! Good show, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ホレちゃいますよねー！ カッコイイなあ、御剣検事さん。
English: What a hunk! He's my hero, really.

Japanese: （ぼくのチカラいっぱいの “異議あり”はどこへ消えたんだ）
English: (What about my objection? No one noticed?)

Japanese: さあ‥‥いかがですかな、証人！
English: Well, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥こちら。ケチャップ弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: You got the Crime Scene Set, right?

Japanese: あ、ど。どうも‥‥
English: Uh... oh, thanks.

Japanese: 血とケチャップを見まちがう人間は いない‥‥そう信じておりました。
English: I always believed that no one could ever mistake ketchup for blood....

Japanese: でも、今日からは あらためるコトにいたしますわ。
English: But now, I realize that such mistakes are possible.

Japanese: つまり‥‥ “見まちがい”‥‥だった、と？
English: So... You're saying you mistook something... for blood?

Japanese: ナイフを振り上げたとき‥‥ムネの あたりに見えた気がしましたの。
English: When she lifted her knife, I thought I saw blood at her breast...

Japanese: ‥‥返り血が。
English: Splattered blood from her victim!

Japanese: だから、“少なくとも２回は 刺したはず”と思ってしまった‥‥
English: That's why I thought she must have stabbed him at least twice.

Japanese: “返り血”を何と見まちがえたか ‥‥証言をおねがいします！
English: Then tell us what you saw that you thought was blood! Testify!
That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！ 被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: おキョウさん！　セツメイして いただきたいことが‥‥
English: Ms. Starr! I demand an explanation...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。やはり、キミは 捜査官には向いていないようだ。
English: The witness is clearly not suited for detective work.

Japanese: な‥‥何を言うの！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: 被告人は、被害者を刺したとき‥‥ マフラーなど、していなかった。
English: The suspect was not wearing a scarf or muffler of any kind when she stabbed the victim.

Japanese: 自ら立証しているではないか‥‥ この写真でッ！
English: And you've proved it yourself! With this photograph!

Japanese: あ‥‥そ。そんな‥‥ そんな、バカなッ！
English: Huh? B-but that... That can't be!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥カンちがいも そこまで行けばホンモノだ。
English: Only a true professional could be so clueless.

Japanese: リッパな弁当屋に なれることだろう。
English: I'm sure you'll make a good lunchlady, have no fear.

Japanese: む‥‥むふゥッ！ シビれますなあ。
English: Hmm! Harsh words! But good!

Japanese: やっぱり、御剣検事さんですよね。 最終的には。
English: In the end, Mr. Edgeworth prevails!

Japanese: （ぼくのムネいっぱいの “異議あり”はどこへ消えたんだ）
English: (What was my objection, chopped liver!?)

Japanese: あ、あれは‥‥スカーフ‥‥いえ！ ワタクシは見たはず。赤い‥‥
English: B-but it was there, a scarf, no, not that, but something red! Really!

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Well now, where were we?

Japanese: 証人のユカイな幕間劇には、 じゅうぶん笑わせてもらった。
English: The witness has given us an entertaining interlude, now back to business.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well, witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: 事件を目撃したあなたは、 被告人の身柄をカクホした‥‥
English: You saw the crime, and apprehended the suspect...

Japanese: そのことについて。
English: Tell us about that.
Very well.

Japanese: どうやら、ワタクシも 思い出したコトがあるようですわ。
English: I do remember some things accurately, at least.

Japanese: （ケッキョク‥‥証言のイチバン 重要な部分は、くずせなかった）
English: (Ultimately, we couldn't shake the most important part of her testimony.)

Japanese: 重要な部分‥‥？
English: The most important part...?

Japanese: “ともえさんが被害者を刺した” というポイントだよ。
English: The part where your sister stabs the victim!

Japanese: （次の証言が、本当の 勝負かもしれないな‥‥）
English: (This next testimony might just be the moment of truth!)

Japanese: ～被告人のタイホ～
English: -- Apprehending the Suspect --

Japanese: 犯行後、被告人はワキにあった ついたてのウラ側に逃げたのさ。
English: After the murder, the suspect attempted to run behind a partition off to her side.

Japanese: すぐに後を追って、被疑者の権利を 説明したあと、身柄をカクホ‥‥
English: I quickly caught her, explained her rights to her, and arrested her on the spot.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。タイホの瞬間、彼女は マフラーのことを口走ったの！
English: Ah yes. When I arrested her, she mentioned the muffler!

Japanese: それで先ほどの証言で、カンちがい してしまったのでしょう。
English: That's what had me confused in my earlier testimony!

Japanese: 主席検事サマはお逃げになったケド ‥‥おキョウにはムダなコト！
English: The chief prosecutor made to escape, but against Angel Starr, resistance is futile!

Japanese: こだわりますなあ、エリマキに。
English: You are quite determined about this scarf, aren't you?

Japanese: ヘビのようにしつこく、 ハブのように噛む！
English: I strike like a snake and bite like a cobra!

Japanese: ‥‥それがおキョウですわ。
English: That's me. Angel Starr.

Japanese: ハブはある種、 ヘビだと思いますけど‥‥
English: That wasn't a very good metaphor. First of all, a cobra is a kind of snake.

Japanese: ヘリクツ並べるんじゃないよ！ 噛みたおしてやろうかッ！
English: Don't bother me with details, unless you want to get bitten!

Japanese: す、すみませんッ！
English: N-no thanks!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士、 毒ヘビに噛みたおされる、と。
English: Note to self: Attorney Wright gets bitten by snake.

Japanese: ‥‥主席検事殿の抵抗は、 それは見苦しいモノでしたわ。
English: The chief prosecutor tried to resist, but her efforts were in vain.

Japanese: この手を振りはらって ドラム缶をケリ倒してみたり‥‥
English: She knocked my hands aside, kicked over an oil drum...

Japanese: ドラム缶‥‥ （想像もつかないな）
English: O-oil drum? (Hard to imagine...)

Japanese: ‥‥カワイイカオをして、 ワリと女豹でしたわねェ‥‥
English: Oh, she's beautiful, but deadly! A predator, this one! A leopard woman! Rowr!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Your cross-examination, if you will.

Japanese: ～被告人のタイホ～
English: -- Apprehending the Suspect --

Japanese: 犯行後、被告人はワキにあった ついたてのウラ側に逃げたのさ。
English: After the murder, the suspect attempted to run behind a partition off to her side.

Japanese: すぐに後を追って、被疑者の権利を 説明したあと、身柄をカクホ‥‥
English: I quickly caught her, explained her rights to her, and arrested her on the spot.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。タイホの瞬間、彼女は マフラーのことを口走ったの！
English: When I arrested her, she mentioned the muffler!

Japanese: それで先ほどの証言で、カンちがい してしまったんだろうねえ。
English: That's what had me confused in my earlier testimony!

Japanese: 主席検事殿は逃げようとしたケド ‥‥おキョウにはムダなコト！
English: The chief prosecutor made to escape, but against Angel Starr, resistance is futile!

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥ 逃げようとしたのか）
English: (So Ms. Skye tried to run?)

Japanese: あの。すみません、成歩堂さん‥‥ お姉ちゃん、アヤしくて。
English: I'm sorry my sister is so suspicious, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥あやまられてもコマるけど。
English: Not as sorry as I am.

Japanese: でも、お姉ちゃんじゃないんです！ それだけは、信じてください！
English: But she didn't do it! You have to believe me!

Japanese: カベの電話をあきらめ、携帯電話を 使った‥‥すべて目撃しましたわ！
English: She gave up trying to use the phone on the wall and just used her cell phone!

Japanese: “ついたて”‥‥というのは、 上面図でいうと‥‥
English: So where is this "partition" on the floor plans?

Japanese: クルマの横にある“カベ”の コトだろう。‥‥もちろん。
English: I'm sure she means this wall next to the car.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (That's right...)

Japanese: （あそこには、高さ２メートル ほどのカベがあったっけ‥‥）
English: (There was a wall there... about 6 feet high.)

Japanese: とにかく、主席検事サマは 身をかくしたかったのでしょう。
English: She was obviously trying to hide herself.

Japanese: ‥‥犯罪者として、 ごく自然な心理ですわね。
English: Quite a natural thing for a criminal to do!

Japanese: そこで‥‥証人はどうしたのか？
English: And what did you do then?

Japanese: “すぐに”というと‥‥あなたは、 被告人の近くにいたわけですか？
English: You say "quickly"... were you close to the suspect?

Japanese: ‥‥さっきも言ったじゃないか。
English: As I just said!

Japanese: １０メートルも離れていない 場所におりましたのよ、ワタクシ。
English: I was only 30 feet away from her the whole time.

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥もう少し 詳しく聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Hmm... maybe I should press her for more details?)

Japanese: （おキョウさんが、どれくらいの 距離で目撃したか‥‥？）
English: (How far away was she when she witnessed the murder?)

Japanese: （彼女が撮影した写真を見れば、 だいたいわかるからな）
English: (I guess I could just look at the picture to find out.)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well, you may continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 念のため、上面図で カクニンさせていただきます。
English: I'd like to see this on the floor plans, just to be safe.

Japanese: ベントーランドのクルマは‥‥
English: The Lunchland car was...

Japanese: “外来者”にあたる。‥‥当然、 Ｂブロックに停められていたハズ。
English: She was a "visitor," thus, she was parked in B Block.

Japanese: すると‥‥証人が目撃したのは、 このあたり、になりますね？
English: So, you witnessed the murder from... here?

Japanese: クルマから１０メートルといえば ‥‥そのあたりでしょうな。
English: That would make it about 30 feet from the car, yes.

Japanese: おキョウさん。 まちがいありませんね？
English: Is that correct, Ms. Starr?

Japanese: そ、そうですわねえ‥‥
English: Y-yes, that's right.

Japanese: しかし‥‥目の前に、金網が あるようですが、これは‥‥？
English: But, there was a chain link fence in front of you...?

Japanese: 乗り越えましたわ、モチロン。
English: I went over it, of course.

Japanese: さ。さすがは 《ゲロまみれのおキョウ》ですッ！
English: Amazing! The Cough-up Queen, lunchlady athlete, indeed.

Japanese: 金網を乗り越えたのなら、 少し時間がかかったはずですね。
English: It would have taken her a little time to climb over the fence.

Japanese: おキョウさんが現場に かけつけるまで。
English: So she couldn't have gotten to my sister THAT fast...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥３メートルは あったからな、あの金網）
English: (Yeah, that fence was about nine feet high, too.)

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥なぜ、 逃げなかったんだろう？）
English: (How come Ms. Skye didn't get away?)

Japanese: “マフラーのこと”‥‥ セイカクには、なんと言いました？
English: She mentioned the muffler? What exactly did she say?

Japanese: セイカクに覚えていれば、 そのように証言しておりますわ。
English: If I remembered exactly, I would have told you in my testimony!

Japanese: （ムカつくなあ‥‥）
English: (Cheeky!)

Japanese: ‥‥それに。ワタクシが聞いたのは “マフラー”のヒトコトのみ‥‥
English: Anyway, all I heard her say was the word "muffler."

Japanese: “ヒトコトのみ”‥‥？
English: Just that one word?

Japanese: では‥‥それは、証人と被告人の 会話ではなかった、というコトか。
English: So... what you heard wasn't the suspect talking to you, but to someone else?

Japanese: そう‥‥主席検事サマは、電話に 向かって話しておられましたわね。
English: Yes. The chief prosecutor was talking on her phone!

Japanese: （“デンワ”‥‥もしかして、 あの携帯電話のこと、か‥‥？）
English: (Her phone? You mean... this cell phone?)

Japanese: （この、ともえさんの携帯電話。 考えてみたら‥‥）
English: (Ms. Skye's cell phone...)

Japanese: （最後に通話した番号は、 あかねちゃんの携帯電話だ）
English: (The last number she called was her sister, Ema!)

Japanese: （ヘタにつっこむと、 また噛まれそうだな‥‥ヘビに）
English: (Hmm... if I press this too hard, who knows who the snake will bite...?)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証言を つづけていただこうか。
English: The witness may continue with her testimony.

Japanese: “デンワ”というのは‥‥ 現場で発見された、コレですか？
English: By phone... do you mean this cell phone, discovered at the crime scene?

Japanese: そう‥‥最終的には、ね。
English: Yes, ultimately.

Japanese: “最終的”というのは？
English: Ultimately?

Japanese: このおキョウの記憶力‥‥ シャケのようにさかのぼりますの。
English: My memory... it's like a salmon, heading upstream, you see.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: N-no, the court doesn't see, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: 主席検事サマはまず、カベの デンワを使おうとなさいましたの。
English: The chief prosecutor first attempted to use the phone hanging on the wall.

Japanese: カベの‥‥？
English: On the wall?

Japanese: （たしか‥‥ついたてのウラに、 非常電話があったな）
English: (That's right! Near the car... there was an emergency phone on the wall!)

Japanese: どうやらアレが、 故障していたらしくてねェ‥‥
English: Apparently, it was out of order.

Japanese: それで、やむを得ず 携帯電話を使ったわけですか？
English: And so she used her cell phone?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あの非常電話は、 事件当日、不通だったようだ。
English: Indeed, the emergency phone was out of order that day.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥さすが、証人。 シャケ並の記憶力です！
English: Hmm. Good witnessing, witness!

Japanese: （そりゃモンダイだろ‥‥）
English: (Good witnessing? What ever happened to good testifying?)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、今の発言を 証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: You should of course add this to your testimony.

Japanese: ホント‥‥ 世話の焼けるボウヤたちだよ。
English: The things I do to please this rookie defense attorney.

Japanese: 証拠品《携帯電話》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Cell phone updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: それでは‥‥あなたは マフラーを見ていないのですか？
English: So, you never saw this muffler?
Witness?

Japanese: 見ることは不可能だった。 写真がそれを物語っている。
English: As the photograph proves, she couldn't have seen it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ そういうコト、ですわね‥‥ ...
English: That's true.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ さっきから、どうもヘンですよ。
English: Ms. Starr... is acting strangely.

Japanese: “返り血”を“マフラー”と 見まちがえて‥‥
English: First she mistakes a scarf for splattered blood...

Japanese: 今度はその“マフラー”も 見ていない、なんて！
English: And now she says she didn't see any scarf!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥そんなイイカゲンな 証人には見えない）
English: (She doesn't strike me as the kind of witness to make careless mistakes, either...)

Japanese: （いったい‥‥彼女は 何を“見た”のか‥‥？）
English: (I'm sure she saw something red... but what!?)

Japanese: “逃げようとした”‥‥ 詳しく聞かせていただけますか？
English: She "made to escape"... Can you be more specific?

Japanese: あの女はねェ。おキョウの手を 振りきると、逃げようとしたのさ。
English: She brushed aside my hand and ran!

Japanese: それは見苦しいものだったさ。 サンマの苦いトコロのように‥‥
English: It was a terrible sight to see, like a dollop of lard on a pate of foie gras!

Japanese: （なんだそれ‥‥）
English: (Huh?)

Japanese: このおキョウに、ドラム缶を ケリたおしてきたんだからねェ‥‥
English: She even kicked over an oil drum at me!

Japanese: ど、ドラム缶‥‥？
English: A-an oil drum!?

Japanese: 現場にはたしかに、たおれた ドラム缶がありましたな。
English: There was an oil drum lying on its side at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: しかし‥‥少し、妙だな‥‥
English: But, it's strange...

Japanese: ？　何がですかな？
English: Hmm? What's that?

Japanese: 逃げるつもりならば‥‥なぜ、 反対側に走らなかったのか‥‥？
English: If she wanted to escape... why didn't she run the other way?

Japanese: 反対側‥‥あ。 クルマの入庫口‥‥
English: The other... ah! The car entrance!

Japanese: そ、そうですよ！
English: Th-that's right!

Japanese: ついたての後ろからドラム缶の方に 逃げるなんて‥‥不自然です！
English: It doesn't make any sense that she would run from behind the partition to the oil drums!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥よしよし。また、 ナゾが増えましたね！
English: Excellent! More mysteries!

Japanese: 増える前に、１コずつ かたづけたいな‥‥ナゾ。
English: I wish we could solve a few before finding more, though...

Japanese: ええと‥‥あなたが目撃したことを 整理してもらえますか？
English: Um... do you think you could restate your testimony for the court?

Japanese: あ。それ！　私も おねがいしようと思ってました！
English: Ah hah! I was going to ask the same thing!

Japanese: ‥‥一度しか言わないから よく聞くんだよ、ボウヤたち‥‥
English: I'll only say this one time, so listen close, Rookies.

Japanese: 被害者を刺した主席検事サンは、 ついたてのウラへ回ったのさ。
English: The chief prosecutor stabbed the victim, and ran behind the partition.

Japanese: そこでカベの非常電話を取った ものの‥‥不通だった。
English: Then she picked up the emergency phone on the wall, but it was out of order.

Japanese: だから、ご自分の携帯電話を ポッケからつまみ出したワケさ。
English: So she pulled her own cell phone out of her pocket.

Japanese: そして、そのあいだに証人は 金網を乗り越えて‥‥
English: And during that time, you climbed over the chain link fence...

Japanese: このおキョウがウデをむんずと つかんだワケね。‥‥そのとき！
English: Then, when I boldly grabbed her arm...

Japanese: 主席検事サマは、あわてて通話を 打ちきったのですわ‥‥
English: The chief prosecutor hung up her phone!

Japanese: あなたは‥‥それを、 “目撃できた”と言うのですか‥‥
English: And you saw her doing this?

Japanese: ‥‥？ どういうコトですか、成歩堂さん。 ...?
English: What is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥宝月　巴さんに、 個人的なウラミがあるようですね。
English: I have to conclude that you have a personal grudge against Ms. Lana Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥この証人は、 かつては捜査官だった。
English: The witness is a former detective.

Japanese: 個人的な感情によって 証言が左右されることはない。
English: Her testimony is unmarred by personal bias.

Japanese: ‥‥心外ですわね。 アナタにかばっていただくとは‥‥
English: Well, who would have thought you would be my knight in shining armor, prosecutor?

Japanese: ２年前‥‥主席検事と手を組んで ワタクシを追い出した、アナタに。
English: You who, together with the chief prosecutor, kicked me out two years ago!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それならば、おキョウさん。
English: Well, Ms. Starr...

Japanese: 今の証言の、致命的なムジュン‥‥ どう説明するつもりですか？
English: This is a fatal contradiction with your testimony... How do you explain this?

Japanese: ふ‥‥フン！ どういうことサ。
English: Hmph! I don't know what you're talking about.

Japanese: この、おキョウ‥‥ ナメたらゲロの味がするよッ！
English: Mess with me... and I'll make you cough it ALL up!

Japanese: 上面図を見ていただきましょう。
English: Ahem. Let's look at the floor plans.

Japanese: あなたは、このポイントから 事件を目撃したと証言しています。
English: You said you witnessed the crime from this point.

Japanese: しかし！ もし、それが本当ならば‥‥
English: However, if that's true...

Japanese: ともえさんが電話で話したのを 目撃できるハズがないッ！
English: You couldn't possibly have seen Ms. Skye making that phone call!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! もう、おわかりでしょう。
English: I believe you see what I'm getting at.

Japanese: 非常電話は、ついたての ウラ側に設置されています。
English: That emergency phone was on the back side of this partition.

Japanese: Ｂブロックにいるかぎり！ それが見えるはずがないのです！
English: If, indeed, you were in B Block... You couldn't have seen it!
Wha...

Japanese: くくくくくくくくくくくくくぅッ！
English: Waaaaaaaaaaarrrgh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ これは、どういうコトですかッ！
English: Order! Order! What is the meaning of this?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです、裁判長。
English: It's simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: 《ゲロまみれ》である以前に、 この証人はウソにまみれているッ！
English: She's not coughing up lunch... she's coughing up lies!!!

Japanese: ぐぅぅ‥‥ッ！
English: Grrr!

Japanese: ‥‥それならば。 弁護士にうかがおうか。
English: That's quite a claim, Mr. Wright... perhaps you will allow me a question?

Japanese: いったい‥‥この証人は、いかなる イツワリを述べたというのかッ！
English: Tell us exactly what lie this witness has told the court!

Japanese: （これは、反撃の糸口になる。 ミスをするワケには行かない！）
English: (Here's where the counter- attack begins! I can't afford to get this wrong!)

Japanese: 証人の“ウソ”。それは‥‥
English: The witness lied about...

Japanese: もちろん、目撃した“内容”です！ つまり‥‥
English: She lied about what she saw! In other words,

Japanese: おキョウさんは、被告人が非常電話 を使うところなど、見ていない！
English: she didn't see Ms. Skye using that emergency phone!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！ ヒジョーにロンリ的ですッ！
English: It does seem hard to imagine how she could have! Very logical!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （よしよし‥‥ コトバもないみたいだな）
English: (What's the matter, Starr? Cat got your lunchbox?)

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。 ガッツポーズ中、悪いんですけど。
English: Um, Mr. Wright... I hate to bother you while you're celebrating your victory...

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥どうして、 そんなウソを？
English: But why would Ms. Starr lie like that?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “非常電話を使おうとしたけど、 使えなかった”‥‥
English: Why would she say that my sister had tried to use the phone, but failed?

Japanese: そんなウソ、つく必要なんて ないじゃないですか。
English: It doesn't make any sense! Why lie about something so insignificant?

Japanese: あ‥‥ （たしかに、そうだよな‥‥）
English: Oh... (Dang, she's right!)

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥そこは、 本当に見たんじゃないかなあ。
English: I mean, maybe she really did see her try to use the emergency phone.

Japanese: ‥‥考えるまでもあるまい。
English: I see no room for doubt here.

Japanese: ‥‥スイカのタネ弁当、 よろしかったかしら。
English: You ordered the P'tooey on rice, right?

Japanese: な、なんですか弁護人ッ！ 感心してソンしたではないですか！
English: Mr. Wright! And I thought you had something there!

Japanese: うううう‥‥ （も、もう一度だッ！）
English: Ugh. (One... one more try!)

Japanese: フッ。その目‥‥まだ、 コリていないようだ。
English: Hmph. I see it in your eyes. You haven't learned your lesson, have you?

Japanese: 目撃した“順序” だったのではないでしょうか。
English: She lied about the order of events!

Japanese: ともえさんが非常電話を使った のは、犯行の“前”だった！
English: Ms. Skye used that emergency phone BEFORE the murder!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほど！ それは思いつきませんでした！
English: I-I see! I hadn't thought of that!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （よしよし‥‥ コトバもないみたいだな）
English: (That took the wind out of her sails!)

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。 ガッツポーズ中、悪いんですけど。
English: Um, Mr. Wright... I hate to bother you while you're celebrating your victory...

Japanese: それって‥‥そもそも、 どういうイミなんですか？
English: But... why would anyone use the emergency phone before the murder?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥苦しまぎれも ここまで来ると、感動的だな。
English: Just when you think he can't sink any lower, he amazes us. I applaud you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ミカンのカワ弁当、 よろしかったかしら。
English: You ordered the Orange-peel Lunchbox, right?

Japanese: ウッカリ感心してしまいましたが、 “順序”をまちがえたようです！
English: I guess that teaches me to not get excited BEFORE the evidence!

Japanese: “非常電話を使おうとしたが、 不通だった”‥‥
English: She tried to use the emergency phone... but it was out of order.

Japanese: この証言自体には、 それほど重要なイミはありません。
English: What is significant about this fact?

Japanese: つまり、そこでウソをつくイミも ない、ということになります。
English: Nothing. It would be pointless for her to lie about it!

Japanese: ウソをつく“イミ”‥‥
English: Pointless to lie... I see!

Japanese: 証人は、ジッサイに被告人が 非常電話を使うのを見たのです。
English: The witness did actually see Ms. Skye using the emergency phone.

Japanese: ということは、つまり‥‥
English: In other words...

Japanese: おキョウさんは、ベツの場所から 事件を目撃したのです！
English: Ms. Starr witnessed the crime from a different location!

Japanese: 目撃した“場所”だと‥‥？
English: A different location!?

Japanese: それこそ、なんのイミもない “ウソ”ではないかッ！
English: Now that's a pointless lie if I ever heard one!

Japanese: そのウソにイミがあるかどうか‥‥ ついてみなければ、わからないッ！
English: Before you call my lie pointless... at least let me tell it!

Japanese: ‥‥今、ハラの立つほどテキトーな 反論をした弁護人にうかがいます。
English: Let me ask a question to our clever wordsmith, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この証人が、事件のあった瞬間‥‥ “どこ”にいたというのですかッ！
English: Just where was the witness when she saw the crime!?

Japanese: （今までの証言のすべてが、 ある１点を指している‥‥）
English: (All the testimony we've heard until now points in one direction...)

Japanese: おキョウさんが、事件を目撃した 場所‥‥それは、ここです！
English: The place from where Ms. Starr witnessed this crime was... here!

Japanese: おそらく‥‥この辺だったの かもしれないですよね！
English: I think! I mean, it's highly likely that's where she was.

Japanese: ‥‥“かもしれない”？
English: You "think"?

Japanese: ‥‥“ですよね”？
English: It's "highly likely"?

Japanese: キメたポーズと発言の内容が ムジュンしてたみたいですね。
English: Your cavalier attitude stands in stark contrast to your feeble argument, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: その場所では、状況は 何も変わらないではないかッ！
English: Her being there wouldn't change a thing!

Japanese: おねがいしますぞ、弁護人！
English: Please, Mr. Wright, think before you speak.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥スミマセン。
English: Ah... uh, yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ここならば、非常電話を 見ることができます！
English: If she was here, she could see the emergency phone!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！ ナゾはすべて解けましたッ！
English: Th-that's true! That solves the mystery!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、非常電話は 見えるだろう。しかし！
English: That would allow her to see the emergency phone, yes...

Japanese: そもそも、証人が そんなトコロにいたのならば‥‥
English: But if she was there...

Japanese: 携帯電話をかけなおす前に タイホできたハズではないかッ！
English: She would have been able to arrest her well before she dialed her cell phone!
Oh...

Japanese: このおキョウ‥‥５０メートルを ９秒で走りぬきますわよ！
English: You doubt my speed? I can run 150 feet in nine seconds, you know!

Japanese: （ビミョーなタイムだな）
English: (Is that really that fast?)

Japanese: あなたのコタエほどでは ありません！
English: Not as fast as your witty rejoinders!

Japanese: モチロン‥‥ ここしか考えられません。
English: This is the only place where she could have been.

Japanese: 守衛室、ですか‥‥
English: The security guard room?

Japanese: たしか、地下駐車場の守衛室は‥‥
English: Indeed, the security room in the underground parking lot is well positioned...

Japanese: 駐車場全体を見わたすため、 ２階部分に設置されていたはずだ。
English: It's built on the second level, so you can see the entire lot.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥そこからならば、 非常電話が見えるでしょう。
English: Hmm... She would have been able to see the emergency phone from there.

Japanese: しかし‥‥何も、この守衛室とは かぎらないのでは？
English: But why there? There are many other places where she could have seen the phone.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、ここしか あり得ないのです。
English: Not in this case, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検事局の関係者ではない証人は、 Ａブロックには駐車できない‥‥
English: The witness, not being part of the Prosecutor's Office, couldn't park in A Block.

Japanese: 《非常電話》と《事件》の両方を 目撃できるのは、ここだけです！
English: The only place where she could have seen the crime and the back of the partition is here.

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、あなたは、 こう証言していましたね。
English: I remember in your testimony, you said...

Japanese: 毎日、守衛室の“カレ”に 弁当をとどけている、と‥‥
English: You brought a lunch to your "boyfriend" in the security guard room, yes?

Japanese: いかがですか、おキョウさん！
English: Well, Ms. Starr?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 長年、オトコたちのクチを 割ってきたモンさね‥‥
English: How many years have I been getting the better of men...?

Japanese: まさか‥‥このおキョウが、 オトコにクチを割らされるとはね。
English: To think that the tables could be turned... Today, a man has got the better of Angel Starr!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Order! Order! Witness!

Japanese: 元・捜査官でありながら、 ウソの証言をしたのですかッ！
English: What have you done!? You used to be a detective! You should know better!

Japanese: ‥‥このおキョウ、 逃げもかくれもいたしませんわ。
English: I'm not turning back.

Japanese: ツミのある者に、罰を。 カクゴはできておりますのよ。
English: The guilty will be punished. And I'll do what I must to make sure justice prevails.

Japanese: （“ツミのある者”‥‥ ともえさんのコト、なのか？）
English: (The guilty... is she talking about Ms. Skye...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。 なんか、ヘンじゃないですか？
English: Um, Mr. Wright? Doesn't this strike you as odd?

Japanese: どうして、おキョウさん‥‥ こんなウソをついたんでしょう？
English: Why did Ms. Starr lie? It doesn't make sense!
Huh?

Japanese: 正直に《守衛室から目撃した》って 証言しても‥‥
English: She could have just said she saw the crime from the security guard station.

Japanese: ベツに、何も変わらないと 思うんですけど‥‥
English: It wouldn't change anything!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Exactly!

Japanese: この写真がすべてを物語っている。 被害者を刺したのは、被告人‥‥
English: This photograph tells all! It was the defendant who stabbed the victim!

Japanese: そのジジツは、何も変わらないッ！
English: That truth still stands!

Japanese: “何も変わらない”‥‥？ そんなハズはありません。
English: It "still stands"? I disagree, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: ウソの証言をすれば、罪になる‥‥ 捜査官ならば、よく知っています。
English: If a witness is found to be lying, they're guilty of perjury. She knows this.

Japanese: つまり！　そこには‥‥何か 理由がなければならない！
English: She wouldn't risk that without a good reason!

Japanese: いったい！　その “理由”とは、なんなのですかッ！
English: So, tell us what her reason was, Mr. Wright!
Huh? M-me?

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょうッ！
English: Who else!?

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん！ もう一度、整理してみましょう！
English: Mr. Wright! Let's review what we know!

Japanese: （おキョウさんは、本当は 守衛室で事件を目撃した‥‥）
English: (Ms. Starr witnessed the crime from the security guard station...)

Japanese: （なのに“Ｂブロックから 目撃した”と、ウソをついた）
English: (But she lied and said she saw it from B Block...)

Japanese: （そのウソによって変わってくる、 “重要なちがい”とは‥‥？）
English: (It must make a vital difference... but what? What would change...?)

Japanese: それは、モチロン“角度”です！
English: Why, the angle at which she saw the crime occur would change!

Japanese: かくど‥‥？ どういうコトですかッ！
English: The angle...? What do you mean!?

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥その。
English: Uh, um, well...

Japanese: 守衛室は２階ですから。 なんというか、あの。
English: The security guard station is on the second floor... and um...

Japanese: より、立体的に事件が 目撃できたのではないか、と。
English: She would have sort of a more 3-D view of the crime.

Japanese: いいじゃないですか。 そっちのほうが。
English: And this is important... why?

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Um...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あなたの“重要なカンちがい”に ペナルティを与えます！
English: Perhaps you'd like to reconsider, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: それは、モチロン“明るさ”です！
English: It's a difference in lighting!

Japanese: あかるさ‥‥？ どういうコトですかッ！
English: Lighting...? What does that mean!?

Japanese: それは、やっぱり‥‥その。
English: Well, it means, uh...

Japanese: 守衛室は２階ですから。 なんというか、あの。
English: See, the security guard station is on the second level...

Japanese: より、明るい状態で事件が 目撃できたのではないか、と。
English: So, uh, she would have seen the crime in better lighting conditions.

Japanese: それは、モチロン“距離”です！
English: It changes the distance between her and the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、弁護士‥‥ 上面図を見れば、アキラカだ。
English: My condolences, Mr. Wright... But one look at the floor plans and it's quite clear.

Japanese: 守衛室から現場までの距離も、 約１０メートル。
English: The distance between the scene of the crime and the guard station is 30 feet.

Japanese: 見えるものは、Ｂブロックと そう変わらないハズだが‥‥？
English: I don't see how that would change what she could see.

Japanese: 重要なのは‥‥ “何が見えるか”ではありません。
English: What she saw is not in question here.

Japanese: 現場まで“何分かかるか”です。
English: What matters is the time it would take her to reach the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! おキョウさん！　あなたは 守衛室で事件を目撃した！
English: Ms. Starr! You witnessed the crime from the security guard station!

Japanese: そこから、現場で被告人を タイホするまで‥‥
English: Now, how long did it take you to go from there...

Japanese: いったい、どれぐらいの時間が かかったのですかッ！
English: to the scene of the crime, where you arrested Ms. Skye!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... い、いかがですか、証人！
English: Well, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥そちら。
English: You...

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Y-yes...?

Japanese: 輪ゴム弁当‥‥で よろしかったかしら？
English: You ordered the Squid Wheels, right?

Japanese: （食べられないじゃないか！）
English: (The quality of my lunches has gone from low to inedible.)

Japanese: ワタクシ、守衛室のカレに カレー弁当を届けておりましたの。
English: I was bringing a PB&J lunch with fresh boysenberry jam to my boyfriend.

Japanese: む、むうう‥‥ カレに、カレー弁当を！
English: Hmm... Boysenberry for the boyfriend!

Japanese: カレは部屋にいなくて、 ワタクシ、待っておりましたわ。
English: He wasn't in the station, so I waited.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、ガラス張りの守衛室から 事件を目撃したワタクシは‥‥
English: I witnessed the crime from the glass-walled station...

Japanese: 気がついたら、現場に向かって 走っておりましたの。
English: and before I knew what I was doing, I found myself running towards the scene.

Japanese: でも‥‥このドアはカギが かかって、開かなかったのです。
English: But... the door was locked. I couldn't open it.

Japanese: だから‥‥外来者用のＢブロックへ 向かうしかなかった‥‥
English: That's why I had to go through the visitor's parking in B Block.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドい遠回りですな。
English: That's quite a detour.

Japanese: 事件を目撃してから‥‥おそらく ５分ほど、かかったでしょう。
English: It probably took me at least five minutes to get to the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ごごごご、ごふん‥‥ですか！
English: F-f-f-five minutes!?

Japanese: むうう‥‥それでは、 ハナシが変わってくるような‥‥
English: Hmm... This changes things considerably!

Japanese: しかし！　被害者を刺したのは、 そこの被告席のオンナなのさ！
English: But, it was that woman over there in the defendant's chair who stabbed him!

Japanese: この写真があるかぎり‥‥ それだけは、まちがいない！
English: I know it! I have photographic evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥このおキョウ、 そのワリバシにかけてッ！
English: I swear it... I swear it on my finest plastic spork!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに 認めざるを得ないようです。
English: You have a point. And the spork is a wonderful invention.

Japanese: ‥‥こちら。キャビア弁当 オカワリ、よろしかったかしら。
English: Would you like another Caviar Lunch...?

Japanese: よろしかったですぞッ！
English: Absolutely!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥まいったな‥‥）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！ ‥‥なんとかしないと！
English: Mr. Wright! You have to do something!

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ 反証できる材料はあるのか？）
English: (Do I have any evidence to stop this...?)

Japanese: （料理も異議も、いい材料が なければイミがないよな‥‥）
English: (I think I need more evidence before I go sticking my spork in this mess...)

Japanese: むほッ！　キャビアが‥‥ キャビアが目にしみますッ！
English: Woo! Caviar! Ah... how it makes my eyes tingle!

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: どんな材料も、カガク的に キャビアには勝てません！
English: No evidence can win against the raw power of caviar! It's a scientific fact!

Japanese: 成歩堂さんに残されてるのは‥‥ もう、ウデっぷしだけなんですよ！
English: The only thing that's left... Is your strong presence and deft powers of deduction!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! おねがい！　あのキャビア、 ネジふせちゃってください！
English: Let's screw the lid back on those overpriced fish eggs!

Japanese: 目撃からタイホまで、“５分”！ その重要性を考えてください！
English: Five minutes between the witnessing of the murder and the arrest! Think about it!

Japanese: カップラーメンのおかわりが 作れる！　‥‥カタめでよければ。
English: You could make pasta in that amount of time! If you like it al dente!

Japanese: ‥‥ラーメンばかり食べてないで、 おキョウの弁当にしなさいな。
English: I've got lunchboxes that tie pasta into knots, Rookie!

Japanese: この、空白の“５分”は‥‥ あまりに不自然なのです。
English: A five minute "blank"... Isn't that strange!?

Japanese: 不自然‥‥だと？
English: Strange...?

Japanese: 裁判長。あなたが犯人ならば‥‥ ５分あったら、どうしますか？
English: If you were a criminal... What would you do with five minutes, Your Honor?

Japanese: そりゃ、まあ。 現場から逃げるのでは‥‥‥‥あ！
English: Well, um... I guess I'd flee the scene. Hey!

Japanese: いや！　私は決してその。 サツジンなど、考えたコトも
English: D-don't get the wrong idea! I didn't kill anyone...

Japanese: そう！　だれでも逃げるでしょう。 しかし！　今回は、ちがった！
English: But you have the instincts of a killer! You would run! But this time was different!

Japanese: ともえさんは、現場でモタモタして 写真まで撮られている！
English: Ms. Skye dawdled at the scene of the crime... she even had her picture taken!

Japanese: 本当の“ハンニン”ならば、 こんなコトは、あり得ないッ！
English: No true criminal would act this way! It's inconceivable!
Y-

Japanese: ぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぐぅッ！
English: yeeeaaargh!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥やはり、 この証人は、ここまでのようです。
English: Well then. It seems we've come to the end of this testimony.

Japanese: 被告人に敵意がある上、 その証言には“空白”が目立つ‥‥
English: She has a grudge against the defendant, and there is a blank in her testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥‥ッ！ ...! 御剣検事。次の証人の ジュンビはありますかな？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, is the next witness ready to go?

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥
English: Unfortunately...

Japanese: モト捜査官であるこの証人を、 やや過大評価していたようだ。
English: I appear to have overestimated this witness on account of her professional history...

Japanese: や‥‥やりましたよ！ ネジふせちゃいましたね！
English: We did it! We screwed that can shut, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （あ‥‥あぶなかったな‥‥ 今回も、また）
English: (Th-that was too close!)

Japanese: 《ゲロまみれのおキョウ》も 今はムカシのユメ、でしたな。
English: I'm afraid that the Cough-up Queen has been dethroned.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: And with that, court is adjourned!

Japanese: 輪ゴム弁当、よろしかったかしら ‥‥御剣検事サマ？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, you ordered the Squid Wheels, right?

Japanese: （さっき、ぼくにくれた ヤツじゃないか！）
English: (That's the one she tried to foist off on me!)

Japanese: 弁護人のオサガリは受け取らぬ。 ‥‥まだ、何か？
English: I prefer to not take the defense team's leftovers. Anything else to say?

Japanese: ワタクシ‥‥アナタを助けることが できるかもしれなくてよ。
English: I... might be able to save you.

Japanese: ‥‥決定的な、証拠によって。
English: I have decisive evidence.

Japanese: な、なんですと‥‥！
English: Wh-what was that!?

Japanese: （まさか‥‥まだ、弁当‥‥ 切り札をかくしているのか！）
English: (Is this another one of her trick lunchboxes!?)

Japanese: ザンネンですが。 あなたに対する尋問は、終了‥‥
English: My apologies, but we have no further questions to ask of you, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: ‥‥こちら‥‥
English: Ah...

Japanese: スペシャル弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: Is this your jumbo lunchbox?

Japanese: うほほおおッ！ さ‥‥３段重ね‥‥ッ！
English: Whoo hoo! A triple-decker!

Japanese: 証人のカクゴの重さに免じて、 あと１回、証言を許可します！
English: Out of deference to the witness's determination, I'll allow one more testimony!

Japanese: あなたの持っているという、 “決定的な証拠”について！
English: Let's hear about this decisive evidence.

Japanese: このおキョウ‥‥弁当と同じく、 失望させませんコトよ！
English: Like the Lunchland motto says, you won't be disappointed!

Japanese: （‥‥おキョウさんの執念‥‥ いったい、何が出てくるんだ！）
English: (What's she going to pull out of her lunchbox this time!?)

Japanese: ～決定的な証拠品～
English: -- Decisive Evidence --

Japanese: 約５分間、現場から目を離したコト ‥‥申し上げるべきでしたわね。
English: I should have mentioned those five minutes when I wasn't looking at the crime scene.

Japanese: では。被害者のクツが脱げたコト。 ‥‥これはお話ししたかしら？
English: And now, to the matter of the victim's shoe... Did I not bring this up...?

Japanese: 調べてみたら‥‥２種類の血痕が。 １つはモチロン、被害者のモノ。
English: Two types of blood were found on this shoe! One was of course the victim's.

Japanese: そして、もう１つは‥‥そう！ 被告人・宝月　巴のモノさねッ！
English: And the other was...! The defendant, Ms. Lana Skye's blood!

Japanese: このクツは‥‥なんのムジュンも ない、カンペキな証拠品ですわ！
English: This shoe proves it! It's flawless, decisive evidence!

Japanese: な、なんですって！ 被害者のクツに‥‥血痕がッ！
English: Wh-what!? There was blood found on that shoe!?

Japanese: 決定的な証拠品とお弁当は、 《ベントーランド》に、ご用命を。
English: Try Lunchland, for all your lunch and decisive evidence needs!

Japanese: どういうつもりだ、証人‥‥
English: Witness, what's the meaning of this?

Japanese: このようなシロモノ‥‥ なんの報告も受けていないが？
English: Why is this the first time I've heard of this evidence?

Japanese: カンタンなコト。 ‥‥前にも申し上げたでしょう？
English: Simple. As I've already said...

Japanese: ワタクシ、信用しておりませんの。 検事サマの、証拠品のあつかい‥‥
English: I don't trust you with evidence, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: それゆえ、ワタクシ自身が 調査してさしあげましたのよ。
English: That's why I took the liberty of investigating this myself.

Japanese: しかも‥‥すでに、血液検査まで 済まされているようだが？
English: And... you had blood tests performed?

Japanese: 申し上げませんでしたかしら。
English: Didn't I mention?

Japanese: ‥‥ワタクシ、鑑識課の職員に ３名ほど、カレがいますのよ。
English: I have three boyfriends in forensics.

Japanese: とにかく、裁判長！ これは、正当な証拠品ではないッ！
English: In any case, Your Honor, I can't accept this as evidence!

Japanese: なんだって‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: 法廷における《証拠法》の２大原則 ‥‥知らないわけではあるまい。
English: You must know the two rules of evidence law!

Japanese: 《その１：警察局の認可のない 証拠品の提示は認められない》
English: Rule 1: no evidence shall be shown without the approval of the Police Department!

Japanese: つまり、このクツは違法な証拠だ。 少なくとも、現時点では‥‥
English: In other words, this shoe is illegal evidence! At least, for the time being!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！ 成歩堂さん！
English: I-is that right, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: た‥‥たぶんね。 御剣のヤツ、勝ちホコってるし。
English: It seems so. Edgeworth sure is celebrating.

Japanese: ‥‥そうはいかないよ、検事サマ。
English: Not so fast, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! 忘れてもらっちゃ困るねェ。 このおキョウは‥‥元・捜査官！
English: Don't forget... I used to be a detective!

Japanese: ‥‥先ほど、 申し上げたハズですわよ。
English: As I mentioned previously...

Japanese: このクツは、すでに鑑識課員による 検分が済んでおりますの。
English: This shoe has already been tested by a member of the forensics department!

Japanese: このとおり。今日の日付で 警察局の認可も受けておりますの。
English: As you can see, it was approved by the Police Department as of... today.

Japanese: 一般人であっても、正式な証拠品の 提示はできますのよ‥‥検事サマ。
English: Even the general public can produce official evidence, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ぐ‥‥‥ぐッ！
English: Nuh... Ungh!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！ 成歩堂さん！
English: I-is that right, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: た‥‥たぶんね。 御剣のヤツ、ダマっちまったし。
English: It seems so. Edgeworth is looking pretty sullen.

Japanese: もう！　《証拠法》ぐらい ベンキョウしておきましょうよ！
English: You could at least study some evidence law! Really!

Japanese: 検察側の事情はともかく‥‥
English: The prosecution's complaints notwithstanding...

Japanese: 《証拠法》の第１原則は、 モンダイないようです。
English: It appears that this evidence satisfies the first rule of evidence law.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥あなたに対する 処罰がまた１つ、増えましたな。
English: Well... It seems you have yet another count against you, witness.

Japanese: ‥‥罪人に、正しい裁きを。 すべてはカクゴの上ですわ。
English: Anything to ensure that the guilty are properly judged.

Japanese: 《被害者のクツ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victim's Shoe added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright, you may cross-examine the witness!

Japanese: ～決定的な証拠品～
English: -- Decisive Evidence --

Japanese: 約５分間、現場から目を離したコト ‥‥申し上げるべきでしたわね。
English: I should have mentioned those five minutes when I wasn't looking at the crime scene.

Japanese: では。被害者のクツが脱げたコト ‥‥これはお話ししたかしら？
English: And now, to the matter of the victim's shoe... Did I not bring this up...?

Japanese: 調べてみたら‥‥２種類の血痕が。 １つはモチロン、被害者のモノ。
English: Two types of blood were found on this shoe! One was of course the victim's.

Japanese: そして、もう１つは‥‥そう！ 被告人・宝月　巴のモノなのさ！
English: And the other was...! The defendant, Ms. Lana Skye's blood!

Japanese: このクツは‥‥なんのムジュンも ない、カンペキな証拠品ですわ！
English: This shoe proves it! It's flawless, decisive evidence!

Japanese: （証拠品をかくしておく なんて‥‥ズルいぞ！）
English: (That's pretty sly, hiding evidence like that!)

Japanese: 法律を武器に戦う弁護士さんが “ズルい”はないです！
English: There's nothing sly about a lawyer using the law as a weapon!

Japanese: とにかく！　カガクはいつも あたしたちのミカタですから！
English: In any case, science is always on our side!

Japanese: 忘れないでくださいね！ これからの捜査は、カガクですっ！
English: Don't forget! Scientific investigation is the wave of the future!

Japanese: （証拠品を‥‥“調べる”か‥‥）
English: (Hmm... maybe I should "investigate" this evidence a little more closely...)

Japanese: どうして、そんなウソを？
English: Why did you lie about those five minutes?

Japanese: 結果だけを見てほしかった‥‥ そう言うべきかしらねえ。
English: I guess you could say, I just wanted people to look at the results.
The... results?

Japanese: 何度も申し上げますが、ワタクシ。 この目でハッキリ見ましたのよ。
English: How many times do I have to say this?

Japanese: 主席検事サマが、被害者を ナイフで刺す、その瞬間を！
English: I saw the chief prosecutor stab the victim before my very own eyes!

Japanese: “５分間の空白”など‥‥ ササイなコトにすぎない！
English: Compared to that... A five minute "blank" means nothing!

Japanese: そう思うのならば‥‥ 真実を証言すればよかった。
English: Then why didn't you just tell the truth?

Japanese: ‥‥笑わせるねえ‥‥
English: Don't make me laugh!

Japanese: 相手は、この世で最も信用できない 検事局のトップ、主席検事サマ。
English: We're dealing with the most untrustworthy of the vile lot known as prosecutors!

Japanese: “でっち上げ”“証言の取引” “もみ消し”“証拠品の操作”‥‥
English: Falsified evidence, arranged testimonies, erasing and manipulating evidence...

Japanese: そんなバケモノと戦うのならば！ こちらも手段は選ばないさ。
English: When you fight monsters, you need to use every trick in the book!

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥“自分がやった” って、言い張ってるんだけどな）
English: (This when the suspect is admitting she did it?)

Japanese: ‥‥イツワリの証言こそ、 最もニクむべき犯罪です。
English: False testimony is the most despicable crime of all, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: 必要な証言だけ、さっさと すませていただきたいですな。
English: Let's just get this over with.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥そのクツを 現場で発見したのですか？
English: And, you found this shoe at the scene of the crime?

Japanese: 主席検事サマの身柄をカクホして、 警察局に通報をして‥‥
English: I detained the chief prosecutor, and notified the Police Department...

Japanese: 刑事の到着までに、何かするべき コトがないか、考えましたの。
English: I wanted to make myself useful while I was waiting for the police to arrive.

Japanese: それで‥‥イタズラ好きな子犬の マネをした、というワケか。
English: So, like an ill-trained pooch, you snuck off with a shoe!

Japanese: “主席検事の犯罪”‥‥検事局は、 それをモミ消すかもしれない。
English: I was afraid someone would erase the chief prosecutor's crime.

Japanese: そうなったときの最後の手段として ‥‥このクツをカクホしましたの。
English: This shoe was my secret weapon if that should happen.

Japanese: おキョウのウデにくいこんだ、 このシャレたバスケット‥‥
English: See this fashionable basket I have here...?

Japanese: 運ぶのは、なにも弁当だけとは かぎりませんのよ！
English: It carries more than lunchboxes, gentlemen!

Japanese: （ぼくをニラまれてもなあ‥‥）
English: (I'm happy for you and your lunchbox bag, really.)

Japanese: とにかく、あなたは重要な証拠品を 現場から持ち去りました。
English: In any case, you removed valuable evidence from the scene of the crime.

Japanese: それからどうしたか、 証言をつづけてください！
English: Now, tell us what you did next.

Japanese: たしか‥‥“鑑識課”に 持ちこんだのでしたね？
English: So, you brought it to the forensics department?

Japanese: 証拠品として提示するならば、 警察局の認可が必要‥‥
English: If you're going to submit something as evidence in court, you need it approved.

Japanese: そのためには、証拠品の検分‥‥ すなわち“鑑識”が必要ですわ。
English: To do that, evidence must be analyzed... by a forensics expert.

Japanese: （元・捜査官のおキョウさんだから できた、必殺の荒技だな‥‥）
English: (And she got away with her little coup because she used to be a detective...)

Japanese: ‥‥クツには、たしかに 血痕が残っていますな。
English: The shoe does appear to have bloodstains on it.

Japanese: ヒトが刺されたのですから トーゼン、ですわね。
English: Makes sense. After all, a man was stabbed here.

Japanese: やはり、被害者・多田敷捜査官の 血液だったわけですか。
English: And that blood belonged to the victim, Detective Goodman?

Japanese: 申し上げたハズ。‥‥そのクツには ２種類の血痕が付着している‥‥
English: As I said, there were two types of blood found on the shoe.

Japanese: 血液検査だけで、被告人と 断定することはできません！
English: You can't say for sure the blood belonged to the victim with a blood test!

Japanese: ボウヤ‥‥アンタ、血液検査の 何を知っていると言うのさ。
English: You claim to know something about blood tests, Rookie?
Huh...

Japanese: このおキョウに、話してごらんよ！
English: Well, speak up!

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥血液型には、Ａ型と Ｂ型とＯ型とＡＢ型があって‥‥
English: Uh, well... Blood comes in four types... A, B, O, and AB...

Japanese: あと、たしか‥‥ Ａ型は“神経質”です！
English: However! You can't tell from a blood test whether a murder was performed... in cold blood!
That's just a figure of speech, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 血液の分類法をゼンブ合わせたら、 数百万種になるってウワサですよ！
English: Actually, if you combine all the various blood tests, there are millions of types!

Japanese: ほぼ、個人を特定することが 可能なんです！　ウワサですけど。
English: It's practically impossible to narrow a blood sample down to one person! Or so I hear.

Japanese: す、すうひゃくまん‥‥
English: M-millions of types?

Japanese: もう少し時間があったら、 ＤＮＡ鑑定の結果も出たけどねェ。
English: If I had a little more time, I would have gotten DNA test results...

Japanese: 宝月　巴の血液だと考えて、 まずまちがいないそうですわよ。
English: But they said there's very little doubt it could be anyone's but Ms. Lana Skye's.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥被害者のクツに、 被告人の血液、ですか‥‥
English: Hmm... So the suspect's blood was found on the victim's shoe...

Japanese: 被告人は‥‥多田敷捜査官の死に、 直接的に関わっていたコトになる。
English: That ties her directly to the death of Detective Goodman!

Japanese: （なんてこった‥‥）
English: (I was afraid he was going to say that...)

Japanese: （‥‥おキョウさんの主張を 認めるわけにはいかない！）
English: (I can't let this evidence go through without a fight!)

Japanese: こちら‥‥シオカラ弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: You ordered the peppered fish guts, right?

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! オトナの味を おぼえるコトだねェ、ボウヤ。
English: Some like it hot, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタの依頼人は、取り返しの つかないおイタをしちまったのさ。
English: Some, like your client. She's in enough hot water to make a whole batch of soup.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　このクツ、 何かモンダイはないんですか？
English: Mr. Wright! Do you or don't you have a problem with this shoe!?

Japanese: “モンダイ”‥‥
English: A problem...?

Japanese: （ここは、重要なポイントだ。 被害者のクツ‥‥どうなんだ？）
English: (This is critical! What's wrong with the victim's shoe?)

Japanese: （ダメだ！　ムリにケチを つけたら、逆効果だ‥‥）
English: (No... there's nothing there. And if I just stab blindly at it, I'll hurt my case.)

Japanese: 成歩堂さんなら！ きっと見つけてくれますよね！
English: C'mon, Mr. Wright, I know you can find something!

Japanese: 何かテキトーな“ムジュン”を！
English: Some kind of off-the-cuff contradiction!

Japanese: 何かテキトーなコトを 言わないでほしいな‥‥
English: I'm trying to avoid saying things off-the-cuff today.

Japanese: ぼくの、気のせいでなければ‥‥
English: If I'm not imagining things...

Japanese: このクツの、ある“ポイント”に、 大きな“ムジュン”があります！
English: I'd say there is one critical problem with this evidence... A clear contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥その、目‥‥ やっぱり、ボウヤだねェ。
English: That gleam in your eyes... You're still young, Rookie.

Japanese: シオカラ弁当のシオからさ‥‥ とっくり味わうがいいさ！
English: I'd give you a peppered fish gut now, but you couldn't take the heat, could you!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人に うかがいましょう。
English: Let's hear what Mr. Wright has to say!

Japanese: 被害者のクツの、“どこ”に ムジュンがあるのか‥‥？
English: What is contradictory about the victim's shoe?

Japanese: モンダイのあるポイントを 指摘してください！
English: Show us the problem with this evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥モンダイのある点。 それは、まさに“ここ”です！
English: The problem with this evidence... is here!

Japanese: どこですか？
English: Where?

Japanese: え。
English: Uh...

Japanese: その指先‥‥自分のアタマでも さしておくことだ。
English: Take that finger and point it at your own head, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （うう‥‥慣れないコトで とまどってしまったかな）
English: (Hmm... guess that wasn't it.)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。証拠品‥‥もっと キチンと調べてみましょうよ！
English: Mr. Wright, let's be scientific about this! Examine the evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、やはり。 ムダだったようですな。
English: As I thought, a waste of time.

Japanese: ここまで来てアレですが‥‥ 証言に戻っていただきます。
English: Well, that was a nice break. Let's return to the testimony, shall we?

Japanese: 証人。‥‥気づいていましたか？ このクツ底には、血痕があります。
English: I wonder if you noticed... there's blood on the bottom of this shoe!

Japanese: ‥‥おキョウを、あんまり ナメないコトだねェ‥‥
English: Don't mess with me, Rookie...

Japanese: おキョウのクツ底に、ボウヤの 血痕がコビりつくことになるよ！
English: Or it'll be your blood on the bottom of my shoe!

Japanese: クツ底‥‥たしかに、血痕が ベッタリ残っていますな。
English: Hmm... Indeed, there is quite a bit of blood on the bottom of the shoe.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者は、ナイフで刺された。 自然なコトだと思われるが‥‥
English: It makes sense. The victim was stabbed with a knife!

Japanese: いったい！　クツ底の血痕が、 何とムジュンしていると言うのか！
English: What could possibly be contradictory about blood on the bottom of his shoe!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... このクツで一度、カオを 踏まれてみるコトをオススメする。
English: I'm guessing your shoe has blood on it too... you just shot yourself in the foot!
Arrgh...

Japanese: おキョウのカカトで メリメリ言わせてやろうかッ！
English: Would you like a grinder, or a grind from the heel of my boot, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: （どうやら、 ハズしたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Man... tough crowd.)

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥この証拠は “カンペキ”からは、ほど遠い！
English: Unfortunately for you... this shoe is far from being perfect evidence!

Japanese: なんだってェ‥‥？
English: What!?

Japanese: 現場の状況と、このクツは‥‥ アキラカにムジュンしています！
English: This shoe directly contradicts what we know about the crime! The shoe doesn't fit!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: このクツに‥‥ “ムジュン”がある、と‥‥？
English: You're claiming there's a contradiction with this shoe?

Japanese: どう考えても‥‥ 現場の状況に合わないのです。
English: As I said, the shoe doesn't fit what we know about the scene of the crime.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人に うかがいましょう。
English: Very well, let's hear what Mr. Wright has to say about the shoe!

Japanese: このクツのどこが、現場の状況と ムジュンしているのか‥‥？
English: What about this shoe contradicts what we know about the crime?

Japanese: モンダイのあるポイントを 指摘してください！
English: Show us the problem with this evidence!

Japanese: モンダイは‥‥“足跡”です。
English: The problem lies... in the footprint.

Japanese: ‥‥あしあと‥‥？
English: The... footprint?

Japanese: このとおり、被害者のクツの底には 血痕がハッキリ、残っています。
English: Note that the bottom of the victim's shoe is covered in blood.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば！
English: Then... isn't it strange?

Japanese: 現場であるクルマのそばには、 “血の足跡”が残るハズです！
English: Why weren't any bloody footprints found by the scene of the crime!?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah hah!

Japanese: しかし、このとおり！ 現場には、なんの痕跡もない！
English: As you can see... There were no traces of any such footprints at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: これはあきらかに、 ムジュンしています！
English: That contradicts your claim about this shoe!

Japanese: この写真で見えるのは、床の一部！ 血痕がなかったとは、言い切れぬ！
English: This picture only shows part of the floor, so there could have been bloody footprints.

Japanese: もし、床に血痕が残っていれば！ 捜査でイヤでも発見されるハズ！
English: If there were bloody prints they would have been found.

Japanese: ぼくたちも現場は調べました。 しかし、そんな血痕はなかった！
English: We checked the scene and found nothing of the sort!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 証人！　どうなのですかッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! Well, witness!?

Japanese: え‥‥ッ！　そ、それは‥‥
English: What!? Huh? I, uh...

Japanese: さ。さすが、成歩堂さんですっ！ でも‥‥
English: Great going, Mr. Wright! But...

Japanese: たしかに、足跡がないのは ムジュンしてますけど‥‥
English: It's true that the lack of a footprint is a contradiction...

Japanese: じゃあ、どうして ないんですか？　足跡。
English: But then we have to ask why there wasn't a footprint!
Oh!

Japanese: たしかに！　モンダイは、 足跡が残っていない“理由”です！
English: That's true! There has to be a reason why there wasn't a footprint!

Japanese: 教えてください、成歩堂さんっ！
English: Think, Mr. Wright, think!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥まあ。ぼくは、ムジュンを 見つけるのがトクイなヒトだから。
English: Hey, I don't know why it's not there. I'm just good at finding contradictions.

Japanese: なんですか、それ！
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど、な。 そういうコト、だったか‥‥
English: I see... Now I get it!

Japanese: （な、なんだ？ 何をナットクしている‥‥？）
English: (Get what!?)

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、ヤッカイな証人だ。 最後まで、我々をまどわせる‥‥
English: Our witness is more devious than I gave her credit for! We were hoodwinked to the very end!

Japanese: しかし。‥‥その証言の中に、 ヒントはかくされていたようだ。
English: But she slipped! There is one vital hint to the truth in her testimony...

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですかッ！
English: Wh-what are you talking about?

Japanese: 思い出していただきたい。 被告人をタイホしたときの証言‥‥
English: Think back to when she told us about apprehending the suspect...

Japanese: ‥‥主席検事殿の抵抗は、 それは見苦しいモノでしたわ。
English: The chief prosecutor tried to resist, but her efforts were in vain.

Japanese: この手を振りはらって ドラム缶をケリ倒してみたり‥‥
English: She knocked my hands aside, kicked over an oil drum...

Japanese: ‥‥カワイイカオをして、 ワリと女豹でしたわねェ‥‥
English: Oh, she's beautiful, but deadly! A predator, this one! A leopard woman! Rowr!

Japanese: ‥‥冷静な主席検事にしては、 妙な行動だと思っていたのだが‥‥
English: I thought that was a strange thing for the normally cool- headed chief to do.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (No kidding!)

Japanese: さて、証人。１つだけ カクニンさせてもらいたい。
English: Now, witness. Allow me to ask a very simple question.

Japanese: その“ドラム缶”‥‥ 中は、カラだったのだろうか？
English: This "oil drum"... was it empty?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ そういうコト、かい。 ...
English: Oh, that, hmm?

Japanese: アンタの根性は気に入らないさ、 御剣検事サマ。でも‥‥
English: I'm not sure I like your attitude, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: やっぱり、アタマの回転は 悪くないようだねェ。
English: Though apparently you're not the slowest conveyor belt in the lunchbox factory.

Japanese: 証人！　で、では！ ドラム缶の中には‥‥？
English: Witness! W-well? Was the oil drum empty...?

Japanese: 主席検事サマが倒したドラム缶‥‥ 水が入っていたよ。
English: The oil drum kicked over by the chief prosecutor... was brimming with water.

Japanese: み‥‥みず、ですか‥‥？ （どういうコトだ‥‥）
English: W-water? (What does that mean?)

Japanese: まだ、わからないのかな？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: Still don't get it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 被告人がドラム缶を倒した‥‥ その、本当の理由がッ！
English: Do you want to know the reason she knocked it over? The REAL reason?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。あああああッ！ ま、まさか‥‥
English: Aaaa haaaaah! You don't mean...!

Japanese: そう。被告人が、ムリヤリ ドラム缶を倒した理由は、１つ！
English: Yes, the suspect knocked over that oil drum for one reason and one reason alone!

Japanese: 自らの罪の痕跡である血痕を 洗い流すためだったのだッ！
English: To erase the blood stains that would become evidence against her!

Japanese: う‥‥うおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: どうやら、カンゼンに コタエが出たようです！
English: That ties things up quite nicely!

Japanese: 被害者のクツに残った血痕は、 被告人と犯行を結びつけています！
English: The blood stains left on the victim's shoes tie her quite clearly to this murder!

Japanese: そして、犯行後！　犯行の痕跡を 消すため、ドラム缶を倒した！
English: Then, after the deed was done, she knocked over the oil drum to erase the telltale signs!

Japanese: 検事サマたちの得意ワザですもの。 ‥‥“証拠のインメツ”は。
English: Why, that's a prosecutor's specialty... erasing evidence!

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥たしかに、 右手にケガをしていた）
English: (That reminds me... Ms. Skye's right hand was hurt...)

Japanese: （『刺したとき、手を切った』 ‥‥そう言ってたな、たしか）
English: (Didn't she say she'd cut herself when she stabbed him...?)

Japanese: クツについた、お姉ちゃんの血‥‥ やっぱり、そのときに‥‥？
English: So my sister's blood on the shoe... That's when it happened?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥これ以上、 審議の必要はないようです。
English: Well... I see no reason to prolong this trial.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん！ なんとかして！　おねがい！
English: M-Mr. Wright! Do something! Please!

Japanese: む‥‥‥ムリだよ！
English: W-what!? What can I do?

Japanese: ともえさんは、罪を認めてるし‥‥ 証拠をかくす工作までしている！
English: Your sister has confessed to the crime, and she tried to conceal it!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！
English: B-but...

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでだ。
English: Enough.

Japanese: すでにコトバは必要ない。 ‥‥判決をおねがいしよう。
English: There is no need for further debate. The verdict, Your Honor!

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥それでは。
English: Very well...

Japanese: おキョウさん、検察側の証人だよ！ 検事のミカタなんだよ！
English: But Angel Starr is on the prosecution's side!

Japanese: ドラム缶に水が入っていたなんて ‥‥ウソかもしれないじゃない！
English: She could have been lying about the water!

Japanese: 被告人、宝月　巴に 判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: そこのコムスメ‥‥ 今、なんて言ったのかしら？
English: Little girl... What did you just say?

Japanese: え‥‥？ あ、あたし、ですか？
English: Huh...? M-me?

Japanese: この、おキョウが‥‥ 検事の“ミカタ”だって‥‥？
English: Did you say that I, Angel Starr... was on the prosecution's side?

Japanese: だ、だって。そうじゃないですか！
English: W-well, yeah, you are!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、証拠を‥‥ 血痕を、消そうとしたなんて！
English: You're saying my sister hid evidence by erasing the bloody footprints!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥もう１ぱい、 オカワリがほしいようだねェ。
English: Well. I thought you'd had your fill, but here you are, demanding a second helping!

Japanese: 証拠品という名の、 とりそぼろ弁当がッ！
English: Another lunchbox... A lunchbox called "evidence"!

Japanese: ま、まさか、証人‥‥ あなたは、まだ何か証拠を‥‥
English: W-wait... Witness, don't tell me you have something else?

Japanese: すでに審理は終了した！
English: You've reached your verdict, Your Honor!

Japanese: これ以上の勝手な発言には、 法廷侮辱罪の適用を要請するッ！
English: Any further comments will be held in contempt of court!

Japanese: どうせゲロまみれさ。 そんなオドシは通用しないよ！
English: Your threats don't scare the Cough-up Queen!

Japanese: ‥‥これを見るがいいさッ！
English: Look at this!!!

Japanese: ‥‥この、写真は‥‥
English: A photograph...?

Japanese: あの白いクツが、 本当に被害者のものかどうか‥‥
English: I had this just in case anyone had the gall to suggest...

Japanese: 弁護士がケチをつけたら、 たたきつけようと思っていたのさ。
English: that the white shoe didn't belong to the victim!

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥さすが、 ヌカリがないですなあ‥‥
English: Hmm... I see no room for error in this evidence.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん！　見て！ この、写真のアスファルト‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright, wait! Look at the asphalt in this photo!

Japanese: あ‥‥！　ハッキリと‥‥ ぬれているのがわかる！
English: Hey! It's clearly wet!

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ、 ギモンの余地はありません。
English: Erasing the last trace of doubt from the court's mind.

Japanese: 事件の起こった直後‥‥現場は、 水によって洗い流されていた！
English: Immediately after the murder, the crime scene was washed with water!

Japanese: ご、ゴメンなさい‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: I-I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥あたし。 ダメ、だったんですね‥‥
English: I guess I... I couldn't help after all.

Japanese: （‥‥しかたなかったんだ‥‥ 最初から、わかっていた）
English: (It's not your fault... I knew I couldn't win this case from the beginning.)

Japanese: （それに、この結末は‥‥ ともえさんが望んだコトだ！）
English: (And... it seems this is what your sister wanted anyway!)
(I'm sorry, Mia...)
Wright... wet or not...

Japanese: ‥‥うつむくのは早いわ、 なるほどくん‥‥
English: Don't be so quick to throw in the towel...

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! ‥‥カオを上げて‥‥ もう一度、よく見るの‥‥
English: Get yourself up off the asphalt... take another good look...

Japanese: ‥‥最後まで あきらめちゃダメ‥‥
English: Don't give up... Not until the bitter end.

Japanese: （これが‥‥最後の証拠品！）
English: (This is the last piece of evidence...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、今度こそ！ 判決を言いわたします！
English: Very well! This time I'd like to declare a verdict for good!

Japanese: 裁判長！　お待ちください！
English: Your Honor, wait!

Japanese: な、なんですか！ みんなして、判決をジャマして！
English: What is it with you people!? Can't I hand down my verdicts in peace anymore!?

Japanese: ちょっと、あとにして もらえませんかな！
English: Whatever it is, can it wait?

Japanese: い、いやいや！ それじゃ、遅いですから。
English: N-no it can't. Then it will be too late!

Japanese: 最後に提出された、この写真‥‥ もう一度、見てください！
English: Look at this photograph, the last one submitted...

Japanese: この証拠品だけは‥‥まだ、 じゅうぶんなギロンをしていない！
English: This trial isn't over... until we give each piece of evidence proper consideration!

Japanese: すると、キサマは‥‥
English: So, Wright...

Japanese: この写真に何か、モンダイがある。 ‥‥そう主張するのか？
English: Are you saying there's a problem with this latest piece of evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥え！ （“考える”のは、後でいい‥‥）
English: Yeah! (I'll think later!)

Japanese: そう、モンダイがあります！ （今は、前に進むんだ‥‥！）
English: Yeah, there's a problem! (Right or wrong, I've got to go ahead with this!)

Japanese: ここまで来た以上、すべての ギロンは尽くされるべきです。
English: I suppose since we've come this far, we should give every claim a fair shake.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 示していただきましょう！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この写真の“モンダイ”‥‥ いったい、どこですかッ！
English: Show the court the problem in this photograph!

Japanese: それは、この辺でしょう！ よくわかりませんが！
English: Well... it's probably here! Don't you think?

Japanese: ‥‥人生のセンパイとして、 ヒトコト。アドバイスしましょう。
English: As your elder, Mr. Wright, let me give you a piece of advice...

Japanese: 人さし指をつきつけるとき、 目をつぶってはいけませんな。
English: When you point at something, at least remember to keep your eyes open?

Japanese: ここまで来て、ハッタリは 通用しない‥‥それだけのコト。
English: I believe he's trying to say that no one's falling for your bluffs, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: もう一度！　目を開いて、 カガク的に考えてみましょう！
English: Open your eyes, Mr. Wright! Think scientifically!

Japanese: モンダイになる点は‥‥ モチロン、ここです！
English: The problem in this photograph... is here!

Japanese: こ‥‥、これは‥‥
English: What's this...?

Japanese: クルマのマフラーから、 何かのぞいているではないかッ！
English: There's something poking out of the car's muffler!

Japanese: ちょっと、お待ちなさい！ ‥‥御剣検事ッ！
English: Wait just a moment, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: な、なんだろうか。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: あの‥‥今、“まふらー” と言いましたかな？
English: You just said "muffler"...

Japanese: ‥‥この写真に、エリマキは 写っていないようですが‥‥？
English: However I see no trace of a muffler or scarf of any kind in this photograph!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... “マフラー”とは‥‥ クルマやオートバイの部品、だ。
English: A muffler is also a part on a car or motorcycle, Your Honor.

Japanese: 排気用のパイプ‥‥と おぼえておけば、ハジはかかない。
English: Just think of it as... part of the exhaust system. A pipe...

Japanese: なるほどッ！　たしかに‥‥
English: I see! And... I see!

Japanese: “まふらあ”から、何かアヤシイ 布キレがのぞいているようです！
English: What's that suspicious-looking cloth sticking out of the car's muffler?

Japanese: ‥‥‥フン！ だから、なんだと言うのさ！
English: Hmph! So what if there is something sticking out of the muffler!

Japanese: そんなチッポケな布きれ‥‥ 事件とは無関係に決まってるッ！
English: What does that have to do with this case? Nothing! Absolutely nothing!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥おキョウさん。 そうはいきません。
English: Sorry, Ms. Starr... But it's not going to be that easy!

Japanese: じつは‥‥あなたはすでに、 証言しているんですよ。
English: In fact, you've already told us why this is important to the case...

Japanese: マフラーの中の布きれが、事件に 関係があるという可能性について！
English: You said as much in your testimony!!!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人の考えを うかがいましょう！
English: Let's hear what Mr. Wright has on his mind!

Japanese: マフラーの布きれが、事件に関係 すると考えられる、コンキョとは？
English: Tell us why you think this piece of cloth in the muffler is related to this case.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがでしょうかッ！
English: Well, Your Honor? How do you feel about that?

Japanese: 正直‥‥あまりいいとは言えない ようです。‥‥キゲンなら。
English: Actually, I don't feel well at all. I have this pounding headache...

Japanese: いやいや！　“ゴキゲンいかが” と聞いたワケでは‥‥
English: No, no, I meant, what do you think about the...

Japanese: キゲンを悪くした原因は、 全面的にアナタですぞッ！
English: I'm afraid the reason for that headache is you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あの布きれ‥‥“マフラー”の 中から見つかったんですよね？
English: That cloth was found in the car's "muffler," right...?

Japanese: なんか、どこかで聞いたような。 ‥‥“マフラー”‥‥
English: Muffler... muffler... Where have I heard that word recently? Muffler...

Japanese: おキョウさん。
English: Ms. Starr!

Japanese: 思い出してください。 ‥‥あなた自身の証言です。
English: Recall your testimony for the court...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。タイホの瞬間、彼女は マフラーのことを口走ったの！
English: Ah yes. When I arrested her, she mentioned the muffler!

Japanese: それで先ほどの証言で、カンちがい してしまったのでしょう。
English: That's what had me confused in my earlier testimony!

Japanese: “マフラー”‥‥あ‥‥！ あああああ‥‥ああああああ‥‥
English: Muffler! argh! Yeearrrrgh!

Japanese: あなたが聞いたという、 “マフラー”のヒトコト‥‥
English: Could it be that the "muffler" you heard mentioned...

Japanese: この、排気用パイプのコト だったのではないでしょうか？
English: Was actually this exhaust pipe!?

Japanese: もし、そうならば！
English: If so!

Japanese: その布きれこそは、 重要な証拠物件になるのです！
English: That means this piece of cloth is vital evidence!

Japanese: う‥‥‥‥ きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: Oh... Whooooooooooo ooooooooooooragh!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥本法廷は、 中断せざるを得ないようです。
English: Well... It seems we will have to suspend the proceedings.
Sus... Suspend!?

Japanese: あの布きれに、イミがあるのか？ 私自身は、疑わしく思います。
English: I find myself wondering about that piece of cloth.

Japanese: しかし！　すべての疑問は、 アキラカにされなければならない。
English: If we leave any question unanswered here we do a disservice to the law!

Japanese: 至急、現場のクルマを調査して、 その布きれを提出するように。
English: Have the car at the crime scene inspected at once, and bring me that cloth!

Japanese: 判決は、それからです。 ‥‥よろしいですね？
English: The verdict will wait until after we've seen all the evidence. Agreed...?
I suppose so.

Japanese: （‥‥な。なんとか‥‥）
English: (Whew... that was close.)

Japanese: （しのいだみたいだな ‥‥この場は！）
English: (But... we made it... at least for now!)

Japanese: それでは！　これより、 ３０分間の休憩に入ります！
English: The court will adjourn for a thirty minute recess!

Japanese: そろそろ、おヒルごはんの 時間ですしね。
English: It's lunchtime after all!

Japanese: （まだ食べるのかよ！）
English: (He's still hungry!?)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！ 被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時５６分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 23, 11:56 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Um... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ん？　なに？
English: Huh? What?

Japanese: いつも、こんな調子なんですか？ なんていうか‥‥
English: Are trials... always like this with you?

Japanese: 見上げたら、頭上５センチのトコに 巨大なペンチが落ちてきていた‥‥
English: Like you're swimming up from the bottom of a lake, about to reach the surface...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな、やるせない 手遅れっぽい感じ。
English: But no matter how hard you paddle you never seem to get there...

Japanese: まあ、そうだね。
English: Pretty much.

Japanese: 今日落ちてきたのは、ペンチじゃ なくて、巨大なベンチだったけど。
English: Except today we're swimming in quicksand.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、ともえさんは？
English: So what happened to your sister, anyway?

Japanese: なんだか、裁判長さんの部屋に 呼ばれたみたいです。
English: Apparently she got called off to the judge's chambers.

Japanese: ああ‥‥たぶん、 あの布きれのコトかな。
English: Hmm... Probably something to do with that piece of cloth.

Japanese: さあ！　ここから、キセキの 大逆転が始まるワケですよね！
English: So! This is where we turn this trial around, right?

Japanese: あの、一見どうしようもない、 ちっぽけでササイな布きれから。
English: Our only weapon, a tiny, insignificant piece of cloth!

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥自分の存在が あの布きれみたいな気分だよ。
English: I'm the one who's starting to feel tiny and insignificant to tell the truth.

Japanese: ヘイ、カウボーイ！
English: Hola, Pardner!

Japanese: ウシってヤツは、赤い布きれを 見るとゲンキになる‥‥
English: They say you show a red cloth to a bull, it'll fire up its temper!

Japanese: オレは、そう聞かされて 大きくなったものだけどな。
English: That's what they told me when I was a young'un, at least.

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥！
English: Officer Marshall!

Japanese: 気になったからね、来てみたさ。 どうやら、遅れちまったようだよ。
English: Thought I'd come take a look- see at how the trial's going. Looks like I'm late.

Japanese: あいかわらずの厳戒態勢でね。 巡査が抜け出すのもヒト苦労、さ。
English: They've got the place locked down tighter than a fort in enemy territory!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥ 何か、あったんですか？
English: What is going on over there, anyway?

Japanese: ‥‥きのうから、ようすがヘン ですよね、ケイサツのヒトたち。
English: All the police I've seen these last two days have been really on edge.

Japanese: ヒトのシンパイをしてる場合じゃ ないだろう？　カウボーイ。
English: Don't you got enough on your plate without worrying about other people, compadre?

Japanese: 主席検事さんの、赤いマフラーの 巻き方でも考えておくことさ。
English: You could be worrying about the chief prosecutor's taste in mufflers, for example.

Japanese: あの‥‥罪門さん。
English: Um... Officer Marshall?

Japanese: “マフラー”は、エリマキのコト じゃなかったんですよ。
English: The whole "muffler" thing didn't have anything to do with scarves...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、あのときはマフラーを していませんでしたから。
English: She wasn't even wearing a scarf!

Japanese: へェ‥‥そうなのかい。 そいつは、オカシイなァ。
English: You don't say? Now don't that just beat all.

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ ...? 彼女の細いクビには、いつも 赤い風がそよ吹いているのさ。
English: I've seen the red breeze blow at her slender neck many a time...

Japanese: オレは、見たよ‥‥あの日も。 彼女のクビに、赤いマフラーを。
English: I saw it that day, too. She was wearing a red muffler.
What!?

Japanese: あの日の午後の式典でね。 御剣のヤツも、見ているはずさ。
English: At the awards ceremony that afternoon. Edgeworth's seen it too, I'd reckon.
(What does that mean!?)

Japanese: （現場で撮影された写真では‥‥ マフラーは消えている！）
English: (In the photograph taken at the crime scene... she wasn't wearing a scarf!)

Japanese: ‥‥おキョウさん。 見マチガイ‥‥じゃなかったんだ。
English: So, Ms. Starr wasn't mistaken...

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。そろそろ、時間だね。
English: Well, it's about time.

Japanese: チエをしぼるより、チチをしぼれ。 ‥‥あらくれ野郎どものコトバさ。
English: Remember, pardner, sometimes you gotta grab the bull by the horns...

Japanese: 考えたって、どうにもならない。 コタエは“時”がシボり出すさ。
English: and sometimes you just gotta let that bull go where it will. Time will tell!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ なんだか、イヤな予感がする）
English: (Ugh... I have a bad feeling about this.)

Japanese: いったい‥‥今度はナニが 落ちてくるんでしょうね。
English: So... what are we swimming in now, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 巨大なウシなら、オレがサバいて ステーキ弁当にしてやるさ！
English: If it's steak sauce, I can hook you up with some fine ribs! Ooh-wee!

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時３２分 地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 23, 12:32 PM District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: それでは、審理を‥‥再開‥‥‥‥
English: I'd... like to... resume...?

Japanese: （なんだ？　裁判長、検事席の方を 見て、カタまってるけど‥‥）
English: (What's up? The judge keeps looking over at the prosecution...)

Japanese: どうされたのですかな？ 御剣検事。
English: Is something wrong, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: カオがマッサオで、クチビルが ムラサキ、ひたいのアブラアセ‥‥
English: Your face is blue, your lips are purple, you're sweating bullets...

Japanese: ミケンのシワに、その歯ギシリ、 ぶるぶるフルえた上、ナミダ目‥‥
English: That furrowed brow, those grinding teeth, those watery eyes...

Japanese: さらにその目は焦点が合っておらず 全身がシボんで、背中はネジ曲がり
English: What's more your eyes are unfocused, you're doubled over, your back is bent...

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ぬッ！ こんな‥‥こんなコトがァァァ！
English: It... can't... be!! This... can't... happen!

Japanese: な、何かあったのかな、 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: I wonder what happened to Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: それでは、気にせず つづけたいと思います。
English: Well then, I believe it is time we continued on with this trial.

Japanese: さて。先ほどの休憩中、 検察側には、緊急の調査を‥‥
English: During our recess I had requested that the prosecution conduct an investigation...

Japanese: ‥‥バカなッ！ ユルされるコトではないッ！
English: Th-this is unacceptable!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ すっかり、放心状態ですな。
English: Hmm... It seems our prosecutor is quite beside himself.

Japanese: あ。ちょっと。 ちょっといいかな、みんな。
English: Ah, er, excuse me. Knock knock?

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ どなたですかな。 ...?
English: Who's there?

Japanese: （な、なんだ‥‥このオジサン）
English: (What's with this guy?)

Japanese: （妙にアツ苦しいオーラが、 法廷中に乱反射しているような）
English: (A strange, stuffy aura seems to be filling the courtroom.)

Japanese: あのオジサンが現れてから、 室温が３度、上がりました！
English: Hey! The temperature rose 5.7 degrees when that man came in!

Japanese: （いったい‥‥ナニモノなんだ！）
English: (Who on earth is he...?)

Japanese: あ。あなたは‥‥‥
English: Ah, it's you...

Japanese: や。どうもどうも。いやいや。
English: Oh! Oh, heh heh.

Japanese: 道がこんでてさー。チョイ、遅く なっちゃった。ゴメンねホント。
English: Sorry I'm late, Udgey! The roads were packed. It's just me!

Japanese: や。や。ひさしぶり、チョーさん。 最近どう？　泳いでる？
English: Long time no see, eh, Udgey? How ya been? Swim much these days!

Japanese: あ‥‥！　そのセツはどうも。 最近は忙しくて‥‥
English: Ah! Hello, hello. No, I've been so busy...

Japanese: あ。ダメダメ。ダメよチョーさん！ ヒマはさ。作るモンだよコレ。
English: Busy! Busy-smizzy, Udgey, my boy! You have to make time to relax!

Japanese: は、はあ。マッタク。 おハズカシイ‥‥ほっほっ。
English: Y-yes, indeed.

Japanese: “チョーさん”‥‥って、 裁判長のコト、かな‥‥
English: Udgey... seems to be his nickname for the judge...?

Japanese: オソロシイことに‥‥ どうやら、そうみたいだね。
English: I'm afraid you're right. Very afraid.

Japanese: あの‥‥すみません。あなたは？
English: Um... sorry, but... who are you?

Japanese: あ。あ。キミがナルホドちゃん？ 弁護士の。ウワサ聞いてるよー。
English: Ah hah! So you're Wrighto! The attorney! I've heard good things about you, son!

Japanese: え！　はあ、どうも‥‥
English: Eh? Uh, th-thanks...?

Japanese: なんか、ゴメンねホント。ウチの 御剣ちゃん、メイワクかけてさ。
English: So sorry about our little Worthy giving you all that trouble, eh?

Japanese: 今度さ。今度。どう、コレ。 泳ぎに行こうよ。みんなでさ。
English: You know, we should all go swimming together sometime! Jolly!

Japanese: み‥‥‥‥‥ “みつるぎちゃん”‥‥？
English: Little... Little Worthy...?

Japanese: コラッ、弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなた、地方警察局長のカオも 知らないのですかッ！
English: You don't know the district Chief of Police!?

Japanese: け‥‥‥‥‥ ケーサツキョクチョー‥‥？
English: Chuh....? Chief of Police...!?

Japanese: このへんの警察で イチバン偉いヒトです！
English: He's the top ranking police officer in the entire district!

Japanese: あ。巌徒　海慈（がんとかいじ）。 よろしく。よろしくね、みんな。
English: Name's Gant, Damon Gant. Pleased to meet you, everyone!

Japanese: あの‥‥それで、今日はいったい どうしたのですかな？
English: So, uh, to what do we owe this honor today?

Japanese: あなた自身が法廷にお出ましなど ‥‥２年ぶり、ではないですか。
English: It's been over... two years since you last came to this courtroom, hasn't it?

Japanese: いや、ホラ。御剣ちゃんたらさ。 あんな感じ、じゃない？
English: Well, it's Worthy, here. Look at the poor fellow!

Japanese: だからね。とりあえず‥‥ボク。 持ってきたワケ。コイツをね。
English: I just thought I'd help out... by bringing this.
