English: I-I'm sorry! I don't know!

Japanese: 右、０．４で、左０．２‥‥ メガネをかけたまえッ！
English: Well, that's 20 for the left, but nowhere near that for the right... Get glasses, officer!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ケーレイじゃなくて、 視力検査をしてるみたいですね。
English: That wasn't a salute... it was an eye test!

Japanese: ‥‥いいコンビになりそうだ。
English: They make a good pair.

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 23 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさん、 取調べ中らしいね。
English: Looks like Ms. Skye is in questioning...

Japanese: 刑事さんに、ドナられてるのかな。 お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: I hope the detectives aren't yelling at her...

Japanese: 《検事局と警察局の事件‥‥ どうやって同時にやったッス？》
English: "How did you kill him in two places at the same time!"

Japanese: ‥‥なんて。
English: Can you imagine?

Japanese: 答えにくい質問だよなあ。 なんにしても。
English: How's she supposed to answer that!

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Wait a second...)

Japanese: （警察局側の“ハンニン”が タイホされたハズだよな‥‥）
English: (The Chief said they'd caught the criminal at the Police Department!)

Japanese: あとで、また来てみましょう！
English: Let's come back later!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 検事局で起きた殺人事件に、 警察局で起きた殺人事件‥‥
English: One murder at the Prosecutor's Office... one at the Police Department...

Japanese: 次はここじゃないかと、 震え上がっている。
English: He must be frozen with fear thinking that he might be next...

Japanese: 勝手なコメント、しない！
English: Don't scare the poor guy!

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 23 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: でも‥‥あたし。ほんの 少しだけ、安心しちゃいました。
English: Still, I do feel better about things. A little.

Japanese: だって、多田敷刑事さんを刺した ハンニン、つかまったんでしょ？
English: I mean, they caught the person who stabbed Detective Goodman, didn't they?

Japanese: ええと‥‥。まあ、そうなのかな。
English: Uh, yeah, I guess they did.

Japanese: （ギロンするのはやめておこう。 ‥‥こんがらがるばかりだ）
English: (Best to not go too far down that road right now. Things will just get confusing.)

Japanese: キイイィィ‥‥ィィイイ‥‥ンン！
English: *WHEEEEEEEEEEEEN*

Japanese: な‥‥、なんですか？　今の‥‥
English: Wh-what was that!?

Japanese: ‥‥だから！　本官はあの。 加害者というのはどうかとその！
English: Sir! That's what I'm saying! Me, a perpetrator?

Japanese: ‥‥むしろ！　本官はこの。 被害者というべきであるとその！
English: I-I-I'd say I-I-I was the perpetrated against, sir! That's what I'd say!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。ど、どうも！ こんにちはッ！　‥‥であります！
English: Ah, oh, uh. Hi. Greetings, sir!

Japanese: あ、あなたは。たしか‥‥
English: Wait, I know who you are...

Japanese: スミマセン！　御剣検事ってヒト、 いらっしゃるでありますかッ！
English: Excuse me! But is Mr. Edgeworth, uh... anywhere on the premises?

Japanese: 本官！　警察局長のお使いで本官！ 報告書を持ってきたでありますッ！
English: I'm here, sir, at the request of the Chief, sir! I've got your report, sir!

Japanese: へえー。おまわりさん、 留置所の看守だったんですか？
English: Officer Meekins! So you're a guard here at the detention center?

Japanese: いえッ！　本官、アレであります！ 子羊のような巡査でありましてッ！
English: No, sir! I'm not, sir! I'm a little lost patrolman, like a little lost lamb, sir!

Japanese: あ、わかった！　今日は、こっちに 報告書を届けに来たんですね？
English: Oh, I get it. You're here to deliver a report?

Japanese: い。いやその本官。あの。 なんというかこの、本官その。
English: No, sir, I, uh, how should I say this...
(Wait... he isn't... is he?)

Japanese: もしかして、あなたが‥‥ 多田敷　道夫捜査官を？
English: You... Officer Meekins... You didn't... did you?

Japanese: ハンニンの原灰（はらばい）巡査で ありますからしてェェェェェェッ！
English: Perpetrator Officer Meekins reporting, sir!!!
What...!?

Japanese: ええええええええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: （これは‥‥予想外の展開だな）
English: (Now this is an unexpected turn of events...)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 被告人が巡査ということで、明日は 我が身か、と震え上がっている。
English: Seeing a fellow patrolman in here must make him worry about his own fate in life.

Japanese: 勝手なコメント、しない！
English: No comments from the peanut gallery!

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: ハラバイ巡査が、ときおり ビクッ！　と振り返っている。
English: Poor Officer Meekins... I think he knows he's being watched.

Japanese: 本官！　本来は、総務課の 巡査でありますからしてェェェッ！
English: Sir! I'm a patrolman with General Affairs, sir! SIR!

Japanese: きき、聞こえてますから、 フツーにおねがいします！
English: Ow. I can hear you fine, Officer Meekins.

Japanese: ‥‥あの日、たまたま用があって 証拠保管室へ行ったところ‥‥
English: I had some business that day, sir, and so I went to the evidence room, sir...

Japanese: 保管室前の警備室が、 カラっぽだったのであります。
English: The guard office in front of the room was empty, sir!

Japanese: つまり‥‥いつもは、保管室は 警備員が見張っているんですね？
English: So, normally there's a guard at the evidence room?

Japanese: なにぶん証拠保管室は、証拠を 保管しているでありますから。
English: That's right, sir! Because evidence is kept in the evidence room, sir!

Japanese: 保安課の巡査‥‥罪門巡査が 警備を担当しているでありますッ！
English: Now, the security officer... was none other than Officer Marshall!

Japanese: （ざ、ザイモン‥‥？）
English: (M-Marshall!?)

Japanese: それで、本官。ふと警備室の モニターを見たら‥‥
English: Then, sir, I happened to glance at the security room monitor! That's when I saw him, sir!

Japanese: 保管室にその。いわゆる不審者が。 そう！　不審者を見たであります！
English: A suspicious person in the evidence room! A suspicious person, sir!

Japanese: 不審者ッ！ あいててててててててててッ！
English: A suspicious person! Yowwowowowowowow!!!

Japanese: （ナニやってんだ、このヒト‥‥）
English: (What the heck is this guy doing?)

Japanese: それで、どうなったんですか？
English: So what happened then?

Japanese: その後は、本官‥‥カーッとこう、 アタマがまっ白になっちまって。
English: After that, sir, I... I... everything went white! I saw red! I blacked out!

Japanese: ‥‥フト気がついたら、今日。 こうして留置所に。
English: And... when I came to, I was here. In the detention center.

Japanese: （長すぎるだろ！ まっ白の時間が‥‥）
English: (How long were you out!? Days!?)

Japanese: あの‥‥ところで。 その手、どうなさったんですか？
English: Um, might I ask... what happened to your hand?

Japanese: はッ！　本官。ホータイを 巻いてくれるヒトもいないので！
English: Sir! There was no one to bandage me, sir!

Japanese: 自分なりにこう、グルグルとコレ、 巻いてみたのであります！
English: So I did what I could to wrap it up, sir!

Japanese: （手に包帯といえば‥‥ ともえさんといっしょだな‥‥）
English: (A bandage on his hand... just like Ms. Skye.)

Japanese: ‥‥どこまでも“同時”ですね。 警察局と、検事局の事件‥‥
English: Yet another similarity between this case and the one at the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: とにかく‥‥聞かせてください。 その手のケガについて。
English: First things first... tell us how you hurt your hand!

Japanese: あの。失礼ですけど、 ハンニン‥‥なんですよね。
English: Um, I don't mean to pry, but you are the perpetrator, correct?

Japanese: 多田敷　道夫捜査官を、 証拠保管室で殺害した‥‥
English: You killed Detective Bruce Goodman in the evidence room... right?

Japanese: 本官を‥‥‥そんなカナしい目で 見つめないでほしいでありますッ！
English: Sir... Please don't look at me with those sad puppy dog eyes, sir!

Japanese: ヒトを、あえて“被害者”と “加害者”にくくるとすればッ！
English: If you have to label me as persecutor or victim, sir...

Japanese: さしづめ本官などは かなり“被害者”のほうに‥‥
English: Then label me victim!

Japanese: いやいや！　こうして つかまってるワケじゃないですか。
English: Um, I would, but you happen to be in detention. And alive and well at that.

Japanese: は。まあ。たしかに。まあ。 本官アレなんでありますけれども。
English: Ah, yes, well, that's true, sir. I suppose you could say that.

Japanese: 被害者の多田敷捜査官とは、 知り合いだったんですか？
English: Did you know the victim, Detective Goodman?

Japanese: その！　ヒトをあえて“他人”と “赤の他人”にくくるとすればッ！
English: Well, sir, if I had to label him as a "stranger" or a "total stranger"...

Japanese: さしづめ本官などは、 “まっ赤”寄りでありましてェェ！
English: Then I'd say he leans heavily on the "total stranger" side!

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥ 知らなかったワケですか？
English: So... you didn't know him?

Japanese: 本官、日の当たらない部署で 働いているでありますからして。
English: Sir! I work in a tiny department, devoid of light or other creature comforts!

Japanese: 捜査官のカタに、知り合いなど いないのでありますからして！
English: I don't know any detectives!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥なぜ、知らないヒトを 刺しちゃったんですか？
English: So, if he was a total stranger, why did you stab him?

Japanese: だ、だから本官！　殺すつもり なんて、なかったであります！
English: Sir! I had n-no intention of killing him, sir! None!

Japanese: というか本官！　殺したつもりも ないのでありますからしてェェッ！
English: N-nor do I have any recollection of k-killing him, sir!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ コンランしてるみたいだな）
English: (At least someone around here is more confused than I am.)

Japanese: “思い出したこと”‥‥ 聞かせていただけますか？
English: Can you tell me what it is you do "remember"?

Japanese: 本官‥‥なにぶん総務課の、迷える 子羊のような巡査でありますから。
English: Well sir, you might say I'm a lost little patrolman. A lost little lamb, if you will!

Japanese: 保管室にいた多田敷捜査官殿は、 存じ上げなかったであります。
English: I didn't know Mr. Detective Goodman who was in the evidence room.

Japanese: だから‥‥“不審者”だと 思いこんだワケですか‥‥
English: And that's why you thought he looked suspicious...

Japanese: 保管室に入った本官は、ＩＤカード の提示を求めたであります！
English: Sir, I entered the evidence room, and asked the man to display his ID card!

Japanese: なんだ。やるべきコト、 キチンとやってるじゃないですか！
English: Well, that sounds pretty much by the book so far.

Japanese: そ、そうなんであります！　本官！ それが言いたかったであります！
English: Th-that's right, sir! That's what I've been trying to tell you!

Japanese: それで‥‥ＩＤの提示を求められた 多田敷捜査官は？　どうしました？
English: So you asked Detective Goodman to show his ID card. What did he do?

Japanese: なんとッ！　いきなり、本官に ナイフを向けたであります！
English: That's the thing! Suddenly he pointed a knife at me!
What...!?

Japanese: 本官。もう、ナニがなんだか わからなくなって。
English: Sir, I assure you I was as flustered as you are right now!

Japanese: 奇声を上げて、飛びかかって いっちゃったであります！
English: So I whooped and leapt at him!

Japanese: （多田敷捜査官が‥‥ ナイフを向けてきた？）
English: (Detective Goodman pointed a knife at him?)

Japanese: 『やられる前に、やれ！』‥‥ 原灰家の家訓でありますからして！
English: "Do unto to others before they do unto you"! My own father's words, sir!

Japanese: そ。それから、どうなりました！
English: Wh-what happened then?

Japanese: それから‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: 本官。アタマがまっ白になって。 気がついたら、ここに。
English: My eyes, sir... everything went white. When I awoke, I was here.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Right...)

Japanese: その、手‥‥やっぱり、 事件があったときに？
English: And your hand... that happened when Detective Goodman was stabbed?

Japanese: まあそのコレ本官ンンンンッ‥‥‥ ‥‥ジツは、そうでありまして。
English: Well! You see, sir, I, er...

Japanese: ダメじゃないですか！ ちゃんと、自首しなくちゃ。
English: Don't you think you should just confess?

Japanese: だって！　本官、でも。だって！ しかたなかったでありますッ！
English: But, sir! Sir! But! There was nothing I could do!

Japanese: しかたなかった‥‥？
English: "Nothing you could do"...?

Japanese: じつは本官、アレ！ アレであります本官からしてッ！
English: Sir, to tell the truth, sir, when it happened...

Japanese: 捜査官殿にナイフを向けられて、 “わー”ってなっちゃって。
English: When the detective pointed that knife at me, I just hollered, sir!

Japanese: 気がついたら、キゼツ していたのであります！
English: And the next thing I knew, I was unconscious!

Japanese: き、キゼツ‥‥
English: The next thing you knew you were... huh?

Japanese: そして、本官。同僚の巡査に たたき起こされたでありますが‥‥
English: Then, when I opened my eyes...

Japanese: 保管室には、ダレもいなかった！ 本官、なんとヒトリぽっち！
English: I was alone in the evidence room, sir! All alone!

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官殿は‥‥ 消えたのでありますからしてェッ！
English: Alone, because... Because Detective Goodman had disappeared!!!

Japanese: えええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: フト気がつけば、手ェから どくどく血がコレ本官！
English: Then when I looked down, I was gushing blood from my hand, sir!

Japanese: さみしかったでありますッ！ ‥‥本官、ジツにコレッ！
English: Oh, the shock! Oh, the sorrow, sir! Can you imagine how I felt?

Japanese: （被害者の死体が‥‥ “消えた”‥‥？）
English: (The victim's body... disappeared...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。 ビミョーな話ですねえ‥‥
English: Hmm... That's some story.

Japanese: ケッキョク‥‥ハラバイさん、 どうしてタイホされたんですか？
English: So, Officer Meekins, why was it that they arrested you?

Japanese: どういうこと？　あかねちゃん。
English: What do you mean, Ema?

Japanese: ちょっと、情報を 整理してみましょうよ。
English: Let's look at what we know.

Japanese: ハラバイさん、多田敷さんのカオを 知らなかったんですよ。そして‥‥
English: Now, Officer Meekins didn't know Detective Goodman...

Japanese: 現場で会った“被害者”は、 ＩＤを提示してくれなかった。
English: And the "victim" whom he met at the scene of the crime didn't show his ID card.

Japanese: つまり‥‥それが“ホンモノ” だったかどうか、わからない‥‥
English: In other words, we have no way of knowing if the victim was really the victim!

Japanese: それに、死体が証拠保管室から “消えた”ってコトは‥‥
English: And if this "body" just "disappeared" from the evidence room...

Japanese: そもそも、ホントに死んだのか どうかもわからないハズです！
English: We don't even know if anyone actually died!

Japanese: そう！　それ、本官。それ！ それを言いたかったであります！
English: That's it, sir! That! That's what I wanted to say!

Japanese: ‥‥っていうか、それに近いコトを 言ってみたであります。
English: That is... I did say something along those lines.

Japanese: え！　‥‥でも。結果的に、 ここにいるワケですよね。
English: Huh? But you still ended up here...?

Japanese: 本官。言われちゃったであります。 なんでも、あの日。あの時間‥‥
English: They told me that it had to be him, sir. "On that day, at that time...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官殿が証拠保管室に 入ったのは、マチガイないとか。
English: Detective Goodman was definitely in the evidence room." That's what they said.

Japanese: でも‥‥あなたのキオクは ハッキリしないんでしょう？
English: But... you don't remember the events clearly?

Japanese: でも‥‥ハッキリ。 映っていたでありますからねェ‥‥
English: No... but the video tape is quite clear.

Japanese: え。“映っていた”‥‥？
English: Huh? Video tape...?

Japanese: 防犯カメラであります。
English: From the security camera.

Japanese: 本官のニクむべき犯行が、すべて！ 録画されていたでありますッ！
English: The crime, my crime, the crime I swore to stamp out! It's there! It's me! It's on tape!
And you wait until now to tell us this!?

Japanese: ハッ！　本官、その。アレ。 メンボクないでありますからして！
English: I'm sorry, really sorry, sir! I'll hand over my badge! I don't deserve it!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥とにかく、 現場を調べないとな）
English: N-no thanks, I have my own. (Well, guess we'd better go check out the crime scene.)

Japanese: ハラバイさん。 これ、なんですけど。
English: Officer Meekins, have a look at this.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それで。本官に。 本官に、勝ったつもりですかッ！
English: Go ahead, sir, laugh! Laugh at me, sir!
Ha ha... huh?

Japanese: ヨコの女の子と“オソロイ”だと。 こう言いたいワケですかッ！
English: I know what you want to say! You're going to tell me how she has one just like it!

Japanese: あ‥‥あたしですか？
English: M-me? Why would I...?

Japanese: どーせ本官。“オソロイ”のお守り を持ってくれる女の子なんて本官。
English: I know, sir, I know. I'm the only one without a girl with matching badges!

Japanese: どこにも、いないであります！ それが、いけないコトですかッ！
English: Nowhere! I'm alone! All alone! Is that so wrong!?

Japanese: アオいハルが青春だなんて、本官。 決して認めないでありますッ！
English: Life isn't all about high school sweethearts and youthful romance, sir!

Japanese: （“オソロイ”って‥‥ バッジ、のコトか？）
English: (Is he talking about those badges on her coat?)

Japanese: ‥‥ふふん。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: （弁護士バッジと いっしょにしないでほしいな）
English: (I'd like to think there's a difference between my badge and a fashion accessory...)

Japanese: あの‥‥これ。 見ていただけますか？
English: Um... do you think you could take a look at this?
Hey!

Japanese: ソレであります！　ソレ、本官。 ソレでありますからしてェェェッ！
English: That's it, sir! That's it! That's it!!!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニ。なんですか！
English: That's what!?

Japanese: 今、このシュンカンまで‥‥本官の アタマ、まっ白でありました！
English: My head was a blank until this very moment!

Japanese: しかし、すべて本官！　ここに今、 本官。思い出したでありますッ！
English: But, sir, now I remember! I remember, sir!

Japanese: “思い出した”‥‥って、 事件のコト、ですか？
English: You mean you remember what happened?

Japanese: そうです！　いわゆる本官、 ソイツのせいで、こんな目にッ！
English: Correct! That card, that card was the cause of it all!

Japanese: この、ＩＤカード‥‥ですか？
English: This... ID card?

Japanese: そうであります！ ジツに、そうなのであります！
English: Exactly, sir! That's exactly it!

Japanese: ある種、そう言わざるを得ない のでありますからしてェェェェッ！
English: Nothing could be more exact, sir! Nothing!

Japanese: （‥‥詳しく 聞いてみたほうがよさそうだな）
English: (I'd better pry into this one a little deeper.)

Japanese: ハラバイさん、コレなんですけ
English: Officer Meekins, this is for you!

Japanese: きゃあああああッ！ ナイフ、コワいであります本官ッ！
English: Eeeek! I-I'm scared of knives, sir!

Japanese: だ、大丈夫ですよ。 見てもらいたいだけですから
English: It's okay, I just wanted you to take a look at it.

Japanese: コレ本官！　ゆうべも本官！ アレでありまして本官ッ！
English: That's it, sir! Last night, sir! That's the one!

Japanese: 本官がリンゴになって、つるつる カワをむかれるユメを‥‥
English: I was an apple, sir, in my dream, sir, and I was... I was being peeled!

Japanese: ‥‥スミマセン。 もう、いいです。
English: On second thought, you don't have to look at the knife.

Japanese: （‥‥ヒドい目にあったんだろう。 しかたないな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He's overreacting to the knife, but I guess he's been through a lot.)

Japanese: アレ。なんでありますか？ ‥‥コレ。
English: Hey! Why do you have that? That's...

Japanese: あの日の、同じ時間。 検事局で撮影された写真です。
English: From the day... It was taken at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: トランクから、多田敷さんの 死体が見つかったんですけど‥‥
English: The day that Detective Goodman's body was found in the trunk...

Japanese: ‥‥と、するとコレ。 コレ、つまりヤッパリ本官‥‥
English: So this means...

Japanese: 本官、ヤッパリ！　ウスウス！ ムジツだったのでありますねッ！
English: This means I'm a free man! Innocent!

Japanese: ああ‥‥コレが、ユメならば‥‥
English: If this is a dream, sir...

Japanese: コレがユメならば、いつまでも さめないでほしいでありますッ！
English: Then I hope I never wake up!

Japanese: （コレがユメなら、 さっさとさめてくれえ‥‥）
English: (If this is a dream you'd better wake up right now!)

Japanese: ハラバイさん。 コレ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Officer Meekins, could you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。もう。ダメ、本官。 ゼンゼンわからないであります！
English: I'm sorry, sir, really sorry, but I have no idea what that is.

Japanese: それはもう。御剣検事殿にでも 聞いてほしいでありますッ！
English: Maybe you should ask Mr. Edgeworth, sir?

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、コシの低い “お役所シゴト”みたいですね。
English: He's passing the buck, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 御剣にシゴトをタライ回すなんて、 なかなか大物だよなあ。
English: It takes a special kind of man to pass the buck to Edgeworth...

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 23 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥今日もシャレてますね。
English: Well, this place is certainly tidy today.

Japanese: きっと、明日も シャレてるんじゃないかな。
English: And it will be tidy tomorrow, too.

Japanese: そんなことより‥‥ いないぞ、アイツ。
English: Incidentally, he's not here.

Japanese: きっと、元気に 捜査してるんですよ！
English: I'm sure he's off doing important investigations!

Japanese: それならいいけど、ね。
English: I hope that's what he's doing.

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ ...? （とにかく‥‥あとで、 また来てみよう）
English: (I guess we'll have to come back.)

Japanese: ひゃあ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: すごいながめですねー。 １２階でしたっけ。ここ。
English: What a view! It must be nice to have an office on the 12th floor.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なんか、 エラくなった気分になるね。
English: I guess you would feel... important.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに。
English: Incidentally...

Japanese: ここから飛び降りたとき、地面に 激突するときのスピードは‥‥
English: Were you to jump out this window, the speed at the time of impact would be...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 出ましたっ！ 時速１１３．８キロメートルです！
English: Got it! Approximately 70.71 miles per hour!

Japanese: （‥‥だから、なんなんだ）
English: (That's handy to know...)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、 キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。 使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーく みがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so high I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: あのネームプレート、おみやげに 持って帰ろうかなあ。
English: Maybe I'll take that name plaque as a souvenir.

Japanese: やめたほうがいいよ。起訴される。
English: Don't. He'll sue you.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ 事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。アイツ、 地震がニガテなはずだから‥‥
English: Odd...

Japanese: こんな危なっかしい本棚を 使うとは思えないんだけど。
English: I thought Edgeworth wasn't good with heights.

Japanese: だいじょうぶです！
English: He must have someone get them for him.

Japanese: ものすごく大きなボルトで、カベに メリこむほど固定されています！ すごいパワーだなあ。 御剣検事さんがやったのかなあ。 （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: でもさ。地震が起こったら、 部屋中にファイルの雨が降るね。 だいじょうぶです！
English: He must study these case reports so closely... He's so cool!

Japanese: ギュウギュウに詰まってますから、 ちょっとやそっとじゃ抜けません！ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: You wouldn't say that if you saw him sweating bullets up on that ladder.

Japanese: やっぱり、なんか おかしくない？　それ。 あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥ 追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって 青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士” “ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!" And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: 赤い“剣”が、青い“歩”を 追いつめている‥‥ 御剣検事さん、チェス好きかあ。 ‥‥どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth must be an avid chess player! What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （気のせいだよな、きっと）
English: (Edges... surrounding a pawn with spiky hair... Nah... It's nothing.)

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ ステキなティーセットですよ！
English: Oooh! Cute! What a pretty tea set!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、ティーバッグ派だから。
English: I go more for the instant tea bags myself.

Japanese: すごい！　下のタナに、茶葉と缶が ギッシリ詰まってます！
English: Amazing! The drawer below is filled with packets of tea leaves!

Japanese: 産地別にキチンと並んでますよ。 フレバリーも、種類ごとに！
English: They're all sorted by place of origin and flavor!

Japanese: お。このロイヤル、ミゴトなまでに 暴力的なブレンディングです！
English: Look at this Royal blend! What an exquisitely splendid concoction!

Japanese: （‥‥シュミって、なんでも 奥が深いものなんだなあ‥‥）
English: (There's such a thing as taking a hobby too far.)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。 御剣検事さんに、ピッタリ！
English: My, my, my! What an amazing bouquet!

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。 カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《起死回生　　　カオル》
English: "Back from the Dead -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥ どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥ 巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And here beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。 御剣検事さん、マニアなのかなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: イヤだな、それ。‥‥あ。 足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《青息吐息　　　カオル》
English: "Between a rock and a hard place. -- Wendy"

Japanese: カオルさんって‥‥御剣検事さんの フィアンセ、ですか？
English: Wendy? Is she Mr. Edgeworth's fiancee?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、ちがうと思うよ。
English: Um... I don't think so.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。 ふかふかですよ、ソファ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth has such a comfy sofa!

Japanese: こんなリッパなソファ、 座った瞬間に眠くなるよ。
English: Sofas like this make me want to curl up and take a nap.

Japanese: きっと、夜おそくまで 資料を調べて、つかれきって‥‥
English: I bet he pours over his case files here until the wee hours of the morning...

Japanese: 上着をぬいで、シャツのソデなんか オシャレにまくっちゃって。
English: Then he takes off his jacket, rolls up his sleeves...

Japanese: ウデまくらで、ついウトウト 寝ちゃうんですよねー。
English: And goes to sleep using his arms as a pillow!

Japanese: （彼女の目には、見えている らしいな‥‥オソロシイことに）
English: (I don't believe it. She's actually daydreaming about Edgeworth working...)

Japanese: 寝グセや、ホッペにソファの カドのアトがついちゃって。
English: I bet in the morning he has sofa hair, and little creases in his cheek from the seams!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいなあ。
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいかあ？
English: Sofa-hair is cool!?

Japanese: うわ！　なんか、いちだんと ヒラヒラした服ですよ！
English: Wow! This jacket is even lacier than his usual ones!

Japanese: いわゆる“勝負服”かな。 御剣検事さんの！
English: This must be his lucky trial jacket!

Japanese: 勝負服、ねえ‥‥。 ぼくは見たことないけどな。
English: Lucky jacket, right... I've never seen him wear it.

Japanese: 額に入れてかざっちゃって。 ナニか“いわく”がありそう！
English: I'm sure there's a story behind why it's in a frame!

Japanese: ちょっとシツレイして。 写真、撮っちゃお！
English: Maybe I'll be naughty... and take a picture!

Japanese: （やれやれ。 コーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (She's getting way too excited about this.)

Japanese: せっかくもらったのに、 ほったらかしですね。
English: He won such a prestigious award. Why would he just leave it on the couch?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥トノサマンを 置いておくよりはいいと思うけど。
English: Better this than that giant Steel Samurai.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥アイツ、 ムカシからそうだったな）
English: (Edgeworth has always been like that.)

Japanese: え！　なんですか？ メモしますから、教えてください！
English: Really? Hey, I should write this down. Tell me what he was like as a kid!

Japanese: アイツ、小学生のころ、やたら いろんな賞をもらってたんだよ。
English: Back in elementary school, he won awards for all kinds of things.

Japanese: 弁論大会とか、論文とか、 フルートとか、ゴルフとか。
English: Speech contests, essays, playing the flute, golf tournaments...

Japanese: ‥‥どことなく、小学生っぽくない ラインナップですね。
English: That doesn't exactly sound like typical elementary school stuff.

Japanese: でも‥‥アイツ自身は、 賞をもらうのがキライだったんだ。
English: But he always hated receiving awards. He'd make some smart remark like,

Japanese: 「自分が歩いてきた道ならば、 自分自身が知っているのだよ」
English: "I know the path I've walked. No one else needs to tell me."

Japanese: ‥‥とか、なんとか。
English: That's how he's always been.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ハラ立ちますね。
English: Kind of gets under your skin, doesn't it?

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 23 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥おや。 これはシツレイいたしました。
English: Ah, guests! My apologies.

Japanese: あ！　あなたは‥‥！
English: Oh! It's you!

Japanese: どこかでお会いしましたか？
English: Have we met somewhere...?
Huh!?

Japanese: それでは、シツレイいたします。 ‥‥御剣さま。
English: Mr. Edgeworth! I beg your leave. So long!

Japanese: （御剣‥‥いるのか？）
English: (Is Edgeworth here...?)

Japanese: あ。窓辺でたたずんでますよ！ ティーカップを片手に。
English: There, standing by the window, a teacup in his hand!

Japanese: キミか‥‥
English: Ah, it's you.

Japanese: （コイツ‥‥ホテルから紅茶を 持ってこさせているのか‥‥）
English: (He has the hotel bring him tea service!?)

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥ 地方検事局の、査問会から？
English: Mr. Edgeworth... you're back from the District Prosecutor's Office inquiry?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Precisely.

Japanese: そういえば‥‥ イトノコ刑事が探してたぞ。
English: By the way, Detective Gumshoe was looking for you.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そういえば。 情報を持ってきてくれたようだ。
English: Oh yes... He brought me some information, it seems.

Japanese: え！　役に立つんですか？　それ。
English: Really? Was it helpful?

Japanese: この近くに、うまいフランス料理の レストランがオープンするようだ。
English: Apparently, a new French restaurant is opening near here.

Japanese: ‥‥刑事の、せいいっぱいの なぐさめ、なのだろう。
English: I think he was trying to console me, somehow.

Japanese: （御剣‥‥いちおう ウラだぞ、そっち側は‥‥）
English: (Er, I think the report is on the other side, Edgeworth...)

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥タマシイが 抜けちゃったみたいですね‥‥
English: Poor Mr. Edgeworth... I think this whole thing is really taking a toll on him.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、あかねさん。 主席検事から、うかがったのだが。
English: By the way, Ema. The Chief Prosecutor wanted to know something...

Japanese: は、はいッ！ お。お姉ちゃんですか？
English: M-my sister? What?

Japanese: 今も、目指しているのだろうか。 その‥‥科学捜査官を。
English: You still working on that scientific investigation?

Japanese: はいっ！　もちろんです！
English: Huh? Y-yes! Of course!

Japanese: 今日だって、成歩堂さんと コレを使って‥‥
English: Why, just today Mr. Wright and I were using this...

Japanese: ルミノール試薬‥‥か。
English: Luminol testing fluid... Hm?

Japanese: それならば‥‥ これも使ってみるといい。
English: Well then... You might have use for this.

Japanese: 指紋をとるための “アルミ粉”‥‥ですか！
English: Aluminum powder for taking fingerprints!

Japanese: 吸着をよくするために、 化学処理がほどこされている。
English: It's been chemically treated for better adhesion.

Japanese: い。いいんですか？ あたしたち‥‥“テキ”ですけど。
English: F-for me? Are you sure? We are the "enemy" you know.

Japanese: 今日の私に、捜査の指揮権はない。 ‥‥好きにさせてもらうさ。
English: I've no say in today's investigation... do as you will.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥
English: Edgeworth... I'm really--

Japanese: 礼は無用だ。
English: No need to thank me.

Japanese: 関係者の指紋データといっしょに 持っていきたまえ。
English: Here, take your powder and these fingerprint files for everyone involved.

Japanese: い、いや。なんていうか‥‥ （イトノコ刑事にも貸してやれよ）
English: I, uh, th-thanks! (How about giving these to Detective Gumshoe as well!?)

Japanese: 御剣から《指紋検出用具》と 関係者の指紋データを受け取った。
English: Fingerprinting Set and fingerprint file received.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥最後に、もう一度！ 調べてみましょうよ！
English: Well, let's get going! One last investigation!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Right!

Japanese: （たしか‥‥どこかで、気になる “手のアト”を見たよな‥‥）
English: (I do seem to remember seeing a suspicious handprint somewhere...)

Japanese: それで‥‥どうだったんだ？ 査問会ってヤツは‥‥
English: So, how did the inquiry committee go?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、彼らは 認めることにしたようだ。
English: Actually, they decided to treat this not as a case of concealing evidence...

Japanese: 今回の一件は“証拠品の隠匿”では ない。“捜査上の連絡ミス”だと。
English: But as a communications error during the investigation.

Japanese: “いんとく”‥‥
English: "Concealing evidence"...?

Japanese: この私が、証拠品をかくした‥‥ 彼らは、そう信じていたようだ。
English: Yes. Apparently, there are some who believe... that I concealed evidence.

Japanese: 最後に警告されたよ。 『今回もイノチ拾いしたようだな』
English: They gave me a warning. "You were lucky this time... again."
"Again"...?

Japanese: そのセリフを聞くのは、 もう何度目になるだろうか。
English: I've heard them say that so many times.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件から‥‥な。
English: Ever since that case two years ago...

Japanese: 明日の法廷は大丈夫なのか？
English: Are you okay for the trial tomorrow?

Japanese: とりあえず、担当検事を つづけることになった。しかし‥‥
English: Well, I'm still the presiding prosecuting attorney. However...

Japanese: 何か‥‥あったみたいだな。
English: Something happened?

Japanese: 捜査の指揮権を、 警察局側に移された。
English: They gave control of the investigation over to the Police Department.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: The Police Department!

Japanese: この事件は今後、巌徒局長の 指揮下で捜査が進められる。
English: Yes. Any further investigation for this case will be directed by the Chief of Police Gant.

Japanese: 私は、その結果を待つこと以外 ‥‥何もできないのだ。
English: I can do nothing but wait for his results...

Japanese: そう‥‥なのか。
English: I... see.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥だ！
English: Why, I ask you? Why!?

Japanese: 私は、信じる道を歩んできた。 恥じるコトなどない！
English: All along, I've done only what I believe is right. I have nothing to be ashamed of!

Japanese: それなのに‥‥
English: But still...

Japanese: （かなりマイってる みたいだな‥‥）
English: (Wow, I've never seen him this out of sorts...)

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ 凶悪な連続殺人事件だった。
English: The SL-9 Incident was a heinous serial killing case.

Japanese: 捜査責任者は、当時、警察局副長 だったオトコ。‥‥巌徒　海慈。
English: The head of investigation was the Deputy Chief of Police at the time... Damon Gant.

Japanese: （あのオジサン‥‥２年前は、 捜査に関わっていたのか‥‥）
English: (That wacky old coot was involved in the case two years ago too then...)

Japanese: 最も優秀な捜査官との、初シゴト ‥‥私も多少、舞い上がっていた。
English: He was the best we had, and it was my first time working with him... I was nervous.

Japanese: 御剣検事さんでも、 そんなコトがあるんですね。
English: Wow, you get nervous, too, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: （それより、局長が“捜査官” のほうが意外だな‥‥）
English: (What I want to know is why was a Deputy Chief of Police on the investigation?)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥いつもより、 多少は強引な手を使った。
English: In truth, I used slightly more... extreme methods than normal.

Japanese: 相手は危険なサツジン犯だ。 野放しにするなど、私がユルさぬ。
English: We were dealing with a vicious murderer. If I let him go, the blood would be on my hands.

Japanese: その結果、有罪判決が下って‥‥ ハンニンは処刑された。
English: We won our guilty verdict, and the killer was executed.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、おまえ‥‥
English: Wait, you didn't...

Japanese: ‥‥バカな！ 証拠に手など加えていない。
English: Of course not! I didn't touch the evidence.

Japanese: たしかに私は、判決のためならば あらゆる手段を使う。
English: Yes, I will do anything in my power to win a trial.

Japanese: しかし‥‥ 私自身のルールは存在するのだ。
English: However... I do have a code, and I follow it faithfully.

Japanese: 御剣。これ、なんだけど‥‥ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... キミのことだ。すでに 情報はつかんでいるのだろう。
English: I know you. You've probably got a hold of some information already, right?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥おまえが 関わった事件、なんだよな。
English: It all has to do with that case you were on... the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: この事件のせいで‥‥ “黒いギワク”に包まれた、とか。
English: And some "dark suspicion" you were wrapped up in.

Japanese: ‥‥キミは、私の最悪の記憶を 蘇らせたオトコだ。
English: You are the man who revived the worst memory of my life, as I recall.

Japanese: いつか、話すことに なるだろうとは思っていた。
English: I figured I'd be telling you about this sooner or later.

Japanese: （あの‥‥エレベータの事件、か）
English: (A bad memory... he's talking about the murder in the elevator...)

Japanese: じゃあ、御剣。聞かせてくれ。 ホントのところを。
English: Okay, Edgeworth. Why don't you tell me about it. Tell me the truth.

Japanese: あ‥‥そうだ！ これだけは、たしかめないと。
English: Oh, right! I'd better check this now...

Japanese: だから、私は‥‥
English: As I was saying, I...

Japanese: ！　それは‥‥ ＩＤカードの使用記録、か。
English: ! What's this? A record of ID card usage?

Japanese: おまえ‥‥あの日、 証拠保管室に入ってるみたいだな。
English: Edgeworth, you went into the evidence room that day, didn't you?

Japanese: しかも。あの事件が起きる 少し前に、だ。
English: Just before the incident occurred, no less.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。たしかにな。
English: Yes, that's true.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか‥‥ 御剣検事さん？
English: W-why, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: そ、そんな目で 見つめないでいただきたい！
English: Please don't look at me like that.

Japanese: ただ‥‥たのまれただけだ。 巌徒局長に。
English: I... was asked to go. By Chief Gant no less.

Japanese: 局長さん‥‥？
English: The Chief of Police...?

Japanese: 半年前に解決した事件の 証拠品だ。
English: He wanted evidence for a case that wrapped up half a year ago.

Japanese: 検事局のオフィスで保管して おいてほしい、と言われた。
English: He told me he wanted me to keep it here in the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: でも‥‥もう、解決したんだろ？ 保管庫にあった、ってコトは。
English: But... it was solved, right? It would have to be if the evidence was already filed...

Japanese: 巌徒局長は、自分の考えを セツメイしたがらないからな。
English: The Chief is never one to explain himself.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥事件の日、私は これを持って、ここへ戻ってきた。
English: In any case, on the day of the stabbings, I brought this back here.

Japanese: あの！　ちなみに、 どんな事件なんですか？　それ。
English: Can I ask what kind of case it was?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、言えない。
English: I... can't say.

Japanese: さすがに、今回の事件とは カンケイないからね。
English: It really has nothing to do with the current case.

Japanese: （そう言われると、気になる。 いちおう、記録しておこう）
English: (Now I'm curious about this other case. I'd better make a note of it.)

Japanese: 無関係の証拠品《ドライバー》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Unrelated evidence: Screwdriver added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あいかわらず、ガンコなオトコだ。 カンケイないと言ってるのに‥‥
English: Stubborn as always. I told you this has nothing to do with the current case.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まさか、成歩堂。 担当検事である、この私が‥‥
English: Wright, please. I'm the prosecutor on this case!

Japanese: キミと事件の話をしながら お茶を飲むとでも‥‥？
English: You don't expect me to sit here and discuss the case with you over a cup of tea!?

Japanese: いや。お茶はいいんだ。 事件のハナシだけを‥‥
English: I'll pass on the tea. Just tell me about the case...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　御剣検事さんは、 イキに断ってるんです！
English: Mr. Wright! Mr. Edgeworth just told you "no" in a very polite manner!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Whose side are you on, anyway?)

Japanese: （本当に必要なモノだけ、 鼻先につきつけてやろう）
English: (Maybe if I just show him my best evidence I can get some reaction out of him.)

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 23 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: なんか‥‥やっぱり、 殺気立っているなあ。
English: Wow, everyone looks deadly serious here...

Japanese: 警察局内で、捜査官が 殺害されちゃったワケですからね。
English: Well, there was a vicious murder of a detective in this department, after all.

Japanese: でも。その多田敷捜査官なら 同時に、検事局の駐車場でも‥‥
English: Yes, but the same detective was also killed at the same time in the Prosecutor's lot.

Japanese: ううう‥‥この話題、 ヤヤコシイから、キライです。
English: Ugh... It makes my head hurt.

Japanese: とりあえず、こっちの現場を 調べたいんだけどなあ。
English: Well, first things first. I want to check out the crime scene here.

Japanese: なにぶん、殺気立ってますからね。 ヘタしたら、コロされます！
English: Yes, you sound "dead"-set on investigating! But don't mess up, or we could wind up... dead!

Japanese: ‥‥彼らも、これ以上、死体は 増やしたくないんじゃないかな。
English: I doubt anyone wants more mysteries or dead bodies around here right now.

Japanese: （なんにせよ‥‥ハナシを聞いて くれるフンイキじゃないな）
English: (But... it doesn't look like anyone's going to help us much, either.)

Japanese: 警察のマスコットキャラクター ‥‥って、こんなんだったっけ？
English: So this is the police mascot, is it?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。 未来の警察のスターさ。
English: The Blue Badger! The future star of the police force!

Japanese: ちょっとデザインがちがいますね。 ‥‥ソトでおどっている子と。
English: The design's a little changed from the one outside...

Japanese: ああ。まだ、試作段階だがね。 《ミスター・ダンシングタイホ》。
English: Ah, well, the Dancing Blue Badger(tm) is still under development, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥なんで、英語なんだろ。
English: You have it trademarked?

Japanese: アレはね。少し“今”ふうの センスを取り入れてみたんだよ。
English: Absolutely! It's cutting edge stuff. Very "now."

Japanese: このぬいぐるみ、ムスメに見せたら “コワい”と泣かれてしまってね。
English: I showed this doll here to my daughter and she burst into tears!

Japanese: （‥‥あの動くベニヤ板を見たら、 走って逃げ出すぞ、きっと）
English: (Don't show her the moving mock-up outside then, you'll give her nightmares.)

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “わかった！　ハンニンは ドライアイスを使ったのだ！”
English: "I know! The killer used dry ice!"

Japanese: “カンヌキとドアの間に挟めば、 自動的に密室が完成するのだ！”
English: "If you put it between the latch and the door, the room could be sealed shut!"

Japanese: ‥‥コイツは新しいな。 新人賞はイタダキだろう。
English: This is good! I'll win a writer's award for sure.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 報告書‥‥じゃなくて、 小説を書いているようだ。
English: He's not writing a report... he's writing a novel.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: That must be the Head Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ “証拠保管室で、捜査官が殺害”
English: Wha...!? "Detective killed in the evidence room"!

Japanese: “警察局外には、ゼッタイ 情報をもらさないこと”だと？
English: "Tell no one outside the Police Department"!?

Japanese: しまった！　定食屋のオバチャンに ゼンブしゃべっちまったぞ！
English: Nooo! I told that old lady at the restaurant everything!

Japanese: （ナニやってんだよ‥‥）
English: (Someone's getting a demotion...)

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 23 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: あ‥‥あれ。成歩堂さん！ カチョーさんの席‥‥
English: H-hey, Mr. Wright! Look who's standing at the Head Detective's desk!

Japanese: （あれは‥‥ガント局長？）
English: (It's Chief Gant!)

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、コレだけよね？ カチョーちゃんさ。
English: Are you sure this is all, hmm?

Japanese: 見落としがあるとね。 トンでもないコトになるから。
English: You know what it means if there is anything missing!

Japanese: はっ！　たぶんおそらく、 もーカンペキでありますッ！
English: Sir! I'm sure it's most likely totally perfect!

Japanese: 机の引き出しもロッカーもゴミ箱も カバンもポケットもボウシのウラも
English: We checked the drawers, the lockers, the garbage cans, the coat pockets...

Japanese: ザブトンのウラもパソコンのウラも 湯飲みの中も調べたでありますッ！
English: the pillowcases, behind the computer monitors, the coffee machine...

Japanese: ‥‥そ。じゃ、もし何か出たらさ。 すぐにね。連絡くれるかな。
English: I see. Well, if anything does turn up, you call me right away, deal?

Japanese: は、は‥‥ははははあッ！ も一度！　調べてみるですッ！
English: Y-y-y-yessir!!! We'll scour the place again, sir!

Japanese: （‥‥課長さん、 みっともなく舞い上がってるな）
English: (The Head Detective looks a little flustered...)

Japanese: ‥‥あれェ、ナルホドちゃん！ どうよ、最近。泳いでる？
English: Ah hah! Wrighto, my boy! How ya been? Swim much?

Japanese: はははあッ！　ガント局長殿ッ！ ごくろーさまですッ！
English: Oh ho ho, Chief Gant! Reporting for duty, sir!

Japanese: なんで成歩堂さんが ケーレイしてるんですかっ！
English: Why are you saluting him, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “わかった！　ハンニンは カセットテープを使ったのだ！”
English: "I know! The killer used a cassette tape!"

Japanese: “なんて巧妙なトリックだろう‥‥ あの銃声は、ニセモノなのだ！”
English: "What a crafty trick! That gunshot was a fake!"

Japanese: ‥‥今の時代に、あえて カセットテープってのがイイな。
English: This is good! No one will expect a cassette tape in this day and age!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 報告書‥‥じゃなくて、 小説を書いているようだ。
English: He's not writing a report... he's writing a novel.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥みっともないトコ、 見られちゃった。
English: Ooh, sorry you had to see that.

Japanese: あの。何をたのまれたんですか？ 局長さんに。
English: Uh... what exactly did the Chief of Police want you to do?

Japanese: いやね。そこにホラ。被害者の 多田敷クンの席があるんだけど。
English: Well, see over there? That's Goodman's desk.

Japanese: 手がかりになりそうな遺留品を、 ゼンブ提出しろ、って。
English: He wanted me to check it for anything that might be a clue...

Japanese: ゴミ箱のゴミまで、ゼーンブ 持っていっちゃったよ。
English: They took away every last piece of garbage in the trash can.

Japanese: じゃあ、もう多田敷さんの遺品、 ここにはないんですね。
English: So nothing belonging to Detective Goodman is still here?

Japanese: モチロン。 残ったのは、コイツだけだよ。
English: Of course not! Well, except for this.

Japanese: 残しちゃダメじゃないですか！
English: What!? You kept something!?

Japanese: だってこれ、 書類の書きそこないだから。
English: Sure, why not? It's not important. He didn't even finish writing it!

Japanese: 《遺失物届》‥‥途中までしか 記入されてないしね。
English: It's a lost item report but it's only half complete.

Japanese: “いしつぶつ”‥‥ 何か、なくしたんだ。多田敷さん。
English: A lost item? Did Detective Goodman lose something?

Japanese: 書類の日付は‥‥２月２１日、か。 （いちおう、記録しておこう）
English: The date on it is February 21. (I'll make a note of that just in case.)

Japanese: 証拠品《多田敷の遺失物届》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Goodman's Lost Item Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく）
English: (I should probably get a quick look around the crime scene...)

Japanese: （なんとかして、 現場を調べないとな‥‥） あの‥‥大丈夫ですか？ 御剣のヤツ‥‥
English: Um... is Edgeworth going to be okay?

Japanese: あー。御剣ちゃん。 今ね、アレ。検事局の査問会。
English: Oh, Worthy? Oh, you know, they're doing a little inquiry committee with him.

Japanese: “さもんかい”って‥‥ 不吉なヒビキですね。
English: Sounds like an inquisition...!

Japanese: カレもホラ。イロイロあったから。 年末からこっち。イロイロ、ね。
English: Yep, well, we've had no end of trouble with the boy since last year...

Japanese: もしかして‥‥あの。 ひょうたん湖の事件ですか？
English: You mean... the incident on Gourd Lake?

Japanese: ま。御剣ちゃん自身が被告人って いうのもシャレにならないケド。
English: It doesn't look good having one of our top people sitting in the defendant's seat.

Japanese: ナルホドちゃんが有罪にした ヒト、いたでしょ？
English: Now, you got someone else found guilty in that case, right, Wrighto?

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔検事‥‥）
English: (von Karma...)

Japanese: ４０年間無敗をホコった、 検事局の“生きる伝説”‥‥
English: A legend he was, undefeated in his forty year career!

Japanese: アレが“ねつ造”ってコトに されちゃったでしょ？　キミにさ。
English: But in court you fixed it so he was caught for forging evidence...

Japanese: い、いやいや！　ベツに、 ぼくがワルいわけでは‥‥
English: W-wait! I didn't do anything wrong! He did forge evidence.

Japanese: とにかく、今。アチラさん タイヘンなのよ。検事局ね。
English: In any case, the Prosecutor's Office is in a bit of turmoil, you might say.

Japanese: 信頼を回復するためなら、 なんだってやっちゃうよ。
English: Why, they'd do just about anything to restore their reputation.

Japanese: 査問会しだいでは‥‥ アブナイね。御剣ちゃん。
English: Now, depending on what that inquiry committee decides... It could be bad for Worthy.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: いやー。さすがにコマっちゃうね。 同時でしょ？　向こうさんと。
English: It's downright odd, I tell you. I mean, it happened at exactly the same time!

Japanese: （検事局、か‥‥）
English: (The murder at the Prosecutor's Office...)

Japanese: でも！　そんなの、カガク的に あり得ないじゃないですか。
English: Scientifically speaking, it's impossible!

Japanese: でも！　‥‥今のところさ。 そう言ってるワケ。証拠がね。
English: Yes, but that's what the evidence is saying.

Japanese: “多田敷ちゃんは、２つの場所で 同時に刺された”‥‥って。
English: "Goodman was stabbed in two locations at the same time!" That's what it says.

Japanese: その“証拠”というのは‥‥？
English: What evidence is this...?

Japanese: お。ちょっとそれは。 カンベンしてよ、ナルホドちゃん！
English: Now, now, Wrighto, I can't give away all our secrets just like that!

Japanese: そのヘン、ビミョーでさあ。 ワルいけど、言えないの！
English: And this in particular, well it's a little sensitive... and I can't talk about it.

Japanese: （まあ‥‥ そうかもしれないな）
English: (I wasn't expecting much anyway.)

Japanese: ボクね。隠しゴトみたいなの、 キライなワケ。コレ、ホントに。
English: You know, one thing I hate most of all is hiding stuff. Secrets. Can't stand 'em! But you know...

Japanese: ‥‥だから。カチョーちゃんにも キビしく言ってあるんだけどさ。
English: It's a full-time job just keeping the Head Detective's trap shut!

Japanese: あ。そういえば、いじめてましたね ‥‥カチョーさんのコト。
English: Ah, he was the one you were picking on earlier?

Japanese: あ。やだなー。 アレ、見てたの？　あはは。
English: Huh? You saw that? Whoops!

Japanese: （カチョーさん‥‥ 何をたのまれたんだろう？）
English: (I wonder what it was that he wanted the Head Detective to do?)

Japanese: ちょっと、コッソリ 聞いてみましょうよ！
English: Let's see if we can kind of discreetly ask him.

Japanese: あの‥‥ところで。 おねがいがあるんですけど。
English: Actually, I was wondering if I could ask you a favor?

Japanese: あ。ナニ？　めずらしいね、 ナルホドちゃん。おねがいなんて。
English: Hmm? Well, I never thought the day would come when Wrighto asked me for help!

Japanese: 《証拠保管室》の調査を させてもらえませんか？
English: I was wondering if we could investigate the evidence room?

Japanese: あのさ、成歩堂ちゃん。
English: Now, Wrighto...

Japanese: あ。すみませんでしたッ！ ケッコウでありますッ！
English: A-actually, I'm sorry, I d-don't need to investigate after all!

Japanese: ‥‥ボクさ。そんなね。 ケチなオトコじゃないワケ。
English: Wrighto, please, do I look like a selfish man?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ダレにたのまれても、 ５０００円までなら貸すし。
English: Heck, if anyone asked me "sir, can I borrow $50?" I'd give them $50, no problem.

Japanese: ‥‥ぜんぜんモンダイないよ。 好きに調べてくれていいから。
English: So, go ahead! Investigate that room to your heart's content! Knock yourself out!

Japanese: 言ってみるモンですねー。
English: It just goes to show, you never know until you ask!

Japanese: じゃ、そこのおじょうちゃん。 コレ。貸してあげるから。
English: And for you, here, you can borrow this.

Japanese: あ‥‥それ！　刑事さんの ＩＤカード‥‥ですか？
English: Huh... hey! This is a detective's ID card, isn't it?

Japanese: お客さん用のトクベツだから。 なくさないでね。
English: That's a special card for guests, so don't lose it.

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！ 光栄でありますッ！
English: Y-yessir! It's an honor!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。とにかく、がんばってよ。 じゃ。またね。
English: You just run along and do your best, now. Later, folks!

Japanese: へへ。ムネにつけちゃった。 ホンモノですよ！
English: Heh heh! It looks pretty cool on my lapel, doesn't it? Just think, a real ID!

Japanese: うれしそうだね。ものすごく。
English: You seem... happy.

Japanese: 本官が入れてあげますからしてッ！ ‥‥証拠保管室。
English: Yes, sir! Because, sir, we get to go into the evidence room now, sir!

Japanese: （‥‥ヘンな影響を 受けているみたいだな）
English: (I think this place is a bad influence on the girl.)

Japanese: あの。ちょっと 見ていただきたいんですけど。
English: I was wondering, could you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。ワルいけど。
English: Ah, sorry, Wrighto.

Japanese: そういうのボク、もう 卒業しちゃったから。
English: I'm through with that stuff. Through, I say.

Japanese: そのへんをヒラヒラしてる コートのヒトにでも見せてあげて。
English: Go find that guy who can't seem to sit still out there. The busy one...

Japanese: ヒラヒラ‥‥ あの刑事さんのコトかな。
English: The guy who can't sit still... does he mean Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: せめて“フラフラ”と 言ってあげてほしいね。
English: Either him or the Dancing Blue Badger.

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 証拠保管室前／警備室
English: February 23 Evidence Room Entrance Guard Station

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。この、きわめて シュミ性の強い空間は‥‥
English: What's with the decor in this place? It's very... eccentric.

Japanese: パンフレットによると、 《保管室前・警備室》ですけど。
English: According to the pamphlet, this is the guard station for the evidence room.

Japanese: じゃあ、あのドアの向こうが 証拠保管室‥‥“殺人現場”か。
English: So, beyond that door is the evidence room... the scene of the crime?

Japanese: そういうことになりますね。 ‥‥‥‥‥うううう‥‥‥‥‥
English: It sure seems that way... Oh. Ohhh...

Japanese: ど。どうしたの？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: ヨコのサボテンくんの存在感に圧倒 されて、考えがまとまりません！
English: It's those cacti! They're so prickly... so imposing! It's hard to think straight.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (If you can't handle the cacti, stay out of the desert...)

Japanese: そもそも、警備室なのに、 どうして警備のヒトがいないんだ！
English: What I want to know is, if this is a guard station, where is the guard?

Japanese: “警備のヒト”‥‥ だいたい、想像はつきますね。
English: I have a feeling I know who this guard is already...

Japanese: かなり、チクチクしますね。 ‥‥ホンモノかな、コレ。
English: Yipes, that sure is prickly. It must be the real deal.

Japanese: このデカいのが１コあれば、 じゅうぶんだと思うけど。
English: I would think just one big one would be sufficient.

Japanese: この、サボテン。 ‥‥お姉ちゃん、みたいですね。
English: This cactus... is a lot like my sister, actually.

Japanese: と、ともえさん‥‥？
English: M-Ms. Skye?

Japanese: 冷たくてカタいカラをかぶって、 さらにトゲで身を守って‥‥
English: Encased in a cold, rigid shell, with thorns pointing in every direction...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、そっくり。
English: Just like her.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 悪いけど、どう見ても ともえさんには似てないよ。
English: You know, I've been looking at this cactus a while now, and I don't see the resemblance.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あたしも、フンイキで 言っちゃいました。
English: It's more an attitude thing than a physical similarity.

Japanese: 証拠保管室の映像が 映し出されているみたいだ。
English: It looks like there's a video feed from the evidence room here.

Japanese: モニターの下に、 ランプが光ってますよ。
English: There's a light blinking below the monitor.

Japanese: 《録画中》‥‥って 書いてあります！
English: It says "Recording"!

Japanese: このパソコンで、出入りするヒトを チェックしてたみたいですね！
English: I bet we could use this computer to check on who went in and out of here!

Japanese: 警備員さんの服が つるしてあります！
English: There's a security guard uniform hanging here.

Japanese: “制服”じゃないコトは たしかだね。
English: It looks more like a costume than a uniform, honestly.

Japanese: カワのジャンパーに、 カワのズボンに、カワの‥‥
English: A leather jacket, leather pants, a leather...

Japanese: あれ。これ、なんでしたっけ！
English: What was that called again?

Japanese: パンチ‥‥ピンチ‥‥ペンチ‥‥ あ、ポンチ！
English: A punchy? A paunchy? A pinchy? I know! A poochy!

Japanese: ううん‥‥今ひとつ ピンと来ないなあ‥‥
English: Hmm... Wait, maybe that wasn't it.

Japanese: （“ポンチョ”だけど、もう少し ダマっておこう‥‥）
English: (It's a "poncho," but I think I'll keep that information to myself for the time being.)

Japanese: こんなところに、 投げナワがありますよ。
English: Look, on the floor, a lasso!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、“ワナ”っぽい ムードがただよってるなあ。
English: Hmm... looks like it's set up to trap something.

Japanese: ワナっぽい‥‥ねえ‥‥ あ！　できました！
English: A trap, here? Wait, I know!

Japanese: 《“ワナげ”な投げナワ》 ‥‥逆から読んでも同じです！
English: Maybe someone was trying to catch a wild bull in here... But the lasso missed!

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、スゴイね。
English: You sure have an active imagination.

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: さあ、ハリきって行きましょう！ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: Let's just walk in!

Japanese: ... 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: そりゃ、そうだろうね。
English: You thought it'd be open?

Japanese: （現場を調べるには、 だれかの協力が必要だな‥‥）
English: (I think we'd need someone's permission to go in there first...)

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: ガント局長さんに ＩＤカードも借りてきたし。
English: And we have the ID card from Chief Gant!

Japanese: さあ、ハリきって行きましょう！ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: Let's just walk in!

Japanese: ... 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: あ！　カード端末の 電源がオフになってるぞ。
English: Ah hah! The card reader is turned off, see?

Japanese: もう！　警備のヒト、 ちゃんとシゴトしましょうよ！
English: What is that security guard thinking?

Japanese: わあ。西部のサルーンみたい！ この、スイングドア。
English: This swinging door makes the place look like some kind of saloon!

Japanese: でも‥‥クギで打ちつけてあるぞ。 入れないよ、これじゃ。
English: But look, it's nailed shut. You can't get in that way.

Japanese: だってホラ。ここから入ったら、 サボテンが倒れてきて‥‥
English: Of course not! If you went in through here...

Japanese: チクッ、てなりますから。
English: The cactus would fall over. Ouch!

Japanese: ‥‥“チクッ”じゃ すまないぞ、きっと。
English: I'd say it'd be more of an "Yeeeeaargh," myself.

Japanese: おやおや‥‥バンビーナが ごきげんナナメのようだよ。
English: Howdy, pardners. Well, well, what's made my bambina's skies so gray?

Japanese: ざ、罪門さん！ （やっぱり、このヒトか‥‥）
English: O-Officer Marshall! (Somehow, I knew...)

Japanese: なんだい？　その“やっぱり、 このヒトか‥‥”みたいな目は。
English: What's that "somehow I knew" look for?

Japanese: ‥‥お察しのとおり。 ここはオレのサルーンさ。
English: As you may have surmised, this here's my saloon.

Japanese: あの‥‥あたしたち、 現場を調査したいんですけど。
English: Um... we're here to investigate the crime scene.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ほお‥‥そのムネのカード。 新世界へのティケットかい。
English: Yeehaw! That card you got there on your chest. That's better than a sheriff's badge in these parts.

Japanese: てぃけっと‥‥
English: Y-yeehaw?

Japanese: いいさ。サツジン現場で 開拓者気分を味わってくることさ。
English: Well, what ya standin' there for? Get along, little dogies. The crime scene's a waiting!

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: （カードリーダーの 電源がもどったみたいだな）
English: (Looks like the card reader's on again.)

Japanese: ‥‥ついでに、お話を 聞かせていただけますか？
English: While we're here, I was wondering if we could ask you some questions?

Japanese: 悪いがカウボーイ。 キミたちの相手をする気はないさ。
English: Sorry, cowboy, but I got no mind to tangle with you hombres.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥忙しいんですか？
English: You're... busy, then?

Japanese: そんなコトは言ってないだろう？
English: Did I say that?

Japanese: “相手をする気はない”‥‥ それだけのことさ。
English: I only said I didn't wish to speak with you.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Actually, you said you had "no mind to tangle with us hombres.")

Japanese: かなり、チクチクしますね。 ‥‥ホンモノかな、コレ。
English: This cactus sure is prickly. It must be real.

Japanese: ビリーに近づかないほうがいい。 ‥‥ケガ、するぜ。
English: Best keep your distance from Billy there. You're liable to get hurt.

Japanese: びりー‥‥ このサボテンのコトですか？
English: Billy... The cactus has a name?

Japanese: 左ウデに、カウボーイハットを かぶってますね。ビリー。
English: Billy, you're wearing your cowboy hat on your hand.

Japanese: ああ‥‥、そのカウスキンの ローハイドかい。
English: Ah, my rawhide cow skin 10-gallon hat!

Japanese: 買ったばかりだったんだけど。 ビリーが気に入ったみたいでね。
English: I just picked that up, but Billy took a fancy to it.

Japanese: （‥‥トゲに引っかかって とれなくなったみたいだな）
English: (Looks like it got stuck on the thorns and he couldn't get it off.)

Japanese: ずいぶんいっぱい モニターがありますね！
English: You sure have a lot of monitors in here!

Japanese: 証拠保管室は、ゼンブで ８つ、あるからね。
English: There's eight cameras in the evidence room in all.

Japanese: １部屋に１つ、防犯カメラが 設置されているのさ。
English: One for each section.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。事件があった保管室の モニターは、どれですか？
English: Which monitor shows the section where the crime took place?

Japanese: そいつは‥‥“神のみぞ知る” ってヤツだな。
English: Why do cattle sit before the rains come? It's not for man to know.

Japanese: ‥‥警備員シッカクですね、 罪門さん。
English: Unless you're the security guard responsible!

Japanese: カワのジャンパーに、 カワのズボンに、カワの‥‥
English: A leather jacket, leather pants, and a leather...

Japanese: ズボンじゃないさ。チャップスに ‥‥そっちは、ポンチョだね。
English: Those aren't pants, those are chaps. And that's a poncho up there.

Japanese: あ、それ！　ポンチョ！
English: Ah hah! A poncho!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいけど、 名前はかわいらしいですね。
English: It sure looks cool, but the name is a little silly.

Japanese: こんなところに、 投げナワがありますよ。
English: Look, on the floor, it's a lasso.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、“ワナ”っぽい ムードがただよってるなあ。
English: Hmm... looks like it's set up to trap something.

Japanese: さっき、デカいウシを ひっとらえたぜ‥‥そのワナで。
English: I just roped me a steer with this here lasso.

Japanese: え！
English: What!?

Japanese: “もーッス！”“もーッス！” ‥‥しきりに、鳴いてたっけな。
English: It was a' bellowing "moo, pal! moo!"

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事か‥‥）
English: (Detective Gumshoe, I presume...)

Japanese: このドアの向こうが 《証拠保管室》だね。
English: The evidence room is beyond that door.

Japanese: カードリーダーの電源は 入れておいたぜ。
English: I turned on the card reader.

Japanese: せいぜい、楽しんでくることさ、 カウボーイ！
English: Go have yourself a ball, pardners!

Japanese: このＩＤカード、 あたしが借りたんですからね！
English: And I have the ID card! A real ID card!

Japanese: （‥‥早いトコ、現場を 調べたほうがいいかな‥‥）
English: (Let's get this investigation started already...)

Japanese: わあ。西部のサルーンみたい！ この、スイングドア。
English: Look at this swinging door! Just a like a wild west saloon!

Japanese: おっと‥‥。 今日はもう、閉店だぜ。
English: Ah... Sorry, pardner, but we're closed for the day.
Huh?

Japanese: 早く帰ってくれねえかい、 アンタたち。
English: Y'all get along home now!

Japanese: お店のマスター気取りですね、 罪門さん。
English: I think Officer Marshall really wanted to be a wild west bartender.

Japanese: ‥‥一度、このセリフが 言ってみたくてね。
English: I always wanted to say that.

Japanese: この先に、あたしたちの 新世界が広がっているワケですね！
English: Beyond that door, a whole new world awaits us!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人現場の証拠保管室、だね。
English: Right, the evidence room where the murder took place...

Japanese: 新世界へのティケットなら、 ここにありますし。
English: And our ticket to this new world is right here.

Japanese: ‥‥局長さんから借りた お客さん用のＩＤ、だね。
English: The visitor's ID we borrowed from the Chief of Police I'm guessing...

Japanese: さあ。いざ！ このサルーンを出発しましょう！
English: Right! Let's leave this saloon behind us in the dust!

Japanese: （‥‥罪門巡査から、よくない 影響をモロに受けているな‥‥）
English: (She's definitely the impressionable type...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。おハナシを うかがいたいんですけど‥‥
English: Um, I was wondering if we could talk to you...

Japanese: 悪いが、バンビーナ。
English: Sorry, bambina.

Japanese: オレは今、タンブル・ウィードと ともに、荒野をかけめぐってるよ。
English: But I'm off to roam the lands, like a tumbleweed on the wide prairie.

Japanese: ガンスリンガーが、マスケットに 弾丸をこめるよう、祈りながらね。
English: Like a gunslinger loading his six-shooter, I say a little prayer.

Japanese: ‥ぐぎゅるるぐぐ‥‥ぅぅ‥‥う‥
English: GRROOOOORRK

Japanese: ‥‥で。ケッキョク、どういう コトですか？　成歩堂さん！
English: What was that all about, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ハラをすかせて、しゃべる気分じゃ ない。‥‥そういうコト、かな。
English: I think he was just too hungry to talk.

Japanese: それ‥‥、最後の“音”だけで キメつけてませんか？
English: You're just saying that because his stomach was growling! You have no idea what he was talking about either!

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 なんとかしないとな‥‥）
English: (Well, in any case, we need to get cracking on this investigation, pronto!)

Japanese: 罪門さん。証拠保管室の 警備を担当してるんですよね？
English: Officer Marshall, you're in charge of security for the evidence room, right?

Japanese: ダレにでもできる、ありがたい シゴトさ。‥‥よく知ってるね。
English: You got good eyes, pardner. It's an easy job, and I'm grateful for it.

Japanese: 留置所の、原灰巡査から 教えてもらいました。
English: Actually, Officer Meekins at the detention center told us.

Japanese: ああ‥‥あの、 カワイソウなボウヤか。
English: Ah, that poor little dogie?

Japanese: ２回に１回は、自分の名前を “灰原”と書きまちがえる‥‥
English: Poor guy, I keep getting his name wrong and calling him "Meekly."

Japanese: 彼は‥‥こんなコトも 教えてくれました。
English: He told us something.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、あなたは 警備室にいなかった‥‥と。
English: He said that, when the stabbing occurred... you weren't at your station.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ こんなコト、言っちゃマズいが。 ...
English: Well, maybe I shouldn't be telling you this...

Japanese: 今から、２年前。 捜査官からハズれてから‥‥
English: But since I got demoted from detective two years ago...

Japanese: 見た目ほど、ヤル気マンマン ってワケじゃないんだよ。
English: Well, it might not look it, but I lost my fire for the job, you know?

Japanese: あの‥‥何をしてたんですか？ 事件が起こった、５時１５分ごろ。
English: So... what were you doing around 5:15 when the murder took place?

Japanese: さあね‥‥。ジップ・コチーズ号で 街道をギャロップで流してたかな。
English: Well... I reckon I was galloping down the highway on the back of my steed, Zippy.

Japanese: ‥‥名馬にまたがって 高速道路を走っていた、と。
English: Note: he was riding down the highway on his horse named "Zippy."

Japanese: とりあえず、ここには ニンゲンは必要ないのさ。
English: There's no need for people here, anyhow.

Japanese: 最新のキカイたちが、キチンと 見張ってくれるらしいからね。
English: These newfangled machines do a bang-up job of keeping an eye on the place.

Japanese: 最新のキカイ‥‥ “防犯システム”ですか。
English: You mean the security camera system?

Japanese: キカイ‥‥か。
English: I don't take to machines much.

Japanese: ステーキのヨコの、ゆでた ブロッコリーの次にニガテだよ。
English: Kinda like that stewed broccoli they sneak in next to your steak, you know?

Japanese: おキョウさんから聞きました。
English: Ms. Starr told us something...

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥２年前まで 捜査官だったんだ、って。
English: She said that you were a detective until two years ago.

Japanese: “事件現場のブッシュワッカー” ‥‥荒野にうずめたユメのアトさ。
English: It was always my dream to be a rawhide wrangler on the scene of the crime... That's all gone now... Like a drinking hole in a prairie fire.

Japanese: 今も、調べてるんですよね。 ＳＬ９号事件‥‥おキョウさんと。
English: You're still investigating the SL-9 Incident with Ms. Starr, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥あれは、オレの事件だった。
English: That was my case...

Japanese: 記録の上では《解決》している。 お得意のイカサマポーカーさ。
English: It's all "solved" on the record books. But it smells like a bad game of poker.

Japanese: しかし。オレはナットクできない。 ‥‥それだけのコト、だよ。
English: I can't let it go... That's all there is to it.

Japanese: あの。どんな事件だったんですか？ 《ＳＬ９号事件》って。
English: What kind of case was it, anyway?

Japanese: 名前だけ、よく聞くんですけど‥‥ ダレも教えてくれないんです。
English: We've heard the name so many times, but no one tells us what actually happened.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 知らないほうがいいコトも あるよ、バンビーナ。
English: There are some things you're better off not knowing, Bambina.

Japanese: それに‥‥あの事件は、２日前。 今度こそ、完全に死んじまった。
English: Anyway, that case is officially dead as of two days ago.

Japanese: “２日前”‥‥ 事件があった日、ですか？
English: Two days ago... the day of our case!

Japanese: そう‥‥ 《証拠品の申し送り》さ。
English: That's right... The evidence transferals.

Japanese: （御剣も言ってたな ‥‥《申し送り》）
English: (Edgeworth was talking about the transferals, too.)

Japanese: オレが知っているキカイは とりあえず、２つさ。
English: I know what maybe two of the machines in here do.

Japanese: と、“とりあえず”‥‥？
English: O-only two of them? There must be a dozen!

Japanese: 言っただろ、バンビーナ。 オレにとってキカイは‥‥
English: Like I said, bambina, Me and machines, well...

Japanese: ステーキのヨコの、ゆでた カリフラワーの次にニガテなのさ。
English: I like them about as much as I like stewed cauliflower with my steaks.

Japanese: まず。わかりやすいのは 《防犯カメラ》だろう。
English: The easiest ones to understand are these here security cameras.

Japanese: （ハラバイ巡査も言ってたな）
English: (Those are the ones that Officer Meekins mentioned.)

Japanese: 数時間ごとに、異常がなければ 消去されちまうらしいけどね。
English: If nothing happens, the tapes are automatically erased every few hours.

Japanese: そのカメラに、映ってたんですか？ 原灰巡査と‥‥多田敷捜査官が。
English: And Officer Meekins and Detective Goodman, are they on one of those tapes?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんね。
English: I reckon they might be.

Japanese: （セキニンシャが “たぶん”はないだろう‥‥）
English: (You're the security guard and you "reckon"!?)

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。
English: One more thing.

Japanese: 証拠保管室に入るときには、 ＩＤカードが必要なんだよ。
English: When you go into the evidence room... You need an ID card.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ドアのワキに カードリーダーがあるな）
English: (Thus the card reader by the door.)

Japanese: ＩＤカードを使うと、記録が残る。 ありがちだが‥‥わかりやすいさ。
English: The card reader leaves a record of every ID card passed through.

Japanese: （これが、ＩＤの使用記録‥‥）
English: (So this is the ID card record...)

Japanese: あ‥‥‥あれ！ 見たことがあるナンバーが‥‥
English: Hey! I've seen that somewhere before!

Japanese: おっと‥‥！　これ以上は 見せられないな、バンビーナ。
English: Sorry, Bambina. I can't show you more than that.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: コイツが事件にカンケイするか‥‥ オレは聞かされてないからね。
English: I haven't heard whether this is related to the case, yet.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　今の記録。 見おぼえのあるナンバーが‥‥
English: Mr. Wright! I saw a number on that record just now! I've seen that number before!

Japanese: （使用記録と事件との“カンケイ” ‥‥つきつけてやるんだ！）
English: (Maybe there's some way I can prove that record is tied to the stabbing.)

Japanese: すみませんけど‥‥そもそも、 《申し送り》って、なんですか？
English: Sorry, but could you explain what this whole "transferal" thing is about?

Japanese: 保管室には、解決した事件の 証拠品だけが眠っている。
English: We keep only evidence from solved cases in this room.

Japanese: それぞれの担当捜査官の管理で、 ２年間。ここに保管されるのさ。
English: They're kept here under the presiding detective's supervision for two years.

Japanese: 万が一、解決がまちがっていた場合 ‥‥再捜査のためにね。
English: So we can re-investigate them if it turns out there was a mistake, see?

Japanese: じゃあ、２年たった証拠品は どうなるんですか？
English: So, what happens to the evidence after two years?

Japanese: 警視庁の地下倉庫で、 永久に眠ることになる。
English: It goes to sleep forever in the underground vault at the Police Station.

Japanese: それが、《申し送り》ってヤツさ。 ‥‥年に一度、２月に行われる。
English: That's what we call "transferal." We do it every February.

Japanese: そう、だったんですか‥‥
English: I see now...

Japanese: 《申し送り》は‥‥そう。 事件の“葬式”とも言えるよ。
English: "Transferal" is like a funeral for old cases.

Japanese: 解決から、２年が経過したとき。 その事件は完全に終わって、死ぬ。
English: Two years after a case is solved... it's closed forever. Dead.

Japanese: もう、追跡捜査をすることは できない。‥‥永久に。
English: Never to be reopened again. Never to be reinvestigated.

Japanese: （そして‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》は ２日前、死んだワケか‥‥）
English: (And that happened to SL-9 two days ago...)
That smell... Ah! Reminds me of Texas!

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥ テキサスのヒトだったんですか！
English: So, Officer Marshall... You're from Texas?

Japanese: こないだテレビで見たさ。 ‥‥ベイビイからの差し入れかい？
English: No, I just saw a special on television the other day. Is this from my baby?

Japanese: え、ええ。おキョウさんから‥‥
English: Uh, yeah, Ms. Starr...

Japanese: こ‥‥コイツは‥‥ッ！
English: Wh-what's this!?

Japanese: ど、どうしました？
English: What? What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥フィレ・ステーキ弁当‥‥！ そうか！　そういうコトかッ！
English: A filet steak lunch! I see... I see!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうイミがあるのかな、 あのお弁当。
English: I don't see. I wonder what it means?

Japanese: 罪門巡査に 《ステーキ弁当》をわたした。
English: Steak Lunch given to Officer Marshall.

Japanese: オーライ、バンビーナ。 アンタの勝ちだ。
English: Alright, bambina. You win.

Japanese: なんでもぐもぐもぐもぐもぐもぐ。 聞いてごくごくごくごくごくごく。
English: Ask mmph mmph munch munch Anything mmph munch smack.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥）
English: (Finally, it seems like...)

Japanese: ハナシをしてくれる気に なったみたいですね！
English: He's willing to talk!

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、テキサスの保安官は、 証拠品なんか信じなかった。
English: The sheriffs back in the wild west didn't place much faith in evidence.

Japanese: 信じたのは‥‥自分の ウデっぷしだけ、だったのさ。
English: About the only thing they trusted was their shooting hand.

Japanese: あの。ここは“ムカシ”じゃないし “テキサス”でもないですけど‥‥
English: Um... this is neither "Wild" nor "West" here...

Japanese: ハッ！　それとコレとは、 ハナシがちがうんじゃないかな？
English: Ah hah! But that and this are two different things entirely!
I... guess so? Huh? I'm lost.

Japanese: （決定的にカンケイがある証拠品 以外、見せてもムダみたいだ）
English: (Looks like we need some evidence to get anywhere with this guy.)

Japanese: これ、見てください！ 被害者のＩＤカードなんですけど。
English: See this? This is the victim's ID card.

Japanese: ああ。‥‥たしか、現場の 地下駐車場に落ちていたな。
English: Ah, the one that was on the ground in the parking lot?

Japanese: コイツの番号なんですけど‥‥ 《５８４２１８９》です。
English: The number on this is...

Japanese: "5842189." 罪門さん！　さっきの記録‥‥ ＩＤの使用記録を見せてください！
English: Officer Marshall! Show us the ID number on that ID card record!

Japanese: この、４番目のナンバー‥‥ カンゼンに一致します！
English: Look, the fourth number! It's a perfect match!

Japanese: 使用された時刻は、５時１４分。 事件が起こった時間‥‥だ！
English: It was used at... 5:14. Right before the stabbing!

Japanese: しかし！　ＩＤカードは、この世に １枚しか存在しないはずだぜ！
English: What's more, there's only one of them cards in the world!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥事件があった時間、 多田敷さんは証拠保管室に‥‥
English: So, when the incident occurred, Detective Goodman was in the evidence room!

Japanese: （でも、待てよ‥‥ 原灰巡査は、こう言っていた！）
English: (But wait, what did Officer Meekins say?)

Japanese: 保管室に入った本官は、ＩＤカード の提示を求めたであります！
English: Sir, I entered the evidence room, and asked the man to display his ID card!

Japanese: それで‥‥ＩＤの提示を求められた 多田敷捜査官は？　どうしました？
English: So you asked Detective Goodman to show his ID card. What did he do?

Japanese: なんとッ！　いきなり、本官に ナイフを向けたであります！
English: That's the thing! Suddenly he pointed a knife at me!

Japanese: （そのとき、彼がＩＤカードを 持っていたのならば‥‥）
English: (If he had his ID card then...)

Japanese: （原灰巡査に切りかかる必要 なんか、なかったハズだ‥‥）
English: (Why would he have pointed a knife at Officer Meekins?)

Japanese: ‥‥オーライ、カウボーイ。 アンタの勝ちさ。
English: Alright, compadre, you win.

Japanese: この記録‥‥アンタに わたしたほうがよさそうだ。
English: I guess I can give you this ID card record.

Japanese: 証拠品《ＩＤカード使用記録》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: ID Card Record added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （このリスト‥‥）
English: (I've got an idea...)

Japanese: （ＩＤを持っていそうな人物に 見せてみたほうがいいな）
English: (Maybe I should show this list to other people with IDs here...)

Japanese: ２月２３日　某時刻 証拠保管室／第３号庫
English: February 23 Evidence Room Sector Three

Japanese: （静かだな‥‥ 捜査は終わってるみたいだ）
English: (It's quiet... the investigation must be over here.)

Japanese: 証拠保管室‥‥かあ。 ホント、お墓みたいですね。
English: So this is the evidence room? It really is kind of like a graveyard.

Japanese: “お墓”というより‥‥ “死体置き場”だよ。このムード。
English: Graveyards are supposed to have grass and trees. This feels more like a morgue.

Japanese: ここ、こ。怖がらせようとしても む。ムダですからね。
English: N-nice try, M-Mr. Wright. Y-you can't scare me!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: Eeeeeeek!

Japanese: ほほほおおおおおおおおおおいッ！
English: Whoooooah!

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさい。 悪霊かと思ったんです。
English: S-sorry, I thought you were a ghost!

Japanese: ‥‥悪霊に、いきなり ビンタはどうかと思うッス。
English: I wouldn't recommend going around smacking ghosts on the head, pal.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥ホントッスね？ さっきのハナシは！
English: So, is it true what I heard?

Japanese: ‥‥ボクさ。そんなね。 ケチなオトコじゃないワケ。
English: Wrighto, please, do I look like a selfish man?

Japanese: ダレにたのまれても、 ５０００円までなら貸すし。
English: Heck, if anyone asked me "sir, can I borrow $50?" I'd give them $50, no problem.

Japanese: ‥‥ぜんぜんモンダイないよ。 好きに調べてくれていいから。
English: So, go ahead! Investigate that room to your heart's desire! Knock yourself out!

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥ホントですよ。
English: Yeah, it's true.

Japanese: 巌徒局長殿が‥‥
English: So, Chief of Police Gant...

Japanese: ５０００円も貸してくれるッ！ この自分がたのんでもッ！
English: will loan anyone 50 bucks? Even me!?

Japanese: ‥‥そっちですか。
English: Oh, so that's what you were talking about...

Japanese: 今日は自分、捜査班長を 任命されていたッス。
English: Actually, I was put in charge of the investigation for today.

Japanese: １日だけですか。
English: Just for today?

Japanese: “１日班長さん”ですね。
English: Boss for a day...

Japanese: しかし、これからはッ！ アンタが１日班長ッス！
English: But guess what!? You got permission from the Chief... So now you're boss for a day!

Japanese: （うれしくないヒビキだな）
English: (Gee, thanks.)

Japanese: とりあえず、これ。 持っておくといいッス。
English: First of all, you'll want to have this.

Japanese: 証拠品《証拠保管室の上面図》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Evidence Room Floor Plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥おッ！ もう、こんな時間ッスか。
English: Hey, pal, look at the time!!

Japanese: あれ。何か用事でもあるんですか？
English: Was there something you needed to be going to?

Japanese: そろそろ、御剣検事の査問会が 終わるッス。
English: It's just that Mr. Edgeworth's inquiry committee should be letting out soon.

Japanese: 自分、今日の捜査の報告書を わたしてくるッス！
English: I'm going to go give them my report for the day.

Japanese: 何かの助けになるかも‥‥
English: It might help, you know...

Japanese: ほ、報告書、って‥‥ その、チラシですか？
English: R-report? You mean the note written on the back of that flyer?

Japanese: しかも、ウラに《異常なしッス》 しか、書いてありませんけど。
English: The one that says nothing but "No problems"!?

Japanese: 御剣検事なら！　こんな報告書すら 役に立ててくれるハズッス！
English: Hey, it's Mr. Edgeworth we're talking about! I'm sure he can use a report like this.

Japanese: 自分、信じてるッス！
English: I believe in him!

Japanese: （アツ苦しい信頼を寄せられる 立場も、キノドクなもんだな）
English: (Who needs enemies when you've got friends like Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃっ！ ひとっ走り、行ってくるッス！
English: I'm off, pal! Later!

Japanese: （御剣‥‥ぼくも、話を聞いて おいたほうがいいかな）
English: (I should probably see what Edgeworth has to say, too.)

Japanese: さっき入ってきたドア、ですね。
English: That's the door we just came in through.

Japanese: 出るときは、いらないみたいだ。 ‥‥ＩＤカード。
English: Looks like you don't need an ID card to get out.

Japanese: 罪門さんが電源を切ったら どうなっちゃうんでしょうね。
English: I wonder what would happen if Officer Marshall cut the power while we were inside?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （そのへん、ちゃんと考えてる のかな、あのヒト）
English: (Let's hope he remembers we're in here.)

Japanese: うわ。おっきなガラクタが つみ上げられてますよ！
English: Wow! Look at this big pile of junk in the corner!

Japanese: これは‥‥クルマのドア、だね。
English: That looks like... a car door.

Japanese: ドアのフレームに 手錠がついてますよ。
English: There's a pair of handcuffs attached to the frame.

Japanese: ‥‥タイホした怪盗に 逃げられたのかな。
English: Maybe the guy they caught was some sort of escape artist and he got away.

Japanese: こっちはアレですね。 現場検証用のヒト型。
English: Hey, that's one of those human profiles for range testing.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒタイを キレイに撃ち抜かれてるな。
English: He's been shot square in the forehead.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんも、撃ち抜かれない ように、気をつけてくださいね。
English: Better him than us.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、ハミ出してるね。
English: There's something sticking out of here.

Japanese: シャツ‥‥かな。 何かの証拠品でしょうか。
English: Looks like a shirt. I guess it must be evidence for some case.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: I wonder if Detective Gumshoe put this here.

Japanese: ヒトを“だらしないヤツ”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kinda slob!

Japanese: 自分の保管庫は、そこじゃ ないッスから。‥‥とは言え。
English: I'm not responsible for the evidence here. That said...

Japanese: おそらく、これは‥‥最近。 開けられた保管庫ッスね。
English: I bet that evidence locker was opened recently.

Japanese: どうしてわかるんですか？
English: How do you know?

Japanese: こんなふうにしたら、 証拠品がイタむッスからね。
English: If you leave things hanging out like that, the evidence gets dirty or ripped.

Japanese: 見回りの巡査がチェックして、 担当の捜査官が注意されるッス。
English: The guard checks on that kind of stuff and notifies the detective responsible.

Japanese: ‥‥まったく、 うるさくてかなわないッス！
English: How many times have I had him breathing down my neck about some silly evidence...

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事さん、いつも 注意されてるみたいですね。
English: Sounds like Detective Gumshoe leaves evidence hanging out a lot, too.

Japanese: きっと、コートの下では、シャツが ズボンからハミ出てるんだろうね。
English: I bet he doesn't tuck in his shirt under that trench coat, either.

Japanese: ‥‥カゲグチなら、聞こえない トコでやってほしいッス。
English: If you're going to talk behind someone's back, don't do it right in front of them, pal!

Japanese: 白い布がハミ出てるね。 ‥‥シャツかな。
English: A piece of white cloth is sticking out... looks like a shirt.

Japanese: ちゃんとしまっておかないと シワになっちゃいますよ！
English: You'd better tidy it up or it will get wrinkled!

Japanese: ぼくをニラまないで くれるかな。
English: Hey, don't look at me! I didn't leave it hanging out like that!

Japanese: この中に、パンパンに詰まってる ワケですね。ユメと、証拠品が。
English: This place is stuffed with evidence... stuffed with dreams.

Japanese: ユメはどうかと思うけどね。
English: I'm not so sure about the dreams.

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Mmph!

Japanese: 開かない。
English: It won't open.

Japanese: そりゃ、そうだろうなあ。
English: I guess I should have known.

Japanese: まあ。最新式のセキュリティで 守られているッスからねえ。
English: Hey, pal, our security is high-tech around here!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、こまごまとした キカイが、ホコリをかぶってるね。
English: Some sort of bulky equipment is gathering dust here.

Japanese: かわいそうなほど ボロっちいツリザオがあります。
English: What a sorry looking fishing pole that is.

Japanese: あ、それ！　自分のッス。
English: Ah! That's my personal pole!

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 使ったことないッス。
English: I never did get around to using it...

Japanese: あれ！　これ、どこかで 見たような‥‥
English: Wait, I've seen that somewhere before...

Japanese: あ、それ！　金属探知機ッスよ！
English: Right, pal. That's that metal detector!

Japanese: ホレ。ひょうたん湖の事件を 解決にみちびいた‥‥
English: The one that led to the solving of that case out on Gourd Lake, remember?

Japanese: ああ！　なつかしいですね。
English: Oh, right! Wow, that feels like it was ages ago.

Japanese: そして、これは‥‥ 見たコトないですね。
English: And... hmm, I don't think I've seen this one before.

Japanese: あ、それ！　電波探知機ッス。
English: Oh, that! That's a radiolocator!

Japanese: 近い将来、なんらかの事件を 解決にみちびくかもしれないッス。
English: I'm sure it will come in handy in solving some case sooner or later.

Japanese: （このチャチなハコが‥‥？）
English: (That cheap-looking box?)

Japanese: 人間とキカイは、見た目じゃない！ ‥‥“マゴコロ”ッス！
English: You can't judge a person or a machine by their cover! You gotta look at their heart!

Japanese: うらぶれたガラクタたちが、 やつれ果て、打ちひしがれてます！
English: A pile of junk lies battered, worn and forsaken!

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、そこまで 言わなくてもいいんじゃないかな。
English: Aren't you being a little dramatic?

Japanese: ツリザオに、金属探知機。 これは‥‥知らないな。
English: Looks like a fishing pole... and a metal detector. Not sure what this one is.

Japanese: やるせなくホコリをかぶって、 ウツロな目でこっちを見てます！
English: They cast a withering gaze in our direction that screams, "dust me!"

Japanese: ‥‥だから、わざわざ文学的に 表現しなくていいよ。
English: Can't junk just be plain old junk?

Japanese: ここだけ、開いてる‥‥ インジケータがついたままです！
English: Look, this one's open! And there's a indicator tag stuck on it still!

Japanese: そこが、多田敷刑事の 証拠保管庫ッス。
English: That locker is coded with Detective Goodman's fingerprint.

Japanese: （被害者の‥‥保管庫！）
English: (Detective Goodman's locker!)

Japanese: いいんですか？　開けっぱなしで。
English: Are you sure it's okay to leave it open like that?

Japanese: 一度閉めちゃうと、 開けるのがタイヘンッスから。
English: Well, it'd be hard to get it open again if we closed it.

Japanese: （中身は、カラ。‥‥さすがに どこかへ移されたようだな‥‥）
English: (It's empty... they must have taken the contents elsewhere.)

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官の保管庫ですね。 中身はカラッポです。
English: Detective Goodman's evidence locker. It's empty.

Japanese: 保管庫を開けられるのは、 多田敷さん本人だけ‥‥
English: Only Detective Goodman himself can open this locker.

Japanese: つまり、このナイフを持ち出した のも、多田敷さん、ってコトかあ。
English: So, the person who took out this knife had to have been Detective Goodman.

Japanese: （なぜ、それがマフラーに包まれて マフラーから出てきたんだ？）
English: (And why was it wrapped in the "scarf" muffler stuck inside the "car" muffler?)

Japanese: こ‥‥これは‥‥血痕！
English: Wh-what's this...!? Blood!

Japanese: カスれてますけど、 アキラカに、手のアトですッ！
English: It's a little worn... but there's definitely a handprint here!

Japanese: ふき消そうとして 残ってしまったみたいだな‥‥
English: It looks like someone tried to wipe it off.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　この部屋‥‥
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 他にも、血痕が 残っているかもしれません！
English: What if there are other bloodstains left in the room!?

Japanese: （‥‥あのクスリを使って、 よく調べたほうがいいな）
English: (We should use her testing fluid to check it out.)

Japanese: こんなところに、手袋が ‥‥かたっぽだけ。
English: Someone left a glove here... but only one.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: Detective Gumshoe, maybe?

Japanese: ヒトを“うっかり屋”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kind of absent- minded detective!

Japanese: ソイツも‥‥ “例の事件”の証拠品ッスね。
English: That's evidence from "the" case, you know.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ですか‥‥ （たしかに、タグがついてるな）
English: You mean SL-9? (It does have a tag on it...)

Japanese: 証拠品《ゴム手袋》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Rubber Glove added to the Court Record.

Japanese: きのう、初めて見ました。 この、黄色いテープ。
English: That yellow crime scene tape! You know, yesterday was my first time seeing that!

Japanese: 群衆をかきわけて、これを くぐるのがユメだったんですけど。
English: It was always my dream to push through the crowd and just step over the tape!

Japanese: ２日連続で出くわすと、ちょっと ヘコんじゃいますね！
English: It's a little daunting to encounter it again so soon!

Japanese: （‥‥そうは見えないけど）
English: (I think maybe you're overreacting a little...)

Japanese: ‥‥ハデに割れたみたいですね。 何か、大きなものが。
English: Wow, someone must have broken something big to make all these pieces.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事ですか、コレ。
English: Detective Gumshoe, perhaps?

Japanese: ヒトを“いたずらっ子”みたいに 言わないでほしいッス。
English: There you go, pal, making me out to be some kind of hooligan!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥“例の事件”の 証拠品みたいッスね。
English: That's apparently from "the" case.

Japanese: 例の事件‥‥？
English: "The" case?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ッス。 カケラの１枚に、小さなシールが。
English: The SL-9 Incident, pal. See the sticker on one of the pieces there?

Japanese: （《ＳＬ９号事件》の証拠品が また出てきたか‥‥）
English: (Another piece of SL-9 evidence...)

Japanese: （‥‥やめておこう。 時間がもったいない‥‥）
English: (Let's not waste too much time with this now.)

Japanese: あとで、ちゃんと 調べてみましょうね。
English: We can check it out later.

Japanese: このカケラを組み合わせると、 こんなツボになるワケだね。
English: If you put these pieces together, it makes a jar.

Japanese: ‥‥気になる点が、 ２つほどありますね。
English: There are two things that bother me.

Japanese: １つは‥‥ なぜ、カケラが足りないのか？
English: One: why are some of the pieces missing?

Japanese: そして、もう１つは‥‥ ちょっと不安定すぎます！
English: Two: doesn't it seem a little unstable!?

Japanese: そりゃ、割れますよ！
English: No wonder it broke!

Japanese: ‥‥今度、ツボを作ることが あったら、気をつけるよ。
English: I'll make sure to remember that next time I make a jar.

Japanese: なんでこんなところに、 ペンキとノコギリが‥‥
English: What is a saw and paint doing here?

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシから。
English: Since the dawn of time...

Japanese: ゲージュツはいつも、 《迫害》との戦いだったッス。
English: True art has always been a war against oppression.

Japanese: ゲージュツ？　そういえば‥‥ このペンキ、水色と黄色ですね。
English: True art...? I noticed that there's blue and yellow paint here.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ここで 生まれたんですか？　タイホくん。
English: Perhaps we're witnessing the birthplace of the Blue Badger?

Japanese: まあ‥‥自分にとっては、 アトリエ？　みたいなモノッスね。
English: Well... You might say this is my studio...

Japanese: （いいのか‥‥？）
English: (Here? In the evidence room!?)

Japanese: 青いペンキと黄色いペンキが ありますね。
English: There's blue and yellow paint here.

Japanese: これだけあれば、成歩堂さんの 絵、描けますよ、あたし。
English: This is all I'd need to paint a portrait of you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: いちおう、ネクタイも してるんだけどな、ぼく。
English: I am wearing a red necktie, you know...

Japanese: １日班長さん、なんですよね？ 刑事さん。
English: So, Detective Gumshoe, you're boss for the day?

Japanese: そッスよ！　メーヨな任務ッス！
English: That's right! It's an honor!

Japanese: なんせ、警察局内で発生した サツジン事件ッスからねえ‥‥
English: After all, the murder took place right here, in the Police Department!

Japanese: それにしては‥‥ ヒトリぽっちじゃないですか。
English: But, if you're boss... why are you all alone!?

Japanese: 他のヒトたちは‥‥？
English: Where are your underlings?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... きのうの捜査をもとに、明日の 法廷のジュンビをしているッス。
English: They're using our findings from yesterday's investigation to prepare for the trial.

Japanese: （‥‥つまり。今日もハズされて るのか。捜査班から‥‥）
English: (In other words, Detective Gumshoe got kicked out of the investigation again...)

Japanese: でも自分、メゲないッス！
English: I'm adamant, though!

Japanese: 担当捜査官の座をモギとって、 この事件を眠らせてみせるッス！
English: I'm going to take control and put this case to rest!

Japanese: ‥‥自分の証拠保管庫にッ！
English: And in my own evidence locker, pal!

Japanese: イトノコさんの保管庫も あるんですか？　ここに。
English: You have a locker in here, too, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ハッハッ。そりゃそッス。 自分は‥‥刑事、ッスからねえ。
English: Hah hah, of course! I am a detective, after all.

Japanese: 自分にしか開けられない保管庫を、 バッチリもらってるッス。
English: They gave me a locker that only I can open, pal!

Japanese: “自分にしか”開けられない‥‥？
English: "Only you can open"...?

Japanese: 自分は‥‥御剣検事を 信じているッス。何があっても。
