English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: さっきの尋問で、 ぼくが立証したのは、ただ１つ。
English: This cross-examination has proven one thing and one thing only.

Japanese: 《防犯カメラの映像は、 殺人事件の瞬間ではない》‥‥
English: The security video did not show the actual murder.

Japanese: しかし！　これが、検事局の事件と 無関係であるはずがない！
English: However! It cannot be said that it is unrelated to the murder in the parking lot.

Japanese: ‥‥ジッサイ、証拠保管室からは 大量の血痕が発見されている！
English: Specifically, large amounts of blood traces were found in the evidence room!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 弁護側は、 事実の追求を要請します！
English: The defense demands further examination into the truth of the matter!
...Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: そういうコトになれば、 さらなる証人が必要になります。
English: If this court were to examine this further, other witnesses will be necessary.

Japanese: 検察側に、 その用意がありますかな？
English: Is the prosecution prepared?

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら。
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor...

Japanese: 検察側としては、警察局の件は あくまで、予備審理と考えていた。
English: The prosecution considered the incident at the Police Department to be unrelated.

Japanese: ‥‥この事件に関して、 他に証人の用意はない。
English: We have not prepared any other witnesses for this incident.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ ここがチャンスのようだな）
English: (This just might be my chance.)

Japanese: （“あの人物”を 法廷に引きずり出す‥‥！）
English: (Time to call a certain Texas ranger to the stand!)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥ やっぱり、あのヒトを‥‥？
English: Mr. Wright... Do you mean...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側には‥‥ 証人の心当たりがあります。
English: The defense would like to request a specific witness.

Japanese: ほほお‥‥ それは、いかなる人物ですかな？
English: Oh? Whom do you have in mind?

Japanese: おそらく、証拠保管室の事件‥‥ その真相に深く関係する人物です。
English: Someone we have reason to believe knows the truth... The truth behind the activities that took place in the evidence room.

Japanese: ‥‥その人物の名前を 聞かせてもらえるだろうか。
English: The prosecution requests to hear this person's name

Japanese: 喚問に応じるかどうかは、 それから‥‥だ。
English: before deciding whether or not to comply.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！
English: Very well, then. Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたの考えている“証人”‥‥ いったい、どなたですかな？
English: This person whom you would have testify... What is his or her name?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、コンキョを 聞くまでもないようです。
English: Mr. Wright, I'm not even going to bother asking you why.

Japanese: そこまでチカラ強く泳いでいる目、 はじめて見ますからな。
English: I've never seen a more obvious stall tactic in my entire career!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！ 今朝、言ってたじゃないですか！
English: Mr. Wright! Don't you remember what you said this morning!?

Japanese: “最強のカードがある”って。
English: You know, about your "trump card"?

Japanese: （そ、そうだった！ 保管室で見つけた“痕跡”‥‥）
English: (That's right! Those bloody fingerprints we found in the evidence room...)

Japanese: （彼を引きずり出すチャンスは “今”しかないッ！）
English: (Now's my chance to bring him in to testify!)

Japanese: 罪門　恭介‥‥巡査です。
English: Officer... Jake Marshall.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、その男を？
English: Why him?

Japanese: （今、切り札を見せるワケには いかない‥‥）
English: (I can't let him know everything just yet...)

Japanese: 彼は、保管室の警備責任者です。 ‥‥証言を聞くべきと考えます。
English: He's in charge of the evidence room. I feel we should hear what he has to say.

Japanese: ‥‥‥よかろう。
English: The prosecution agrees to the defense's request.

Japanese: たしかに、責任者として 証言を聞くべき‥‥そう考える。
English: Since he was responsible for guarding the room, we should hear his testimony.

Japanese: さいわい彼は、警察局内の勤務。 準備に２０分以上はかかるまい。
English: Fortunately he works in the Police Department. We shouldn't need longer than 20 minutes to prepare.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 証人を召喚するあいだ、３０分間の 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: The court will take a 30 minute recess while the witness is subpoenaed.

Japanese: 検察側は、証人の手配を よろしくおねがいします。
English: Will the prosecution please prepare the witness during this time?

Japanese: ‥‥心得ている。
English: We will, Your Honor.

Japanese: それでは！ これより、休憩に入ります！
English: Court in recess!

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時３２分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 24, 11:32 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... やはり‥‥あなたを止めるのは 不可能ですね、成歩堂弁護士。
English: There's no stopping you is there, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことですか？
English: Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: あなたは、罪門　恭介を喚問した。
English: You called for Jake Marshall.

Japanese: もう、おわかりなのでしょう。 ‥‥何もかも。
English: It seems you've figured everything out.

Japanese: は、はあ。 （わかってないぞ。‥‥何もかも）
English: Uh... (I haven't figured anything out...)

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Lana...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんこそ！ 何もかも知ってるんでしょ！
English: You're the one who knows everything!

Japanese: あかね‥‥
English: Ema...

Japanese: いつも、そうだよ。 どうして、教えてくれないの！
English: You always know everything! Why don't you just tell us!?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥こんなに がんばってくれてるのに。
English: Mr. Wright is trying his hardest to protect you!

Japanese: わ‥‥私は。 たのんだおぼえはありません。
English: I... I don't recall ever asking for his "protection."

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドいよ。
English: How can you be so cold...

Japanese: あたしが‥‥あたしたちが、 そんなに信用できないの？
English: Don't you trust us? Don't you trust... me?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... シツレイするッス！
English: Hope I'm not interrupting anything, pals.
Oh... Guess I am. I'll come back later.

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコ刑事！ どうしたんですか？
English: Oh, Detective Gumshoe! What is it?

Japanese: アンタ！　まったく、 シツレイしちゃうッス！
English: You've got a lot of nerve, pal! Making a detective run all around while on duty,

Japanese: 現職の刑事に使い走りさせた上、 来てみれば、この空気！
English: and to top it off you call me here... I've seen happier people at funerals!

Japanese: ゴクロウさま、イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Sorry, Detective.

Japanese: まったく‥‥‥あ。
English: You better be, pal...Hey...

Japanese: ああ。
English: Hey!

Japanese: あああ。
English: Hey!!

Japanese: ああああ。
English: Hey!!!

Japanese: あああああああああああああッ！
English: Hey!!!!

Japanese: オツカレサマであります、 宝月主席検事殿ッ！
English: I didn't see you there, Ms. Skye!

Japanese: たしかに、最近つかれぎみです。 それで‥‥
English: That's ok. So... Have you brought what I asked?

Japanese: 持ってきてもらえたかしら？ は。
English: Oh...

Japanese: はは。
English: Oh ho!

Japanese: ははは。
English: Oh ho ho!

Japanese: はははは。
English: Oh ho ho ho!

Japanese: はははははははははははははああ
English: Oh ho ho ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: コレでありますねッ！
English: You mean this, right!?

Japanese: ゴメンなさい。 私、今はとらわれの身ですから。
English: My apologies, Detective. Due to my present circumstances,

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士の名前を使って、 おねがいしましたの。
English: I was forced to use Mr. Wright's name when making my request.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくですか！
English: My name!?

Japanese: まさか、アンタから 指令が下るとは思わなかったッス。
English: Never in a million years would I have thought it was you who asked me,

Japanese: 『ちょっと、ＳＬ９号事件の資料を 持ってきてくださるかしら』
English: "Could I bother you to bring me the SL-9 Incident files?"

Japanese: 『おヒルまでに』 ‥‥以上ッス。
English: "I'll need them by noon." Talk about crazy...

Japanese: え‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》？
English: The "SL-9 Incident"?

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　それは‥‥
English: But Lana! That's...

Japanese: 弁護士に必要だろうと、 手配しておきました。
English: I thought Mr. Wright might need them, so I had them brought here.

Japanese: あの事件の資料です。 読んでおくとよいでしょう。
English: Here. You might do well to read them.

Japanese: 自分、おどろいちまったッス。 主席検事殿も、証人だったとは！
English: I can't believe you, the Chief Prosecutor, were a witness in that case!

Japanese: ともえさんが‥‥“証人”？
English: Ms. Skye... was a witness?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件の資料》の ファイルを受け取った。
English: Received file of SL-9 Incident Files.

Japanese: いやー、連続殺人事件とは。 オソロシイ世の中ッスねー。
English: Take it from me, you don't want anything to do with serial murders.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... お、なんスか！　ヒトをアゴで 使った上、ムシッスか。
English: Oh, what? Now that I've brought you your stuff, you're just gonna ignore me?

Japanese: ‥‥‥あ、あかねちゃん！ どうして‥‥‥
English: E-Ema! But why...

Japanese: この事件資料に、 きみの名前があるんだ？
English: Why is your name in here?

Japanese: え！　あ、あたしの名前？ し、知りません‥‥‥あ。
English: What? My name's in there!? I don't know... Unless...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥‥お姉ちゃん！ 《ＳＬ９号事件》って‥‥
English: No, it couldn't be... Lana! This "SL-9 Incident"... Is that...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それは、警察がつけた 事件分類ナンバーです。
English: That's the classification number the police filed it under.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥世間では。 《青影事件》と呼ばれていました。
English: Two years ago, the rest of the world knew it as... the "Joe Darke Killings."

Japanese: あ‥‥あおかげ‥‥ そんな！　そんなのって‥‥！
English: The... Joe Darke... No... No, Lana!

Japanese: 今になって‥‥そんなの、ないよ！
English: That's over with! No!!!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　あかねちゃん！ （出て行ってしまった‥‥！）
English: Ema, wait! (She ran away...)

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥‥自分はこれで。 シツレイしちゃうッス！
English: Uh, ya know what? I just remembered... I gotta be somewhere. Sorry pal, but I'm outta here!

Japanese: （罪門　恭介、市ノ谷　響華、 巌徒　海慈、御剣　怜侍‥‥）
English: (Jake Marshall, Angel Starr, Damon Gant, Miles Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （そして、ともえさんに、 あかねちゃん‥‥）
English: (Not to mention Lana and Ema...)

Japanese: ‥‥すべての関係者が、 ２年前の事件に名前を連ねている。
English: Everyone involved in this case is connected to those killings two years ago...

Japanese: “グーゼン”‥‥では ありませんよね、これは。
English: This can't be just a "coincidence."

Japanese: ‥‥あなたならば。 いずれ、わかるかもしれません。
English: Knowing you, you just might be able to figure it out.

Japanese: そろそろ時間です。 行きましょうか、成歩堂弁護士。
English: Time to get back to the trial, Mr. Wright. Best of luck.

Japanese: （とにかく、この資料‥‥ よく読んでおいたほうがいいな）
English: (I'd better take a good look at this file...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！ 被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時１４分 地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 24, 12:14 PM District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開します。
English: The court will now reconvene for the trial of Ms. Lana Skye.

Japanese: （あかねちゃん‥‥ケッキョク、 戻ってこなかったな）
English: (Ema didn't come back...)

Japanese: では。事件当日‥‥
English: Allow me to call the next witness to the stand:

Japanese: 証拠保管室の警備にあたっていた 巡査を入廷させていただこう。
English: the officer in charge of guarding the evidence room on the day of the crime.

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業を。
English: Witness, please state your name and occupation.

Japanese: 大都会の荒野‥‥古い荷を負って ウシの群をかわして進む、旅人さ。
English: Me, pardner? Oh, I'm just a man, same as you, wanderin' the trails of civilization,

Japanese: 大通りで立ち往生したお年寄りを おぶって、交差点をわたる職業だ。
English: occasionally helping the elderly cross intersections when needed.

Japanese: あ。わかりました！ “おまわりさん”ですね。
English: Oh, I know! You're a "patrolman"!

Japanese: オレの名前なら‥‥サバクをわたる からっ風が、悲しく呼んでいるよ。
English: As for my name, if you listen hard 'nuff, you can hear the howling wind calling it out.

Japanese: セイカクには、罪門　恭介‥‥だ。
English: To be exact, it's Jake Marshall... Your Honor.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ、 “からっ風”あつかいか‥‥）
English: ("Howling wind"...? I've never heard Edgeworth described that way before...)

Japanese: さて‥‥証人。 さっそく、うかがおう。
English: Now, Mr. Marshall. Let me ask you something.

Japanese: 事件当日は、警備責任者として 証拠保管室の警備にあたっていた。
English: You were in charge of guarding the evidence room on the day the crime took place.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないだろうか？
English: Is this correct?

Japanese: 書類上はな。‥‥検事サマ。
English: According to the papers, pardner.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 紙キレ１枚で、荒野のならず者を シバることはできないさ。
English: A desperado's soul is as boundless as the desert sands. No "paper" can sum it up.

Japanese: どうやら、さっさと尋問に 移ったほうがいいようです。
English: Maybe it's best we get on with this quickly.

Japanese: 事件当日のことを 証言していただきましょう。
English: Please share with us your testimony of the day of the crime...

Japanese: ‥‥この国のコトバで！
English: in English!

Japanese: ～事件当日のこと～
English: -- Day of the Crime --

Japanese: オレのシゴトは、あの暗い墓場を 暗い目で見守るコトなのさ。
English: My job was to keep a wary eye on that bone orchard.

Japanese: １日に３回、見回りに行くキマリが あるそうだよ。‥‥関係ないがね。
English: They said I was supposed to make rounds three times a day, but that ain't my style.

Japanese: 警備室は、２重の防犯システムで 守られて‥‥オレは必要ないのさ。
English: Besides, the room's protected by two security systems, anyway.

Japanese: 事件があった時間は、たしか‥‥ 街道ぞいのサルーンにいたかな。
English: If I remember right, I was at a street-side saloon at the time it went down.

Japanese: この事件には、無関係の旅人さ。 ‥‥弾丸がないなら、帰るぜ。
English: I'm just an innocent travelin' man, so if you're out of ammo it's time I hit the trail.

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、あなたのタイドは 感心できませんねえ。
English: I can't say I particularly care for your attitude...

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、アンタのヒゲが 気に入らないんだろうさ。
English: I can't say I care for your beard, but you don't see me complainin'.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、 気になったんですけど‥‥
English: Wait a minute...

Japanese: “２重の防犯システム” というのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean by "two security systems"?

Japanese: 防犯カメラに、ＩＤカードの ナンバーを記録するシステムさ。
English: I mean the security cameras and the ID card reader.

Japanese: アンタも知ってるだろ？ カウボーイ。
English: I reckon even a cowpoke like you knows about those.

Japanese: あの。保管庫には、《指紋》の ロックもありますけど‥‥これは？
English: Yes, well, what about the fingerprint activated locks inside the evidence room?

Japanese: しもん‥‥？ 聞いたことねえぜ。
English: Fingerprint activated locks? What kind of new-fangled doohickeys are those?

Japanese: （“ねえぜ”と来たか‥‥）
English: (He's not being very helpful...)

Japanese: キカイに弱いというか、 使命感に弱いというか‥‥
English: He's not that good with machines... or with following orders.

Japanese: ヒトは弱いモノ、だぜ。 なあ、検事のボウヤ‥‥
English: Everyone's got their weaknesses, now don't they, Mr. Prosecutor?

Japanese: 何やら、モンダイのありそうな 証人ですな。
English: This one seems like trouble.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Okay Mr. Wright, he's all yours.

Japanese: ～事件当日のこと～
English: -- Day of the Crime --

Japanese: オレのシゴトは、あの暗い墓場を 暗い目で見守るコトなのさ。
English: My job was to keep a wary eye on that bone orchard.

Japanese: １日に３回、見回りに行くキマリが あるそうだよ。‥‥関係ないがね。
English: They said I was supposed to make rounds three times a day, but that ain't my style.

Japanese: 警備室は、２重の防犯システムで 守られて‥‥オレは必要ないのさ。
English: Besides, the room's protected by two security systems, anyway.

Japanese: 事件があった時間は、たしか‥‥ 街道ぞいのサルーンにいたかな。
English: If I remember right, I was at a street-side saloon at the time it went down.

Japanese: この事件には、無関係の旅人さ。 ‥‥弾丸がないなら、帰るぜ。
English: I'm just an innocent travelin' man, so if you're out of ammo it's time I hit the trail.

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥こっちには、 最強の“武器”がある）
English: (Okay... I have a trump card up my sleeve, so I'd best keep my cool.)

Japanese: （火薬がシメっちまう前に、 投げつけておくとするかな‥‥）
English: (Before I use it though, I'd better up the ante...)

Japanese: 具体的には‥‥どうやって “見守って”いるんですか？
English: How exactly did you "keep an eye" on the evidence room?

Japanese: 防犯カメラの、動かない映像だよ。 あそこじゃ、時間すら死んでるさ。
English: I just made sure nothing moved in the security camera monitor. That room's so still,

Japanese: オレは、その墓の番人らしいぜ。 カメラの映像を管理するだけの‥‥
English: even time dies in there. I was just a caretaker who interred the recordings.

Japanese: “管理”というのは？
English: You "interred" them?

Japanese: 動かない映像は、ムダだからね。 時間が来たら、消去するのさ。
English: Videos of nothing aren't that useful. When the time would come, I'd erase the tape.

Japanese: 規定では、異常がない場合、 ６時間ごとに記録を消去している。
English: If nothing unusual is recorded, tapes are to be erased every six hours.

Japanese: ‥‥映像を消去するたび、自分の 人生も消してるような気がするさ。
English: Each time I'd erase a tape, it felt like I was erasing a part of my life.

Japanese: （言ってるコトはカッコいいけど、 グチばっかりだな、さっきから）
English: (This guy has a flare for the dramatic, but it isn't going to do him any good.)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ジッサイに 保管室には入らないんですか？
English: So, in actuality you don't physically enter the evidence room?

Japanese: それでは、事件当日も当然、 見回ったわけですね？
English: But you made your rounds on the day of the crime, right?

Japanese: ヒトの話を聞くことさ、 カウボーイ‥‥
English: Ain't you heard a word I said, pardner?

Japanese: 言ったろ？　『関係ない』って。
English: I told you that ain't my style.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ あの。イミがわかりません。 ...
English: Um, I'm afraid I don't understand.

Japanese: 荒野のあらくれどもを、ルールで シバることはできないのさ。
English: No desperado I know lets "rules" get in his way.

Japanese: （あらくれどもは、警察に 就職しないと思うけどな）
English: (No desperados I know join the police force...)

Japanese: つまり、証人。事件当日は‥‥？
English: So, Officer Marshall. On the day of the crime...?

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥あの日、 保管室には入っていないさ。
English: Just between you and me, I didn't set foot in the evidence room that day.

Japanese: 被害者の保管庫に、手袋が 挟まっていましたが、コレは？
English: There was a rubber glove stuck in the victim's locker. Do you know anything about that?

Japanese: 知らないさ。 ‥‥もっと正直なトコロ‥‥
English: Sorry pardner, can't say I do.

Japanese: もう何週間も、あの墓場には 足を踏み入れていないからね。
English: I haven't been in that crypt in weeks.

Japanese: （よく、クビにならないな‥‥）
English: (How does this guy avoid being fired...?)

Japanese: あなたは、元・捜査官‥‥つまり。 あの保管庫を使ってたんですよね？
English: You used to be a detective, so you've used the evidence room in the past, correct?

Japanese: オレの保管庫は、証拠の ゴールドラッシュだったよ。
English: Of course. Back in the day my locker was a goldmine of evidence!

Japanese: それなのに‥‥《指紋》のロックを 知らなかったんですか？
English: And yet... you didn't know about the fingerprint locking mechanism?

Japanese: すまないね、カウボーイ。 キカイには弱くてね。
English: Sorry, pardner. I ain't good with machines.

Japanese: 自転車がなぜ走るのか？ そんなコトも知らないさ。
English: I couldn't even tell you how a bike works.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、 それは知っておきましょうよ‥‥
English: That's quite, uh... incredible.

Japanese: たしかに、取っ手に組みこまれた センサーは、目には見えない。
English: The sensors on the locker handles cannot be seen.

Japanese: たまに、シカケを知らない 捜査官もいるようだ。
English: It's well known that some detectives are unaware of their presence.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事も言ってたな）
English: (Now that he mentions it, Detective Gumshoe said something like that too...)

Japanese: とりあえず、事件には 関係ないようですな。
English: At any rate, it doesn't seem that this is relevant to the crime.

Japanese: 犯行があったときのコト、 聞かせてもらえますかな？
English: Can you tell us what you were doing when the crime took place?

Japanese: な。ナニしてたんですか、 そんなところで。
English: What were you doing in a place like that?

Japanese: スパゲッティ食ってた。
English: I was eating spaghetti.

Japanese: あそこのイカスミとボンゴレは、 おキョウのステーキも勝てねえさ。
English: Not even Angel's steak lunches can beat that parlor's vongole sepia pasta.

Japanese: で、では‥‥あなたは！
English: Do you mean to tell us...

Japanese: 警備のシゴトをほったらかして、 洋風メンをすすっていたのですか！
English: you abandoned your police duties to eat some noodles?

Japanese: ‥‥荒野の用心棒に、 うどんは似合わないさ‥‥
English: Not all desperados eat tacos, pardner.

Japanese: カッコつけてないで‥‥ おねがいだから、反省するように！
English: That's not what I meant... I hope this has at least taught you a lesson!

Japanese: （ちょっと、気になるな。 御剣のヤツ‥‥）
English: (That's strange...)

Japanese: （なぜか、トクイのフレーズが 出てこないぞ、今回は）
English: (This is usually where Edgeworth says,)

Japanese: （“来月の給与査定、 楽しみにしておくことだ”‥‥）
English: ("Did you not want a raise this year?"...)

Japanese: “弾丸”‥‥ですか？
English: Out of "ammo," officer Marshall?

Japanese: “証拠品”‥‥そう言いかえても かまわないさ。
English: That's right, pardner. Or as you'd call it, "evidence."

Japanese: オレと、事件を結びつける気なら、 スパッとやってくれないかな。
English: If you plan to pin me to this crime, then you'd better draw.

Japanese: もし、それができないのなら‥‥ 時間のムダだよ。
English: Otherwise, you're just wastin' my time.

Japanese: オレは太陽を追って、西へ走るさ。 ジップ・コチーズ号にまたがって。
English: My steel horse is waitin' to carry me back west into the sunset.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですな。 “セキニンシャ”と言うワリに‥‥
English: Hmm... One thing seems clear. Despite being responsible for guarding the evidence room,

Japanese: なんにも見ていない ようですからねえ。
English: the witness doesn't appear to have seen anything.

Japanese: テキサスの男は、使命感と女には めっぽうヨワいのさ。
English: Texans don't take orders from anyone. Everyone knows that.

Japanese: （‥‥怒られるぞ。 テキサスのヒトに‥‥）
English: (Apparently your superiors don't...)

Japanese: ‥‥罪門さん。 オカシイと思いませんか？
English: Officer Marshall. Doesn't it strike you as odd?

Japanese: なぜ、あなたが‥‥ ここに呼ばれたのか？
English: That is, you being called in to testify like this?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 事件のあった時間、あなたは 警備室にいなかった‥‥
English: After all, you weren't in the security room at the time of the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥だが。オレはこうして ここに呼ばれている。
English: And yet you dragged me down here.

Japanese: 聞かせてもらえるかい。 その“理由”とやらを。
English: Explain yourself, pardner.

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: It's quite simple.

Japanese: あなたは、現場に大きな “足跡”を残しているのです。
English: You left a very large trail behind at the scene.

Japanese: ‥‥いや。セイカクには “手”のアトですが‥‥
English: Or, to be exact... a handprint.

Japanese: ハッ！　ヒトのハナシは 聞いておくべきさ、カウボーイ！
English: Hmph! Listen real good, pardner.

Japanese: オレは、あの墓場の番人なんだよ。
English: Like I said, I'm the caretaker of that crypt.

Japanese: 月に１回ぐらいは、墓参り‥‥ 見回りをするさ。
English: I pay my respects... that is, make my rounds, about once a month.

Japanese: オレの指紋が残っているのは、 大自然のオキテ、だよ。
English: It's only natural my fingerprints would be in there.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、そうは行かない！ なにしろ‥‥
English: I only wish it were, Officer, but you see...

Japanese: その指紋は、 血にまみれていたのですからね！
English: your fingerprints were covered in blood!

Japanese: しょ、証人！ どういうことですか！
English: Witness! What's the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 殺人現場に、血染めの 指紋、とは‥‥！
English: Your bloodstained fingerprints were at the crime scene...!?

Japanese: 血のアトは、ふき取られていた。 ‥‥しかし！
English: The blood was wiped away. However!

Japanese: ルミノール試薬によって、 ハッキリ検出されたのです！
English: A luminol test clearly revealed this!

Japanese: さあ証人！　いかがですかッ！
English: Well, Officer Marshall?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... どうやら‥‥自分のアタマで 考えるヤツは、いないようだよ。
English: It seems to me... there ain't a person in this room with a head on his shoulders.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! つまり、“セツメイできる” ‥‥そういうコトかな？
English: I take it you have an explanation then, Officer Marshall?

Japanese: その“血染めの指紋”について！
English: About the "bloodstained" fingerprints?

Japanese: それでは、つづけて 証言をおねがいします！
English: Very well, you may begin your testimony about your fingerprints,

Japanese: 現場に残された、あなたの “手のアト”について！
English: found at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ～血染めの手のアト～
English: -- Bloodstained Fingerprints --

Japanese: その保管庫にオレの指紋が 残っているのは、当然なのさ。
English: Like I said, it's only natural for my fingerprints to be in that evidence room.

Japanese: そのうちのひとつが、たまたま 血の手形と一致したんだろうね。
English: One of them just happened to be at the same place as the bloodstained handprint.

Japanese: ハンニンはグーゼン、オレの指紋の 上から、その保管庫に触ったのさ。
English: The murderer touched the locker where my fingerprint was by chance.

Japanese: “血痕”と“指紋”は別のモノ。 ‥‥知らなかったかい。
English: The bloodstain and the fingerprint are completely unrelated.

Japanese: そもそも、犯人は手袋をしていた。 ホラ。オレとは無関係だろう？
English: Or didn't you know the murderer was wearing gloves? See? I had nothing to do with it.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 スジはとおっているようですな。
English: Hmm... The witness's explanation appears valid,

Japanese: ‥‥いくらか、ギモンの余地は あるようですが。
English: although there's room for doubt.

Japanese: ギモンがあるからこそ、人生は 楽しい‥‥オレはそう思うさ。
English: Life wouldn't be fun without any doubt, pardner.

Japanese: とにかく、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: The defense may now cross- examine the witness.

Japanese: （‥‥罪門さんは、この事件に 関係しているはずだ）
English: (This guy's hiding something, I can feel it!)

Japanese: （今度こそ、それを ハッキリ立証する‥‥！）
English: (Now's my chance to prove it.)

Japanese: ～血染めの手のアト～
English: -- Bloodstained Fingerprints --

Japanese: その保管庫にオレの指紋が 残っているのは、当然なのさ。
English: Like I said, it's only natural for my fingerprints to be in that evidence room.

Japanese: そのうちのひとつが、たまたま 血の手形と一致したんだろうね。
English: One of them just happened to be at the same place as the bloodstained handprint.

Japanese: ハンニンはグーゼン、オレの指紋の 上から、その保管庫に触ったのさ。
English: The murderer touched the locker where my fingerprint was by chance.

Japanese: “血痕”と“指紋”は別のモノ。 ‥‥知らなかったかな。
English: The bloodstain and the fingerprint are completely unrelated.

Japanese: そもそも、犯人は手袋をしていた。 ホラ。オレとは無関係だろう？
English: Or didn't you know the murderer was wearing gloves?

Japanese: カメラの映像に、オレの姿でも 映っていればよかったのになあ‥‥
English: Too bad it wasn't me in that video, right, pardner?

Japanese: （のらりくらりと言い逃れされる のも、メンドウだ）
English: (I can't let him squirm out of this one.)

Japanese: （罪門さんと、この事件を 結びつける、決定的な“証拠”）
English: (I've got to find something,)

Japanese: （‥‥この尋問で、なんとか 探し出すんだ‥‥！）
English: (something decisive, to tie Officer Marshall to this crime!)

Japanese: 『月に一度、墓参りをするから』 ‥‥ですか？
English: That's because you... how did you put it... "pay your respects," once a month?

Japanese: それもあるが‥‥ジツはな。 オレの保管庫なのさ。そこは。
English: Yeah, that's right. That, and one more thing... That locker happens to be mine.

Japanese: え！　ど、どういうことですか？
English: What!? What do you mean?

Japanese: たいしたことじゃないよ。
English: I mean what I said.

Japanese: 捜査官だったころ、使っていた 保管庫を、今も使っているだけさ。
English: That's the locker I used when I was a detective... the locker I still use.

Japanese: まあ‥‥入ってるのは、ガラクタの ようなユメばかり、だけどね。
English: All that's in there now though is a heap of broken dreams.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: 自分の保管庫だ。指紋ぐらい、 たっぷりつけさせてほしいなァ。
English: It'd be strange if my prints WEREN'T all over that locker.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥登録された指紋の データが、そのままみたいだな）
English: (Apparently his fingerprint data was never changed.)

Japanese: （ご本人は、指紋のロックのコト、 知らずに使ってるみたいだけど）
English: (He must have been using the fingerprint lock without even knowing it.)

Japanese: 証拠品《罪門の指紋》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Marshall's Fingerprints updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥この “血染めの手のアト”は‥‥？
English: So then... What about the bloody handprint?

Japanese: オレとは無関係に決まってるさ。 カンタンなハナシだろう？
English: Wasn't mine. It's no mystery.

Japanese: ど。どういうコトですかな？
English: Please explain.

Japanese: オレの保管庫には、オレの指紋が うなるほど、ついている。
English: My locker is covered with my fingerprints.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: It just so happened...

Japanese: そんなグーゼン、あるハズがない！
English: The chances of that happening are a million to one!

Japanese: こんなグーゼン、ないハズがない！ ‥‥そうとも言えるだろうね。
English: On the contrary, one could argue just the opposite. The chances of that not

Japanese: １つだけハッキリしてるのは、 カウボーイ。
English: happening are a million to one!

Japanese: その指紋だけで、オレのクビに かかった懸賞金はいただけないさ。
English: Get one thing straight, pardner. You ain't gonna get no reward for me with a mere fingerprint.

Japanese: なぜなら‥‥
English: You wanna know why?

Japanese: べ、ベツのモノ‥‥？
English: Unrelated?

Japanese: ‥‥たとえるならば、そう。
English: They're as different as night and day.

Japanese: オモチャ“つき”お子様ランチと、 オモチャ“入り”お子様ランチ‥‥
English: Kinda like "cereal" an' "serial."

Japanese: それぐらい、ちがうのさ。
English: One's got to do with breakfast while the other's a type of murder.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥似たようでいて、 まったくイミがちがいます！
English: He's right... although seemingly alike, they're totally different.

Japanese: （それにしても、テキトーな たとえバナシだな‥‥）
English: (I don't see what homonyms have to do with this...)

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして、そんなコトを 知っているんですか！
English: How do you know that!?

Japanese: これでも、警察の関係者だぜ。 報告書ぐらい、読んでるさ。
English: I may be a loner, but I still do my job. I keep up on the reports.

Japanese: ‥‥現場に、１つ。犯人が 残したと思われる血痕があった。
English: There was a bloodstain at the scene, thought to be left by the murderer.

Japanese: ああ‥‥‥イトノコ刑事の 保管庫に残っていたヤツですね。
English: That's right, it was found on Detective Gumshoe's locker.

Japanese: その“手のアト”からは‥‥ 指紋が検出されなかったのだ。
English: However, no fingerprints were detected on that handprint.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ぼくたちも やってみたっけ）
English: (Oh yeah, I think we tried that too.)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。そうなると、 その手袋をつけた犯人は‥‥
English: Hmm...So that would mean... the murderer, wearing gloves,

Japanese: やはり、罪門巡査の指紋の上に、 グーゼン手形を残したワケですか。
English: happened to place his hand on top of Officer Marshall's fingerprint.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えざるを 得ないだろうねえ。
English: That's the only logical conclusion.

Japanese: ‥‥どういう意味ですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: オレを、この事件と結びつけたい ‥‥そうなんだろ？　カウボーイ。
English: You want to tie me to this crime, isn't that right, pardner?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥ ... それならば‥‥直接的な証拠は このカメラの映像しかねえぜ。
English: If so, that video is the only direct evidence you have.

Japanese: し、しかし！　この映像には、 “死角”が多すぎます！
English: But that video is next to useless! It's full of blind spots!

Japanese: しかく‥‥？
English: Blind spots?

Japanese: “見えていない”部分です！
English: Places you can't see!

Japanese: カメラはクビを振ってるし、 床は映っていないし‥‥
English: The camera's panning back and forth, the floor isn't shown...

Japanese: カメラの位置を知っていれば、 姿を映さずに部屋を脱出できます！
English: If someone was familiar with the camera's position, he could leave the room without being caught on tape!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人のグチを 聞いているヒマはない‥‥
English: We don't have time for your speculations, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: どうなのですか？　弁護人。 防犯カメラの映像には‥‥
English: Well, Mr. Wright. If you can show us evidence in this video that indicates

Japanese: 罪門　恭介の“痕跡”が 映っているのですかな？
English: Officer Marshall was present, please do so now.

Japanese: （これは‥‥罪門さんの“ワナ” かもしれない‥‥）
English: (I might be walking right into Officer Marshall's trap.)

Japanese: （もう少し、キチンと情報を 集めてみようかな）
English: (I'd better try and find out a little more information.)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥証言をつづけてもらう ほうが、よさそうだ。
English: It seems we should be moving back to the testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: それでは、証人。もう一度、 最初からおねがいします。
English: Officer Marshall, can you please give your testimony again?

Japanese: ‥‥いいでしょう。
English: Very well.

Japanese: あなたの“ミス”を 教えてあげましょう。
English: Allow me to point out your mistake, Officer Marshall!

Japanese: もしくは‥‥これからキサマが 犯すことになる“ミス”か。
English: Tread carefully, Mr. Wright, or you might wind up being the one making the mistake.

Japanese: それでは‥‥映像の 検証に移りたいと思います！
English: Now then, let's have another look at the video.

Japanese: この映像に残された‥‥ 証人・罪門　恭介の“痕跡”とは！
English: Show us this incriminating evidence of the witness... Officer Jake Marshall!

Japanese: さあ、どうでしょうか！ この“痕跡”はッ！
English: There! How's that for evidence!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... モウシワケない、裁判長。 こんなオトコなのだ。
English: Please allow me to apologize for my colleague, Your Honor. He gets carried away sometimes.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、しかたないですな。 今に始まったコトでなし。
English: Yes, well, this certainly isn't a first.

Japanese: ユルしてやってはもらえまいか。 ワルいオトコではないのだ。
English: Will you forgive him too, Officer Marshall? He's not a bad man, just a bit... disillusioned.

Japanese: オーライ。オレだって、 血もナミダもないワケじゃないよ。
English: Of course. I'm not one to gun down unarmed boys.

Japanese: （御剣にベンゴされちまった。 “ハズレ”ってことか‥‥）
English: (Oh great, now Edgeworth is defending me. Guess that means I missed the mark.)

Japanese: それでは、もう一度！ 尋問に戻るとしましょう。
English: Very well, let's get back to the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、アンタも わかっただろ？　カウボーイ‥‥
English: Are you starting to get the picture, pardner?

Japanese: な、なんですか‥‥？
English: The picture...?

Japanese: こんな血判状‥‥荒野じゃ、 ハゲタカのエサにもならない。
English: This seal of blood... in the desert, it's just food for the buzzards.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ! ただ１つ。たしかな現実は ‥‥コイツさ。
English: There's only one reality, and that's this.

Japanese: （防犯カメラの映像、か‥‥）
English: (The security tape...)

Japanese: 映像に、オレの足跡がないかぎり ‥‥オレは認めねえさ。
English: So long as my trail isn't in there... you can't say otherwise.

Japanese: ‥‥ハラのさぐり合いは イミがないようです。
English: This isn't getting us anywhere, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 弁護人。よく考えて 尋問をするように。
English: Please consider carefully where you're going with this cross-examination.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください！
English: Now then, continue your testimony, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: ぼくたちの目を、防犯カメラの 映像に向けてしまったのは‥‥
English: Bringing our attention back to the security camera...

Japanese: どうやら、シッパイでしたね。 ‥‥罪門さん。
English: is a mistake I'm afraid you'll soon not forget, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... テキサスは、日が短い。だから、 オトコたちの気も短い‥‥
English: The days are short in Texas... and so are our tempers.

Japanese: 言いたいコト、２５文字以内で まとめてくれないかな。
English: Could you sum up what you have to say in eight words or less?

Japanese: ‥‥いいでしょう。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 《この映像に、あなたの姿が ハッキリ映っているのです！》
English: You can clearly be seen in this video!

Japanese: ピッタリ２５文字、か‥‥ 悪くないぜ、なかなか。
English: Exactly eight words... Not bad, pardner.

Japanese: モンダイは‥‥ 映像に映った“保管庫”です。
English: The key... lies in a certain locker shown in the video.

Japanese: 白い布がハミ出ている‥‥これは たしか、証人の保管庫でしたな。
English: That locker with the white cloth sticking out... that was the witness's, I believe...

Japanese: それでは‥‥この映像を、少し 戻して見てみましょう。
English: Now then, let's rewind the video a bit.

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ああああッ！ 白い布が‥‥消えています！
English: Oh! The white cloth... it's gone!

Japanese: いったいコレは！ どういうことなのですかッ！
English: What's the meaning of this, Officer Marshall!?

Japanese: 事件が起こった瞬間‥‥ 白い布はまだ、ありませんでした。
English: When the crime took place... The white cloth wasn't there.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、コツゼンと現れた。 そのコタエは、１つです。
English: Then... it suddenly appeared! There's only one explanation:

Japanese: 罪門さん！　あなたは事件当時、 証拠保管室にいたのです！
English: Officer Marshall! You were in the evidence room at the time of the crime!

Japanese: そして、カメラに映らない タイミングで、保管庫を開けた！
English: What's more, you opened your locker when the camera was turned away!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ たしかに、そうとしか考えられ
English: Order! Order! It would seem that's the only-

Japanese: 待てッ！　悪いが、カウボーイ‥‥ それは認められないよ。
English: Hold your horses! Sorry, pardner... but you got the wrong man.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... オレの保管庫が開けられた‥‥ だから、なんだと言うのさ？
English: So what if my locker was opened?

Japanese: そいつを開けたのは、 オレとはかぎらないだろうよ！
English: That doesn't mean I'm the one who opened it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ハンニンは、何かをかくす 必要があった。
English: The murderer needed to hide something, so he opened a locker and stuck it in.

Japanese: それで、たまたま開けたのが オレの保管庫だったのさ！
English: It's not my fault he happened to choose mine!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... なんだ、アンタたち。 おたずねモノを見る、その目は‥‥
English: Why's everyone staring at me like I'm a wanted man?

Japanese: （罪門さん‥‥ トボけているワケじゃない！）
English: (This guy isn't just playing dumb...)

Japanese: （ホントにわかってない みたいだな‥‥どうやら）
English: (He really doesn't know!)

Japanese: たいへんキノドクですが‥‥
English: Uh, I hate to rain on your parade,

Japanese: この保管庫を開けられたのは、 あなただけなんですよ。
English: but you're the only person who can open that particular locker.

Japanese: ば、バカな！ じゃあ‥‥じゃあ、見せてみろ！
English: Oh yeah? I call your bluff.

Japanese: あの保管庫を、オレが開けた ‥‥そんな証拠があるのかッ！
English: You say I opened that locker... Now prove it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... やっぱりハッタリばっかりだったり するようだぜ‥‥カウボーイ！
English: You can talk the talk alright, but you got a long ways to go before you can walk the walk!

Japanese: さすがに、この私でも それはあり得ませんぞ、弁護人ッ！
English: Mr. Wright... I think even I could prove this one.

Japanese: す‥‥スミマセン‥‥
English: S-sorry, Your Honor...

Japanese: （アレが罪門さんの 保管庫だとしたら‥‥）
English: (If that's Officer Marshall's locker...)

Japanese: （罪門さんしか、開けられない。 わかってるハズじゃないか！）
English: (Then only he can open it. I should know this!)

Japanese: さあ‥‥リボルバーの弾丸は ５発までさ。知ってるかい？
English: Come on, pardner. A revolver only holds five bullets. The question is, how many you got left?

Japanese: し、《指紋》のセンサー‥‥だと？
English: A... "fingerprint" sensor?

Japanese: 先ほども、少しハナシが 出ましたが‥‥
English: We talked about this earlier today.

Japanese: 保管庫は、担当捜査官しか 開けることができないのです。
English: The lockers can only be opened by the detectives they belong to.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥バカなッ！
English: W-what kinda crazy talk is this!?

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥ イトノコ刑事も言っていた）
English: (Well, Detective Gumshoe did mention something about this...)

Japanese: とにかく、パッと見た感じが あまりに自然なシカケなので‥‥
English: In any case, the locks aren't that obvious.

Japanese: 捜査官の中には、指紋のロックを 知らない連中もいるみたいッスね。
English: There's even some people in the force that don't know about the fingerprint locks!

Japanese: さあ、罪門さん！
English: So, sheriff!

Japanese: 反論があるのならば！ ２５文字以内でおねがいします！
English: What do you have to say... in eight words or less?

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろうさ。
English: I only got one word for you, pardner.

Japanese: 《ちくしょおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおお！》
English: NOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 証人！　これは、いったいッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! Witness! Explain yourself!

Japanese: トンだ、お笑いグサさ。 モト捜査官が聞いてあきれるぜ‥‥
English: If this is a joke, it's the worst I've ever heard...

Japanese: さあ‥‥ 聞かせていただきましょうか。
English: I assure you this is no joke, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、 あの保管室で、何をしていたか！
English: Now then, please tell us what you were doing in the evidence room at the time of the crime?
Olé! Please answer the question!

Japanese: （闘牛士だぞ、それ）
English: (What is he now, a bullfighter?)

Japanese: ザンネンですが、罪門さん。
English: That's alright, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: ここまで来れば、コタエを 聞かなくても、想像はつきます。
English: I believe we can figure the rest out from here.

Japanese: そうなのですか！
English: We can?

Japanese: 上面図を見てください。
English: Have a look at these floor plans.

Japanese: あの証拠保管室に、 身をかくせる場所はありません。
English: There is no place for someone to hide in the evidence room.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥原灰巡査は、 罪門さんの姿を見ていない。
English: Yet, Officer Meekins didn't see Officer Marshall.

Japanese: それでは、いったい‥‥ 証人は、どこにいたのでしょう？
English: If that's so, then... where was the witness?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人には 考えがあるようだ。
English: It seems Mr. Wright has an answer.

Japanese: （そう‥‥ コタエは、１つしかない！）
English: (That's right. The only possible conclusion!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人の考えを うかがいましょう！
English: Well then? Let's hear it.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき‥‥ 罪門巡査は、どこにいたのか？
English: Where was Officer Marshall at the time of the crime?

Japanese: 罪門さんは、このポイントに つっ立っていたのです！
English: Office Marshall was standing right here!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: その場所に立っていて、原灰巡査が 見落とした、というコトは‥‥
English: So Officer Meekins didn't notice him standing there...

Japanese: 罪門巡査、そうとう カゲがウスいようですな。
English: That's almost as credible as Meekins's warp theory.

Japanese: いくらウスいカゲでも、 そこのカウボーイほどじゃないさ。
English: Your chamber's empty, pardner. Better reload.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥立場がないな。 ここまで言われると‥‥）
English: (Now they're ganging up on me!)

Japanese: 罪門巡査の“立ち場”‥‥ もう一度、考えてもらいましょう。
English: Perhaps... you should think a little more about where Officer Marshall was.

Japanese: （現場で、ハラバイ巡査は 罪門さんを“見ている”ハズだ）
English: (Officer Meekins should have seen him in the evidence room.)

Japanese: （と、なると。罪門さんが 立っていた場所は‥‥）
English: (That means the only place he could have been would be...)

Japanese: 罪門さんは、このポイントに 立っていたのです！
English: Officer Marshall was standing right here!

Japanese: そ、そこは‥‥
English: There? But that's...

Japanese: 被害者の、多田敷捜査官が いた場所ではないですか！
English: That's where the victim, Detective Goodman, was!

Japanese: ‥‥正しくは、 “多田敷捜査官”ではなかった。
English: Correct... unless the man wasn't Detective Goodman.

Japanese: そう！　カメラに映っている “被害者”は‥‥罪門さん！
English: I believe the "victim" in the video is... Officer Marshall!

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官のカッコウをした、 あなただったのです！
English: It was you, dressed up like Detective Goodman!

Japanese: バカなッ！　原灰巡査は、 現場で多田敷捜査官を見ている！
English: But that's preposterous! Officer Meekins witnessed the detective at the crime scene!

Japanese: カオを見れば、 気づかないハズがないッ！
English: Once he saw the man's face, he'd know for sure!

Japanese: しかし、ハラバイ巡査は 多田敷捜査官を知らなかった。
English: May I point out though, that Officer Meekins did not know Detective Goodman.

Japanese: ‥‥それに彼は、 こんな証言をしています！
English: He also testified about the man's reaction when confronted.

Japanese: 保管室に入った本官は、ＩＤカード の提示を求めたであります！
English: When I entered the evidence room, I asked him to show his card, sir!

Japanese: それで‥‥ＩＤの提示を求められた 多田敷捜査官は？　どうしました？
English: Yes? And how did Detective Goodman respond?

Japanese: なんとッ！　いきなり、本官に ナイフを向けたであります！
English: He suddenly pulled a knife on me!

Japanese: ‥‥ずっとフシギでした。
English: Something about the officer's story puzzled me.

Japanese: ＩＤカードを持っていたのなら、 なぜ、見せなかったのか？
English: If the man had his ID card, why didn't he just show it?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥それを使って 保管室に入ったわけですからな。
English: Yes, he would have needed it to enter the evidence room, so he must have been carrying it.

Japanese: コタエは、シンプルです。 『見せることができなかった』
English: The answer is simple. He couldn't show it.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ! ＩＤカードには、 写真が入っています。
English: As you can see, Detective Goodman's picture is on his ID card.
Oh, I get it.

Japanese: これでは、ＩＤカードを 見せた瞬間に、バレてしまいます！
English: If he showed that, his cover would have been blown!

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官のカッコウをした “別人”であるコトが‥‥
English: Officer Meekins would have realized the man wasn't Detective Goodman.

Japanese: いかがですか、罪門さん！
English: Do you have anything to say to this, Officer Marshall?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... カウボーイにしておくには もったいない想像力‥‥だよ。
English: You've got quite an imagination, pardner.

Japanese: だがね。そういうのをオレたちは “状況証拠”って呼ぶらしいさ。
English: We got a term for that. It's called "circumstantial evidence."

Japanese: ‥‥じょうきょうしょうこ？ （まだ、認めないか‥‥）
English: Circumstantial evidence...? (He's still denying it!)

Japanese: ザンネンだが‥‥捜査官を 自白させるには、ツメが甘いさ。
English: You're gonna have to do better than that to break a detective.

Japanese: このオレが、被害者に変装していた ‥‥それを立証する証拠がないと。
English: Unless you have hard evidence proving I dressed up as the victim...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あなたの、その非協力的なタイド ‥‥どうかと思いますぞ。
English: I can't say I particularly care for your uncooperative disposition...

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、アンタのヒゲが 気に入らないんだろうさ。
English: I can't say I care for your beard, but you don't see me complainin'.

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人！ 何か、証拠はあるのですか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Do you have any evidence? Any evidence proving beyond a shadow of a

Japanese: この罪門さんが、ジッサイに 被害者に変装していた証拠ですッ！
English: doubt that Officer Marshall dressed up as the victim?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ （ムリだ！　そんな証拠は‥‥）
English: Well... (Who am I kidding? I don't have anything like that...)

Japanese: ‥‥そうそう。追いつめられた インパラの目‥‥悪くないぜ。
English: I can see the fear in your eyes, pardner.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥荒野にホネを さらすのは、アンタのほうさ。
English: Seems you're the one who couldn't take the desert heat!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！ （バカな‥‥）
English: Ack! (This can't be happening!)

Japanese: （どう考えてもアキラカなのに！ なにか、手はないのか‥‥？）
English: (It's so obvious he's the one! What can I do...?)
Hmph.

Japanese: ここへ来て‥‥経験不足が カオを出したようだ。
English: It looks like your lack of experience has finally been exposed.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! 私も、あるヒトに よく言われたものだ。
English: I'll pass onto you what someone told me when I was just starting out.

Japanese: 追いつめられたときこそ‥‥ キホンに立ち返れ、と。
English: When you've run into a wall with no place to go, return to the basics.

Japanese: き、キホン‥‥
English: The basics...

Japanese: （ぼくのキホンは‥‥ 千尋さんの、あのコトバだ！）
English: (For me, that would be what Mia used to tell me!)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 発想を逆転させましょう！
English: Nick! Try thinking outside of the box!

Japanese: （“罪門さんが変装していた” その証拠を探すのではなく‥‥）
English: (I shouldn't look for proof that Officer Marshall was in disguise...)

Japanese: （変装したことによって “発生した”証拠を探すんだ！）
English: (but rather I should look for evidence that came about because he was in disguise!)

Japanese: ‥‥そもそも。なぜ、 この保管庫は開かれたのだろうか？
English: Why do you think this locker was opened in the first place?

Japanese: どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: フツウに考えれば、わざわざ 犯行時に保管庫を開く必要はない。
English: There's no reason for Officer Marshall to open his locker at the time of the crime.

Japanese: なのに、こんな大きな手がかりを 残した。‥‥と、いうことは‥‥
English: Yet he did, despite the chance it might be discovered later as it has been.

Japanese: （保管庫を開けたのは、 “計算外”のコトだった‥‥！）
English: (Which means he didn't originally plan to open his locker...!)

Japanese: 弁護人の主張によれば‥‥
English: According to the defense's argument,

Japanese: 罪門　恭介は、事件当時。 多田敷捜査官に変装していた。
English: Officer Jake Marshall dressed up as Detective Goodman at the time of the crime.

Japanese: そして、“事件”のあと‥‥
English: Then, after the crime was "committed,"

Japanese: なぜか、自分の証拠保管庫を 開けています。
English: he opened his own locker for some unknown reason.

Japanese: 保管庫から“白い何か”が ハミ出ている以上‥‥
English: The fact that a white "cloth" is sticking out of the locker seems to indicate that

Japanese: それをしまうため、保管庫を開いた ‥‥そう考えるべき、だろう。
English: he opened it in order to put the cloth inside.

Japanese: いったい‥‥その“白い何か”の 正体は、なんだったのですかな？
English: So... just what exactly is this piece of cloth?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥
English: Perhaps...

Japanese: すべてのコタエは、 カメラがとらえているハズです！
English: Perhaps the video is the key to all our unanswered questions.

Japanese: （“証拠”はない。何かあると すれば、この映像だけだ‥‥！）
English: (I don't have any evidence, so this video's my only shot!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... それでは‥‥もう一度。 映像の検証に入ります。
English: Very well. Let's take yet another look at the security tape.

Japanese: 犯行後、証人は“白い何か”を しまうため、保管庫を開きました。
English: After commiting the crime, the witness opened the locker to put away the white cloth.

Japanese: 証人が、保管庫を開けざるを得なく なった“理由”を示してください！
English: Please show us why the witness had to open his locker!

Japanese: いかがでしょうか、みなさん！
English: Well, what do you have to say to that!?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥わかったよ。
English: Alright, pardner.

Japanese: あの保管庫を開けざるを得なかった “理由”‥‥が。
English: You really want to know the reason I had to open my locker?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Why?

Japanese: カウボーイ！　あんたを丸めて、 詰めこむためさ！
English: So I could stuff you in there!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh!?

Japanese: 一度、《申し送り》されてみた ほうがいいと思うね、オレは。
English: I'm sure the world would be a better place with you sent off to the boneyard.

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥未解決の事件は 保管庫に入れることはできない。
English: Unfortunately unsolved cases can't be stored in the evidence room.

Japanese: （《未解決》あつかい されてしまった‥‥）
English: (Now I'm an "unsolved case"?)

Japanese: （‥‥保管室で、予想できなかった アクシデントが発生した‥‥）
English: (Something went wrong in that evidence room...)

Japanese: （だから罪門さんは、自分の 保管庫を開けざるを得なかった）
English: (That's why Marshall had to open his locker.)

Japanese: （その、“アクシデント”‥‥ それこそが、コタエだ！）
English: (The accident itself is the reason!)

Japanese: （‥‥もう一度！ 考えなおしてみよう‥‥）
English: (I'd better have another crack at this...)

Japanese: あなたは、何かの理由があって 多田敷捜査官のカッコウをして‥‥
English: For some reason, you disguised yourself as Detective Goodman,

Japanese: 証拠保管室に侵入しました。 その理由はまだ、わかりません。
English: and entered the evidence room. I don't know what that reason was... yet.

Japanese: “まだ”ねえ‥‥
English: "Yet"?

Japanese: しかし‥‥そこで、 予想外のコトが起こったのです。
English: However, something unexpected happened.

Japanese: 原灰巡査の登場です。
English: Officer Meekins barged in on you.

Japanese: ＩＤカードの提示を求められて、 あなたはナイフで脅そうとした。
English: When asked to show your ID card, you pulled a knife on him.

Japanese: ‥‥ところが！
English: However!

Japanese: 原灰巡査は、パニックになって しまった。そして‥‥
English: Officer Meekins panicked,

Japanese: あなたが着ていた白いコートは、 血で汚れてしまったのです！
English: and the white coat you were wearing was soiled with blood!

Japanese: 血のついた、白いコート‥‥
English: A bloody white coat...

Japanese: そのまま出ていくワケには 行かない。そこで、あなたは‥‥
English: You couldn't just walk out like that,

Japanese: 自らの保管庫に、そのコートを しまいこんだのです。
English: so you hid the coat in your locker.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... やるじゃねえか、カウボーイ‥‥
English: Not bad, huh "pardner"?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。
English: Now then, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: 聞かせていただきましょうか。 “本当のコト”を‥‥
English: Are you ready to tell us the truth?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥アンタたちを 見くびっていたようだね。
English: Looks like I underestimated y'all.

Japanese: なァ、御剣検事さんよォ。
English: I hope you're happy now, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ２年前。 アンタが、今日と同じように‥‥
English: Two years ago...

Japanese: 正々堂々と、あの事件を 扱ってくれていればなァ。
English: if you were only half as persistent then as you are today,

Japanese: きっと、こんなコトには ならなかっただろうに‥‥
English: we all wouldn't have to be here, now would we?

Japanese: 罪門さん、聞かせてください！ あなたがやったことを‥‥すべて。
English: Officer Marshall! Tell the court what you did... all of it.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら。
English: Alright.

Japanese: そうするときが 来たみたいだね。
English: It seems the time has come.

Japanese: ～罪門の自供～
English: -- Marshall's Confession --

Japanese: あの日しかなかった。オレは、 アイツを死なせたくなかったのさ。
English: I had to do it that day. I couldn't just stand by and let it die.

Japanese: ＩＤを盗んで、ヤツに変装して‥‥ 証拠品を運びだすつもりだった。
English: I stole the detective's ID and dressed like him. I planned to take out the evidence.

Japanese: 原灰のジャマは、予想外だったさ。 なんとか殴ってキゼツさせて‥‥
English: I wasn't expecting Officer Meekins. I knocked him out...

Japanese: 逃げ出したよ。カメラに 映らないエリアは調べてあったし。
English: and managed to escape. I knew which areas wouldn't be caught on the camera.

Japanese: ５時１５分‥‥あのとき、 保管室で殺人事件はなかったのさ。
English: There wasn't any murder in the evidence room at 5:15.

Japanese: やはり、あの“被害者”は、 あなただったのですか‥‥
English: So the supposed "victim" was really you...

Japanese: しかし‥‥妙だな。
English: But there's one thing I still don't understand.

Japanese: 現場である保管室の床には、 かなりの血痕が残っている。
English: Large quantities of blood traces were found on the floor of the evidence room.

Japanese: 殺人事件がなかったとしたら、 これらは、いったい‥‥
English: If no one was murdered, then how could that be?

Japanese: 原灰のヤツ、自分で勝手に 手をケガしたからね。
English: Officer Meekins managed to cut his own hand.

Japanese: 血の気の多いワカモノだった、 ってコトじゃないかな。
English: My guess is he's the donor.

Japanese: （そうは見えなかったけど）
English: (It looked like too much blood for that...)

Japanese: ～罪門の自供～
English: -- Marshall's Confession --

Japanese: あの日しかなかった。オレは、 アイツを死なせたくなかったのさ。
English: I had to do it that day. I couldn't just stand by and let it die.

Japanese: ＩＤを盗んで、ヤツに変装して‥‥ 証拠品を運びだすつもりだった。
English: I stole the detective's ID and dressed like him. I planned to take out the evidence.

Japanese: 原灰のジャマは、予想外だったよ。 なんとか殴ってキゼツさせて‥‥
English: I wasn't expecting Officer Meekins. I knocked him out...

Japanese: 逃げ出したよ。カメラに 映らないエリアは調べてあったし。
English: and managed to escape. I knew which areas wouldn't be caught on the camera.

Japanese: ５時１５分‥‥あのとき、 保管室で殺人事件はなかったのさ。
English: There wasn't any murder in the evidence room at 5:15.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》を、忘れない‥‥ その理由、アンタにわかるかな？
English: I can't just forget the SL-9 Incident... You know why?

Japanese: （いつかは、ここにつながると 思っていた‥‥）
English: (I had a feeling we'd wind up here sooner or later...)

Japanese: （今回の関係者が全員、なんらかの カタチで登場する《事件》‥‥）
English: (Everyone involved here is related in some way to that case...

Japanese: （もう一度、資料をチェックした ほうがいいかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better take another look at the files.)

Japanese: “アイツ”というのは、 やっぱり‥‥
English: When you say "it," you mean...

Japanese: 聞くまでもないだろ？ カウボーイ‥‥
English: Do you even have to ask, pardner?

Japanese: （《ＳＬ９号事件》のコトか）
English: (The SL-9 Incident...)

Japanese: 解決されてから、２年‥‥
English: Two years have passed since that case was closed.

Japanese: あの日の《申し送り》で、事件は 完全に“終わる”ハズだった。
English: It was going to completely end with the transferal that day.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは、認めねえさ。 あの事件は、まだ終わっていない！
English: Not if I have anything to do with it. That incident's not over!

Japanese: でも‥‥保管室に忍び込んで、 どうするつもりだったんですか？
English: But what did you hope to accomplish by sneaking into the evidence room?

Japanese: ‥‥解決した事件の証拠品は、 担当捜査官しか調べられない。
English: When a case is closed, only the detective who was in charge of it can look through

Japanese: どんな手を使っても‥‥ この目でもう一度。調べたかった。
English: the evidence. I wanted to have a look at it myself one more time... no matter what the cost.

Japanese: あの事件は‥‥そう。 オレの事件、なのだからね。
English: I don't care what anyone says, pardner. That case is mine.

Japanese: （罪門さんは、あの事件の 捜査のセキニンシャではない）
English: (But Officer Marshall wasn't in charge of that investigation.)

Japanese: （なのに、なぜ。そこまで こだわるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Why does he care so much about it?)

Japanese: あの日は、最後のチャンスだった。 だから、オレは‥‥
English: That day was my last chance. That's why I...

Japanese: なぜ、多田敷さんに 変装なんかしたんですか？
English: Why did you disguise yourself as Detective Goodman?

Japanese: ‥‥あくまで、多田敷が証拠品を 《申し送り》したコトにしないと。
English: If I didn't make it look like Goodman was carrying out the evidence transferal...

Japanese: せっかく証拠品を盗み出したのに、 タイホされちまったら意味がない。
English: I'd be arrested for stealing evidence, which wouldn't get me anywhere.

Japanese: モチロン‥‥防犯カメラを 意識してのコトだろう。
English: So you did it to fool the security camera.

Japanese: 被害者のＩＤカードも やはり、あなたが‥‥？
English: And the detective's ID card?

Japanese: 事件当日の、朝。 盗みだしておいたよ。
English: I stole that the morning of the incident.

Japanese: （そういえば、遺失物届が 提出されていたな‥‥）
English: (So that really was why Goodman started filling out that lost item report.)

Japanese: ヤツのＩＤカードなら、 シッカリ返しておいたぜ。
English: I returned his ID card.

Japanese: 検事局の地下駐車場の‥‥ 床の上に、な。
English: I left it on the floor in the Prosecutor's Office parking lot.

Japanese: （ぼくが見つけた、あのＩＤ‥‥ 罪門さんが置いたのか！）
English: (The ID card I found was left there by Officer Marshall!)

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョクのところ。
English: So essentially...

Japanese: キミが優秀でないおかげで 計画が成功した、というワケか。
English: You managed to succeed despite your lack of foresight.

Japanese: ‥‥どういう意味だ？　ボウヤ。
English: What do you mean... pardner?

Japanese: 《指紋》のロックだ。モチロン‥‥
English: I mean the fingerprint- activated lock, of course.

Japanese: いくら、うまく変装をしても、 指紋を変えるコトはできない。
English: No matter how well you disguise yourself, you can't change your fingerprints.

Japanese: 本来ならば。あの保管庫は 開かなかったハズなのだ。
English: Normally, that locker shouldn't have opened.

Japanese: （グーゼン《手袋》が挟まっていた から、保管庫は開いた‥‥）
English: (So it opened because a rubber glove was stuck in the door by chance...?)

Japanese: （つまり‥‥罪門さんより前に、 多田敷さんが開けていた‥‥！）
English: (Then Detective Goodman must have opened the locker before Officer Marshall!)

Japanese: ナイフをつきつけて 追い払おうとしたんですよね？
English: You pulled a knife on Officer Meekins and tried to drive him off?

Japanese: ‥‥オレとしたことが、 少し動転しちまったさ。
English: Let's just say I was a little surprised.

Japanese: 予定では、警備室を空けるのは、 ほんの５分ぐらいのハズだった。
English: I only planned on being in the evidence room for no more than five minutes.

Japanese: まさか、その短いあいだに、 ダレか来るとは思わなかったさ。
English: I didn't think anyone would actually come in during that short time.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なかなか得がたい 人材と言えますな、その巡査。
English: Officer Meekins... certainly is a one-in-a- million type of person.

Japanese: 《刑事》と《不審者》をカンちがい して、ＩＤの提示を求めるとは‥‥
English: Mistaking a detective for an intruder and demanding to be shown his ID...

Japanese: 来月の給与査定‥‥ 少し、考えてみるとしよう。
English: I'll have to think a little more about his raise this year...

Japanese: （すごい権力を持ってるよな、 アイツ‥‥）
English: (When did Edgeworth get so much influence...?)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥アイツが暴れるから、 少しキズつけてしまった。
English: Anyway, he threw himself at me, and I ended up cutting him slightly.

Japanese: すまなかったと思ってるぜ。 思いきり殴って、キゼツさせたし。
English: I'm sorry it had to turn out that way... with me knocking him out and everything.

Japanese: ちなみに‥‥ハラバイ巡査に向けた ナイフは、どこに？
English: By the way, what happened to your knife?

Japanese: ああ。コイツのコトかい？
English: Oh, you mean this one?
(I don't know what to say...)

Japanese: むむう‥‥それで、そのあと どうしたのですかな？
English: Hmm... So, what happened next?

Japanese: やはり‥‥事前に 調べてあったんですね？
English: So you did your research beforehand.

Japanese: 荒野の狩人は、用心深いのさ。 役に立つとは思わなかったよ。
English: Those who go into the desert unprepared don't live long, pardner.

Japanese: 録画されたカメラの映像は、 ６時間ごとに消去される。
English: I didn't think it would make a difference though. The security tape is erased every

Japanese: うまくいけば、なんの証拠も 残さずにすんだのになあ‥‥
English: six hours. If all had gone as planned, no footage would have been left.

Japanese: ところが‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: 原灰巡査の抵抗によって、 コートに血痕が付着したわけだ。
English: You bloodied your coat in your struggle with Officer Meekins.

Japanese: 警備室に戻ったとき、 ヒトがいたらオシマイだからね。
English: If someone was in the security room when I came out, the jig would have been up.

Japanese: 保管庫を開けて、念のために コートを脱いでおいたんだよ。
English: I opened my locker and stashed it in there.

Japanese: そのとき、ハラバイ巡査は‥‥？
English: What was Officer Meekins doing during that time?

Japanese: キモチよさそうに寝てたな。 ‥‥ハラバイで。
English: What else? He was sleeping like a baby.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、証人。 そのとき、保管室では‥‥？
English: So what you're saying is, on that day...

Japanese: しかし。現場に残った血痕は、 事件があったことを示しています！
English: But the blood found at the scene certainly indicates a crime took place!

Japanese: ‥‥しつこいカウボーイは きらわれるぜ、カウボーイ！
English: What are you, blind?

Japanese: カメラに映っていた“被害者”は、 このオレ‥‥こうして生きてるさ。
English: The "victim" shown on that tape is me, and I'm not dead yet, pardner.

Japanese: ‥‥それで。キミは、保管庫から 証拠品を盗み出したワケか？
English: So, you stole the evidence from the locker?

Japanese: それが‥‥ そうはいかなかったさ。
English: Actually... no, I didn't.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだろうか？
English: Why not?

Japanese: オレが保管庫を開けたとき‥‥ 証拠品はすでに、消えていたのさ。
English: When I opened the locker, the evidence was already gone.

Japanese: な、なんですと！ 御剣検事！　その証拠品は‥‥？
English: What!? Mr. Edgeworth. Where is that evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥今もなお、行方不明‥‥だ。
English: It's still missing, Your Honor.

Japanese: （多田敷さんの、 カラッポの保管庫‥‥）
English: (Detective Goodman's locker was already empty...)

Japanese: （証拠品は、盗まれていたのか！）
English: (Someone else stole the evidence!)

Japanese: でも‥‥その事件は、２年前。 すでに、解決してるんですよね？
English: But that case was solved two years ago, wasn't it?

Japanese: だからこそ、証拠品が保管庫に しまわれているワケですからな。
English: That's the reason the evidence was stored in the evidence room.

Japanese: 青影　丈（あおかげじょう）‥‥ 断罪されるべき犯罪者だった。
English: Joe Darke was convicted for those crimes.

Japanese: 判決は、当然だった。 ‥‥私には、そう断言できる。
English: One thing I can say for sure, he deserved his sentence.

Japanese: 覚えています。青影　丈‥‥ 連続殺人犯、でしたね。
English: I remember the Joe Darke case... It involved serial murders, didn't it?

Japanese: その結果にケチをつけるつもりは ないさ。‥‥しかし。
English: I don't intend to complain about how it turned out,

Japanese: ‥‥どうしても、 ナットクできないことがある。
English: but there's something that still bothers me.

Japanese: あの事件の裁判には、何かあった。 “語られていない”物語が‥‥
English: Something went down at that trial. Something no one will talk about.

Japanese: いったい、それは‥‥？
English: What happened?

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、わからない。 そいつを調べたかったのさ。
English: I don't know. That's what I'm trying to find out.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （彼がなぜ、そこまで あの事件にこだわるのか‥‥？）
English: (Why is he so concerned with that incident?)

Japanese: （その理由と考えられる“事実”。 ‥‥つきつけてみるか‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should present him with what I think his real reason is...)

Japanese: ‥‥罪門さん。 １つ、聞きたいことがあります。
English: Officer Marshall. May I ask you one thing?

Japanese: 聞くのは自由さ、カウボーイ。 ダレも止めやしないよ。
English: Fire away, pardner. It's a free country.

Japanese: そして、モチロン‥‥ 答えるのは、オレの自由さ。
English: Just remember, I'm also free to decide whether or not to answer.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、こんなコトを したんですか？
English: Why did you do this?

Japanese: 捜査官のＩＤを盗みだして、 ハラバイ巡査までキズつけて‥‥
English: Stealing a detective's ID, injuring a police officer...

Japanese: リッパな犯罪じゃないですか！
English: This is no small offense!

Japanese: しかも‥‥キミは巡査だ。 これはもう、重罪と言えるだろう。
English: Moreover, you're an officer yourself. This will have serious consequences.

Japanese: 来月の給与査定で片づく モンダイではない。
English: It can't just be forgiven with a simple cut in salary.

Japanese: （‥‥もともと、そういう モンダイじゃないけどな）
English: (Not that salary cuts are ever a valid solution...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ 言っただろう？　御剣のボウヤ。 ...
English: Like I said.

Japanese: あれは、アンタの事件じゃない。 この、オレの事件なんだ。
English: This isn't your case. This one is mine.

Japanese: ナットクの行くコタエを見つける ためなら‥‥なんだってするさ。
English: And I'll do anything it takes to get an answer I'm satisfied with.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: その、異常とも言える“熱意”。 証言に加えていただきましょう。
English: The witness has an unusual amount of zeal. Let's hear more.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。 さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！ 被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ‥‥罪門さん。 なんとなく、わかる気がします。
English: Officer Marshall. I think I understand.

Japanese: あなたが《ＳＬ９号事件》に こだわる理由が。
English: I think I know why you care so much about the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: きっと‥‥サボテンの精の イタズラだろうさ。
English: Sounds like you've been sipping too much cactus juice, pardner.

Japanese: ここに‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》の 資料があります。
English: I have the SL-9 Incident file here.

Japanese: この中に、“罪門”の名前を 見つけました。
English: The name "Marshall" is mentioned in here...

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ! 連続殺人事件の、 被害者のヒトリです。
English: in a list of murder victims.

Japanese: 《罪門　直斗》‥‥ あなたと関係のある人物ですね？
English: "Neil Marshall"... Are you related to this man?

Japanese: “ざいもん　なおと”‥‥
English: Neil Marshall...?

Japanese: ‥‥ボウヤは当然、 聞いたことがあるだろうさ。
English: Yeah, I'm sure you've heard the name.

Japanese: なんせ、２年前‥‥
English: Two years ago...

Japanese: アンタと同じ、くだらねえ賞を もらった、名検事さんだからな。
English: he received the same lousy prosecutor award you got.

Japanese: なんですと！　検事‥‥
English: What!? A prosecutor...?

Japanese: （“賞”って‥‥やっぱり、 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》か）
English: (He must be talking about the King of Prosecutors award.)

Japanese: 思い出した‥‥罪門　直斗検事。
English: Now I remember... Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: 私が担当した《ＳＬ９号事件》の 前任者だった。
English: He handled the SL-9 case before I did.

Japanese: そうだ。アイツが殺されて‥‥ アンタが担当することになった。
English: That's right. He was killed... and the case fell into your hands.
But what's his relation to you...?

Japanese: 罪門　直斗は‥‥オレの弟だ。
English: He was my brother.

Japanese: 当時の主席捜査官・巌徒　海慈と 組んで、捜査にあたっていた。
English: He was investigating the murders with Damon Gant, Chief Detective at the time.

Japanese: その下で動いていたのが、 オレたち捜査班だった。
English: The group of detectives I was part of worked under them.

Japanese: あの連続殺人犯を起訴するために、 みんな必死でね‥‥
English: We were desperate to prosecute the killer.

Japanese: 青影　丈‥‥‥
English: Joe Darke...

Japanese: 弟はヤツと格闘して、殺害された。 そして、その際‥‥
English: My brother fought Darke and was killed.

Japanese: 青影は初めて“証拠”を残した。 それがヤツの運のつき‥‥
English: That was the first time Darke left behind any evidence. That was all we needed.

Japanese: ついに起訴されて、判決を受けた。 ‥‥めでたし、めでたし。
English: He was arraigned and incarcerated. The case was finally closed...

Japanese: 公式記録には、そう書かれている。
English: at least, according to the public records.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、モンダイが？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 弟は、青影に殺されるような ヤツじゃない。
English: My brother couldn't have been killed by Joe Darke.

Japanese: 格闘の際の、ヤツの集中力‥‥ オレが、一番よく知っているさ。
English: I knew my brother better than anyone... No one could have beaten him in a fight.

Japanese: ‥‥たったそれだけのコトで、 今回のようなバカなコトを‥‥？
English: And that's it? That's your reason for your insane actions?

Japanese: 弟がイノチを落としたウラには、 ゼッタイに“何か”ある。
English: There's more to my brother's death than what the records say.

Japanese: アンタたちが、隠そうとしても！ ‥‥オレは知っているのさ。
English: No matter how much you try to hide it, you can't fool me.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... どうやら‥‥ これで、ハッキリしたようです。
English: Well... at least one thing's for certain.

Japanese: ‥‥事件当日。 警察局で、何があったか‥‥
English: Now we know what happened at the Police Department on the day of the crime.

Japanese: あの日は、《ＳＬ９号事件》の 再捜査権が失われる日だった。
English: That was the last day the SL-9 case could be reopened.

Japanese: ２年前の解決にナットクできない 罪門　恭介は、証拠の強奪を計画。
English: Not satisfied with its resolution, Officer Marshall planned to steal the evidence.

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官のＩＤを盗みだし、 彼になりすまして、証拠保管室へ。
English: Disguising himself as Detective Goodman, he entered the evidence room.

Japanese: そこで、原灰巡査に見とがめられた ため、キゼツさせて逃亡‥‥
English: Officer Meekins confronted him, so he rendered him unconscious and fled.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、これで ハッキリしたようだ。
English: Yes, this mystery has finally been cleared up.

Japanese: やはり、あの日。 警察局では、殺人事件はなかった！
English: No murder took place at the Police Department that day!

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ! グーゼンとは恐ろしい‥‥
English: The things that happen by chance never cease to amaze...

Japanese: 検事局で殺人があったのと、 まったく同じ時間‥‥
English: At exactly the same time as the murder at the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: 警察局にて“ニセモノ”の 格闘があったとは‥‥な。
English: this fake murder was going on at the Police Department.

Japanese: （“グーゼン”‥‥？ そんな、ツゴウのいい話が‥‥）
English: (Chance...? It's got to be more than just that...)

Japanese: 事件当日、警察局で殺人事件が なかった‥‥となれば。
English: So if no one was murdered at the Police Department on the day of the crime,

Japanese: 当然。検事局の地下駐車場の 事件こそ“事実”というコトです。
English: that means the murder in the Prosecutor's Office's parking lot was the real one.

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥やはり。 犯行が可能なのは１人きり、だ。
English: Which, in turn, means... only one person could have committed the crime:

Japanese: ‥‥宝月　巴主席検事。
English: Chief Prosecutor Lana Skye.

Japanese: し、しかし！　きのうの審理で、 結論は出なかったはず‥‥
English: B-but wait! A verdict wasn't reached in yesterday's trial!

Japanese: だから、今日！　こうして 警察局の事件を審理したのだ。
English: Which is why we examined the incident at the Police Department today.
But...!

Japanese: きのう、宝月　巴に判決が 下されなかった理由は、１つ。
English: There's only one reason the defendant was not convicted yesterday:

Japanese: 警察局で発生した“同時殺人”の ナゾが残されていたからだ。
English: there yet remained the mystery of the simultaneous murder at the Police Department.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ボウヤのほうが １枚、上手だったようだぜ‥‥
English: It seems to me... this boy's got the draw on you, pardner.

Japanese: 警察局の、すべてのナゾは解けた。 ムジュンするものは、もうない。
English: All the mysteries at the Police Department have been uncovered. No contradictions

Japanese: “殺人”は、検事局で発生した！ 可能だったのは、宝月　巴のみ。
English: remain. The murder took place at the Prosecutor's Office! The only suspect is Lana Skye.

Japanese: 目撃者・市ノ谷　響華の証言に、 マチガイはなかったのだ！
English: There were no errors in the testimony of the witness, Angel Starr!

Japanese: 何か反論があれば‥‥ ２．５文字以内でおねがいしよう。
English: If you have a response... make it one word or less.
ARRRGHHHH!!!

Japanese: 以上、だ。
English: I rest my case.

Japanese: どうやら、結論が出たようです。 ギモンの余地は、ありません。
English: It seems this trial has reached its conclusion. There's no room for doubt.

Japanese: 警察局の《同時》殺人事件‥‥ 私が出るまでもなかったようだ。
English: Well done, Mr. Wright. Thanks to you, I didn't need to waste my time...

Japanese: ご協力をカンシャしよう。 ‥‥弁護人。
English: disproving the alleged "murder" at the Police Department.

Japanese: （‥‥今日、ぼくが立証したことに マチガイはなかったハズだ‥‥）
English: (There's no doubt what I proved today is true...)

Japanese: （防犯カメラに残された映像は、 ニセモノだった！）
English: (The apparent murder on the security camera's tape really was fake!)

Japanese: （でも、それが‥‥）
English: (But I didn't realize...)

Japanese: （ともえさんの有罪を 立証してしまうなんて‥‥！）
English: (that would end up proving Lana guilty!)

Japanese: それでは！　宝月　巴に 判決を言いわたします！
English: Now then, the time for the verdict has arrived. This court finds the defendant...

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと 待ってください！
English: Y-your Honor! Wait!

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃん！
English: Ema!

Japanese: ベンゴ側には‥‥その。 カガク的に、イギがありますっ！
English: The defense has an objection. A scientific objection!
Right?

Japanese: “ね”と言われても‥‥
English: What do you mean, "right?"

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたですか？ この子のホゴシャは。
English: Are you this girl's guardian?

Japanese: ‥‥え！ は、はあ‥‥まあ、なんと
English: Your Honor! Oh, uh, in a sense...

Japanese: おねがいします、５分だけ！ ‥‥あたしの話、聞いてください！
English: Please, Your Honor. All I'm asking is for a minute of your time! Please hear me out!

Japanese: 御剣検事さん！　おねがい‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth, please...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ギモンを残すのは、 私の望むところではない。
English: I don't want to leave any loose ends.

Japanese: ‥‥３分、時間を与えよう。
English: You want a minute? I'll give you three.

Japanese: あたし‥‥ショックだったんです。 《ＳＬ９号事件》が、まさか‥‥
English: I... I was kind of in shock.

Japanese: あの《青影事件》だった、なんて。
English: I mean, finding out the SL-9 incident referred to the Joe Darke Killings!

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Now that she mentions it...)

Japanese: （資料の中に、宝月姉妹の名前も 出てきていたっけ）
English: (the names of both Skye sisters were in that file.)

Japanese: でも。それで‥‥あたし。 なんとなく、わかったんです。
English: But that's when I figured it out.

Japanese: 罪門さんが、あの日。 何をやろうとしていたのか‥‥
English: I mean, what Officer Marshall was trying to do that day...

Japanese: だから、罪門さんの指紋は 事件にカンケイない、って。
English: So I knew his fingerprint had nothing to do with the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥残るのは、コレ。 もう１つの“手のアト”ですよね。
English: That left only one thing... the other handprint!

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事の保管庫から 発見された血痕、か‥‥
English: You mean the traces of blood found on Detective Gumshoe's locker...

Japanese: しかし‥‥その手形からは、 指紋は検出されなかったのでは？
English: But no fingerprints were found on it, right?

Japanese: でも！　カガク的に 調べれば、手がかりがあるかも！
English: No, but I figured if I examined it scientifically... I'd be sure to find a clue!

Japanese: そう思って‥‥ひとっ走り、 調べなおしてきたんです！
English: So I ran over there and looked at it again!

Japanese: ‥‥！ そ、それで！　何かわかったの？ !
English: So did you find something?

Japanese: ええと‥‥‥いいえっ！
English: Um... No!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ザンネンですが。 あたしのチカラでは、どうにも。
English: Sorry. I guess I'm not much of a scientific investigator after all.
Um... Is that all?

Japanese: なんといっても、あたし。 かよわい女子高生ですから。
English: Please don't be mad. I'm just a high school student!

Japanese: （こっちだって、 かよわい弁護士なんだけど）
English: (And I'm just an attorney...)

Japanese: でも、でも！　もう、手がかりは この血のアトしかないんです！
English: But Mr. Wright! Those traces of blood are the only clue we have!

Japanese: ここに、何か“ムジュン”を 見つけないと‥‥
English: If we can't find something wrong with them...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、プロだし。 あとは、おねがいしますっ！
English: Please, Mr. Wright. You're a professional. If anyone can save Lana, it's you!

Japanese: え‥‥‥ ええええええええッ！
English: Me? Oh boy...

Japanese: ‥‥時間です。 コタエをうかがいましょう。
English: Time's up. Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 警察局の“事件”について‥‥
English: With regard to the incident at the Police Department...

Japanese: 何か、まだ“ギモン”が 残されているのですか？
English: Does any reasonable doubt remain?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護側は、 もう１つの血痕が気になるようだ。
English: It appears the defense is troubled by the other blood mark.

Japanese: 上面図で言えば‥‥このあたりに 付着していた“手のアト”だな。
English: Looking at the floor plans, a handprint was discovered around here.

Japanese: これに、何かモンダイでも？ ‥‥おじょうさん？
English: Is there a problem with this?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、ごめんなさい！ あたし、チカラになれなくて。
English: Mr. Wright! I'm sorry I can't be of more use...

Japanese: でも‥‥！
English: But still!

Japanese: この血痕に、何か“ムジュン”が ないと‥‥お姉ちゃんは！
English: If you can't find anything wrong with that blood mark, Lana will be...

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人！ これ以上は、待てませんぞ。
English: Please answer my question, Mr. Wright. We don't have all day.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ （よく、考えるんだ‥‥今こそ！）
English: Y-yes, Your Honor. (If ever I've needed to concentrate, it's now!)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事の保管庫に 残された、“手のアト”‥‥）
English: (What could be wrong with that handprint on Detective Gumshoe's locker?)

Japanese: （何か‥‥何か、ないのか！ “ムジュン”は‥‥）
English: (Could there be something I'm missing...?)

Japanese: （正直なトコロ‥‥ まだ、ハッキリしない！）
English: (I've got to be honest... I don't see anything wrong with it!)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥）
English: (Still...)

Japanese: （ここでぼくが立ち止まれば、 今度こそ、ともえさんは‥‥！）
English: (If I give up now, Lana's going to be convicted for sure...)

Japanese: 現場に残された、 この“手のアト”は‥‥
English: This handprint left at the crime scene...

Japanese: おそらく、アキラカに ムジュンしています！
English: clearly shows a contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥アキラカに目が 泳いでいるようだな‥‥弁護士。
English: The only thing that seems clear is you're grasping, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: さきほどから‥‥現場の上面図を 穴のあくほど見つめていますが‥‥
English: You've been staring pretty intently at those floor plans.

Japanese: そこに、何かモンダイが あるのですか？
English: Tell me... is there a problem with them?
(Yes, this is strange...!)

Japanese: よく見れば、この上面図‥‥ 何か“足りないモノ”があります。
English: Take a good look at these floor plans. Something is missing.

Japanese: “足りないモノ”‥‥？ 描かれていない、というコトかな？
English: "Missing"...? You mean, something hasn't been drawn on there?

Japanese: そして、その“足りないモノ”を 描きこむことによって‥‥
English: Yes. Something that, when drawn,

Japanese: この血痕の“イミ”は、まったく ちがったモノになるのです！
English: will completely change the meaning of the blood mark!

Japanese: ‥‥今のコトバが、弁護人の 言い逃れでないことを祈ります。
English: Let us pray the defense isn't simply trying to buy time.

Japanese: それでは。うかがいましょう！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （今回は、やたら証拠品が たくさんある‥‥）
English: (With all this evidence here...)

Japanese: （何か、あるだろう‥‥）
English: (there's got to be something I can use!)

Japanese: 上面図に“足りないモノ”‥‥ いったい、なんですかな？
English: The question is... which item can prove something is missing in the floor plans?

Japanese: “ヘタなテッポウも、数を撃てば 当たる”‥‥西部のコトワザさ。
English: As they say back west... "Even a blind man can hit with buckshot."

Japanese: ただし‥‥弾丸がシメって いなければ、のハナシだがね。
English: That is... so long as he's facin' the general direction.

Japanese: どうやら。弁護士の弾丸は ビショビショのようだ‥‥
English: It seems Mr. Wright's not sure which direction to face.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　やたら証拠品が あるということは‥‥）
English: (It's no use!)

Japanese: （それだけ、ハズレが多いと いうことじゃないか‥‥！）
English: (The more evidence there is, the more wrong choices!)

Japanese: 事件があったときの、保管室‥‥ 落ちついて、思い出しましょう！
English: Just calm down, Mr. Wright. Try and remember what the evidence room was like on the day of the crime!

Japanese: （なぜ‥‥この血痕が、妙に 気になるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What is it that bothers me about this blood mark..?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、おねがいします。 今度こそ、まちがいありません！
English: Please allow me another chance, Your Honor. I'll prove it this time!

Japanese: そのベニヤ板が、どうかしたかい。
English: What about that piece of plywood?

Japanese: 《タイホくん》‥‥ さすが、将来のマスコットです。
English: The Blue Badger! Mascot of the police force!

Japanese: 彼はその身をもって、 決定的な証拠を守っていました。
English: Defender of truth, guardian of proof!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ、弁護人。
English: Explain yourself, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 現場の上面図を見てください。 ここには‥‥“彼”がいません。
English: Please look at the floor plans of the crime scene. The Blue Badger is not here.

Japanese: ウスっぺらいヤツには、 ウスっぺらい友が、よく似合うさ。
English: So?

Japanese: それでは‥‥ウスっぺらい ぼくの友だちを置いてみましょう。
English: So watch what happens when we put him in.

Japanese: 事件当時、彼が おどっていた場所は‥‥ここです。
English: This is where he was dancing at the time of the crime.

Japanese: いかがですか？　“あるコト”に 気づきませんか‥‥？
English: Well...?

Japanese: “あるコト”と言われましても‥‥
English: Well... what?

Japanese: あ‥‥あああああ‥‥ッ！
English: ! *gasp!*

Japanese: そう。この場所でタイホくんが おどっているかぎり‥‥
English: That's right. So long as the Blue Badger is dancing here...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の保管庫に、 手のアトをつけるコトはできない！
English: it would be impossible... to place a handprint at this spot on the locker!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだとおおおッ‥‥！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥つまり！ どういうことなのですかッ！
English: So that means... Uh... just exactly what does that mean!?

Japanese: いや、だから！ 不可能なんですよ！
English: It means it can't be done!

Japanese: いやいや、だから！ ジッサイ、血痕は残っています！
English: What are you saying? Blood traces were undeniably found on that locker!

Japanese: いやいやいや、だから！ そんなコト、ぼくに聞かれても
English: Don't look at me, I didn't put it there!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！ カガク的に考えてみましょう！
English: Mr. Wright! Think it through scientifically!

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃん‥‥！
English: Ema!

Japanese: あの日の午後‥‥‥
English: On that afternoon...

Japanese: タイホくんを保管室に運んだのは、 原灰巡査ですよね。
English: Officer Meekins was the one who brought the Blue Badger to the evidence room, right?

Japanese: それ以降、後ろの保管庫に “手のアト”を残すのは、不可能。
English: After he put it down, it would be impossible to leave a handprint on that locker.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ ということは、この“血痕”は‥‥ ...!
English: So that must mean this blood mark...

Japanese: タイホくんが置かれるより “以前”につけられた‥‥？
English: was left there before the Blue Badger was brought in...?

Japanese: 待ってください！ いくらなんでも、それは‥‥！
English: Just one moment! I will not allow such farfetched balderdash in my courtroom!

Japanese: たしかに、信じがたい‥‥でも。 そう考えるしかありません！
English: It may sound farfetched, Your Honor... but it's the only possible explanation!

Japanese: ‥‥２月２１日。 あの日、証拠保管室では‥‥
English: On February 21st... in the Police Department's evidence room...

Japanese: “２回”、血が 流されたコトになります！
English: blood was spilled not once, but twice!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: B-but how...!

Japanese: １回は、この防犯カメラに 映っている“事件”‥‥
English: One time was captured on this tape, taken by the security camera.

Japanese: 原灰巡査が手をケガした、 ササヤカなものでした。
English: Officer Meekins cut his hand, from which a trivial amount of blood fell.

Japanese: そして‥‥モンダイは、 “もう１つ”の事件です。
English: The problem is... the "other" time.

Japanese: この格闘より“以前”にも、 保管室では、血が流された。
English: Someone bled prior to the struggle shown on this tape.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: It had to have been...

Japanese: それこそが、“ホンモノ”の 多田敷捜査官の殺害事件なのです！
English: Detective Goodman, when he was really murdered!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなコトを！ その可能性は、否定された！
English: That's ridiculous! I refute you!

Japanese: たしかに、防犯カメラの映像は “ニセモノ”でした！
English: The murder portrayed in the security tape has been proven to be a fake.

Japanese: しかし！　それだけでは、 保管庫の血痕に、説明がつかない！
English: However! That does not explain the blood mark found on the locker!

Japanese: それでは‥‥キサマの言う “もう１つ”の事件は‥‥
English: So then... assuming this "murder" you purport really happened.

Japanese: いったい“いつ” 起こったというのだ！
English: When did it take place!?

Japanese: 事件が起こったことを示す、 証拠の提示を要求するッ！
English: I demand you show evidence that proves it occurred!

Japanese: （“１回目”の事件が 起こった時間‥‥）
English: (When did the first incident occur...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の主張は こういうことですね？
English: To surmise, the defense claims that...

Japanese: 多田敷さんに変装した罪門　恭介が 原灰巡査をキズつけるより、以前。
English: prior to Officer Meekins being cut by Jake Marshall, who was disguised as Detective

Japanese: あの日の証拠保管室では、 “もう１つの事件”があった。
English: Goodman, another "incident" took place in that evidence room.

Japanese: 保管庫の血痕が、 それを証明しています。
English: The blood mark on the locker proves this.

Japanese: では‥‥いったい、その “１回目”の事件は‥‥
English: Very well. Then tell us...

Japanese: “いつ”起こったのでしょうか？ その手がかりが必要です。
English: When did this "first" incident occur? Proof must be presented.

Japanese: （犯行時刻を示す手がかり‥‥ １つだけ、ある！）
English: (Proof that shows when the murder took place...) (There's only one piece of evidence that can show that!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護側に 提示していただきましょう！
English: Now then. Will the defense please present its evidence?

Japanese: “１回目”の犯行が行われた 時刻を示す、証拠品とは！
English: What shows when the "first" crime took place?

Japanese: これが、その証拠です！ ‥‥カンタンなコトですよ。
English: This is the proof! It's quite simple, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコトなら まちがわないでもらいたいな。
English: If it's so simple, then don't get it wrong.
Huh?

Japanese: “あれ”で済めば、 ケーサツはいりません！
English: If "huh" was an acceptable excuse, we wouldn't need police.

Japanese: “１回目”の犯行は、タイホくんが 置かれる前に起こったんですよね。
English: The first crime took place before the Blue Badger was brought to the room, right?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥タイホくんは、“いつ” 置かれたんでしたっけ。
English: Say... When was the Blue Badger brought there, anyway?

Japanese: たしか‥‥“時刻”がわかる 証拠品が、どこかにあったね。
English: Don't we have a time chart among our evidence items that lists the time?

Japanese: 事件が証拠保管室で起きた以上、 この部屋に入らなければならない。
English: If the crime took place inside the evidence room, then the perpetrator would had to have

Japanese: そして‥‥そのためには、 ＩＤカードが必要です。
English: entered the room. In order to do so, an ID card is required.

Japanese: ＩＤカード‥‥‥あッ！ 《使用記録》ですかッ！
English: An ID card... Oh! The ID Card Record!

Japanese: 原灰巡査が、タイホくんのパネルを 持ちこんだ時刻は‥‥
English: Officer Meekins brought the Blue Badger panel into the evidence room at...

Japanese: 《４時５０分》でしょうな、 モチロン。
English: Let's see here... 4:50 PM.

Japanese: １回目の事件は、“それ以前”に 起こったわけですから‥‥
English: If the crime took place before that time, then it would be...

Japanese: ああああああああああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAHHH!

Japanese: み。み。み。み。み。み。み。み。
English: M-M-M-M-M-M-M-M-

Japanese: みみみみみみみみみみ、御剣検事！ あなた、なんというコトをッ！
English: M-M-M-M-M-M-M- Miles Edgeworth! Just what have you done!?

Japanese: まさか‥‥アンタだったとはなァ、 御剣のボウヤ。
English: I never figured you had nerve, boy.

Japanese: しらじらしいな‥‥証人。
English: Put off the act, witness.

Japanese: わかっているはずだ。 ‥‥考えるまでもあるまい。
English: It doesn't take a lot of thought to figure out it couldn't have been me.

Japanese: フン‥‥‥！ ザンネンながら、ね。
English: Hmm... Nope, I ain't gettin' it...

Japanese: フン‥‥‥！ 考えても、わかりません。
English: Hmm... I'm afraid I don't understand either.

Japanese: 現場のルミノール反応を見れば、 そこで“流血”があったのは明白。
English: It's clear from the luminol test that blood was there.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。“２回目”の 事件が起こった際‥‥
English: However, when the "second" crime took place...

Japanese: 原灰巡査も、罪門　恭介も、 その痕跡に気づいていない。
English: both Officer Meekins and Officer Marshall failed to notice the blood.

Japanese: つまり‥‥“１回目”の事件の 痕跡は、インメツされたわけだ‥‥
English: That means... the blood from the first crime was wiped away...

Japanese: “真犯人”によって。
English: by the real murderer.

Japanese: 被害者を殺害して、死体を運び、 床に残った血痕を処理する‥‥
English: I would have had just ten minutes to murder the victim, carry his body away,

Japanese: 私に与えられた時間は、１０分。 ‥‥できるはずがない。
English: and clean up the blood... Unfortunately that's physically impossible.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥
English: That would mean...

Japanese: 犯行は、さらに《前》に 起こったことになります！
English: the crime must have taken place before Mr. Edgeworth entered the evidence room.

Japanese: もう一度、記録を見てみましょう。
English: Let's look at the chart again.
There's only one other card number remaining: "7777777"!

Japanese: とんだラッキーナンバーだね、 コイツは‥‥
English: Talk about a lucky number...

Japanese: し‥‥しかし。 それでは、不自然です！
English: But wait... That doesn't make sense!

Japanese: 被害者自身‥‥多田敷捜査官が、 保管室に入れないではないですか！
English: How could Detective Goodman have entered the evidence room?

Japanese: 使用記録がない以上‥‥ 《真犯人といっしょに入った》
