English: Since there's no record of his card being used beforehand... he must have entered along with the real murderer.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるしかありません。 《７７７７７７７》の人物と！
English: That's the only plausible explanation. He went in with "7777777"!

Japanese: 御剣検事！ 大至急、調べてください！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Please look into this ASAP!

Japanese: なななななななななななななななの ＩＤの人物を！
English: Find out whose ID number is "sevensevensevensevenseven- sevensevenseven"!

Japanese: “な”が１回多いようだ、裁判長。
English: That's one "seven" too many, Your Honor.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か‥‥そのナンバーを 調べることは不可能だ。
English: Unfortunately... I'm unable to look up the owner of that ID card.

Japanese: 少なくとも‥‥今の時点では。
English: At least, at present.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: What!?

Japanese: どういうコトだ？　ボウヤ‥‥
English: Explain yourself, son.

Japanese: 《７７７７７７７》 このナンバーは、警察署長以上‥‥
English: The ID number "7777777" belongs to someone with a rank of Captain or higher...

Japanese: いわゆる“上層部”と呼ばれる グループのものだ。
English: Someone who is a so-called "executive officer."

Japanese: それを調べる権限は、キホン的に ‥‥我々には、ない。
English: We don't have the authority to inquire into such a person's identity.

Japanese: そんなバカな！ じゃあ、いったい‥‥
English: But that's ridiculous! Just how...

Japanese: 最後まで聞きたまえ。
English: I'm not finished talking, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 我々に、捜査権が発生する ケースが、１つだけある。
English: There is one situation in which we can be granted such authority.

Japanese: 上層部の特定の人物に、公式な 容疑が認められた場合、ですな。
English: If an official charge filed against an executive is accepted.

Japanese: ‥‥“公式な容疑”‥‥
English: An "official charge"...

Japanese: アンタたちは、いつもそうさ。 ‥‥身内をかばい合う。
English: You're all alike, aren't you?

Japanese: “いんぺい”と“ねつ造”‥‥ それが、検事局のやり方なのさ！
English: With your "cover-ups" and your "forgeries"... That's how the Prosecutor's Office operates!

Japanese: ‥‥私は、自らのシゴトに ホコリを持っている。
English: I take pride in my work, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: コンキョのない中傷は やめてもらいたいな‥‥証人。
English: I would appreciate it if you would keep your slander to yourself.

Japanese: “中傷”‥‥？　おもしろい。
English: "Slander," is it? Okay.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥１つだけ。 質問させてもらうぜ。
English: Let me ask a question.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: Yes?

Japanese: アンタじゃない。 被告席に座ってる、カノジョだよ。
English: No, not to you. To her, the defendant sitting over there.

Japanese: アンタの言う“上層部”の代表さ。
English: Your own little "executive."

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥？）
English: (L-Lana?)

Japanese: ‥‥バカなコトを。 彼女には、容疑がかかっている。
English: Don't be stupid. She's been charged with murder.

Japanese: 当然、ＩＤナンバーは調べた。 ‥‥《７７７７７７７》ではない。
English: Of course we've looked up her ID number... and it's not

Japanese: "7777777." そうじゃない。 わかってるだろう？　ボウヤ。
English: Don't play me for a fool, pardner. That's not what I want to ask.

Japanese: オレの知りたいのは、ただ１つ。 あの事件のコトだけさ。
English: All I want to know is one thing... about that incident.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》ですか‥‥
English: The SL-9 Incident?

Japanese: 主席検事、答えてくれ！ ２年前の、あの裁判で‥‥
English: Answer me this, Chief Prosecutor!

Japanese: アンタたちは、本当に‥‥ 正当な証拠だけを使ったのか？
English: In that trial two years ago... Did you really only use legitimate evidence!?

Japanese: 質問は聞かれたと思うが‥‥ 主席検事。
English: Do you need the witness to repeat his question, Chief Prosecutor?

Japanese: うかがいました。
English: I heard him fine, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥私自身が この事件の審理を担当した。
English: Two years ago... I was in charge of the prosecution for that trial.

Japanese: 当然、我々は、違法な証拠など
English: At the time, we...

Japanese: ‥‥ときとして。
English: Occasionally... we felt the powerlessness of the law.

Japanese: 法の無力を感じることがあった。 ‥‥少なくとも、私は。
English: At least... I did.
L-Lana...

Japanese: 私は、犯罪を法でしばるため、 このシゴトを選びました。
English: I became a prosecutor in order to suppress crime with the law.

Japanese: しかし、気がついてみれば。 しばられているのは、我々のほう。
English: But before I realized it, we were the ones being suppressed by the law.

Japanese: ひ、被告人！　あなたは‥‥ 何を言おうとしているのですか！
English: Defendant! Just what are you saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、聞こう。 主席検事サマ。
English: I'll ask you again, Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの裁判‥‥
English: During that trial two years ago...

Japanese: アンタたちは本当に、すべての 証拠を法廷に提示したかい？
English: did you really present all the evidence in court?

Japanese: ジッサイに捜査をしていた オレの目を見て、断言できるかい？
English: Can you look me, an investigator in that crime, in the eye and say that you did?

Japanese: 主席検事！　まさか、あなたは‥‥
English: Chief Prosecutor! You didn't...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ その必要は認めません。 ...
English: I don't have to, Officer Marshall.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！ なぜだ！　なぜ、答えない‥‥ !
English: Why don't you answer him!?

Japanese: 真に恐ろしい犯罪には、 こちらも恐ろしい手を使う‥‥
English: Drastic crimes require drastic measures...

Japanese: いたしかたのないことです。
English: That's just the way it is.

Japanese: 彼に正当な裁きを与えるため‥‥ 私は、手段を選ばなかった。
English: We did what we had to... in order for him to get the verdict he deserved.

Japanese: そんな！　お、お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: But Lana!

Japanese: それが、たとえ‥‥‥“ねつ造”と 呼ばれる結果になろうとも。
English: Even if it involved "forging" evidence.

Japanese: つまり、そういうことだよ。 ‥‥御剣のボウヤ。
English: See? That's what I'm talking about.

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥‥ なんだとオオオオオオオオオオッ！
English: No... NNNNOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 静粛にィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! ORDERRRRR!!!

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんの発言は、 あまりに衝撃的だった‥‥
English: Lana's remarks caused such a stir...

Japanese: ‥‥騒ぎはおさまらず、 審理は中断。すべては明日‥‥
English: the chaos in the courtroom could not be quelled. The conclusion of the trial...

Japanese: ‥‥最終日に持ち越された‥‥
English: would have to wait until the following day...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。 さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！ 被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ２月２４日　午後　３時１２分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: February 24, 3:12 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あの‥‥ごめんなさい、 成歩堂さん。
English: I'm so sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが‥‥いきなり、 あんなコト言いだして‥‥
English: I'm sorry for what my sister said...

Japanese: 真に恐ろしい犯罪には、 こちらも恐ろしい手を使う‥‥
English: Drastic crimes require drastic measures...

Japanese: いたしかたのないことです。
English: That's just the way it is.

Japanese: 彼に正当な裁きを与えるため‥‥ 私は、手段を選ばなかった。
English: We did what we had to... in order for him to get the verdict he deserved.

Japanese: あたし‥‥知りませんでした。 まさか、《ＳＬ９号事件》が‥‥
English: I... didn't know. I never knew that the "SL-9 Incident" was just another name for...

Japanese: 《青影事件》の コトだった、なんて。
English: the "Joe Darke Killings."

Japanese: （有名な事件みたいだけど‥‥ 聞いたことないんだよな、ぼく）
English: (Sounds like everyone's heard about these killings but me...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、どうしても 青影をタイホしたかったんです。
English: Lana wanted Darke convicted so badly!

Japanese: だから、あの日‥‥‥ あたしの事件を、利用したの。
English: That's why she used me... That's why she used what happened to me.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと待って！ “きみの”事件だって？
English: What do you mean, "what happened" to you?

Japanese: 資料にも書いてありますが‥‥
English: It's all there in the file...

Japanese: 青影　丈の最後の被害者は、 検事の、罪門　直斗さんでした。
English: Joe Darke's last victim was Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: 罪門さんの弟を殺害したとき、 致命的な証拠を残したんです。
English: When he murdered Officer Marshall's brother, he left behind an incriminating piece of evidence.

Japanese: その事件に‥‥あかねちゃん。 きみが関わっているの？
English: But what did you have to do with those killings, Ema?

Japanese: 青影　丈は、あのとき‥‥ あたしを殺そうとしていたの。
English: On the night Prosecutor Neil Marshall was murdered, Joe Darke... tried to kill me.

Japanese: なんだって！　きみを‥‥！
English: What? He tried to kill you!?

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事‥‥罪門さんの 弟は、あたしを助けようとして‥‥
English: Officer Marshall's brother... Neil... was only trying to save me.

Japanese: じゃ‥‥じゃあ、きみは‥‥
English: So that means you...

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 《青影事件》の法廷で、 目撃者として証言台に立ちました。
English: I was a witness in the Joe Darke trial.

Japanese: （‥‥なんてこった‥‥！）
English: (I didn't see that one coming!)

Japanese: （いろいろ聞いてみたほうが よさそうだな‥‥） 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's plant, "Charley."

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがいなくなってからも、 こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: I've been taking care of him in Maya's absence.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？ なんか今日は、妙に黄色いな。
English: Huh? He looks a little yellow today...

Japanese: あ。さっき、お水あげて おきました。たっぷり、混ぜて。
English: Oh, I watered him just now. I mixed in a lot, too.

Japanese: “まぜて”って‥‥ナニを？
English: Mixed in... what?

Japanese: あかねちゃん特製のカガク添加物。 どうですか？　チャーリーくん。
English: Why my very own scientific additive! Well, Charley? You like it?

Japanese: だから、妙に黄色いよ！
English: I told you, he's turning yellow!

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: あ。さっき、あたしも ベンキョウしてみたんですよ。
English: Oh, I tried studying one of those just now.

Japanese: ホラ。きのうの法廷で モンダイになった‥‥
English: Remember what they were talking about in the trial yesterday...?

Japanese: ああ、“証拠法”ね。 どう？　わかった？
English: Oh, right, evidence law. So, did you learn anything?

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなっちゃいました。
English: Well, when I tried reading it made my head hurt.

Japanese: はあ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ついでに、しまうときに 足の上に落っことしちゃいました。
English: Then, when I closed it, it slipped out of my hand and fell on my foot.

Japanese: （好感の持てる子だな。 ‥‥意外と）
English: (Oddly enough I find myself identifying with her on this one...)

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ‥‥ふと目をやったら、 あのボーイと目が合ってしまった。
English: Egad! The bellboy was staring right at me.

Japanese: なんか、コシが低いように見えて、 それでいて、キレがあるような。
English: He looks wimpy, but you can tell, there's a sharp edge under that facade.

Japanese: 将来、大物になりそうな フンイキのボーイさんですね。
English: That bellboy is going to be someone someday.

Japanese: （ぼくも言われてみたいもんだ）
English: (Wish someone would say that about me...)

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていたデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 依頼を引き受けなくなってから、 ますます座る機会がへった。
English: I sit here even less now that I've stopped taking cases.

Japanese: あ。来週、英語のテストがあるから ベンキョウしないと！
English: Uh oh! I have a math test coming up next week! I'd better study!

Japanese: デスク、借りますね！
English: I'm borrowing this desk, okay?

Japanese: “インボー、タンテイの 重要なシゴト”‥‥と。 1 + 1 = 2 1 + 2 = 3 1 + 3 = 4 （あまりデキのよくない ゴロ合わせだな‥‥）
English: (If that's what they're teaching in high school math, we're in serious trouble.)

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターが 張ってある。
English: There's a poster of the Steel Samurai on the wall.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、帰りぎわに 張りかえていったみたいだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here on the day that she left.

Japanese: せっかくの置きみやげだから、 そのままにしてあるんだけど‥‥
English: I didn't have the heart to take it down.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... たまに、お客さんの視線が 妙にイタいときがある。
English: I do sometimes get strange looks from the clients, though.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの日。 ちょうど今ごろの季節でした。
English: It happened two years ago... it was right about this time of the year, too.

Japanese: 雨が降っていて‥‥季節はずれの カミナリも近づいていました。
English: There was a terrible thunder storm that day... Unusual for the season.

Japanese: あたし‥‥お姉ちゃんの オフィスにいたんです。
English: I was alone in my sister's office.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんのシゴトが終わったら、 晩ゴハンを食べに行こう、って。
English: We were planning to eat dinner together once she finished her work.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥そこへ、 あのヒトが逃げてきたの！
English: Then suddenly... This terrifying man came bursting into the office!

Japanese: ‥‥青影　丈‥‥
English: Joe Darke...

Japanese: どうやら、追われていたみたい。 ナイフを向けて‥‥叫んでた。
English: It seemed like he was running from someone. He pulled out a knife and screamed at me.

Japanese: あたし、もう何がなんだか わからなくなっちゃって。
English: I didn't know what was going on.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥オフィスに、 罪門検事さんが現れたんです。
English: Just then, Prosecutor Marshall showed up.

Japanese: （罪門　直斗検事‥‥弟さんか）
English: (Jake Marshall's brother...)

Japanese: 青影は、あたしをヒトジチに とって、ナイフを向けました。
English: Joe Darke tried to take me hostage,

Japanese: でも！　一瞬はやく、罪門さんが アイツに飛びかかってきました。
English: but before he could... Mr. Marshall tackled him.

Japanese: そして、そのとき‥‥
English: Then...

Japanese: 何が起こったの？
English: What happened?

Japanese: あたし、一生忘れません！
English: I-I'll never forget it!

Japanese: カミナリが落ちて、 部屋の電気が消えて。
English: Lightning struck, and the lights went out. Suddenly...

Japanese: 窓のソトに、はげしいイナズマが 走って、目がくらんで。
English: a bolt of lightning flashed outside the window, lighting up the office for an instant.

Japanese: あたしの目に、焼きついていたの。 あの一瞬が‥‥今も、忘れない‥‥
English: What I saw then burned a permanent picture in my mind. I... I can still see it now...

Japanese: （目に、焼きついた“一瞬”‥‥）
English: (A permanent picture...?)

Japanese: あたし‥‥青影が罪門さんを刺した “瞬間”は、覚えてないんです。
English: I don't remember the moment when Darke stabbed Mr. Marshall.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥そのことは 証言できなかったんだ。
English: So you weren't able to testify about that?

Japanese: はい。あたし、自分が襲われた コトだけ、聞かれました。
English: No, I was only asked about when I was attacked.

Japanese: だから、お姉ちゃんは‥‥ 事件を“作った”んだと思います。
English: That must be why Lana... why she "made up" the crime.

Japanese: “作った”‥‥それって、 証拠品の“ねつ造”‥‥かな。
English: Made it up? You mean, provided bogus evidence?

Japanese: あの有罪判決は、検事局にとって、 もっとも大きな勝利だったんです。
English: The Prosecutor's Office wanted that guilty verdict so badly.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが証拠を作って、 それを使って、御剣検事さんが‥‥
English: Lana forged the evidence, and Mr. Edgeworth used it...

Japanese: み、御剣が‥‥！
English: Edgeworth!?

Japanese: あ。御剣検事さん、きっと 知らなかったんだと思います！
English: Yes. But I'm sure he didn't know anything about it!

Japanese: まさか、ねつ造された証拠を わたされていたなんて。
English: He couldn't have known he was being given false evidence.

Japanese: でも‥‥ それからなんです。
English: Even so... that's when it all started.

Japanese: 御剣検事さんに、イヤなウワサが。 ‥‥ゼンブ、あたしのせい。
English: The rumors about Mr. Edgeworth, I mean. It's all my fault...

Japanese: あたしがしっかり証言できて いれば、あんなコトには‥‥
English: If I could have just testified properly, none of this would have happened!

Japanese: （‥‥カタチはどうであれ、 御剣のヤツ、関わっていたのか）
English: (So it's true. Even though he may not have known it...)

Japanese: （検事局の“ねつ造”に‥‥！）
English: (Edgeworth really was involved in falsifying evidence!)

Japanese: そして、お姉ちゃんも。 あの事件がカイケツしてから‥‥
English: After that case ended, Lana was never the same.

Japanese: あんなに、冷たいヒトに なっちゃったんです。
English: She became cold, like she is today.

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ。彼女に対して ココロを閉ざしてしまっている）
English: (She must not have been able to face up to what she did... Especially not to Ema.)

Japanese: 事件が起こった“一瞬”‥‥ いったい、何を見たの？
English: What did you see in the instant that crime occurred?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 青影が、罪門さんを押し倒して、 凶器のナイフをふりあげたの。
English: Darke knocked down Mr. Marshall and raised his knife.

Japanese: （罪門　直斗は‥‥‥この子の 目の前で刺されたのか！）
English: (Neil Marshall was stabbed right in front of this poor girl!)

Japanese: そのアトは‥‥覚えていない。 キゼツしちゃったみたいで。
English: I don't remember what happened after that. Apparently I passed out.

Japanese: 気がついたら、お姉ちゃんが あたしを介抱してくれていた‥‥
English: When I came to, Lana was cradling me in her arms.

Japanese: ‥‥かわいそうに。 ヒドい目にあったんだね。
English: Poor Ema... You've been through so much.

Japanese: あたし‥‥あの“一瞬”を うまく証言できなかったの‥‥
English: I-I couldn't bring myself to testify about that instant.

Japanese: 必死に、がんばったけど‥‥ コトバが出なかった。
English: I tried, but the words just wouldn't come out.

Japanese: 絵で描いたりもしたけど。 それもあたし、へたくそで。
English: I drew a picture, but it wasn't any good.

Japanese: ２年前といえば‥‥１４才だ。 ムリもないよ。
English: Two years ago... You must have been 14. That's understandable.

Japanese: あの事件のあと‥‥ あたし、決心したの。
English: Once it was all over... I made up my mind.

Japanese: カガク捜査官になって、今度こそ。 自分のコトバで犯罪と戦いたい！
English: I decided that when I grew up, I'd become a scientific investigator. I want to be able to fight crime with my testimonies!

Japanese: そして、決定的な証拠を見つける。 そうすれば‥‥
English: And find the evidence to make an airtight case...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、証拠を“作る” 必要なんて、ないでしょう？
English: That way, Lana would never have to forge any.

Japanese: そう、だったのか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: （‥‥あかねちゃんのコト、 やっとリカイできた気がする）
English: (I think I'm finally starting to understand what makes Ema tick.)

Japanese: （でも‥‥事件のハナシには、 少し気になるコトがあるな）
English: (But there's still something that bothers me about that crime.)

Japanese: ひとつ‥‥気になることが あるんだけど。
English: There's something that's puzzling me, Ema.

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: きみは、２年前‥‥ともえさんの オフィスにいたんだよね？
English: You said you were in Lana's office at that time, right?

Japanese: そうですよ。
English: That's right.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥連続殺人犯が、 そんなところに逃げてきたのかな。
English: Why then would a serial killer come running in there?

Japanese: しかも、それを追ってきたのが 《検事》だった、なんて‥‥
English: Not only that, but he was being chased by a prosecutor?

Japanese: ああ。それならカンタンです。
English: Oh, there's no mystery there.

Japanese: 青影　丈は、あの日‥‥ 取調べ中に逃げ出したんです。
English: Joe Darke had been taken in for questioning that day.

Japanese: “取調べ”‥‥というと、警察に？
English: Taken in for questioning? You mean by the police?

Japanese: ええ。 モチロン、舞台は警察局ですよ。
English: Of course. This happened at the Police Department.

Japanese: 事情聴取の途中に逃げ出して、 お姉ちゃんのオフィスに‥‥
English: He tried to run away halfway through the interview, and fled into my sister's office.

Japanese: いやいや、だから。なんでそこで ともえさんのオフィスなのかな。
English: But why did he run all the way over to your sister's office?

Japanese: だって。取調べ室とオフィスは、 エレベータで直通だから。
English: Because the detective offices and the questioning room are right across from the elevator.
Across from the elevator?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ともえさんは、 “主席検事”だったよね？
English: But Lana was the Chief Prosecutor, wasn't she?

Japanese: あ！　もしかして‥‥ あたし、言ってませんでした？
English: No, silly! Didn't I tell you?

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ ２年前まで、捜査官だったんです。
English: Two years ago... Lana was a detective.

Japanese: 警察局でナンバーワンの スゴウデだったの。
English: She was the best in the entire force!

Japanese: え‥‥‥えええええッ！ （はじめて、聞いたぞ！）
English: Whaaaaat!? (That's news to me!)

Japanese: あの《青影事件》の直後、 検事局に移ったんです。
English: After the Joe Darke case,

Japanese: “主席検事”として。
English: she was transferred to the Prosecutor's Office and made Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: （ともえさんが‥‥かつて “捜査官”だった‥‥？）
English: (Lana used to be... a detective?)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥本人のハナシを 聞いたほうがいいみたいだ！）
English: (I'd better have another talk with her!)

Japanese: ホラ。これが弁護士バッジだよ。
English: See this? It's my Attorney's Badge.

Japanese: ふむふむ。ほおほお。 これがウワサの‥‥
English: Ahh! Well! I've never seen a real one before.

Japanese: （こんなに食いついてくる子は 初めてだな‥‥）
English: (You're the first one who's actually been interested in mine, believe me.)

Japanese: 材質は銀。金メッキが少し はがれてきてますね。
English: Its composition is mostly silver. The gold plating is flaking a bit.

Japanese: （カガク的に分析されてしまった）
English: (She analyzed it. Scientifically...)

Japanese: 硫化物による腐食は、まだみたい。 ５０００円ぐらい、でしょうか。
English: There doesn't appear to be any corrosion due to sulfides. I'd give you $50.00 for it.

Japanese: 悪いけど、売る気はないよ。 ‥‥まだ。
English: Sorry, but it's not for sale. Yet.

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥多田敷さん、 なくしていたんですよね、コレ。
English: Mr. Goodman didn't have this on the day of the crime, right?

Japanese: うん。事件が起こる前に、 《遺失物届》を提出している。
English: Right. A few days before he was murdered, he had filed a lost item report.

Japanese: （“なくした”というより “盗まれた”んだけどな）
English: (Although "stolen item" is more like it...)

Japanese: （‥‥あの、巡査のカッコウを したギャングに）
English: (Swiped by that lone wolf who dressed up like him.)

Japanese: そんなコトするヒトには 見えなかったのに‥‥罪門さん。
English: I never would have thought Officer Marshall the type to do something like that.

Japanese: ぼくには、いかにもやりそうに 見えたよ。ワルいけど。
English: It didn't come as much of a surprise to me...

Japanese: 凶器、ですよね。 御剣検事さんのナイフ‥‥
English: So... Mr. Edgeworth's knife was the murder weapon?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。おキョウさんが、 目撃しているんだよ。
English: Right... or so the witness, Ms. Starr, claims.

Japanese: ともえさんが‥‥このナイフで、 多田敷捜査官を刺したトコロを。
English: She saw your sister stab Mr. Goodman with this knife.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ゼンブ おキョウさんがワルいんですっ！
English: Ugh... You know, all of this is that Angel Starr's fault!

Japanese: ヒトのせいにしちゃダメだよ。
English: She's not the one on trial here.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥おキョウさんの 見マチガイですっ！
English: Well, no, but she must have seen it wrong!

Japanese: 気のせいにしちゃダメだよ。
English: Her eyesight isn't on trial here, either.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんもほしいですね。 《ベンゴ・オブ・ザ・イヤー》。
English: I bet you'd be pretty happy with a "King of Attorneys" shield, too, huh?

Japanese: 弁護士たちって、どうも まとまりがないからね。
English: We defense attorneys are a scattered, unrecognized bunch.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥どうして “割れた盾”なんでしょうねー。
English: Still... I wonder why the trophy shield is broken?

Japanese: たしかに、気になるなあ。
English: It is pretty odd, huh?

Japanese: （‥‥まさか、事件にカンケイ してるワケ、ないけどなあ）
English: (I doubt it has anything to do with this case though...)

Japanese: おキョウさんの証言によると ‥‥事件の、直後。
English: According to Ms. Starr's testimony...

Japanese: 彼女は、携帯電話を使っている。 相手は‥‥キミ、だね？
English: Ms. Skye used her phone right after the murder... she called you, right?

Japanese: あ、あの！ ‥‥すぐ、切れちゃったんです。
English: B-but...! She hung up right away.

Japanese: なんだか、デンパの調子も 悪くて‥‥よく聞こえなかったし。
English: And it was a bad connection. I couldn't hear anything.

Japanese: （地下駐車場だったから、かな）
English: (It was an underground parking lot, I suppose...)

Japanese: たしかに、“マフラー”のコト、 話してましたけど‥‥
English: She did say something about a "muffler"...

Japanese: すぐに、切れちゃったから‥‥
English: But like I said, she hung up right away.

Japanese: こ、こんな写真‥‥ おキョウさんの見マチガイですっ！
English: Th-this photograph... Ms. Starr must have been seeing things!

Japanese: イミがわからないぞ。
English: And photographing things?

Japanese: （事件現場に、たしかに ともえさんは、いた‥‥！）
English: (This proves Ms. Skye was there at the scene of the crime!)

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥事件にカンケイして いると考えるしかないよな）
English: (She is connected to this crime somehow... but how?)

Japanese: どうして、こんなものが クルマの排気パイプに‥‥
English: Why would this have been in the car's exhaust pipe?

Japanese: ムカシの事件の証拠品、 なんですよね？
English: It's evidence from an old case, right?

Japanese: そう、だね。《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ 解決した事件みたいだけど。
English: Right... the SL-9 incident. It was solved, apparently.

Japanese: （証拠品の《申し送り》の当日に 盗み出された証拠品‥‥）
English: (This knife was stolen on the day of the evidence transferal...)

Japanese: （やはり、調べないとな。 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥か）
English: (Maybe I should look into this "SL-9 Incident"...)

Japanese: 高いんですよー、コレ。
English: This stuff is pretty expensive.

Japanese: へえ。‥‥いくらぐらい？
English: You don't say. How much?

Japanese: そうですね。１グラム、 １０００円以上するんですよ！
English: I'd say about $10 per gram!

Japanese: おこづかいためて 買ったりするんですけどね。
English: I save up my allowance to buy it.

Japanese: “おこづかい”‥‥ あの。ともえさんからもらうの？
English: Your allowance...? Does your sister give you that?

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥両親はいないから。
English: Yes. We don't have parents...

Japanese: そう、か‥‥
English: R-right...

Japanese: たまに、現物支給のときも あるんですよ、おこづかい。
English: Sometimes she even gives me a bottle instead of my allowance!

Japanese: げ、げんぶつしきゅう‥‥？
English: A bottle of luminol testing fluid...?

Japanese: （おこづかいが、ルミノール試薬 ‥‥うれしくないなあ）
English: (Man, I'd just take the allowance myself...)

Japanese: たしか、このクツにも ナゾがあったなあ。
English: There was something strange about this shoe, wasn't there?

Japanese: なんだっけ？
English: Let's see, what was it...?

Japanese: クツの底に残った血痕、ですね。
English: The traces of blood on its sole.

Japanese: 検事局の地下駐車場には 血痕はありませんでしたから。
English: There wasn't any blood found at the Prosecutor's Office's underground parking lot.

Japanese: たしか、どこかの床に 痕跡が残っていたなあ。
English: But we did find blood on the floor somewhere, didn't we?

Japanese: どこだっけ？
English: Where was that again...?

Japanese: 警察局の証拠保管室、ですよ。
English: In the evidence room at the Police Department.

Japanese: あたしをメモ帳がわりにしない！
English: Now stop using me as your memo pad!!!

Japanese: 残るナンバーは、１つですね！
English: There's only one number left to check out.

Japanese: 他のナンバーは、殺人事件とは 無関係だとわかっているからね。
English: That's right. We now know the other numbers aren't related to the murder.
An "executive officer."

Japanese: ななななななななななななななな、 かあ。
English: Sevensevensevensevenseven- sevensevenseven...

Japanese: “な”が１回多いよ、 あかねちゃん。
English: Uh, I believe that's one "seven" too many...

Japanese: （何か決定的な証拠がないかぎり、 ナンバーを調べることは不可能）
English: (We can't find out whose number this is unless we have some incriminating evidence.)

Japanese: （明日までに、なんとか なるんだろうか‥‥？）
English: (That's going to be hard to dig up by tomorrow...)

Japanese: 見ていると不安になるほど 不安定なツボだね。
English: This jar's pretty fragile.

Japanese: こんなの、いつ割れても おかしくないですよ！
English: It looks like it's about to break apart!

Japanese: だから、ちゃんとこうして 割れているじゃないか。
English: What do you mean, "about to"? It's already broken.

Japanese: ‥‥トクイげに言われてもなあ。
English: I meant again...

Japanese: （このカケラは、多田敷捜査官の 証拠保管庫に入っていた‥‥）
English: (This piece was found in Detective Goodman's evidence locker.)

Japanese: （と、いうことは‥‥）
English: (That means...)

Japanese: どこかで、“あの事件”に 関係しているんでしょうか‥‥
English: This is somehow related to that other incident...

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、御剣のところに あったドライバーだ。
English: This screwdriver was at Edgeworth's office, wasn't it?

Japanese: この事件とはカンケイない 証拠だ、って言ってましたね。
English: Yes. He said it didn't have anything to do with the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... じゃ、なんでこんなの 持ち歩いてるんだ？　ぼくたち。
English: Then what are we doing carrying it around?

Japanese: あたしに聞かないでください！
English: Hey, don't ask me! I'm not the one making the decisions around here!

Japanese: （たしか‥‥）
English: (If I recall...)

Japanese: （事件当日、巌徒局長に言われて 検事局に持ち帰ったんだよな）
English: (Edgeworth said Chief Gant asked him to take this to the Prosecutor's Office.)

Japanese: いいヒトですよね。御剣検事さん。 こんなの、くれちゃって。
English: Mr. Edgeworth is so kind, giving us something like this.

Japanese: あ！　もしかして‥‥‥ あたしのコト、好きなのかも！
English: Hey! You don't think he... he... likes me, do you?
Are you okay, Ema?

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん‥‥早く！ に、逃げて‥‥ください
English: Mr. Wright! Hurry! RUN!!!

Japanese: はっくしょ！
English: AHH-CHOOOO!!!
Here, I'll wipe those fingerprints off your face.

Japanese: （‥‥洗ってオチるんだろうか、 このコナ‥‥）
English: (Might as well wipe my dignity away too while you're at it.)

Japanese: 血のついたコートを、保管庫に しまうときについた指紋だね。
English: This fingerprint was left on the locker when Marshall was stashing his bloody coat.

Japanese: この“血のアト”‥‥多田敷さんの モノじゃないんですよね。
English: I guess that blood wasn't Mr. Goodman's after all.

Japanese: ああ。あの、元気いっぱいの オマワリさんの血、だね。
English: Nope. Just that of an overly zealous patrolman.

Japanese: やっぱり、あの時間‥‥警察局では なかったんですね。サツジン。
English: So there wasn't a murder at the Police Department at 5:15 in the end.

Japanese: （これから、どうなるんだろう ‥‥罪門さん）
English: (I wonder what's going to happen to Officer Marshall now...)

Japanese: 今日の法廷の 主役でしたよね、コレ。
English: We owe our entire success in court today to this tape.

Japanese: 最初は“なんだコレ”って 思ったけど‥‥
English: At first I thought it was a joke,

Japanese: 手がかりのゴールドラッシュ だったよな、この映像は。
English: but it turned out to be a goldmine of evidence.

Japanese: さっき数えてみたら、 ５コもありましたよ。手がかり。
English: I counted a total of five clues in it.

Japanese: タイホくんも、いい宣伝に なったんじゃないかな。
English: It also gave the Blue Badger some publicity...

Japanese: ‥‥よくないイミで。
English: of the negative sort, that is.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥すべて、この事件に つながっているみたいだ。
English: It seems everything is somehow related to this incident.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ですか。
English: What was it labeled, again? The "SL-9 Incident"?

Japanese: 今回の事件の関係者の名前が、 全員登場しているからね。
English: Yes. The name of everyone connected to this trial is listed in here.

Japanese: あとは‥‥
English: All that's left...

Japanese: “それが、今回の事件に どう関わってくるのか？”
English: is to figure out just what role everyone plays in this case.

Japanese: それを調べれば‥‥ コタエは見つかると思うよ。
English: If we search hard enough, I think we'll find our answers.

Japanese: （ともえさんが、何をやって‥‥ 何をやらなかったか‥‥？）
English: (What exactly did, or didn't, Lana do?)

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、コワいです。 すべてを知ってしまうのが‥‥
English: I'm not sure I want to know everything...

Japanese: あの。これ、なんだけど。
English: Here, see this?

Japanese: ふむふむ。やはり弁護士さんは 見せたがり屋である、と。
English: Ahh! I've noticed that defense attorneys have a tendency to want to show people things.

Japanese: （弁護士の生態をカガク的に カンサツされてしまった‥‥）
English: (What is this? A behavioral study of lawyers?)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 24 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、取調べ中みたいです。
English: Looks like they're questioning Lana again.

Japanese: （今日こそは、彼女のハナシを 聞かなくちゃならないのに‥‥）
English: (I guess we're not the only ones who want to question her...)

Japanese: せっかくだから、ハラバイさんに 会っていきましょうか。
English: Well, since we've come all this way, why don't we go see Mr. Meekins?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ やめておこう。つかれるから。
English: Uh... Let's not and say we did.

Japanese: （あとで、また来てみよう）
English: (I'd rather come back and do that later.)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 検事局で起きた殺人事件に、 警察局で起きた殺人事件‥‥
English: One murder at the Prosecutor's Office... one at the Police Department...

Japanese: 次はここじゃないかと、 震え上がっている。
English: He must be frozen with fear thinking that he might be next...

Japanese: 勝手なコメント、しない！
English: Don't scare the poor guy!

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 24 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あ。お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: L-Lana...

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士‥‥あなたに メイワクをかけたようです。
English: Mr. Wright. It seems I keep causing you trouble.

Japanese: 証拠品の“ねつ造”‥‥ まさか、と思っていました。
English: Falsifying evidence... I didn't think you were the type.

Japanese: 犯罪者は手段を選びません。 こちらも、カクゴが必要なのです。
English: Criminals don't mind playing foul, why should we?

Japanese: ‥‥でも！　お姉ちゃんたちが まちがえていたら！
English: But Lana! If you're wrong... an innocent person might be found guilty!

Japanese: 無実のヒトを有罪に しちゃうかもしれないんだよ！ たしかに‥‥ そのキケンは、承知しています。
English: Believe me... I understand the risks.

Japanese: ともえさん。
English: Lana.

Japanese: あかねちゃんから、 あなたのことを聞きました。
English: Ema told me about you.

Japanese: ‥‥なんのコトかしら。
English: Oh?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥２年前は 捜査官だったそうですね。
English: About how you were a detective two years ago,

Japanese: そして、《ＳＬ９号事件》を キッカケに、検事局に移った。
English: and how the SL-9 Incident was the reason for your transfer to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: 聞かせていただけますね？ その不自然な“異動”のコトも。
English: Could you fill me in on the details? Especially about that unusual change of jobs.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あなたには、聞くケンリが あるでしょう。成歩堂弁護士‥‥
English: I suppose you have a right to know, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク‥‥
English: Let me get this straight.

Japanese: 全員が関係者だったんですね。 《ＳＬ９号事件》の‥‥
English: You were all involved in the SL-9 Incident?

Japanese: そう‥‥ フシギなもの、ですね。
English: That's right. Quite a coincidence, hmm?

Japanese: ぼくは、そう思いません。
English: I don't buy it.

Japanese: え！　どういうことですか？
English: W-what are you saying?

Japanese: 関係者が全員、過去の事件に 関係しているなんて‥‥
English: There's no way everyone involved in this trial was also involved in that incident

Japanese: グーゼンとは考えられないよ。
English: just by "chance."

Japanese: でも‥‥！　２年前に 解決した事件なんですよ！
English: But that case was solved two years ago!

Japanese: その“解決”が認められなくて、 彼は、あんなコトをしたんだ。
English: At least one person went to extremes because they didn't believe it was truly solved.

Japanese: 罪門巡査‥‥彼のコトは、 私もずっと、気にしていました。
English: Officer Marshall... Yes, his actions came as a surprise to me as well.

Japanese: 弟さんを亡くしてから、すっかり ヒトが変わってしまって‥‥
English: Ever since his brother died, he's changed completely.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥青影の裁判に、 ナットクできなかったのかな。
English: I guess he wasn't convinced with the ruling against Joe Darke.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... １つの事件が終わっても‥‥ 人生は終わってくれないのです。
English: Life doesn't end with the closing of a case.

Japanese: その記憶を一生、 抱えたまま生きるしかない。
English: Everyone has to live the rest of their lives with their memories.

Japanese: その“事件”‥‥まだ、 終わっていないかもしれません。
English: That "case" just might not be over yet.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! あかねちゃんが襲われたのは、 警察局なんですね？
English: Ema was assaulted by Darke at the Police Department, right?

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈と私が、いっしょに 使っていたオフィスです。
English: Yes, in the office that Damon Gant and I shared.

Japanese: 現在は《警察局長室》‥‥ 巌徒局長が１人で使っています。
English: The office that Mr. Gant now occupies by himself: the Chief's office.

Japanese: （行ってみるか。局長室‥‥ 《ＳＬ９号事件》の現場へ！）
English: (Maybe we should have a look at the Chief's office - the site of the final SL-9 murder!)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: この部屋の空気の重さに、 ちぢみ上がって動けないようだ。
English: He seems frozen in place, cowering under this room's oppressive atmosphere.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、いろいろなコトが わかりました。
English: A lot of revelations were uncovered at the trial today, not the least of which was

Japanese: やはり‥‥２年前の事件が 大きく影響していること。
English: the fact that this case is largely connected to another one, two years ago.

Japanese: あの事件の証拠品が、盗まれた‥‥ なんとなく、予想はしていました。
English: Evidence from that case was stolen... I expected as much.

Japanese: 罪門‥‥巡査の執念は、 よく知っていましたから。
English: I know how obsessive Officer Marshall can be.

Japanese: ‥‥２年前の、あの裁判。 やっぱり、不正があったんだね。
English: That trial... it really wasn't "fair," was it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... お姉ちゃんを信じてたのに！
English: I believed in you, Lana!

Japanese: どんなコトがあっても、真実だけを 見つめてくれる、って‥‥
English: I believed that no matter what happened, you'd always stick to the truth!

Japanese: ‥‥しかたないのです、あかね。
English: It couldn't be helped, Ema.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件で‥‥私は タマシイを売ったのですから。
English: At that trial two years ago... I sold my soul.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。 今日の法廷のケツロンは、１つ。
English: Well, all drama aside, the fact of the matter is

Japanese: 事件当日の、午後５時１５分。 《警察局で、殺人事件はなかった》
English: at 5:15, there was no murder at the Police Department.

Japanese: ウソだよね！　証人が‥‥ おキョウさんが言ったコト。
English: Tell me it's not true, Lana! What the witness... Ms. Starr said!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、多田敷さんを ナイフで刺した、なんて‥‥
English: About you stabbing Mr. Goodman with a knife!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... お姉ちゃん！ いいかげんにしてよ！
English: Lana! I don't understand!

Japanese: どうして教えてくれないの？
English: Why won't you tell us?

Japanese: ‥‥あかね。これは、 私だけのモンダイではないのです。
English: Ema... This doesn't involve just me.

Japanese: （ともえさん‥‥こんな苦しそうな カオをするの、初めてだ‥‥）
English: (I don't think I've ever seen Lana look so fazed before...)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥２年前。 私は警察局の一員でした。
English: It's true. I was a member of the police force two years ago.

Japanese: すごかったんですよ、お姉ちゃん。
English: She was amazing!

Japanese: ガント局長とお姉ちゃんのチーム。 今でも刑事課の伝説なんですから！
English: They still talk about all the cases she and Chief Gant cracked together!

Japanese: 巌徒局長‥‥？
English: Chief Gant?

Japanese: 当時は、警察局副長でありながら 捜査の現場にいたのです。
English: Then he was the Vice-Head of Criminal Affairs, but he still worked the crime scenes.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈は‥‥私にとって、 アコガレの捜査官だったのです。
English: Damon Gant... he was everything I aspired to be.

Japanese: ふたりでコンビを組んで、スカッと カイケツ！　カッコよかったなあ。
English: They were the best team ever! They solved crimes before the reports could even be filed!

Japanese: （ジマンのお姉さん、か‥‥ うれしそうだな、あかねちゃん）
English: (Ema really idolizes her big sister.)

Japanese: でも‥‥今は、主席検事ですよね。 何があったんですか？
English: But now you're Chief Prosecutor... What happened?

Japanese: ‥‥私はもともと、 検事を志望していたのです。
English: I always planned on becoming a prosecutor. The reason I became a detective was...

Japanese: 警察局で捜査の現場を学んで、 転向するつもりだったんだよね！
English: to gain experience investigating crime scenes, so you could later use that experience in court.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》の解決で、 巌徒のチカラは決定的なモノに。
English: Gant's help in the SL-9 case was critical in its resolution. After that,

Japanese: 彼は、警察局長に就任して‥‥私の 検事局への異動を決めたのです。
English: he became Chief of Police, and arranged my transfer to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （２年前の“捜査”‥‥ もう少し、詳しく聞いてみよう）
English: (Maybe I should ask more about this "investigation" of theirs two years ago.)

Japanese: ２年前‥‥私は、副主席捜査官と して、青影を追っていました。
English: Two years ago I was second-in- command of the detectives investigating Darke.

Japanese: “副主席”‥‥すると当然、 主席は‥‥巌徒　海慈、ですか。
English: "Second-in-command"? That means the Chief Detective was, no doubt, Damon Gant?

Japanese: 私と巌徒捜査官は、オフィスも共有 して、捜査に取り組んでいました。
English: Yes. Detective Gant and I shared the same office, and the same investigations.

Japanese: （巌徒局長と、オフィスまで いっしょだったのか‥‥）
English: (They even had the same office...!)

Japanese: 私たちの下についたのも、 みな優秀な捜査官でした。
English: We led a team of the best detectives on the force:

Japanese: 捜査責任者の多田敷捜査官。 罪門　恭介に、市ノ谷　響華‥‥
English: Detective Goodman, whose case it was, Jake Marshall and Angel Starr.

Japanese: 特に罪門は、初めて弟さんと組んで ‥‥はりきっていました。
English: It was the first time Marshall worked with his brother. He was quite... "gung ho."

Japanese: 青影　丈は、まちがいなく 連続殺人事件のハンニンでした。
English: Without a doubt, Joe Darke was the serial killer.

Japanese: 私たちは、本人に任意同行を求めて 証拠をつかもうと必死だった‥‥
English: We asked him to come in for questioning. We were desperate for evidence.

Japanese: そんなとき。 あの事件は起こったのです。
English: That was when the last murder took place.

Japanese: （あかねちゃんの“事件”‥‥！）
English: (When he tried to murder Ema...)

Japanese: あたしを助けようとした 罪門検事さんが、青影に‥‥
English: Prosecutor Marshall was trying to save me from Darke...

Japanese: あの事件‥‥第一発見者は、 この私自身だったのです。
English: You see, the first person who happened upon the scene of the crime... was me.

Japanese: ‥‥！ （初めて聞くぞ‥‥その情報は） ...!
English: (Now you tell us...)

Japanese: あの日。取調べは、巌徒捜査官と 罪門　直斗検事が担当しました。
English: Detective Gant and Prosecutor Marshall were the ones questioning Darke that day.

Japanese: 捜査は大詰めを迎えていて‥‥ 青影もアセっていたのでしょう。
English: The investigation was in its final stages, when Darke must have panicked.

Japanese: 巌徒捜査官たちのスキをついて、 取調室から逃走したのです。
English: He waited until Gant and Marshall let their guards down, then fled the room.

Japanese: 青影が逃げこんだのは‥‥ 巌徒捜査官と私のオフィスでした。
English: From there, he ran straight to... the office shared by Detective Gant and myself.

Japanese: ‥‥そこに、 あたしがいたんだね‥‥
English: That's where he found me.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥最初に かけつけたんですか？　現場に。
English: So you were the first person to run to the scene, Lana?

Japanese: そのようです。
English: It appears so.

Japanese: 私は、取調べには立ち会わず、 資料の整理をしていました。
English: I was filing some papers while Gant and Marshall were questioning Darke.

Japanese: オフィスに戻ると‥‥３人の人影が 倒れて、血のニオイがしました。
English: When I returned to my office, I saw three bodies on the floor and smelled blood.
Three bodies?

Japanese: 殺害された罪門　直斗検事と、 キゼツした、あかねちゃんと‥‥
English: Prosecutor Marshall, the victim, Ema, who had passed out,

Japanese: ハンニンの青影　丈です。
English: and the suspect, Joe Darke.

Japanese: 格闘の際に‥‥罪門検事は、 最後の抵抗をしたようです。
English: During the struggle, it seems Mr. Marshall stuck a final blow before he died.

Japanese: 青影は現場で、軽い脳しんとうを 起こして倒れていました。
English: Joe Darke had incurred a minor concussion, and lay unconscious.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、あなたは？
English: What did you do?

Japanese: さすがの私も、パニックに なってしまって‥‥
English: To be honest, I panicked.

Japanese: とにかく、あかねを現場から 運びだして、介抱しました。
English: I picked up Ema, carried her out of the room and just held her.

Japanese: （そりゃそうだよな‥‥ 妹さんが襲われたんだから）
English: (Can't blame her, after all her sister must have gone through.)

Japanese: そして‥‥青影を、 緊急タイホしたのです。
English: After that, I placed Darke under immediate arrest.

Japanese: 弁護士と検事が、法廷外で 証拠品を論じるのは、タブーです。
English: Attorneys and prosecutors have no business showing evidence outside court.

Japanese: これより、あなたと証拠品に ついて話すコトは、拒否します。
English: It's taboo. Especially when the interests of both parties are involved.

Japanese: （‥‥これまでにない、 ホンキの拒絶を感じるぞ）
English: (She really means it...)

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Lana...

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 24 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥そう、ですか。 局長が、あかねを任意同行‥‥
English: I see. So the Chief asked Ema to come in for questioning...

Japanese: 考えてもしかたありません。 明日は、最後の法廷です。
English: It's no use thinking about it. Tomorrow's the final day in court.

Japanese: ぼくは、ぼくにできるコトをする ‥‥そう思って、ここに来ました。
English: I'm committed to doing everything I can to defend you, which is why I'm here.

Japanese: でも。私にはもう、 お話しできることは‥‥
English: But I've already told you all I can...

Japanese: この２日間。あなたは‥‥
English: What you've told me over these past couple of days...

Japanese: 何も話してくれませんでした。 ダイジなことは、何ひとつ。
English: is absolutely nothing. Not a single, useful thing.

Japanese: ‥‥そうでしょうか。 １つ、あるではありませんか。
English: Really? I believe I did mention something quite important.

Japanese: 最初からハッキリ言っています。
English: Something I told you right at the beginning.

Japanese: 《多田敷捜査官を 刺したのは、この私》と。
English: I said that I was the one who stabbed Detective Goodman.

Japanese: あなたの、そのコトバのウラに ひそむ、“ある人物”‥‥
English: You know, I think I've finally figured it out...

Japanese: ぼくにも、やっと 見えてきた気がするんですよ。
English: Who it is you're hiding behind those words.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 千尋さんも、いい弁護士を育てた ものね。‥‥うらやましいわ。
English: Mia did a good job mentoring you. I'm rather jealous...

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! やはり‥‥御剣くんの 言うとおり、でしたわね。
English: It seems Edgeworth was right.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣‥‥ですか？
English: Edgeworth...?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥信じこんだら最後、 決してあきらめない。
English: Once you're convinced you know something, no one can persuade you otherwise.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤッカイなオトコ。
English: "Thick headed" is the term he used, I believe.

Japanese: （最後に、ともえさんから すべてを聞き出すんだ！）
English: (Now's my chance to get her to tell me the rest of the story!)

Japanese: 最初から、フシギでした。
English: I have to admit I was more than a little perplexed at first.

Japanese: “自分がやった”と繰り返すのに、 決定的な証拠は、どこにもない。
English: You insisted you "did it," yet there was no incriminating evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。あなたは、 “話そうとしない”のではない。
English: That's when it hit me. It's not that you're unwilling to tell the truth,

Japanese: “話すことができない”のです。 ‥‥ある人物のために。
English: it's that you're incapable of doing so, because of a certain individual.

Japanese: おもしろいですね。 “ある人物”‥‥ですか。
English: What an intriguing notion. A certain, "individual," you say?

Japanese: 私が、その人物を かばっている‥‥とでも？
English: So you think I'm... protecting this person?

Japanese: どうでしょうか。むしろ‥‥ 恐れているのかもしれません。
English: Protecting? No. I think "afraid of" is more like it.

Japanese: ！ ! あなたは、恐らく‥‥その人物から “口止め”されているのです。
English: If I'm not mistaken, the person in question may have persuaded you to silence.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 成歩堂弁護士。ハッキリ お聞きして、よろしいでしょうか。
English: For argument's sake, Mr. Wright, whom may I ask is this person you're speaking of?

Japanese: 私が“恐れている”人物。 ‥‥どなたのコト、なのかしら？
English: The one I am supposedly so "frightened" of? What is this person's name?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが“恐れている”人物。 いかがでしょうか！
English: The person whom you fear... is this!

Japanese: 正直に申し上げて‥‥ もっと、恐ろしい人物がいますわ。
English: To be honest, there's someone a bit scarier.

Japanese: ど、どなたですかッ！
English: W-who would that be?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたです、モチロン。
English: Why you, of course.

Japanese: え。
English: Me?

Japanese: こんなデタラメを言うために、 わざわざこんなトコロまで‥‥？
English: Yes. You seriously believe what you're saying, don't you? Now that's scary.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: I, uh...

Japanese: ウソつきは、ドロボーの始まり。 今夜は、おとなりで休みますか？
English: You seem to have the makings of a criminal in you, what with all your fallacious accusations. Care to spend tonight in the next cell?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥トモエさんの視線の ほうが、よっぽどコワいよ‥‥）
English: (If you ask me, you're the scary one...)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、ともえさん。
English: Well, Ms. Skye?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あなたがコトバをかわしている のは‥‥主席検事です。
English: Mr. Wright. You are addressing the Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: そのジジツを、お忘れなきよう。
English: Do not forget your place.

Japanese: （‥‥カンタンにしゃべる 気はない、ということか）
English: (I take it she's still not ready to spill the beans.)

Japanese: 申しわけありませんが‥‥
English: My apologies.

Japanese: もう少し、詳しく 聞かせていただけるかしら。
English: Could you please tell me a bit more about the circumstances?

Japanese: ２年前まで、いっしょにシゴトを。 ソンケイする捜査官、でしたわね。
English: We were partners until two years ago. I respected him as a detective.

Japanese: もし、巌徒局長がソンケイできる 人物ならば‥‥なぜ。
English: Assuming he is respectable, then tell me something...

Japanese: 彼は、自らの罪を かくそうとするのでしょうか？
English: Why would he try to hide his crimes?
His "crimes"...?

Japanese: これから‥‥あなたと御剣は、 罪を問われることになります。
English: Both you and Edgeworth will be brought before a board of inquiry for what you did.

Japanese: 証拠品の“いんぺい” そして、“ねつ造”‥‥
English: Specifically, hiding and forging evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥捜査官として、あっては ならない、重罪ですから。
English: Of course, these are serious offenses...

Japanese: なぜ、そこに巌徒局長の 名前がないのですか？
English: Why is it, though, that Chief Gant's name was never mentioned?

Japanese: きょ、局長が‥‥？
English: Chief Gant...?

Japanese: 御剣は、ねつ造のジジツを 知りませんでした。
English: Edgeworth didn't know the truth behind the forgery.

Japanese: その証拠をジッサイに 操作できたのは‥‥
English: The only party who could have possible investigated that evidence was...

Japanese: 私ですわね。副主席捜査官で、 あの事件を担当していましたから。
English: Me. I had access because I was second in command of that investigation.

Japanese: そして、もう１人。主席捜査官の、 巌徒　海慈がいます。
English: Yes you, but also one other: Damon Gant.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... そこまで言うのならば‥‥ 見せていただけますか？
English: If you intend to accuse Chief Gant, you'll need more than just words.

Japanese: 巌徒局長が、あの事件の証拠に 不正を行ったという証拠を！
English: Show me proof that Chief Gant falsified evidence in that case!

Japanese: ‥‥局長の不正を立証する証拠。 いかがでしょうか！
English: You want proof of the Chief's wrongdoings? Here it is!

Japanese: その証拠‥‥局長以外の人物の 不正を立証しておりますわ。
English: That "evidence" proves someone is doing something wrong all right, but it's not the Chief.

Japanese: ‥‥つい、さっき。 局長室の金庫から発見しました。
English: I just found this in a safe in the Chief's office.

Japanese: この“ツボのカケラ”と、 “布きれ”‥‥
English: This jar piece, and this strip of cloth.

Japanese: あなたなら、おわかりですね？ 《ＳＬ９号事件》の証拠品です。
English: Do you know what these are? They're pieces of evidence from the SL-9 Incident!

Japanese: さ‥‥さあ。私には‥‥
証拠品をかくしていたのは、 巌徒局長自身だったのです。
English: The person concealing evidence was none other than Chief Gant himself.

Japanese: なぜ！　その罪を、あなたが かばおうとしてるんですかッ！
English: Now tell me! Why are you taking all the blame for him!?
Touché, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 私は、巌徒のコトバに 逆らうことができないのです。
English: It's as you surmised. I cannot disobey the Chief's orders...

Japanese: ‥‥たとえ、何があっても！
English: even if it means being found guilty for murder.

Japanese: いったい‥‥なぜですか？
English: Why not?

Japanese: 言えるわけがありません。
English: Come now, Mr. Wright. You can't possibly expect me to be able to tell you that.

Japanese: だから。３日前の、あの日も‥‥ 私は、協力するしかなかった。
English: Three days ago... I had no choice but to cooperate.

Japanese: （多田敷捜査官の“殺害”‥‥！）
English: (In the murder of Detective Goodman!)

Japanese: “協力”というより、“命令”。 そのほうが、よりセイカクかしら。
English: Or perhaps I should say, "follow orders." Yes, that's more accurate than "cooperate."

Japanese: ‥‥詳しい事情は話せません。
English: Although I can't tell you the details,

Japanese: あの日、私は‥‥ ある“命令”を受けたのです。
English: I can say that I was given an order that day.

Japanese: 《多田敷　道夫の死体を かたづけてもらいたい》
English: "I need you to dispose of Bruce Goodman's body."

Japanese: 《死体は、御剣　怜侍の クルマのトランクの中だよ》
English: "You'll find it inside the trunk of Miles Edgeworth's car."

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥そうだったんですね。
English: Just as I suspected. Despite what everyone believes...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官を殺害したのは‥‥ あなたじゃなかった！
English: you were not the one who murdered Detective Goodman!

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: Correct.

Japanese: 私は、御剣くんのクルマで 死体を運び出そうとしました。
English: I was trying to take the body out of Edgeworth's car.

Japanese: ‥‥トランクのフタは コワされていました。
English: The trunk was broken.

Japanese: 死体をカクニンしたとき‥‥
English: I discovered that murder weapon while inspecting the body.

Japanese: 胸に刺さったままのナイフ‥‥ あの“凶器”が目に入ったのです。 “凶器”‥‥ この、御剣のナイフですか？
English: The murder weapon... You mean, Edgeworth's knife?

Japanese: そうではありません。
English: No.

Japanese: 私が見たとき刺さっていたのは ‥‥このナイフだったのです。
English: When I found the body, this was the knife stuck in it.

Japanese: （《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ 殺人犯・青影　丈のナイフか！）
English: (The knife from the SL-9 Incident... serial killer Joe Darke's knife!)

Japanese: ‥‥そのナイフを、そのままに しておくことはできなかった。
English: I couldn't just leave that knife in him,

Japanese: それで、それを死体から抜いて、 別のナイフを‥‥‥刺したんです！
English: so I took it out and stabbed him with another knife.

Japanese: それが‥‥この、御剣のナイフ というワケですか。
English: That would be Edgeworth's knife?

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: 死体とはいえ、ナイフを刺すのは 本当に、おそろしかった‥‥
English: Even though he was already dead, my hands were shaking at the thought of stabbing him.

Japanese: だから‥‥そのとき。 手を切ってしまったんです。
English: That's why I ended up cutting my hand.

Japanese: それが‥‥その、 右手の包帯、ですか。
English: And that is the reason for the bandage on your right hand?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。被害者のクツも よごしてしまったようですけど。
English: Yes. It seems that I got blood on the victim's shoes as well.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And then...

Japanese: ‥‥その瞬間を、彼女に 目撃されてしまったのです。
English: she saw me just as I plunged the knife in.

Japanese: （おキョウさん、か‥‥）
English: (Ms. Starr...)

Japanese: なぜ‥‥そこまでして、 このナイフをかくしたんですか？
English: Why did you need to hide Darke's knife so badly?

Japanese: ‥‥２年前。なんとか、あの事件を 終わらせることができました。
English: It took a lot of work to finally close the Darke case two years ago.

Japanese: もう、二度と。 捜査をされたくなかったのです。
English: It was over with. I didn't ever want it to be opened again.

Japanese: たとえ、どんな手を使っても‥‥ それを阻止するつもりでした。
English: My intent was to prevent that by whatever means possible.

Japanese: だから‥‥あのナイフを かくそうとしたんですか。
English: So... you hid Darke's knife?

Japanese: 《捜査官を刺殺した“凶器”が 青影事件の証拠品だった》‥‥
English: The weapon used to stab the detective was evidence in the Joe Darke case...

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥まちがいなく、 マスコミは飛びつくでしょう。
English: If word got out, which it would, the reporters would have a field day with that.

Japanese: そこで、ナイフにスカーフを 巻いて‥‥かくしたんですね。
English: So you wrapped the knife in your scarf, and hid it.

Japanese: 御剣のクルマの‥‥排気パイプに。
English: In Edgeworth's exhaust pipe...

Japanese: そう。そして、妹に‥‥ あかねに、電話をかけたのです。
English: Right. Then I called my sister,

Japanese: 事情を説明して、マフラーの中の 証拠品をかくしてほしい、と‥‥
English: to tell her what happened, and to ask her to hide the knife that was inside my muffler.

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃんに？
English: You asked Ema?

Japanese: ‥‥できれば、警察関係者には 知られたくなかったのです。
English: I didn't want anyone on the force to know about this.

Japanese: （だから‥‥あかねちゃんは、 最初から知っていたのか‥‥）
English: (That would explain why Ema is so confident...)

Japanese: （ともえさんの“無実”を！）
English: (about Lana's innocence!)

Japanese: 電話といえば‥‥ 悪い予感は、していたのです。
English: Speaking of phone calls, I had a bad feeling about one of them that day.

Japanese: 悪い予感‥‥？
English: A bad feeling?

Japanese: じつは‥‥巌徒から あの指令を受けたとき。
English: The truth is, after I received those orders from Chief Gant.

Japanese: 私は、すぐに電話をかけたのです。 ‥‥罪門巡査に。
English: The first thing I did was make a phone call. A phone call to Patrolman Jake Marshall.

Japanese: ざ、罪門さんに‥‥？ いったい、なぜ！
English: To Marshall? Why on earth would you call him?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》の主任捜査官が、 巌徒によって、殺害された。
English: The lead investigator for the SL-9 Incident had been murdered.

Japanese: そのジジツを、なんとしても かくしとおさなければならない。
English: I wanted that fact to be kept hidden, and I needed help.

Japanese: たよれるのは、彼しかいない‥‥ あのとき、私はそう思ったのです。
English: He was the only other person I could trust. Or at least, I thought I could trust him at the time.

Japanese: しかし‥‥彼は、 予想外の行動に出ました。
English: However, it seems that after I spoke to him he went off on an escapade of his own...

Japanese: あ！　ま、まさか‥‥
English: Oh! You mean...

Japanese: 彼は、事件を“死なせない”ため、 彼自身の計画を実行した‥‥
English: Not wanting the case to die, he decided to take things into his own hands.

Japanese: （多田敷捜査官に変装して‥‥ 証拠品を盗み出そうとした！）
English: (He disguised himself as Detective Goodman and tried to steal the evidence!)

Japanese: 彼は、あの日。多田敷捜査官の ＩＤを盗んでいたものの‥‥
English: He had already stolen the ID card...

Japanese: 証拠保管室へ侵入する決心が、 つかなかったようなのです。
English: but it seems he still hadn't made up his mind to break into the evidence room.

Japanese: でも。私の電話によって、 その迷いがなくなった、とか。
English: After my phone call, any remaining doubts he had must have disappeared.

Japanese: あなたの電話が‥‥証拠保管室の 事件を引き起こしてしまった‥‥！
English: So your phone call caused the incident in the evidence room!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士。 お話しできることは、以上です。
English: I'm afraid that's all I can tell you.

Japanese: ともえさん‥‥
English: But Lana...

Japanese: あなたは、尊敬にあたいします。 捜査官としても、弁護士としても。
English: You've earned my respect, Mr. Wright. Both as a defense attorney and an investigator.

Japanese: でも‥‥おねがいです。
English: Now please...

Japanese: 明日の法廷では、これ以上の追求は しないでほしいのです！
English: Don't pursue this any further in court tomorrow!

Japanese: （明日の法廷‥‥ 彼女の闇を払う方法は、１つだ）
English: (Tomorrow's trial... There's only one way to drive off Lana's demons...)

Japanese: （‥‥すべてに、ケリをつける！）
English: (I've got to get to the bottom of everything!)

Japanese: （多田敷捜査官を殺害した、犯人。 そして‥‥２年前の、あの日）
English: (Detective Goodman's real murderer...)

Japanese: （‥‥局長室で、いったい 何が起こったのか‥‥？）
English: (and what went down in the Chief's office two years ago...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: （‥‥ここまできて、 証拠品を見せてもしかたがない）
English: (Showing evidence isn't going to do me any good now.)

Japanese: （ともえさんから、 すべての話を聞き出すんだ！）
English: (I've got to get Lana to tell me everything she's hiding!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたは日々、進化している ようですね、成歩堂弁護士。
English: You're evolving by leaps and bounds.

Japanese: ‥‥とても、いい目をしています。
English: I'm starting to see the hunger in your eyes.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察局・エントランス
English: February 24 Police Department Entrance

Japanese: よお。バンビーナ。
English: Howdy, Bambina.

Japanese: あ‥‥ざ、罪門さん！
English: Oh, Mr. Marshall!

Japanese: 今朝、目がさめたとき‥‥こんな １日になるとは思わなかったよ。
English: I never thought things'd turn out this way when I woke up this morning.

Japanese: 人生なんて、ケ・セラ・セラ。 わからないものさ、バンビーナ。
English: Que será será. You never know where life'll lead you, eh Bambina?

Japanese: ビリーがつけた花が枯れたのが、 すべてを予言していたのかもな。
English: I should've known my luck had run out when ol' Billy dried up this morning.

Japanese: びりー‥‥？
English: Billy...?

Japanese: サボテンじゃないかな、たぶん。
English: Must be his pet cactus.

Japanese: あの‥‥どこか、行くんですか？
English: Say, where are you headed?

Japanese: ちょっと検事局まで、 事情聴取に、ね。
English: Just over to the Prosecutor's Office for a little interrogation.

Japanese: 《任意出頭》というカタチだが‥‥ もう、戻ってくるコトはないさ。
English: It's a "voluntary" appearance, but we all know I won't be coming back.

Japanese: ワルいが、今日は 証拠保管室には入れねえぜ。
English: Sorry, but you can't go in the evidence room today, pardner.

Japanese: 罪門さん‥‥‥ どうして、あんなコトを！
English: But Mr. Marshall... Why did you do it? Why do prospectors head west?

Japanese: さあな‥‥あの事件だけは、 コタエを知りたかったのかな。
English: If ever there was a case I needed to know the truth about, it was that one.

Japanese: ‥‥すみませんけど。
English: Before you turn yourself in, Mr. Marshall...

Japanese: 検事局へ出頭する前に、 ハナシを聞かせていただけますか？
English: would you mind telling us exactly what happened?

Japanese: フン‥‥‥今日は ステーキ弁当はナシ、のようだ。
English: Hmph... Looks like I won't be getting a steak lunch today.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: So there you have it.

Japanese: オレの話は、こんなトコロだ。 楽しんでくれたかい、カウボーイ。
English: That's my story. Did you enjoy it, pardner?

Japanese: ‥‥参考になりました。
English: It was certainly... enlightening.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、ハッキリわかった。
English: There's one thing for sure I found out in court today.

Japanese: オレの敵は、御剣のボウヤじゃ なかったんだ、ってね。
English: That boy Edgeworth isn't my enemy.

Japanese: ２年前。不正な証拠で有罪判決を モギ取ったのは、あの男だった。
English: He was the one who used falsified evidence to get a guilty verdict,

Japanese: しかし。証拠をそろえて、戦術を 組み上げ‥‥与えたヤツがいた。
English: but someone else was the one who gave him that evidence and planned everything.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈だ。
English: That someone is Damon Gant.

Japanese: まあ‥‥もうすぐ、巡査ですら なくなるオトコのタワゴトさ。
English: Don't believe me? Well I don't blame you. I won't even be a patrolman after today.

Japanese: 一度、組んでみたかったよ。 科学捜査官になったアンタと、ね。
English: Too bad I won't be around to work with you... when you become a real scientific investigator.

Japanese: それじゃあな、バンビーナ！
English: Adios Bambina!

Japanese: ‥‥追い出されちゃいましたね。
English: Looks like we aren't welcome.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥あの事件は、 終わっていないんだよ、きっと。
English: It seems that case isn't over with yet, after all.

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 巌徒局長は、“現場”を 調べられることを、拒否した。
English: Chief Gant denied our request to search the "crime scene."

Japanese: そこには‥‥かならず。 何か“理由”があるはずだよ。
English: That means there must be a reason he doesn't want us looking around in there.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ！ あの部屋には、手がかりが‥‥
English: You mean like, a clue!?

Japanese: （‥‥何か、ないかな。 局長室を調べる方法‥‥）
English: (There's got to be a way we can get inside the Chief's office...)

Japanese: ‥‥だから、自分。 あれほど言ったッスのに。
English: Look, pal! If I've told you once I've told you a thousand times...

Japanese: 局長室はダメだと。やめておけと。 それなのに、アンタがムリヤリ‥‥
English: The Chief's office is off limits! But no, you just had to go sneaking in there like that, didn't you!?

Japanese: クビをカクゴしてたんじゃ なかったんですか？
English: I thought you said you didn't care anymore if you were fired.

Japanese: ジッサイにクビになるのが こんなにショックなモノならば‥‥
English: Yeah, but if I knew it'd be like this,

Japanese: カクゴなんか しなきゃよかったッス！
English: I never would've said it!

Japanese: （局長室に眠っていた “証拠品”‥‥）
English: (Now that I've seen the evidence Chief Gant was hiding in his office...)

Japanese: （やっと、見えてきた気がする。 いったい、なぜ‥‥）
English: (I think I'm finally starting to get the picture...)

Japanese: （彼女が、不自然なチンモクを 守りとおしてきたのか‥‥？）
English: (It's hard to believe anyone could keep quiet about it all this time...)

Japanese: とにかく！　自分はもう一度、 局長とかけあってくるッス！
English: Anyway, you listenin' to me!? I'm gonna try to smooth things over with the Chief again!

Japanese: じゃっ！
English: Later, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥その後、あかねちゃんから 連絡が入った。
English: After that, I heard from Ema. She said the police want to ask her some questions,

Japanese: ‥‥任意同行の上、調書をとる ので、今日は帰れない、と‥‥
English: so she'll be busy for the rest of the day...

Japanese: 今日もうごめいてますね、 タイホくん。
English: The Blue Badger is still writhing around today.

Japanese: “おどっている”と 言ってあげようよ、せめて。
English: Let's give him the benefit of the doubt and say he's dancing.

Japanese: （“おどっている”といえば‥‥）
English: (Speaking of "dancing"...)

Japanese: （警察局全体が、おどり狂ってるん だよな‥‥ここ数日）
English: (The whole Police Department has been dancing about like crazy since the other day...)

Japanese: ‥‥デンチ、抜いて おきませんか、アレ。
English: Can I take out his batteries?

Japanese: これ以上、泳がせておくのは キケンだと思います！
English: I just can't help but feel he's going to do something naughty!

Japanese: 刑事さんたちが見える。 みんな、忙しそうだ。
English: The detectives in there look pretty busy.

Japanese: あのドアの向こうで、 今、まさに！
English: Just imagine! Right now...

Japanese: くり広げられているんですね。 ‥‥“刑事ドラマ”がッ！
English: Behind those doors...! A police drama in action!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
English: (Somehow the thought fails to excite me...)

Japanese: 掲示板には、おなじみの ポスターが張ってある。
English: The usual wanted posters are hanging up on the bulletin board here.

Japanese: 《このカオに、ピン！　と来たら １１０番》‥‥か。
English: «Do you know this face!? If you do, dial 911!»

Japanese: ‥‥でも。 こういうポスターのカオって‥‥
English: You know, Mr. Wright, I've always thought it was kind of funny...

Japanese: なーんか、 ピン！　と来ないんですよねー。
English: I've never seen anyone who looked like the people in these posters. They hardly even look human!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （たしかに、マチガイではないけど ‥‥ミョーにモヤモヤするな）
English: (She has a point...)

Japanese: 《真冬の防犯フェア・開催中！》 ‥‥って、書いてあります。
English: The banner here is announcing the "Crime Fighting Campaign."

Japanese: 大ざっぱなスローガンだな。
English: Nice slogan...

Japanese: ルミノール試薬とか、 安く売ってないかなあ。
English: I wonder if they'll be selling fingerprinting sets.

Japanese: ‥‥バーゲンセールじゃ ないからね。
English: I don't think it's that kind of campaign.

Japanese: 家庭に１本あると、 安心だと思うけどなあ。
English: What family wouldn't want a set at home?

Japanese: ‥‥サツジン事件が 起きたとき、とか。
English: It's good for finding out who snuck into the cookie jar.

Japanese: 安心のためなら、他のものを 買ったほうがいいと思うよ。
English: I think most families can figure that out without the extra help.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　パトカーの色、 なんでシロクロか知ってますか？
English: Mr. Wright! Did you know why patrol cars are painted black and white?

Japanese: 知らないよ。どうして？
English: No idea. Why?

Japanese: あたしね。アレ、おむすびを デザインしたんだと思うんですよ。
English: Well, I think they're designed after a panda!

Japanese: お、おむすび‥‥？
English: A panda...?

Japanese: まあ。カガク的なコンキョは ありませんけどね。
English: Not that I have scientific proof. It's just a theory.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、 どうしてそう思うの？
English: Um... do you mind me asking how you came up with that theory?

Japanese: 小学生のころ、遠足で見学に来た とき、パッとヒラめいたんです！
English: It was when I was on a school trip! I saw a patrol car and it came to me!

Japanese: ‥‥お弁当の時間に。
English: We had just been at the zoo, see...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 今度、パンダを見に行くときは、 サンドイッチにしたほうがいいよ。
English: What about zebras? Or did they not have those at your zoo?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　警察のマーク、 どういうイミか、知ってますか？
English: I always get excited when I come to the police station.

Japanese: 知らないよ。なんなの？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: あたしね。コレ、宇宙ステーション だと思ってたんですよ。ムカシ。
English: It just feels like I've jumped into a movie.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 小さいコロの話ですけど。 ホラ、カタチが似てるでしょ？
English: You know, with all the police and criminals.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。見たことないから。 ‥‥宇宙ステーションは。
English: W-well, I don't know if this is all that exciting.

Japanese: でも、“それはあり得ない”って 気づいたんです。去年。
English: Sure it is! Look at those two officers over there.

Japanese: だって、“ポリスステーション” ですからね。“警察署”は。
English: They're probably talking about the latest bust!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... もっと早く気づきたかったね。 それも、別のカタチで。
English: Funny, I thought they were talking about the weather.

Japanese: おまわりさんがケーレイしている。 相手は刑事さんだろうか。
English: Look, that patrolman is saluting that other guy. He must be a detective!

Japanese: そこで、手錠をかけたんだが‥‥ ヤッコさん、言うわけだ。
English: So I cuff the lout, right? Then you know what he says?

Japanese: 『バカ野郎、ハンニンはあっちだ！ オレは、オトリ捜査官だ！』
English: "What are you, a moron? I'm an undercover agent!"

Japanese: あちゃあ。‥‥でも、 よくあるコトでありますッ！
English: Oh, man. I hate it when that happens!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ケーレイじゃなくて、 失敗談に共感してるみたいですね。
English: I guess he wasn't saluting, he was just listening to how his friend messed up.

Japanese: ‥‥いいコンビになりそうだ。
English: They make a good pair.

Japanese: ‥‥優秀な検事だったよ。オレとは ちがって、アタマがよくてなァ。
English: I got the looks, but he got the brains. He was one of the best prosecutors around.

Japanese: 当時、捜査官になったばかりの オレと組んで、ひとシゴト‥‥
English: I had just made detective when it went down.

Japanese: そんな矢先の事件だったぜ。
English: It was our first case together...

Japanese: いくつ、だったんですか？ 弟さん‥‥
English: How old was he... your brother?

Japanese: ‥‥当時、２７才だったよ。
English: He was 27 at the time.

Japanese: まさにあの日、最高の栄誉を つかんだばかりでね‥‥
English: He was awarded the highest honor that very day.

Japanese: “最高の栄誉”‥‥？ それって、もしかして‥‥
English: The... "highest honor"? You don't mean...

Japanese: 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》さ。
English: King of Prosecutors.

Japanese: （で、出たよ‥‥）
English: (Not that again...)

Japanese: そんなカオ、しないでくれよ。 検事にとっては、名誉なんだろ？
English: What are you looking at me like that for? That's an honor for a prosecutor!

Japanese: （罪門さん‥‥弟さんのコトに なると、やさしい顔になるな）
English: (Mr. Marshall must have really been close with his brother.)

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》が起こった日。 それって、まさか‥‥
English: The day the SL-9 Incident took place, that wasn't the same day as...

Japanese: そう。‥‥今回と同じ、 証拠品の《申し送り》の日だった。
English: That's right. It was the day of the evidence transferal.

Japanese: ‥‥！ そうだったんですか‥‥ ...!
English: Interesting...

Japanese: 朝から冷たい雨がふっていて‥‥ 夜には、カミナリが鳴りだした。
English: It was drizzling that morning, and by nightfall there was thunder.

Japanese: ‥‥あれから、２年。
English: I can't believe two years have gone by already.

Japanese: オレは、事件を死なせないために 証拠品を奪おうとしたのさ。
English: I tried to steal the evidence so the case wouldn't die.

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ダレかが、 それを阻止しようとしたんだ）
English: (Apparently someone tried to stop you.)

Japanese: （多田敷さんは殺害されて‥‥ 保管庫はカラッポになっていた）
English: (Detective Goodman was murdered... and the evidence locker was empty.)

Japanese: ‥‥あの裁判は、 最初からアヤしかった。
English: Something was fishy about that trial from the beginning.

Japanese: それは、恐らく‥‥捜査にあたった 全員が感じていたはずだ。
English: It wasn't just me either. All the detectives thought so.

Japanese: “アヤしかった”というのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean, "fishy"?

Japanese: オレたちの捜査とくいちがう “ジジツ”が報告された。
English: Some of the facts reported were inconsistent with the evidence we found.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥凶器だ。
English: For example... the murder weapon.

Japanese: “凶器”‥‥この、 先の折れたナイフですか？
English: The murder weapon? You mean... that switchblade knife with the broken tip?

Japanese: たしかに、そいつは 青影　丈のものだったさ。
English: That was Joe Darke's, all right.

Japanese: しかし‥‥最初の解剖記録では、 ひとつのギモンが記されていた。
English: But... in the initial autopsy report, a question was raised.
A question...?

Japanese: あのナイフは‥‥被害者の傷口と ビミョーな“ズレ”があった。
English: The blade of the knife was not a perfect match with the wound the victim sustained.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What does that mean?

Japanese: 本当の凶器は、あのナイフじゃ なかった‥‥そういう可能性さ。
English: It means there is a good chance that knife was not the murder weapon.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! しかし‥‥提出された記録からは、 その“可能性”は消えていた。
English: However, in the report that was finally submitted, that possibility had been erased.

Japanese: （それが‥‥“インペイ”と “ねつ造”だったのか‥‥？）
English: (Could the facts have been concealed with forged evidence...?)

Japanese: そう。あの事件は、オレたちに 大きなキズを残したのさ。
English: That case left behind scars on all of us.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》が残した、 “ツメ跡”‥‥‥
English: The "scars" that the SL-9 Incident left behind...

Japanese: この事件には、ウラがある‥‥ 裁判のあと、思い知らされたよ。
English: There was something going on behind the scenes in that case. We all knew that later.

Japanese: 捜査に関係した捜査官が １人をのぞいて全員、整理された。
English: Every detective involved in that investigation, save one, was... taken care of.

Japanese: おキョウはクビを切られ、 オレは小部屋に押しこまれた。
English: Ms. Starr was fired and I was demoted and boxed away in a tiny room.

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官は‥‥ “整理”されなかったんですか？
English: What about Detective Goodman?

Japanese: アイツまで切っちまったら、 総監クラスの耳に入っちまう。
English: If they did something to him too, the commissioners would get suspicious.

Japanese: ヤツらには、ツゴウが 悪かったんだろうぜ。
English: No, they were careful enough not to be too obvious.

Japanese: “ヤツら”っていうのは‥‥
English: "They"...?

Japanese: いったい、ダレですか！
English: Who are you talking about!?

Japanese: ‥‥気を悪くするなよ、 バンビーナ。
English: Don't get upset, Bambina.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈と‥‥宝月　巴さ。
English: I mean Damon Gant... and Lana Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒　海慈主席捜査官と、 副主席の宝月　巴‥‥
English: Chief Detective Damon Gant and his second-in-command, Lana Skye...

Japanese: 警察局では知らない者のない 名コンビだったぜ。
English: There wasn't a person on the force who hadn't heard of that duo.

Japanese: 彼らが、そのキャリアに決定的な 王手をかけたのが、あの事件さ。
English: That case was the biggest step in both of their careers.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》のあと、彼女は 検事局へ移ったんですよね。
English: After the case ended, Lana transferred to the Prosecutor's Office, right?

Japanese: ああ。新局長に就任した、 巌徒　海慈が手配したのさ。
English: Yeah. Damon Gant, the new Chief of Police, arranged for that to happen.

Japanese: ‥‥検事局に異動してからさ。 彼女のようすがおかしくなった。
English: She's never been the same since she left.

Japanese: ？ ? 彼女を知っているヤツは、 クチをそろえて言ってるぜ。
English: Everyone who knew her said so.

Japanese: 『宝月主席検事は、捜査官だった ころとは別人だ』‥‥ってな。
English: "Chief Prosecutor Skye was totally different when she was a detective."

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥あかねちゃんも 言ってたな、そんなコトを）
English: (Now that he mentions it, Ema said something like that too...)

Japanese: いったい‥‥お姉ちゃんに 何があったんですか！
English: Tell me... what happened to my sister!

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンだが。 彼女の闇は深すぎてね。
English: Sorry, Bambina, but her secret is too well guarded.

Japanese: オレの手には負えなかったよ。
English: I never found out.

Japanese: （ともえさんの“ヒミツ”は‥‥ ２年前から始まっていたんだ）
English: (Lana's "secret"... It all started two years ago.)

Japanese: 罪門さん！　これ、 見ていただきたいんですけど‥‥
English: Mr. Marshall! Could you please have a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけど、バンビーナ。
English: Sorry, Bambina.

Japanese: オレはもう、捜査官でもなければ、 巡査ですらない。
English: I'm not a patrolman anymore, and I'm certainly not a detective.

Japanese: ならず者にして、おたずね者。 荒野をさすらう流れ者のお調子者。
English: I'm nothing more than a wanted man, now.

Japanese: もう、オレの目には 何もうつらないんだよ。
English: These old eyes of mine have seen too much as it is.

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんから情報を聞こう。 それで、調査は終了だ！
English: I need to hear what Lana has to say. Then I can finish up the investigation.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 24 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: 今日は、ダレもいないね。 ‥‥おキョウさんも。
English: No one's here today... Not even Ms. Starr.

Japanese: 証拠保管室のナゾが キョーレツでしたからねー。
English: Everyone's probably busy looking into what exactly went down in the evidence room.

Japanese: 刑事さんたち、あっちに 行ってるんじゃないかなあ。
English: That must be where the detectives are.

Japanese: でも、今日の法廷で ハッキリ立証したからね。
English: But we proved in court today that no one was murdered in the evidence room at 5:15 PM,

Japanese: 《事件当日の５時１５分、 証拠保管室で、事件はなかった》
English: on the day of the crime.

Japanese: それって、なんか‥‥１歩、 前進したような気がするケド。
English: Yeah. I thought we were finally making some headway in our case!

Japanese: ケッキョク、ともえさんが アヤシイ、ってコトになるかな。
English: But instead it looks like we just ended up making Lana look even more guilty.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Hang in there, Lana...

Japanese: （明日までに‥‥すべて、 コタエを見つけないと！）
English: (I've got to find all the answers by tomorrow!)

Japanese: ここは‥‥クルマの出口ですね。
English: This is where the cars leave the lot.

Japanese: 床のヤジルシを見ると、 “入口”みたいだけどね。
English: The arrow on the ground makes it look more like an entrance.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ どう見ても出口じゃないですか！
English: What are you talking about? It's plainly an exit!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥出口であると同時に、 入口でもある、というコトかな。
English: Well, maybe it's both. Kind of a dual purpose?

Japanese: あ！　それ！　あの有名な “ソータイセー理論”ですね！
English: Ah hah! The theory of relativity!

Japanese: え！　は、はあ‥‥
English: What? Uh...

Japanese: メモしとかないとね‥‥ あ！　ね。ね。成歩堂さん。
English: I've got to write this down. Ah! Hey, hey, Mr. Wright! Maybe you know...

Japanese: どこの国のヒトでしたっけ？ ソー・タイセーさんって。
English: Was Mr. Relativity, German? Or was he British?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないよ。
English: Mr. Relativity? Are you sure that was his name?

Japanese: あ。ハシゴですよ。
English: Ah hah! A ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: Um... That's a stepladder.

Japanese: どこが違うんですか？ カガク的に証明してください。
English: What's the difference? In scientific terms, please.

Japanese: か、カガク的‥‥
English: S-scientific huh?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ましょうよ、 成歩堂さん。
English: Look at the basic nature of the thing, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なぜだろう‥‥あまずっぱい キモチになってきた）
English: (This all seems so horribly familiar somehow...)

Japanese: お。こんなところに イミありげなドアが！
English: Look! A door! This must mean something!

Japanese: ドアに“イミ”なんてないよ。
English: I'm not sure that doors "mean" anything.

Japanese: クッ！　開きません！ ナゾのカギがかかってます！
English: No! It won't open! A mysterious lock!

Japanese: カギに“ナゾ”なんてないよ。
English: I fail to see what's mysterious about it.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。 もっと人生を楽しみましょうよ。
English: Mr. Wright... You need to learn to enjoy life more.

Japanese: 先に調査をすませようよ。
English: Let's finish our investigation first, shall we?

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ あそこから見たんですね‥‥事件。
English: So that's where Ms. Starr saw the incident from.

Japanese: 高いぶん、フツウよりも よく見えたかもしれないね。
English: You can probably see quite a lot from up there.

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥）
（“彼女が刺したのを見た”と いう証言も、ウソとは思えない）
English: (That probably means she wasn't lying when she saw Ms. Skye stab him...)

Japanese: そもそも‥‥警備員は ナニをしてたんだろう。
English: Where's the security guard, anyway?

Japanese: ええと。あたしが 聞いてきたトコロによると‥‥
English: Well, this is just something I heard...

Japanese: どうやら、おキョウさんのお茶を 買いに行ってたみたいです！
English: But apparently he went out to buy coffee for Ms. Starr.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (That woman is a force to be reckoned with...)

Japanese: これが‥‥モンダイの ドラム缶だね。
English: So, this is the famous oil drum.

Japanese: ちょっと、ケリ倒してみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well, no time like the present! I'll try to kick it over myself!

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Hii-yah!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ... な、泣かないでほしいな。 （ともえさん‥‥スゴいパワーだ）
English: Th-that's okay. Don't cry. (That Lana Skye must be a powerful woman...)

Japanese: これが‥‥あの “ついたて”ですね。
English: So, this is the famous divider.

Japanese: おキョウさんの証言のウソを 立証してくれたカベ、だったな。
English: It sure helped us knock a hole in that testimony yesterday.

Japanese: あるイミ、このカベこそが ホンモノの証人だったのかもね。
English: Come to think of it, this divider helped our case more than the actual witness.

Japanese: 《カベに耳あり、正直メアリー》 ‥‥まさに、そんな感じですね。
English: The great divider! A chip off the old parking block!

Japanese: メアリーはないよ、あかねちゃん。 （今の子は知らないのかな、障子）
English: It's just a wall. (Scientifically speaking...)

Japanese: お。電話がありますよ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Here, a phone. Let's see if it works...

Japanese: こらこら。 勝手にさわらないほうがいいよ。
English: Hey. Don't touch stuff we don't need to be touching.
I can't hear anything!

Japanese: あたしの耳、ヘンになったのカモ！ 気圧のせい、でしょうか‥‥
English: My ears! No, my ears! Maybe it's due to the barometric pressure...

Japanese: （ナニを、バカなコトを‥‥）
English: (What is she babbling about?)

Japanese: あ、そこ！　なんですか！
English: H-hello!? What did you just say?

Japanese: 聞こえてるじゃないか！ 電話がコワれてるんだよ！
English: Look, you can hear just fine. The phone's broken!

Japanese: まっかなすぽーつかーですね。
English: That's about as red as a sports car can get.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。ジツにまっかだ。
English: Yup, it's pretty red alright.

Japanese: （アイツのクルマから 被害者の死体が発見された）
English: (The body was found in Edgeworth's car trunk...)

Japanese: （トランクのカギも コワされている）
English: (And the lock on the trunk was broken.)

Japanese: （ともえさんは‥‥なぜ、 アレを選んだのか‥‥）
English: (So, the question is why did Ms. Skye choose his car?)

Japanese: で？　で？　なんていう クルマなんですか？　アレ。
English: So, what model car is it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... “セダン”か“クーペ”だと 思うんだ。クルマの名前なら。
English: I think it was called a "Sedan" or a "Coupe." Something like that.

Japanese: ‥‥それ、車種です。 成歩堂さん、オトコの子でしょ！
English: Those are car types, Mr. Wright. Not models. You're a guy, aren't you, Mr. Wright? You're supposed to know these things!

Japanese: （この事件が解決したら、 免許をとろう‥‥）
English: (Maybe it is about time I got my driver's license...)

Japanese: 《Ａブロック》‥‥ 検察関係者のエリアですね。
English: "A Block"... This area is reserved for prosecutors.

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士だから、 《Ｂブロック》になるのかな。
English: Defense attorneys are relegated to "B Block."

Japanese: いつかあたし、ここに クルマをとめるのがユメなんです！
English: I dream of the day when I will be able to park my car here!

Japanese: それで、Ｂブロックの成歩堂さん から、ハンバーグ弁当を買うの。
English: I'll go over to B Block to buy my hamburgers from you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥とうぶん、弁護士を やめるつもりはないよ。
English: I'm not giving up my job that soon.

Japanese: この向こうが《Ｂブロック》 ‥‥外来者用のエリアだね。
English: "B Block" is through there. That's where visitors park.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ この金網を乗り越えたんですか！
English: So... Ms. Starr climbed over this fence!?

Japanese: そ、そうみたいだね。 （３メートル以上あるぞ‥‥）
English: I-it seems so, yeah. (That fence is 9 feet high at least!)

Japanese: ちょっと、のぼってみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well no time like the present! I think I'll give it a little try!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeeek!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ... だ。だから、泣かないでほしいな。 （おキョウさん‥‥さすがだな）
English: I-it's okay, don't cry. (Maybe there's a Lunchland Olympics team?)

Japanese: このロープ‥‥やっぱり？
English: This rope... is it...?

Japanese: うん。被害者の死体のカタチを ロープでなぞってあるんだよ。
English: Yep. They laid it in the outline of the victim's body.
So wait...

Japanese: 被害者‥‥トランクを閉めるとき、 挟まって死んだのでしょうか！
English: The victim must have died when the killer closed the trunk on him!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... （こんな発想をする子、 他にはゼッタイいないだろうな）
English: (You have got to be the only person I know that would come to that conclusion.)

Japanese: ここにあったんですよね。 ‥‥このメモ。
English: That's where we found that note.
"2/21 SL-9"... The SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: 事件があった日、《申し送り》に なるはずだった事件。
English: A case that was due for transferal the day of the murder.

Japanese: まさか、それが‥‥ あの事件だった、なんて‥‥
English: I never knew... The "SL-9 incident" referred to...

Japanese: 《青影事件》‥‥か。
English: The Joe Darke Killings.

Japanese: （今回の事件の関係者が全員、 何かの形で関わっている事件）
English: (Everyone in this case played some part in that incident.)

Japanese: （ゼッタイに‥‥ グーゼンなんかじゃない！）
English: (This has to be more than just sheer coincidence!)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 24 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: ‥‥そちら‥‥
English: Excuse me...

Japanese: 最高級和牛のシンタマ、 ９０グラムでよろしかったかしら。
English: Would either of you care for a quarter pound of roast beef?

Japanese: お、おキョウさん！ （すでに弁当ですらないな）
English: Ms. Starr! (I guess she's out of lunches.)

Japanese: あなたたちも、よほどのモノ好きで いらっしゃるコトですわねぇ‥‥
English: You certainly are the curious sort, aren't you?

Japanese: まるで‥‥そう。この世で初めて ナットウを口にした殿方のごとく。
English: Kind of like the first person who sucked a cow's nipple to discover milk.

Japanese: あの‥‥２年前の事件まで レンジであたためなおすとは‥‥
English: Still, I never thought you'd go digging up that case from two years ago.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》は‥‥関係者 すべての名前が登場する事件です。
English: Everyone in this trial was involved in the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: それが《申し送り》によって完全に 終わる日、今回の事件が起こった。
English: Not only that, but the murder occurred on the very day the evidence from that case was

Japanese: グーゼンとは思えません。
English: due for transferal. This can't all be attributed to mere "coincidence."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ワタクシの見た、あの光景‥‥ よもや、お忘れじゃないでしょう？
English: Aren't you forgetting something? You know, that little scene I happened to witness?

Japanese: （ともえさんが、多田敷捜査官に ナイフを突き立てた、瞬間‥‥）
English: (The instant Lana stabbed Detective Goodman with a knife.)

Japanese: 過去のヒミツを暴いても‥‥ その瞬間を変えるコトは、不可能。
English: No matter how much of the past you dig up, it won't change what I saw.

Japanese: ソテーにしたシンタマは、味わって 飲みくだすしかないんだよ。
English: Roast beef is meant to be savored when eaten.

Japanese: （おキョウさんの、ともえさんに 対する“悪意”‥‥）
English: (Ms. Starr's hatred toward Lana...)

Japanese: （すべては、やはり‥‥ ２年前に始まっているんだ！）
English: (It all dates back to two years ago!)

Japanese: 青影　丈‥‥その名前は、 今も忘れちゃいないさ。
English: Joe Darke... That's a name I'll not soon forget!

Japanese: ヒドい緊張とプレッシャーの下で、 アイツを追っていた、半年間。
English: We trailed him for half a year. Oh, the pressure!

Japanese: 今、思えば。あれほど充実していた 日々はなかったねぇ。
English: Still, I don't think I was ever more alive than I was then.

Japanese: ひたすら熱く、濃く煮つめた グレービーソースのような毎日‥‥
English: Those days were steamier than a bowl of hot gravy!

Japanese: もっとも‥‥罪門　恭介にとっては ジゴクのような日々だったろうが。
English: Poor old Jake Marshall, though, must have been going through hell.

Japanese: それは‥‥やっぱり、 弟さんの“死”ですか。
English: You mean, because of his brother's death?

Japanese: ‥‥罪門兄弟は、それは仲が よかったからねぇ。
English: They were close, those two.

Japanese: ナオトが死んでからのキョウスケの 執念‥‥見ていて恐ろしかった。
English: After Neil died, something took over Jake. He became obsessed.

Japanese: そんなアイツを見て‥‥あの女も ほっておけなかったんだろうさ。
English: Seeing Jake like that made her all the more desperate.

Japanese: あの女‥‥？
宝月　巴だよ。
English: Lana Skye.

Japanese: お‥‥‥お姉ちゃん、ですか‥‥
English: My sister...?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》には、最高の 捜査班が組まれたモノさ。
English: The best of the best were put on that SL-9 case.

Japanese: 当然‥‥その指揮には、 伝説のコンビがあたった。
English: Of course they were lead by that legendary duo.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんと‥‥巌徒局長！
English: Lana... and Chief Gant!

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈に、宝月　巴‥‥
English: Damon Gant and Lana Skye...

Japanese: ２年前は、主席捜査官と、副主席。 警察捜査のトップだった。
English: Two years ago Gant was Chief Detective, and Lana second in command. They were the best.

Japanese: ふたりで、数々の難事件を カイケツしたんですよね！
English: They solved all kinds of cases together, didn't they?

Japanese: ‥‥特に、巌徒　海慈の磁力は、 異常なほどだったねぇ。
English: Damon Gant's magnetism in particular was almost unreal.
His "magnetism"?

Japanese: “証拠品”を 引きつけるチカラだよ。
English: By that I mean his ability to attract evidence.

Japanese: 彼が扱った事件では‥‥不自然な ほど、意外な証拠が出たものさ。
English: He'd produce the most incredible evidence in the cases he handled.

Japanese: （“意外な証拠”‥‥まさか！）
English: ("Incredible evidence"? You mean...)

Japanese: そう。そのころから、 ある種の“ギワク”はあった。
English: Oh, yes. There were rumors about him even back then.

Japanese: ‥‥ダレも、口には 出せなかったけどね。
English: No one dared confront him, though.

Japanese: （“ねつ造”だろうな ‥‥言うまでもなく）
English: (I take it she's talking about forged evidence.)

Japanese: あのころの宝月　巴は、 捜査官全員のアコガレだったよ。
English: Back then, everyone looked up to Lana. All the detectives wanted to be like her.

Japanese: ！　そうだったんですか？
English: ! Really?

Japanese: このおキョウも、そのひとりさ。 ‥‥バカみたいだけどねェ。
English: Oh yes... myself included. I was a fool, really.

Japanese: 曲がったコトがキライで、いつも 捜査官のコトを考えてくれていた。
English: She hated anything crooked, and always watched out for the other detectives.

Japanese: だから‥‥あの事件でも、 キョウスケのコトを気にしてたよ。
English: That's why she was so concerned for Jake.

Japanese: 罪門さんを‥‥
English: Mr. Marshall...

Japanese: 弟を殺害された苦しみを、 自分のコトのように感じていたね。
English: When Jake's brother was murdered, she felt as if she had lost her own brother.

Japanese: キョウスケが、弟の死に耐えられた のは、彼女のおかげだった。
