English: If it wasn't for her, I don't think Jake would ever have recovered from his shock.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、クヤシイけどね。
English: That's what makes it all the more infuriating.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥
English: Ms. Starr...

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥よけいに、 あの女がユルせないのさ。
English: That's why... I'll never be able to forgive her.

Japanese: どうして。あんなふうに、 冷たい女に変わっちまったのか‥‥
English: Why did she have to turn so cold after that...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥ ... 《伝説のコンビ》を信じていたから ‥‥キビしい捜査をつづけられた。
English: That legendary pair was the reason we were able to keep up our investigation.

Japanese: それだけに、あの事件の結末は あまりにショックだったね‥‥
English: That's why we're so shocked over how it turned out...

Japanese: 証拠品の“ねつ造”‥‥ですか。
English: You mean, with the forging of the evidence?

Japanese: たしかに、青影　丈は ムクイを受けた。‥‥それはいい。
English: Don't get me wrong. Joe Darke got what he deserved.

Japanese: でも！　法廷に提出された証拠は、 あきらかに“操作”されていた！
English: Still, it was obvious the evidence produced in court was being manipulated.

Japanese: 捜査班の知らない証拠や‥‥ 逆に、提出されなかった証拠。
English: Items our team never found would suddenly appear, while other items were kept secret.

Japanese: でも。ジッサイに 不正があったという証拠は‥‥
English: But you didn't have proof anything illegal was done...

Japanese: このおキョウを見れば‥‥ 何があったか、アキラカだろうさ。
English: I'm proof enough of what happened.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! 事件のあと‥‥多田敷をのぞいた 捜査官たちは、その任を解かれた。
English: After that case, all of us save Goodman were relieved of our duties...

Japanese: ‥‥ほとんど、なんの説明もなく。
English: most without even so much as an explanation.

Japanese: そして‥‥宝月　巴は、主席検事 として、検事局に移転したのさ。
English: Then Lana Skye transferred to the Prosecutor's Office and became Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ムカシから、 検事を志望していたから。
English: Lana always wanted to be a prosecutor.

Japanese: ‥‥コトは、そう タンジュンじゃないよ。
English: Nothing's quite as simple as it appears.
Huh?

Japanese: 宝月　巴は‥‥操られていた。 おキョウは、そうニラんでるねェ。
English: Lana Skye was merely being used as a pawn. That's my take on the matter.

Japanese: “操られていた”‥‥‥？
English: She was being used?

Japanese: ともえさんが移転したのは‥‥ ２年前、だったそうですね。
English: Lana transferred to the Prosecutor's Office two years ago, didn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。巌徒《局長》の、 強力な後押しのおかげで、ね。
English: Yes, thanks to "Chief" Gant's powerful influence.
Chief...

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》を解決した功績で 巌徒は、警察局のトップに立った。
English: That's right. Having solved the SL-9 case, his position as chief was secured.

Japanese: その彼が、次に狙うエモノは‥‥ 当然、ひとつしかない。
English: There was only one thing left for him to control, and then no one could stand in his way:

Japanese: ‥‥検事局さ。
English: the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ！　ま、まさか‥‥ そのために、ともえさんを！
English: What? You mean... That's why Lana was transferred!?

Japanese: 《主席検事》を押さえれば、事実上 検事局を動かすことができる。
English: If he could control the Chief Prosecutor, he could control the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、巌徒　海慈の 狙いだったんだろうねェ‥‥
English: That must have been his goal all along.

Japanese: でも！　なぜ、お姉ちゃんが 局長さんの言いなりに‥‥
English: B-but how could he control Lana?

Japanese: それは、わからないさ。‥‥でも。
English: I don't know, but one thing's for sure.

Japanese: 彼女が“変わった”のは、 あの事件が終わってから、だ。
English: Ever since that case ended, she's never been the same.

Japanese: そこに“何か”ある‥‥ そう考えるべきじゃないかねェ。
English: It's only logical to conclude... there must have been a reason for her change.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (At last...)

Japanese: （やっと、この事件のポイントが 見えてきたような気がするな）
English: (I'm finally getting close to the bottom of this ugly mess.)

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとうこざいます。 おキョウさん。
English: Thank you, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: ボウヤ‥‥
English: You listen to me, Rookie.

Japanese: 食材を活かすも殺すも、 料理のウデしだい、さ。
English: It takes more than just ingredients to create fine cuisine.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに、この情報を 活かすコトができるかしらね‥‥
English: I hope you turn out to be a better chef than I've been...

Japanese: ‥‥今さら、ボウヤにあげる 情報など、ございませんわ。
English: I don't have anything else to tell you.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥
English: Ms. Starr...

Japanese: ワタクシにあげられるのは‥‥ ゴマシオ弁当ぐらいかしら。
English: The only thing I can give you now, is a poppyseed rice set.

Japanese: （安いな、コイツは‥‥）
English: (Talk about cheap...)

Japanese: ゴマシオを食べて‥‥明日は シロクロ、ハッキリすることね。
English: Eat this, and maybe you'll be able to tell black from white in court tomorrow.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 24 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥いませんね。御剣検事さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth isn't here.

Japanese: また、査問会で シボられてるのかな。
English: Maybe he's being questioned by an inquiry committee?

Japanese: （今日もヒドかったからな ‥‥法廷）
English: (He took a real beating in court today...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、認めちゃいました からね‥‥２年前の、不正。
English: Yeah, with Lana admitting to falsifying evidence two years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、 またあとで来てみようか。
English: I guess we'll just have to come back later.

Japanese: せっかくもらったのに、 ほったらかしですね。
English: He won such a prestigious award. Why would he just leave it on the couch?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥トノサマンを 置いておくよりはいいと思うけど。
English: Better this than that giant Steel Samurai.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥アイツ、 ムカシからそうだったな）
English: (Edgeworth has always been like that.)

Japanese: え！　なんですか？ メモしますから、教えてください！
English: Really? Hey, I should write this down. Tell me what he was like as a kid!

Japanese: アイツ、小学生のころ、やたら いろんな賞をもらってたんだよ。
English: Back in elementary school, he won awards for all kinds of things.

Japanese: 弁論大会とか、論文とか、 フルートとか、ゴルフとか。
English: Speech contests, essays, playing the flute, golf tournaments...

Japanese: ‥‥どことなく、小学生っぽくない ラインナップですね。
English: That doesn't exactly sound like typical elementary school stuff.

Japanese: でも‥‥アイツ自身は、 賞をもらうのがキライだったんだ。
English: But he always hated receiving awards. He'd make some smart remark like,

Japanese: 「自分が歩いてきた道ならば、 自分自身が知っているのだよ」
English: "I know the path I've walked. No one else needs to tell me."

Japanese: ‥‥とか、なんとか。
English: That's how he's always been.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ハラ立ちますね。
English: Kind of gets under your skin, doesn't it?

Japanese: ひゃあ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: すごいながめですねー。 １２階でしたっけ。ここ。
English: What a view! It must be nice to have an office on the 12th floor.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なんか、 エラくなった気分になるね。
English: I guess you would feel... important.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに。
English: Incidentally...

Japanese: ここから飛び降りたとき、 イノチを落とすカクリツは‥‥
English: Were you to jump out this window, your chance of fatality would be...
Got it! 99.9998 percent!

Japanese: （‥‥０．０００２パーセントは 助かるのか‥‥）
English: (There's a 0.0002% chance you'd live?)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、 キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。 使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーく みがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so high I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: あのネームプレート、おみやげに 持って帰ろうかなあ。
English: Maybe I'll take that name plaque as a souvenir.

Japanese: やめたほうがいいよ。起訴される。
English: Don't. He'll sue you.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ 事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。アイツ、 地震がニガテなはずだから‥‥
English: Odd...

Japanese: こんな危なっかしい本棚を 使うとは思えないんだけど。
English: I thought Edgeworth wasn't good with heights.

Japanese: だいじょうぶです！
English: He must have someone get them for him.

Japanese: ものすごく大きなボルトで、カベに メリこむほど固定されています！ すごいパワーだなあ。 御剣検事さんがやったのかなあ。 （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: でもさ。地震が起こったら、 部屋中にファイルの雨が降るね。 だいじょうぶです！
English: He must study these case reports so closely... He's so cool!

Japanese: ギュウギュウに詰まってますから、 ちょっとやそっとじゃ抜けません！ ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ やっぱり、なんか おかしくない？　それ。
English: You wouldn't say that if you saw him sweating bullets up on that ladder.

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥ 追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって 青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士” “ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!" And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: 赤い“剣”が、青い“歩”を 追いつめている‥‥ 御剣検事さん、チェス好きかあ。 ‥‥どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth must be an avid chess player! What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （気のせいだよな、きっと）
English: (Edges... surrounding a pawn with spiky hair... Nah... It's nothing.)

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ ステキなティーセットですよ！
English: Oooh! Cute! What a pretty tea set!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、ティーバッグ派だから。
English: I go more for the instant tea bags myself.

Japanese: すごい！　下のタナに、茶葉と缶が ギッシリ詰まってます！
English: Amazing! The drawer below is filled with packets of tea leaves!

Japanese: 産地別にキチンと並んでますよ。 フレバリーも、種類ごとに！
English: They're all sorted by place of origin and flavor!

Japanese: お。このロイヤル、ミゴトなまでに 暴力的なブレンディングです！
English: Look at this Royal blend! What an exquisitely splendid concoction!

Japanese: （‥‥シュミって、なんでも 奥が深いものなんだなあ‥‥）
English: (There's such a thing as taking a hobby too far...)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。 御剣検事さんに、ピッタリ！
English: My, my, my! What an amazing bouquet! Just right for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。 カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《恐怖政治　　　カオル》
English: "To be or not to be -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥ どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥ 巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。 御剣検事さん、マニアなのかなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: イヤだな、それ。‥‥あ。 足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《三割打者　　　カオル》
English: "To every cloud, there is a silver lining -- Wendy"

Japanese: なんか、きのうまでと モンクが変わってませんか？
English: Was this the same message that was here yesterday?

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。 ふかふかですよ、ソファ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth has such a comfy sofa!

Japanese: こんなリッパなソファ、 座った瞬間に眠くなるよ。
English: Sofas like this make me want to curl up and take a nap.

Japanese: きっと、夜おそくまで 資料を調べて、つかれきって‥‥
English: I bet he pours over his case files here until the wee hours of the morning...

Japanese: 上着をぬいで、シャツのソデなんか オシャレにまくっちゃって。
English: Then he takes off his jacket, rolls up his sleeves...

Japanese: ウデまくらで、ついウトウト 寝ちゃうんですよねー。
English: And goes to sleep using his arms as a pillow!

Japanese: （彼女の目には、見えている らしいな‥‥オソロシイことに）
English: (I don't believe it. She's actually daydreaming about Edgeworth working...)

Japanese: 寝グセや、ホッペにソファの カドのアトがついちゃって。
English: I bet in the morning he has sofa hair, and little creases in his cheek from the seams!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいなあ。
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいかあ？
English: Sofa-hair is cool!?

Japanese: うわ！　なんか、いちだんと ヒラヒラした服ですよ！
English: Wow! This jacket is even lacier than his usual ones!

Japanese: いわゆる“勝負服”かな。 御剣検事さんの！
English: This must be his lucky trial jacket!

Japanese: 勝負服、ねえ‥‥。 ぼくは見たことないけどな。
English: Lucky jacket, right... I've never seen him wear it.

Japanese: 額に入れてかざっちゃって。 ナニか“いわく”がありそう！
English: I'm sure there's a story behind why it's in a frame!

Japanese: ちょっとシツレイして。 写真、撮っちゃお！
English: Maybe I'll be naughty... and take a picture!

Japanese: （やれやれ。 コーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (She's getting way too excited about this.)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 24 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: （御剣‥‥戻ってるかな）
English: (I wonder if Edgeworth is back yet.)

Japanese: あ。いましたよ！ ‥‥何か、書いてます。
English: There he is! It looks like he's writing something.

Japanese: な、なんだ！　こんなところに‥‥
English: Huh? What are you doing here?

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥書いてたモノを あわてて床に捨てたぞ）
English: (He sure was quick to throw that paper on the floor...)

Japanese: タイヘンだったな。 ‥‥今日の法廷も。
English: Tough day in court, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: この２年間、私はつねに ギワクのキリに包まれてきた。
English: I've had to live the past two years with rumors flying around.

Japanese: 今さら、どうということもない。
English: What's another allegation to me?

Japanese: 元気出してくださいね！ あたし、オウエンしてますから！
English: Cheer up, Mr. Edgeworth! I'm rooting for you!

Japanese: （コイツは、 ムカシからそうだった‥‥）
English: (That's Edgeworth for you...)

Japanese: （‥‥本当に苦しいときこそ、 それをかくそうとする‥‥）
English: (Always trying to hide his real feelings.)

Japanese: それで‥‥なんの用だ？ こう見えて、私も忙しいのだが。
English: So, what do you want? Unlike some people, I don't have all day.

Japanese: せっかくもらったのに、 ほったらかしですね。
English: He won such a prestigious award. Why would he just leave it on the couch?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥トノサマンを 置いておくよりはいいと思うけど。
English: Better this than that giant Steel Samurai.
What is it?

Japanese: これ、《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》 の賞品ですよね？
English: This is the "King of Prosecutors" award, right?

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥何を今さら。
English: Of course it is. You know that!

Japanese: なんか、今日。どこかで、 コレと似たものを見た気が‥‥
English: I saw something today that looked like this, somewhere...

Japanese: “似たもの”‥‥？
English: Something that looked like that?

Japanese: でも、どこかちがうような‥‥ なんだっけなあ、あれ。
English: Yeah, only different. What was it...?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥何か、引っかかる。 アイツに見せてみるか‥‥？）
English: (She's right. Something's amiss here. Maybe I should show him a certain item...)

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥さっき アイツ、何を書いていたんだ？）
English: (I wonder what he was writing before?)

Japanese: ね。ちょっと調べてみましょうよ、 成歩堂さん！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright! Let's take a look!

Japanese: え！　‥‥やめとこうよ。 シュミが悪いし。
English: Are you crazy? Edgeworth is sitting right there!

Japanese: じゃ。検事さんの目をそらして くださいね。おねがいします！
English: Just distract him. I'll check it out.

Japanese: え‥‥！ え。ええと、御剣‥‥
English: Uh... Hey, Edgeworth.

Japanese: 窓のソト、コートをハタめかせて イトノコ刑事が落ちていくぞ！
English: Is that Detective Gumshoe out the window there? Oh no! He's falling to the ground!!!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトより、この子は 私の足元で、何をしているのだ。
English: Hold on. First let me see what this girl's doing crawling around my feet.

Japanese: （見もしない）
English: (He didn't even look.)

Japanese: あ！　こ、これは！ じ‥‥じ。じ‥‥‥
English: What!? "Letter of r-r-r-r..."

Japanese: 読めないのなら、教えてあげよう。 《じひょうとどけ》‥‥そう読む。
English: If you can't read, I'll read it for you. It says, "Letter of Resignation."

Japanese: じ‥‥“辞表”？ 御剣！　おまえ‥‥
English: "Resignation"!? Edgeworth, you don't mean...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 少し、つかれたようだ。
English: I'm tired, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 私の中の“何か”が死んでしまった ‥‥そんな気がしている。
English: I feel as if... something inside me has died.

Japanese: でも！　御剣検事さんは、 なんにも悪くないのに‥‥
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! None of it is your fault!

Japanese: 自分が歩いてきた道は 私自身、よく知っている。
English: I know the path I've walked. You don't need to tell me.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。それは必ずしも、 正しいものではなかったようだ。
English: And the path I've walked... hasn't been a just one.

Japanese: 私には、それがユルせないし‥‥ また、ユルされるべきでもない！
English: I can't forgive myself for what I've done... and no one else should forgive me either.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥ホンキだぞ！）
English: (Uh oh. I think he's serious!)

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん！ なんとかしないと、検事さんが！
English: Mr. Wright! Please, you have to do something!

Japanese: （この、辞表‥‥ 何かに使えないか‥‥？）
English: (This Letter of Resignation... I wonder if I can use it for anything?)

Japanese: 《御剣の辞表》を ポケットにしまいこんだ。
English: Put Letter of Resignation in pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥イイワケは、無意味だ。
English: There's no excuse for what I've done.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥私は、ニセの証拠に よって、有罪判決を得た。
English: Two years ago, I used false evidence to obtain a guilty verdict.

Japanese: これは、ぬぐい去るコトの できない“ジジツ”なのだからな。
English: That's what it all breaks down to, and nothing I do can erase that fact.

Japanese: でも！　おまえは 知らなかったんだろう？
English: But you didn't know, did you?

Japanese: それが、“ねつ造”された 証拠品だった、なんて。
English: I mean, that the evidence was falsified.

Japanese: ‥‥警察局と検事局は、 ２つで１つなのだ。
English: The Police Department and the Prosecutor's Office share a bond of trust.

Japanese: その“信頼関係”がコワれたとき ‥‥我々は、すべてを失うだろう。
English: If that bond is broken, we stand to lose everything.

Japanese: 警察局のミスは、私のミスで‥‥ 担当検事である、私のセキニンだ。
English: The Police Department's error is my error, my responsibility as the prosecutor in charge.

Japanese: 理由など、モンダイにはならぬ。
English: That fact remains the same no matter what excuses I might have.

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 私は‥‥自分のシゴトに ホコリを持っている。
English: I take pride in my work.

Japanese: それなのに、なぜ！ ここまで、ココロが乱れる‥‥
English: So tell me why! Why has it all come to this...

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ショックは かくしきれないみたいだ）
English: (Even Edgeworth can't keep this kind of emotion bottled up...)

Japanese: 明日の法廷‥‥ おまえ、やれそうなのか？
English: Are you up for the trial tomorrow?

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 去年の事件といい、 キミにシンパイされてばかりだな。
English: First last year's trial, and now this one. It seems all you do is worry about me.

Japanese: 正直‥‥歯がゆい。
English: To be honest, you're getting on my nerves.

Japanese: まさか！　担当検事を はずれる、なんてコトは‥‥
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! You can't just walk out on the trial...

Japanese: 明日は最終日だ。今さら、担当を 変えることなど、できない‥‥
English: Tomorrow is the last day. It's too late to change prosecutors.

Japanese: それが、上層部のホンネだろう。
English: I'll bet that's what my superiors are banking on.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件が‥‥ 今さら、私の足元をすくうとは。
English: I never thought that case would come back to haunt me like this.

Japanese: それは‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: あの事件の証拠品リストだ。 たしかに、妙に少ない気がする。
English: That list of evidence. It seems too short.

Japanese: リストの大きさも‥‥ いつもの半分ほど、だしな。
English: Most lists... run twice as long.

Japanese: はんぶん‥‥ （そりゃ、たしかに少ないな）
English: It's only half as long as most lists? (That is odd...)

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事が殺害されて‥‥ 私は、彼のかわりに法廷に立った。
English: After Neil Marshall was murdered, I became prosecutor for that case.

Japanese: 捜査には加われなかったが、 与えられた証拠で、罪を立証する！
English: I may not have been part of the investigation,

Japanese: ‥‥当時は、そのコトしか アタマになかったよ。
English: but at the time there was only one thing on my mind: I'd use the evidence I was given to prove the suspect guilty!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥そのころの 写真を見かけましたね。
English: Say, we just saw a picture taken around that time.

Japanese: （あの写真‥‥ 何か“違和感”があったっけ）
English: (That picture... Something seemed strange about it...)

Japanese: 最後に、もう一度‥‥事件が あった日のコトを聞かせてくれ。
English: Could you tell us again about what happened that day?

Japanese: ‥‥多田敷捜査官が 殺害された、あの日‥‥
English: The day Detective Goodman was murdered?

Japanese: おまえ、警察局の式典に 参加していたんだよな？
English: You were participating in a ceremony over at the station, right?

Japanese: 式典はスキではない。しかし‥‥ 欠席するわけには行かなかった。
English: I've never cared for ceremonies, but I had to attend that one.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥コレ、ですか？
English: Because you were awarded this?

Japanese: 表彰される本人が 欠席するワケにも行くまい。
English: Those receiving awards can't exactly skip out on the ceremony.

Japanese: シゴトを午前中にかたづけて、 クルマで警察局へ向かったのだ。
English: I finished up at the office in the morning, then drove over to the Police Department.

Japanese: “シゴトをかたづけた”‥‥？
English: You, "finished up" at the office?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 つまらぬ事務シゴトだ。
English: Yes. Just odds and ends- clerical stuff.

Japanese: その日はもう、検事局に 戻るつもりはなかったからな。
English: I didn't plan on returning to the office that day.

Japanese: あの、たのみごとを されるまでは。
English: That is, until I was asked to take something back.

Japanese: “たのみごと”‥‥ですか？
English: Take something back?

Japanese: コイツだよ。
English: This.

Japanese: ああ。巌徒局長さんから たのまれたんでしたっけ、それ。
English: Oh yeah. Chief Gant asked you to hold onto that, didn't he?

Japanese: すでに、半年前。 解決した事件の証拠品だ。
English: Yes. It was a piece of evidence in a case that was closed half a year ago.

Japanese: これを、検事局に持って帰って ほしい‥‥そう言われたのだ。
English: He asked me to bring it back to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （たしか‥‥ きのうも聞いたな‥‥）
English: (That's the story we heard yesterday...)

Japanese: あの日、検事局に帰ってきたのは、 局長に言われたから、なんだな？
English: So you came back here to the Prosecutor's Office because the Chief asked you to?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そういうことになるが。
English: That's right.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ムジュン‥‥《矛盾》と書く。 このコトバの由来は‥‥
English: This award originates from an ancient Chinese tale.

Japanese: ヤリのような武器《矛（ホコ）》と 防具である《盾（タテ）》だ。
English: In Chinese, the word "contradiction" is written with two characters:

Japanese: このコトバには、有名な物語が あるのだが‥‥知っているな？
English: the first means "halberd," and the second means "shield." Have you heard this story?

Japanese: あ、ああ。聞いたことはあるよ。 ‥‥ちょっと、ド忘れしたけど。
English: Me? Oh, uh... sure. Everyone knows that! Why don't you tell it though... for Ema's sake.

Japanese: ‥‥中国の、古い故事だ。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ムカシ、楚（そ）の国に、 ある武器商人がいた。
English: Long ago in the kingdom of Chu, there was an arms merchant.

Japanese: 王の前に出た彼は、 ２つの商品を取り出した。
English: One day, he presented the king with two items.

Japanese: １つ目は‥‥すべてを貫く《矛》。 どんな防具も貫く、最強の武器だ。
English: The first was a halberd he claimed could slice through any shield or armor.

Japanese: もう１つは、決して破れぬ《盾》。 どんな攻撃も防ぐ、最強の防具だ。
English: The second was a shield he claimed could withstand any weapon.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥‥アレ。
English: Hmm... Wait a minute.

Japanese: その証人の発言は、 アキラカにムジュンしているッ！
English: Those claims contradict each other!

Japanese: “その証人”ではない。 “楚の商人”‥‥だ。
English: Very perceptive. But then again, you've heard this story before, right?

Japanese: そのとおり。この《矛》と《盾》は 互いに、あり得ない存在だ。
English: Anyway, as you mentioned, the very descriptions of these items discredit them both.

Japanese: ‥‥王は、商人にたずねた。 『ならば、その《矛》で《盾》を 突いたら、どうなるのか？』と。 ‥‥その商人は、 コトバを失ったという。
English: When the king pointed this out, the merchant was left speechless.

Japanese: そこから、《ムジュン》という コトバが生まれた。
English: And thus, the Chinese word for "contradiction" was born.

Japanese: あ！　もしかして。 《割れた盾》と《折れた剣》は‥‥
English: Oh, I see! So the "chipped shield" and "broken knife" symbolize...

Japanese: その賞品は‥‥ 楚の商品、というわけだ。
English: Precisely so. They symbolize the merchant's items.

Japanese: 中国の物語は、商人が答えに 詰まったところで終わっている。
English: The ancient tale ends with the merchant at a loss for words,

Japanese: しかし‥‥我々は、その先にある 《答え》を追求しなければならぬ。
English: but it's in our nature to pursue matters to their conclusion...

Japanese: その結果が‥‥たとえ、このように ミニクイものであっても、な。
English: even if it results in something as ugly as this.

Japanese: へええ。ベンキョウに なりましたね、成歩堂さん！
English: Wow. Thanks, Mr. Edgeworth! I learned something new today!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。ちょっと、 ヘンじゃないか？
English: That's funny...

Japanese: じゃあ、おまえの賞品には なぜ、《盾》しかないんだ？
English: If that's so, then why were you only given a shield?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ 私にも、よくわからない。 ...
English: You'll have to ask Chief Gant.

Japanese: ２年前、巌徒局長の提言で、 《矛》は廃止されたようだ。
English: Two years ago, he had the "halberd" part of the award abolished.

Japanese: （‥‥巌徒局長が‥‥）
English: (Chief Gant...)

Japanese: 《検事局賞の盾》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: King of Prosecutors Trophy updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、あこがれていた 時期があったものだ。
English: I once dreamed of being a defense attorney, a long time ago.

Japanese: え！　御剣検事さんが、 弁護士に‥‥？
English: What? You wanted to be a defense attorney, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、進むべき道が目の前に のびている以上‥‥
English: Yet, a clear path is laid out before me.

Japanese: 今さら振りかえるわけには いかないのだろうな。
English: I have no time to look back on what might have been.

Japanese: 巌徒局長の部屋に、 こんな写真がかざってあったよ。
English: This picture was hanging on the wall in Chief Gant's office.

Japanese: ‥‥罪門　直斗検事、か。 将来がキタイされていたよ。
English: Prosecutor Neil Marshall... He had just started making a name for himself.

Japanese: 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》を 受賞したときの写真のようだ。
English: Looks like this was taken when he received the King of Prosecutors trophy.

Japanese: あ。そういえば‥‥ 気になることがあるんですけど。
English: Speaking of that, there's something that bothers me.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 罪門検事さんが、 手に持っている“賞品”‥‥
English: The trophy Mr. Marshall is holding...

Japanese: 御剣検事さんのと、 ちょっと、ちがいますよね。
English: it's a little different than yours.
Yes, you're right.

Japanese: ... ‥‥そう。思い出したようだ。
English: I remember now.

Japanese: ？　何をだ？
English: Remember what?

Japanese: ２年前まで‥‥検事局賞の 賞品は、あのカタチが正式だった。
English: That was the official prosecutor trophy used until that time.

Japanese: モチロン‥‥それには、 ちゃんとした由来がある。
English: There's a story behind it.

Japanese: ゆらい‥‥？
English: A story?

Japanese: （ちょっと、キョーミがあるな） 聞かせてくれるか？　そのハナシ。
English: (Sounds interesting.) Would you mind telling it to us?

Japanese: カンタンなコトだがな。
English: It's simple, really.

Japanese: 《ムジュン》‥‥ それが、コタエだ。
English: "Contradiction." That's what the award's based on.

Japanese: いつまで置いておくんだよ。 せっかくもらったのに。
English: You know, it wouldn't hurt if you put this up somewhere, like on a shelf.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなものには、すでに なんのイミもない。
English: That has no meaning for me anymore.

Japanese: “すでに”‥‥って。
English: What do you mean, "anymore"?

Japanese: それは、去年の私の姿だ。 ‥‥振り返るコトに、何かイミが？
English: That's who I was last year. What good is it to dwell on the past?

Japanese: 問いかけられたよ。
English: He's asking me?

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ハラ立ちますね。
English: That makes me kind of mad.

Japanese: あの‥‥それより、あたし。 少し、気になるんですけど。
English: Actually, something's been troubling me about this shield.

Japanese: この《盾》‥‥ なんか、ちがいませんか？
English: Look... Do you notice anything different?
Different...?

Japanese: ほら。法廷記録に、もう１つ あるじゃないですか。《盾》が。
English: Yeah, don't you remember? The other shield in the Court Record?

Japanese: （見せてみるか‥‥ もう１つの《盾》を）
English: (I guess I'd better present this other shield...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まさか、成歩堂。 担当検事である、この私が‥‥
English: Come now, Mr. Wright. You can't seriously expect me to just sit down...

Japanese: キミと事件の話をしながら お茶を飲むとでも‥‥？
English: and chat with you about the case over a cup of tea.

Japanese: いやいや。これは 関係ないんだろ？　事件とは。
English: But this isn't related to the case, remember? You said so yourself.

Japanese: む‥‥むゥ、たしかに。
English: So I did...

Japanese: あの日、局長さんから たのまれていたんですよね？
English: Chief Gant gave this to you that day, didn't he?

Japanese: 警察局から、ここへ 持ってくるように、って。
English: He asked you to bring it here from the Police Department...

Japanese: あの局長‥‥
English: The Chief is a very competent man...

Japanese: ヤリ手なのだが、たまに イミがわからないことがある。
English: but sometimes he does things that don't make any sense.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まあ。ヤリ手ではない上、 イミがわからないよりはイイが。
English: At least it's better than being incompetent and doing things that don't make sense.

Japanese: （ある刑事さんのカオを 思い浮かべたようだ）
English: (A certain detective comes to mind...)

Japanese: 今回の事件‥‥何から何まで、 私を疑わしく見せているようだ。
English: It seems everything in this case is designed to cast doubt on me.

Japanese: ぼくをニラまないでほしいな。
English: Hey, don't look at me.

Japanese: 事件があった日‥‥たしかに、 私は証拠保管室に入った。
English: Yes, I went into the evidence room on the day of the crime.

Japanese: 巌徒局長から、急に たのまれたものでね。
English: Chief Gant asked me to do something.

Japanese: 《半年前に解決した事件の証拠を 検事局に持ち帰ってほしい》‥‥
English: To take evidence from a case solved six months ago back to the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: あそこには、私自身の 保管庫が用意されているのだ。
English: I have a locker in there as well.

Japanese: そのとき、保管室のようすは？
English: Did you notice anything amiss when you were in the evidence room?

Japanese: ‥‥あの部屋は、いつも暗い。
English: That room's always dark and I was in a hurry.

Japanese: 私も急いでいたし、 特に気になることはなかったな。
English: I didn't see anything out of the ordinary.

Japanese: “火のないところに ケムリは立たない”‥‥
English: They say where there's smoke, there's fire.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥そのケムリに巻かれて 真実を見失っていたようだ。
English: Apparently I was so caught up in the smoke, I lost sight of the truth.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: 彼らは正しかった。私には‥‥ 検事席に立つ資格など、ない。
English: The others are right. I have no right to serve as a prosecutor.

Japanese: でも！　検事さんは、自分の シゴトをしただけじゃないですか！
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! You were only doing your job!

Japanese: 私のなすべきコトは、つねに 私自身が決めてきた。
English: I've always made my own decisions about what I can or cannot do.

Japanese: 今回のコト‥‥ 自分をユルすことはできない。
English: That hasn't changed. Forgiving myself... is something I cannot do.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥ホンキだぞ！）
English: (Uh oh. I think he's serious!)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん！ なんとかしないと、検事さんが！
English: Mr. Wright! Please, you have to do something!

Japanese: （この、辞表‥‥ 何かに使えないか‥‥？）
English: (This Letter of Resignation... I wonder if I can use it for anything?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まさか、成歩堂。 担当検事である、この私が‥‥
English: Wright, please. I'm the prosecutor on this case!

Japanese: キミと事件の話をしながら お茶を飲むとでも‥‥？
English: You don't expect me to sit here and discuss the case with you over a cup of tea!

Japanese: いや。お茶はいいんだ。 事件のハナシだけを‥‥
English: I'll pass on the tea. Just tell me about the case...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　御剣検事さんは イキに断ってるんです！
English: Mr. Wright! Mr. Edgeworth just told you "no" in a very stylish manner!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Whose side are you on, anyway?)

Japanese: （本当に必要なものだけ 鼻先につきつけてやろう）
English: (Maybe if I just show him my best evidence I can get some reaction out of him.)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 24 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: イトノコ刑事は‥‥ 見あたらないね。
English: I don't see Detective Gumshoe anywhere.

Japanese: なんか今日は、特に 静かな気がしますね。刑事課。
English: Things seem kinda quiet around here today.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにね。 （課長さんはあいかわらずだけど）
English: You're right. (The head of the department seems the same though.)

Japanese: あの。もう少し、他のヒトの ハナシを聞いてみませんか？
English: Why don't we go look for some other people to talk to?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだなあ。 また、あとで来てみようか。
English: Right. We can come back here later.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “わかった！　ハンニンは タイムマシンを使ったのだ！”
English: "That's it! The villain used a time machine!"

Japanese: “なんて巧妙なトリックだろう‥‥ これでアリバイは崩れたのだ！”
English: "Very clever indeed... That would explain the alibi!"

Japanese: ‥‥時代を先取りした物語‥‥ 案外、今ここで生まれてるのかな。
English: "The future might not be so far away after all!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 報告書‥‥じゃなくて、 小説を書いているようだ。
English: He's not writing a report... he's writing a novel.

Japanese: 警察のマスコットキャラクター ‥‥って、こんなんだったっけ？
English: So this is the police mascot, is it?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。 未来の警察のスターさ。
English: The Blue Badger! The future star of the police force!

Japanese: ちょっとデザインがちがいますね。 ‥‥ソトでおどっている子と。
English: The design's a little changed from the one outside...

Japanese: ああ。まだ、試作段階だがね。 《ミスター・ダンシングタイホ》。
English: Ah, well, the Dancing Blue Badger(tm) is still under development, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥なんで、英語なんだろ。
English: You have it trademarked?

Japanese: アレはね。少し“今”ふうの センスを取り入れてみたんだよ。
English: Absolutely! It's cutting edge stuff. Very "now."

Japanese: このぬいぐるみ、ムスメに見せたら “コワい”と泣かれてしまってね。
English: I showed this doll here to my daughter and she burst into tears!

Japanese: （‥‥あの動くベニヤ板を見たら、 走って逃げ出すぞ、きっと）
English: (Don't show her the moving mock-up outside then, you'll give her nightmares.)

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: That man must be the Head Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's staring at the screen so hard it might shatter.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ “青影事件に、デッチ上げ疑惑”
English: What!? "Darke Case May Have Been Fabricated."

Japanese: ホレ、見たことか。当時からワシ、 そう思っていたんだよな。
English: That's what I thought all along...

Japanese: ダレにも言わず、ココロに そっと秘めていたけど。
English: I just never bothered to tell anyone.

Japanese: （‥‥ダレかに言えよ！）
English: (Now there's a guy who cares about his work...)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 24 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ここ、いつもヒトが少ないけど‥‥ 今日は特にヒドいな。
English: This place is always pretty empty, but today it's deserted.

Japanese: 刑事課が静か、ということは。 忙しいのかな、やっぱり。
English: That must mean everyone's busy solving crimes.

Japanese: ああ。みんなは今、大会議室で ドンチャン騒ぎだよ。
English: Oh, if you're looking for the others, they're all in the conference room.

Japanese: あ‥‥‥どうも。 （課長さんが話しかけてきた）
English: Uh... thanks. (Wow, he actually talked to us!)

Japanese: 主席検事のバクダン発言に、 御剣検事の、これからの処置。
English: With the Chief Prosecutor saying what she did and the decision about what to do

Japanese: マスコミ対策に、明日の裁判‥‥ もう、ヒドいコトになってるな。
English: about Mr. Edgeworth, not to mention our statement to the media and tomorrow's trial...

Japanese: まるで、そう。 盆と正月がイッペンに来た感じさ。
English: there's more chaos going on than Thanksgiving and Christmas put together!

Japanese: （それは、おめでたいのでは‥‥）
English: (I think "festive" is the word usually used for those...)

Japanese: あの。あたしたち、巌徒局長さんの お部屋に行きたいんですけど。
English: Um, sir? We'd like to have a look around Chief Gant's office.

Japanese: わたりろうかでトナリのビルに 行って、エレベータで最上階だよ。
English: Just head across the hall to the other building and take the elevator to the top floor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥え。 あの。行っていいんですか？
English: Really? You mean, it's okay for us to go in there?

Japanese: あたしたち、いわゆるその。 イッパンジンですけど。
English: I mean, we aren't police officers or anything.

Japanese: あ！　イカン、そうだった！ ダメだよ、行っちゃ。
English: Hey, you're right! You can't go in there! It's off limits!

Japanese: （さすが、イトノコ刑事の 上司だけのコトはあるな‥‥）
English: (Now I see where Detective Gumshoe gets his unique charm...)

Japanese: じゃ。さっそく 行ってみましょうか。局長室。
English: Well, what are we wating for? Let's head to the Chief's office!

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 24 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: あ、アンタたち！
English: Hey, pal!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！ 今まで、会議ですか？
English: Detective Gumshoe! Were you in a meeting?

Japanese: ま‥‥ちょっと一休みッス。 カタがコっちまったッス。
English: I was uh... just taking a breather. My feet hurt.

Japanese: そんなカタくるしい 会議だったんですか？
English: From sitting so long in the meeting?

Japanese: いや。その。自分は‥‥
English: Actually...

Japanese: お茶をいれてたッスから。 ‥‥みんなの。
English: I had to serve everyone coffee.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事‥‥ 今日もハズされてるのか）
English: (Sounds like Detective Gumshoe's still out of the loop...)

Japanese: それより、アンタ！ 御剣検事には会ったッスか？
English: Say, have either of you seen Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 御剣‥‥？ どうかしたんですか？
English: Edgeworth? No. Why do you ask?

Japanese: もー、検事局も警察局も、 エラいイキオイで暴走してるッス。
English: He's under fire from both the Police Department and the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: まるで、裁判中のアンタと 御剣検事のごとく！
English: It's almost like the battles between you two in court!

Japanese: それは‥‥きっと、 大暴れなんでしょうね。
English: That sounds serious...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥騒ぎの原因は お姉ちゃんの発言、ですか？
English: Is it because of what my sister said?

Japanese: ‥‥そッスね、ヒラたく言えば。 《２年前の“ねつ造”》ッス。
English: That's basically what it all boils down to. That falsified evidence two years ago.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、今。 世界中をテキに回しているッス。
English: Now Mr. Edgeworth has the whole world after his blood!

Japanese: そんな‥‥
English: Yikes...

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず‥‥
English: Well, there you have it in a nutshell.

Japanese: 自分にわかるのは、 こんなトコロ、ッスかねえ。
English: That's all I know.

Japanese: あの、最後にもう１つだけ。 おねがいがあるんですけど‥‥
English: Can I ask you one more thing?

Japanese: なんス？　おカネなら、巌徒局長に たのんだほうがいいと思うッスよ。
English: What is it? If it's money you need, you should ask Chief Gant.

Japanese: その、巌徒局長のコトです。 局長室は“現場”なんですよね？
English: It's not money, but it does concern the Chief. His office is a "crime scene," right?

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事が 殺害された事件の‥‥
English: It's where Prosecutor Neil Marshall was murdered...

Japanese: 局長さんが出かけちゃって、 ロックされちゃったんです。
English: The Chief's out now, and his office is locked,

Japanese: あの部屋‥‥調査して みたいんです！　あたしたち。
English: but we'd like to have a look around if that's okay.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ジツは、捜査官のＩＤカードを 使えば、ロックは開けられるッス。
English: Well, any Detective's ID card can unlock the door.

Japanese: え！　そうなんですか！
English: What? Really!?

Japanese: でも‥‥一般のニンゲンを 局長室に入れたら‥‥
English: But if I let a civilian in there,

Japanese: 背任罪にあたるッス。
English: I'd be charged with breach of trust.

Japanese: はいにん‥‥？
English: Breach of trust...?

Japanese: わかりやすく言えば、 クビになっちゃうッス。
English: Simply put, I'd be canned.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: ワルいッスけど‥‥自分。
English: Sorry, pal.

Japanese: アンタのためにクビになるのは ゴメンナサイッスから。
English: I don't plan on getting fired because of you.

Japanese: あ。じゃ、この 多田敷捜査官のＩＤで‥‥
English: How about this ID card? It was Detective Goodman's...

Japanese: それはダメッス。亡くなった日に データは抹消されたハズッス。
English: That won't work either. The data was deleted the day he died.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Oh.

Japanese: （つまり。あの部屋を調べるには、 このヒトにたのむしかない‥‥）
English: (So in other words, Gumshoe is our only chance of getting into that office.)

Japanese: 何か、刑事さんの気持ちを変える ようなモノが必要ですね。
English: I wonder if there's something we could show him that would make him change his mind...

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “わかった！　ハンニンは この私だったのだ！”
English: "I got it! I'm the culprit!"

Japanese: “なんて巧妙なトリックだろう‥‥ 私は二重人格だったのだ！”
English: "Very clever indeed... Who would have thought of multiple personalities?"

Japanese: ‥‥他に何も浮かばない。 まあ、こんなものだろう。
English: "I don't see how else he could have done it. I guess it's time to have my head checked."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... さっそく、トリックの ネタがつきたらしい。
English: I think it's way past time for that.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: That man must be the Head Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's staring at the screen so hard it might shatter.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ “警察局、致命的なミス”だと？
English: What!? "Police Department Makes Fatal Error"

Japanese: こ、こうしちゃおれん！ ダレか！　アルバイトニュースを！
English: What do I do!? Quick! Someone bring me the classifieds! Help Wanted section!!!

Japanese: （‥‥さっそく 保身に走っているようだ）
English: (So much for "duty"...)

Japanese: ‥‥でも、どうして御剣が？ アイツは、何も知らなかったのに！
English: But why would Edgeworth be blamed? It's not like he knew the evidence was forged!

Japanese: ジッサイに不正をしたのは、その。 ともえさん‥‥だったんですよね？
English: Lana Skye is the guilty party here, isn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まあ‥‥ジッサイに法廷に立つのは 検事ッスからねぇ。
English: Regardless, the prosecutor is responsible for the evidence he presents in court.

Japanese: それに、もう１つ。
English: Not only that,

Japanese: 知ってのとおり、御剣検事には ずっと、黒いギワクがあったッス。
English: but as you know there've been a lot of rumors going around about Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ただ、御剣検事には、ズバ抜けた 才能があったッスから‥‥
English: Those who don't like him haven't been able to do anything because of his

Japanese: 今まで、反対派も手を出すことが できなかったッス。
English: amazing talent as a prosecutor. But now with this...

Japanese: テキが多かったんですか？ 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: Are there really so many people who hate him?

Japanese: ‥‥検事局と警察局は、才能だけが モノを言う世界ッス。
English: In our world, only those with talent rise to the top. Mr. Edgeworth not only had that,

Japanese: 若い、ムキ出しの才能は テキを作る‥‥それは、宿命ッス。
English: but he's young. There's no better recipe I know of for making enemies.

Japanese: よォ、イトノコ。 がんばってるな、今日も！
English: Hey, Dick! Keep up the good work!

Japanese: ッス！
English: Yes sir!

Japanese: 今度、またメシおごってやるぞ、 イトノコ！
English: Let's go out for lunch again sometime, my treat!

Japanese: ッス！
English: Yes sir!

Japanese: ノコさん！　また連れていって くださいね！　あの店。
English: You gotta take me back to that joint sometime, okay Dick?

Japanese: ッス！
English: Yes sir!

Japanese: ‥‥よかったですね。刑事さんには テキがマッタクいなくて。
English: It seems you don't have any problem with enemies.

Japanese: ま。自分はウマく やってるッスからね。
English: Yeah, well... I'm careful not to stick out.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥自分はシンパイッス。
English: Anyway, I'm a bit worried about him.

Japanese: このイヤな流れに‥‥ 御剣検事が、折れてしまわないか。
English: Under all this pressure, I'm afraid Mr. Edgeworth just might crack.

Japanese: ジツは‥‥資料を調べてみたッス。 お茶をいれながら。
English: Actually, I took a look at the file earlier, while the coffee was brewing.

Japanese: （本気でシンパイしてるんだな、 御剣のこと‥‥）
English: (He seems genuinely concerned for Edgeworth...)

Japanese: それで‥‥何かありましたか？
English: Well? Did you find out anything?

Japanese: ズバリ、すべての手がかりは、 ヤツの最後の犯行にあるッス！
English: The only evidence Darke left behind was during his final attack.

Japanese: 最後の犯行、というと‥‥
English: His final attack? You mean...

Japanese: 少女を救おうとした罪門検事が 殺害された事件ッス。
English: When he killed Prosecutor Marshall, who was trying to protect some girl.

Japanese: あたしのコト、だ‥‥
English: Me...

Japanese: （この子のコト、気づかなかった みたいだな。イトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (It seems Detective Gumshoe never realized Ema was the girl...)

Japanese: この事件で、青影は致命的な 証拠を残してしまったッス。
English: That's when he left the most incriminating evidence of all.

Japanese: その“証拠”というのは？
English: Well? What was it?

Japanese: え。えーと。たしか‥‥ 《凶器》だったッスけど。
English: Oh, um, let's see... I think it had something to do with the murder weapon...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... まあ。詳しいコトは、 コレのどこかに書いてあるッス。
English: Oh, I forget. Look, it's all written somewhere in here, okay?

Japanese: （‥‥ホント、コシャクな 刑事さんだよな‥‥）
English: (His powers of recollection never fail to impress.)

Japanese: 見せてみましょうか。 “キョーキ”を。
English: Maybe we should show him the murder weapon.

Japanese: 何か、思い出してくれるかも しれません！
English: It might jog his memory!

Japanese: 青影　丈‥‥犯行当時、４２才。 ごくフツーの会社員だったッス。
English: Joe Darke was 42 at the time of the crime. He was just your run-of-the-mill businessman.

Japanese: 連続殺人犯‥‥なんですよね？ なぜ、そんなヒドいことを‥‥
English: A businessman? What made him take to serial killing?

Japanese: ある日。会社からの帰りに、 クルマで人をひいてしまったッス。
English: One day on his way home from work, he hit someone with his car.

Japanese: クルマで？ それって‥‥事故、ですよね？
English: With his car? So... it was an accident?

Japanese: しかし、その瞬間から！ ヤツは、キケンな野生動物にッ！
English: An accident, yes. But it transformed him into an animal!

Japanese: やせいどうぶつ‥‥
English: An animal...

Japanese: その事故を目撃した男性を、殺害。 その犯行を見ていた女性も、殺害。
English: He killed a man that witnessed the accident. Then he killed a lady who saw the second crime.

Japanese: そこに通りかかった学生を、殺害。 その死体を埋めているところを
English: A kid walked by just then, so he killed him too. Then, when he was burying the bodies,

Japanese: サンポ中の男性に見られて、殺害。 ‥‥そこでやっと、任意出頭を。
English: a jogger came upon the scene and was killed as well! Finally he turned himself in.

Japanese: ずいぶん うかつな野生動物ですね。
English: Seems he was a pretty careless animal.

Japanese: 今、アンタに話したのは、 あくまで“スイソク”ッス。
English: Of course, this is all conjecture.

Japanese: 証拠は、マッタクなかったッス。
English: There wasn't a single shred of evidence.

Japanese: それで、任意出頭を‥‥
English: So, he turned himself in...

Japanese: 取調べ中に逃げ出して、最後の犯行 ‥‥罪門検事を殺害したッス。
English: Yes, but in the middle of his questioning, he fled and murdered his final victim... Prosecutor Marshall.

Japanese: その場でタイホされたッスが‥‥ その犯行すら、見られていたッス。
English: That crime was witnessed by someone too, but fortunately he was arrested on the spot.

Japanese: 最後の目撃者がブジで、 ホント、よかったッスね。
English: It's a good thing that last witness wasn't killed.

Japanese: （最後の犯行の“目撃者”‥‥）
English: (That last "witness"...)

Japanese: （‥‥この子、か）
English: (He must mean Ema...)

Japanese: このナイフ‥‥ 青影のもの、なんですか？
English: This knife... it was Joe Darke's, wasn't it?

Japanese: そッス。買った店も判明して、 青影の指紋も付着していたッス。
English: That's right. We traced it back to the store he bought it at, and it had his fingerprints on it too.

Japanese: でも。ジッサイにコレで刺したか どうかは、わからないんじゃあ‥‥
English: But no one actually witnessed him using it to murder anyone, right?

Japanese: そこが、ヤツの 運のワルいところッス。
English: That's where his luck ran out.

Japanese: ナイフをよーく見ると‥‥‥
English: When you take a good look at the knife...

Japanese: なんと！　刃がポッキリ、 折れているッス！
English: you'll see it's broken!

Japanese: よく見なくてもわかりますよ、 それぐらい。
English: You don't have to take a good look to notice that.
Yeah, well anyway!

Japanese: 折れた刃の先が発見された“場所” ‥‥それがキメ手になったッス。
English: Take a guess where the broken- off tip of the knife was found. That's what did him in!

Japanese: ど、どこだったんですかっ？
English: Where was it?

Japanese: 被害者‥‥‥罪門　直斗検事の “体内”ッス。
English: The victim, Neil Marshall was carrying it... inside his own body!

Japanese: ‥‥！ ! 刺された際の、キズの奥から ナイフの刃のカケラが。
English: It was found deep inside the stab wound.

Japanese: それが、青影のナイフに‥‥？
English: Did it match Darke's knife?

Japanese: ピッタリ、一致したッス。
English: You bet. Down to the last fiber.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥カガク的ですねえ。
English: That's pretty... conclusive.

Japanese: 証拠品《罪門　直斗の解剖記録》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Neil's Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《飛び出しナイフ》の データを書きなおした。
English: Switchblade Knife added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 刑事さん。ほら、これ。 弁護士がつけるバッジですよ。
English: Detective... here is my attorney's badge.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、会うたびに 見せるッスね、それ。
English: You show this to me every time we meet, pal.

Japanese: 男はだまって警察手帳ッス！
English: Real men show their police badge. 'Nuff said!

Japanese: いいなあ。あたしも 早くほしいな、身分証明‥‥
English: I wish had a badge... Even an ID card would be nice...

Japanese: 事件の被害者・多田敷刑事の ＩＤカードッス。
English: This ID card belongs to the victim, Detective Bruce Goodman.

Japanese: 今日び、なんでもカードに暗証番号 ‥‥気が休まるヒマがないッス。
English: You can do just about anything these days with a card and a secret number... scary!

Japanese: それは、イトノコ刑事が ぽろぽろ落とすからですよ。
English: It's only scary because you keep dropping your card, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 暗証番号もぽろぽろ忘れるッス。 ‥‥自分がコワくなるッス。
English: I forget my secret number a lot, too. I'm scared of myself!

Japanese: ダレが見たって、自分は自分！ それでいいじゃないッスかッ！
English: But... but I'm me, after all! And what could be wrong with that!?

Japanese: （開きなおった‥‥）
English: (I think I'll stay out of this conversation.)

Japanese: 御剣検事のクルマに、 御剣検事のナイフ‥‥
English: Found in Mr. Edgeworth's car, stabbed with Mr. Edgeworth's knife, huh...

Japanese: どうして宝月主席検事殿は、 そんなコトをしたッスかねえ‥‥
English: What would drive Chief Prosecutor Skye to do such a thing?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あ。あやや。も、モチロン！ ハンニンは、ベツのニンゲンッス！
English: W-wait, I didn't mean... I mean, sure, of course someone else really did it!

Japanese: そのハンニンは、まるで‥‥
English: Someone who must have, um...

Japanese: 御剣検事に、ウラミでも あるみたいッスねえ‥‥
English: Someone who must have a grudge against Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: お、それ！
English: Hey, that's it!

Japanese: 御剣検事がもらった 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》ッス！
English: That's the "King of Prosecutors" award that Mr. Edgeworth got the other day!

Japanese: 授賞式、イトノコ刑事も 出席していたんですか？
English: Were you at the awards ceremony, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: そりゃそッス！ 自分ももらったッスよ。努力賞。
English: Of course, pal! I got an award for diligence, myself.

Japanese: はあ‥‥おめでとうございます。
English: Ah... congratulations.

Japanese: それにしても、 なんで《盾》なんですか？
English: I was wondering, why is the award a shield?

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、割れてるし。
English: And... why is it broken?

Japanese: それはモチロン、 ちゃーんと理由があるッス。
English: Oh, there's a reason.

Japanese: ええと‥‥‥‥‥‥ また、今度教えてあげるッス。
English: Erm... I'll tell you what it is later.

Japanese: （忘れたらしいな、どうやら）
English: (Apparently, he's forgotten.)

Japanese: この写真を、おキョウさんが‥‥
English: That's the photo that Ms. Starr took.

Japanese: 何か、ココロ当たりありますか？
English: Anything you can tell us about it?

Japanese: スゴいヒトッス。おキョウさんは。 ‥‥この自分、１６才の冬‥‥
English: That Ms. Starr is quite the lady. Why, I remember it was winter... I was 16.

Japanese: あの事件をしゃべらされたのは、 あのヒトが最初で‥‥最後ッス。
English: She was the only one who ever got me to talk about what happened.

Japanese: １６才‥‥あたしと同い年だ。 何があったんだろ。
English: 16... that's how old I am now! I wonder what happened?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事も、 ムカシは高校生だったんだな）
English: (I wonder if Detective Gumshoe wore a trench coat in high school, too.)

Japanese: これ、なんですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: こッ、これは！ もしかして、あれッスか？
English: Hey! Don't tell me that's...

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》と書かれた タグがついています。
English: It has a tag attached to it, with the label "SL-9 Incident" on it.

Japanese: 先の折れたナイフ‥‥ あの事件の《凶器》ですよね？
English: I believe this would be the broken "murder weapon" you were speaking of?

Japanese: なんで、アンタが このナイフを知ってるッス？
English: What are you doing with that!?

Japanese: あの事件はとっくに解決されて、 証拠保管庫に入っているハズッス。
English: Ever since that case was closed, that knife's been locked away in a locker.

Japanese: 事件のあった日、証拠保管庫から コツゼンと消えて‥‥
English: On the day Detective Goodman was murdered, this suddenly disappeared from the locker,

Japanese: 御剣検事さんのクルマの マフラーから出てきたんです。
English: and was found in Mr. Edgeworth's car muffler.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ あああッ！　思い出したッス！ ...
English: That's it!

Japanese: 青影　丈が、最後の犯行で 残した“致命的な証拠”‥‥
English: Now I remember what that incriminating piece of evidence was!

Japanese: そのナイフを見ていたら、 ピン！　と来たッス！
English: When you showed me that knife, it all came back to me!

Japanese: また忘れないうちに、早く 聞かせてください！　刑事さん！
English: Well, what is it, Detective? Quick! Before you forget again!

Japanese: あ。ジツは自分も 買って来たッスよ。それ！
English: Hey, I just bought some of that stuff!

Japanese: これで自分も、今日から 血痕をとりまくるッス！
English: Now I can go around detecting blood traces too!

Japanese: へえ！　あれ、新製品ですか？ ‥‥見たことないボトルですね。
English: Wow! Is that a new type? I've never seen that bottle before.

Japanese: 《シャンプーするだけで、 おもしろいようにとれます》
English: "Add three inches to your base height."

Japanese: “シャンプー”‥‥？ ちょっと、見せてください！
English: "Base height"? Hey, let me see that!

Japanese: 《ノミトール》‥‥ ノミをとるシャンプーですね。
English: "Looming Tall." "New and improved growth formula."

Japanese: あれ！　とれないッスか？ 血痕は！
English: You mean this can't detect any blood traces!?

Japanese: ど。どうでしょうね。 《ルミノール》だし。あたしのは。
English: Uh, well... It's not quite the same thing as luminol.

Japanese: そういえば、クスリ屋のオバチャン ‥‥カユそうな目で見てたッス。
English: So that's why the lady at the counter had that smirk on her face...

Japanese: 今日は、コイツのおかげで ヒドい目にあいましたよ。
English: This guy almost made us lose the case today.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるッスか！
English: What are you talking about!?

Japanese: サイゴに決め手になった、 証拠保管庫の“血痕”！
English: He was guarding the bloodstain on that evidence locker with his life!

Japanese: 身をもって、それをかくしていた のは、タイホくんッス！
English: That's more than you can say about most officers nowadays!

Japanese: かくされていなければ、 もっと早く気がついたのに。
English: It would have saved us a lot of trouble if he hadn't guarded it so well.

Japanese: （でも、たしかに‥‥）
English: (I have to admit he's right though...)

Japanese: （アイツのおかげで、もう１つの 可能性を示すことができた）
English: (Thanks to the Blue Badger, we were able to prove another possibility today:)

Japanese: （あの日の５時１５分より“前”に 事件が起こった可能性‥‥）
English: (The possibility that another murder took place prior to 5:15 PM...)

Japanese: ＩＤカードの使用記録、ッスか。
English: That's the ID card record, isn't it?

Japanese: 残ったナンバーは、１つ。 午後４時２０分‥‥
English: Yes. There's only one number left to investigate. At 4:20 PM...

Japanese: 被害者の多田敷捜査官は、 このとき保管室に入ったはずです。
English: the victim, Detective Goodman, must have entered the evidence room along with someone else.

Japanese: “上層部”のナンバーを持った、 ある人物といっしょに‥‥
English: Someone with an "executive officer" number:

Japanese: なななななななななななななななな ‥‥ッスかあ。
English: "Sevensevensevensevenseven- sevensevenseven"?

Japanese: “な”が２回多いですよ、 イトノコ刑事。
English: That's one "seven" too many, Detective.

Japanese: （“上層部”‥‥ ココロ当たりがあるような‥‥）
English: (An executive officer. Hmm... I just might have a hunch.)

Japanese: 指紋が一致しないかぎり、 開けることはできないッス。
English: No locker can be opened without a fingerprint match.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥
English: Besides...

Japanese: ハンニンのヤツ、わざわざ 自分の保管庫をさわらなくても‥‥
English: There's no reason for the murderer to touch his own locker...

Japanese: そういえば。現場の血のアト、 刑事さんの保管庫でしたっけ。
English: Hey, wasn't your locker the one with the blood on it, Detective?

Japanese: （指紋のない‥‥手袋をつけた “真犯人”の手のアトか‥‥）
English: (The handprint of the real murderer's gloved hand... without any fingerprints...)

Japanese: 自分がタイホされたときには！ よろしくたのむッスよ、アンタ！
English: SSH! If they come to arrest me, you'll defend me, won't you!?

Japanese: 御剣検事にも、 よろしく言っておきますよ。
English: If that happens, I think you'll do better pleading with Edgeworth.

Japanese: お。遺失物届ッスね。
English: Oh, a lost item report, huh?

Japanese: さすが、刑事さん！ すぐ、わかるんですね。
English: Very impressive, detective! You knew what it was right off the bat!

Japanese: ま。自分‥‥落としモノと なくしモノの名人ッスから。
English: Well, I am a master of misplacement, you know.

Japanese: ちょっと、カッコイイですね！ 《名人》っていうヒビキ。
English: "Master"... That has such a cool ring to it!

Japanese: “すべては、神の思し召し”‥‥ これが、《落とし》のゴクイッス。
English: The way I see it, if things are meant to be lost, then they're meant to be lost. There's a higher power at work here.

Japanese: “思し召し”‥‥と。
English: Wow, a higher power...

Japanese: （この子には、証拠品を あずけないほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (Maybe I shouldn't let Ema hold any evidence...)

Japanese: そういえば、このツボ‥‥ どこかで見たことがあるッス。
English: About that jar... I think I've seen it before somewhere.

Japanese: “どこかで”‥‥？
English: "Somewhere"?

Japanese: あるいは‥‥“前世のキオク”、 かもしれないッスけどねえ。
English: Or maybe it's one of those memories people have from "previous lives."

Japanese: （ホント、参考にならない 刑事さんだよな‥‥）
English: (This must be the most uninformative detective I've ever met...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、見ていると 落ちつかない気分になるッス。
English: Something about it makes me feel uneasy.

Japanese: あたかも‥‥局長室に呼ばれて、 ニコヤカに怒られてる、みたいな。
English: It's like I'm in the Chief's office, and he's yelling at me.

Japanese: （巌徒局長‥‥？）
English: (Chief Gant?)

Japanese: いったい、 どこで見たッスっけねえ‥‥
English: Where could I have seen that before...?

Japanese: お。指紋を採取するコナッスね。
English: Hey, that powder's used for detecting fingerprints.

Japanese: 御剣検事さんから もらったんですよ！
English: Yeah, Mr. Edgeworth gave it to us.

Japanese: ズルいッス！　御剣検事、 自分には小麦粉しか‥‥
English: He did what!? All I have is some flour...
Are you okay, Detective?

Japanese: あ、アンタ‥‥早く！ に、逃げるッス‥‥
English: Quick! RUN!!!

Japanese: はっくしょ！
English: AHH-CHOOOO!!!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... 自分なんて、ペンキぬりたての ベンチで昼寝したことがあるッス。
English: If it's any help, one time I took a nap on a bench with wet paint.

Japanese: （気休めにならないな‥‥）
English: (Why am I not comforted?)

Japanese: まさか、罪門センパイが、 あんなコトをするとは‥‥
English: I can't believe Officer Marshall would do something like that...

Japanese: 知ってるんですか？ 罪門さんのコト。
English: You know Officer Marshall?

Japanese: モチロンッス！ センパイッスからねー。
English: Of course I do! He was like a mentor to me!

Japanese: 自分が新人のコロ、ちっちゃな サボテンをもらったッス。
English: When I first started out, he even gave me a small cactus.

Japanese: へえ。
English: Really?

Japanese: “ノコ”のヤツ、自分のなやみを なんでも聞いてくれるッス。
English: He said, "Dick, she'll listen to all your troubles."

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事は夜、 サボテンに語りかける、と。
English: Note to self: Detective Gumshoe talks to a cactus...

Japanese: あんなスゴウデが、巡査なんて！ ダレかが替わってあげるべきッス！
English: Hard to believe he's just a "patrolman" now. Someone oughta trade places with him!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... なんスか。そのビミョーな視線は。
English: What? Why are you looking at me like that?

Japanese: 最近の愛読書ッス。 御剣検事の立証は、カンペキッス。
English: I've been studying up on those Files. There's nothing wrong with Mr. Edgeworth's

Japanese: そこに“不正”があったなんて‥‥ 自分は、信じないッス！
English: presentation. To think people are accusing him of injustice... I for one ain't buyin' it, pal!

Japanese: 御剣検事さんのために 調べているんですか？
English: You're looking into the case for Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 当時、かなり話題になった 事件ッス。
English: Yeah. It was a pretty big deal while it was going on, you know.

Japanese: なにしろ‥‥ 連続サツジンッスからねえ。
English: After all, a serial killer was on the loose!

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ともえさんは ハッキリ認めている‥‥）
English: (But Lana was pretty clear in her confession.)

Japanese: （青影　丈を有罪にするために、 証拠の“ねつ造”をした、と）
English: (She forged evidence in order to prove Joe Darke guilty.)

Japanese: イッピキの刑事として、 これだけは断言しておくッス！
English: Let me share a little advice with you as a detective.

Japanese: ココロ当たりのないモノには、 断固！　チンモクを守るッス！
English: If you don't have a clue, keep yer trap shut!

Japanese: か、かまいませんよ、それで。
English: I'll, uh, keep that in mind.

Japanese: ‥‥ホッ。
English: Sheesh!

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: February 24 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: お、アンタたち。また来たッスか。
English: Oh, you're back.

Japanese: 刑事さんも、まだいたんですか？
English: You're still here?

Japanese: 捜査資料を１５０部、 コピーしてるッス。
English: I gotta make 150 copies of these files.

Japanese: お茶を入れて、コピーして‥‥ もう、まるっきりＯＫッス！
English: Brewing coffee, copying files... I'm turning into a regular DJ!

Japanese: “おーけい”‥‥‥？ どういうコトかな。
English: You're a "DJ" as well?

Japanese: ハナシの流れから察するに‥‥ “オフィス・ケイジ”の略かな。
English: If I'm not mistaken, I think he means "Desk Jockey."
Oh, that DJ...

Japanese: アンタも、何度もゴクロウッスが ‥‥ダメッスよ。アレだけは。
English: I gotta admire your persistency, but my answer's still no.

Japanese: “アレ”‥‥？ ? 巌徒局長室の調査ッス。
English: I'm not letting you in the Chief's office, period.

Japanese: アンタたちを入れてしまったら、 自分のクビが火を吹くッス！
English: It'd be my neck on the line.

Japanese: （局長室は、《ＳＬ９号事件》の 最後の現場だ）
English: (That office is the last crime scene in the SL-9 Incident.)

Japanese: （調べないわけにはいかない！）
English: (I have to take a look in there!)

Japanese: 何か、いい手はありませんか？ 刑事さんのキモチを変えるような。
English: There's got to be something we can do to make the Detective change his mind.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “なあ、いいだろうヒロコさん。 ボクはもう‥‥”
English: "Oh, Francesca. You're more lovely than a marsupial in..."

Japanese: “ああ。オトコとオンナは ミステリーね、タケオさん”
English: "Quiet, Jean Marc. Words will just spoil the moment. Now hold me close..."

Japanese: ‥‥こっち方面なら、いくらでも ナゾを作れそうだな。
English: Yes, this approach allows for much more intrigue.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... スイリ小説は あきらめたらしい。
English: It seems he's given up on the mystery genre.

Japanese: なんスか。この シワシワの紙クズは。
English: What's this crumpled up piece of paper?
N-no way! Mr. Edgeworth can't be serious!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ ホンキ、みたいなんです。
English: Is he ever not serious?

Japanese: そ、そこまで 追いつめられているとは‥‥
English: I can't believe they've pushed him this far...

Japanese: セキニン感が強そうですよね。 御剣検事さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth really feels responsible.

Japanese: ‥‥自分も、最初は冷たいだけの オトコだと思っていたッス。
English: At first I thought he was as cold as ice,

Japanese: でも。今は知っているッス。
English: but now I know different!

Japanese: 御剣検事が、ワレワレ刑事を 信じてくれていることを。
English: He trusted us detectives to provide him with sound evidence,

Japanese: それなのに‥‥
English: but we just...

Japanese: その信頼を‥‥ワレワレ警察は、 ウラ切ってしまったッスね‥‥
English: we betrayed him!

Japanese: 刑事さん‥‥
English: Detective...
That's it. I've made up my mind!

Japanese: え。
English: But...

Japanese: このＩＤカード、 持っていくッス。
English: Here, take my ID card.

Japanese: でも！　そんなコトしたら、 あなたが‥‥
English: We can't do that. If someone found out...

Japanese: 今年３枚目の遺失物届で 済むモンダイじゃないですよ！
English: they wouldn't let you off the hook with another lost item report!

Japanese: 見てのとおり‥‥自分は今、 捜査からハズされているッス。
English: Look at me. It's no secret I'm already out of the loop.

Japanese: 御剣検事とイチバンつながりの 強い捜査官だったッスから。
English: After all, I'm friends with Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 事件の決着によっては、 この自分も‥‥
English: Depending on how this case turns out, I may already be as good as terminated...

Japanese: （な、なんだって‥‥）
English: (What...?)

Japanese: だから、せめて‥‥最後ぐらいは。 検事のチカラになりたいッス！
English: So at least let me do this... for Mr. Edgeworth's sake!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 お借りします。
English: All right, Detective. Thank you.

Japanese: 《イトノコ刑事のＩＤ》を ポケットの中でニギりしめた。
English: Gumshoe's ID tucked swiftly into your pocket.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察局・局長室
English: February 24 Police Department Chief's Office
Whoa... Where am I?

Japanese: なんだ？　ここ。 《局長室》ですよ。 ソトに、そう書いてあったから。
English: In the Chief's office, silly! At least, that's what it said on the door.

Japanese: あの、巨大なパイプオルガン‥‥ 信じられないけど、ホンモノだぞ。
English: Check out that pipe organ. That's real, isn't it?

Japanese: あたしね。ようちえんのころ、 オルガン習ってたんですよ！
English: Hey, I used to take organ lessons in kindergarten!

Japanese: 《ゆり組のバッハ》なんて 呼ばれて、天才きどりでしたねー。
English: They used to call me "Little Miss Bach."

Japanese: ‥‥どれどれ。 “ド”はどこだったっけ。
English: I thought I was a genius until they tried teaching me notes.

Japanese: ‥‥死ぬかと思った。
English: I never could remember where C was...

Japanese: あれー？
English: Hm?

Japanese: なんだキミたち。来てたんだー。
English: Oh, it's you two.

Japanese: きょ‥‥局長さん！
English: Chief Gant!

Japanese: （読んでいた書類を、デスクの 引き出しにしまいこんだぞ‥‥）
English: (He put that paper he was reading in his desk.)

Japanese: どーなのよ、ナルホドちゃん。 泳いでる？　最近。
English: So, Mr. Wright. Have you been swimming lately?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。 最近ちょっと、忙しくて‥‥
English: Uh... no, I haven't. I've been kind of busy lately.

Japanese: こっちもタイヘンよ、ホント。 罪門のアニキの不始末とかね。
English: I can appreciate that. I've had my hands full too, with Mr. Marshall's misconduct

Japanese: トモエちゃんまで、トンでもない 発言してくれちゃってさあ。
English: and Lana's provocative statement.

Japanese: “トンでもない”‥‥ああ。 証拠品の“ねつ造”ですか。
English: "Provocative statement"... oh, you mean about the forged evidence.

Japanese: あの事件から、２年ねー。 かー！　早いモンだわ、ホント。
English: Two years have passed since that incident. My, how time flies!

Japanese: ‥‥ホラ。あそこのカベに でっかい写真があるでしょ？
English: See that big picture on the wall over there?

Japanese: トモエちゃんと、ナオトくんと ３人で撮ったんだけどさ。
English: That's a picture of Lana, Neil and me.

Japanese: （これが‥‥罪門さんの弟。 罪門　直斗検事、か‥‥）
English: (So this is Mr. Marshall's brother... Prosecutor Neil Marshall...)

Japanese: 『３人で、がんばっていこう！』 なんて言ってたんだけどねー。
English: We took it to commemorate our work together.
(Something's not right with this picture.)

Japanese: （ビミョーな“違和感”が あるような‥‥）
English: (I can't quite seem to put my finger on it though...)

Japanese: 証拠品《２年前の写真》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Gant Team Picture added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あー、悪いんだけどさ。 ボク、アトシマツで忙しくて。
English: Anyway, I'd like to reminisce all day, but there are matters that need my attention.

Japanese: 今日はここ、もう閉めちゃうから。 いっしょに出ようか。
English: I'm going to lock up here, so let's go out together.
Oh...

Japanese: あ。でも‥‥ここって、その。 現場、なんですよね。２年前の‥‥
English: But this office... it was a crime scene two years ago, wasn't it?

Japanese: あの事件、もう終わったんだから。 今さら捜査する必要、ないってさ。
English: That case has long since been over. There's no need to investigate it any more.

Japanese: でも、できれば 調べたいんですよ、ぼくたち。
English: All the same, we'd still like to have a look around.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、言わせるの？ 今さら捜査する必要は、ない。
English: Perhaps you didn't hear me. I said there's no need to investigate it any more.

Japanese: ‥‥！ ...! じゃ。出て出て。そろそろ カイギに行かないとさ。
English: Now hurry up and get out. I have a meeting to attend.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 警察局・局長室
English: February 24 Police Department Chief's Office

Japanese: じゃ。行きますよ、成歩堂さん。
English: Here goes, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥カチ‥
English: *click*

Japanese: ‥ピー‥
English: *beep*

Japanese: ‥‥ついに、来ちゃいましたね。
English: Open sesame.

Japanese: こんなところを見つかったら、 カクジツに‥‥
English: If anyone finds us now,

Japanese: クビ、だね。 ‥‥イトノコ刑事が。
English: Detective Gumshoe's a goner.

Japanese: そのときは、よろしく たのむッスよ、アンタ。
English: If that happens, I'm counting on you to bail me out!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: EEEEEEEEK!

Japanese: ほほほおおおおおおおおおおいッ！
English: GAAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさい。 死神かと思ったんです。
English: Sorry, I thought you were a ghost.

Japanese: ‥‥死神に、いきなり ビンタはどうかと思うッス。
English: I didn't even know you could slap a ghost.

Japanese: いいいい、イトノコ刑事ッ！ なんですか急にッ！
English: AAAAH! Detective Gumshoe! What are you doing sneaking up on us like that!?

Japanese: いいいい、いやそのッ！ ヤッパリ自分もその。気になって。
English: I-I-I wasn't "sneaking." I was just worried something might go wrong...

Japanese: ‥‥来ちゃったッス。
English: so I came too.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥イミないじゃ ないですか！　コレ！
English: If you're here, then what's the point in giving us your ID card!?

Japanese: 《イトノコ刑事のＩＤ》を ポケットの中でニギりつぶした。
English: Crumpled Gumshoe's ID Card in pocket.

Japanese: きゃー！　そんなコトしちゃ ダメッス！
English: Hey, don't do that to my card!

Japanese: とにかく。局長室に入るなんて、 めったにないッスから‥‥
English: I hardly ever get a chance to come in here,

Japanese: いっしょけんめい捜査したいッス！ ‥‥一生の思い出に。
English: so I figured I'd have a look around myself! Besides, we're all in this together now.

Japanese: クビになる気、マンマンですね。
English: You really do want to get fired, don't you?

Japanese: さ。自分がついているッス。 思うぞんぶん、やってみるッス！
English: Not if we're lucky. Now come on, let's see what we can find out!

Japanese: （ちょっぴり、不安だ‥‥）
English: (I've got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: ‥‥どッスか？ 役に立ってるッスか？　自分。
English: Well? Was I any help?

Japanese: モチロンですよ。
English: Of course!

Japanese: 刑事さんのＩＤカードのおかげで、 重要な証拠が手に入りました。
English: Thanks to your ID card, we were able to get some hard evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドいなー、それ。
English: Now that's not very kind, is it?

Japanese: つまり、“ＩＤカードがなければ なんの価値もない”‥‥
English: In other words, if it wasn't for his ID card, he would have been useless...

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトでしょ？ そのコートのヒト。
English: Isn't that right, you in the coat?

Japanese: きゃわはあああっ！
English: EEEEEEEK!

Japanese: ガントキョクチョー！
English: C-Chief Gant!

Japanese: ‥‥戻ってくるとは 思いませんでした。
English: We didn't think you'd be back so soon.

Japanese: ホラ。イヤな予感ってさ。 当たるでしょ？
English: Fortunately I'm a man who believes in signs.

Japanese: さっき、歩いてたらさ。
English: As I was walking to my meeting,

Japanese: よそ見しながら走ってきて、電柱に ゲキトツしたノラ犬を見たわけ。
English: I happened to look out a window and saw a stray dog run right into a pole.

Japanese: その瞬間。ある男のカオがさ。 ピンと浮かんだのよ。
English: Just then I thought of a certain detective.

Japanese: それ、自分のコトッスか‥‥
English: Do you mean... m-me, sir?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。 出ていってくれる？　みんな。
English: Now then, I'm afraid I'm going to have to ask you all to leave.

Japanese: は、はい！ すみませんでした‥‥
English: Y-yes sir! Sorry...

Japanese: あ。コートのキミさ。
English: Oh, you in the coat.

Japanese: は、自分ッスか！
English: Me, sir!?

Japanese: ＩＤは置いてってよ。 もう、いらないでしょ、キミ。
English: Drop off your ID on the way out. You won't be needing it anymore.
But sir...

Japanese: ‥‥消えなよ。早く。
English: Now get out!

Japanese: は‥‥ははは、はははあッ！
English: Y-y-yes sir!

Japanese: あ‥‥じゃあ。 ぼくたちも、これで‥‥
English: We'll be on our way too, then.

Japanese: あー。ちょっと待って。 キミ、残ってくれるかな。
English: Wait. You, the one without the spiky hair.

Japanese: ‥‥そっちの、アタマの とがってないほうのカノジョ。
English: Don't go yet.

Japanese: え！　あ、あた、あたしですか！
English: M-me, sir!?

Japanese: ハナシがあるんだよね。 ‥‥おりいって。
English: I'd like a word with you.

Japanese: で、でもあたし！　まだ正式には カガク捜査官の免状はありませんし
English: But sir! I'm not a licensed scientific investigator yet!

Japanese: あ。アタマのとがったほうのカレ。 ‥‥キミはいいよ。出て行って。
English: You with the spiky hair. You're free to go.

Japanese: なな、成歩堂さあん‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Wright!!!

Japanese: こういう、デカい ガラス窓を見ると‥‥
English: Look at that giant window...

Japanese: パリーンとこう、つき破って 脱出してみたくなるッスね。
English: Makes you want to crash through it and jump outside.

Japanese: １５階ですよ、ここ。
English: Uh, this is the 15th floor.

Japanese: わかってるッス！ “たとえば”のハナシッス！
English: I know! I was just saying...

Japanese: （なんの“たとえ”だよ‥‥）
English: (Saying what?)

Japanese: せっかく刑事になった以上、 やっぱり、ユメッスね。それは。
English: Ever since making detective, I've always dreamed about doing something like that.

Japanese: ‥‥刑事さんは、ユメ多き トシゴロである、と。
English: Note to Self: Detective Gumshoe has a lot of dreams...

Japanese: 刑事をクビになって、 そのユメが破れたとき‥‥
English: So long as he doesn't go crashing through that window

Japanese: パリーンと行かなきゃいいけど。
English: when he gets fired.

Japanese: そういうコト、言わない！
English: Don't say that!

Japanese: ２年前の、あの日‥‥ 撮影されたものですね。
English: This was taken on that day two years ago.

Japanese: （青影　丈が取調べ室から 逃走して、あかねちゃんを‥‥）
English: (The day Joe Darke ran out of the questioning room and tried to kill Ema...)

Japanese: 罪門検事は、検事賞をもらった あと、ここで記念写真を撮って‥‥
English: After receiving his award trophy, Mr. Marshall took a picture here,

Japanese: 巌徒局長といっしょに、 青影の取調べを行ったッス。
English: then went along with Chief Gant to question Darke.

Japanese: まさか‥‥その数時間後、 イノチを落とすコトになるとは‥‥
English: I bet he never knew he'd be dead just a few hours later...

Japanese: （思わなかっただろうな ‥‥だれも）
English: (Gee, you think?)

Japanese: ともえさんのデスク、だ。 キレイにかたづいている。
English: This was Lana's desk. It sure is tidy.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、スッキリしたのが 好きだったから‥‥
English: Lana's always been a meticulous cleaner.

Japanese: ホコリもかぶっていない。 今も、手入れされているみたいだ。
English: There's not even any dust on it! Looks like someone's still keeping it clean.

Japanese: ともえさん、今もここへ 戻ってきたりするんですか？
English: Does Lana ever come back here?

Japanese: そんなコトはないッス。 ただ‥‥
English: No.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈と宝月　巴のコンビは、 捜査官の伝説ッスからね。
English: Chief Gant must still keep it clean in memory of their partnership.

Japanese: きっと‥‥なんらかのカタチで、 思い出を残しておきたいッス。
English: They were the stuff legends are made of!

Japanese: （それといっしょに、 事件のキオクも残るワケか‥‥）
English: (Does he keep it in memory of her, or in memory of the crime...?)

Japanese: 局長のオルガンは、それはそれは ミゴトなモノッス。
English: The Chief's organ sure is a sight to behold.

Japanese: ２階の刑事課にも、たまに キレイな音が聞こえてくるッス。
English: Occasionally we hear him playing it from the Criminal Affairs department.

Japanese: （‥‥１５階だよな、ここ‥‥）
English: (That's on the 2nd floor, and this is the 15th!)

Japanese: 刑事がデカいミスをすると、 局長室に呼ばれて‥‥
English: When a detective screws up, the Chief calls him to his office...

Japanese: 局長のオルガンを３時間、 みっちり聞かされるッス。
English: and makes him listen to the organ for hours.

Japanese: いいじゃないですか！ ココロが洗われますよね！
English: What's so bad about that? Music soothes the soul!

Japanese: そのかわり‥‥
English: After that,

Japanese: それから３日は耳鳴りがやまず、 なんにも聞こえないッス。
English: the detective can't hear anything for days except for the ringing in his ears.

Japanese: （バツゲームか‥‥）
English: (So it's an instrument of punishment... literally.)

Japanese: でも‥‥局長さんの耳は 大丈夫なんですか？
English: But aren't the Chief's ears affected?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... あのカタは、いつも ヒトのハナシを聞かないッスから。
English: He never listens to anyone anyway.

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃ ないだろう‥‥）
English: (That's besides the point...)

Japanese: タナの中は、 ほとんどカラッポだ。
English: These shelves are mostly empty.

Japanese: 検事局へ異動するとき、 かたづけられたのだろう。
English: Lana must have cleaned them out when she transferred over to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥左のタナに、 小さな写真立てがある。
English: There's a small picture frame on the left shelf.

Japanese: あ！　これ‥‥
English: Hey!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんと、遊園地に 行ったときの‥‥
English: This is when Lana and I went to that theme park...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... お花のマーク、ですね。
English: This mark looks like some kind of flower.

Japanese: なんでも、検事がつける 記章のデザインらしいッス。
English: Word is, it's designed after the insignia on the prosecutor's badge.

Japanese: “きしょう”‥‥？
English: "Prosecutor's badge"...?

Japanese: アンタがエリにぶら下げている、 それッス。
English: Yeah, like the one hanging from your collar.

Japanese: え！　検事にもバッジが あるんですか！
English: What!? They have badges too!?

Japanese: なんでも、刑罰のキビしさを あらわしたデザインらしいッスね。
English: The design's supposed to portray the severity of the punishment system.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥チクチクして イタそうですねー。
English: Now that you mention it, it does look all pointy and kind of painful.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、御剣はバッジなんか つけていませんよ。
English: But Mr. Edgeworth never wears a badge.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、ホラ。 オシャレさんッスから。
English: That's because he's a sharp dresser. A badge like that wouldn't go too well with his outfit.

Japanese: オシャレさんなら つけなくていいんですか？
English: So sharp dressers don't need to wear badges?

Japanese: ま。大目に見てもらってるんじゃ ないッスかねー。
English: I guess everyone just kind of lets it slide.

Japanese: （どこが“刑罰のキビしさ”だよ）
English: (I don't see how that's supposed to signify severe punishment...)

Japanese: さすが、局長さん！ リッパなデスクですねー。
English: Wow! Look at the size of Chief Gant's desk!

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば、さっき‥‥）
English: (Speaking of that, when we were here earlier...)

Japanese: なんだキミたち。来てたんだー。
English: Oh, it's you two.

Japanese: きょ‥‥局長さん！
English: Chief Gant!

Japanese: （読んでいた書類を、デスクの 引き出しにしまいこんだぞ‥‥）
English: (He put that paper he was reading in his desk.)

Japanese: いったい‥‥ 何を読んでいたのかな？
English: I wonder what he was reading?

Japanese: コイツは‥‥事件の 証拠品のリストッスね。
English: This looks like a list of evidence... used in a case.

Japanese: 証拠品の‥‥？
English: A list of evidence?

Japanese: いつもは、この倍ぐらいの 大きさがあるッスけど。
English: In most cases the list runs twice as long as this.

Japanese: あ！　この、事件名のところ！
English: Hey, look at the case name!

Japanese: え‥‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》！ どうして、こんなところに‥‥
English: Huh? "SL-9 Incident"! I wonder what this is doing here...

Japanese: ！　ちょっと待ってください。 ‥‥イトノコ刑事、今、なんて？
English: Hold on, Detective! What did you just say?

Japanese: ‥‥え。 なんか言ったッスか？　自分。
English: I said, "I wonder what..."

Japanese: 証拠品のリスト‥‥いつもは、 《倍の大きさ》がある、とか。
English: No, about evidence lists. Normally they're twice as long?

Japanese: ‥‥そッスよ。よっぽど 証拠品が少なかったッスかねー。
English: That's right. I guess there wasn't a lot of evidence.

Japanese: （大きさが半分の、 証拠品リスト‥‥）
English: (A half-sized list of evidence...)

Japanese: あの事件の証拠品リストだ。 たしかに、妙に少ない気がする。
English: That list of evidence. It seems too short.

Japanese: リストの大きさも‥‥ いつもの半分ほど、だしな。
English: Most lists... run twice as long.

Japanese: （なぜ、こんなところに‥‥ もう半分のリストがあるんだ？）
English: (What would the other half of the list be doing here?)

Japanese: やっぱり！　局長さん、あの事件の コト、何かかくしてるんですよ！
English: I knew it! The Chief must be hiding something about that case!

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えざるを得ない、ね。
English: It would appear so.

Japanese: 証拠品《証拠品リスト》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Evidence List added to the Court Record.

Japanese: さすが、局長さん！ リッパなデスクですねー。
English: Wow! Look at the size of Chief Gant's desk!

Japanese: ‥‥その引き出しから、 コイツを見つけた。
English: We found this inside the drawer.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥ その、証拠品のリストだ。
English: A list of evidence from the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: もう半分は、御剣検事が 持ってるんですよね。
English: Mr. Edgeworth had the other half of that list!

Japanese: （なぜ、ここに‥‥ こんなリストがあるのか？）
English: (What would this list be doing here...?)

Japanese: （詳しく調べておいたほうが いいな‥‥このリスト）
English: (We'd better look a little more into this list.)

Japanese: これって‥‥金庫、ですよね？
English: This is a safe, isn't it?

Japanese: キンコ‥‥なんと、アマくキケンな カオリのするコトバッスか‥‥
English: "Safe"... that word is ripe with intrigue!

Japanese: そんなコトを思うの、 イトノコ刑事ぐらいですよ。
English: Uh, okay. If you say so...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、暗証番号を入力する みたいですね。パネルに。
English: It looks like a code needs to be entered in this panel to open it.

Japanese: （７ケタの数字、か。 ココロ当たり、あるかな‥‥）
English: (A seven-digit number. I think I just might know what it is...)

Japanese: （まちがった数字を入力したら、 何が起こるかわからない‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure what will happen if we enter the wrong code.)

Japanese: （もっと、ハッキリした手がかりを 見つけるまで、やめておこう）
English: (I'd better wait until I find something more definite.)

Japanese: ７ケタの数字‥‥ なんか、どこかで見たような。
English: A seven-digit number... Didn't we see one of those somewhere?

Japanese: とりあえず、自分の誕生日でも 入れてみるッスか？
English: How about we try entering my birth date?

Japanese: お！　ナニか ココロ当たりがあるッスか？
English: Do you know what it is?

Japanese: ボンヤリ‥‥ですけど。
English: I have a hunch...

Japanese: あ。アレッス！ ちなみに、自分の誕生日は‥‥
English: Oh, I know! You want to try my birth date? It's...

Japanese: （とにかく、やってみよう！）
English: (I have a better idea. Here goes nothing.)

Japanese: ダメ‥‥みたいですね。
English: I guess that wasn't it.

Japanese: なんか、わかったような 気がしたんだけどなあ。
English: I was so sure that was the number!

Japanese: 法廷記録、もう一度 チェックしてみましょうよ！
English: Maybe we should check the Court Record again!

Japanese: （７ケタの数字、か‥‥）
English: (A seven-digit number...)

Japanese: ‥‥開いた‥‥
English: Bingo.

Japanese: ど。どんな番号だったんですか！
English: What number did you enter!?

Japanese: ‥‥ダレの！ ダレの誕生日だったッスかッ！
English: Whose birthday was that, pal?
The final ID card number on that record.
What?

Japanese: 正体不明の“上層部”の ＩＤカードの持ち主さ。
English: The number of the mysterious "executive officer" who entered the room that day.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ！
English: You mean,

Japanese: ななななななななななななな‥‥ のＩＤナンバーは！
English: "Sevensevensevensevenseven- seven"? That ID number!?

Japanese: “な”が１回少ないようですが ‥‥どうやら。
English: I think you're one "seven" shy this time.

Japanese: 巌徒局長のナンバーだった！ ‥‥そういうコトになりますね。
English: This can only mean one thing: That's Chief Gant's ID number!

Japanese: と、とにかく！　そのキンコ‥‥ よーく調べてみましょうよ！
English: Say... Anyone care to look inside?

Japanese: い、いくら！　いったい、 いくら、入ってるッスかッ！
English: Is there any money in there? How much does he have stashed away!?

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　こ、これは‥‥！
English: Look! It's a... a...

Japanese: なんだ。割れたおちゃわんの カケラッスか‥‥
English: a shard from a broken cup.

Japanese: （見たコトがあるぞ！ このカケラ‥‥）
English: (This somehow looks familiar. Where have I seen this before...?)

Japanese: まだ、ナニか入ってますね。
English: There's something else in here too.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？　コレ。 カワの布地、みたいだけど‥‥
English: What's this? It looks like a piece of leather cloth...

Japanese: これ、って‥‥ 手のアト、ですよね。
English: This is a handprint, isn't it?

Japanese: それがッ！　ウワサの 《約束手形》というヤツッスか！
English: Hey! I saw someone wearing a shirt like that once!

Japanese: 銀行に持っていくと、おカネが うなるほど出てくる、マホーの札！
English: You think the Chief made up the design?

Japanese: ‥‥ちがいます。
English: Uh... I don't think so.

Japanese: そんな目で見ないでほしいッス。 ウスウス、わかっていたッス。
English: Oh. Well, it was just a thought.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、それだけですか！ キンコの中身は！
English: Is that it? This is all that was in the safe?

Japanese: そうだね。もう、カラッポだよ。
English: Apparently so. It's empty now.

Japanese: 手のアトが残った布きれに、 おちゃわんのカケラ‥‥ッスか。
English: A piece of cloth with a handprint on it, and a broken shard from a cup.

Japanese: あの、その証拠品ですけど。 よかったら‥‥
English: They look like pieces of evidence,

Japanese: 局長室のキンコの中身ッスよ！ カンタンには、あげられないッス。
English: but unless you can prove they have something to do with this case, I'm afraid I can't just let you take them.

Japanese: 今回の事件と関係なかったら‥‥ 自分のクビが火を吹くッス！
English: After all, it's my neck on the line here!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥手に入れるには、 “関連性”を証明するしかない）
English: (Great. Now I have to prove their "relevancy" to get them.)

Japanese: （“２つの証拠品”と、 《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥）
English: (How are these two items related to the SL-9 Incident?)

Japanese: 刑事さんに、いろいろ つきつけてみましょうよ！
English: Come on! There's got to be something we can show the detective!

Japanese: これ‥‥ホンモノ、ですよね。 ヨロイも、武器も。
English: This is the real deal, isn't it? This armor and these weapons?

Japanese: 局長は、ホンモノを愛する オトコ、ッスからねえ。
English: Sure is, pal. The Chief doesn't care for imitations.

Japanese: ヨロイに、パイプオルガン‥‥ というか、そもそも、この部屋。
English: First the pipe organ, now this armor...

Japanese: みんなの税金を注ぎこんで つくったワケですよね。
English: Do you know how many taxpayer dollars must have gone into this room?

Japanese: えー、ヒドい！　あたし、もう ゼッタイ払いません。税金。
English: What? You mean we're paying for this!? That's it. I'm not paying one cent of my taxes!

Japanese: （‥‥払ってないクセに）
English: (You don't have any taxes to pay.)

Japanese: シッ！　ヘタなコト、 言っちゃダメッス！
English: Ssh! Be careful of what you say!

Japanese: ヨロイの中に、局長が かくれているかもしれないッス！
English: Who knows? The Chief may be hiding in this armor as we speak!

Japanese: あの体型じゃ、ムリでしょう。
English: I don't think he'd fit in there.

Japanese: たとえ入っていたとしても、 ヒトリじゃ出てこられませんよ。
English: Even if he did, he'd never be able to get back out.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタたちは、局長の イミ不明のコワさを知らないッス。
English: Cut it out! You guys don't know how scary that guy can be!

Japanese: １５階ともなると、ながめが ちがいますねー。
English: You can see pretty far from 15 stories up.

Japanese: ちなみに。ここからヨロイを 落としたとき‥‥
English: If you were to drop that suit of armor from here...

Japanese: 局長、最初はステンドグラスに したいと言っていたッス。
English: At first the Chief wanted to use stained glass for this window.

Japanese: え。なんで、やめたんですか？
English: Really? Why didn't he?

Japanese: このながめが見えなくなっちゃう から、だそうッス。
English: They say he changed his mind because he wouldn't be able to see the view.

Japanese: うーん、たしかに。
English: Oh.

Japanese: （それにしても、デカい 窓だな‥‥）
English: (Stained glass or not, it's a huge window...)

Japanese: あの、部屋の反対側にある デスクは‥‥お姉さんの？
English: That desk on the other side of the room... Was that your sister's?

Japanese: そうです。 ２年前の、あの日‥‥
English: Yes. That's where I was waiting for Lana...

Japanese: あたし、あそこで待ってた ハズですから。お姉ちゃんを。
English: on that day two years ago.

Japanese: あの‥‥今は、だれか 使っているんですか？　あそこ。
English: Is anyone using it now?

Japanese: いや。巌徒局長が１人ッスよ。
English: No sir. This is entirely Chief Gant's office now.

Japanese: “現場保存”をテッテーする ヒトッスからねえ、局長殿は。
English: He practices a strict policy of "preserving the crime scene."

Japanese: （そういうコトなのか‥‥？）
English: (That's a strange reason to leave it there...)

Japanese: ‥‥いつまでも残しておいて、 警察局のイマシメにする‥‥
English: He leaves it as a warning to everyone else. He wants us to always be alert.

Japanese: いつか、局長が そう言っていたッス。
English: He told us so himself at our New Year's party.

Japanese: 忘年会のドンチャン騒ぎの席で。
English: Of course, he was pretty intoxicated at the time.

Japanese: ‥‥軽いですね。
English: I see.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あそこは、 ダレも手を触れていないんですか？
English: So ever since Lana left, no one ever touches that desk?

Japanese: ‥‥あの事件があってから。 そッス。局長と‥‥毎朝来る、 そうじのオバチャン以外は。
English: No one except Chief Gant... and the cleaning lady who's in here each morning.

Japanese: （‥‥なんと言っても、 ２年前の事件だ）
English: (Still, two years have passed since that incident.)

Japanese: （手がかりが残ってるとも 思えないんだけど‥‥）
English: (There can't possibly be any clues remaining.)

Japanese: １つ‥‥カクニンして おきたいコトがあるッス。
English: Can I ask you something?

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Sure.

Japanese: アンタたち‥‥ここへ来たのは、 あくまで、捜査のためッスよね？
English: You only came here to look around, right? Because it's one of the SL-9 crime scenes.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》の“現場”と いうことで。
English: I mean, that's your only reason for coming here, isn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ どうして、そんなコトを？ ...
English: Why do you ask?

Japanese: まさか‥‥いや。 まさか、アンタたち‥‥いや。
English: You don't think... Nah! You wouldn't be... No.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ ... いや。ヤッパリ、いいッス。 気にしないでほしいッス。
English: No, there's no way. Never mind. Don't worry about it.
